
Chapter 201 Furious, Punishments Issued


Heinous crimes.

Extremely vicious.

These descriptions are insufficient to depict Young Master Yun.

Headquarters also possesses information about the Yun family, and Young Master Yun's criminal record is extensive.

The lack of action is due to the inability to act.

He's been overseas, making arrest impossible; the only recourse is to continuously escalate the wanted level.

Yunbao is the primary drug trafficking route, bar none.

Moreover, the family's operations involve massive human trafficking and drug smuggling, among other issues.

Meanwhile, Zhao Wei and Li Zijin, disheveled and exhausted, rushed over, hands on their knees, breathing heavily.

Just moments ago...

Jiang Tian had them assist the trainees in their evacuation. In reality, their presence inside would have been pointless; to Jiang Tian, ​​they would have been a significant burden.

After they organized the trainees' evacuation and hurriedly notified the armed police and the municipal police bureau, both of them had already arrived.

After the two sides met, Zhao Wei and Li Zijin ran back without stopping.

They were exhausted from the journey.

Of course, at this moment, they first saw the injury on Jiang Tian's hand, and then, after confirming that there were no other bullet wounds on his body, they breathed a sigh of relief.

Then, their gaze fell on Zeng Fan, who was standing not far from them, trembling all over.

For Zeng Fan and his apprentice Gong Zhe,

the situation today was absolutely bewildering, and they couldn't accept this reality.

Right under their noses, such a huge achievement!

Right under their noses, a major wanted criminal of headquarters, and also an important heir of the Yun family, was hidden.

What did capturing this person mean?

As people from headquarters, they understood all too well what this result meant, because they now had a legitimate reason to exert pressure through Mr. Yun.

If those overseas criminal groups continue to act with impunity, they can use this Cloud Leopard to settle scores at any time.

This achievement...

was right under their noses.

Not only did they fail to notice.

Most terrifyingly, they would even face severe punishment.

Because at that moment...

The riot police who rushed in first were already carrying the bodies of interns, one after another, past them.

One...

Two...

Eight...

As he counted the later ones, Gong Zhe's voice trembled.

And the joy felt by everyone present at the capture of Mr. Yun vanished instantly, replaced by a sudden sense of heaviness.

Soon...

The eight interns' bodies were laid out in a row, while on the other side, the bodies of the armed forces who had come to rescue Mr. Yun were also laid out in rows.

Completely annihilated.

Thirty-two people were neatly arranged, and the air reeked of blood.

More than forty corpses lay on the ground.

The forensic doctor was also present, filming each body with a camera.

"Come on, let the trainee officers in the back take a look," Gao Xiaolong said after a moment of silence, waving his hand.

Accompanied by the sound of dripping water, hundreds of newly graduated police trainees stood before rows of corpses, their legs trembling.

"What are your thoughts?" After a long silence, Gao Xiaolong finally spoke in a deep voice.

Silence.

This scene was incredibly shocking for ordinary people, especially for the trainees who had graduated less than a month ago and were still largely in the learning phase of their police careers.

An ordinary person.

Suddenly, more than forty corpses were laid out before them.

Such overwhelming shock was unsettling.

Even Jiang Tian, ​​when he killed for the first time, felt a primal urge of panic and nausea.

"This time, you lost eight people. These eight were unlucky enough to escape behind you, but have you considered what would have happened to you without your examiners? What would have happened to you without Comrade Jiang covering your retreat?" Gao Xiaolong roared.

Enraged.

On his way there, he learned about the situation.

If everyone had followed Jiang Tian's orders and retreated in an orderly fashion, so many people wouldn't have been killed.

But what was the final result?

Because of their disobedience, when faced with the drug dealers' threat, despite being able to retreat in an orderly manner, everyone panicked, some even throwing away their issued pistols.

This ultimately led to chaos.

In fact, if Jiang Tian hadn't been there, instantly killing anyone who showed themselves, several armed drug dealers in quick succession, the death toll wouldn't have been ten or eight.

Gao Xiaolong had been holding back, waiting to see how Jiang Tian fared.

Only after seeing that Jiang Tian was out of danger did Gao Xiaolong finally breathe a sigh of relief.

Then, a surge of anger welled up within him.

Good, good, good…

Gong Zhe, a major drug lord right under his nose, didn't even see him, yet he confidently declared there was absolutely no danger, patting his chest to guarantee 100% safety.

Then, a large number of armed men arrived, and they were even a group with terrifying fighting capabilities.

The situation was already fraught with danger, and Jiang Tian also had to cover the retreat of a group of sheltered young women.

The key issue was that they disobeyed orders during the retreat.

This ultimately led to serious consequences.

One factor was that the retreat took too long, causing Lin Jinxuan to miss her best escape time, allowing the drug traffickers to occupy advantageous terrain.

In a short period of time,

on-site professionals had already conducted a highly professional analysis of the entire scene.

All the details were exposed.

This was what enraged Gao Xiaolong.

“Drug enforcement is a dangerous profession. Every day, we face countless temptations from criminals. You've all seen the danger. If it weren't for your examiners today, you would all be wiped out here. Now, if you don't want to, and you're all from investigation backgrounds, you can even transfer to criminal investigation. There's still danger, but it's infinitely safer than drug enforcement,” Gao Xiaolong said calmly.

Some people below lit up with excitement.

But others kept their heads down, remaining silent.

Standing to the side, watching the evasive glances of his former classmates, Jiang Tian couldn't help but laugh.

Do you really think the police force is your personal property, that you can just transfer officers however you want?

If they really did transfer,

they wouldn't be able to go to criminal investigation, they probably wouldn't even be able to do their internship, and they might even end up being assigned directly to a small rural police station.

Jiang Tian, ​​who spent over ten years at the bottom of the system in his previous life, was already a seasoned veteran, and he understood all this official jargon perfectly.

Especially Gao Xiaolong's personality, Jiang Tian knew it all too well.

He hated evil like poison and couldn't tolerate any injustice.

If these people chose to give up and transfer directly, their future was completely ruined, unless some major change occurred, like finding a merit award on the street.



Chapter 202, with Gong Zhe's approval, completely blew Zeng Fan away.


"To save you, Comrade Xiao Jiang will cover your retreat. As for the number of qualified officers today, my decision is that none of them are qualified. I will advise your superiors that, given your abilities, not only will you not be promoted early, but your internships will be postponed until you learn to follow orders."

Boom!!!

Accompanying Gao Xiaolong's words,

many people on the scene collapsed.

Their faces were filled with panic.

None of them were qualified?

But for Gao Xiaolong, this was merely the beginning of his thunderous rage.

Gao Xiaolong didn't actually mind Gong Zhe trying to steal the credit, because vying for credit meant high enthusiasm.

That was actually a good thing.

However, Gao Xiaolong absolutely could not accept that Gong Zhe, as a veteran police officer, had made such a mistake.

"Gong Zhe has committed multiple fatal errors during the mission and is temporarily suspended from all duties pending further discussion with Lao Tan," Gao Xiaolong continued.

But, what happened next was earth-shattering.

The moment those words were spoken,

the scene erupted in an uproar.

If the failure of all one hundred police officers was merely an appetizer, then the suspension of Gong Zhe was the true feast.

"I will personally discuss all the details of the mission with Lao Tan," Gao Xiaolong said expressionlessly.

But those who knew Gao Xiaolong knew...

Just like when Gao Xiaolong angrily berated Jiang Tian, ​​the angrier he was, the calmer his face became.

"On what grounds?" At this moment, Zeng Fan could no longer hold back and stepped forward, his eyes red.

He'd traveled all the way from Kyoto, expecting to crush his opponents and reap the rewards.

But in the blink of an eye...

His master had been suspended. How could he accept that?

Gao Xiaolong's previously calm face grew increasingly grim. "On what grounds? Because of your master's erroneous intelligence, leading to such serious consequences! Because of your brick factory's carelessness, which nearly resulted in the deaths of dozens of hostages!"

"Zeng Fan, shut up! Don't say another word!" Just as Zeng Fan was about to speak, Gong Zhe stepped forward to stop him.

In that instant, Gong Zhe seemed to have aged considerably.

"Chief Gao, I take full responsibility for all the mistakes in this mission, and..."

Gong Zhe paused slightly, then turned to look at Jiang Tian.

At that moment, Jiang Tian had just pushed aside two people carrying a stretcher. What a joke! He didn't need a stretcher.

He had a gun in his hand, not a bullet in his leg.

As Gong Zhe looked over, Jiang Tian squinted at him. He knew that when Gong Zhe arrived, he had been incredibly arrogant, blatantly belittling the Yunhai Narcotics Control Team and showing deep contempt for them.

Just as Jiang Tian thought Gong Zhe was about to say something...

*Snap!*

Gong Zhe saluted Jiang Tian.

This salute stunned Jiang Tian.

He had seen from the beginning that Gong Zhe was arrogant and somewhat conceited, and Jiang Tian had no good feelings towards him.

But now, Gong Zhe's actions were truly unexpected, leaving Jiang Tian utterly bewildered.

Gong Zhe took a deep breath and said, "Comrade Jiang, before this, I apologize for my prejudice. Thank you today for averting a major loss for the Party and the people."

Gong Zhe wasn't stupid.

He simply harbored deep prejudice against Yunhai.

He harbored deep resentment towards the Yunhai Municipal Public Security Bureau, coming here with the intention of finding fault, educating them, and even eager to demonstrate his abilities to show the Yunhai Municipal Bureau how a true anti-drug police officer should act.

But now, he admitted he was wrong.

“Master, even you…” Zeng Fan's eyes widened, his composure crumbling.

Gong Zhe glanced at Zeng Fan and angrily said, “Zeng Fan, stop fooling around. Haven't you seen clearly? If it weren't for Jiang Tian today, all the trainees would have been wiped out because of my negligence. Eight comrades sacrificed their lives, but there's still a chance to salvage the situation. If we were all wiped out, we would be condemned to death. Do you understand?”

Zeng Fan's head buzzed as he took two steps back.

Why, even his teacher sided with Jiang Tian?

Just because he was a little lucky.

Indeed, in Zeng Fan's eyes, Jiang Tian was just lucky. He was simply careless—this time, no, twice, oh no, three times—each time just a hair's breadth away from success. If he had been more careful, he could have done a thousand times better than Jiang Tian.

His team members were all elites, the cream of the crop in drug enforcement.

He despised Jiang Tian's so-called individual heroism even more.

No matter how strong an individual is, they can't compare to the power of a team—that's what his master had always taught him!

Jiang Tian might be slightly stronger individually, but in terms of professionalism in apprehending criminals, Zeng Fan always believed Jiang Tian was just lucky.

He had spent eight years, enduring countless hardships, to become a First-Class Superintendent. Why was Jiang Tian already a Third-Class Inspector, a higher rank than him, before even graduating?

Now, what was even more devastating was that the great achievement that was within his grasp had been taken by Jiang Tian.

He couldn't accept it.

*Slap!*

The next moment, Gong Zhe slapped Zeng Fan across the face: "Apologize to Comrade Jiang immediately."

"Master," Zeng Fan's eyes were bloodshot.

*Slap!*

Gong Zhe slapped him again: "I said I apologize."

Zeng Fan's face flushed red, and with his bloodshot eyes, he gritted his teeth and looked at Jiang Tian, ​​saying each word slowly and deliberately, "I'm sorry."

"There's no such thing as good luck in drug enforcement. Those who catch drug dealers deserve everyone's respect, not question whether they were lucky. Most importantly, it's not your place to question that," Gong Zhe said, almost enunciating each word.

Zeng Fan, head bowed, face flushed, clenched his fists and said, "I understand, Master."

At this moment, Gong Zhe turned his gaze solemnly to Jiang Tian and said, "There may have been many misunderstandings before, but, Comrade Jiang, we've been in Kyoto for so many years, perhaps we really were too naive. In the anti-drug industry, you are countless times more outstanding than me."

Jiang Tian nodded without saying anything, because his hand was still dripping blood, so he turned and left, leaving everyone with only his back view.

Facing the sunlight, his shadow stretched long.

It left those present unable to shake off the feeling for a long time.

But without a doubt.

Today was explosive.

Especially, Jiang Tian was completely blown away internally.

One person, covering the retreat of over a hundred trainee police officers, such behavior.

It can be said that he set a shining example.

Especially Gao Xiaolong's eyes flickered, looking at Jiang Tian's back with great admiration.

This time, Jiang Tian's case could even be recorded in the history of drug enforcement.

Because if all one hundred-plus trainee police officers died here, it would be a devastating blow to the entire drug enforcement industry.

Gao Xiaolong could already imagine the uproar this news would cause.

Meanwhile,

Inside the ambulance parked on the road below the mountain, two doctors and two nurses, wearing masks, were waiting outside.



Chapter 203 Minister Tan was dumbfounded. Had he made another great contribution?


"Director, are there still injured people on the mountain?" The young nurse looked curiously at the doctor beside her.

Just now, all the injured trainees had been taken away in ambulances.

This place was still unused.

The doctor, addressed as "Director," glanced down at the nurse, then nodded slightly after a moment.

The nurse looked up. To be honest, for ordinary people like them, this mysterious scene certainly aroused great curiosity.

"Director, what kind of operation is this?" the nurse asked immediately.

"Xiao Meng, this is a police arrest operation. They're definitely criminals. I don't know the specifics!" The director, though wearing a mask, had very fair eyes and half his face visible.

His voice was also very magnetic.

"But I've never seen so many police officers before, and military vehicles too." Xiao Meng's curiosity grew.

"Maybe there are more criminals," the director patiently answered the curious child.

“But we haven’t seen any criminals either. They’ve only brought out people in police uniforms,” Xiao Meng said.

Sure enough, even the director’s eyes lit up with curiosity at this.

Because it was true.

Over this period, almost all those brought out were injured people in police uniforms; there hadn’t been any criminals.

Just as the director was about to say something…

The next moment.

Suddenly, a large group of dark figures appeared in the distance.

The next moment.

Appearing before them were pairs of corpses, all with their arms and legs limp.

The scene was chilling, because they weren’t covered at all; their faces were exposed directly in front of them.

“Director, look,” Xiao Meng pointed at the corpses.

The director gasped in shock: “So many corpses?”

Even the director felt dizzy.

Because there were just too many corpses.

No wonder they hadn’t noticed one of the criminal’s injured victims earlier.

Because each one was unrecognizable.

"Quick, quick, ambulance and paramedics, the captain's wounded!"

A loud shout rang out; it was Zhao Wei, waving and yelling from afar.

"Stretcher, quick!" the chief physician directed the people inside the ambulance.

When the chief physician rushed to the injured man,

"Where's the injured man?" the chief physician asked urgently.

"Are you blind, woman? Can't you see the injured man?" Zhao Wei shouted angrily, pointing at Jiang Tian.

"Zhao Wei, what's the rush?" Jiang Tian quickly stopped Zhao Wei's outburst.

"Huh?"

The chief physician looked at Jiang Tian.

Sure enough, his face was slightly pale, but his gait and movements didn't resemble an injured man at all.

"What are you standing there for? Didn't you see the captain was shot?" Zhao Wei pointed at Jiang Tian's arm.

Sure enough, at that moment, drops of blood were still dripping.

"You've been shot?"

The director looked at Jiang Tian's young face. The narcotics captain was startled, for he couldn't tell Jiang Tian had been shot at all.

"Yes, here," Jiang Tian said, extending his arm.

The director quickly reached out and unbuttoned Jiang Tian's sleeve. Jiang Tian noticed that the director had long, slender hands with very fair skin, but when his hands touched Jiang Tian, ​​they felt noticeably cold.

Hiss!!!

"You were shot, why did you run down? This is ridiculous!" the director said hastily.

Because at this moment, there was a circular, bloody hole in Jiang Tian's arm, flesh rolled outwards, and blood was still oozing out—a horrific sight.

The director quickly bandaged Jiang Tian's wound, his eyes glancing at Jiang Tian's young face. Watching the bodies being carried away behind him, the scene deeply shocked him.

Because Jiang Tian's face was unusually young.

Once Jiang Tian was in the ambulance, lying on the stretcher with his eyes closed, resting.

The doctors and nurses in the ambulance stared blankly at Jiang Tian's calm expression.

"Aren't you in pain?" the director finally couldn't help but ask.

Meanwhile, Jiang Tian was actually checking his system feed. After eliminating this group of drug dealers, Jiang Tian's sin value surged again.

Jiang Tian's sin value had previously dropped to just one point after upgrading to the red-level "King of Criminal Investigation."

Now, after apprehending this group of drug dealers, Jiang Tian's sin value had returned to 10,000 points.

Jiang Tian was now checking the "Rebirth" skill.

If there was one weakness Jiang Tian currently had, it was undoubtedly his mortal body.

His combat power, speed, and strength were all sufficient; the only thing lacking was his injury recovery ability.

Jiang Tian tentatively spent 100 sin points to upgrade the white-level "Rebirth" skill to green.

[Ding, Rebirth skill upgrade successful.]

[Rebirth (White) - Rapid Healing (Green)] (You will have more than three times the injury recovery rate of an ordinary person.)

The effect was immediate; the wound that had been bleeding profusely had gradually stopped bleeding.

[Spot on target (Gold)]

[Scanning radar (Gold)]

[Extraordinary human (Gold)]

[Martial arts master (Red)]

[King of criminal investigation (Red)]

[Photographic memory (Blue)]

[Rapid healing (Green)]

Special Attributes

[Eye of Judgment]

1: [God who governs body shape]

2: [God of Wealth's blessing (automatically generates four yuan per minute)] (Reserve funds: 15772) (5/6) Next stage reward: Rolls-Royce.

Current sin value: 13100

Jiang Tian was satisfied and didn't add any more points. If he continued, the wound would probably heal itself before he even reached the hospital.

Just then, the director's very magnetic voice opened, instantly attracting Jiang Tian's attention to the director's chest.

Don't misunderstand.

It's just that the nameplate on his chest reads: Liao Ying, Director of the Emergency Department of Yunhai City Hospital.

Liao Ying?

Jiang Tian looked at the photo on the nameplate, then glanced at Director Liao.

He was quite certain that Director Liao was no more than twenty-five years old. Such a young director?

"It doesn't hurt." Jiang Tian wasn't lying; as a superhuman, he really didn't feel anything.

But others didn't think so.

The doctors and nurses inside the ambulance silently looked at Jiang Tian, ​​their eyes flashing with shock and admiration.

In their eyes, what doesn't hurt? Getting shot and not feeling pain? That's utter nonsense!

What could have turned such a young man into this? Without a doubt, there's only one answer: an iron will.

"Brother, are you from the Yunhai City Police Bureau?" Nurse Xiaomeng helped Director Liao bandage his wound.

"Yes, what's wrong?" Jiang Tian asked casually without resistance.

"I heard the Yunhai City Police Bureau is very..." Xiaomeng began.

"Xiaomeng, shut up!" Director Liao hurriedly scolded, then apologized to Jiang Tian, ​​"Comrade, I'm sorry."

"It's alright, I understand." Jiang Tian's lips twitched.

Then, silence fell in the carriage. Jiang Tian ignored it, simply closing his eyes to rest, because he really needed to.

It was already noon when they arrived at the hospital.

They arrived at the hospital around 1 PM.

Then, the surgery proceeded without pause. It wasn't anything serious; just local anesthesia, which had little effect on Jiang Tian. He essentially endured it, but for him, it made no difference.

He didn't get back to his ward until 3 PM.

During their conversation on the way back, Jiang Tian learned that Liao Ying was a genius. She had skipped grades multiple times, entering high school at twelve, graduating from university at fourteen, and now, at twenty-four, she had already earned her doctorate.

She also had a younger brother who had skipped school.

This was Jiang Tian's basic understanding. After all, the feeling of having his life in someone else's hands was enough to trigger a stress reaction in him.

The entire Yunhai region was in complete uproar.

Not only Yunhai, but even headquarters was in complete chaos.

For no other reason than that.

Because Jiang Tian had been shot?

And he had been shot while protecting over a hundred trainees, covering their retreat.

He ensured the survival of over a hundred trainees.

This was far more meritorious than simply eliminating the drug traffickers.

Especially considering the sheer number of merits Jiang Tian had accumulated.

Minister Tan was astonished upon hearing this.

"What did you say? This kid covered the retreat of over a hundred trainees, ensuring their survival, then counter-encircled and wiped out the drug traffickers, and even captured Yun Bao, the heir of the Yun family and a major wanted criminal by headquarters?"

"He's done another great service?"

Gao Xiaolong said quietly, "Wrong, a great service."



Chapter 204 The Protagonist of the Sister-Killing Case? Director Liao Wants a Chat


A quiet hospital room.

At this moment, only Lin Jinxuan was present.

Jiang Tian hadn't let Jiang Peng tell his family, mainly because he didn't want his sister and mother to worry.

After all, it wasn't a particularly serious injury for him.

If Jiang Tian wanted, he could level up and recover quickly.

As for the two team members, Zhao Wei and Li Zijin, they needed to handle some follow-up issues at the police station, including supplementing and confirming detailed information.

So in the end, only Lin Jinxuan was allowed to get into the police car.

“Officer Jiang, your girlfriend is quite pretty.” After examining Jiang Tian, ​​Liao Ying, hands in her white coat, glanced at Lin Jinxuan with her beautiful eyes.

This startled Lin Jinxuan: “No, no, she's just a comrade.”

Liao Ying nodded and explained some post-operative precautions.

As she spoke, Liao Ying's eyes kept darting to the blood-stained police uniform hanging on the rack beside her.

Two stripes and one badge were still hanging on its shoulder.

On the hospital bed above Jiang Tian's head, his age was clearly written:

Twenty-one years old.

Only twenty-one years old.

Just a year or two older than her younger brother, yet he had already risen to a leadership position in the police force and even fought drug dealers.

Liao Ying thought of her brother, and her heart ached even more.

"Do you understand?" Liao Ying asked, lifting her mask.

The mask was off, allowing Jiang Tian to see Liao Ying's face clearly.

"I understand."

Jiang Tian nodded, but his gaze was fixed intently on Liao Ying, his mind flashing with memories.

Actually, although Liao Ying was beautiful, Jiang Tian didn't have much interest in women, especially since everyone around him was stunningly beautiful.

What truly stunned Jiang Tian was the memory of a case from his past life: the murder of his sister.

The younger brother, addicted to heroin, asked his sister for money, and when she refused, he killed her and stole the money.

This case was rather heinous. In his previous life, Jiang Tian worked in the archives and had a strong recollection of this case; it was a case transferred from the criminal investigation department to the anti-drug department.

The female lead's name seemed to be Liao Ying.

But before Jiang Tian could delve deeper into it, a shout suddenly came from the doorway: "Director Liao!"

Jiang Tian turned to look at the door and saw a person in a white coat.

"Dean Wang." A flash of disgust crossed Liao Ying's eyes, clearly caught by Jiang Tian.

Under Jiang Tian's gaze, Dean Wang was a bald man, and the name tag on his chest read "Vice Dean."

Jiang Tian subconsciously wanted to light a cigarette, but realizing where he was, he quickly abandoned the thought.

At that moment, Vice Dean Wang walked in and angrily said to Liao Ying, "Who gave you permission to open this VIP ward without authorization?"

Liao Ying's face was not pleasant as she replied, "Dean Zhou arranged it. Is there a problem?"

"Is he qualified?" Dean Wang said, suppressing his anger.

Before Liao Ying could speak, Dean Wang said directly, "Now, immediately transfer him to a regular ward. Another patient will be transferred in soon. Remember, from now on, don't just transfer anyone to a VIP ward."

"Stop right there! Why should we transfer him?"

Just then, Lin Jinxuan, who was accompanying her in the ward, stood up angrily.

Normally, Lin Jinxuan was rarely angry and was very well-mannered, but at this moment, she couldn't hold back anymore.

Jiang Tian, ​​who saved over a hundred interns, wiped out dozens of drug dealers, and captured a wanted criminal from Zhongda Tong, making outstanding contributions, doesn't deserve to be in this ward. Who else could?

"Why?"

Dean Wang even smiled directly: "Because you don't deserve it, okay? Besides, this ward has already been assigned to other people. You can go to a regular ward without causing any trouble. Don't go looking for trouble."

"Hey, Jiang Tian, ​​where are you going?" Lin Jinxuan was about to speak when she suddenly saw Jiang Tian had run to the cupboard and was rummaging through something.

Hearing the sound, Liao Ying turned around and was instantly startled.

Heaven knows, Jiang Tian had just had surgery and had just been given anesthesia. Even if he wasn't completely unconscious from the anesthesia, what if his wound reopened from all this running and jumping?

Liao Ying hurriedly ran up to stop Jiang Tian: "You just had surgery, you can't move around like that. Even if you're being transferred to a ward, you can't rush it. I'll call Dean Zhou."

Yes.

In her mind, Jiang Tian must have gotten up to change rooms after hearing Dean Wang's words.

But you just had surgery!

Dean Wang stopped in his tracks.

Looking at Jiang Tian's actions, he smiled mockingly: "Director Liao, your thinking is seriously flawed. You're not even as self-aware as the patient. Look how considerate you are! If you dare to make decisions on your own again, you'll be suspended."

These words nearly made Liao Ying's nose twist with anger.

She knew that Jiang Tian had just fought criminals on the front lines, returning bloodied and battered.

He carried immense glory.

And this is how you treat him?

Does protecting the country not deserve a VIP ward?

"Dean Wang, then I'd like to ask, if anti-drug police officers protecting the people don't deserve VIP wards, then who does?" Liao Ying questioned angrily.

Dean Wang wasn't surprised at all.

Because he had just checked and found that all the VIP wards were full, and among all the VIP wards, only Jiang Tian's was the simplest.

Moreover, he works in a dangerous branch of the police force, so he probably doesn't have any powerful connections. Plus, the person about to be admitted is very important, so he directly arranged this ward for him.

As for the injured police officers, arranging them in regular wards wouldn't be a problem, since some injured trainee officers are already in regular wards.

“The son of a health department leader has kidney stones. Is a lowly policeman like him fit to be here?” Dean Wang bluntly stated.

“You…I need to see Dean Zhou.”

Liao Ying took out her phone, but Jiang Tian had already broken free of her hand, leading Liao Ying to believe he was in a hurry to leave: “Officer Jiang, don't rush off. Let me make a call. I need to talk to Dean Zhou.”

Dean Wang, sporting a receding hairline, smiled and said, “Young man, if you know your place…”

He trailed off.

Out of the corner of his eye, Dean Wang caught a glimpse of a dark figure.

He looked closer and suddenly realized that what Jiang Tian was holding wasn't luggage, but a dark, hard baton.

No, a police baton.

“What are you doing?” Dean Wang exclaimed in shock.

*Bang!*

"What are you to me?" Jiang Tian slammed his stick directly onto the bald man's receding hairline.

Dean Wang landed on his backside with a thud, his eyes nearly bulging out.

How dare he?

How dare he?

"You son of a bitch, you're finished..." Dean Wang clutched his head.

Bang!!!

The next moment, Jiang Tian kicked him directly in the face, looking down at him coldly and saying, "Do you deserve it? You think you deserve it? So what if you're a policeman? Without the police, could you live so comfortably? Without us low-ranking policemen, who would you turn to if you got beaten up? Without us in front of you, where would your privileged life come from?"

Jiang Tian was now burning with rage.



Chapter 205: Applying for First-Class Heroic Model Status – A Complete Explosion


It wasn't because of him personally.

Even if someone had a conflict with Jiang Tian, ​​or a strained relationship, their sacrifice would be a huge blow to him.

Personal relationships didn't matter.

But Jiang Tian had seen too many sacrifices within the anti-drug team.

Too many people sacrificed themselves in unknown corners.

They had no names, walked in the darkness, faced hail of bullets, and died without even leaving a will.

These were all junior police officers, but why didn't they deserve to be here?

Without them, countless drug dealers and criminal gangs would have swept through the city.

Wasn't the pain left by the Opium Wars enough?

“Madman, you're a madman!” Dean Wang wailed, clutching his head.

Meanwhile…

In an office in Kyoto, Minister Tan held his phone. After a moment of silence, he stood up, walked to the window, and looked at the bustling traffic outside.

Taking a deep drag of his cigarette, Minister Tan finally calmed his agitated mind.

Another meritorious deed.

The key is, another monumental one.

The Special Merit Citation had just been awarded, and this kid had already accomplished something so significant.

This merit, to put it another way, saved a hundred comrades and turned the tide of a small-scale battle.

Wait…

“You just said you captured Yun Bao, the heir of the Yun family?”

Minister Tan jumped to his feet.

“That’s right.” Gao Xiaolong understood Minister Tan’s feelings.

“Is he alive?” Minister Tan asked quickly.

“He suffered some injuries, but his life isn’t in danger. Jiang Tian was too ruthless; two batons almost crippled him.” Gao Xiaolong complained.

Meanwhile, on the other end of the phone, Minister Tan’s face was almost black with rage.

Boasting.

This was definitely boasting.

However, Minister Tan wasn't concerned with those things right now; the main issue remained the same.

Gao Xiaolong's tone gradually became serious: "Once we capture Yunbao, we can make a direct breakthrough."

Minister Tan paced back and forth in his office, stopping beside a potted plant before speaking: "How is Xiao Jiang's injury?"

"He was shot in the arm," Gao Xiaolong replied.

At this point, Gao Xiaolong paused slightly, then said, "Xiao Jiang's actions this time have saved the Party and the people from enormous losses; we must acknowledge this."

"What are you thinking?" Minister Tan rubbed his temples.

A first-class merit was no longer quite right.

A special-class merit, Jiang Tian had just received.

In current history, there's no precedent for receiving two special-class merits in consecutive days, not even in recent decades.

However, even without Yunbao, Jiang Tian's achievements far exceeded a first-class merit.

And most importantly, capturing Yunbao alive truly held strategic significance.

Perhaps even Jiang Tian hadn't anticipated this.

A single clouded leopard.

Could even cause a change in the entire Cloud Sea—no, it should be said, a change far exceeding the Cloud Sea itself.

A terrifying storm was brewing.

"I request that Comrade Xiao Jiang be nominated for the title of First-Class Hero," Gao Xiaolong said in a deep voice.

Buzz!!!

Minister Tan abruptly stood up, his eyes wide. "You know how much resistance there was at the meeting to awarding the Special Merit Citation? As for the First-Class Heroic Model, Jiang Tian's age and experience are far from meeting the requirements. The meeting won't approve it. The Second-Class Heroic Model is already the limit, and adding someone to the already drafted list at the last minute will be even less likely."

"In the police force, there's only merit, not seniority. Whoever disagrees, let them personally arrest drug dealers!" Gao Xiaolong roared.

Minister Tan rubbed his temples with a wry smile, already pondering how to persuade those at the meeting.

After all, Jiang Tian's age was too young!

And he lacked the experience and maturity of time.

If it were ten years later, even if Jiang Tian hadn't achieved anything in those ten years, he could easily award Jiang Tian the First-Class Heroic Model title. But now, it was too difficult.

"It seems only the First-Class Heroic Model title is worthy of this merit," Minister Tan's gaze gradually hardened.

Isn't genius meant to break the rules?

After hanging up the phone, Gao Xiaolong turned to look at Gao Yangyang, who was staring at him expectantly.

She was pouting, looking quite angry.

"Fine, are you satisfied now?" Gao Xiaolong said irritably.

"It was enough," Gao Yangyang replied with a smirk.

This made Gao Xiaolong give Gao Yangyang a thorough once-over. As perceptive as he was, he sensed something was amiss.

"Xiao Jiang is currently receiving treatment at the hospital. At this time, he probably just had surgery not long ago and needs to rest," Gao Xiaolong said, frowning.

As he spoke, he glanced at the car. Xu Poye, as a high-ranking official in the anti-drug department, was there; Gao Xiaolong approved of him going along.

Jiang Peng, needless to say, was Jiang Tian's father. After finishing some arrangements for the case at the city bureau, it was perfectly understandable that he was going to visit his son.

Li Zijin and Zhao Wei, well, these two were Jiang Tian's teammates. It would be unreasonable to refuse them to go, given their concern for their captain.

One last look.

Song Liguo, Jiang Tian's direct superior.

Team leader.

Visiting a subordinate, he couldn't simply drive him away.

After looking around, he saw a whole car full of people; all nine official vehicles were occupied. Because of the presence of bigwigs like Gao Xiaolong and Xu Poye, Jiang Peng even had to arrange two police cars in front and behind the vehicles for protection.

"Let's make it clear first, once inside, everyone stay quiet. Don't disturb Comrade Jiang. Look quietly, and then leave." Gao Xiaolong understood everyone's feelings, as if it were his own.

The key point was, after looking around, everyone should go, except for his granddaughter, Gao Yangyang. What was wrong with her?

"Officer Jiang is my savior; I should visit him." Gao Yangyang walked ahead with her head held high and hands behind her back.

"Comrade Jiang has made a great contribution this time, and everyone has worked hard. Don't worry, you'll all receive the rewards you deserve."

The three cars had already arrived at the hospital parking lot, moving without obstruction.

What a joke.

With an official car and two police cars accompanying them, who would dare to stop them?

Gao Xiaolong led the way, walking ahead with Xu Poye.

The others, all dressed in police uniforms, followed.

Moreover, apart from Zhao Wei and a few others, almost everyone else was dressed in a white shirt.

They attracted a crowd of onlookers along the way.

They didn't even know what was going on, thinking some major criminal had appeared.

Upon reaching the hospital entrance, Dean Zhou was already waiting.

He and his entourage greeted them warmly.

"Chief Gao, rest assured, our hospital used the most advanced equipment for treatment and surgery. The surgery went very smoothly, and the person is perfectly safe," Dean Zhou said immediately, knowing their concerns.

"Good, good, good."

Everyone was overjoyed.

Dean Zhou felt a chill run down his spine. So many big shots in white shirts—just who was this Officer Jiang?

Dean Zhou wondered to himself as he led the way: "He's upstairs. I'll take you up."

Soon, after navigating the corridor, they arrived directly at the top floor of the 17th floor, the VIP ward area.

Before getting off the elevator, Gao Xiaolong reiterated, "Everyone, please keep quiet. Don't disturb Comrade Jiang."

Everyone nodded repeatedly.

But as the elevator doors opened,

they were greeted not by the quiet environment of the VIP ward, but by deafening screams.

And...

A woman's sharp, sarcastic voice rang out: "If I hit you, I'll only pay you a couple of cents. If you hit me, your parents will have to kneel down and beg me."

Gao Xiaolong's smiling face gradually froze.



Chapter 206 He's gone mad, this patient has gone mad.


Do you know how devastating those words were to Gao Xiaolong, who hated evil as much as he did?

"So what if you have that skin? You hit Dean Zhou, so all you got was that skin, a few days in jail, and some compensation. But now, do you dare hit me? If you do, even your parents will have to kneel down before me." The woman's arrogant voice rang out again.

Who?

Who was it?

So arrogant in public.

Who was she talking to?

Who dared to say those words?

Gao Xiaolong stepped out of the elevator and around the corner, his expression freezing instantly.

What he saw:

Jiang Tian's arm was bandaged, and he held a police baton.

In front of Jiang Tian, ​​a middle-aged woman was charging at him, baring her teeth and claws.

Buzz!!!

She's gone mad.

She's completely insane.

"You son of a bitch, how dare you lay a hand on a drug-fighting hero who just had surgery last night and performed a great service after being injured?!"

One glance over.

Good heavens.

The scene erupted.

"Stop!!!"

Before anyone could react, including Gao Xiaolong, whose head was spinning, Dean Zhou reacted immediately.

He let out a sharp shout.

The woman remained silent, her emotions running high, and still lunged at Jiang Tian.

At this moment, Jiang Tian was also enraged.

After he hit Dean Wang, this woman rushed over, anxiously asking why his son's surgery was almost over and why a ward hadn't been arranged yet.

Then Dean Wang complained to the woman, saying that he, a lowly policeman, was refusing to leave a high-class bed and even assaulting people. The woman then launched into a tirade against Jiang Tian, ​​hurling insults like, "What kind of person are you to be refusing to leave and assaulting people? How can you be such a scumbag?"

Regardless of whether it was because his son was anxious or not, Jiang Tian showed no leniency.

Then he retorted sarcastically, "Whose dog ran away?"

The woman immediately exploded.

Seeing the police baton in Jiang Tian's hand, she fearlessly lunged forward, repeating the same words.

Fine, fine.

Jiang Tian's anger flared.

He admitted that the anti-drug squad and the overall security situation had some problems before, but this was definitely not the city bureau's fault, because the provincial department was also located there.

It was because the unique geographical environment and location had created a perfect haven for crime.

All the police officers in the city bureau worked overtime like crazy every day for meager salaries, working tirelessly to maintain order.

Especially recently, on average, each person slept less than five hours a day, spending the rest of their time eating or investigating cases and making arrests.

But their status and position did not receive the respect they deserved.

You can insult Jiang Tian.

But you cannot insult this profession.

Because this profession is sacred.

Those who turn their backs to the light create beauty and brightness in the world—Jiang Tian felt this deeply.

Seeing that Jiang Tian remained silent, the woman became even more arrogant, lunging forward with bared teeth and claws.

Soon.

They were almost touching.

The next moment.

Bang!!!

Splat!!!

Jiang Tian's baton struck the woman directly in the face.

Blood instantly gushed from the woman's nose.

The woman was instantly knocked to the ground by the baton.

Then, lying on the ground, the woman looked up, and a hint of clarity appeared in her crazed eyes.

She stared blankly at Jiang Tian, ​​seemingly genuinely surprised that he would make a move.

The woman, pointing at Jiang Tian, ​​trembled, "You're finished! You're dead! Your parents are kneeling before me, you're finished too, your family is finished!"

Finished?

Jiang Tian's gaze gradually turned icy.

The next moment, the woman finally saw Dean Zhou rushing towards her, along with many police officers in white shirts behind him. She was overjoyed, instinctively assuming Dean Zhou had brought her reinforcements.

"Dean Zhou, you've come at the right time! Grab him! Grab this beast! I'm going to tear his face apart!" the woman shouted.

Then she sneered at Jiang Tian, ​​"Kid, you're finished."

Inwardly, she screamed, "Dean Zhou is awesome! He's brought so many leaders! Today, I'm going to kill this bastard!"

But the next moment...

*Slap!*

Just as she was imagining this, Dean Zhou raised his hand and slapped her across the face.

The heavy slap left the woman stunned.

"Who gave you permission to make such a scene in the hospital and even assault a post-operative patient?" Dean Zhou roared.

Dean Zhou had met Jiang Tian before. Last time, when he insisted on admitting Jiang Tian, ​​he had brought Gao Xiaolong into the ward, where Gao Xiaolong had even presented Jiang Tian with awards.

Now, they were coming in such a large group.

Undoubtedly, Jiang Tian had done something again. As a seasoned veteran in the system, Dean Zhou understood all too well.

Being valued so highly by Chief Gao, Dean Zhou naturally knew whose side he was on.

"Zhou Daoguang, are you crazy?" the woman shrieked.

At that moment...

Thump, thump, thump!!!

Fast footsteps sounded.

On the scene.

As the only two people with the lowest ranks, Li Zijin and Zhao Wei, they naturally received a single glance from Gao Xiaolong.

They both instantly understood. Zhao Wei pulled out handcuffs from behind his waist and, with a click, cuffed the woman.

"What are you doing? What are you trying to do?" the woman struggled frantically.

At that moment, Gao Xiaolong approached step by step, saying to the woman, "I don't care who you are, I don't care what your background is, but now, in the name of headquarters, I am arresting you for obstructing official business and publicly assaulting a police officer."

Gao Xiaolong spoke as calmly as possible.

But this completely stunned the woman.

After recovering, the woman's mind went blank. What did this person just say?

In the name of headquarters?

Headquarters?

"And this, he's the one who brought this woman here." Jiang Tian was speechless.

The woman insulted him, and he insulted her back, driving her completely mad.

"Wang Gang?" Dean Zhou looked at Vice Dean Wang lying on the ground, unsure.

At this moment, Vice Dean Wang's face was swollen, looking like he had been badly beaten, making Dean Zhou click his tongue in astonishment. This Officer Jiang was a bit too terrifying!

They were aiming for his face and head.

Wang Gang, lying on the ground, was terrified and pretending to be dead.

He had actually heard everything that had just happened.

Because he had heard it, Wang Gang was completely dumbfounded.

Wait, wasn't he just a lowly policeman?

How did headquarters get involved?

"What happened?" Gao Xiaolong had already approached Jiang Tian and was asking.

Jiang Tian didn't embellish the story, stating directly, "I just finished surgery, and this Vice President Wang came in and told me to move to a different ward. Fine, I'll move him, but then he actually insulted us police officers."

Huh?

As a vice president, he did something like this?

How audacious!

Gao Xiaolong's eyes widened instantly, his breathing rapid. "Arrest them! Arrest them all! I want to see if these people can really control everything!"

Lying on the ground, Wang Gang stared wide-eyed at Jiang Tian.

Then, his entire body trembled with fear as he looked at Gao Xiaolong and Xu Poye.

As a member of the Party, how could Wang Gang not recognize these two?

Because he recognized them, he went mad...

Borrowing the weak, what did he end up with?

What exactly did he do?



Chapter 207 The problem isn't with the Municipal Bureau, it's with other groups.


Lin Jinxuan, standing to the side, jumped out impatiently, her face full of indignation, saying, "Sir, Officer Jiang just finished surgery, and not only did this hospital director jump out, but that woman even came out to insult Officer Jiang and tried to attack him after his surgery!"

"Who is that woman?" Gao Xiaolong's face was grim.

Ignoring Lin Jinxuan, he went directly to Wang Gang, who was lying on the ground. Gao Xiaolong's voice was icy, and a terrifying pressure emanated from him.

Wang Gang's face was covered in blood, making him look terrifying.

But at this moment, Wang Gang looked at Gao Xiaolong, his lips trembling. His gaze shifted shakily from Gao Xiaolong to Jiang Tian.

Isn't this just a lowly policeman?

Isn't this just a nobody?

Why?

Wang Gang's lips trembled.

"I asked you who that woman is, didn't you hear me?" Gao Xiaolong's tone grew even colder.

This voice instantly made Wang Gang tremble all over.

“It’s Leader Jing’s wife,” Wang Gang hurriedly said.

Gao Xiaolong looked somewhat confused.

After all, he was from Beijing, so he wasn’t particularly familiar with the specific situation in Yunhai.

But whoever it was,

Gao Xiaolong was determined to see it through.

He wanted to see just how powerful this woman was, that she could say, “If I hit you, I only need to pay you some money, but if you hit me, your parents have to kneel down and beg her.”

Meanwhile, Jiang Peng’s face darkened.

After all, what that woman had said had made him lose face; Jiang Tian was his son.

Especially when he heard the woman’s identity.

Jiang Peng quickly stepped forward and whispered, “Chief Gao, Leader Jing is the vice mayor who also oversees the health department.”

Hmm?

Hearing this, Gao Xiaolong’s gaze froze.

That’s quite a rank.

Almost on par with Jiang Peng’s rank.

Most importantly…

Jiang Peng had one more thing he didn't say: this person was also a strong competitor and rival in his race for the position of Secretary of the Political and Legal Affairs Commission.

Jiang Peng wasn't stupid; a fool wouldn't be in that position.

Jiang Tian had given him so much help when it was time to compete; how could Jiang Peng not strive for it?

This was the third-highest position in Yunhai.

Once he took it,

Jiang Peng would practically soar to the top.

And Jiang Tian could be said to have completely reversed his fate, forcing Jiang Peng, who should have been investigated and died in despair, to rise even higher through his own efforts.

"Of course, this matter should have nothing to do with Mayor Jing," Jiang Peng proactively defended him, using a strategy of retreat as a means of advancement.

He was gradually gaining the upper hand.

Adding insult to injury at this time would only damage the opinions of many leaders.

Sure enough.

Gao Xiaolong's gaze darkened. After a moment of silence, he said, "I don't care what kind of leader he is, or what he did. Using power to bully others is wrong. For a leader's wife to dare say such things to Xiao Jiang, what would happen to an ordinary person? They'd be eaten alive by that woman."

Gao Xiaolong couldn't bear to think about it.

If it weren't for Jiang Tian, ​​if he weren't here, if he were just an ordinary person, what consequences would he have faced facing that woman's harassment?

Most importantly, there was Wang Gang, who was aiding and abetting the wrongdoing.

After a pause, Gao Xiaolong, hands behind his back, glanced at Dean Zhou before speaking, “Before coming here, I always thought the Yunhai Municipal Public Security Bureau was the problem, leading to the rampant crime. Since arriving, I've seen the Bureau perform exceptionally well. In fact, it's no exaggeration to say that while there may have been many problems in the past, their performance recently has been remarkable. Now, I finally realize that the real problem isn't with the Bureau itself, but with the leadership.”

Gao Xiaolong's voice was icy.

Like father, like son.

Even if that person wasn't involved, doesn't Madam bear any responsibility for those words?

If she weren't habitually arrogant and domineering, how could she say such a thing?

“I will personally report these issues to my superiors and to the Discipline Inspection Commission and other departments,” Gao Xiaolong said, his face grim.

The impact of these words on Gao Xiaolong was unimaginable.

And for those present, the impact was even more terrifying.

Gao Xiaolong's superiors?

Everyone present was in a daze.

Good heavens.

Waking up to find the sky had fallen?

What would happen if this happened?

Everyone's heart skipped a beat.

Meanwhile, at the scene...

The most bewildered and shocked was undoubtedly Liao Ying.

Especially when Liao Ying saw Wang Gang get brutally beaten for targeting Jiang Tian, ​​and even the wealthy woman was beaten up.

The most terrifying thing was...

Jiang Tian himself was completely unharmed, but these two were handcuffed and taken away.

Of course, if Jiang Tian knew Liao Ying's shock, he would simply smile contemptuously.

Because, don't underestimate the destructive power of a new-era Special Merit Award recipient, a drug enforcement vanguard, and a second-class hero in the fight against drugs.

This was practically the invincible invincible force Jiang Tian had created.

"Doctor, is Officer Jiang alright?"

After the area outside was mostly cleared, Wang Gang, looking utterly distraught, was led away. The moment he was dragged up, he knew he was finished.

Completely finished.

He still didn't understand what kind of power Jiang Tian possessed, or why so many influential figures were willing to stand up for him.

But unfortunately, no one explained it to him.

The VIP ward was nice, and most importantly, it was spacious.

However, even with ample space, it couldn't accommodate the large number of people entering.

People like Zhao Wei and Li Zijin could only stand outside.

Jiang Tian was both amused and exasperated.

This gunshot wound wouldn't really harm Jiang Tian. Even for an ordinary person, a gunshot wound to the arm wouldn't be life-threatening; in fact, Jiang Tian would recover much better.

"Fortunately, it didn't penetrate the bone, so the surgery went smoothly," Liao Ying hurriedly explained.

"Will there be any aftereffects?" Gao Xiaolong quickly asked.

That was actually the most important question.

Why all this fuss?

Because if Jiang Tian suffers any lasting damage, it will be a devastating blow to his career.

"If the recovery goes well, he can return to his prime without much impact," Liao Ying said truthfully.

This instantly eased Gao Xiaolong's heavy heart.

"Xiao Jiang, make sure you get plenty of rest during this time," Gao Xiaolong instructed.

"Okay," Jiang Tian nodded.

"Good lad."

Jiang Peng patted Jiang Tian's shoulder, unable to resist making a wink.

Looking at his father's face, Jiang Tian sighed inwardly!

For him, across both his past and present lives, nearly twenty years had passed since he last saw his father's smiling face.

Countless times in the dead of night, Jiang Tian had dreamt of changing all of this.

And now.

Finally, history had been completely rewritten.



Chapter 208 Gao Xiaolong Returns to Headquarters, Undercurrents Surge


Liao Ying stood to the side, her heart pounding as she watched her superior offer words of comfort to Jiang Tian.

You see...

At first, Liao Ying genuinely thought Jiang Tian was just an ordinary junior policeman. After all, he was so young.

Furthermore, she knew little about the police profession, let alone police ranks. Liao Ying simply marveled at how young he was, already fighting crime on the front lines.

But after what had just happened, Liao Ying realized that Jiang Tian wasn't just any ordinary policeman; he was a highly dangerous narcotics officer.

Now, he had so many terrifying powerful figures under his command.

Even with her lack of common sense, Liao Ying understood the significance of a white shirt.

"Did you hear that? Rest well and don't jump around anymore," Gao Xiaolong instructed.

Jiang Tian nodded absentmindedly.

Aftereffects?

What a joke. For him, they didn't exist.

After everyone left, Gao Yangyang was still somewhat reluctant to part.

Jiang Tian felt genuinely awkward meeting Gao Yangyang.

Because, while they weren't exactly lovers, they weren't exactly friends either, given the two incidents they'd had together.

"I'll come back to see you again," Gao Yangyang waved to Jiang Tian.

Gao Xiaolong was incredibly busy, especially now that the police station was so busy, he couldn't possibly have much time to catch up with Jiang Tian.

In the end, even Jiang Peng left.

Seeing that Jiang Tian was alright, he needed to hurry back; this visit was purely by squeezing in time.

Heaven knows how busy the city bureau was right now.

There were still many issues to address, even requiring handover to the provincial department.

Finally, only the seemingly idle Lin Jinxuan and Dr. Liao Ying remained in the room, along with Song Liguo.

"Uncle Song, is there anything else?" Jiang Tian didn't believe Song Liguo could stay here at the city bureau.

Sure enough.

Song Liguo sighed, "You've gotten yourself into some trouble this time."

Even Liao Ying and Lin Jinxuan, the two women, unconsciously pricked up their ears to listen.

"What trouble? That Vice Mayor Jing?" Jiang Tian's gaze sharpened slightly.

Song Liguo glanced behind him, seeing only the two women, and couldn't help but sigh, "Do you know that your father is currently competing with Vice Mayor Jing for the position of Secretary of the Political and Legal Affairs Commission?"

Jiang Tian genuinely didn't know about this.

Although Jiang Peng usually taught him many things and shared a lot of information, lately he had been so busy that Jiang Tian had rarely been home.

"Uncle Song, just say what you want to say," Jiang Tian said, narrowing his eyes.

Reborn, although Jiang Tian spent most of his time focused on achieving merit and eradicating drug traffickers, what was the purpose of all this, this desperate effort?

Besides protecting his country, it was to change the fate of his family and himself.

Although he was unaware that Jiang Peng was competing, Jiang Tian keenly sensed something unusual.

“Actually, your father originally wanted to give it a try, so he pulled some strings. But his chances were slim to begin with; he was far inferior to Vice Mayor Jing in every way. However, you unexpectedly rose to prominence, and your father benefited from your success. Now he's very popular and has received many votes in the Standing Committee meeting. But today you hit Vice Mayor Jing's wife. Regardless of the reason, it will affect the direction of the votes.”

Song Liguo shook his head and said, “You were too young just now. If you hadn't acted, that woman's slightest touch would have sealed your fate instantly.”

Jiang Tian's eyes were deep. He still had a deep impression of Vice Mayor Jing.

Just like before, if Jiang Tian remembered correctly, Sun Haibo's background was Vice Mayor Jing.

It was precisely because he had Vice Mayor Jing's backing that he was able to clash with Jiang Tian's father multiple times within the Municipal Bureau.

And in the future, although Sun Haibo fell, Xiao Bo became Vice Mayor Jing's top subordinate.

The two had a close relationship; Xiao Bo had even boasted about it to him more than once.

In his past life, Jiang Tian lived a miserable life, constantly harassed by his wife.

Back then, Jiang Tian complained about how fate treated him, comparing himself to others who had achieved success in both career and love, with Mu Ya at home, and whose social circles and meals were frequented by people of the rank of Deputy Mayor Jing.

Once, Jiang Tian even shamelessly approached Xiao Bo, hoping that Xiao Bo would consider his past help and give him a promotion.

In return, Xiao Bo slammed his fist on the table and mocked him.

And, with an air of righteous indignation, stood on his moral high ground, pointing fingers at him. In his past life, Jiang Tian felt deeply ashamed.

"Oh no, after Dad fell, Deputy Mayor Jing was promoted within days and even led the investigation into Dad."

This wasn't because Jiang Tian had forgotten.

It's just that in this life, he thought he had nothing to do with the other party anymore.

But he never expected to run into him so soon.

Even so, Jiang Tian had no regrets, even if it happened all over again.

For no other reason than the fact that the other party showed contempt for the police, Jiang Tian couldn't help but react.

Putting everything else aside, Jiang Tian remembered that his father had died in despair because he had developed health problems during an investigation.

The cause of all this was this very same leader, Jing, who was about to be promoted in his previous life.

"Uncle Song, you're being a bit too cautious. You've forgotten who did all this. It was Chief Gao who arrested that woman. Go find Chief Gao if you dare," Jiang Tian chuckled, completely unconcerned.

Song Liguo sighed deeply upon hearing this, "You brat, I forgot to tell you, Chief Gao just had a meeting before we came. Because we captured Yunbao, there's going to be a major operation soon. Chief Gao and Director Xu are both returning to headquarters."

As he spoke, Song Liguo looked intently at Jiang Tian.

It was common knowledge that Chief Gao initially had the biggest problem with Jiang Tian, ​​but later, he became Jiang Tian's biggest backer.

But it was that Chief Gao.

Because of Chief Gao, Jiang Tian could charge into battle with increasing impunity.

Because Chief Gao had everything under his protection.

And now, Chief Gao is leaving?

“But don’t worry too much. With your current status and position, there won’t be any problems. It’s just that your father will probably have a hard time at the Standing Committee meeting and face some difficulties. Alright, I’m leaving now.” Song Liguo stood up to leave.

Jiang Tian stared at the ceiling, lost in thought.

“Forget it, why think so much? Things will work themselves out.” Jiang Tian shook his head and smiled.

“By the way, did you see your sister Wan’er? She’s leaving on a flight tomorrow night.” Song Liguo casually mentioned before leaving.

“So soon?”

Jiang Tian jumped up.



Chapter 209 Good, good, good, so he's suspended?


This Wan'er was absolutely a thorn in Jiang Tian's side.

He, Jiang Tian, ​​had wronged many people, and he had changed everything since his rebirth, but not Wan'er.

Jiang Tian's heart was filled with absolute guilt.

Because if it weren't for him, Wan'er wouldn't have gone abroad, and that accident wouldn't have happened in his previous life, ending her young life tragically.

In his previous life, he was blinded by greed, and only after learning of Wan'er's death abroad did Jiang Tian suddenly come to his senses. From childhood, Wan'er had always given him everything she had—good food, fun toys, everything.

She even saved many of the delicious foods for him, things she couldn't bear to eat herself.

Wan'er's allowance from childhood was all his.

But now, he had pushed away such a wonderful Wan'er.

"What should I do? Should I leave with Wan'er to protect her?"

"But Wan'er probably hates me to death."

Jiang Tian frowned deeply.

He couldn't understand it.

But no matter what, he was determined to save Wan'er.

To avoid worrying his family, Jiang Tian kept his injuries a complete secret and didn't tell them.

His superiors, considering Jiang Tian's special circumstances, selectively kept them confidential.

The injuries weren't serious for Jiang Tian, ​​but Lin Jinxuan insisted on staying with him, and Jiang Tian could only agree with a wry smile.

Meanwhile, while Jiang Tian was hospitalized,

a massive earthquake struck the entire Yunhai.

Due to the urgency, Chief Gao and Director Xu, after returning from the hospital, almost immediately transferred to a transport vehicle to return to headquarters.

And at the same time,

the arrest of Mayor Jing's wife that day ignited a storm in Yunhai's officialdom.

Because this was tantamount to blatantly slapping Leader Jing in the face.

That evening.

Jiang Tian's phone rang.

"The wontons I bought for you are here."

Lin Jinxuan held two servings of wontons, placing one on the table and carrying the other to the window, where she opened the box and rubbed her hands together.

"Thank you, Teacher Lin," Jiang Tian said, holding the phone.

Lin Jinxuan rolled her eyes. She was already used to this form of address, but she always felt it didn't carry a friendly connotation.

"Yangyang, what's up?" Jiang Tian asked, eating his wontons.

That's right.

The call was from Gao Yangyang.

However, Gao Yangyang sounded very angry on the other end, breathing heavily.

"What, someone bullied you?" Jiang Tian squinted.

Don't be fooled; the relationship between Jiang Tian and Gao Yangyang was complicated.

Sometimes he didn't even want to see her, but for Jiang Tian, ​​Gao Yangyang was the first woman he encountered after his rebirth.

In Jiang Tian's mind, this had become a sore spot.

"No one bullied me, but someone bullied you," Gao Yangyang said angrily.

"Tell me more," Jiang Tian said, intrigued.

Now Jiang Tian was truly fearless. He asked himself if he had a clear conscience in everything he did.

Whoever dared to mess with him, he would destroy.

"Mayor Yang just came and took away the woman you arrested this afternoon. He also gave Uncle Jiang a severe scolding and demanded that you be suspended from your duties immediately for psychological counseling and treatment before being reinstated. He said you have psychological problems. Isn't this targeting you?" Gao Yangyang said angrily.

After all, her grandfather had just passed away, and this had happened.

Thinking of this,

Gao Yangyang became even angrier.

Those people probably dared to do this because they saw her grandfather leave.

"Okay, I understand," Jiang Tian said calmly.

"Aren't you angry? Aren't you shocked?" Gao Yangyang asked in astonishment.

"Even if it's not today, there's always tomorrow. What's the big deal? Taking a break is actually more relaxing for me." Jiang Tian wasn't surprised.

In this system, you can be outstanding, but you can't be too monstrous.

If you're too high-profile, you'll always be targeted.

Since he dared to act this way, he naturally wasn't afraid of trouble. Before, with Gao Xiaolong keeping things under control, no one dared to jump out. Now that Gao Xiaolong had just left, someone was causing trouble—Jiang Tian understood perfectly well.

As for Mayor Yang, he was, of course, Jiang Tian's father's superior.

Gao Yangyang on the other end of the phone was instantly speechless.

And the next moment, Jiang Tian's words shocked her even more.

"And I even suspect that Madam Jing's presence here today is no coincidence."

Jiang Tian, ​​now possessing the title of "King of Criminal Investigation," mentally processed the information.

Various details surfaced, and Jiang Tian's eyes flashed with a sharp light.

Political struggles are often bloodless.

The moves are made silently, but by the time you notice, it's too late.

"Indeed, one cannot underestimate anyone."

Jiang Tian felt he needed to examine himself. His life after his rebirth, under Chief Gao's protection, had been too smooth, even causing him to lose some of his vigilance.

But those people...

Don't they know he's been nominated for a first-class hero?

That's right.

Before leaving, Gao Xiaolong

specifically whispered to Jiang Tian that he had been nominated for a first-class hero. Although the resistance was great, the chances were also great, because the merit this time was immense, far greater than Jiang Tian had imagined.

As for how the other party knew Gao Xiaolong's departure time, frankly, anyone with a bit of inside information should have known.

Gao Xiaolong was only here to supervise the anti-drug operation, not to hold a formal position, and should have left by now.

What Jiang Tian didn't expect was that the other party would actually go to such lengths.

It seemed they had underestimated the level of importance he was held in at headquarters.

Actually, Jiang Tian was quite curious to see what those people's faces would look like when the First-Class Hero Award was presented.

A twenty-one-year-old First-Class Hero.

A Special Merit Warrior.

An Anti-Drug Hero.

Thinking of this, Jiang Tian felt excited.

This was true glory for his ancestors!

But just then.

At the window, Lin Jinxuan, eating wontons, felt bored and turned on the TV. It was news time.

Most importantly, as soon as she turned it on, Jiang Tian saw a scene on the news that shocked him.

Recently, the nationwide anti-drug campaign has achieved a major breakthrough, especially in Yunhai. In recent anti-drug operations, Yunhai police have strictly adhered to organizational red lines, maintaining zero tolerance and zero leniency towards drugs. Thanks to the persistent efforts of Yunhai's anti-drug police, multiple drug trafficking groups have been dismantled, and more than ten major wanted criminals and 97 suspected drug traffickers have been arrested. These individuals trafficked enormous quantities of drugs, and their crimes were extremely heinous. They will all face the strictest legal punishment.

The news anchor spoke in a clear, high-spirited voice.

Praised?

Jiang Tian was surprised.

Has Yun Hai ever been featured on the news before?

Of course, but that was as a negative example, publicly named and shamed for inaction.

But being publicly praised now is a first.

Most importantly...

What does this praise represent?

The higher-ups are watching closely.

"You've been praised?" Lin Jinxuan hissed, drawing in a sharp breath.

What does this represent?

It represents approval from above!

It seems like recognition for Yun Hai, but who handled these cases and made the real contributions?

Does the higher-ups not know?

So, while it appears to be praising Yun Hai, in reality, it's no less than a direct naming and shaming of Jiang Tian.



Chapter 210 News Interview? Playing This Big?


It was all very sudden.

The praise came very suddenly.

Because Jiang Tian had never even been interviewed before.

This left Jiang Tian feeling somewhat regretful.

But just then.

Jiang Tian's phone rang.

He took it out and saw it was Zhao Wei.

"Holy crap, Captain, did you see the news? You're on the news!" Zhao Wei's voice was filled with excitement.

"Just saw it. Calm down. So what if I'm on the news?" Jiang Tian said calmly.

Having been reborn, Jiang Tian was much more relaxed about these things.

Besides, this news was just praising Yun Hai; it had nothing to do with him now.

Zhao Wei on the other end of the line fell silent instantly.

Okay, okay, so I was just too excited?

But...

"My sister just told me something." Zhao Wei's tone grew increasingly excited.

Jiang Tian had no idea what he was so excited about.

However, Zhao Qiuni's name instantly caught Jiang Tian's eye. It wasn't that Jiang Tian had any feelings for Zhao Qiuni; although she was indeed very beautiful, what made him suddenly think of her was that, if he remembered correctly, she had considerable connections in Beijing.

Right now, Jiang Tian was thinking about how to deal with Sister Wan'er. He had just called Yu Hui to investigate her.

However, the information he found wasn't particularly good for Jiang Tian.

The investigative authority of the anti-drug system was extremely high.

In some ways, even higher than that of criminal investigation.

But even so, the investigation only yielded superficial information, at most revealing Song Liguo; nothing deeper was found.

However, after Yu Hui sent him the information, Jiang Tian, ​​with his seasoned investigative mind, sensed something was amiss.

Jiang Tian's relationship with Sister Wan'er stemmed from his previous complete break with her while pursuing Wang Yifei.

At that time, Wan'er, in a fit of anger, went abroad to study, having only been there for a couple of days. It's impossible that she hadn't prepared beforehand.

Thinking back to the foreigner she encountered, Yu Hui investigated him as well, only to find he was the president of a foreign pharmaceutical group, and his passport was a tourist passport.

It seemed normal, but Jiang Tian keenly sensed something was amiss.

The narcotics squad's authority was too low to find detailed information about Wan'er, but what about Zhao Qiuni, with her extensive network of connections?

"I'm going to invite your sister to dinner tomorrow," Jiang Tian said, feeling he could use Zhao Qiuni to break things off.

"Captain Jiang, I was just about to talk to you about this."

Zhao Wei's breathing quickened: "You'd never guess, the media wants to do an exclusive interview with you, asking if you're free tomorrow?"

"An exclusive interview? Me?"

Jiang Tian was dumbfounded. Was this appropriate for someone in narcotics enforcement?

"I won't show my face," Zhao Wei said.

Jiang Tian's brow twitched. If it wasn't about not showing his face, Jiang Tian naturally wouldn't disagree. Besides, considering the recent situation, this might be the key to breaking the deadlock.

"Qiu Ni will bring reporters tomorrow. By the way, this interview will be broadcast on tonight's news. Captain Jiang, you're going to be awesome!" Zhao Wei said excitedly.

This interview...

is essentially a direct boasting of Jiang Tian's achievements.

And in terms of achievements, just look at those who've been interviewed—who can rival Jiang Tian in terms of accomplishments?

A 21-year-old anti-drug pioneer, a 21-year-old police inspector, a 21-year-old recipient of the Special Merit Citation, and multiple First-Class Merits.

Even a Heroic Model Award.

Wow!!!

Thinking about this, Zhao Wei couldn't even imagine the sensation and impact it would have if broadcast on national news.

"Okay, let's do it together tomorrow. Oh, and have your sister help me investigate someone," Jiang Tian said.

Then Jiang Tian told Zhao Wei about Sister Wan'er.

Zhao Wei patted his chest and said, "Captain Jiang, you're too kind. You could have just contacted me for this. Why bother with Qiu Ni? Just wait, I'll get back to you right away."

Huh?

Looking at the disconnected phone, Jiang Tian suddenly realized he'd almost forgotten that Zhao Wei was practically a playboy in Beijing.

Even if he'd been relegated, his old network of connections still existed.

"I almost forgot," Jiang Tian slapped his forehead.

Then, Jiang Tian picked up his phone and dialed Wan'er's number.

His gaze lingered on it for a moment, then he finally put the phone down.

He must have hurt Wan'er deeply that time.

Jiang Tian sighed.

And just as Jiang Tian put down his phone...

At the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

Zhao Wei hung up the phone with a grave expression, then found Jiang Tian's number and dialed it again. Jiang Tian answered immediately, showing his urgency.

"How did it go? Did you find anything?" Jiang Tian asked quickly.

"Captain Jiang, how did you get involved with that group?" Zhao Wei asked, finding a secluded spot.

"That group?"

Jiang Tian couldn't help but straighten up and ask back.

Zhao Wei explained, “I just called to investigate for you, Brother Jiang, and the result gave the person I contacted a real shock. The person you're investigating belongs to the national security protection department. These people mostly investigate and handle major cases. The person you asked me to investigate belongs to that department, and it almost scared the person I contacted to death.”

I understand.

Jiang Tian's eyes lit up; he finally understood.

Why did Sister Wan'er die unexpectedly abroad in his previous life, and why was the final conclusion that she was shot by her suitor?

But now, considering the foreigner he encountered last night,

it seems that their group was investigating this foreign individual or organization, and the unexpected happened because the other party had already detected it.

In other words, if Jiang Tian did nothing,

Sister Wan'er's trip abroad would have been a trap, and she would have been assassinated.

What to do?

Jiang Tian's gaze remained thoughtful.

"Teacher Lin, aren't you leaving yet?" Jiang Tian asked helplessly, looking at Teacher Lin who was making the bed beside him.

"You just had surgery, I have to stay here and watch over you. Or, why don't you find someone else to stay with you?" Lin Jinxuan rolled her eyes.

Fine, fine, Jiang Tian is lying in bed, finding someone else? What a joke! Is Jiang Tian's injury something to be proud of?

If his mother knew, she'd probably be worried sick.

Who knows what she'd think about future missions.

...

As the first rays of morning sunlight streamed into the hospital room, Jiang Tian's phone rang rapidly.

Jiang Tian took out his phone and, sure enough, it was Zhao Qiuni calling.

After answering, Zhao Qiuni eagerly asked, "Officer Jiang, are you at the hospital?"



Chapter 211 Determining Responsibility: Is This My Fault?


Jiang Tian glanced at Lin Jinxuan, still fast asleep on the cot next door. The first rays of morning sunlight shone down, partially obscuring her face with her long hair, creating a strikingly beautiful scene.

However, Jiang Tian dared not dwell on it. He was already deeply troubled by the relationships around him.

Especially for Jiang Tian, ​​how to handle his relationship with Gao Yangyang was now the most important thing.

As for Sister Wan'er, all Jiang Tian cared about was her happiness.

All he had done was try to save Sister Wan'er.

If it weren't for him, perhaps Sister Wan'er would never have joined that organization, much less chosen to go abroad. Ultimately, it was he who had hurt Sister Wan'er.

And then there was Mu Ya. Jiang Tian was even more unsure how to handle his relationship with Mu Ya.

As for Lin Jinxuan, he dared not think about it at all.

"Whoever marries her is truly blessed," Jiang Tian sighed inwardly. He had to admit, Sister Wan'er was indeed a gentle woman, extremely conservative and with her own set of principles.

Regardless of their values ​​or family background, if it weren't for those complicated relationships, Jiang Tian wouldn't mind pursuing Teacher Xiao Lin.

"Yes, I'm at the hospital," Jiang Tian said into the phone.

"Officer Jiang, the reporters from Beijing are here. I'm bringing them to you now. Do you have time?" Zhao Qiuni asked impatiently.

"Sure, come over now," Jiang Tian agreed.

Jiang Tian still had plenty of time. Last night, he already had a general idea of ​​what to do with Sister Wan'er, so he wasn't in a rush.

"Okay, Brother Jiang, we'll be there now," Zhao Qiuni hung up the phone.

A smile unconsciously crept onto Jiang Tian's lips.

Compared to his past life, he seemed to have changed so much without even realizing it.

It could be said that hearing Zhao Qiuni's voice, and recalling her, was Jiang Tian's most profound impression, or rather, his first impression of her, always of his past life.

Jiang Tian opened his eyes, still groggy from a hangover.

The hotel room door opened.

Zhao Qiuni stood in the doorway, facing him with an expression of utter disappointment.

It was an expression of extreme disappointment, accompanied by a sigh of utter frustration.

But now?

Zhao Qiuni was speaking to him, even addressing him as "Brother Jiang."

This transformation was drastic. Of course, the most important factor was that Jiang Tian had saved Zhao Wei and Zhao Qiuni at the hotel, instantly turning Zhao Qiuni from his hater into his devoted fan.

Just after Jiang Tian hung up the phone, Zhao Qiuni on the other end looked excitedly at the woman beside her.

The woman was probably in her thirties, with a mature and sophisticated air about her.

She was dressed in a suit jacket and sat in the passenger seat, with two subordinates carrying large black bags in the back.

"Auntie, yesterday Officer Jiang saved over a hundred trainee police officers. In extremely dangerous circumstances, he wiped out over thirty elite armed drug traffickers and captured a major foreign drug lord alive. He single-handedly turned the tide of the war!" Zhao Qiuni praised him excessively.

She had completely become a devoted fan.

The mature woman in the passenger seat froze for a moment, then hurriedly pulled out her files, only to find nothing recorded: "The files were just done yesterday, and they've been updated today?"

"Tsk tsk, what can you expect from Officer Jiang, who's so awesome?" Zhao Qiuni puffed out her chest and drove with an air of arrogance.

The mature woman narrowed her eyes dangerously and said, "Qiuni, the anti-drug team's missions are all classified. How did you know this? Even when I was on an interview assignment yesterday, I didn't know anything about what happened. How did you find out faster than me?"
