Anti-drug missions aren't exactly top-secret, but they certainly can't be publicized, especially ongoing missions, which have extremely high levels of secrecy.

Otherwise, the drug dealers would escape before you even acted.

Furthermore, the identities of anti-drug officers must be kept secret.

Zhao Qiuni actually knew about the anti-drug situation; this was a violation of regulations.

"It must have been through your brother, right? You're just taking advantage of your brother's honesty." The mature woman's face showed some pity when she spoke of Zhao Wei.

Zhao Qiuni stuck out her tongue.

The mature woman remained silent. They had come from Beijing, and she herself was a very famous television host.

In her hand was an interview request from her superior.

It contained a partial resume of Jiang Tian's achievements.

Just looking at it was enough to make the mature woman's heart skip a beat.

Especially noteworthy was the clear statement that since joining the police force, he had eliminated hundreds of drug dealers and dismantled multiple drug trafficking dens.

He had received numerous first-class merit awards, even special-class merit awards, and had been selected as a model worker and awarded honorary titles, among other things.

Any of these achievements would be widely publicized by an ordinary person.

In fact, in any other job, such a resume would be touted as a national sensation.

But within the anti-drug team, he was like a hero without a spotlight; so many merits, yet not a single word of them had leaked out.

Even the mature woman had never heard of these things before. If her superior hadn't specifically sought her out, she wouldn't have known that in this peaceful corner of the world, beneath the surface of peace, there was a group of great people silently making sacrifices.

These achievements seem glorious.

But the mature woman could already imagine the mountains of corpses and seas of blood they had endured, the countless near-death experiences, and the hail of bullets they had faced.

"You just said he's in the hospital?" the mature woman suddenly realized and asked in surprise.

"Yes, he was shot during yesterday's operation." Zhao Qiuni was actually very worried.

She had originally intended to go to the hospital, but Zhao Wei had returned too late yesterday, and Zhao Qiuni had to use some questioning and interrogation to get the information out of him.

Because she hadn't had a chance to visit him, Zhao Qiuni had barely slept a wink all night, and now she had dark circles under her eyes.

Soon.

Zhao Qiuni drove almost entirely on the highway.

By the time she arrived at the hospital, it was already past eight o'clock.

Actually, the mature woman had arrived here from Kyoto last night.

She had originally planned to go to the Municipal Public Security Bureau first to conduct some interviews, but Zhao Qiuni dragged her here. Of course, for the woman, it didn't matter who she interviewed first, the Municipal Public Security Bureau or this heroic police officer.

"Here."

Zhao Wei was already waiting at the entrance to greet her. Seeing the mature woman, he quickly walked up and called out happily, "Auntie!"

"Xiao Wei, you've really made something of yourself here." The mature woman looked at the police rank insignia on Zhao Wei's shoulder, surprised to find he'd been promoted to a full officer.

"Heh, it's all thanks to Officer Jiang. With his useless abilities, without Officer Jiang, it's questionable whether he'd even be alive now," Zhao Qiuni said irritably.

"You work under Officer Jiang?" The mature woman asked, her face full of astonishment.

The legendary figure she had just wanted to interview was her nephew's superior?

"Haha, that's right, my captain." Zhao Wei grinned and led the way.

"I'm getting more and more curious about that Officer Jiang." The mature woman's interest grew stronger.

Soon.

They approached Jiang Tian's high-rise ward.

"You've really taken good care of this officer; there are even two officers standing guard at the door."

On the stairs, the mature woman looked surprised and somewhat impressed, inwardly admiring the unity of the Yunhai police.

Thinking to himself, "No wonder such a hero appeared."

Given this united atmosphere, it wasn't surprising at all.

"Protect?" Zhao Wei was completely bewildered.

When they arrived at the ward door,

they found several people standing inside.

Especially as they approached the door, they heard a voice.

"Comrade Xiao Jiang, after careful consideration by the organization, it has been determined that your current mental state is seriously problematic. It has been decided that you will be formally suspended from duty and undergo mental rehabilitation treatment."

"You should now thank Ms. Sun, whom you hurt yesterday. Leader Jing, considering your past contributions, has not pursued further action against you, but you need to apologize to Ms. Sun."

"Finally, after the organization's observation and determination that you are mentally sound, you will be allowed to return to the team. This is your suspension notice."



Chapter 212 Furious, Is this how you treat a hero?


Before even entering the room, merely standing at the doorway,

everyone heard this one sentence.

And...

it was Jiang Tian's suspension from duty?

The people at the door were all bewildered.

They didn't even understand what was happening.

The next moment...

a calm yet confused voice rang out: "Who are you?"

To be honest,

Jiang Tian really didn't know who this guy was.

He just walked in and issued him some kind of suspension notice.

If he were just told, he would know; was it really necessary to come all this way?

And...

an apology to that crazy woman from yesterday?

Are you sure you're not mistaken?

What do you take him for, Jiang Tian?

"Let me introduce myself. My name is Li Qishan, former director of the Xicheng branch, currently serving as the deputy director of the Yunhai City Bureau, just recently appointed," Li Qishan added at the end.

Jiang Tian narrowed his eyes.

He hadn't expected the deputy director to act so quickly and so suddenly.

Oh no.

Perhaps it had been premeditated all along.

He had always heard Jiang Peng say that the war within the system was bloodless, a lesson Jiang Tian had learned in his past life—one wrong step could lead to an endless abyss.

And now, Jiang Tian felt this even more acutely.

Some people acted too quickly, too swiftly.

But, wasn't this underestimating him, Jiang Tian?

Asking him to apologize to that Ms. Sun—no, it should be said, Mrs. Jing—wasn't that a mistake?

"Moreover, this isn't a discussion, it's a notification. Although I've only recently taken office, my decision regarding your case is basically finalized," Li Qishan continued.

Jiang Tian calmly replied with an "Oh," and said, "I understand. You can leave."

Hmm?

Li Qishan frowned. He had considered that Jiang Tian might be angry or give in before he arrived, but he had anticipated both. The only thing he hadn't expected was Jiang Tian's calm response.

"It's best if you accept it," Li Qishan nodded in satisfaction, thinking that the other party had probably resigned himself to his fate.

However...

Li Qishan couldn't help but click his tongue in disbelief.

To be honest, Jiang Tian's achievements were impressive, very impressive.

But what good were all those achievements?

Didn't he know that the struggles within the system were far more complex and terrifying than imagined?

On the battlefield, Jiang Tian might indeed be unmatched.

However, within the system, it requires strategy; a person needs to be a talent.

Even Li Qishan couldn't help but admire him.

It's just a pity; perhaps it's because they weren't on the same path.

"I've received the notification letter, Teacher Lin, see him out." Jiang Tian closed his eyes to rest.

Lin Jinxuan stood up from the stool in the corner, smiled broadly, and walked to Li Qishan, gesturing for him to rest: "Director Li, Officer Jiang is tired and needs to rest."

"Comrade Jiang, please rest well. It wasn't a severe punishment. Take good care of your mental health. The Municipal Bureau and I both welcome you very much. Well, I need to go to the Municipal Bureau for my report as soon as possible, so I'll be leaving now."

After saying this, Li Qishan noticed that Jiang Tian was still resting with his eyes closed, seemingly too lazy to even open them.

A slight displeasure rose in his heart, and he couldn't help but frown, saying, "Comrade Jiang, is this how you treat your superiors?"

"Superior?"

Jiang Tian finally opened his eyes, and the next moment, he sat up abruptly, pointing to the door: "Get out."

For Jiang Tian, ​​if he couldn't make it here, he could just ask Boss Gao to transfer him to Beijing. With his abilities, he could still thrive.

Where wouldn't everyone be eager to claim his credit?

And then there was his father. If things really didn't work out, he could retire early and manage the assets rewarded by the system. Jiang Tian's accumulated assets were already terrifying.

Li Qishan, his face ashen, waved his hand dismissively, saying, "Fine, fine, you'll regret this."

He reached the door and saw several people standing there.

Because they weren't familiar with him, and it was obvious they were there to visit Jiang Tian,

Li Qishan said nothing and left with his men.

Lying in bed, Jiang Tian was quite satisfied with Teacher Lin. To be honest, a woman like her—a wealthy lady in public, and a woman who could bring prosperity to the family at home—was truly blessed.

A traditional woman with exceptionally refined manners.

Whoever marries her is incredibly lucky. If she were to be his nanny, she would be a perfect fit. Jiang Tian even dared to consider this possibility.

Meanwhile, at this moment,

Zhao Wei, standing at the door, was utterly astonished.

Gao Yangyang had learned of the news yesterday, but Zhao Wei genuinely didn't know the specifics.

So he was completely bewildered.

The mature woman, meanwhile, frowned deeply.

If she hadn't misheard, had Jiang Tian, ​​in a fit of stress, harmed an innocent woman?

If that were the case,

she even had to consider whether to broadcast the interview and news report, and even suggest it to her superiors.

Such an unstable person was simply not suitable for publicizing.

After all, the common people were innocent.

The mature woman scrutinized Jiang Tian lying on the hospital bed, then followed Zhao Wei into the VIP ward.

"Having made so many contributions, coupled with the intense battlefield experience, psychological trauma is inevitable. But harming ordinary people without any reason is a serious problem," the mature woman thought to herself.

"Brother Jiang, you've been suspended?"

At this moment,

Zhao Wei was the most shocked.

Good heavens.

Jiang Tian suspended?

Was he sure he hadn't misheard?

Zhao Wei was utterly incredulous.

And what about that so-called newly appointed Li Qishan? He wanted Brother Jiang to apologize to Ms. Sun?

Who is this Ms. Sun?

Zhao Wei was completely bewildered.

"Brother Jiang, who is this Ms. Sun? Apologize for what? And why were you suspended?"

Zhao Wei was completely confused; his mind was a complete mess.

"Wasn't he supposed to have performed a great service? Wasn't he supposed to have performed a great service? How come he's suddenly suspended and on medical leave?"

"What mistake did he make?"

Zhao Wei's words only made things worse.

Speaking of which...

Even the usually mild-mannered Ms. Lin explained angrily, "This Ms. Sun is the woman who was swearing yesterday."

"Swearing?"

The mature woman standing nearby twitched her ears slightly and immediately asked in surprise.

"Swearing?

Neither Zhao Qiuni nor she knew."

But Zhao Wei was there yesterday and knew the whole story.

“Auntie, yesterday a woman tried to kick Captain Jiang out of this ward, threatening that if she hit him, she'd only have to pay him some money, but if he hit her, his parents would have to kneel down and apologize. Captain Jiang said one thing, and then the woman started flailing her arms, trying to attack Brother Jiang who had just had surgery. Chief Gao immediately took her to the police station,” Zhao Wei explained.

Boom!!!

The mature woman's face turned ashen, her chest heaving with anger.

“And that person just now, because of this, is going to suspend Officer Jiang?” The mature woman's face was filled with rage, her gaze fixed on Jiang Tian's bandaged arm, her blood boiling.

Is this how they treat a hero who silently sacrifices and carries a heavy burden?



Chapter 213 is perfectly executed, causing a sensation throughout the audience; young man, you're going to reach for the heavens!


"What exactly happened, Captain Jiang?"

Zhao Wei saluted Jiang Tian, ​​his face also filled with anger.

He knew nothing about this matter. He had been working overtime yesterday interrogating drug dealers; at his rank, he wasn't even qualified to attend the meeting.

"Officer Jiang, are they going to suspend you? I'm going to the Municipal Bureau, to find the leaders! Chief Gao just left, and they dare to attack you?" Li Zijin was practically bursting with rage; her head was spinning.

Captain Jiang had just performed a great service yesterday, and he was still injured.

The mature woman took deep breaths, her voice filled with anger: "Officer Jiang, if there's a problem, just say it. Although I'm a TV host, I have some influence in Beijing. I don't believe some bureaucrats can act with impunity."

Just hearing what she heard was enough to make the mature woman furious.

She couldn't even imagine what it would be like if she were in their shoes.

"Does that woman have connections? What kind of connections? Officer Jiang, you can just say it directly. I want to see if there's any place in Yunhai where reason prevails. If not, don't worry, I can find someone in Kyoto," the mature woman continued.

"Sit."

Under the mature woman's gaze, Jiang Tian, ​​lying on the hospital bed, smiled slightly and gestured for her to sit.

This mentality, this psychological endurance, left the mature woman almost dumbfounded. No, I'm about to explode with anger! What's wrong with you?

You know, Zhao Qiuni had already told her about Jiang Tian's experiences in the files and on the way, and she was also aware of Jiang Tian's situation.

After making such a great contribution, and being treated like this, you're not even angry?

So, all this time, I was angry for nothing?

"Officer Jiang, hello, my name is Zhao Cainan, a TV host, in charge of this interview, and also the aunt of Zhao Wei and Qiuni." Zhao Cainan's hands were trembling with anger.

Jiang Tian raised his hand and lightly shook hers without much contact.

Jiang Tian's observation skills were keen. The woman was elegant and refined, but also quite old, and her demeanor suggested she had been married.

Jiang Tian stood up from the hospital bed.

"Officer Jiang, aren't you angry?" Zhao Cainan asked, her face full of resentment.

"Why should I be angry?" Jiang Tian countered.

Buzz!!!

Zhao Cainan looked bewildered. "That kind of woman did that to a drug enforcement hero like you, and you were merely acting in self-defense, yet your superiors treated you like this. Aren't you angry?"

Yes.

Zhao Cainan felt that if it were her, she would absolutely be furious.

How could Jiang Tian do this?

"What the superiors do is their business, while drug enforcement is my job. The two are unrelated," Jiang Tian explained slightly.

Buzz!!!

Zhao Cainan's mind went blank.

This is...

What a profound thought!

So young.

But it was so noble.

At that moment, a powerful anger rose within Zhao Cainan, anger at this great anti-drug police officer.

She even felt that if she didn't do something, how could she be worthy of such a great hero?

Thinking this, Zhao Cainan clenched her fists.

How could she possibly stand by and watch a great hero bleed, and then shed tears?

Zhao Cainan was almost moved to tears.

“You’re here for an interview, right? Let’s begin,” Jiang Tian said.

Worried? What’s there to worry about? What’s there to be angry about? If he couldn’t make it here, he’d just go to Kyoto.

He really wasn’t afraid of anything.

“Is it convenient to conduct the interview here?” Zhao Cainan tried to calm her trembling heart.

Jiang Tian looked around. The environment was quite nice, and the space was spacious, so he nodded slightly. Actually, the most important thing was that Jiang Tian was in a hurry.

He needed to go see Sister Wan’er that afternoon.

“Don’t worry, we’ll blur your face. If there’s anything you’d rather not answer, you don’t have to.” Zhao Cainan assumed Jiang Tian had some concerns.

“It’s fine, let’s do it here.”

“Could you show us your hand injury?” Zhao Cainan, true to her professional nature, immediately grasped the key point.

Jiang Tian clicked his tongue inwardly. Luckily, he hadn't over-aggregated the rebirth attribute beforehand; otherwise, recovering right after surgery would have caused serious problems.

Then, Jiang Tian slowly unwrapped his bandages.

Zhao Cainan quickly had the people behind her come forward and give Jiang Tian's arm wound a close-up shot.

As far as the eye could see, there was still a lot of blood around the wound.

The flesh was raw and torn, but it was stitched together; the gunshot wound was still extremely gruesome.

Feeling uncomfortable, Zhao Cainan looked up and looked away, meeting Jiang Tian's eyes.

It has to be said that today's experience had been incredibly shocking for Zhao Cainan.

She couldn't even imagine what this young man, this young man who was the same age as her nephew Zhao Wei, was doing.

What were other young people in their twenties doing?

Still in school, or just drifting around aimlessly in society?

But this young man before her already possessed so many merits, having become a third-level police inspector, a recipient of the anti-drug honorary title, and a candidate for model worker.

Eyes met, and Zhao Cainan, looking into his clear gaze, felt a surge of admiration. She knew that beneath Jiang Tian's seemingly glorious achievements lay countless traumas; otherwise, how could he remain so calm even after being targeted like this, having just risked his life to achieve such a great feat?

Thinking of this, Zhao Cainan sat down by the hospital bed, her expression becoming even gentler. "Have your family worried about your profession, or even known about it?"

Jiang Tian thought of his mother and sister, a slight smile playing on his lips. "Of course they know. However, they are all proud of me. There is some worry, but more than anything, they are happy that I can contribute to the construction of our country and add to its glory. I myself am also honored."

Good, good, good.

Going straight for the kill, huh?

Jiang Tian's voice was infectious.

Zhao Cainan clenched her fist tightly.

Such a hero, having already shed blood, is now expected to shed tears as well?

Thinking about it, Zhao Cainan's mind was spinning.

But being a professional, she wouldn't let her near-explosive emotions affect her professional interview. Composing herself, she continued, "In such a short time, you've cracked multiple major drug trafficking cases, arrested numerous drug lords in their hideouts, and even infiltrated their strongholds, facing countless pursuit and interception. You must have encountered many desperate situations. When faced with such dire circumstances, what things do you think about to alleviate your distress and help you survive?"

Knowing the question was complex, Zhao Cainan gently reminded him, "Of course, if it's difficult, you can skip to the next question."

Jiang Tian's gaze gradually turned melancholic. Finally, under Zhao Cainan's shocked gaze, he smiled slightly and slowly said:



Chapter 214 An earth-shattering belief, how could he be so perfect?


"I've faced dire straits. Back then, I thought, if I died there, the criminals who hate me would probably celebrate with glee, my family would be devastated, and given the extent of their hatred, my body would be subjected to their vengeful rampage after my death. My corpse would be chopped into pieces and eaten by wild dogs. The only trace of me left in the world would likely be the longing of my family and the criminals' revelry. Perhaps, preventing the criminals from celebrating was the belief that kept me going."

Boom!!!

These words were spoken.

Everyone was stunned.

Staring blankly at Jiang Tian.

Especially Zhao Cainan, trembling as she looked at Jiang Tian by the bedside.

What a noble thought, what a tragic consequence.

Yes!

He looked at the blood-stained record book, every single achievement earned by this young man at the cost of his life!

And such a hero...

ultimately suffered such injustice?

In a daze.

When Zhao Cainan came to her senses, she wiped her face and realized that she had been crying uncontrollably.

Mainly because Jiang Tian's answer was too shocking.

"I'm sorry, I lost my composure," Zhao Cainan quickly apologized, wiping her face.

"It's alright," Jiang Tian said, his heart filled with emotion.

This wasn't a lie.

Because if he died, the events of his previous life would continue, all the regrets would come true.

Yunhai would still be a paradise for those drug lords and criminal groups.

Reborn, Jiang Tian felt that besides changing himself, he also needed to do something for people.

"One last question, after facing so many bloody battles and desperate situations, annihilating so many enemies, do you think your mental state is still healthy?" Zhao Cainan asked softly, seemingly afraid of stirring up painful memories for Jiang Tian.

"My mental state is healthier than ever before," Jiang Tian said easily.

“Actually, I think so too,” Zhao Cainan said earnestly after staring intently into Jiang Tian’s eyes for a moment.

This startled Jiang Tian slightly.

This question, the last one, was quite unexpected for Jiang Tian; it seemed as if the host had specifically asked it for him.

What was the purpose?

Jiang Tian pondered.

“Thank you for today’s interview. This is my contact information. Please rest assured, I believe that the last injustice in this world should befall heroes.” Zhao Cainan gripped Jiang Tian’s arm tightly.

Her eyes, at this moment, were more resolute than ever before.

The anger in her heart was also boiling like never before.

She vowed that she would never let heroes bleed and weep again.

“Brother Jiang, I’m going to find the city bureau leaders. I’m going to find Uncle Jiang to ask what happened,” Zhao Wei said angrily.

At the door, he didn’t even know what had happened.

He was initially stunned and didn’t even know who the leader was or what he did.

"Zhao Wei, calm down," Jiang Tian said irritably.

Zhao Wei suddenly reacted and angrily retorted, "Captain Jiang, isn't it rumored that Uncle Jiang is going to be the top law enforcement official in Yunhai? Is someone using you as a scapegoat?"

“Then I’ll have to break their teeth.” Jiang Tian could only say that if he were to take action against him, those people would probably be out of their minds.

Oh no.

After all, the desire for power is immense.

Sometimes it’s normal to do things without thinking.

“Brother Jiang, what exactly is that woman’s identity?” Zhao Wei couldn’t help but ask.

“That woman is the wife of the leader in charge of sanitation,” Lin Jinxuan blurted out.

“The wife of the leader in charge of sanitation can bully people like this?” Zhao Wei’s face was full of anger.

“Calm down, Zhao Wei. How many times have I told you? If you want to have a long-term career in our anti-drug team, you must learn to stay calm, understand?” Jiang Tian frowned.

“Wait…”

Zhao Wei’s expression suddenly became suspicious, because based on his understanding of Captain Jiang, he didn’t seem like someone who would suffer in silence.

Could it be...?

Sure enough, the next moment, Jiang Tian smiled and handed Zhao Cainan a memory card: "I have a habit of always having a backup plan. There's video here of that woman insulting the police and saying those things to me."

Is Jiang Tian the type to take a beating without fighting back?

Isn't this ridiculous?

Doesn't he know that one can't act impulsively within the system? Since that leader Jing, who's competing with Jiang Peng, can use his wife to influence the direction and agenda of meetings,

if things are now unfavorable for Jiang Peng, then Jiang Tian wouldn't mind doing the same.

And don't underestimate the destructive power of this move.

If news of Jiang Tian assaulting Mrs. Jing gets out, it will leave a subjective impression on all the leaders that he's relying on his past achievements to disregard their authority with impunity.

Luckily, Jiang Tian has the video to break this deadlock.

Perfect timing, Zhao Cainan is here, saving Jiang Tian the trouble of finding reporters.

And this is going to be broadcast on the news!

National news.

Jiang Tian didn't know if the video could be uploaded, but even if it were just broadcast in a small news segment, he was certain it would cause a massive uproar.

Zhao Cainan took the memory card, inserted it into the device, and glanced at it. Instantly, she felt a surge of anger.

Let alone Jiang Tian, ​​the person directly involved, just one glance and one sentence from him made her head spin.

"I need to go back to Kyoto immediately. Don't worry, if I can't give you justice, I'll lose all face." Zhao Cainan was trembling with rage.

How could someone be so arrogant?

In a small place like Yunhai, even a leader's wife could be so arrogant and domineering. If it were an ordinary person, they would be devoured!

Zhao Cainan couldn't stay a moment longer.

"Auntie, I'll take you home," Zhao Qiuni followed behind.

After leaving the ward and getting back into the car, Zhao Cainan was still increasingly angry.

"How could they do this? How could they be so heartless?"

Zhao Cainan was filled with rage.

If it were paid leave due to injury, that would be understandable.

But suspension for a mistake, suspension of a hero who had just bled and sacrificed his life?

Zhao Cainan had never heard of such a thing anywhere.

"Auntie, expose it! This has to be exposed!" Zhao Qiuni nodded frantically.

“The exposure is necessary, but we must also consider the impact on Officer Jiang. We can't let him suffer a second time.”

A young man in his early twenties, almost a teenager to Zhao Cainan.

No matter how strong one's heart is, facing this situation would cause a huge sense of loss.

Before boarding the plane, Zhao Cainan didn't forget to remind him, “After I leave, it'll probably be on the news in the next couple of days. Keep an eye out.”

“Thank you, Auntie,” Zhao Qiuni thanked her profusely.

Zhao Cainan shook her head and chuckled, “Why thank me? Tell Zhao Wei to work hard with Officer Jiang. I didn't expect that leaving Kyoto would be so liberating for Xiao Wei. Even for Xiao Wei's sake, I won't turn a blind eye.”

Just as Zhao Cainan was leaving…

Inside the city government building.

Li Qishan, who had just left the hospital, was giving a report in an office.

Sitting opposite him was a composed middle-aged man wearing glasses.



Chapter 215 Have you ever heard of the Six Merits King?


After hearing Li Qishan's report, the man smiled slightly and said, "It seems Comrade Jiang's psychological trauma is indeed quite severe. This kind of irritability aftereffect is very dangerous. We must ensure he's fully recovered before letting him return to his post."

"But, he's made so many contributions, won't the higher-ups..." Li Qishan worriedly asked.

Jing Feifan then said with a serious expression, "Headquarters has a major operation planned for the next two days. Gao Xiaolong is just a high-ranking leader at headquarters; he doesn't have jurisdiction over the local areas. Don't worry, they won't have time for that. By the time they remember, I'll already be in charge of political and legal work."

"Vice Mayor Jing, does that mean I'll soon be calling you Secretary?" Li Qishan's eyes lit up.

Jing Feifan shook his head and said, "There are always variables until it's finalized. It always requires maneuvering. I just didn't expect things to go so smoothly. Sometimes, within the system, what's needed is prestige, face."

Finally, Jing Feifan stood with his hands behind his back at the window, looking at the scenery outside with a slight smile.

This step.

How will Jiang Peng break the deadlock?

As for Jiang Tian,

Although he was talented,

in his eyes, he was nothing more than a brave but reckless soldier, destined to be nothing more than a frontline soldier, posing no threat whatsoever.

Of course, Jiang Tian was unaware of what was happening at the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

Because Jiang Tian had already left the hospital.

He sent Zhao Wei back to the Municipal Public Security Bureau to assess the situation, and then told Lin Jinxuan to go back first, or wait at the hospital.

This hospitalization would last at least ten days to two weeks.

Having been shot, being discharged in a few days was definitely out of the question; Jiang Tian didn't want to be dissected.

This time, Jiang Tian had slipped out of the hospital.

Lin Jinxuan was left bewildered in the empty hospital room.

She hailed a taxi.

Jiang Tian arrived at the apartment building.

He reached the building's apartment unit.

Jiang Tian thought about Wan'er all night but couldn't come up with a solution, so he finally decided to get straight to the point.

If Wan'er really was a member of that organization, then Jiang Tian wouldn't mind helping her complete the mission.

As fate would have it, Jiang Tian stood downstairs at the apartment building.

I happened to run into Sister Wan'er, who was coming downstairs with a trash bag.

With her long hair flowing down her back, Jiang Tian vividly remembered asking Wan'er why she never cut her hair when they were children. Wan'er had jokingly said that once her hair was cut, their relationship would be completely severed.

Whether she was joking or not, Jiang Tian felt a sense of relief seeing her hair almost reaching her hips.

Song Qiuwan glanced at Jiang Tian, ​​her expression complex, then looked away, turning away without a word to leave.

"Wait," Jiang Tian suddenly said.

Song Qiuwan stopped, her body heaving slightly as if she were taking deep breaths to calm herself. After a moment, she turned around, her face expressionless, and asked, "Is something wrong?"

"No, I'm leaving now." Wan'er, her back to Jiang Tian, ​​seemed to be trying to suppress the trembling in her shoulders.

But just as she was about to leave, Jiang Tian grabbed her wrist.

"I'm sorry, Wan'er, please forgive me." The moment Jiang Tian uttered those words,

he felt a sense of relief wash over him, both inside and out.

In both his past and present lives, across two lifetimes, Jiang Tian had dreamt of these words countless times.

For over a decade, Jiang Tian still frequently dreamt of Sister Wan'er.

The woman who had cared for him since childhood.

And when Jiang Tian uttered those words...

Song Qiuwan trembled violently, tears streaming down her face uncontrollably.

"Let me go! Let me go! I hate you! I'll never forgive you!" Song Qiuwan pounded on Jiang Tian, ​​trying to break free.

But Jiang Tian's hands were like vises.

After a while...

Drip, drip!

The sound of water flowing on the ground rang out.

At first, Song Qiuwan thought it was her own tears.

However, when she vaguely saw the crimson color on the ground, her face turned deathly pale.

Then she looked at Jiang Tian's arm.

"Sigh, that's why Jiang Tian is so lucky he had the rebirth skill; otherwise, he feels his hand injury would never heal."

Song Qiuwan's frantic pounding had reopened his recently stitched surgical wound.

Blood soaked through the bandages and his clothes.

Song Qiuwan's eyes widened; at that moment, she felt like she couldn't breathe.

"You're injured?" Sister Wan'er cradled Jiang Tian's injured arm in her hands.

This confirmed Jiang Tian's suspicions.

Sister Wan'er still cared for him.

A smile appeared on Jiang Tian's face.

"Smiling, you're still smiling! You're injured, do you even know that? How can you still smile!" Sister Wan'er frantically covered the wound, tears streaming down her face uncontrollably.

She was still as prone to crying as ever.

"Seeing you makes me happy, Sister Wan'er, please forgive me," Jiang Tian whispered.

And those words...

This caused Wan'er's worried face to fall silent.

"Wan'er, you still won't forgive me?" Jiang Tian asked.

"I..." Wan'er's lips were dry.

Even if she forgave me, what difference would it make? It was too late now.

If she died outside, it would only cause Xiao Jiang to suffer more.

"It's too late, it's all too late, but I can't tell you." Wan'er shook her head.

The next moment, Jiang Tian said directly, "Wan'er, you've just entered a special national security department, what can't you say?"

Song Qiuwan's mind went blank, staring at Jiang Tian in disbelief, her eyes wide.

She seemed unable to believe how Jiang Tian knew.

The level of secrecy surrounding this identity was terrifying.

"If it's because of that, there's absolutely no need." Jiang Tian had assumed Wan'er was worried about that.

“It’s not because of that. Since you know I’m in this department, you should know I’m on a mission—a very dangerous, terrifying mission, almost S-level, with a high chance of death,” Song Qiuwan said.

“S-level? How dangerous? If you can’t handle it, I happen to have some free time and can do this mission for you,” Jiang Tian smiled slightly.

No matter how dangerous, he didn’t believe it could be as dangerous as being surrounded by hundreds of people overseas.

Just as Jiang Tian finished speaking,

a sneer suddenly rang out: “Kid, you’re so young but have such a big mouth. It seems you know nothing about S-level missions in the national security department.”

Jiang Tian turned his head to look.

A man and a woman were seen in the stairwell, looking as if they had just heard a huge joke.

Their faces were filled with sneer.

Especially the speaker, a square-faced man in his forties with terrifying muscles, exuding a fierce aura, his eyes filled with contempt. He glanced at Jiang Tian, ​​then looked at Song Qiuwan and said, "This is that good-for-nothing friend you've been thinking about. He really is a coward. Besides his mouth, he can't be tough anywhere else. Does someone like that deserve your continued attention?"

"Xiao Wan, this time you've violated organizational discipline, actually revealing your identity and mission to such a boastful person." Standing next to the man was a petite woman, leaning against the wall, playing with a dagger, her expression full of amusement and danger.

"I, I didn't..." Qiuwan frantically pulled at Jiang Tian.

"Still saying you didn't? Look at this piece of trash. If you hadn't told him, how would he know?" The middle-aged man stepped forward, pressing him.

Jiang Tian gently pulled Wan'er behind him, his expression deepening with anger.

He had initially thought a direct confrontation would allow him to rescue Wan'er, but he hadn't expected everyone here to be so arrogant.

Their noses practically flew to the sky.

"Don't go over there, he's a trained fighter, you're no match for him," Wan'er said, pulling Jiang Tian back.

"Hahaha, you think you can hit me?" The man clutched his stomach, laughing maniacally.

"Do you know what I hate most?" Jiang Tian's gaze gradually turned cold.

"Come on, hahaha, I'm listening. What do you hate most? You're a bragging cripple, can you even lift your hand? Come on, let me hit you, can you even manage it?" The man laughed maniacally.

He walked up to Jiang Tian, ​​even pointing at himself and mocking him.

But the next moment...

Jiang Tian grabbed the man's hair, his knee slamming into the man's face. It was a slight movement, yet it was already the pinnacle of their power, impossible to dodge.

The man felt a sharp pain in his scalp, as if it had exploded.

Then…

*Thud!*

The man: "My face?"

For a moment, he felt as if he had lost all sensation, the illusion that his face was gone.

But was it an illusion?

The man was dumbfounded.

At the same time, Jiang Tian's calm voice rang in his ears: "To be honest, I've never heard of such a request in my life."

And, have you ever heard of the Six-Fold King?



Chapter 216 Taking Wan'er away, I'm not doing this mission anymore.


What is the title "Six Merits King"?

It was Zhao Cainan, that mature woman, who asked Jiang Tian how to address him in the interview and subsequent news broadcasts.

Of course, he couldn't use his real name; he had to use a code name or a pseudonym.

After seeing Jiang Tian's honors and considering his contributions, Zhao Cainan ultimately decided on the code name: "Six Merits King."

A recipient of one Special Merit and five First-Class Merits—how many people throughout history have achieved such a great feat?

Using "Six Merits King" as a direct and straightforward designation was the best approach.

Many people might not know the concept of "Six Merits King" now, but after the news broadcast, Jiang Tian felt that the name would become a national sensation.

Jiang Tian's gaze was indifferent. After a moment's thought, he lowered his head slightly, looking at the man crouching on the ground, trembling all over.

The Ministry of State Security?

Jiang Tian shook his head.

The name sounds impressive, but if it's truly that powerful, why was Wan'er brutally murdered and sacrificed in his past life?

And why?

Jiang Tian's gaze gradually turned cold. He recalled the words the foreign man had spoken before leaving yesterday. The other party had already completely discovered it.

And these people hadn't noticed at all.

Do they even deserve to be in this kind of department?

Who planned this mission?

And who carried it out?

The scene was now filled with silence.

Not a sound.

Because both Wan'er and the young woman with the girlish face who had just emerged from the stairwell were staring at Jiang Tian in shock.

Because they knew this man too well.

Even more so because Jiang Tian's attack was so ruthless.

That move even made the woman at the building entrance instinctively cover her face, feeling a strange pain.

That knee strike was too brutal.

"Lü Chun," the woman at the building entrance hurriedly ran up to help the man.

The burly man, supported by the petite woman, stood up, his face covered in blood.

His eyes glared fiercely at Jiang Tian, ​​as if he wanted to kill him at any moment.

“You fucking ambush me?” Lü Chun’s face burned with embarrassment, especially in front of the woman beside her. She felt utterly ashamed and tried to intimidate him with a fierce expression.

“Ambush?”

Jiang Tian sneered, but he had no intention of getting involved with them. Even without knowing their identities, Jiang Tian could tell from the information he had gathered that the enemy already knew who they were, yet they had foolishly walked right into their trap. Jiang Tian knew their capabilities were probably limited.

For Jiang Tian, ​​the most important person was Sister Wan’er.

He hadn’t had a chance to make amends in his past life; in this life, he would make up for it all.

“Sister Wan’er, you can’t go abroad,” Jiang Tian said, pulling Song Qiuwan’s hand with his uninjured right hand.

"Why?" Song Qiuwan asked softly, her head lowered as Jiang Tian grabbed her wrist.

Jiang Tian's eyes lit up; he secretly thought, "This is promising."

Thank goodness, Jiang Tian absolutely did not have any ulterior motives towards Song Qiuwan. To him, everything he did was simply to save Sister Wan'er.

The moment Jiang Tian learned of Wan'er's death, he suddenly realized the extent of his guilt towards her.

"Wan'er, your mission's target already knew your identity. If you went abroad, you were definitely dead," Jiang Tian said with absolute certainty.

What a joke.

The outcome was already clear in his past life, plus what Jiang Tian had overheard.

He was certain that, let alone these people, even if he went there himself and fell into the mysterious encirclement of some unknown force, he wouldn't be confident of escaping unscathed.

Because a missile might hit him the moment he got off the plane.

Anything can happen abroad.

"What did you say, you coward?"

"Chu Jing, did you hear that? Hahaha, I'm dying of laughter! This coward said we've been exposed!"

Upon hearing this, Lü Chun burst into a mocking laugh.

Even Chu Jing, the woman beside him, frowned deeply, her gaze towards Song Qiuwan filled with displeasure: "Qiuwan, what about the organization's confidentiality regulations? How could you let this good-for-nothing know these things, and yet he's still spouting nonsense here?"

In their organization, personnel changes and new recruits undergo background checks countless times stricter than those for civil servants. The screening of interpersonal relationships is practically flawless.

When Song Qiuwan agreed to join, the organization had already thoroughly investigated her entire resume and detailed past experiences.

At that time, Jiang Tian was indeed rather pathetic.

He was treated like a sucker by a scheming woman—no, he practically put a chain around his own neck. He rejected the more beautiful, gentler, and loving Qiuwan, even choosing to sever ties with her, instead frantically fawning over a scheming woman.

Then, the story of Qiuwan's pathetic childhood sweetheart spread throughout the organization, becoming the subject of ridicule for many.

They were already displeased that Jiang Tian knew this information, and now, hearing his words, they sneered even more.

"No, I didn't say that," Qiu Wan explained nervously, her eyes as clear as water.

"Heh, you didn't say that? How did you, this good-for-nothing Qingmei, know? And you even have the audacity to say the enemy found out? It's ridiculous!" Lü Chun's face was full of disdain, though his blood-covered face looked terrifyingly ferocious.

Jiang Tian narrowed his eyes. If it weren't for Song Qiu Wan, he would have already crippled him with a baton.

"Qiu Wan, I won't lie to you," Jiang Tian said softly.

"I believe you," Song Qiu Wan said, looking into Jiang Tian's earnest eyes.

She nodded firmly at Jiang Tian.

Seeing this, Jiang Tian couldn't help but smile slightly.

"Qiu Wan, are you crazy?"

"Do you know how many resources and sacrifices we've made to get this far, even the death of a comrade? And you think we've been exposed just because of Qingmei's words? How can you face the comrade who sacrificed his life?" Chu Jing finally couldn't hold back any longer and stood up angrily.

"The organization has regulations; if we're exposed, we can't continue the mission. I'll report this to the captain personally," Song Qiu Wan said directly.

"Fine, fine, fine! For your sake, you cowardly childhood sweetheart, you won't even believe your fallen teammates. We misjudged you!" Lü Chun was furious.

"Sacrifice is heroic, but just because a teammate died doesn't mean the intelligence was accurate and hadn't been leaked," Jiang Tian said calmly.

Before this, when he went to Tuchai for a mission, Zeng Fan's group was wiped out because of an inside betrayal.

Then, Jiang Tian directly pulled Song Qiuwan away.

Since it was almost certain death, how could Jiang Tian agree to let Song Qiuwan continue the mission?

As for the others...

Jiang Tian glanced at them.

If they chose to believe, let them go and carry it out themselves.

If they die, it's their own fault. After all, you can't reason with a ghost that's already dead. That captain didn't seem to be a capable person either.

They've already been exposed, and they didn't even know it, dragging their team members to their deaths.

Gripping Wan'er's arm tightly, Jiang Tian couldn't help but smirk slightly as he turned to leave.

But the next moment...

Lu Chun moved.

Quickly.

After Jiang Tian turned around, Lu Chun attacked immediately, rushing over and kicking directly at Jiang Tian's back.

"Lu Chun, stop! How dare you!"



Chapter 217 Mental Breakdown: My All-Out Attack Couldn't Break Through His Defense?


When Song Qiuwan caught a glimpse of Lü Chun making his move, she felt a jolt of shock wash over her.

Others might not know,

but she knew how terrifying Lü Chun's fighting prowess was.

He had once single-handedly killed thirteen retired mercenaries abroad.

His combat ability was terrifying.

Even during training a few days prior, Lü Chun had kicked a trainee so hard that it ruptured the trainee's spleen.

Fortunately, a medical team was nearby to immediately perform surgery to stop the bleeding; otherwise, the trainee would have died on the spot.

If Lü Chun had kicked him from behind,

his spine could have been easily broken, potentially paralyzing Jiang Tian.

This was going to cripple Jiang Tian.

Song Qiuwan's eyes turned bloodshot.

"Lu Chun, stop!" Song Qiuwan's mouth gaped open.

But Lu Chun was too fast.

His attacks were utterly reckless.

He really dared to do this; this was no joke. Song Qiuwan knew the organization's power all too well.

To put it bluntly, they even had a license to kill, to act first and report later.

As long as there was a reasonable excuse, they wouldn't even be held accountable.

Jiang Tian knew the mission information, and to be precise, he met this condition. For the sake of secrecy, and because he doubted the other party's identity, he could act like this, and even the organization wouldn't blame him.

In that instant, Song Qiuwan understood.

Her face turned deathly pale, and she didn't even have time to shout.

Bang!!!

A loud, muffled thud resounded.

The next moment...

Lu Chun's foot landed solidly on Jiang Tian's lower back.

Silence.

Dead silence.

Buzz!

After a brief silence.

Song Qiuwan's eyes widened.

"No..." Only then did Song Qiuwan's voice emerge.

But...

The scene she saw made her stare wide-eyed. Before her eyes, Lu Chun's foot had indeed landed a solid kick on Jiang Tian's lower back.

But the scene she had imagined—Jiang Tian flying several meters and collapsing to the ground struggling—did not occur. On the contrary, Jiang Tian remained standing in place.

His body didn't even sway.

At this moment, Lu Chun's expression froze, while Jiang Tian slowly turned his head to look at Lu Chun, tilting his head and saying calmly, "Is that all the strength you have?"

Buzz!

Lu Chun's pupils contracted.

The woman standing behind Lü Chun froze her mocking smile at the scene before her.

"Kid, I heard you're a narcotics cop. I didn't expect you'd need an iron plate on your back. You almost got one! No wonder everyone calls you a coward. Who else would they call a coward for being so afraid of death?" The next moment, Lü Chun seemed to realize something, wiping the cold sweat and blood from his face with a look of sudden understanding.

"So that's how it is. I didn't expect a cowardly coward like you to be so timid." Chu Jing, standing behind him, breathed a sigh of relief.

The next moment, caught off guard, Lü Chun pulled back his foot and threw a punch at Jiang Tian's cheek.

"You have an iron plate on your body, but you don't have one on your face." Lü Chun unleashed his fury. Killing a bull with this punch might be an exaggeration, but killing a person with it was entirely possible.

Bang!!!

The fist struck Jiang Tian's face.

But Jiang Tian's head didn't even budge.

What a joke! Do you even understand the concept of "superhuman"?

Jiang Tian's physical abilities are now almost superhuman.

Bullets couldn't withstand it; their penetrating power was too strong. But now, Jiang Tian, ​​from the inside out, his muscles, bones, and even his skin, possess terrifying resilience.

This punch was powerful.

It was still lacking compared to Jiang Tian.

And the shock caused by this scene was countless times more terrifying than before. There was something protecting his waist; surely there couldn't be something protecting his face too.

"Impossible, this is impossible, you coward!" Lü Chun's eyes widened, his head practically exploding.

This punch.

A searing pain, as if he had struck a concrete wall. The throbbing pain almost made Lü Chun scream.

"Finished?" Jiang Tian turned around and asked calmly.

"You..." Even Lü Chun, if he was stupid, sensed something was wrong.

Chu Jing was also terrified, taking two steps back and pointing at Jiang Tian as if she had seen a ghost.

"What are you?" Lü Chun cried out in despair.

Jiang Tian's expression remained unchanged. He grabbed Lü Chun's wrist with a backhand and applied slight force.

*Crack!!!*

Lü Chun's arm, which resembled a cow's leg, had its joint dislocated, and Lü Chun screamed in agony.

*Boom!!!*

Jiang Tian delivered a seemingly effortless kick.

Lü Chun was sent flying five or six meters, landing in front of Chu Jing, sprawling on the ground. He struggled to his feet, then looked up at Jiang Tian like a dead dog, his eyes now filled with terror, devoid of any arrogance.

"Holy shit!!!" Chu Jing leaned against the wall, staring at Jiang Tian.

*Pfft!!!*

Lü Chun coughed up blood, completely stunned.

They all knew Jiang Tian was a narcotics officer.

During their investigation of Song Qiuwan, they had thoroughly investigated Jiang Tian's background, discovering that he was a spineless coward in the narcotics unit, serving tea and water to others.

What else could this be but a spineless coward, a lackey's fate?

But now.

They stared at Jiang Tian in horror.

Who?

Who the hell investigated this information? Step forward, and they promise not to kill him.

You fucking said in the intelligence report that this kind of person is a simp?

A spineless coward?

A lackey?

A piece of trash not worth mentioning?

His police academy grades, if not at the bottom, are utterly worthless garbage?

At this moment, Jiang Tian gently raised Wan'er's hand, looking at them calmly, and said, "My patience has its limits. Out of respect for Wan'er, I've already shown you mercy. If you continue, I'll cripple you."

After saying that, Jiang Tian turned and left with Song Qiuwan, who was now dumbfounded, her face frozen in shock.

Leaving behind were only Lu Chun and Chu Jing, Song Qiuwan's two teammates, looking like dead dogs, stunned and unable to react for a long time.

After what seemed like an eternity, Chu Jing finally managed to stammer, "Pervert."

That's right.

He was a pervert.

Besides "pervert," she couldn't think of any other adjective. She knew Lü Chun's strength all too well.

But what was most terrifying about this man wasn't that he defeated Lü Chun, but that he completely humiliated her.

Her scalp tingled.

"Lü Chun, are you alright?" Chu Jing helped the battered Lü Chun up.

"He held back just now, otherwise that kick would have shattered my internal organs," Lü Chun said, wiping away cold sweat in terror.

Remembering how he'd just called him a coward, Lü Chun wanted to slap himself. How could he have been so vile? Why was his mouth so foul?

"That terrifying?" Chu Jing gasped.

"More than that! I thought I'd kicked a steel plate, that's why I said that. But when that punch landed on his face, it felt like hitting steel too! He could easily kill me! Who the hell called a coward a man like that?" Lü Chun cursed.

He'd never seen anyone so monstrous.

"Stop talking! I need to report to the captain right now. Qiu Wan's gone; the mission can't continue." Chu Jing said anxiously, pulling out her phone.



Chapter 218 Joint Operation Abroad? Pick My Peach and I'll Crush Your Teeth


Lu Chun fell silent. At first, he felt ashamed, but now, he felt no such shame.

Because of such skill.

Just surviving was a miracle, especially after hearing Chu Jing's phone call on speakerphone. Lu Chun struggled to sit up, his pale face gradually returning to normal.

He was right; Jiang Tian had indeed held back. Otherwise, one kick would have crippled him.

Jiang Tian had only dislocated his arm joint, not completely crushed it.

That was out of consideration.

Lu Chun might have problems, or he might be useless, but he was Qiu Wan's teammate, and ultimately, he was acting for the people and protecting the country. That's why Jiang Tian hadn't killed him. Otherwise, a single, casual move would have ended Lu Chun's career.

“What is it?”

Soon, a woman's voice came from the other end of the line, crisp and almost emotionless.

“Our mission information has been leaked.” Chu Jing felt a chill run down her spine upon hearing the voice.

"Who knows?" The captain's voice remained emotionless, even icy.

"Qiu Wan's intelligence report mentioned a spineless childhood sweetheart," Chu Jing quickly interjected.

"Spineless?"

"Who said that?"

On the other end of the line, in an office, the captain stood up, phone in hand, his tone filled with surprise.

"That's what everyone's been saying!" A sense of foreboding rose in Chu Jing's heart.

"His name is Jiang Tian?" The captain's voice quickened.

"Yes, Captain, you know too?" Chu Jing nodded quickly.

Lu Chun, enduring the pain and covered in cold sweat, also pricked up her ears.

A moment later, a voice came from the phone: "Coward? If the coward you're talking about, Qingmei, is Jiang Tian, ​​then let me tell you, he's a legendary figure who has earned five individual first-class merits, one special-class merit, and was nominated by Minister Tan of Public Security at a meeting to be a first-class hero, a pioneer in the fight against drugs—a true hero who made history! Do you still call him a coward?"

Boom!!!

Lu Chun, on the verge of death, sat bolt upright in shock.

At this moment, he even forgot his pain, sitting up abruptly, his eyes wide, pupils contracting rapidly.

Chu Jing's mouth gaped open, her voice trembling: "Captain, you...you're not joking, are you?"

Is this something a human could do?

Their team had risked their lives, carried out so many missions, and so far, Lu Chun only had three second-class merits and one collective first-class merit.

And this was already his greatest honor.

And now, you're telling him that guy had five First-Class Merits, and even a Special-Class Merit?

Didn't he lose the Special-Class Merit?

The captain didn't speak, because when he first investigated this information, he was just as shocked, even more shocked than them.

"Captain, how...how do you know so much?" Chu Jing asked, her voice trembling.

"Because the leadership contacted me, hoping to recruit him into our organization, so I investigated him," the captain said seriously.

"Then, Captain, what about tonight's mission?" Lü Chun asked, sweating profusely.

"I'll go personally."

The captain hung up the phone after saying that.

At this moment, Lü Chun and Chu Jing suddenly realized what was happening.

"You didn't lose unjustly just now," Chu Jing said after a moment of silence, addressing Lü Chun, who was still in shock and had forgotten his pain.

One First-Class Merit is luck; five First-Class Merits are legendary, plus a Special-Class Merit.

Is this even something a human could achieve?

They almost thought they were listening to a myth.

However, while they were still reeling from the news of Jiang Tian's six merits, Jiang Tian had already returned to the hospital with Song Qiuwan.

Because Director Liao had called Jiang Tian, ​​asking where he had been and that it was time for his dressing change.

By the time they arrived back at the hospital,

Director Liao had been waiting for a long time.

"Who told you to run around like that? Don't you know you can't run around with your injuries?" Director Liao scolded Jiang Tian irritably.

"Fine, fine, fine."

For the first time since his rebirth, Jiang Tian felt no anger at being scolded; after all, Director Liao was only trying to help him.

Reaching out, Director Liao immediately noticed the blood on Jiang Tian's hand.

Rolling up his sleeve, she saw blood soaking through the gauze.

"What have you been doing?"

Liao Ying instantly exploded, carefully cutting open the wound with scissors, revealing a gruesome sight.

Following behind Jiang Tian, ​​Song Qiuwan, who had remained silent, witnessed this scene, overwhelmed with guilt—a guilt so profound it made her contemplate suicide.

"Director Liao, everything I do needs to be kept confidential. I can't disclose anything. You just need treatment. I'll bear any problems myself. If I die, it's just bad luck," Jiang Tian said, his expression turning slightly cold as he noticed Song Qiuwan's pale face.

Liao Ying was slightly startled by Jiang Tian's change in attitude and quickly explained, "Officer Jiang, you just had surgery yesterday. You need to be careful and rest. Be mindful next time, otherwise, the wound will get worse with each treatment."

"Alright, I know," Jiang Tian said impatiently.

Then, Jiang Tian changed the dressing with a blank expression, rubbing the raw, bleeding wound. His expression remained unchanged.

Liao Ying subconsciously swallowed.

After changing the dressing, Liao Ying turned and left without a word, seemingly angry.

"This doctor was only doing it for your own good. Why were you so harsh with him?" Song Qiuwan held Jiang Tian's arm, tears welling in her eyes.

It's all her fault! She deserves to die!

Jiang Tian remained silent. Seeing Song Qiuwan's guilty expression, he knew that if he continued, she might even do something drastic.

Knowing Song Qiuwan as he did, extreme guilt could indeed lead her to do something.

But just then, hurried footsteps sounded at the door.

Along with this came Zhao Qiuni's cheerful voice: "Brother Jiang, I'm here again!"

Immediately following was Zhao Wei's voice: "Captain Jiang, I'm so angry! That new deputy director, Li Qishan, came in and immediately started interfering with the anti-drug work. That's bad enough, but headquarters sent a team from the 21st Bureau and another team from the Yunhai anti-drug unit to conduct a joint operation with the neighboring authorities to crack down on the Yun family's criminal gang. But do you know who's leading them? It's that Li Qishan! I'm furious!"

Jiang Tian frowned.

He could only say that this guy's appetite was far too big. But wasn't he afraid of breaking his teeth if he picked the peach like that? Jiang Tian's eyes flashed.

As the voices rang out, Zhao Qiuni and Zhao Wei stood side by side at the ward door.

Zhao Wei is alright.

Zhao Qiuni? ???

Hehe....

No hehe....



Why did Chapter 219 suddenly feel like a battleground?


Zhao Qiuni, with her naturally carefree and playful personality, was already chattering away even before entering the ward.

To be honest, in his past life, Jiang Tian had always thought Zhao Qiuni was the aloof, domineering female CEO type. The only image he had of her was the look of disappointment in her eyes when she entered his room.

It wasn't until after his rebirth that Jiang Tian discovered it was completely different. Her personality was a stark contrast to her appearance.

She was aloof before, but once you got to know her, she was a goofball.

Especially in front of Jiang Tian. Back in the hotel, when Jiang Tian rescued Zhao Qiuni, wow, even with a slight draft from the window, Zhao Qiuni opened her arms wide to stand beside him, shielding him from the wind and lighting his cigarette.

This made Jiang Tian thoroughly familiar with Zhao Qiuni's personality.

However, today, Zhao Qiuni, standing at the door, looked drastically different from before.

Her smiling face was completely stiff.

Especially Zhao Wei, completely oblivious to the unusual rage, exclaimed, "Captain Jiang, you know how infuriating this is? In the Municipal Bureau, Uncle Jiang's words carry no weight anymore. Li Qishan keeps bringing up the Yunhai Standing Committee member, constantly opposing Uncle Jiang, even openly refuting Uncle Jiang's efforts and disapproving of your mission execution methods, believing that individual heroism leads nowhere. Most importantly, those leaders actually agree with his line of thinking!"

He was furious.

Zhao Wei had already been furious back in the Municipal Bureau, slamming his fist on the table and standing up, not even giving Li Qishan any face, directly pointing at Li Qishan's face in his rebuttal.

This left Li Qishan utterly humiliated.

"Brother Jiang, you didn't see it. There were even leaders from the Organization Department in the meeting; their expressions were priceless."

Zhao Wei vividly described what happened to Jiang Tian.

This was indeed quite a novel experience for Jiang Tian.

However, there weren't many surprises, because...

"Leader Jing has gained most of the support in the Standing Committee. Through the pressure exerted by the Standing Committee, he has gained strong control over the Municipal Bureau. This operation is also a statement from Leader Jing. If it goes well, the position of Secretary of the Political and Legal Affairs Commission should be his," Jiang Tian said calmly.

Jiang Tian hadn't been involved much in politics, and didn't even possess much political acumen, but he possessed the talent of a master detective. This talent gave Jiang Tian more than just terrifying deductive reasoning abilities.

That would be underestimating the terrifying nature of the talent of a master detective.

Deducing the opponent's purpose from their actions, and inferring what they might do from their past actions, wasn't difficult for Jiang Tian.

"Holy crap, Captain Jiang, what do you mean?" Zhao Wei's eyes widened.

He hadn't expected that this simple personnel reshuffle would involve the power struggle for the position of Secretary of the Political and Legal Affairs Commission under Uncle Jiang.

Doesn't that mean...?

“That’s right. If Li Qishan succeeds in this operation, power, fame, everything will be within their grasp,” Jiang Tian said expressionlessly.

He is a narcotics officer, that’s true, and there are many things he can’t do in public.

But at this time, if they need to bring out a public celebrity proxy,

that’s Li Qishan.

Jiang Tian's eyes turned icy. This wasn't just a simple act of reaping rewards; it was a direct kick away of Jiang Tian.

The scheme was orchestrated by Madam Jing.

To lure Jiang Tian into the trap.

Because no matter how Jiang Tian defended himself, how Madam Jing had offended him, or what she had said, it didn't matter.

What mattered was that Jiang Tian had defied authority and assaulted Madam Jing. With such arrogance, what were the leaders thinking? If he dared to assault Madam Jing today, what would happen to him and his father if they continued to hold power?

The other side only needed to let the Standing Committee and the leaders know about this, and then they could easily suppress Jiang Tian.

The only flaw in their calculations was that they hadn't anticipated that Chief Gao and the other leaders would find out about this.

But now that Chief Gao had returned to Beijing and didn't have time to intervene, their plan was progressing smoothly.

To infiltrate the Municipal Bureau with Li Qishan.

Then, leading a large force in a joint operation, if the plan succeeds, Li Qishan will gain both fame and fortune, and seize control of the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

Leader Jing will also be able to rise to power.

Jiang Tian hadn't considered this before; in fact, things were going too smoothly at first, causing him to lower his guard.

However, after beating Madam Jing, seeing Leader Jing's reaction, and then Li Qishan's subsequent appointment as deputy director of the Municipal Public Security Bureau,

Jiang Tian's entry on the "King of Criminal Investigation" quickly made everything clear to him.

This also made him realize that the most terrifying and cruel battles are often those fought without bloodshed.

Buzz!!!

Upon hearing Jiang Tian's explanation, everyone in the room erupted in shock.

Especially Zhao Qiuni.

Her previously stiff, smiling face, now filled with utter depression, nearly exploded with rage upon hearing Jiang Tian's explanation.

"How could they do this?" Zhao Qiuni's face flushed crimson with anger; she was practically bursting with rage.

Just thinking about it made her furious. What to do?

"That Li Qishan, have you investigated his background?" Jiang Tian suddenly asked Zhao Wei.

Zhao Wei shook his head, looking confused.

Jiang Tian nodded thoughtfully, saying nothing. The plan was good, but its success depended on its execution.

For Jiang Tian, ​​personal interests could be set aside.

If the operation went smoothly, striking at the Yun family while stabilizing the situation in Yunhai and curbing the arrogance of the criminal group, a few small gains were insignificant.

His biggest fear was that Li Qishan was an incompetent fool, greedy for quick results and reckless.

Ultimately, this could lead to the deaths of many.

That was the last thing Jiang Tian wanted to see.

As Jiang Tian fell silent, an eerie atmosphere filled the room.

Zhao Qiuni frequently glanced at Wan'er.

At first, Wan'er smiled amicably, but as she was stared at more often, she sensed something was amiss, and the two women began to exchange glances.

By the time Jiang Tian noticed, the atmosphere in the room had become extremely tense.

Even Lin Jinxuan had quietly huddled in a corner.

"What's wrong with you two? Are your eyes bothering you?" Jiang Tian asked, turning to her, puzzled.

He genuinely hadn't thought much of it, because he had no impure thoughts about either of the women.

Saving Zhao Qiuni was purely coincidental, and rescuing Wan'er was also to make up for Jiang Tian's regrets from his past life.

In Jiang Tian's heart, there were only Mu Ya and Gao Yangyang. He didn't know how to deal with either of them, especially Mu Ya, who was his ideal woman.

However, because of his mother-in-law's issues, Jiang Tian hadn't figured out how to approach her. As for Gao Yangyang, it was purely accidental, and Jiang Tian didn't know how to handle her.

Jiang Tian had never had any improper thoughts about the two women beside him.

"Little sister, your eyes are uncomfortable. This is a hospital. Go see an ophthalmologist," Zhao Wei said, showing his concern.

What he received in return was Zhao Qiuni's death stare.

Swish!!!

Cold sweat broke out on Zhao Wei's forehead; a feeling of impending death enveloped him.

Bang bang bang!!!



Chapter 220 Major Smuggling Clue, The Truth Behind the Mission


Just then, a knock sounded at the door.

Everyone's attention shifted to the doorway.

The next moment.

A woman with an icy demeanor stood in the doorway.

Swish!!!

Zhao Qiuni and Lin Jinxuan's gazes instantly turned to Jiang Tian, ​​their eyes filled with a death stare.

This moment made Jiang Tian freeze, because he had already sensed the awkward atmosphere and was trying to figure out how to defuse it when the woman appeared at the door.

Jiang Tian racked his brains, but couldn't recall any information about this woman.

Fortunately.

The next moment, Song Qiuwan spoke, dispelling the strange atmosphere in the room.

Song Qiuwan stood up and hurriedly said to the woman, "Captain."

Captain?

The others looked somewhat confused.

But Jiang Tian, ​​on the hospital bed, instantly sat up straight, his gaze fixed intently on the woman.

She's the captain?

Jiang Tian sized up the woman, while she sized him up in return.

Jiang Tian's expression gradually turned cold upon seeing the captain. He figured she was most likely there to cause trouble.

After all, he had just nearly crippled her team member, interrupted her mission, and forcibly taken Wan'er away.

But the next moment, the woman's icy face softened slightly. She nodded slightly to Wan'er, then looked at Jiang Tian and said, "Officer Jiang, may I speak with you privately?"

Du Qing looked at Jiang Tian's face. Even though she had already reviewed his file, she was still taken aback by his youth.

So young, so inexperienced.

Yet he already possessed a Special Merit Citation and five First-Class Merit Citations.

Such achievements felt like a dream to Du Qing.

"Captain, Officer Jiang is just worried about me. Please don't bother Officer Jiang anymore," Song Qiuwan hurriedly stood up, assuming the captain was there to cause trouble.

She knew her captain well; he was a truly terrifying figure.

Born into a mysterious martial arts family, even Lü Chun was no match for him in close combat, and his battlefield prowess was even more terrifying.

In the past, she always thought her captain was practically invincible, but after witnessing Jiang Tian's skills, she no longer thought so.

Good heavens, withstanding Lü Chun's attacks head-on was far more shocking than simply crushing Lü Chun.

However, her captain had always been very protective of Song Qiuwan, both within the organization and so on. Song Qiuwan genuinely didn't want her captain to clash with Jiang Tian.

Regardless of who won, it wouldn't look good. She had been thinking about how to explain things to her captain. As for how Jiang Tian knew about the enemy, she didn't know and didn't want to ask, but she was willing to trust Jiang Tian.

But before she could figure out how to persuade her captain, he came to her door directly. Was it for revenge for Lü Chun?

"Don't worry, I just have some things I want to ask Captain Jiang," Du Qing explained calmly.

Song Qiuwan looked at Jiang Tian with concern.

No one in the room spoke, but their eyes were fixed on Jiang Tian.

Especially Zhao Qiuni and Zhao Wei, both from prominent families in Kyoto, were very sensitive to certain people.

They especially sensed something unusual about Du Qing.

"Damn, which organization?" Zhao Wei suddenly remembered the woman Jiang Tian had asked him to investigate—wasn't that Song Qiuwan? And Song Qiuwan called him "Captain"?

"Sure, I'll wrap it up later, otherwise the doctor will yell at me again." Jiang Tian stared for a moment, then slowly wrapped the bandage around his arm and hung it around his neck.

"You're afraid of doctors too?" Du Qing asked, somewhat surprised.

"After all, doctors are quite scary," Jiang Tian said quietly.

"If the hundreds of drug dealers you killed knew this, they'd probably jump out of their coffins, Mr. Six-Kung Fu King." Du Qing led the way into the back garden, his tone casual.

"You investigated me?" Jiang Tian's languid demeanor vanished, his tone gradually turning cold as he stood in the back garden.

Knowing even the codename "Six-Kung Fu King" meant that the other party had conducted a thorough investigation of him.

Even the codename, only confirmed that morning, was known to the other party by the afternoon after Zhao Cainan returned to Kyoto with the interview transcript. This intelligence capability made Jiang Tian take it slightly seriously.

"That's right, I not only know you're the Six Merits King, but I also know your current situation isn't good. You've painstakingly cultivated this peach, only to have it taken away." As he said this, a hint of sarcasm appeared on Du Qing's face.

"What do you want?" Jiang Tian disliked this feeling of being out of control.

"Join us." Du Qing said directly.

"No interest."

Jiang Tian turned and left.

He didn't have much goodwill towards this organization yet.

"Really no interest? Legally carrying a gun." Du Qing reminded him.

"Have you forgotten my current status? I'm legally carrying a gun now." Jiang Tian produced his gun license.

A bright red booklet. What a joke! Given the dangerous situation Jiang Tian was in, how could he not be armed, especially after drug dealers attacked his family?

Gao Xiaolong personally applied for a gun license for Jiang Tian at headquarters. Even Jiang Peng didn't have this license, but Jiang Tian possessed it, precisely because of the special nature of his profession.

"Do you think we don't know they've discovered us? Do you look down on our organization?" Du Qing asked.

This is a talent.

Especially knowing Lü Chun's strength, after learning about Jiang Tian's fight with Lü Chun, Du Qing couldn't sit still any longer.

She rushed to find Jiang Tian immediately.

As before, she had experienced Lü Chun's power firsthand. How could someone in this world be caught off guard by Lü Chun's full-force attack, yet remain unharmed and unable to even break through his defenses? How monstrous must that strength be?

"Isn't it?" Jiang Tian retorted sarcastically.

A bitter smile appeared on Du Qing's usually cold face, her lips slightly dry as she said, "We already knew."

This made Jiang Tian narrow his eyes slightly.

Du Qing licked his lips and smiled bitterly, "Didn't we know that carrying out this mission was a death sentence? Didn't we know the other side discovered it? No, I know everything, but the other side has an important map of drug lord routes. Through this route, they transport more than ten thousand kilograms of drugs into our country every year."

Ten thousand kilograms every year?

This is every year.

Jiang Tian's gaze sharpened, and he couldn't help but gasp. If it were anything else, Jiang Tian might not be interested, but when it came to combating drug crimes, he was definitely interested.

Tens of thousands of kilograms every year! What does that mean?

It could even be a tenth of the national total! This is the whole country!

If this route were cracked down on, how much would the number of drug crimes decrease each year?

Jiang Tian had never forgotten his original intention.



Chapter 221 Seeking Revenge for Mother? A Life Was Lost


“Since you know going is suicide, why did you still choose to go? Because they've discovered you, you'll be useless except for dying. Besides, that foreign man should know about this route, right? You could just arrest him directly, wouldn't you?” Jiang Tian asked.

“It’s not that simple. This route is top secret. They must have some connections within the country to allow it to flow in so freely. And that foreign man is just an outer member of the real drug lord on this route. We wouldn’t get much information from him if we arrested him. So our plan is to go abroad and use ourselves as bait to lure out the people behind him.” Du Qing revealed everything without reservation.

Sacrificing oneself?

Very noble.

But it didn’t move Jiang Tian; it even made him feel it was foolish. Gambling with one’s life?

Jiang Tian couldn’t even understand this method.

“Foolish. Before I understood, I thought highly of you, but now, I’ve lost all interest.” Jiang Tian turned and left.

“You have a plan?” Du Qing asked eagerly.

Jiang Tian glanced at Du Qing but left without a word. If it were him, he would choose to fight his way through.

If he couldn't find it, he wouldn't bother looking; he'd just keep fighting until he found it.

However, as Jiang Tian returned to the ward, he found a large crowd gathered in the lobby, watching something in the corridor.

Jiang Tian had even less interest in this spectacle.

He pressed the elevator button.

Jiang Tian stepped into the elevator.

While his mind was still preoccupied, his phone rang urgently.

Jiang Tian took out his phone and saw it was Zhao Wei.

"What is it? I'm coming right back." Jiang Tian assumed Zhao Qiuni was worried about him and had her brother call him.

"Captain Jiang, don't go back to the ward yet," Zhao Wei said urgently.

"What is it?" Jiang Tian frowned.

“Leader Jing’s son, with a large group of people, all armed with knives, is blocking the ward door. I just went to the restroom and luckily escaped being blocked. You’re still suspended, Captain Jiang, please don’t come back. These people look incredibly fierce; they look like they’d dare to attack someone. If you hurt someone or get injured now, you’ll be in big trouble. I’ve already contacted Branch Manager Song; they’re on their way.” Zhao Wei quickly explained.

“Is this never going to end?”

Jiang Tian’s gaze grew even colder.

He was already in a bad mood.

Now he was completely devastated.

What?

Have they gone completely mad?

His mother schemed against him, he tried to reap the rewards, and now his son is coming to kill him?

Is he easy to bully or what?

Fine, fine.

Jiang Tian, ​​reborn, has always been rational, but now his patience has completely vanished.

Most importantly.

Who are the people inside the room, the ones being held at knifepoint?

Zhao Qiuni, from a prominent family in Kyoto.

Song Qiuwan, from the Special Security Department.

Lin Jinxuan, though seemingly ordinary, is also a police academy instructor.

“It’s too late,” Jiang Tian said to Zhao Wei.

“Captain Jiang, listen to me. We’ll be fine. Without you here, Leader Jing’s son isn’t so lawless as to dare do anything to us. But if you were here, things might be different. If something happens, it will have a huge impact on you,” Zhao Wei pleaded earnestly.

Jiang Tian stood in the elevator, his lips barely moving: "No, I mean, I'm already up."

"Holy crap!!!" Zhao Wei's scalp tingled.

Sure enough.

The next moment, the elevator doors opened.

Five or six people were already standing at the elevator entrance, each wielding a large machete, blocking the ward door.

In 2003, social order wasn't as peaceful as it would be in later years; various thugs and underworld forces existed everywhere.

Especially in Yunhai.

Those involved in organized crime inevitably dealt with the three scourges: gambling, prostitution, and drugs.

These three scourges fueled the growth of dark crime, leading to the extremely arrogant and domineering criminal forces in Yunhai, just like that small-time drug dealer from Yunda who openly bribed him.

It seemed ridiculous.

In reality, reality is always more surreal than imagination.

The moment the elevator doors opened.

This was a VIP ward floor, not many people were coming and going, and Jiang Tian's ward was very close to the elevator.

The moment the elevator doors opened, they came face to face.

Especially the leader, with dyed yellow hair.

He was holding a large machete, standing in front of Jiang Tian's ward.

Blocking the entire doorway.

As the elevator doors opened, they seemed to be talking, their faces full of arrogant and mocking smiles.

"That idiot, he dared to hit my mother, but didn't have the guts to stand up. A coward, no, probably headless, because his head is fucking retracted inside," the yellow-haired leader sneered.

"Brother Rui is awesome."

"He dared to hit my aunt, Brother Rui! Let that bastard step forward, I'll chop him up first!"

"You can run away from a monk, but you can't hide from the temple."

"That bastard's got some luck with the ladies, Brother Rui, look at those women in the ward, they're all pretty."

One of the underlings was drooling in front of Brother Rui.

The blond-haired guy called Brother Rui couldn't help but swallow hard too, but everything changed the moment Jiang Tian appeared.

"Kill him! Go kill him!"

"Damn it, he dared to hit my mom, if I don't kill him, how can I ever show my face again?"

"That idiot finally came."

Brother Rui's blond-haired kid's eyes turned red the moment he saw Jiang Tian, ​​because he clearly remembered his mother saying that Jiang Tian, ​​that beast, even with a gunshot wound to his hand, could still be so ruthless.

Seeing the injury on Jiang Tian's hand, his eyes turned red, and he was the first to grab a knife and rush towards Jiang Tian.

At this moment, the person who had just been cornered in the ward had red eyes.

"This is outrageous! How dare you!" Zhao Qiuni was furious.

Such a thing had actually happened.

She was going to tell her grandfather.

Was there no justice left in Yunhai?
