Jing Feifan's face instantly turned ashen, because behind Jiang Tian, ​​several people had unknowingly risen to stand.

Each of them exuded a clean and decisive, fierce aura.

"Or are you saying you have nothing to do with Jing Feifan?"



Chapter 258 A Turning Point in Fate: Jing Feifan Under Investigation


"Jing Feifan, I hereby unilaterally announce that you have been temporarily suspended from your duties pending further investigation." Zhou Zhao made a swift and decisive decision.

Having experienced various trials within the system, Zhou Zhao understood that a decision, even a statement, was necessary.

Most importantly, given the current situation, Jing Feifan was absolutely finished; he himself was in dire straits.

Thinking this, Zhou Zhao felt immense regret!

He had only wanted to curb the young man's arrogance, which he didn't see as a problem. A young person being too sharp wasn't good; suppressing his arrogance might allow him to grow better in the future.

The fact that father and son were promoted and made rich together had a negative impact. Jiang Peng might not get promoted this time, but he could have another chance. Jing Feifan was different.

As another capable assistant, if Jing Feifan didn't get promoted this time, he would never have another chance.

Jiang Peng had a powerful son, but Jing Feifan had nothing.

In this situation, what's wrong with me, Zhou Zhao, favoring Jing Feifan and letting one of my own, Jing Feifan, take the position?

It would also curb Jiang Tian's arrogance. Perfect, a win-win situation—training talent and cultivating two of our own.

A complete double win!

In this incident, he didn't need to do anything; he just needed to slightly promote Li Qishan, and then wait for the next opportunity. If Jiang Peng went up, Li Qishan would naturally follow.

That way, he'd have everything.

But who could have imagined that, in order to curb his arrogance, you would directly suspend Jiang Tian and even produce a mental illness diagnosis report?

That's going too far.

That's excessive.

Even Zhou Zhao was horrified. They'd gone mad!

Even if this reached Kyoto, the entire Yunhai region would be in trouble, a heroic anti-drug police officer being diagnosed with mental illness.

Who did this?

“Jing Feifan, on behalf of Yunhai, I hereby issue you this urgent notice: Sun Feng, contact all committee members to thoroughly implement the measures regarding Comrade Jing Feifan's work replacement, and formally issue an indefinite suspension of Jing Feifan. Finally, request the Discipline Inspection Commission to conduct a double-regulation investigation of Comrade Jing Feifan.”

Having taken it, Jing Feifan showed no mercy.

If he was going to do it, he'd see it through to the end.

As for whether to protect him, that was madness. In this situation, Zhou Zhao was in dire straits himself!

Getting Jiang Tian to calm down was the most important thing.

"Comrade Jiang, are you satisfied with this outcome?"

After completing all this, Zhou Zhao didn't even glance at Jing Feifan, quickly turning his gaze to Jiang Tian. His sharp eyes instantly softened, as if his expression had changed in a flash.

Jing Feifan was stunned.

Wait, what do you mean?

Suspended from his post and even placed under investigation?

"Secretary..." Jing Feifan uttered in disbelief.

Even if there was no hope for that position.

But you can't treat him like this!

You must know, even without the third-in-command position, Jing Feifan still held a high status.

Don't be fooled by Jiang Tian's blatant disregard for his authority. Jiang Tian doesn't give Jing Feifan the slightest bit of face, and he'll slap him in the face without hesitation. Ultimately, Jing Feifan is already at the top of the Yunhai hierarchy.

He's like the fishmonger who appeared in Jiang Tian's past life—his backer was only slightly higher than Jing Feifan's, yet he became that fishmonger's backer.

That's practically godlike among ordinary people.

He could practically roam freely throughout Yunhai.

Even without promotion, he'd be a superior person in the future. His current status is enough to make him one.

He's still young, not even fifty. So young, there's no doubt he'll have opportunities to advance further.

But now, he's been suspended from his duties.

Jing Feifan's head was buzzing, as if it were about to explode.

Suspension was bad enough.

But Zhou Zhao's last words completely blew him away: "Shuanggui" (double regulation), an investigation?

Are you crazy?

They're going to put him under "Shuanggui" directly?

It's not a big deal if things aren't investigated, but if an investigation really happens, things that are insignificant on ordinary days can be fatal if they're uncovered during a period of double regulation.

It's over.

It's completely over.

Once you're under double regulation, there's truly no chance left.

"Secretary? What secretary? I'm not your secretary anymore. In the end, I'm just a guilty person who recommended Li Qishan, ultimately causing the country huge losses. Although the main mistake was Li Qishan, I, Zhou Zhao, also bear inescapable responsibility for misjudging people. Most importantly..."

Here, Zhou Zhao paused slightly, then his voice gradually turned furious: "Right here in Yunhai, the most important thing is that someone actually diagnosed a hero with a mental illness, using a diagnosis of mental illness to suppress and attack a hero. Jing Feifan, you deserve to die!"

As he spoke, Zhou Zhao's tone became increasingly furious, even directly reciting Gao Xiaolong's insults to Jing Feifan.

"Jing Feifan did it?" Gao Xiaolong asked, his face filled with astonishment.

Zhou Zhao quickly explained, "I just had Secretary Sun investigate, and what do you know? I was shocked by what I found out—it was Jing Feifan who did it."

"Arrest him!" Gao Xiaolong clapped his hands.

*Snap!*

Fang Ming, who had been following Gao Xiaolong, glanced at Chu Jing and Lü Chun upon hearing this. The two of them instantly rushed forward and handcuffed Jing Feifan.

"Who are you? What do you want? Even if you're arresting me, it should be the disciplinary committee who's handling this! Let me go!" Jing Feifan struggled frantically.

But unfortunately.

Faced with two professionals, even Chu Jing, despite her seemingly harmless appearance—a novice easily defeated by Jiang Tian—was already at the pinnacle of skill among ordinary people.

With just a slight exertion, all of Jing Feifan's struggles ceased. In their hands, Jing Feifan was like a weak chicken, utterly powerless.

"Let me go! You have no right to arrest me, no right at all!" Jing Feifan was still struggling.

Unfortunately.

No one spoke.

Even Zhou Zhao just watched silently.

This sent a chill down Jing Feifan's spine as he struggled frantically.

His entire body felt ice-cold.

It's over.

It's completely over.

After Jing Feifan's struggling sounds gradually faded away,

Zhou Zhao's gaze returned to Jiang Tian, ​​and he asked, "Comrade Jiang, are you satisfied?"

Yes.

Zhou Zhao looked at Jiang Tian anxiously.

He felt a little panicked.

Despite his decisive actions just now, Zhou Zhao knew very well that he wasn't even sure if Jiang Tian knew about what he had done.

If Jiang Tian knew that he had tacitly approved of the suppression,

would he simply not forgive him? And if even the primary victim, Jiang Tian, ​​didn't forgive him, he might even be directly labeled as someone who persecuted Jiang Tian.

With that label, would he have a good ending?



Chapter 259 Since they don't believe it, then fight them until they do.


The more Zhou Zhao thought about it, the drier his lips became.

Putting himself in Jiang Tian's shoes, if he were directly diagnosed with a mental illness, would he forgive him?

Whether Jing Feifan was responsible for this was no longer necessary to verify.

Because it was him.

Even if it wasn't him, Li Qishan was the one who led the charge.

But Zhou Zhao himself was innocent. He admitted he had gone too far, tacitly agreeing to the suppression of Jiang Tian and his son, but he genuinely knew nothing about this.

However, putting himself in Jiang Tian's shoes, he understood that forgiveness would be difficult.

As Zhou Zhao spoke, Gao Xiaolong and Fang Ming's gazes both turned to Jiang Tian. Yes, this matter, in the final analysis, depended on Jiang Tian's attitude.

And whether this problem could be resolved depended on Jiang Tian's abilities.

It appeared that Song Liguo was leading the team, but everyone understood that the key to victory and whether the situation could be salvaged lay with Jiang Tian.

It was only because Jiang Tian was too young and his rank was insufficient that he couldn't lead the team.

Otherwise, Jiang Tian would have been directly put in charge of this matter.

If this mistake couldn't be undone later,

if the outcome was bad, Zhou Zhao's continued position would be uncertain.

In fact, if Jiang Tian were to shake his head now, Gao Xiaolong might even call Kyoto to complain on Jiang Tian's behalf.

But...

Jiang Tian, ​​the owner of the "King of Criminal Investigation" title, couldn't possibly be unaware of the intricacies involved. He could easily grasp the crux of the problem.

He also knew that his current attitude might even determine Zhou Zhao's future.

Jiang Tian smiled.

How miserable his past life had been! Jing Feifan's current situation mirrored Jiang Tian's own experience—his family had all fallen, and he himself had been deceived his entire life by a scheming woman.

Jiang Tian's eyes shone as he watched Jing Feifan being dragged away, as if he were seeing the fate of his father in his past life. But this time, things were different.

This time, it wasn't his father.

It was Jing Feifan.

In his past life, Jing Feifan led the arrest of Jiang Tian's father, Jiang Peng, who triumphed, rising through the ranks and reaching the pinnacle of his life. Jiang Tian's father, Jiang Peng, fell into Jing Feifan's hands, enduring countless tortures and being framed with numerous pieces of evidence.

Ninety-nine percent of these were fabricated, but by then, no one could trace them.

Now.

Everything has been completely reversed.

Jing Feifan is the one who has been arrested.

Jing Feifan is the one who has lost his wife and children.

Jiang Peng stands here, looking at him with pride.

It seems he is encouraging him not to worry about anything.

So.

The next moment.

Under the watchful eyes of everyone, Jiang Tian finally spoke.

Zhou Zhao watched Jiang Tian's every move and expression with tense eyes. The moment Jiang Tian nodded, it felt as if a huge weight had been lifted from Zhou Zhao's heart.

It also allowed him to breathe a huge sigh of relief.

Relieved, Zhou Zhao ran up excitedly and grabbed Jiang Tian's hand: "Comrade Jiang, you still have a sense of perspective. This time, your uncle was wrong, I misjudged you. But don't worry, Comrade Jiang, watch how I make amends."

Some things can't be said too bluntly.

Jiang Tian understood, and Gao Xiaolong understood even better.

Fang Ming also watched with a meaningful, ambiguous smile.

After speaking, Zhou Zhao had many important matters to attend to, so he quickly left with Secretary Sun without delay.

Before leaving, Sun Feng shook hands with Jiang Tian, ​​saying, "Little Jiang, don't call me Secretary Sun, it's too formal. I'm being presumptuous. Just call me Brother Sun. Don't be shy in the future. If anything happens, don't forget to call your Brother Sun."

"Definitely, definitely," Jiang Tian nodded slightly.

After the two left, Jiang Tian finally let out a sigh of relief.

Seeing Gao Xiaolong and Fang Ming both smiling, Jiang Tian couldn't help but roll his eyes.

How could he not know that what he had just witnessed was part of Gao Xiaolong's test?

If he had nodded,

Zhou Zhao would almost certainly have been taken down. The problem was serious, and given the parties involved and Jiang Tian's dissatisfied appeal, it would be a miracle if Zhou Zhao could continue in his position.

Based solely on recommending Li Qishan, Zhou Zhao had absolutely no chance or hope of continuing.

Therefore, Jiang Tian's attitude is crucial. If Jiang Tian doesn't pursue the matter, Zhou Zhao still has room to maneuver. If the outcome can be reversed, Zhou Zhao might even be able to continue in his position.

Of course, even if he stays in his post, further advancement is impossible for him. He already had some opportunities based on the achievements brought by Jiang Tian, ​​but now, Zhou Zhao has lost those opportunities.

Jiang Tian is very important here.

However, if Jiang Tian chooses to back down, it might indeed prevent Zhou Zhao from succeeding, but the backlash from Zhou Zhao, and the retaliation from many leaders in Yunhai, would be far more terrifying.

No one likes someone who has gone too far.

Especially a subordinate who is out of control and could potentially eliminate them at any time.

Such a person is unlikely to be cultivated by anyone; it's tantamount to completely blocking one's path in Yunhai.

Conversely, if Zhou Zhao stays, he might be grateful in the future, but if problems arise, Zhou Zhao will definitely be the first to exonerate Jiang Tian.

In other words...

From now on, Zhou Zhao will be Jiang Tian's biggest backer.

It's already clear that Jiang Tian's forgiveness has made Zhou Zhao deeply grateful.

"I didn't expect you to actually forgive him." Gao Xiaolong's eyes showed even greater admiration.

His political thinking is correct, and his actions are almost flawless.

His abilities are terrifying, practically monstrous. No matter what criminal organization, Jiang Tian can almost always handle it.

And this kind of genius also possesses this kind of insight.

How could Gao Xiaolong not be overjoyed?

Excellent, excellent! The organization is lacking talent like this.

"Chief Gao, you're mistaken. The Secretary didn't do anything wrong. There are many things the Secretary doesn't know. Even if he did know, the Secretary must have had his reasons for doing what he did. It must be Jing Feifan who distorted the facts and deliberately misinterpreted many of the Secretary's orders, causing such a huge misunderstanding."

Jiang Tian, ​​in one breath, even arranged almost everything for Zhou Zhao's future.

This directly cleared Zhou Zhao's name, leaving Jing Feifan to take responsibility for all the problems.

Breaking it off only creates more enemies, while backing down gains more friends and even a powerful leader as a backer.

This is inherently a compromise.

"Xiao Jiang, you did a great job. I'm increasingly confident in your mission." Gao Xiaolong stepped forward, patting Jiang Tian on the shoulder. "Those bastards next door don't believe us. When you get there, don't hold back. Make as much of a scene as you can."

At this point, Gao Xiaolong seemed to be gritting his teeth in anger.



Chapter 260 Thunder Shatters the Darkness, the Whole Army Moves into Action


Jiang Tian nodded naturally.

Around 7 PM, all the personnel had gradually arrived.

However,

Jiang Tian glanced at them; many of them possessed a very fierce demeanor.

“Some of them are elite armed police from Kyoto, and others are elite soldiers from the military—all highly skilled. The higher-ups are truly angry this time,” Fang Ming, experienced and knowledgeable, explained to Jiang Tian, ​​having remained on the scene.

Jiang Tian nodded thoughtfully.

“There’s another matter. The relationships between Chu Jing, Lü Chun, and Song Qiuwan have all been officially transferred to your anti-drug squad. From now on, they will work directly under your anti-drug squad, secretly as your captain, and publicly as their captain,” Fang Ming continued.

Jiang Tian couldn’t help but applaud this efficiency.

To be honest,

Song Qiuwan had tried to speak to Jiang Tian several times after her return, but he had ignored or refused to speak to her.

Because it was unnecessary.

Song Qiuwan was Jiang Tian's white moonlight in his past life, and also a deep regret in his heart. Saving her was a relief.

After that mission across the Atlantic,

a huge rift finally appeared in the relationship between Jiang Tian and Song Qiuwan.

"By the way, where's Du Qing?" Jiang Tian suddenly asked.

"Du Qing has been temporarily suspended. Once she's thought things through, she'll be demoted when she returns to the unit, and then she'll be working directly under you," Fang Ming explained.

The problem last time was too serious. Compared to this time, although the consequences of making a mess are somewhat less severe, the harm is still far more complex than it appears on the surface.

In the pitch-black night,

Jiang Tian wasn't standing in the ranks under the red flag, but rather at the back of the reviewing stand with many other leaders.

Song Liguo spoke at the very front.

Following him, a group of leaders gave speeches. This might seem like a waste of time and effort, but it was actually quite something.

For example, right after Song Liguo finished speaking, Gao Xiaolong, standing on the stage, directly said: "Our neighbors look down on us. Because of this incident, they even withdrew from the joint operation. They even told headquarters directly that cooperating with you would make our mission several times more difficult. We initially had some expectations for you, but your performance has been utterly disappointing. This joint operation has barely begun, and there hasn't even been any progress. Your first task is to rescue a few hundred useless people from countless armed groups. We don't have the time or energy to play rescue games with you."

Silence.

Everyone under the red flag was solemn.

But without a doubt.

A tremendous anger rose on everyone's faces.

Yes, they had lost face this time, choosing the wrong person to lead.

They had lost so much face in their very first joint operation.

But this blatant contempt was contempt for everyone.

Gao Xiaolong took a deep breath and said, "This is no longer a simple operation. The leaders of Kyoto are all paying close attention to this operation. This is a landmark counter-offensive. The start has been unfavorable. Are you confident that you can make our motherland proud and the people cheer?"

"Yes, yes, yes!!!"

The deafening roar of the response was like a tsunami.

Morale plummeted instantly with just a few words from Gao Xiaolong.

Even Jiang Tian felt breathless, his eyes reddening, desperate to wipe out the criminal group.

“But don’t be too pessimistic. According to current intelligence, the supporting armed forces, along with the Yun family’s own armed forces, number about a thousand. While these thousand men outnumber us by a factor of two, we are all elites from various fields. Surely we can win! Remember, we have only one mission: to rescue our trapped comrades. Do you all understand?” Gao Xiaolong shouted.

Everyone roared that they understood.

“Move out.”

Song Liguo stepped forward and stood beside Gao Xiaolong. He didn’t say a word, but his resolute expression spoke volumes.

Finally, Song Liguo shouted “Move out!” and everyone quickly and orderly boarded the armored vehicles.

Red and green lights illuminated the darkness of the city police station.

“Jiang Tian, ​​you’re in my car.” Gao Xiaolong pulled Jiang Tian aside, pointing to the nine-seater vehicle at the front. The windows were dark, obscuring the interior.

However, the outside was clearly visible from inside.

Jiang Tian had now been transferred out of the First Brigade.

Previously, Jiang Tian belonged to the First Brigade, his direct superior being Brigade Commander Guo.

Later, after Jiang Tian was promoted to company commander, these matters were stalled due to a lack of suitable subordinates.

But then, after being suspended, Jiang Tian was urgently reinstated.

Fang Ming and Gao Xiaolong arranged things very quickly, though they didn’t know how.

Within a few hours, the identities of Chu Jing and Lü Chun were all arranged.

Not only that.

After Jiang Tian’s reinstatement, these people naturally fell under his command. Considering that many tasks wouldn’t be suitable for Jiang Tian remaining in the brigade, Gao Xiaolong directly transferred Jiang Tian’s organizational affiliation to the position of leader of a special emergency response team.

Actually, being called a team leader was essentially the same as being a squad leader, just no longer operating under the First Brigade. He communicated directly with Squad Leader Song Liguo for easier handling and command, and also made things easier for Jiang Tian.

After boarding the bus, Lü Chun shoved the driver off the bus. Then, Lü Chun sneered, "What trash? You dare to compete with me for Captain Jiang's driver position? You're not worthy!"

The driver was furious.

This guy opened the door and yanked him off. He'd be happier if someone did his work for him.

But you can't be so rough!

And to make matters worse, after pulling him off, you uttered such vulgar words.

Lü Chun completed the whole thing smoothly, even leaping off the bus quickly, and then, before Jiang Tian could reach out, he opened the side door of the nine-seat bus.

Then, with a smile, he said, "Captain Jiang, please come in slowly, be careful not to bump your head."

"Damn it, lackey."

The driver cursed as he left.

Fine, I won't go then.

"What's so great about that?"

Song Liguo glanced at Jiang Tian in astonishment. Jiang Tian's eyelids twitched, and he got into the car with a dark expression.

Following him were Jiang Tian's team members, Song Qiuwan, Chu Jing, and finally, Old Chen from the armed police.

After getting into the vehicle, Armed Police Officer Chen glanced at the driver, Lü Chun, who was driving diligently. He hesitated, then looked out at the woods and finally sighed, "I hope the mission goes smoothly."

Gao Xiaolong's calculation was actually flawed. While it seemed they had the advantage, timing, location, and personnel were paramount. Without these, it was like an endless expanse of water. Warfare is all about logistics. Even with a small force of a few hundred, they had geographical and personnel resource advantages, but these officers lacked those!



Chapter 261 The neighbors want to show them what a real warrior is.


The border...

Just as Jiang Tian and Song Liguo's group set off.

In fact, a large group of fully armed men had already gathered at the border. They were all dressed in uniform dark green and were completely armed, standing in the mountains, seemingly waiting for something.

"Director Mara, won't our presence here with so many people cause them some misunderstanding?"

The two leaders were clearly distinguished by their different attire, standing out from the others, clearly of leadership.

The speaker was a woman, somewhat imposing, with a worried look on her face as she gazed into the distance.

The man called Chief Mara stood at the front, hands behind his back. Hearing this, a cold smile played on his lips before he spoke, "So what if it's a misunderstanding? To be honest, I simply don't understand what the higher-ups were thinking. Why did they approve their joint operation in the first place? This operation, besides being laughable, serves no purpose whatsoever."

"Fortunately, they exposed their true capabilities from the start, letting our leaders see their weakness. Withdrawing from the operation was the most correct choice."

As Mara spoke, he shook his head with a mocking expression, seemingly genuinely regretting it.

No, it should be said that the disappointment on his face was almost completely undisguised.

“Tana, although I know those criminal groups are a cancer, you have to understand, they’ve already become a force to be reckoned with, and they’re deeply intertwined with the local residents. As the saying goes, ‘Pulling one hair affects the whole body.’ If they succeed, that’s fine, but if they fail, the resulting consequences will be far more terrifying and horrific. Neither we nor our superiors can bear the consequences. We gave them a chance, but their performance was laughable. It seems that what happened before was just a coincidence.”

Mara explained to her female subordinate behind her.

As a righteous Burmese police officer, didn’t Mara want to eradicate evil? Good and evil are irreconcilable. When Mara first became a police officer, she too was filled with a sense of justice and fervent passion.

But after truly becoming a police officer, she only saw the tip of the iceberg of those criminal groups.

Ordinary people see them as frogs in a well, but after becoming a Burmese police officer, she sees them as insignificant.

It seems exaggerated, but it’s not exaggerated at all.

Deputy Director Tana looked at Mara, her beautiful face momentarily stunned, before her eyes filled with desolation.

"Tana, don't be sad. You shouldn't have held onto hope from the beginning. If they were truly capable, how could they have been helpless against so many criminal groups for so many years, even nurturing them and allowing our criminal organizations to grow stronger? I never trusted them from the start, and the result is predictably laughable," Mara comforted her.

"So, we'll wait patiently for our opportunity, our chance for revenge, but definitely not now."

Mara spoke with a tone full of emotion.

As for the Third Special District, the territory bordering Yunnan Province, this area was under Mara's jurisdiction, as he was the director of the Third Special District.

Mara knew the situation next door all too well.

As for Tana's feelings behind him, Mara understood as well; as his capable assistant and the successor he had carefully groomed.

Her abilities were absolutely satisfactory to Mara. She was so young, not even thirty, yet she had already been promoted to Deputy Director of the Third Special District. And this wasn't based on her looks; she was promoted through genuine ability, solving countless cases and accumulating merit.

However, she was still too young, and didn't understand many things. Of course, he could understand that.

Tana sighed softly.

Mara turned away without saying anything. He understood Tana's past. Her parents were members of a criminal organization, committing numerous crimes, and were eventually arrested and executed.

Later, a kind policeman took pity on her and took her in when she was starving. However, that policeman was later retaliated against by the criminal organization and tortured to death.

From that moment on, Tana developed an extraordinary obsession with criminals.

Hearing about the joint operation, finally targeting the powerful family, heaven knows how excited Tana was; she'd been waiting for this day for ages.

But in the end, she was met with a devastating blow.

If those people next door had been even slightly more responsible, things wouldn't have turned out this way.

The higher-ups were already deeply concerned about cracking down on criminal groups.

They even feared even more serious consequences, and only reluctantly agreed out of consideration for the neighbors, seeing their recent achievements and choosing to take a gamble.

And who could have predicted this would happen?

Therefore, the higher-ups didn't hesitate almost at all.

They immediately canceled the operation.

Furthermore, some leaders had already made contact with the criminal group. On the other hand, they were also there to guard against the neighboring group carrying heavy weapons or to prevent too many people from arriving.

Fortunately, after receiving the mission, they rushed to the border from the rendezvous point.

"They've arrived."

Finally.

In the pitch-black night.

A large convoy of vehicles gradually drove onto the road at the border crossing.

Dense jungle lined both sides of the road.

There was a wide road connecting the border crossing, very wide, and besides that.

On both sides of the road, there were armed police officers from the People's Liberation Army with police dogs on duty.

Because this was one of the most important and serious crime scenes.

In the past, before Jiang Tian's rebirth.

This road, this place, accounted for almost 60-70% of all drug trafficking cases in Yunhai.

Almost all drug trafficking cases were discovered from this road.

Mara, leading nearly five hundred fully armed police officers, stood imposingly at attention in the middle of the road belonging to their territory in Myanmar.

Before him stood a fence.

This fence marked the border between their territories.

One step forward, they cross the border; one step back, they enter.

"Let these people see our might, let's teach them what police are like."

Mara set an example by standing at attention, completely still.

First impressions mattered.

Having learned from past experiences, although the arrival of the police cars was somewhat intimidating—dozens of vehicles lined up along one street—

they subconsciously believed, based on what had happened before, that this was a paper tiger.



Chapter 262 Song Liguo was humiliated and enraged.


Meanwhile.

Inside the lead official vehicle, Song Liguo, aided by the headlights and the bright streetlights, could clearly see the situation on the opposite side of the road.

While on the road, seemingly concerned that Jiang Tian, ​​who had only recently joined the police force, might be caught off guard, he gave him some basic training, such as how rampant drug trafficking was on this road.

"Drugs could be hidden inside the chassis or even in the tires, so every vehicle on this main road could potentially be carrying drug traffickers," Song Liguo explained.

Jiang Tian wasn't surprised. Having lived both his past and present lives, especially his previous life where he had even worked in the archives department, he was intimately familiar with these matters.

In fact, Jiang Tian knew even better.

What was uncovered was only a small part of the operation, because many fugitives remained deeply hidden, such as those using human transport.

And for various forms of transportation, police dogs weren't always 100% effective. In this era of limited technology, manual investigation still relied heavily on police dogs.

Therefore, this road was of paramount importance.

The profits are so high that many people are willing to risk their lives.

As for country roads and mountain trails, they are countless; criminals there are truly capable of anything.

"You can come over for a stroll more often when you have free time; after all, your nose is sharper than a police dog's," Song Liguo joked, trying to lighten the mood.

The atmosphere inside the carriage was excessively tense, preparing for the fierce battle to come.

"Uncle Song is treating me like a dog," Jiang Tian said, relaxing.

To be honest, this kind of operation wasn't stressful for Jiang Tian at all. He'd experienced being isolated and helpless before; this was nothing new.

As they spoke, the vehicle pulled up behind the fence.

Song Liguo also saw a dense mass of figures standing on the other side of the fence, all standing at attention under the guidance of two men.

"It's the Burmese police," Song Liguo said, somewhat surprised.

He even wondered if these people had changed their minds.

Click!

Song Liguo opened the car door and got out first, followed by Jiang Tian.

Then, Jiang Tian followed behind Song Liguo, along with Uncle Chen, the armed police officer who hadn't spoken until now.

As they got out of the car, the armed personnel in the dozens of police cars behind them also got out, but instead of going anywhere, they stood guard on the sidings.

"Have their identities been verified?" Song Liguo didn't approach immediately but asked the armed police officer on guard.

Upon hearing this, the armed police leader on duty saluted and hurriedly replied, "Don't worry, sir, their identities have all been verified. It's Mara, the director of the Third Special Zone, along with Deputy Director Tana and their operatives. Their identities are flawless."

"Good." Song Liguo's face lit up with joy.

Could these people have really changed their minds?

"Xiao Jiang, it seems the intelligence from headquarters was somewhat flawed. These people are afraid we'll be like the previous ones; they're probably here to pick us up and operate together. With their support and joint operations, I'm over 90% confident of success in this operation," Song Liguo said to Jiang Tian with delight.

However...

Jiang Tian's face remained expressionless, which Song Liguo didn't notice, but Jiang Tian's reasoning was enhanced by his understanding of micro-expressions and psychology.

Judging from these people's micro-expressions, they didn't seem to be there to cooperate.

Instead, they seemed more like...

However, Jiang Tian had no evidence. While he was pondering this, Song Liguo had already rushed forward.

Old Chen followed, waving to Jiang Tian, ​​"Come on, if we cooperate this time, we'll be virtually guaranteed success."

Jiang Tian followed behind.

But just as the two of them made their move, Song Liguo had already jogged to the Third Special Zone Chief on the opposite bank.

"You've come to cooperate? That's great! Eradicating criminal groups is everyone's responsibility. Thank you for understanding and assisting us." Song Liguo greeted them with a broad smile, seemingly wanting to shake hands with Mara.

Enthusiasm ran through Song Liguo, and his excitement was palpable. Operating in a foreign land, the difference between having local support and not having it made a world of difference in combat effectiveness.

But then, a moment later...

*Slap!*

Mara raised her hand and slapped Song Liguo's outstretched hand away.

Then, Mara spoke with a completely expressionless face: "I never shake hands with trash, because it might infect me with trashiness."

*Buzz!*

Song Liguo's head exploded.

He stared at Mara in disbelief. He couldn't believe that this burly, even seemingly honest and simple-minded director of the Third Special District could say something like that.

"Are you really unhappy about this?" Mara's face was full of mockery. "Or do you disagree with the idea of ​​'useless'? But unfortunately, your incompetence is undeniable. You had a very good chance to wipe out the criminal group, but your stupidity has led to this situation. Now you can't even save your own teammates."

Song Liguo's expression gradually changed from furious to angry.

Even Jiang Tian and Old Chen, who were following behind, exchanged a glance. The anger that had risen on their faces suddenly vanished, leaving them speechless.

Because, it seemed, they also wanted to wipe out those criminal groups.

It was just that the stupidity of the team leader had squandered a perfect opportunity.

“Perhaps you’ve misunderstood. We’re not here to support you. We’ve already contacted your headquarters. Our leaders are very worried that your presence will not only fail to strike at our criminal group, but will actually make things more difficult for us, hindering our operations. Therefore, we have cancelled all cooperation with you, and we will not take any action. You are only allowed to bring a maximum of five hundred people into the country; not a single person more.”

Mara spoke at a unhurried pace, using language that both sides could understand.

Damn it, Song Liguo couldn’t help but get angry.

Actually, due to various reasons, many local languages ​​in Myanmar are completely mutually intelligible with those in China, and communication between the two sides is virtually barrier-free.

Everyone could understand Mara’s words because she was speaking the language of her homeland.

In truth, they'd rather not understand.

At least Song Liguo was seething with resentment, wondering why the other side spoke their native language so fluently; otherwise, he wouldn't be so angry and furious.

"I think you have many misunderstandings about us," Song Liguo said, forcing himself to remain calm while suppressing his anger.

Insults were acceptable.

But insulting their hometown crossed their bottom line.

You could curse the team leader, call Li Qishan a piece of trash, a fool deserving of a thousand cuts, but you couldn't use him to represent everyone.



Chapter 263 Are you crazy? You're saying he's old, weak, sick, or disabled?


"It's a misunderstanding. You're overthinking it. There's no misunderstanding. Thanks to your help, our previously stable situation has been completely thrown into chaos, making our work much more difficult. We don't have time to play around with you now," Mara said businesslike, her words harsh and clearly contemptuous.

Tana, standing behind her, quickly stepped forward and spoke first: "It's very dangerous inside now. Many criminal groups are lurking there. More than ten of our teammates were killed on our way here. If you try to rescue them with this small force, I think it's impossible. You should leave."

As members of the same side, both on the side of justice, Tana couldn't bear to see them all die here.

Part of the reason was also the orders from their superiors.

After all, too many casualties would be difficult to explain to those next door.

Because it was indeed extremely dangerous inside.

Compared to when Li Qishan first entered, alerting the enemy was ten times more dangerous.

Those criminal groups seemed to have smelled something. Seeing their own vulnerability, they were almost all desperate to launch a counterattack as quickly as possible.

And to annihilate all those surrounded.

Because they all knew that if this cooperation went smoothly for both sides, and the Yun family so easily vanished into history, even the Yun family's enemies wouldn't stand for it.

Next, no one could be sure they wouldn't be next.

This was also one of the reasons why the Burmese side was frightened by their reaction and dared not continue.

"Even if you go, it's just suicide. That's all I have to say," Mara advised.

"Thank you for your advice, but we don't have the habit of abandoning our teammates. Even if there's only one person surrounded, we'll rescue them without hesitation," Song Liguo said directly.

"Then I must say, there are people waiting to ambush you ahead?" Tana said, "When we came here, we fought a hard battle to break through, and even then, we lost more than ten teammates. Those people are probably still lying in ambush there. You won't be able to get through."

An ambush?

Song Liguo's gaze froze.

Old Chen turned to look at Jiang Tian, ​​whose brows furrowed slightly, seemingly surprised by the situation.

“Then we’ll give it a try.”

As he spoke, Song Liguo waved his hand, and everyone got into the car.

The police cars, flashing red and blue lights, sped off towards Myanmar.

Song Liguo stood by the roadside, waiting for the vehicles to pass, until all the police cars had passed the raised barriers.

Only then did Song Liguo get into the car.

Old Chen remained silent. Actually, this wasn't the first time Jiang Tian had met Old Chen; the man was taciturn, and he was used to it.

In his previous life.

Jiang Tian's family suffered a tragedy.

Countless people took advantage of their misfortune, far too many, and among them, very few genuinely offered help. Besides Uncle Song, only this Uncle Chen stood before him.

They genuinely went to great lengths to help, pulling strings and asking around, even though ultimately it had absolutely no effect. Jiang Tian appreciated their efforts.

Jiang Tian even knew about it.

Because of this incident, Old Chen offended a certain leader. As a result, before Jiang Tian's transmigration, Old Chen was forced to retire in his fifties. It's impossible to say he wasn't involved in that matter, so having the chance after his rebirth, Jiang Tian actually wanted to help Old Chen.

With these thoughts in mind, Jiang Tian was pulled by Old Chen to get into the car.

Song Liguo was also the last to get in.

As the last official vehicle left the fence and drove into the dark Myanmar,

"Chief, are we really just going to watch?" Tana asked nervously, watching the convoy pass by.

She knew all too well the level of those criminal groups inside. Even the most disciplined regular army would face immense pressure from those ruthless local criminal gangs.

Tana couldn't bear to think about them going in.

“The old, weak, sick, and disabled have all come up here, and they still won’t listen to advice. They deserve to die. Anyway, our mission is complete. Even if they are all wiped out, it has nothing to do with us. As long as we have an explanation.” Mara noticed the bandage wrapped around Jiang Tian’s neck. Mara immediately suspected that the neighbors couldn’t find enough people, so they even brought in the old, weak, sick, and disabled.

The more she thought about it, the more Mara almost laughed in anger.

How could these people possibly rescue the person trapped?

She kindly advised them, but they insisted on going to their deaths.

It was utterly foolish.

If it were really that easy to solve, would those criminal groups still exist?

Would his Third Special Zone allow these criminal groups to run rampant?

Suddenly…

Bang bang bang!!!

Bang bang!!!

Just then.

A burst of intense gunfire suddenly came from the direction the convoy had just left.

The intense gunfire was almost continuous.

Even the experienced Mara could tell from the gunfire that the fighting was fierce.

"It seems the criminal group couldn't hold back. But these guys were lucky; they didn't wait for them to get in before ambushing them from behind. Otherwise, they would have suffered the same fate as the previous group, just needless casualties." Mara shook her head regretfully.

"Chief, let's go take a look," Tana couldn't help but say.

She simply couldn't stand idly by in the face of the criminal group.

"Go? What are you talking about? They'll probably be back in a sorry state soon." Mara refused.

Besides, if they went,

it might lead to even more serious consequences.

"There are probably close to one or two hundred people lying in ambush, all hidden in the shadows. And who knows how many reinforcements they have behind them. If they retreat again, they'll probably still have a considerable chance." Mara turned and looked at the end of the road.

Not far away, the sounds of gunfire were almost continuous; for a moment, it was impossible to tell how many guns were firing simultaneously, or how intense the fighting was.

However,

In Mara's imagination, the enemy should have quickly retreated in disarray.

They only needed to wait here, and if the criminal group dared to pursue them, they would then make their move.

But now,

the gunfire was growing increasingly intense.

This was completely unexpected.

“Chief, should we go take a look?” Tana tentatively suggested.

“Take a look, sure.” Mara frowned, then nodded slightly.

Fine, let them live a few more lives. When they make their move, it will enrage the criminal groups, and they can say they were worried about too many casualties and how to explain it to the neighbors. They believe the criminal groups will understand.

Then, they quickly led their men forward.

But halfway there, the booming gunfire gradually subsided, replaced by occasional gunshots, but far fewer than before.

“Less than ten minutes, and they’ve already collapsed? What a bunch of useless trash.”



Chapter 264 No, what right do you good-for-nothings have?


“Chief, they probably arrived in a hurry. After all, some of them were even wounded. Those who lay in ambush would be difficult for even us to defeat. They're definitely the elite of the criminal group. It's quite impressive that they've held out for so long, considering that the previous group collapsed the moment they made contact.”

Tana followed behind the chief. If possible, Tana genuinely wanted to wipe out this group of criminals, or rather, eradicate all evil forces.

Although she lacked the ability, they had good intentions.

In a way, she was helping them solve their problem.

“Tana, don't defend those useless people. Useless is useless. Even the old, weak, and disabled were dragged in to make up the numbers. Do you really think they value the operation? No, I think it's just for show. This kind of behavior will only embolden those criminals. I don't think they've ever taken us seriously.” Mara spoke coldly, a hint of a sneer in her voice.

Not taking them seriously.

This was blatant disregard for them.

Mara led her men to provide support.

He was just in a terrible mood.

Don't let Mara's sarcasm fool you; he, too, once harbored a strong sense of justice and a desire to fight crime—a sentiment shared by everyone new to the police force.

But as time went on, and he truly understood the terror of those criminal gangs.

The power of one move can have far-reaching consequences; it's no exaggeration.

That's why he was excited and thrilled when the opposing team launched their joint operation, expecting to achieve great things.

But what arrived was a bunch of scoundrels.

Or rather, the other side never took them seriously from the start.

The second wave of reinforcements even included the elderly, the infirm, and the disabled.

When Jiang got out of the car, Mara was utterly devastated.

That rope around his neck, his arms bound with gauze—you want someone like that to fight those criminals?

Isn't this a joke?

He probably couldn't even get past the first hurdle, the ambush set up by the criminal gang.

The Third Special Zone is now in chaos, utter chaos.

To put it bluntly, it's practically a complete mess.

When the criminal gangs from various regions heard that the Yun family was about to be besieged, they rushed over like madmen.

The balance that Mara had painstakingly maintained, at least superficially, has been completely shattered. Armed members of those criminal gangs are everywhere.

Even the regular troops who crossed the Third Special Zone to the border suffered more than ten casualties.

And those heading towards the Yun family's location faced relentless pursuit.

Bang! Bang! Bang!!!

In the silent night sky.

Gunshots rang out intermittently.

However, they were far less intense than before, looking more like executions being carried out one by one.

"This is probably just those criminal gangs playing a trick on us," Mara thought, her mood utterly terrible.

If possible, he truly wanted to have nothing more to do with these people.

But now.

As their vehicle moved forward.

The road gradually became rugged from a wide highway.

Although China only began developing in the early 2000s, it had already demonstrated its infrastructure prowess, with well-developed infrastructure across the country.

Myanmar, however, was completely different.

For example, the paved road only lasted about three miles before turning into a muddy mess.

Vehicles struggled to navigate, and large puddles frequently appeared on the road.

"The criminal gang's ambush point is probably less than a mile ahead. Hurry up, don't let them get wiped out. It'll be a mess later. This is really troublesome. I don't understand why they would send such old, weak, and disabled people to cause us trouble if they don't care," Mara said, her temper flaring.

Tana, sitting next to Mara, stared intently at the roadside, seemingly scrutinizing something.

Mara glanced at her, her agitation gradually subsiding. "Tana, what are you looking at?" she asked.

"I'm looking. If they've already been defeated, why haven't we seen anyone fleeing?" Tana asked warily.

Hmm?

Yes!

If it were a true rout, surely a few out of hundreds would have desperately fled on the roads.

But what happened?

Why hasn't a single one appeared?

"Don't panic. Maybe these old, weak, and disabled cowards weren't on guard at all. These criminal gangs wanted to wipe them out, so they ambushed them from behind, catching them off guard. Maybe none of them could have made it this far," Mara thought of a plausible explanation.

But was this explanation really plausible?

A faint hope flickered in Tana's heart.

Mara, leading all the officers from the Third Special District's police force, cautiously stopped their car a mile or two before their ambush location, then led them forward on foot with trepidation.

"These old, weak, and disabled people don't even know basic concealment," Mara muttered as she walked.

But no one answered.

Because at that moment, they finally saw figures running rapidly before them.

"I told you, it's a miracle these old, weak, and disabled people have held out this long," Mara sneered.

He straightened his collar, preparing to thoroughly humiliate his opponent.

But the next moment, Tana stopped him: "Chief, something's wrong. Those people's clothes aren't right."

"Wrong?"

Mara paused, then widened her eyes.

Because they were advancing in the dark, they hadn't considered encountering enemies, assuming they were just the defeated elderly and infirm from next door. They were completely unprepared, and the other side seemed oblivious to them either.

When they were two or three meters apart, they stared at each other.

*Snap!*

Mara switched on her flashlight, shining it clearly on the other side.

"Damn it, be on alert!" Mara's voice was hoarse.

It was almost a sharp, anxious cry.

As Mara's voice rang out,

the people behind her hurriedly raised their guns.

However, the enemy in front of him cried out in panic the next moment: "Don't shoot! There are ghosts behind us! There are devils! They are devils! Don't shoot! I surrender! Don't kill me!"

The man spoke with extreme panic, his voice trembling and pleading.

This stunned everyone present.

But before they could react,

Bang!!!

The next moment.

A gunshot rang out.

The piercing gunshot startled them again.

Mara shone her flashlight into the distance, revealing three figures standing silently before them.

The one in the lead was expressionless, watching silently.

His arm was still strapped to his neck in a sling.

Behind him stood a man and a woman.

Chu Jing and Lü Chun.



Chapter 265 Kneel down, or die.


They were all in the first vehicle, having gotten off with Song Liguo, so Mara recognized almost everyone.

The most familiar face was Jiang Tian, ​​the one bound with zip ties, whom Mara had described as old, weak, and disabled.

That's right.

The three people present were Jiang Tian.

Nearly two hundred men.

Jiang Tian's first battle was against nearly two hundred drug dealers alone; imagine five hundred armed men!

To be honest, Jiang Tian was dumbfounded.

When the scanning radar was activated, he was startled, thinking a large army was waiting.

But when he looked up, he saw only two hundred men—a mere two hundred armed men ambushing their regular army. Were these people arrogant, or was the world too crazy? How dare they do this?

Even if Jiang Tian didn't attack, being caught off guard by the ambush would be a close match.

But with Jiang Tian attacking, and given the prior precautions, the fight was a complete rout, a simple and effortless crushing victory.

The ease with which they fought surprised even Jiang Tian.

Especially their performance. This was Jiang Tian's first direct observation of the two, and he hadn't held their abilities in high regard.

However, in this battle, although the criminal group they faced wasn't particularly strong—at least by Jiang Tian's standards, and even by ordinary standards—they were considered elite.

The two, in terms of both professionalism and speed of elimination,

were incredibly quick and decisive.

Their shots were almost always headshots.

Of course, in Jiang Tian's eyes, they were nothing but easy prey, easily killed. However, within the anti-drug squad or the armed police, they were absolutely top-tier.

They were practically at the very top level among ordinary people.

It must be said that there was a reason they were able to enter that department; if their true abilities were truly as they had portrayed, they wouldn't have been able to get into such a department.

Ten minutes.

The firefight lasted for about ten minutes.

Jiang Tian killed approximately sixty people, not only those accompanying him, but also the armed police and military units behind him – they were no joke.

They were enraged.

This time, the higher-ups were truly enraged.

If the initial extermination of the Yun family was merely a probing move to cooperate with Myanmar, then this operation was a true thunderbolt.

With such a high level of attention and scrutiny in Kyoto, who wouldn't be going crazy, charging forward with reckless abandon?

To these possibly somewhat trained ragtag soldiers, it was nothing short of a catastrophic defeat.

"No matter how many times I watch, I still can't believe it. How does Team Leader Jiang manage to shoot so fast and so accurately?" Lü Chun's lips twitched as he sheathed his pistol.

In his former unit, he was practically an outstanding individual, his marksmanship exceptionally formidable and terrifying.

But compared to Jiang Tian's firing speed, he felt that the moment his hand touched the butt of the gun, Jiang Tian had already completed the firing motion.

Fast, so fast it's practically inhuman.

"Watch more, practice more, learn more." Jiang Tian expressionlessly sheathed his pistol.

What a joke, trying to steal his kills? That's ridiculous!

"How many people has the team leader killed?" Chu Jing asked, her heart pounding.

"The seventy-eighth," Jiang Tian replied calmly.

After speaking,

Jiang Tian seemed oblivious to Mara and his men, turning to leave with them.

However, Jiang Tian's voice, clear as night, reached Mara's ears.

"Stop!"

Just then, Mara suddenly stepped forward and shouted at them.

Jiang Tian and his men didn't falter.

What a joke.

Who do you think you are, stopping just because I tell you to?

"If you move again, I'll shoot."

The next moment.

Mara drew her pistol, and with a clang, after Mara's shout, the five hundred officers behind her simultaneously drew their guns and pointed them at Jiang Tian.

This caused Jiang Tian to pause slightly.

What does this mean?

Jiang Tian didn't know.

"To be honest, if you were the enemy, you'd already be a corpse the moment you drew your gun."

Jiang Tian stopped, then turned around calmly to look at Mara. Despite being pointed at by so many guns, his expression remained indifferent.

"A corpse, hahaha..."

Mara scoffed, "Kid, you sacrificed so many men, went through so much trouble to finally drive away a criminal, only to have him run right up to us and get killed. Don't I think you can't see it? I'm not wrong, you've already been routed. There aren't many survivors left behind. If you hope to use this deceptive method to cover up your inner emptiness and maintain your pathetic pride, then I can only tell you that your actions serve no purpose other than to make us laugh."

Mara had already mentally rehearsed the entire scenario.

Mara's words truly stunned Jiang Tian.

Are these people crazy?

"Kid, beg us now, and we might be able to help you. But if you try to get our help through deception, well, kid, you'll ruin your old, weak, and useless teammates. That kind of behavior disgusts me. So, do you choose to kneel down and beg for my forgiveness, or do you choose to turn around and leave with a straight face? The price is, we won't lay a hand on you again," Mara said coldly.

He had originally intended to attack, but now he changed his mind.

"Chief," Tana pleaded from the side.

As Mara spoke, he suddenly became furious because Jiang Tian and his men had left without a second thought.

A faint, sick voice echoed in the air.

What he hated most in his life were people who lacked ability but loved to show off.

He believed this was the root of all evil.

If it weren't for the potential for conflict, Jiang Tian would have already attacked when they raised their guns.

What? You say they have a lot of people?

Sorry, Jiang Tian's side also has quite a few people.



Chapter 266 Total Annihilation, But the Dead Were Criminals


Mara's face flushed red and then turned pale. He never dreamed that Jiang Tian would dare to show him no respect whatsoever; it was practically suicidal.

Burning with rage, Mara was about to chase after him when his phone rang. He took it out and saw it was his superior, the head of the Third Special District.

Mara answered the phone. When the other party inquired about the situation, Mara first mentioned the other side's lack of respect, sending only the elderly, weak, and disabled. Then, she angrily added, "When we tried to rescue them, an extremely rude and uncooperative disabled person appeared. He was incredibly arrogant and refused to cooperate. His hand was bandaged, and he even watched his teammates die without showing any remorse. They even sent someone like that; it's an insult to us!"

Their repeated failures have the biggest and worst impact on us. The previously balanced power structure will be ruined because of them; all the criminal groups will see their reputations skyrocket. This is simply harming us!"

The head of the Third Special District was furious.

He stood up from the sofa, quickly took out his phone, and dialed the number next door.

The person responsible for contacting him was Minister Tan.

Then, the head of the Third Special Region, speaking angrily and furiously into the phone, rebuked Meng Lu, the head of the Third Special Region, for issuing the order to cancel the mission earlier that day.

However, in the afternoon, Minister Tan patiently and repeatedly assured him that the personnel dispatched were the strongest warriors, and that they would not be sloppy. These were formidable fighters; with them on the job, success was guaranteed.

Therefore, Meng Lu tentatively wanted to observe the situation before replying to Minister Tan, and thankfully, he did not agree.

The situation in Myanmar is different from that in China.

The situation in Myanmar is extremely unique, and the power dynamics are incredibly complex. For example, as the head of the Third Special Region, Meng Lu actually possesses terrifying autonomy. Whether or not to cooperate is not a decision he can make through higher authorities; he alone cannot make such decisions.

Meng Lu has always maintained a relatively good balance with the criminal groups.

But recently, the criminal groups have been rapidly expanding their power through neighboring regions, to the point that Meng Lu feels a significant threat. However, taking action on his own is impossible, as it would be tantamount to making enemies of all criminal groups.

No one wanted to allow any force to wipe out any of their factions, because they understood the principle of "if the lips are gone, the teeth will be cold" better than anyone. If this door was opened, they would never again have such a free and favorable environment; more and more criminal groups would be wiped out in one fell swoop. Therefore, they wouldn't allow it, fearing that one day such a thing would happen to them.

Thus, the fierce resistance from the criminal groups had, to some extent, broken the balance that Menglu had painstakingly built.

How could Menglu not be furious?

He had been given a chance; if they performed well this time, they could still cooperate and act together, even if they were a bit weak, they still represented their neighbor.

Unexpectedly, the other side sent out this kind of thing.

Wearing bandages and still doing such dangerous frontline work—isn't this just filling quotas?

Using this as a starting point, the rest are definitely not good.

After Meng Lu's angry questioning...

Minister Tan was slightly taken aback.

Because just a minute ago, he had received intelligence.

His mind went blank.

Mainly because, in less than twenty minutes, Song Liguo's team had wiped out over two hundred criminal groups, with only two minor injuries as casualties.

One of the minor injuries was from twisting his ankle after stepping into a pothole while running at night.

What a brilliant record!

It instantly contrasted sharply with Li Qishan's previous performance.

After the initial shock, Minister Tan was even angrier.

If Song Liguo had led the team earlier, would this situation have occurred? Of course, Jiang Tian's casualties are even more terrifying; his KDA has already reached four or five hundred.

Perhaps, if Comrade Jiang had been involved in the last operation, this wouldn't have happened.

But it's too late for regrets now; everything must move forward.

Minister Tan had just finished his initial surprise when he received a call from General Meng Lu, his contact.

The content of the call stunned Minister Tan.

"So, our men were all wiped out?"

Minister Tan's head was spinning.

"That's right. We did our best. Blame it on your old, weak, and disabled for courting death. Not only did you court death, but you also dragged us down. That man with the bandage on his hand—I suggest you execute him before we can discuss the next step of cooperation. Otherwise, all our cooperation ends here." Meng Lu spoke with fury.

Who knew...

Minister Tan's head was spinning.

Execute a recipient of the Special Merit Citation?

Execute Comrade Xiao Jiang, the most powerful, terrifying, and historic six-merit king since the founding of the nation.

Is this a joke?

And just as they were talking...

At the scene...

It was a completely different scene.

"Chief, there are a large number of corpses around the corner about two hundred meters ahead. Conservatively speaking, there are close to twenty."

Not long after Jiang Tian left, Chief Mara followed with her men.

By this time, all the gunfire had ceased.

Mara sent someone ahead to scout for any potential criminals.

Sure enough.

Tana spoke first.

That's just how she was; she always had a kind heart.

"Okay."

Those behind Tana swarmed forward.

Mara followed behind them.

But things didn't look right as they reached the corner.

They had reached the corner, the ground muddy.

"What's wrong?" Mara asked, following behind.

When he stood beside Tana, in the dim light of his flashlight, he finally saw clearly, completely.

Corpses lay scattered on the ground, blood mixed with mud.

The air was thick with a horrifying stench of blood.

More than twenty corpses.

Lying there silently.

The scene was extremely gruesome.

"Tell them to be careful, but they wouldn't listen. If you're so useless, don't come here to die. Now look what happened!" Mara cursed.

Huh?

At that sentence,

Mara froze instantly.

My head was spinning.

Because of the light, I could see bloodstains almost everywhere on the ground.

Besides the twenty-odd corpses,

a dozen meters away, along the roadside, in the middle of the road, and even in the bushes, lay corpses scattered haphazardly. Crucially, none of these corpses were wearing police uniforms; unsurprisingly, they were all dressed in brightly colored clothes.

“Chief, these people have been checked. It's confirmed they belong to the criminal group that ambushed us and caused us heavy losses. And everyone has been checked; almost all of them were shot in the head.”



Chapter 267 Shocking Changes Abroad, Situation Changing in an Instant


*Buzz!*

Mara's head was buzzing.

*Thump, thump, thump!*

He walked on the somewhat muddy road. Earlier in the day, a light drizzle had fallen, not so bad that it was impossible to walk, but the road, neglected for years, was full of potholes and mud, making walking quite difficult.

Mara, wearing boots, trudged through the mud, a flashlight in his hand.

As he walked, corpses appeared before him, all drug dealers or armed members of criminal organizations; not a single person was in police uniform.

Of course.

Most horrifying were the bloody holes in their foreheads.

The more Mara looked, the more horrified he became, the more cold sweat poured down his face.

*Thump!*

The moment the light shone on him, a criminal with a bloody hole between his eyebrows, his eyes wide open in death, seemed to be staring straight at him.

This scene didn't frighten Mara.

As the director of the Third Special District, he had seen such horrific scenes before; they weren't enough to scare him. Since taking office, there had been a large number of murders every day.

Society had almost never been stable.

However, these criminal forces didn't completely challenge the authorities' bottom line; they generally maintained a balance. For cases with too much impact, criminal groups would even voluntarily hand over some of their underlings to take the blame—this seemed to have become a consensus.

So what truly made him break out in a cold sweat was that all the bodies at the scene had been shot in the head.

Tana ran over and helped Mara up.

"I'm fine, I'm fine, haha, it's nothing, what could possibly happen to me?" Mara replied.

Fortunately, it was nighttime, and no one could see her expression. Besides, all the officers were staring in utter shock at the corpses at the scene.

The situation here was similar to Yunhai’s—no, it was countless times worse. At least Yunhai had order, but here, order was partly controlled by criminal gangs, who could even openly clash with the Special District.

In the past, they had been hesitant, forced to watch helplessly as criminals, filled with anger but unable to do anything, lay before them.

These Special District officers were almost all filled with excitement.

Because they seemed to see a glimmer of hope.

And indeed.

As they moved,

before their eyes lay corpses.

After a while.

Two hundred and thirty-four are dead, and over ninety percent of them were shot in the head, with some also suffering gunshot wounds to the back of the head. It's horrific!"

Hiss!!!

Mara gasped.

That's right, over two hundred people. That's what they investigated.

They were ambushed on the way here, and after the fighting started, they determined the number to be around two hundred.

In other words.

In less than twenty minutes.

This ambush team of over two hundred people was completely wiped out?

Especially since there was no delay at the scene.

Because just as they arrived and caught up, Jiang Tian had already gotten into the car and left with Song Liguo's convoy.

For them, there was now a most important mission.

The primary objective wasn't to capture these armed personnel or interrogate them for leads, as leads didn't require interrogation.

Before arriving, headquarters and the Third Special District had been in contact. After Li Qishan and his men were taken hostage and lured into a trap, personnel from the Third Special District police investigated, and Gao Yangyang, who had broken through the encirclement, relayed messages and intelligence from the perimeter. The situation on the ground was quite clear.

Upon learning that the surrounded men were in dire straits, Song Liguo didn't dare hesitate and hastily led his men away.

It happened almost instantaneously. Some of the bodies of those who had been shot in the head were still limp.

"Chief, they don't seem to be the elderly, weak, or disabled?" Tana swallowed hard.

She had never witnessed such a horrific battle in her life. Over two hundred people, almost ninety percent of them had been shot in the head, many even with the bullets aimed at the back of their heads. This meant that upon initial contact, these people frantically tried to escape but failed, and were caught and shot in the head from behind.

"My God!"

Tana couldn't even imagine the fighting power this was.

Mara froze upon hearing this.

Didn't he know these were the elderly, weak, and disabled?

Didn't he know how shocking this scene was?

But...

Mara thought back to what he had just said, and at that moment, each corpse felt like a slap in the face.

His face turned red.

But amidst the excitement, Mara also felt a glimmer of hope.

Could it be...

Could it be...

The Third Special District is saved?

Tana, still in a state of excitement, continued, "If these people are really that powerful, this operation might actually work."

Mara snapped out of his shock and the sting of his embarrassment.

"It's not that simple. This is just the beginning, an appetizer—a small group of people supporting the Yun family blocking the road. It's not that simple. However, their actions have indeed surprised me. They're already cooperating with us. Theoretically, wiping out the Yun family is now possible if they follow my orders and don't act recklessly."

Mara pondered for a moment, then a smile appeared on her face.

It was a look of hope.

While this genuinely shocked him, he wasn't optimistic about the future.

With two or three times their strength, he didn't believe they could sustain the advance alone.

But with him, it was different.

The more Mara thought about it, the more excited he became. It had been the same before; they wouldn't have canceled the joint operation if there was a chance, but they canceled because they saw no hope of victory. A fight between the two sides would be mutually destructive; they invited him to help, thinking there was hope, only to be dragged down.

To even want a joint operation, they had to rescue them first—wasn't this just helping those criminal groups make things more difficult for them?

This was also why they initially looked down on them.

But Mara had to admit, somewhat.

These people might have some ability.

If they all followed his command, victory wasn't impossible.

He decided to do it.

Mara immediately arranged his men, then quickly got into vehicles, determined to give chase.

At the same time, Mara took out her phone and called Monru, but before she could speak, Monru's voice came through: "This is Monru. Get those old, weak, sick, and disabled people out of our territory right now. We don't need their help, and I certainly don't want them rescuing those people now."

Buzz!!!

Mara was shocked: "Why?"



Chapter 268 Old Tan was shocked; it had to be Xiao Jiang again!


But he was unaware of this.

While he was on the phone, Minister Tan was communicating with Song Liguo in the car.

Not only that, the entire car fell silent, everyone listening to Minister Tan's voice coming through the receiver.

Of course, most importantly, they listened to what Minister Tan was saying.

“What exactly happened to you? Didn’t you win? Why are the leaders of the Third Special Zone negotiating with the other side and demanding you leave the country within a certain timeframe?” Minister Tan slammed his fist on the table, his voice shaking.

That’s right.

The news they just received was of a great victory!

Why did this sudden change occur?

“What?” Song Liguo jumped to his feet. “Giving in and begging for mercy from those criminal groups? Have they gone mad?”

Even Jiang Tian hadn’t expected the situation to change like this.

“Most importantly, Meng Lu said you’re about to be completely annihilated. I still don’t know what happened,” Minister Tan said through gritted teeth.

Retreat?

How could they possibly retreat?

What were they doing in here?

It was for revenge.

It was also to deter foreign criminal forces, not to enhance their reputation.

If we retreat today, what face will our motherland have left?

We'll lose face internationally; who will bear that responsibility?

"Tell me the truth, what exactly happened between you and that criminal group of over two hundred people? What was the battle like?" Minister Tan slammed his fist on the table.

Song Liguo reported that they had completely annihilated the enemy.

But Meng Lu confidently said that their forces had been routed in less than ten minutes?

Minister Tan's first thought was, had reinforcements arrived? Song Liguo had successfully wiped out the ambush of over two hundred people, only to find his own men wiped out?

Otherwise, why would Meng Lu be so confident?

Thinking this, Minister Tan's heart sank.

If the consequences weren't severe and devastating, Menglu wouldn't have issued him an order to leave, or even suggested negotiating with the criminal group.

Because the damage to the nation's prestige would be terrifying, but the damage to the Third Special Zone would be even more terrifying.

"We have completely wiped out that group, and are currently making normal progress," Song Liguo replied.

"What about casualties? How many of you were killed, and how many were killed?"

Minister Tan asked cautiously, rubbing his temples.

This matter was very important.

Perhaps the casualties were too high, and there was no hope of rescuing people or continuing the fight, so Menglu ordered him to take these people away. Otherwise, how could he explain such a hasty change of heart?

"Our casualties were zero, only two were lightly wounded, and we killed 235. The exact numbers may vary, as we were pressed for time and didn't have time for a second check," Song Liguo said, straightening his chest.

Thinking about the battle just now, Song Liguo was completely stunned.

Even though he had witnessed Jiang Tian's formidable fighting prowess during his first encounter with Tucha, the situation was complicated at the time; he was protecting Commander Gao and hadn't had time to observe further.

Today, when the fighting broke out, Song Liguo was completely stunned.

While they were still exchanging fire below, Jiang Tian had already flanked them from the side.

And then, in less than three minutes...

The enemy gunfire gradually subsided.

By the time they arrived, the enemy had already turned their guns on them.

They attacked from both sides.

In reality, it took less than ten minutes from the start of contact; the enemy had already collapsed.

They scattered and fled. The rest were easily dealt with; they were pursued and almost completely wiped out.

Of course, the enemy numbers were definitely more than 230; some would inevitably escape.

But it was certain that over 90% of them had been completely wiped out.

It was too easy.

Song Liguo felt like he was dreaming. They had so easily eliminated over 200 people?

This was an achievement!

He had never achieved such a glorious feat in his life.

"Minister Tan, in less than ten minutes, Comrade Jiang wiped out over seventy armed enemies, successfully flanking and encircling them from both sides. In less than twenty minutes, they were all annihilated." Song Liguo directly commended Jiang Tian.

His face was beaming.

"Good, well done! It has to be Jiang Tian!"

Bang!!!

Headquarters...

In the conference room, even in the pitch-black night, Minister Tan stood up, his face flushed with excitement, slamming his fist on the table.

Satisfying.

Just two words.

So satisfying.

How could it be so satisfying?

Heaven knows how big a mess Li Qishan made this time; it's the kind of embarrassment that could be felt internationally.

On a micro level, this incident has a huge boosting effect on the criminal forces in Yunhai and throughout Myanmar.

To put it bluntly, this operation almost completely wasted Jiang Tian's previous efforts.

So much hard work, and this is the result?

Who will respect them now?

From a macro perspective, this is a blow to the nationwide crackdown on organized crime.

To put it bluntly, it's a disgrace on the international stage, and even causes huge damage to the country's image.

This was their first joint operation with Myanmar.

They finally made a breakthrough in external cooperation. Combating crime in Yunhai itself isn't enough; they have to start from their homeland and destroy the criminal groups' strongholds.

Then, various criminal activities in Yunhai and those radiating from Yunhai throughout the country, including drug trafficking, will naturally decrease.

This was their first collaboration.

But even a bad start can at least save face.

The higher-ups took it very seriously; therefore, countless simulations and investigations were conducted before this operation.

And the result? Li Qishan's leadership made a complete fool of himself.

When the higher-ups found out, only heaven knows how their heads exploded.

Now.

Hearing this good news, the most crucial thing is Comrade Jiang's performance.

"Hey, wasn't Comrade Jiang Tian injured in the arm?" Minister Tan immediately reacted with concern.

His voice was on speaker, so Jiang Tian could hear him. He casually replied, "Minister Tan, one hand doesn't affect firing."

Hiss!!!

Minister Tan was stunned. One hand and he could be so fierce? If he wasn't injured, wouldn't he be flying high?

Of course, Minister Tan didn't waste any more words and hurriedly said, "I have already noted Comrade Xiao Jiang's merits and will request a first-class merit for him when he returns. However, the situation on your side has changed. The Third Special Zone has changed its mind and wants to negotiate. They may think you have no hope, so they want to negotiate with the Yun family and deport you. It's fine if they want to negotiate, but I want you to rescue your surrounded teammates safely. If there is an opportunity, eliminate as many criminals as possible."



Chapter 269 If even the insurmountable 800 meters can't be done, then I will.


As Minister Tan's voice rang out,

a buzzing sound filled the car, and everyone who heard it felt their heads explode.

If a storm had swept through, their brains would have nearly been splattered with shock.

"Are you crazy?"

"Negotiations?! Negotiations with those criminals? The Third Special Zone is begging the Yun family for mercy?" Song Liguo exclaimed incredulously.

Following this was immediate fury.

Song Liguo was enraged.

If they begged for mercy, where would the entire nation's standing be, and where would the police who had worked so hard to fight crime stand?

If they sued for peace, wouldn't that mean they were wrong?

The criminals were the ones who were right?

Buzz!!!

Song Liguo dared not imagine the terrifying and horrific consequences if this news got out.

"Impossible, Minister Tan! If we do that, even if they return safely, we'll become a laughingstock of the world. We'll be branded as historical figures of shame, mocked by the entire world!" Song Liguo exclaimed, abruptly standing to his feet in shock.

Headquarters Conference Room.

Minister Tan was acutely aware of the implications of this.

"I'll buy you two hours. Within those two hours, make as much of a scene as you can, do as much as you can," Minister Tan said, taking a deep breath.

After Minister Tan hung up the phone,

his face was contorted with rage.

"Minister, what are they thinking?" Xu Poye asked, adjusting his glasses, his face grim.

"How would I know what they're thinking? Who knows what they're up to?" Minister Tan's face was also grim. "What did the Third Special Zone say?"

Xu Poye shook his head. "Meng Lu has already contacted the Yun family. He told us to order the evacuation as soon as possible. Then, those of us surrounded will naturally be released."

"Ridiculous..."

Minister Tan slammed his hand on the table and retorted, "Didn't we tell them we've already annihilated over two hundred people?"

A small setback, and they're already backing down.

Minister Tan was almost laughing in exasperation.

"I did, but unfortunately, they clearly didn't believe me. They said we have elderly, weak, and disabled members among us, and they hoped we wouldn't harm them," Xu Poye complained.

"Elderly, weak, and disabled?" Minister Tan was stunned.

"I thought about it carefully. Of all the personnel deployed, only Comrade Xiao Jiang had his arm in a sling," Xu Poye explained.

"He's elderly, weak, and disabled?" Minister Tan was dumbfounded.
