“Of course, it’s also possible they already knew about the battle. The situation over there is so complicated; who knows what they’re up to? Maybe it’s just an excuse for us to leave, or perhaps they’ve formed some kind of alliance,” Xu Poye speculated.

“Whatever it is, if they want to use us as stepping stones to climb to power, then we’ll crush their teeth. The comrades who are besieged can come back, but not because they let them. Otherwise, we’ll become a laughingstock of the world,” Minister Tan said, his gaze deep and unsettling.

…

“Did you all hear that?”

In the pitch-black night, with stars twinkling outside, Jiang Tian stared out the window, his gaze deep and thoughtful.

Song Liguo’s voice rang out in the carriage, and instantly, everyone’s eyes turned to Song Liguo with fervent emotion.

No one spoke, but everyone's eyes spoke volumes.

"I know it's hard to accept, but we only have two hours. The only thing we can do is rescue our besieged comrades as quickly as possible. They can come back, but they absolutely cannot be released by the enemy. We can't afford to lose face, and neither can our country." Song Liguo didn't continue talking about wiping out the Yun family.

If there were only two hours...

Rescuing comrades would already be difficult.

Moreover, there were deeper reasons behind this.

"We should achieve something greater by rescuing our comrades, showing the Third Special Zone our strength. Then, the Third Special Zone will get involved, and with cooperation, we should be able to wipe out the Yun family."

Jiang Tian stroked his chin thoughtfully.

He had the "King of Criminal Investigation" entry, so reasoning about these things wasn't difficult for him.

Especially since Jiang Tian still had over 100,000 sin points to add, and he had just gained several thousand more.

Now Jiang Tian's sin point reserves were close to 120,000.

If Jiang Tian wanted, he could even create an entry and upgrade it to gold level anytime.

“Xiao Jiang, take your men and try to ambush from the flank,” Song Liguo instructed Jiang Tian directly.

“Yes!” Jiang Tian nodded.

This was already the best arrangement.

“We’ll draw fire from the front, but are you sure your hand is okay?” Song Liguo asked with some concern.

“It’s fine.” Jiang Tian moved his wrist and found he could feel nothing anymore.

The wound had healed about 60-70%.

But for Jiang Tian, ​​especially in this predicament, it was enough.

Bang!!!

Just as Jiang Tian finished speaking,

a gunshot suddenly rang out in the jungle.

Then a bullet shattered the windshield of the car in front with a thud, and then grazed Lü Chun’s ear.

“Quick, everyone on alert!” Song Liguo shouted into the walkie-talkie.

As for Jiang Tian…

Sitting in the car, he had actually been keeping his scanning radar on the whole time.

His radar was no longer able to detect red dots; they had merged into one.

The entire area had turned red.

"Damn, how many people are there?" Jiang Tian had lost count.

Then, Jiang Tian peered out, looking at his surroundings.

Unbeknownst to them, they had entered a plain. About 800 meters ahead lay a field of golden yellow.

But this wasn't a wheat field.

It was a vast drug cultivation base.

Over 100,000 acres of the plain were covered with poppies.

There are many types of drugs, some cultivated, like poppies, and others chemically synthesized, like methamphetamine.

But without a doubt, both types possess a terrifyingly deadly addictive quality; once addicted, a person is essentially finished.

And not just Jiang Tian.

When everyone got out of the car and saw the scene before them, they were all shocked.

"Damn it, these bastards are all hiding in these poppy fields. There's no way a car can get through. That shot just now was probably a warning. They're too arrogant." Song Liguo pounded on the police car, unable to help but grit his teeth.

Their nighttime operation was due to the unique geographical location of this area; it offered virtually no cover, surrounded by vast poppy fields on all sides.

A flat expanse stretching as far as the eye could see.

Entering during the day, with clear visibility, would make them easy targets.

Nighttime, however, greatly enhanced their combat capabilities.

But even at night, the nearly 800-meter distance from the flat plain to the poppy fields felt like an insurmountable abyss.

Li Qishan's move to alert the enemy had terrifyingly increased the difficulty of the mission.

Especially with the element of surprise; countless armed personnel were hidden within the poppy fields, while they faced nearly 700-800 meters of flat land ahead.

Add to that the time lost in the process.

Now, they estimated the enemy had even dug trenches within the poppy fields.

Song Liguo and his men could only hide behind the vehicle as cover, their faces grim.

On the way, he considered many methods of action, but he never dreamed that after arriving and seeing the terrain, he would feel utterly disheartened.

"How are we supposed to fight here?" Song Liguo felt a headache coming on just looking at the flat ground before him.

Forced charge forward, who knew how many people were hiding inside? Perhaps hundreds of men would be wiped out before they even reached the front.

Just then, a voice suddenly rang out from behind them: "Don't waste your time. If it were easy, we wouldn't have been left like this. Although you did surprise me just now, I admit your previous actions impressed me, and I misjudged you. However, the terrain here is too advantageous; unless you deploy heavy weapons, you simply can't get in. Now you know why we canceled the operation, right? Because if we alert them, and with reinforcements from those criminal groups arriving, we absolutely can't get in with light weapons."

"If you can't do it, then I'll do it."



Chapter 270 Giving the Neighbors a Little Shock


"I'll give it a try."

A hush fell over the scene following this voice.

Mara, who had rushed over, was stunned by these words, staring at the 800-meter-long flat plain before her.

A completely flat expanse.

Then, looking at the young man who had spoken, Mara laughed.

"You think you, a useless old man like yourself, can cross this 800-meter stretch of flat ground?" Mara's face was full of sarcasm.

Jiang Tian was organizing his magazines, placing them one by one into his bag.

He looked at Mara upon hearing this.

In fact, they had already noticed Mara's arrival.

Those who came were, after all, elite troops; they had been spotted the moment they approached.

Li Qishan was just a figurehead, but this time the real elite troops had arrived, and they couldn't afford not to be wary of a possible sneak attack.

They had been on guard since Mara's arrival.

However, after Mara spoke, they understood that she was probably not the enemy.

However, the moment Mara uttered the words "Mara is useless," everyone turned to look at her.

Mara glanced around and sneered, "The terrain here is extremely unique. There's a valley behind us. I even thought you'd be blocked there, but they didn't have anyone there. The Yun family's environment is incredibly advantageous, surrounded by mountains on three sides, almost impossible to cross. One person can hold off ten thousand. This open area is the only place we can launch a direct assault, but the cost will undoubtedly be devastating. If you all charged in together with your riot shields, you might have a chance, but try it yourself. How? To die?"

Mara's expression grew increasingly mocking.

Especially when she looked at Jiang Tian, ​​her eyes were filled with displeasure.

Indeed.

This team had earned Mara's approval. He had to admit their marksmanship was excellent; they had wiped out so many people in such a short time.

But…

What he couldn't understand was why, for such an important mission, they had to bring along an old, weak, and disabled person?

"Who are you calling old, weak, sick, or disabled?" Lü Chun, standing beside Jiang Tian, ​​was completely dumbfounded.

Mara pointed at Jiang Tian without hesitation.

"What can he do besides cause trouble here? A hindrance, right? It's harsh to call him trash, but reality is undeniable. Injured trash should stay put and keep quiet. Why is he jumping out to show off his presence? Let's see if you dare!" Mara said, pointing at Jiang Tian.

Song Liguo was stunned.

Everyone at the scene was stunned.

Including Old Chen, the armed police officer, who had just arranged for his men to be on guard and for everyone to take cover. When he arrived, he heard something unbelievable.

However, the words were harsh but the reasoning was sound.

Song Liguo frowned, not because he was worried about Jiang Tian's fighting ability, but because, given the harsh terrain and the unknown number of drug dealers hiding in the poppy field, the enemy was in the dark while they were in the light. At 800 meters, he was a sitting duck.

Even Jiang Tian couldn't possibly rush through!

"Little Jiang, it's too dangerous," Song Liguo whispered to Jiang Tian.

“It’s more than just dangerous. According to information from the Third Special Zone, the Yun family already has nearly seven or eight hundred armed men, a powerful force. After encountering danger, they reinforced themselves with nearly a thousand more. That means, besides the group that initially attacked, there are at least a thousand armed men wielding weapons inside the poppy field. Charging in would be a certain death for them, and even if they all charged at once, their chances were slim.”

Mara paused slightly before continuing, “Although I hate to admit it, a sneak attack was the best solution from the start. After alerting them, the mission failed. We received word from above that we will temporarily refrain from fighting the Yun family, and they have agreed that if you leave, they will release those people and leave each other alone.”

Mara had initially thought there was a chance—perhaps the drug dealers were underestimating them? But after arriving, he realized he had been mistaken.

Since there was no chance, Mara could only regretfully say,

You can leave now.

"What a fine 'keeping to ourselves' attitude! The Yun family has committed countless evils, and those criminal groups have done so much. You think you can just wipe them out with a few words? No way! They deserve to die!" Jiang Tian frowned deeply and rebuked them directly.

His emotions were already quite stable; he wouldn't normally get angry.

However...

Jiang Tian hadn't expected that even their own people would want to make peace.

No wonder the criminal groups here were so lawless; it was because there were too many useless cowards like them.

"Who are you? I have direct contact with your leader. Do you have the right to speak to me, you piece of trash?" Mara glared angrily at Jiang Tian and shouted.

"Please show some respect. This is a hero of our anti-drug squad," Song Liguo couldn't stand it any longer and stepped forward.

Mara's face turned even uglier. In his eyes, he had been treated like dirt.

A hero?

What kind of hero?

A brainless hero?

That's true.

“Perhaps only your brainless heroes could say that someone could cross this 800-meter-wide plain.” Mara sneered, her anger growing.

Unfortunately.

At this moment, Jiang Tian, ​​head down, took the small bucket from Lü Chun and soaked his jacket.

Then, he called over an armed policeman and borrowed a riot shield from him.

It was a riot shield after all; it not only protected against riots but could also stop bullets.

“Xiao Jiang, you can’t go, it’s too dangerous.” Song Liguo grabbed Jiang Tian, ​​his nose catching a pungent odor that startled him even more.

If Jiang Tian rushed up there, what would become of them?

“Uncle Song, you can just suppress them with fire from here.” Jiang Tian shook off Song Liguo.

Not going?

Under normal circumstances, Jiang Tian wouldn’t go, but in this situation, if they didn’t act now, they would miss their chance.

If time drags on, and the Third Special Zone and the Yun family have fully formed a partnership,

the damage to their reputation nationwide will be devastating.

Furthermore, it could even fuel a new wave of criminal activity throughout Yunhai and even across the country.

Of course, Jiang Tian wouldn't deliberately throw his life away if he wasn't confident.

He dared to do this because he was confident.

"Everyone open fire! Suppress the enemy!" Song Liguo roared, his eyes bloodshot.

A deafening roar followed.

Mara froze.

Right now, right before his eyes and within his sight.

Jiang Tian, ​​shield in one hand, legs bent.

The instant the gunshot rang out.

Boom!!!

Yes, a deafening roar.

Mara vaguely heard the roar.

But that wasn't the most important thing.

The most important thing was that Jiang Tian, ​​one hand around his neck, the other holding a shield, charged out of the police car's cover.

One step, and he was practically a blur.

Since these neighbors didn't believe him,

Jiang Tian didn't mind giving them a little disappointment.



Chapter 271 The Battle of Red Cliffs: The Sky Collapsed, All Burned Away in a Single Flame


"Is he courting death? How dare he? This is suicide! Such a death is pointless." Mara was slightly shocked.

"Whoosh...Chief, a fight has broken out?"

At this moment, Tana and her men finally caught up.

There was no other way.

The Third Special District was poor too; the police cars were dilapidated, moving slowly and stopping frequently, unable to keep up with Mara's urgent pursuit. Tana followed behind with her men.

When they arrived, they heard the intense sounds of gunfire.

"Yes, a fight has broken out." Mara nodded slightly.

"Then what about us?" Tana's eyes lit up.

But Mara immediately poured cold water on her enthusiasm, saying, "It's just that useless cripple we saw earlier. He couldn't handle the psychological blow coming here and rushed out to his death."

Tana's eyes widened.

She was very tall, easily over 1.8 meters, wearing a police uniform, with a very slender yet muscular figure.

She exuded a powerful aura.

Tana crossed Mara's path, and finally, she saw the scene ahead.

The weather was fine today; the moonlight was bright, and it had drizzled a little during the day.

The night was clear and cloudless. On the 800-meter-wide plain, a man wielding a shield was charging towards the wheat field.

"Is he... courting death? Why didn't you stop him? This is a pointless suicide mission!" Tana exclaimed anxiously.

To be honest...

Jiang Tian was unaware of what was happening behind him.

As he charged forward, Jiang Tian quickly placed the hand holding the shield on his bandaged arm, and with his uninjured arm, he swiftly drew his pistol.

Just how terrifying was Jiang Tian's physique now?

In the dead of night, few could notice that Jiang Tian's speed was approaching or even surpassing that of a world sprint champion, and a 100-meter track champion no less.

He was so fast that in just thirty seconds, Jiang Tian had already sprinted three hundred meters.

He was only five hundred meters away from the drug field.

At this point, it seemed that the drug dealers inside the field had discovered Jiang Tian.

Bang bang bang clang clang clang!

The sound of bullets striking riot shields rang out.

The impact was intense, but Jiang Tian not only remained unmoved, his speed actually increased.

Seeing this, Song Liguo didn't hesitate for a moment, immediately deploying nearly fifty riot police officers, armed with riot shields, to advance at the forefront.

This also attracted the attention of most of the drug dealers, naturally reducing the firepower on Jiang Tian's side.

Twenty seconds later.

Jiang Tian was now almost touching the poppy field.

Even without using his scanning radar, Jiang Tian could see with his naked eye the armed drug dealers standing in the trenches not far away.

More than a dozen of them were firing at Jiang Tian.

The riot shields, having withstood too much impact, were riddled with cracks, but luckily they hadn't shattered.

"Someone's charging over."

"Alone?"

"Is he going to his death?"

Hidden in the trenches, more than a dozen men were positioned on Jiang Tian's side.

At closer range, the trenches, constructed along the edge of the poppy field, were clearly visible.

It has to be said...

Domestic criminal gangs are nothing compared to these overseas criminal gangs; these people are truly daring!

Of course, every place has its own situation.

Jiang Tian didn't have time to think.

Because at that moment, a dark shadow suddenly flashed before his eyes.

The next moment.

Jiang Tian's gaze sharpened, and he saw a round hand grenade lying less than half a meter to his left.

Jiang Tian didn't even have time to think; his riot shield was already in place, blocking the grenade.

BOOM!!!

A terrifying explosion rang out, and the terrifying impact sent Jiang Tian flying backward instantly.

But Jiang Tian had already adjusted his angle. The shockwave from the grenade's explosion struck the riot shield, instantly shattering the already battered shield, especially as numerous small shards of glass grazed Jiang Tian's skin.

However, thanks to the shockwave, Jiang Tian propelled himself to within three meters of the trench. Before even landing, his pistol was already aimed and fired.

The instant his feet touched the ground, Jiang Tian once again soared into the trench like a roc spreading its wings.

Bang! Bang! Bang!!!

A series of gunshots rang out, but in the pitch-black night, the deafening gunfire went unnoticed.

However, the dozen or so men who had been watching Jiang Tian were stunned.

Before them lay six corpses, their heads blown off.

The remaining four or five men took a step back in fear.

Then, their eyes widened in horror at the figure lying in the trench. By the faint moonlight, they could see clearly.

A young face, with three thin streaks of blood on his cheeks.

Most unbelievably, one of his arms was still in a sling around his neck.

A wounded soldier?

He broke in?

He instantly killed all six of them?

*Whoosh!*

The drug dealers were stunned by what they saw.

"Are you a human or a ghost?" one of the dealers shouted in terror.

"Quick, quick, he's out of bullets! Don't let him reload!"

"Damn it, kill him!"

The remaining four or five dealers reacted, hurriedly raising their pistols to aim at Jiang Tian.

The minor cuts and bloodstains on his face were from the blast of the grenade that shattered his riot shield; the glass shards weren't serious injuries to Jiang Tian.

Of course, the most terrifying thing wasn't that. It was that Jiang Tian stood up, grinned at them, and said, "Is it possible that I actually reloaded before I landed?"

The four or five dealers looked confused.

*Bang! Bang! Bang!*

The gunshots rang out. Jiang Tian stood unharmed, his face expressionless, then silently walked to their side and took out two general-purpose pistol magazines.

Only then did the remaining four or five drug dealers stare wide-eyed and finally die.

"There's no one in this direction!" Jiang Tian looked around, a smirk playing on his lips, his eyes flashing red.

Because the hunt had begun.

Muttering to himself, Jiang Tian slowly took off his coat.

After checking the weather, Jiang Tian took out a lighter, and the instant he lit the coat, which he had soaked in gasoline beforehand, he threw it into the poppy field some distance away.

That's right.

Before setting off, Jiang Tian poured the liquid over himself—a can of gasoline he'd had Lü Chun siphon off after parking the car.

Last time he was here, Jiang Tian wanted to take action on this poppy field, but the weather was too humid, and it was raining.

After several days of drying, although there had been some light drizzle during the day, Jiang Tian found the vegetation was relatively dry.

Especially now, it was a golden expanse, ready for harvest.

That's right.

Today, Jiang Tian was going to set fire to over 100,000 acres of poppy fields, burning them clean.

Boom!!!



Chapter 272 The Hunt: One Man Changes the Course of Battle


Boom!!!

The instant Jiang Tian threw his jacket out of the trench, it burst into flames, the fire landing in the poppy field.

Crackling!!!

Thick smoke billowed. It was already harvest time, and the vegetation was extremely dry. The flames quickly spread, producing thick smoke and escalating the fire within minutes.

At that moment, Jiang Tian, ​​having removed his jacket, was left only in a pristine white T-shirt.

The T-shirt accentuated Jiang Tian's well-defined muscles.

Amidst the crackling sounds...

In the trench that Jiang Tian stood in, about twenty meters long, nestled between two corners, almost touching another trench...

A drug dealer, cigar in mouth, seemingly tired of firing his automatic rifle, retreated back into the trench.

He was still cursing under his breath, "What are these bastards trying to do? Are they crazy? They won't come over, they won't leave, and they're just firing from that far away. They've got too much money, so they're just wasting some bullets."

Beside him, a man who seemed to be a minor leader, still wearing a suit and leaning against the trench with a pistol, chimed in, "Alright, the Yun family just said they're already in talks with the Third Special Zone. Those guys are probably desperate. They can't do anything about it and can't get in, so they're just putting on a show, trying to save face."

At this point, the minor leader exhaled a puff of his cigar, his expression extremely relaxed.

"Boss, you're a genius! You even thought of that?" the cursing underling exclaimed excitedly as he ran to the minor leader's side.

They thought it would be fraught with danger, but it turned out to be such a simple job.

How easy? As long as you didn't foolishly stick your head out of the trench and get hit by a stray bullet, there was basically no danger.

This kind of battle could last three days and three nights without anyone dying.

“Nonsense, otherwise how would I be the boss? You'd only be the underling. Just wait, in less than two hours, those guys will be slinking away.” The foreman said casually.

“Boss, can you smell something burning?” an underling asked.

“Cigar smell, don’t be alarmed.” The foreman didn’t seem to notice anything.

“No, boss, it’s not cigar smell. I smell it too, it smells like a fire.” Another underling hurriedly spoke up.

The foreman looked up at the two underlings who had spoken, about to scold them for smelling the wrong thing, but the next moment, a billowing black smoke rose into the sky before his eyes.

Moreover, the weather wasn’t good today, with a bit of wind, which only fueled the fire.

So, the moment he saw it,

the flames had already begun to spread rapidly.

“On fire?” The foreman stared at the towering flames, his head buzzing.

Fire?

100,000 mu of poppies are on fire?

The sky is falling!

"Fire! Fire!" the foreman roared.

As for the men behind him, he had already forgotten them all; his only thought was to put out the fire.

This was 100,000 mu of poppies, tended for so long, finally blossoming and bearing fruit, finally ready for harvest.

But precisely because it was ready for maturity...

The leaves, withered and yellowed, had lost all moisture; burning them was like tinder, quickly igniting into a massive fire that spread at an alarming rate.

This was only because of the light drizzle during the day; otherwise, the fire would have been as intense as a raging inferno of dry wood.

"Put out the fire!"

"Water! Water!"

The ringleader was just one example; the other drug dealers had gone mad.

Especially some of the Yun family's drug dealers, who were utterly devastated by the sight.

For the Yun family, this was a devastating blow!

Ten thousand acres of poppies burned.

What does that mean?

Do you know how terrifying this income was for the Yun family? Whether sold across the Atlantic or locally, ten thousand acres of poppies represented a terrifying fortune that no one could imagine.

Every poppy burned now was like burning a tree of gold.

They couldn't sit still any longer.

They couldn't sit still at all.

The Yun family members all rushed out, crawling out of the trenches. Time was of the essence; they could only grab their clothes and rush to extinguish the flames.

And surprisingly,

Jiang Tian, ​​hidden in a trench, quietly observed the situation.

At the current rate of burning, if all these people joined in, the fire could be extinguished quickly.

It was just a pity.

A smile played on Jiang Tian's lips. After a hundred or two drug dealers had systematically crawled out of the trenches to extinguish the fire, and Jiang Tian was sure no more drug dealers were coming out, he slowly pulled out an automatic rifle.

This was something Jiang Tian had taken from one of the drug dealers after killing more than ten people shortly after coming down.

Bang! Bang! Bang!

Loading, disengaging the safety, firing—a seamless, uninterrupted motion.

Intense gunfire erupted from the automatic rifles, spitting fire as it surged towards the drug dealers trying to extinguish the fire.

To make matters worse, the reinforcements advancing behind the riot police, though still hundreds of meters away and having stopped in their tracks, continued firing relentlessly.

The exchange of fire was fierce; it even appeared as if both sides were firing into the air.

The purpose of this complex gunfire was to create a greater concealment for Jiang Tian.

Even with Jiang Tian's frantic spray, no one could spot his position.

But in the rice paddy, amidst the poppy fields, over a hundred drug dealers trying to extinguish the fire perished.

Their bodies fell limply to the ground almost without pause.

Target practice.

This was a piece of cake for Jiang Tian. If he couldn't even hit a target accurately, his golden "perfect accuracy" skill was a joke.

Even with a fully automatic weapon, Jiang Tian could get headshots every time.

Fast.

Too fast.

In a few seconds, Jiang Tian had emptied a magazine.

The drug dealers trying to extinguish the fire fell to the ground like wheat.

Jiang Tian barely stopped.

After a few magazines, over a hundred drug dealers trying to extinguish the fire were dead.

And then Jiang Tian became like the Grim Reaper, lying prone, delivering a devastating blow to anyone who approached to try and put out the fire.

And then...

Under everyone's gaze.

The fire grew larger and more terrifying.

Terrifying! Even inside the trenches, a scorching heat was palpable, a truly unbearable feeling.

The fire was spreading.

Chaos reigned. Utter chaos.

Jiang Tian moved through the trenches like a grim reaper, the trenches practically his natural cover.

The first rank of drug dealers was being swiftly eliminated by Jiang Tian.

The pressure on the front lines lessened, as many drug dealers gradually realized something was wrong and began searching for Jiang Tian, ​​while others were ambushed by him.

Jiang Tian alone changed the course of the battle.



Chapter 273 The drug dealers all collapsed, what beasts!


The flames soared into the sky.

With Jiang Tian lurking like a venomous snake in the shadows, those trying to fight the fire couldn't get close.

Crucially, the trenches offered a vast area to the viewer. While many noticed Jiang Tian's approach in the darkness, most attention was focused on the advancing riot police, overlooking his sudden advance.

Or rather, because Jiang Tian was alone, many drug dealers assumed he was going to his death, firing several magazines, waiting for him to break through and die.

This very fact, drawing everyone's attention to the police, actually provided Jiang Tian with a huge advantage. The continuous gunfire and the towering flames threw the drug dealers into complete chaos.

Especially since many of their trenches were dug from the poppy fields, the raging fire was like cooking them, driving the drug dealers mad and driving them forward like madmen.

Without the protection of trenches, Jiang Tian, ​​hidden in the shadows, was nothing more than a series of live targets. What, you say there weren't enough bullets? What a joke! The drug dealers' corpses were riddled with bullets and weapons.

For Jiang Tian, ​​it was like being called out in a roll call.

Don't even mention Jiang Tian; in this environment, anyone who had spent a few years in the military would be driven mad by the sheer volume of bullets fired at them.

Therefore.

When the flames soared into the sky.

The drug dealers' firing intensity towards the armed police decreased significantly, and their advance accelerated.

For the drug dealers, this was a true catastrophe.

Their well-hidden hideout was engulfed in flames.

"It's over."

"The sky has fallen."

The low-level leader stared in horror at the out-of-control fire and the corpses lying on the ground. If they had rushed in to put out the fire, they might have been able to control it while it was still small.

But halfway there, the drug dealers fell to the ground like raindrops.

When the leader, reacting quickly, dropped to the ground and looked up again, all ten-odd henchmen with him were gone, their pupils dilated, lifeless.

It wasn't just them.

The same happened to the other drug dealers who had charged forward like him. He had been lucky enough to survive, but those who had tried to put out the fire were dead.

The fire spread even faster, crackling and popping.

Even if Jiang Tian hadn't fired or tried to stop them, they might not have been able to extinguish the fire, let alone with Jiang Tian hiding in the shadows, firing in the chaotic darkness.

Especially given the environment, the numerous criminal groups that had come to reinforce them, many of whom were complete strangers.

Jiang Tian took off his uniform jacket. In this chaotic environment, with Song Liguo leading the armed police advancing from the flank, and the raging fire, this was practically Jiang Tian's natural hunting ground.

Gunshots rang out.

Anyone who couldn't stand the cooking in the poppy field trenches and ran out was immediately shot by Jiang Tian.

If no one ran out, it was even simpler; the trenches served as natural cover for Jiang Tian.

Now, with his gun in hand in the trench, Jiang Tian had removed his hand from its bandages.

It was clear that although flesh had grown back, the wound still looked rather gruesome. This was because, under the "Extreme Recovery" blue stat, the injury's healing speed was five to six times that of an ordinary person; however, even an ordinary person would need several months to fully heal a gunshot wound.

Even with five to six times the speed, it would still take Jiang Tian more than ten days.

In just a few days, it could only be said that the injury had stopped worsening and was currently in a period of rapid recovery.

Of course, it no longer affected Jiang Tian's actions.

However, firing a gun with his injured hand would inevitably cause the wound to worsen and reopen.

Bang bang bang!!!

But now, Jiang Tian's eyes were glowing; in his eyes, this was all crimson sin value.

Plus, Jiang Tian's Judgment Eye...

A single scan revealed the precise location of the drug dealers on the radar.

The Judgment Eye could instantly determine their guilt level, making the harvesting even faster and more satisfying.

In the end, Jiang Tian even forgot about his upper arm injury, caught up in the killing frenzy.

He held two pistols, his waistbands crammed with magazines.

Stepping around the corner from the trench, he saw more than a dozen drug dealers, drenched in sweat, lying prone inside the trench, firing at the advancing armed police.

They hadn't even noticed Jiang Tian's arrival.

Or rather, the series of events had happened too quickly and too chaotically; too many people simply didn't know what had just occurred.

So much so that...

Bang bang bang!!

Jiang Tian stood to their side, pistols raised, unleashing a barrage of fire. In seconds, he had rounded up over a dozen drug dealers.

He was in a killing frenzy.

He was completely in a killing frenzy.

After killing all the drug dealers in the trench, Jiang Tian looked up and saw flames shooting into the sky. Even he felt the intense heat.

The scorching flames caused sweat to bead on Jiang Tian's forehead.

The ordinary drug dealers, of course, couldn't stay still any longer and jumped out of the trench one after another.

When Jiang Tian glanced around from the edge of the trench, he dropped his two pistols and picked up a fully loaded automatic rifle. He unleashed a volley of fire at the drug dealers, who were almost within arm's reach, and they fell in rows.

By the time Jiang Tian had killed at least three or four hundred people,

even a fool would have realized what was happening.

"Damn it, those beasts!"

"Kill that beast!"

Explosion.

The Yun family's leader was sweating profusely. He had just been in a meeting with numerous support forces.

At first, he had confidently assured them that he had made contact with the Third Special District, and that the Yun family and the Third Special District had reached an agreement; the Third Special District would immediately send people to expel these attackers.

A fight to the death or complete annihilation was impossible; the Yun family wasn't stupid.

What they had done before had already crossed the line. If they didn't leave them any way out and killed them all, the neighboring superpower would be enraged and might send heavy weapons regardless of international repercussions, leaving them only able to wait helplessly for their deaths.

Therefore, resolving the situation peacefully would be the best outcome.

“The young lady was just on the phone telling me that as long as these people didn’t cause any trouble within two hours, everything would be fine. And now…”

The leader was sweating profusely.

His head was spinning, especially after he noticed Jiang Tian shooting at the target. He glanced around and, good heavens, there were corpses everywhere.

A beast!

“Where did this beast come from?” the Yun family leader asked his subordinate.

“Just now, this guy was watching the firing net, using a riot shield, and then he got hit by a bomb. We all thought he was definitely dead and didn’t pay attention. Now it seems he wasn’t killed, and he might even have started the fire. Not only that, he definitely attacked from behind; he killed us all by himself.” The henchman said, his eyes bloodshot.

A beast!

Absolutely a beast!



Chapter 274 Things Have Gone Wrong, Headquarters Commander


"Kill him!" The leader, a man in his forties wearing a floral shirt, roared the order, his voice cracking.

Almost instantly, as soon as the leader finished speaking, dozens of drug dealers surrounded Jiang Tian.

Bang! Bang! Bang!

Just as the drug dealers bent down to approach the corner, Jiang Tian suddenly shot out two dark pistols, aiming them at them simultaneously, and gunfire erupted in rapid succession.

Then the leader heard several muffled thuds, and looking down, saw more than a dozen of his drug dealers lying limply on the ground.

In an instant, more than a dozen men were dead.

The other drug dealers reacted, drawing their guns and firing wildly, but by then, Jiang Tian had already retreated.

The bullets only managed to penetrate the muddy trench around the corner.

After firing for a while, they cautiously tried to approach.

Bang! Bang! Bang!

The next moment, Jiang Tian appeared as if by magic, emerging from the trench behind them. Gunshots rang out, and more than ten men fell limply to the ground.

Simply over twenty drug dealers had lost their lives.

Of course, most importantly, the boss was now the only one left in the entire trench.

Before the boss could do anything,

something hard pressed against the back of his head.

"Don't kill me, don't kill me," the boss cried, his hands raised and his legs trembling.

At this moment, a faint voice gradually came from behind him, seemingly tinged with regret: "Life, sometimes, is so fragile. A bullet that costs only a few dollars can easily take a person's life and their entire existence."

As the voice spoke,

Jiang Tian stepped out from behind the boss and gradually walked towards him.

Only now did the leader clearly see Jiang Tian's face and appearance. Young, excessively young.

Upon first seeing this face, the leader even thought the man wasn't half his age.

"Who are you?" the leader asked, sweating profusely.

"I'm a police officer," Jiang Tian said, tilting his head to glance outside.

At this moment, the fire was burning even more fiercely.

Jiang Tian had already dealt with most of the key figures. He hadn't just been killing; he had also been constantly observing the details.

He had been observing this leader for a long time; he was definitely in charge.

"What do you want?" the leader asked, his lips trembling.

"Take me to your young lady," Jiang Tian said in a low voice.

The continuous killing had further relieved the pressure on the armed police ahead.

So much so that

the advancing armed police were completely stunned.

They moved in shuffling steps, heads down, shields raised, but somehow the more they moved, the fewer bullets remained in the air.

When they finally looked up,

they saw flames shooting into the sky.

It was true, only a few hundred meters away, but the terrifying flames still illuminated their direction.

The fire spread rapidly and intensely.

A slight breeze seemed to act as a powerful catalyst.

Flames rose.

The drug dealers' resistance remained fierce.

"Speed ​​up!"

Song Liguo was anxious.

There were over a thousand drug dealers there!

Jiang Tian was alone.

The longer it went on, the more dangerous Jiang Tian became.

Of course, as time passed, the fire spiraled completely out of control, igniting the 100,000 mu (approximately 6,667 hectares) of poppy fields. Drug dealers near the fields could only dream of hiding.

Even those hiding in the trenches were scalded and jumping around in the intense heat.

Finally, more and more drug dealers emerged.

Without cover, and with riot police blocking bullets from the front, anyone who resisted in place became a sitting duck.

Song Liguo's face was flushed with excitement.

"Charge! Charge!" Song Liguo immediately ordered.

With Song Liguo's command, more than five hundred men charged forward at lightning speed.

The gunfire grew increasingly intense.

When Song Liguo reached the trenches:

The scene was littered with corpses.

And the poppy fields, the sky almost glowing red from the heat.

Song Liguo's heart skipped a beat.

"Where's Xiao Jiang?" Song Liguo asked hurriedly.

"Couldn't find him."

Old Chen ran over, panting heavily.

This was the best of both worlds, because at this moment, very few people were still alive at the scene, and Jiang Tian wasn't among them.

Fortunately, there were no corpses either, which was the only relatively good news.

Because there was even worse news.

"Chief Song, the fire in the poppy field is completely out of control. The road to Yunjia Town is practically covered by fire; it's impassable now." A moment later, an armed man rushed up and said urgently to Jiang Tian.

"Damn it."

Song Liguo cursed on the spot.

His last bit of good mood vanished.

He looked up at the distance; the terrifying sea of ​​fire burned ever brighter.

A whole 100,000 acres of land—these mature poppies were practically nothing but dry tinder.

Even though it was somewhat damp, once the fire was completely out of control, this moisture actually caused the pile of dry firewood to burn while billowing thick smoke.

Song Liguo, his face grim, shouted, "At all costs, by any means necessary, clear a path and rush through!"

"Are you crazy?"

Old Chen grabbed Song Liguo.

Because flames were burning on both sides of the road, rushing across such a long distance, even if one wasn't burned to death, the toxic gases and thin oxygen would be insufficient to sustain anyone.

Song Liguo knew this perfectly well, but said, "Old Chen, if Xiao Jiang isn't here, he must have rushed in before the fire started. We have to go and help."

"But we have to wait until the fire subsides."

Old Chen held onto Song Liguo.

Not only Old Chen.

Just as Song Liguo was about to break free,

the phone suddenly rang.

The next moment...

When Song Liguo took out his phone to check it, he was surprised to find it was an unknown number. Just as he was about to hang up, it rang again.

This prompted Song Liguo to take out his phone and answer it.

"As the person in charge of the operation, remaining calm is your top priority. I know you're anxious; you want to assist Comrade Xiao Jiang and rescue the trapped comrades. But as the commander, what good is impulsiveness other than causing your own comrades to die? I now order you to remain where you are and await further orders." A cold voice rang out from the phone.

"Who are you?" Song Liguo was slightly startled.

The stern, commanding voice on the phone sounded somewhat familiar to Song Liguo; he seemed to have heard it somewhere before, but he couldn't quite place it.

"I am the minister from headquarters. My surname is Tan." The stern voice rose slowly and deliberately.

"What, Minister Tan?" Song Liguo was shocked.



Chapter 275 Infiltration, Miss Yun


"I've inspected the situation on site and have a general understanding. I now order you to remain where you are and wait for the fire to subside before commencing any rescue efforts," Minister Tan commanded ruthlessly.

In the Kyoto headquarters building, the lights were blazing in a conference room on the top floor.

Compared to the Municipal Bureau, the two are not even comparable, not even in terms of the level of luxury Zhuang Heng enjoys.

In terms of spatial area, there is no comparison between the two.

On the blackboard directly in front of the conference room, a huge monitor was displaying a clear video of flames engulfing the area.

This was live video footage transmitted directly from a distance.

Besides, as the camera moved, the ground was littered with corpses.

Minister Tan, chin in hand, stared intently at everything on the screen. The landline phone in front of him rang with Song Liguo's shocked voice: "Minister Tan, how did you know about this place?"

Minister Tan's expression remained unchanged as he said, "Such an important matter, all subsequent operations will be directed by headquarters. You just need to follow orders."

That's right.

This mission is far too important.

It's caused a huge uproar.

Especially after Jiang Tian set fire to over 100,000 acres of poppy fields, it completely shook headquarters.

In the dead of night, headquarters was brightly lit, with people bustling about.

Many leaders sat at the conference room table, but without a doubt, their eyes were all fixed on the video in front of them.

It showed the video of the armed police advancing.

The armed police specialists from headquarters, who had been sent to Kyoto, had already set up cameras and were broadcasting live from the scene to headquarters.

The moment the broadcast started, they saw the raging fire.

“With the current fire intensity, you simply can't get in, especially without gas masks. You'll just collapse halfway there, and even with gas masks, you'd probably be steamed alive,” Minister Tan explained.

“What about Xiao Jiang?” Song Liguo asked.

Although there were a large number of bodies at the scene, burned beyond recognition, Song Liguo was certain that Jiang Tian wasn't among them.

Because Jiang Tian had hand injuries, and Song Liguo had checked each charred body individually, none of them matched the description.

Since he wasn't there, the only possibility was that Jiang Tian had broken in beforehand.

“We can only trust Xiao Jiang now. Most importantly, the Third Special Zone has lodged a strong protest. You can't act rashly now,” Song Liguo said gravely.

This left Song Liguo staring wide-eyed, almost in disbelief.

A protest?

Protesting about what?

They came to help them eliminate the criminal group, what were they protesting?

However, Minister Tan didn't answer his question; his gaze remained fixed on the sea of ​​fire, still filled with shock.

The burning of 100,000 mu (approximately 6,667 hectares) was truly awe-inspiring. Even those who weren't there in person, but only saw it on broadcast, could feel the sheer scale of the scene.

In truth, Song Liguo was unaware.

At this very moment, the Yun family had reached a peace and friendship agreement with Meng Lu, the head of the Third Special Zone.

Once Song Liguo withdrew, the Yun family would release him.

Afterwards, things continued as usual, with everyone keeping to themselves.

Headquarters also needed to consider the impact; the host family didn't welcome them, and they were, after all, just guests helping out.

Now the host family was starting to ask them to leave.

"I thought this mission had failed, and we'd lose face big time, but I didn't expect this!" An old man named Zhang next to Minister Tan said, his eyes shining.

They already knew what had just happened. Not only that, they even knew that Jiang Tian had charged 80,000 meters alone with a riot shield, which was almost shattered by bullets.

Xu Poye's face relaxed somewhat: "It's pretty good now. Even if the mission failed, burning 100,000 acres of poppy fields should have given them a painful blow and a lesson, at least salvaging some face."

It wasn't that they didn't want to take further action.

It was that the Third Special Zone had now changed its mind and didn't want to continue being enemies with the Yun family and these criminal forces.

It was highly likely that a large number of other criminal forces had joined forces to threaten the Third Special Zone.

Just as headquarters had prepared for the worst.

The Yun family's headquarters.

It could also be said to be the very center of Yun Family Town.

There stood a massive mansion.

The mansion was built with magnificent grandeur.

It blended ancient and modern styles, appearing very high-end and sophisticated.

Modernity and ancient style were perfectly combined.

Jiang Tian, ​​following the chief, entered the Yun family compound almost without hindrance.

Contrary to expectations of heavy guard, while there appeared to be many guards protecting the Yun family outside the gate, once inside the mansion, he discovered that there were very few guards.

However, Jiang Tian thought about it again and realized it made sense. Things were in complete chaos; a large number of people in the back mountains were surrounded by the Yun family and their men.

And there were armed police attacking from the front.

They probably already had all their remaining manpower deployed.

It was easy.

To be honest, this was a million times better than fighting their way in. Jiang Tian encountered nothing unusual along the way, leisurely following the leader into the mansion.

They were immediately met by an elderly man in his sixties, with gray hair but dressed in an impeccably neat suit.

"Butler," the leader quickly greeted him.

"The young lady is very angry. This poppy field has already been reserved with buyers; they've even paid a deposit. And what have you done? How could you let it catch fire? Come inside immediately," the butler replied.

"Yes, yes, I'll go in and explain to the young lady right away."

The leader walked forward with his head down.

Jiang Tian followed, about to enter the inner courtyard.

Outside the large house in the mansion, Jiang Tian could even see through the transparent glass the ornate decorations inside, and the faint outline of a slender figure.

“You can’t go in.”

The butler stopped Jiang Tian, ​​who was following the gang leader, with a blank expression.

This made Jiang Tian raise his head slightly and glance at the butler.

“Strangers are not allowed to see Miss. That’s the rule. Wait here,” the butler continued.

“Oh, what if I insist on going in?”

Jiang Tian raised his head slightly.

The leader, who had been standing with his head down, listening intently to the commotion behind him, seemed to recall something terrifying upon hearing these words, and a cold sweat instantly broke out on his face.

"Hahaha, you think you can just see a lowly piece of trash like yourself? You don't deserve it! Kneel here and wait."



Chapter 276 Total Victory, Huge Gains Shock Everyone


"Xiao Huan, where did you find this lackey? So clueless!"

The butler's smile masked his anger.

As the young lady's butler, and arguably the Yun family's butler, he was, as the saying goes, "even a seventh-rank official before the prime minister's gate is no match for a high-ranking official." He was a servant, a butler in the Yun family, but who dared to disrespect him?

And who dared to be so arrogant in front of him?

Xiao Huan, the leader, was now completely frozen.

"Xiao Huan, can't you hear what I said? Immediately have your man kneel at the main gate. How dare he cause trouble here? How can you accept such an ill-mannered bastard?" The butler's face grew increasingly grim and unpleasant.

Xiao Huan nervously turned around and said, "Sir, he's just being ignorant. Please give him some face."

As he spoke, Xiao Huan quickly forced a smile.

The butler's grim expression softened slightly. Considering the tense situation, he didn't continue to reprimand him.

"Alright, I'll give you face and let him get lost. The young lady is already waiting for you inside. Next time, learn your lesson and don't accept just anyone..."

*Thud!*

But the butler's words were cut short.

He couldn't utter another word.

Because at that very moment, Guan Jian turned his head and realized that right in front of him, the underling he had just reprimanded was now standing there.

And most unbelievably, this underling was holding a dagger, but the entire blade was already plunged into his throat.

The underling in front of him, Xiao Huan's face, remained expressionless, utterly calm.

“You… heh heh…”

The butler tried desperately to speak, but only gushing blood escaped his lips.

He stretched out his arms towards the villa, seemingly trying to call for the young lady.

But alas, no sound came out.

Even as he collapsed to the ground, the loyal butler still clung tightly to Jiang Tian's leg, desperately trying to use his last bit of strength to make a sound to alert the young lady.

But in the end, it was all in vain.

“Lead the way.”

Jiang Tian casually kicked aside the butler's corpse blocking his path and spoke calmly to the boss, Xiao Huan.

After taking only two steps, Jiang Tian glanced back at the butler's corpse, then shook his head slightly.

Although the butler's words were unpleasant, such unwavering loyalty was truly rare.

Jiang Tian had encountered countless criminal groups in the past few days, but how many of them possessed any sense of morality?

They all swear they'll live and die together, but when it comes to the point of death, their testimonies are filled with the names of their brothers.

This steward is relatively loyal; this also shows the Yun family's strength.

"Yes, over here."

Xiao Huan, pale-faced, bowed and led the way. Jiang Tian followed unhurriedly. Killing someone, especially someone with heinous crimes, no longer stirred any emotion in Jiang Tian; he had experienced too much in recent days.

Creak!!!

Xiao Huan pushed open the door.

The first thing they saw, directly opposite the door, was a luxurious sofa. Seated on it sat a woman in a silk nightgown, her hair styled in large waves, holding a glass of red wine, her eyes silently observing them.

The woman didn't look at Jiang Tian immediately, but instead focused her gaze on Xiao Huan: "Xiao Huan, won't you introduce yourself?"

Xiao Huan hesitated for a moment before speaking, "Miss, I..."

"However, considering your last act of redemption, I'll let you off this time, but don't let it happen again." The woman, like a queen, elegantly placed her wine glass on the table, stretched, and then looked at Jiang Tian.

Her eyes were full of exploration and interest.

"I didn't expect the Dragon Kingdom police to have someone like you." The woman stood up from the sofa.

Jiang Tian walked into the room seemingly unsuspecting, glanced around, and a slight smile played on his lips. "Surprised?"

"Yes, but even with your strength, you still acted impulsively this time. What a lovely face, so youthful." The young lady walked to Jiang Tian's side, admiring his delicate features.

Jiang Tian, ​​however, instantly raised his hand, hooking his fingers around the woman's chin, his gaze scrutinizing her.

Her eyes were filled with lingering shock.

"You're not the eldest daughter of the Yun family." Jiang Tian's face was cold.

"You mean my sister? You're so clever, you can guess!" The woman smiled slyly.

Thump!!!

Jiang Tian grabbed the woman's throat and slammed her against the wall.

The woman's face flushed red, and she began to pound on Jiang Tian's arm.

"Where is the eldest daughter of the Yun family?" Jiang Tian's face was cold.

According to Yunbao's confession, all the Yun family's crimes were orchestrated by the eldest daughter of the Yun family. Jiang Tian had just scanned the radar and rushed in, eager to kill the butler, because the scanning radar hadn't shown the expected red light.

"Cough cough cough, my sister just got a phone call and left directly by helicopter. I've been running the family's charitable work and was only called back yesterday," the woman coughed as she spoke.

"My sister never lets me get involved in these things. You've got the wrong person," the woman coughed, tears welling up in her eyes.

Jiang Tian had just grabbed her throat, almost killing her.

This reminded Jiang Tian.

"Then why did you say those things when he called you 'eldest daughter'?" Jiang Tian felt he'd been tricked.

"Because, I felt my sister is usually very cool. Oh, right, my sister just left after answering his call." The woman said whatever she knew, with almost no concealment.

Jiang Tian's expression darkened, and his gaze turned to Xiao Huan.

Xiao Huan, sitting on the ground, hurriedly explained, "Officer, I saw the fire and reported it to Miss Yun. I didn't betray you."

Jiang Tian didn't say anything, but took out his phone and dialed Song Liguo's number directly.

"Xiao Jiang, how are you? Are you alright? We've already broken in," Song Liguo's impatient voice rang out.

"I'm fine. Miss Yun has run away," Jiang Tian said, lighting a cigarette.

"Hahaha, it's alright. It's normal for a few to slip through the net. I've caught a big fish; the patriarch of the Yun family has been captured. The people from the Third Special Zone have begun assisting us. We've urgently sealed off the drug manufacturing base in Yun Family Town and the Yun family's parts factory, etc. The remaining manpower is heading towards the back mountain. However, according to the information, those armed drug traffickers who were besieging us have lost their fighting spirit and are preparing to escape. Besides, there's a large amount of criminal evidence. The criminal records and evidence we've salvaged so far are beyond our imagination," Song Liguo said excitedly.

This was good news amidst misfortune.

Even Jiang Tian's heavy expression showed a slight hint of joy.



Chapter 277 The Greatest Loss in History, The Sky Has Collapsed


The evidence of their crimes was too much for Jiang Tian to bear. He couldn't even imagine the terrifying amount of evidence against the Yun family, who had been entrenched in Myanmar and constantly smuggling goods into Yunhai.

Thinking about it, even a minor member of the Yun family, a Mr. Yun, had a horrific criminal record in Yunhai. What about the entire Yun family?

They were finished.

“Xiao Jiang, this mission was a complete success. The eldest young lady escaped, and we captured the patriarch. However, the situation isn't optimistic now. According to the clues, Li Qishan and his group are in great danger, and most of the Yun family's forces are still engaged in a manhunt.”

Jiang Tian hung up the phone without saying much, only instructing Song Liguo to send a few people to detain Xiao Huan and the second young lady of the Yun family.

Through interrogation, Jiang Tian learned that the second young lady of the Yun family was a twin of the eldest young lady. However, although they were twins, their life experiences were completely different.

The second young lady, Yun Wei, was sent to study in the United States from a young age and then took over an overseas charity company, always working in the charitable sector. She had never known or been involved in any of the family's affairs.

But the eldest daughter is different. She's been by the old patriarch's side since childhood, receiving his guidance and training.

All the dark side of the Yun family was actually done by the eldest daughter.

Very well.

Jiang Tian couldn't help but admire the terrifying foundation and foresight of these powerful families. The old patriarch of the Yun family probably foresaw his own potential downfall and the future of this industry.

So he had the second daughter prepare a backup plan.

Even if the Yun family falls, the second daughter will remain innocent no matter how much she's investigated overseas.

“You pinched me too hard just now, and I told you everything. Little brother, you're so heartless, just like when you killed someone at the door.” Yun Wei squatted on the ground, her body shrunken, her voice dripping with a sweet, cloying tone, making Jiang Tian's face turn cold.

“One more word, and I'll slap you twice.”

Jiang Tian raised his hand, and Yun Wei instantly shrank back in fear.

Damn it.

Although Yun Wei was beautiful and alluring, did a woman's contribution matter to Jiang Tian?

What was originally a flawless achievement had been marred by this blemish.

While still perfect, even a small problem was unacceptable to Jiang Tian, ​​who had a slight obsessive-compulsive tendency.

Song Liguo hadn't lied to Jiang Tian.

Almost immediately after Jiang Tian hung up the phone, five or six fully armed police officers rushed in through the gate.

Especially the leader, who surprised Jiang Tian.

"Brother Jiang, are you alright?"

Gao Yangyang looked disheveled, wearing a black bulletproof vest and helmet; Jiang Tian almost didn't recognize her at first glance.

As she spoke, she rushed forward and threw herself into Jiang Tian's arms.

Jiang Tian opened his arms, unsure what to do.

He didn't even know what was going on with Gao Yangyang.

The previous two incidents were unexpected, and Gao Yangyang had said afterwards that she would wait for Jiang Tian to figure things out before giving him an answer, and that she wouldn't bother him until then.

At that time, Jiang Tian hadn't figured out how to handle the problems around him.

But now...

To be honest, seeing Gao Yangyang sobbing and shaking in his arms, Jiang Tian's heart softened. He placed his hands on Gao Yangyang's shoulders and comforted her, "It's alright, it's alright now."

As Jiang Tian spoke, four or five armed police officers behind Gao Yangyang rushed into the house, took out handcuffs, and restrained Yun Wei and Xiao Huan.

"What exactly happened? How did you manage to penetrate so deep behind enemy lines to the back mountains and end up here?"

This was still Jiang Tian's biggest concern.

Or rather, Jiang Tian knew the Yun family's unique geographical location, so he still couldn't understand how Li Qishan had led his men so deep behind enemy lines.

The Yun family was surrounded by mountains on three sides. The back mountains were behind Yunjia Town, and in front of Yunjia Town were 100,000 mu of poppy fields. Beyond that was a valley—a typical easily defensible location. That's why Jiang Tian couldn't believe how Li Qishan had led his men across the poppy fields and Yunjia Town, directly to their back mountains where they were ambushed.

At this point, Gao Yangyang's face flushed with anger. "Brother Jiang, you don't know, Li Qishan planted some kind of mole. He said it was one of his most trusted subordinates, someone he'd sent to give him a shortcut through the back mountains, claiming it led directly to the Yun family's headquarters, where the defenses were very weak. He told us to come up from the back mountain, but who knew the Yun family had already laid an ambush."

"Where are they?" Jiang Tian asked with concern.

He wasn't concerned about Li Qishan, but rather about Captain Guo and his team, as well as Zhao Wei and Li Zijin, who had all participated in the operation.

Jiang Tian was truly worried about them.

"They're still on the mountain, but I chose to break out as soon as I sensed something was wrong. I don't know their exact location," Gao Yangyang shook her head.

After breaking out, she and a few others went straight into Yun Family Town to lie low.

Besides gathering some information, they couldn't offer much help, at most just relaying intelligence to command.

“Jiang Tian, ​​I…” Gao Yangyang took a deep breath, then looked up. Her face was filthy, but her eyes seemed to hold stars, as if she had made a firm decision.

Separation by death.

When Gao Yangyang faced the ambush and the desperate breakout, the only person on her mind was Jiang Tian.

She even genuinely thought she wouldn't make it back and would die here.

If she were caught by the drug traffickers, she would have saved a bullet beforehand, a bullet she kept for herself before their capture.

Because she knew what the consequences would be if the drug traffickers caught her.

This experience had finally made Gao Yangyang see her own heart clearly; at the very least, she needed to express her feelings to Jiang Tian.

“Let’s go find them first,” Jiang Tian said, pulling Gao Yangyang back.

Jiang Tian was in no mood to rest now. Although the battle was progressing smoothly, the situation on the mountain was still dire; a large number of drug traffickers remained surrounding them.

Yunjia Town has a complex environment, surrounded by dense forests, making it easy for someone unfamiliar with the area to get lost.

This is actually why Yunjia has always been difficult to manage; if they can't win a fight, they simply disappear into the mountains, becoming impossible to find.

Especially in the deep forests where there's almost no signal, phone calls are practically impossible.

Of course, Jiang Tian had a good memory, so he didn't get lost in the mountains. Gao Yangyang had also traversed the area beforehand, leading Jiang Tian back to the breakout point.

During this time, Jiang Tian even discovered several red dots. For him, these were not only points of merit and crime value, but also sources of intelligence, making him ruthless in his killings.

Therefore, Jiang Tian was surprised to find that...

Normal locations might be difficult to find, but through these scattered or escaped drug dealers, he had found clues.

He first used scanning radar to locate them, and then, through them, found Li Qishan and his group.

In this way, Jiang Tian's search for clues was incredibly fast.

He navigated the mountain, making countless twists and turns.

After crossing three mountain passes, Jiang Tian could faintly hear gunfire.

And during this time, Jiang Tian even discovered some police corpses along the way.

"Li Qishan truly deserves to die," Jiang Tian thought, his eyes reddening.



Chapter 278 Insolence! Even now, you still dare to shift the blame?


Before him lay a young and all too familiar corpse. It was Jiang Tian's former colleague, a year older than him, young and full of youthful vigor.

He should have had a bright future; he'd even reportedly set a wedding date. Now, he was dead.

All of this was because of Li Qishan.

The closer Jiang Tian got to the source of the gunfire, the heavier his heart became, as more and more police corpses littered the ground.

Hundreds of red dots appeared on Jiang Tian's radar. Clearly, these were members of the Yun family. Because of the lack of signal in the mountains, they were unaware that the Yun family had been captured.

"Wait for me here," Jiang Tian said with a touch of heartache to Gao Yangyang, who was panting heavily.

Gao Yangyang's face was now a mess of ashes, drenched in sweat, making her pretty face even more disheveled.

Yet, she didn't look bad at all; in fact, she looked rather cute.

Of course, don't assume Gao Yangyang lacks ability because of this; quite the opposite.

She crossed three mountains.

She escaped for her life from here. A normal person's legs would probably cramp after crossing just one mountain, let alone three, breaking through to Yunjia Town.

It's not that Gao Yangyang couldn't hide in the deep forests, but in these primeval jungles, deviating from normal routes, the chances of death were much higher than facing Yunjia head-on. On the other hand, Gao Yangyang was able to relay intelligence after reaching Yunjia Town.

Then, without stopping, Gao Yangyang followed Jiang Tian back, traversing mountains and valleys almost without pause. To have persisted this long is already a miracle for an ordinary person.

“Wait, here's the thermal imaging camera.” Gao Yangyang took off the thermal imaging camera from her head.

“No need, you keep it.”

Jiang Tian shook his head. He needed the thermal imaging camera for positioning before, but not now. With his Eye of Judgment, these armed drug traffickers were like live targets in the dark.

It was brighter and more dazzling than thermal imaging.

“Me too,” Gao Yangyang said, enduring her exhaustion as she followed Jiang Tian.

Jiang Tian remained silent, carefully lurking ahead. Finally, at a distance of about a hundred meters, they were in the firefight zone.

Ahead of Jiang Tian, ​​the drug dealers standing behind the trees became easy targets.

While the two sides were still exchanging fire, Jiang Tian simply drew two pistols.

With complete freedom.

Previously, his abilities weren't at their peak, and he hadn't been able to control the two guns, but now, with all his stats perfectly optimized, he could instantly kill more than ten people with just two guns.

So, the next second.

Eleven men appeared before Jiang Tian at an angle.

Almost instantly, a burst of gunfire erupted, and these eleven men fell to the ground.

At the very center of the encirclement.

Li Qishan was now in a sorry state, looking at the people around him, his heart sinking.

They arrived with four or five hundred men.

After being surrounded and ambushed along the way, and being unfamiliar with the area, some got separated and others were killed in the mountains.

Only a little over a hundred remained near Li Qishan.

They were now hiding behind a hillside, desperately fending off the attacks of the Yun family drug traffickers.

Originally, they had surrounded the drug traffickers, but instead, the criminal group had surrounded them instead.

"We have to find a way to break through; there's no way out," Li Qishan gritted his teeth, looking around.

Behind the hillside was a bottomless abyss; in other words, they had nowhere to run.

A dead end.

"Captain Guo, take some men up to draw the drug traffickers' attention," Li Qishan ordered Captain Guo beside him.

Captain Guo's face darkened, but he said nothing, checking his ammunition and glancing at the familiar faces around him.

This operation had been extremely costly.

Countless casualties.

That's for sure.

During the Tucha operation, Jiang Tian's presence meant fewer casualties.

But this time, it was an unprecedented loss in Yunhai, or even in the entire country's history, in decades of devastation.

The kind of loss that would shake the entire nation if reported.

This caused Li Qishan to swallow back his prepared remarks.

Then, a smile appeared on Li Qishan's face. "Captain Guo performed admirably, bravely and fearlessly. He's a stark contrast to that Captain Gao from Kyoto, the one leading the team. If they hadn't deserted, how could we have suffered such a rout? Don't worry, everyone. When we get back, I will definitely recommend you for commendations. Those who sacrificed their lives will have their families taken care of by the country. Those who return alive will all receive their due credit. Defeat isn't terrible; cowardice is shameless. This isn't our fault."

Unfortunately, Li Qishan's words received little response. Everyone was currently gripped by immense grief. Too many had perished; too many familiar faces had been lost or killed.

And their current situation was almost a matter of life and death.

Captain Guo, watching Li Qishan shift the blame, almost laughed in anger. He had caused them such a terrible ordeal, and now he had the nerve to shift the blame? Were they not going to break out? Were they going to follow him to their deaths?

However, seeing the familiar faces of the police officers, Captain Guo endured the pain. In this situation, someone had to sacrifice themselves to draw fire and allow the others to break through.

Otherwise, they would all perish here.

“Captain Guo, follow me and draw fire from the right!” Captain Guo gritted his teeth and led his men outwards.

Instantly, bullets rained down like a storm.

Captain Guo took cover behind a tree; the tree in front of him was almost completely destroyed, splinters flying everywhere.

The enemy firepower was terrifyingly intense.

But just then…

A burst of intense gunfire erupted, and the enemy's firepower decreased.

Before Captain Guo could react, even more intense gunfire erupted, but not towards them, but towards the armed drug traffickers.

Amidst the intense gunfire,

a loudspeaker announced: “We are the Chinese police! Comrades, hold on! We're coming to rescue you!”

BOOM!!!

Those who had almost given up hope were on the verge of tears.

They had suddenly gone from despair to paradise.

And then a sound rang out.

The armed police, who had sprung out from behind the drug dealers, were practically harvesting their prey.

Jiang Tian watched the crime score skyrocket, a relieved smile spreading across his face.

Beside him, Song Liguo, panting heavily, spoke haltingly, "Luckily you managed to communicate. Any later, and these guys would probably be wiped out."

When Jiang Tian found a clue along the way, he had already sent the location's latitude and longitude to Song Liguo via satellite phone.

Song Liguo didn't even have a second to doubt it. Based on the location, he charged at full speed, rushing forward as fast as he could. Just as Jiang Tian had taken down over thirty drug dealers, and the dealers reacted by firing at him,

Song Liguo's arrival caught them off guard, completely stunning them, or rather, driving them into a frenzy.

When they turned their guns on Song Liguo,

the next moment...

Captain Guo was pleasantly surprised to find that the drug dealers were now armed with their backs to them.

Then came the firing from three directions.

The killing was too fast.

After reinforcements arrived, the last wave of Yun family drug dealers was completely wiped out in less than ten minutes.

Only a small number survived and were captured.

Li Qishan, having eliminated the danger, slowly emerged from the back, hands behind his back, craning his neck as if inspecting the group.

The next moment...

*Slap!*



Chapter 279 Utterly insane, daring to dump even the granddaughter of a high-ranking official?


Li Qishan's face was expressionless as he emerged from the dark depths of the jungle with the others. The excitement of surviving the ordeal didn't show on his face; only a somber expression remained.

To this day, he didn't believe he had done anything wrong.

Before his promotion, as the branch bureau chief, Li Qishan had a deep understanding of the industry. In fact, it was precisely because of his previous role as bureau chief that he had in-depth knowledge of these drug cartels and foreign forces.

No matter how powerful the Yun family was, they were ultimately just a criminal organization. Against legitimate forces, there was only one path: death.

Now that victory was assured, Li Qishan had many more things to consider: how to gain greater credit and become a benchmark for drug enforcement and even for the entire Yunhai area.

Now, thanks to Jiang Tian's vigorous efforts, Yunhai had become a major focus of the national police system.

It was even on the verge of becoming a star area.

Jiang Tian never showed himself, and outsiders were quite curious about the situation in Yunhai. Li Qishan possessed sufficient personal ability and experience.

Therefore, after discussing with Leader Jing, Li Qishan reached a conclusion: securing a breakthrough in this crucial mission, and once the massive Yun family criminal syndicate was destroyed, Li Qishan would instantly have all the advantages in the world.

However, this wasn't satisfactory for Li Qishan, because he clearly understood that a steady, methodical approach would only earn him a passing grade at best.

To achieve higher scores, he needed to make a decisive move. Coincidentally, Li Qishan thought of his undercover subordinate from years ago.

This brings us to the fact that almost 70-80% of Yunhai's crimes stemmed from overseas crimes or smuggling, various illegal activities abroad, and even the support of proxies.

Li Qishan understood that this was his opportunity—an opportunity to become famous, an opportunity to become Yunhai's representative. Once he had this halo,

in a sense, he was reaping the biggest rewards by stepping on Jiang Tian's shoulders.

However, it could also be attributed to Li Qishan's insatiable greed and reckless ambition.

But Li Qishan didn't consider this his fault.

Coincidentally.

At this moment, Jiang Tian had just found Li Zijin and Zhao Wei.

Fortunately, although the two were somewhat disheveled, they seemed to be in relatively good condition overall, at least not seriously injured.

"Captain Jiang."

"You've arrived, Captain Jiang."

"Luckily you came, otherwise we might be finished this time."

Zhao Wei was still shaken in front of Jiang Tian.

"Be careful next time, pay attention." Jiang Tian nodded slightly in response, then asked seriously, "Where are you all?"

Jiang Tian glanced around and found that the losses at the scene were excessively heavy.

The Kyoto Armed Police, along with local armed police and the anti-drug squad, totaled nearly five hundred people, but now there were only a little over a hundred left at the scene.

At this point, Zhao Wei's face darkened. "Captain Jiang, many have perished, and some have gone missing. I don't know if we can find them."

As expected.

Jiang Tian had been hoping to split up the forces, but now that hope was completely gone.

Thinking this, Jiang Tian looked grimly at Li Qishan not far away.

Especially at this moment, Li Qishan showed no remorse whatsoever; instead, he was pointing fingers and gesturing wildly.

"Song Liguo, what have you done? Why was the support so slow? Who will take responsibility for delaying the battle? Did you expect us all to be sacrificed before you arrived?"

Li Qishan berated Song Liguo, who was tallying the missing and killed.

"We arrived very quickly," Song Liguo replied, his face grim.

"We were trapped last night, and it took you a whole day and night to send reinforcements. Who gave you the order? We were killed in action at the front; is someone behind the scenes pulling strings, engaging in political infighting, deliberately trying to kill me off here?" Li Qishan shouted.

The atmosphere at the scene was already rather quiet.

Although the drug dealers had been dealt with, the number of casualties was too high; no one could be happy.

"Song Liguo, I order you to immediately take men to the vicinity to hunt down the fleeing drug dealers and search for the missing teammates," Li Qishan ordered Song Liguo after a slight pause.

Ultimately, regardless of rank or position, Li Qishan was the leader.

However, Li Qishan's condescending attitude and his complete lack of awareness of his mistake angered Song Liguo slightly.

"Why aren't you going?" Li Qishan's face was somewhat gloomy.

Now was the perfect time to press the advantage.

Jiang Tian, ​​standing behind, couldn't stand it any longer.

He had already reached his limit of patience with Li Qishan, and now Li Qishan's order only fueled his anger.

Was now the time to press the advantage?

Those drug dealers were all locals, unfamiliar with this place. Pressing the advantage might result in heavy losses.

If a search was really necessary, they should wait until the Third Special Zone arrived.

But the next moment, before Jiang Tian could speak, Li Qishan seemed to have noticed something. He walked up to Jiang Tian with his hands behind his back, but instead of looking at Jiang Tian, ​​he focused his gaze on Gao Yangyang, who was standing beside Jiang Tian.

"Gao Yangyang, why didn't I see you after the battle? Do you know how heinous desertion is?"

Li Qishan showed no remorse for his actions; instead, he was blaming others.

Gao Yangyang was stunned by the question: "You led us into the encirclement. Were we supposed to just wait to die?"

These words...

seemed to have completely enraged Li Qishan.

The next moment, Li Qishan raised his hand to slap Gao Yangyang.

The movement was sudden.

And quick.

Gao Yangyang herself didn't even have time to react, frozen in place, as Li Qishan's slap drew closer and closer to her face.

"You're a coward, yet you have the nerve to talk nonsense."

Li Qishan seemed to have been struck where it hurt; the slap was swift and forceful.

One can imagine the extent of the damage this slap would cause if it landed on Gao Yangyang's face.

"Director Li, what are you trying to do?" Zhao Wei's hair stood on end.

When Song Liguo, following behind, turned his head and saw this scene in the bright moonlight, his scalp tingled.

Because, that was Chief Gao's granddaughter!

Li Qishan, have you gone mad?

Song Liguo's head was buzzing.

He knew Li Qishan was desperate to shift the blame, and the best target was Gao Yangyang, the youngest, seemingly easy to bully, and most importantly, the first to break through the encirclement, who was leading the Kyoto team.

But if it were an ordinary person, this kind of blame-shifting might have a chance of success.

But you're blind as hell, shifting the blame to Chief Gao's granddaughter!

Are you out of your mind to try and shift the blame like this?



Chapter 280 Li Qishan was stunned by the slap. You dare to lay a hand on me?


Song Liguo's scalp tingled with fear. He couldn't even imagine that Gao Yangyang, whom he had painstakingly rescued, had been beaten by the very person she had rescued.

The worst part was that it was someone who had been eager for credit and acted rashly who had been beaten—what would the consequences be?

But when he looked up,

what appeared before him was

Jiang Tian standing expressionlessly beside Gao Yangyang.

The slap didn't land; it stopped about ten centimeters away from Gao Yangyang because Jiang Tian had firmly gripped his wrist.

"Jiang Tian, ​​you dare to lay a hand on me?" Li Qishan said calmly.

The next moment...

*Slap!!!*

Jiang Tian slapped Li Qishan across the face.

The slap was incredibly hard.

It sent Li Qishan flying, his feet leaving the ground, his body spinning half a circle before he collapsed to the ground.

*Whoosh!!!*

With that slap, everyone present froze.

Those tending to the wounded stopped what they were doing, staring in astonishment at the scene before them.

But whatever they were doing, everyone stopped what they were doing.

Under everyone's watchful eyes.
