Li Qishan, sprawled on the ground, covered his face in disbelief, his mouth gushing blood.

"You dare hit me?" Li Qishan was utterly shocked.

He, a deputy bureau chief, had been slapped by that damn Jiang Tian?

Was he insane?

Unexpectedly, the next moment, Jiang Tian slapped him again.

A loud, dull thud echoed, and Li Qishan's head spun.

He'd been a leader for so long, always in a superior position, everyone treated him with utmost respect, and yet he'd been beaten?

At this moment, Jiang Tian sneered, "The first slap was for the comrades you led to their deaths. The second slap was for Officer Gao's desperate breakout, his immediate call for rescue, and his tireless efforts to cross mountains and valleys to bring people to your rescue, yet you turn around and accuse me of the same thing."

Jiang Tian was truly enraged.

Li Qishan really dared to treat people like fools.

Moreover, did Li Qishan not know how big this incident had become?

Who was overseeing and directing everything?

Don't even mention that Gao Yangyang is Chief Gao's granddaughter; even if she were just an ordinary person with no background, the top leaders in Beijing are all too aware of what happened, who made the mistake, and who deserved credit.

Trying to shift the blame?

Under normal circumstances, Jiang Tian couldn't act, but he understood that Li Qishan was finished.

Completely finished.

This serious mistake wasn't just about skinning someone alive.

This was due to a personal mistake—acting without authorization and disobeying organizational orders—leading to serious consequences. To put it bluntly, if thoroughly investigated, he could even face execution.

But Li Qishan seemed completely oblivious to this.

No, perhaps he did have some idea, but he simply hadn't considered the severity of the situation, nor the devastating impact of his failed operation.

The immense loss to the entire nation.

"Fine, fine, Jiang Tian, ​​you're quite something. You even dared to hit the bureau chief. What wouldn't you dare do? You think you can get away with anything just because you've achieved a little bit of merit? Let me tell you, I will report this matter truthfully to the leadership. You can expect to be dismissed." Li Qishan's face was icy cold.

However, seeing countless people surrounding Jiang Tian and ignoring everyone else, Li Qishan's anger flared even more.

He didn't believe it was a mistake in his command.

If Gao Yangyang hadn't left, they might not have been routed by these drug dealers. By then, they might have already completely wiped out the Yun family's stronghold, and the credit would be theirs long ago.

Although he had made a mistake, he figured he could atone for it with merit.

Li Qishan pondered this in his mind, his eyes fierce as he glared at Jiang Tian. After a moment's hesitation, he ultimately refrained from uttering any more threats.

Jiang Tian gave him a cold glance and completely ignored him.

His arrogance only lasted until now.

After descending the mountain, the signal was restored.

Li Qishan realized just how serious his mistake had been.

"Like a dog biting Lü Dongbin," Gao Yangyang almost laughed in anger.

She was nearly exhausted from trying to get reinforcements.

To be honest, without Gao Yangyang, even if Jiang Tian wanted to find her, it would probably take much longer, and by then, countless more people would likely have been sacrificed.

“Don’t worry, they won’t be arrogant for long. Minister Tan personally intervened and directed things this time. He knows exactly what happened. You deserve the most credit,” Jiang Tian’s tone softened considerably.

Jiang Tian’s feelings towards Gao Yangyang were also softening.

Actually, when he thought about it occasionally, he realized that he and Gao Yangyang seemed quite compatible.

Especially looking at Gao Yangyang now, though covered in dirt, she looked even more adorable. Jiang Tian couldn’t help but glance at her a few more times.

“Look openly if you want,” Gao Yangyang leaned closer. “Thank you for earlier, I’ll reward you when we get back.”

Reward?

Jiang Tian’s heart skipped a beat, and he glanced at her discreetly.

Gao Yangyang was tall and slender, a typical northern girl. Even without heels, she was at least 1.7 meters tall.

Given her attire, Jiang Tian’s heart skipped a beat.

"Business comes first."

Jiang Tian quickly rolled his eyes; there were more important things to do now.

Li Qishan was hateful, but his teammates and comrades in this operation were innocent!

There were quite a few wounded at the scene, more than ten people shot, and several already dead, their bodies laid out.

Jiang Tian knelt down and closed the unseeing eyes of the comrade at his feet.

Although Jiang Tian had become almost indifferent to killing criminal gangs and drug dealers,

he felt nothing at all.

However, seeing his close colleague lifeless before his eyes still weighed heavily on his heart.

Song Liguo walked up behind Jiang Tian at some point, patted Jiang Tian on the shoulder and said, "Working in this line of work is like licking blood off a knife. It's been like this since we started. When I graduated, the organization gave me two choices: Yunhai or Zhenshi. All my classmates who were in the anti-drug department chose Zhenshi, but I chose Yunhai. Only after coming here did I realize how shocking the criminals are, and how ruthless those drug dealers and addicts are for drugs. But, from the moment we chose this path, there was no chance to regret it."

Song Liguo was giving Jiang Tian psychological counseling.

He was worried that Jiang Tian might be developing psychological problems.

However, it was clear that Song Liguo was overthinking it.

“It’s nothing, just Branch Chief Song, be quicker next time. If it weren’t for waiting for you, I would have taken care of all these drug dealers by myself,” Jiang Tian laughed.

“Tch.”

Song Liguo just thought Jiang Tian was bragging, but he knew Jiang Tian was fine and could even joke around.

Meanwhile, Li Qishan, who was watching this scene from afar, had an even more gloomy expression.

As the deputy director, Song Liguo’s blatant disobedience, even ignoring his words, had made him lose face and had already enraged him.

Jiang Tian even dared to slap him twice. If he didn’t do something, how could he lead the Municipal Bureau forward, how could he manage the Municipal Bureau?

What kind of leader was he then?



Chapter 281 President Tan arrived in person, and even President Tan was stunned.


Amidst a mix of emotions—some grieving, some anticipating the impending credit, and others harboring ulterior motives—Song Liguo disregarded Li Qishan's advice and led his men back to Yunjia Town.

In truth, Song Liguo's ability to catch up was largely due to Jiang Tian's continuous updates on location and emergency support.

Otherwise, it would have taken Song Liguo at least several hours to arrive, by which time it would have been too late.

Even Jiang Tian, ​​relying on Gao Yangyang's memories and his mental scanning radar to locate the drug dealers' coordinates, and then meticulously following interrogation leads, had taken almost no detours, heading straight for their target, which allowed him to arrive at the crucial moment.

If he had been even a step slower...

For one thing, Captain Guo would almost certainly have been sacrificed.

"In that situation, a tight security perimeter was clearly the right thing to do. With hundreds of drug dealers surrounding them, going out would have been suicide. What were you thinking, Captain Guo?" Jiang Tian asked Captain Guo, curious about the situation.

Within the police force, he was arguably the person he knew best.

Generally speaking, Captain Guo was a veteran narcotics officer, almost entirely uninvolved in other matters, dedicating all his time to searching for clues, finding evidence, and arresting drug dealers.

Because of this, when Jiang Tian was mediocre, Captain Guo paid him little attention. After his rebirth, Jiang Tian's performance completely won over Captain Guo, and their relationship had always been quite good.

But Captain Guo's face darkened at the mention of this.

Zhao Wei, before Captain Guo could finish, recounted the situation.

"They didn't hit him hard enough," Jiang Tian said through gritted teeth.

Don't be fooled by how well this mission was completed; the losses were something no one in the entire Yunhai narcotics enforcement department could afford.

Because so far, the number of people who have sacrificed their lives recently has been too high.

They were already severely short-staffed, and this loss has dealt a heavy blow.

However, it was just as they were retreating down the mountain...

Even Jiang Tian didn't know.

This demonstrates just how serious and terrifying the situation is for those at higher levels.

A helicopter gradually descended from the border.

And at this very moment...

A large number of armed police and even military personnel had gathered at the border.

Jiang Peng and Gao Xiaolong stood at the border crossing.

The entire operation had entered its final, crucial stage.

Although the Yun family had been wiped out, the cost to the country was far greater than imagined.

The Yun family, in the grand scheme of things domestically, is insignificant.

Even wiping them out would only reduce the likelihood of crime and serve as a deterrent to other criminal organizations and groups.

In other words, in the face of the state apparatus, destroying the Yun family is nothing.

However, the problems that arise have serious consequences.

Moreover, the most crucial aspect is the losses incurred in this operation.

Even Minister Tan couldn't remain silent.

He personally flew from Kyoto directly to Yunhai to oversee the situation.

Gao Xiaolong and Jiang Peng stood at the border crossing, their gazes extremely serious.

At this time, the sky was already beginning to lighten.

The morning light was gradually spreading.

Gao Xiaolong squinted, his face grave.

There was no trace of excitement about the successful mission on his face.

Beside Gao Xiaolong were Zhou Zhao and the top official in charge of Yunhai's administration.

Almost all the leaders were present.

The helicopter landed, and a moment later, Minister Tan, with his entourage, stepped off, his face grim.

Upon seeing Gao Xiaolong, he shook hands slightly, then glanced at the assembled leaders, offering only a nod in greeting.

"How is the situation?" Minister Tan asked in a low voice.

"We have just communicated with the leaders of the Third Special Zone, and their personnel have entered the mountains to assist us in searching for our lost comrades," Gao Xiaolong replied immediately, his gaze shifting to another group of leaders outside the gate not far away.

Minister Tan nodded expressionlessly. At this moment, Minister Tan was in a very bad mood, extremely bad.

For him...

This operation was simply a joint operation.

No matter how powerful a criminal group is, it's no match for a regular army.

However, the final result was almost unacceptable to Minister Tan.

“The main problem is that the surrounding area is a primeval jungle, making it easy to get lost. Even locals who go in have a hard time getting out if they enter unfamiliar territory. This has caused great difficulties for the search and rescue operation. However, we have made some progress. Yunhai firmly states that we will spare no effort to rescue our comrades in the operation.”

Zhou Zhao stepped forward to express his stance.

“That's for the best.” Minister Tan didn't even blink.

He had heard too many such empty words. What good were statements? What could they help? This wasn't a problem in Yunhai; it was in neighboring Myanmar.

“Can we contact them now?” Minister Tan asked Gao Xiaolong.

The headquarters was in Kyoto, which was too far away. After everyone went into the mountains, contact between the two sides was lost.

Even if there was contact, it was from outside the mountains, completely unaware of the situation inside. However, Minister Tan arrived at the scene immediately, taking a pre-arranged helicopter.

If it weren't for the special circumstances of being overseas, he would have even gone directly to the crime scene by helicopter.

"We've made contact," Gao Xiaolong nodded.

This was a stroke of luck amidst misfortune.

Even Minister Tan's serious face instantly relaxed upon hearing this.

Because he knew very well that most of the drug dealers wiped out at the scene were merely supporting the various criminal groups affiliated with the Yun family; the core members of the Yun family had all gone to besiege them.

Such a major incident—if Minister Tan hadn't come personally, that would have been problematic.

"What do you mean?" Minister Tan asked, surprised.

Gao Xiaolong's face was somewhat grave.

Seeing Gao Xiaolong's expression, Minister Tan, who had worked with him for many years, instantly froze, his heart skipping a beat, an ominous premonition rising within him.

Just then, a noisy but extremely excited voice suddenly erupted from the crowd.

"They're here!"

"Look, they're here."

"It's our police car."

"They're out! Great!"

Minister Tan turned his head and, sure enough, saw a large number of police cars appearing on the road behind the toll gate.

The sirens could be heard clearly from a great distance.

The next moment, as the toll gate opened, the police cars passed through.

They drove up to them, stopped, and everyone got out.

Minister Tan's expression, while initially showing concern, grew increasingly astonished.

Because...



Chapter 282 Sharing the Burden with Those in the Same boat: The Tragic Leader


*Click!*

With the sound of car doors opening, one after another, sleeping figures were lifted from the vehicles.

That's right.

Almost all the casualties encountered during the retreat had been rescued.

At this moment, bodies were being unloaded from the vehicles.

The road was already lined with ambulances, dozens at a glance—almost half of Yunhai's ambulances and medical personnel were waiting behind them.

Upon seeing the wounded and the deceased, the medical personnel rushed over.

But unfortunately.

The number of injured was not large.

"Director Liao."

The nurse looked at a already stiff corpse, her heart trembling slightly.

She turned to look at Director Liao of the emergency department.

As a top student who frequently skipped grades in her youth, Liao Ying's genius was undeniable. She didn't look at the young nurse, but instead slowly covered the faces of the corpses before her with a white sheet.

"Hurry and treat the wounded."

The scene was chaotic. Medical personnel searched for the injured one by one, and those who hadn't lost vital signs were immediately covered with white sheets.

So, after the initial flurry of activity,

Liao Ying looked back. On the ground, one or two hundred corpses were neatly laid out, covered with white sheets. These people had all lost vital signs; in other words, they couldn't be saved.

And among them, Liao Ying saw many young faces.

She didn't know what this operation was about.

She didn't know what they were doing.

But she knew that a group had recently sacrificed their lives, and now, even more had perished.

Around the corpses, a crowd stood, all with their heads bowed, silent and speechless.

Minister Tan stood at the front of the crowd, silently staring at the corpses on the ground, but a fire burned fiercely in his eyes.

If it were a strategic blunder that caused such a massive and horrific casualty, they would be undeniably responsible.

But that wasn't the case.

All of this was caused by a single act of disobeying orders and acting without authorization.

"Minister,"

"The count is in. One hundred and eighty comrades have been confirmed dead at the scene."

At this moment,

Jiang Peng returned to Minister Tan's side, his face heavy with grief, and said in a low voice.

Minister Tan's heart sank.

As expected.

The scene was horrific. Even though he had mentally prepared himself, seeing so many vibrant comrades now lying dormant here weighed heavily on his heart.

"What about those who weren't at the scene?" Minister Tan took a deep breath.

"A total of one thousand armed police officers were deployed. Six hundred and fifty returned. The number of missing and killed has reached three hundred and fifty," Jiang Peng said.

Minister Tan's mind went blank.

Such a heavy loss of officers and soldiers is almost unprecedented.

This is something our motherland has almost never experienced before.

Except during wartime, in peacetime, the number of casualties lost in a single incident has reached such a terrifying, shocking figure. Three hundred and fifty may not seem like much, but each and every one of them is a hero of our motherland, a talent of our nation.

Moreover, they are talents carefully nurtured by our motherland!

There was no joy of victory, only rage.

“Who acted without authorization?” Gao Xiaolong stepped forward, his face expressionless as he surveyed the room.

Everyone lowered their heads.

As for Li Qishan, his eyes were twitching rapidly.

In truth, the moment he saw Minister Tan, Gao Xiaolong, and even almost the entire top leadership of Yunhai mobilized, Li Qishan felt a chill run down his spine.

He knew this operation would likely have a significant impact, but he never dreamed it would be this terrifying.

After a moment's hesitation,

Li Qishan looked pleadingly at Zhou Zhao.

Because it was Zhou Zhao who had introduced him.

But he didn't know that at this very moment, Zhou Zhao's forehead was already covered in cold sweat.

The cold sweat streamed down his forehead and behind his ears like a waterfall.

When he saw Li Qishan looking at him, Zhou Zhao quickly turned his head away.

"Minister and Chief Gao, I made some mistakes this time. I entered through a small path in the back mountain based on information from my undercover agent and engaged the Yun family in a skirmish. However, it wasn't entirely my fault, because the people from Kyoto deserted at the crucial moment, leading to our utter defeat."

Li Qishan stepped forward and hurriedly said.

"Kyoto deserted at the last minute?"

Gao Xiaolong and Minister Tan exchanged a glance, then their gazes turned almost icy as they looked at Li Qishan. Unbeknownst to anyone, a fierce fire of anger was burning deep within their eyes.

In Li Qishan's mind, while there were problems, they weren't numerous. To this day, he remained convinced that his thinking was correct.

Because even with the support of the Third Special Zone, a frontal assault would be fraught with difficulty; a silent, surprise attack from the rear would be far more effective.

How much less damage would this reduce?

He wasn't an incompetent fool. He had already analyzed the terrain. Those at the lower levels might not understand his brilliant strategic thinking, but Li Qishan believed that these leaders surely grasped his strategic thinking.

If successful, the Yun family could be wiped out almost effortlessly, further preventing the potential casualties of a frontal assault.

Even following the established procedures would only yield a passing grade at best; he wanted a perfect score.

After analyzing the situation in his mind, Li Qishan, feeling confident that he had succeeded, stepped forward and continued explaining, "Minister, they are all heroes. If it weren't for someone being a coward..."

*Slap!*

Before Li Qishan could finish speaking, Gao Xiaolong, unable to contain himself, slapped him across the face.

Li Qishan's already swollen face instantly turned even redder, a clear handprint imprinted on his cheek.

"You mean, Yangyang's cowardice caused your great achievement to fail?" Gao Xiaolong sneered.

Li Qishan's heart skipped a beat.

He actually knew Gao Yangyang's identity when he was at the Municipal Bureau, but knowing was one thing, knowing was another. Li Qishan understood that while he was wrong, someone else was also at fault.

He admitted his mistake and accepted punishment, but how would Gao Yangyang, a deserter from the battlefield, be punished?

Li Qishan wasn't stupid; he had already calculated everything perfectly in his mind.

However, he underestimated those present, especially the leaders, each of whom was a seasoned elite who had spent years navigating the system.

Li Qishan's idea was to drag everyone down with him and accept the punishment, which amused and angered Minister Tan and Gao Xiaolong.

"Sir, that's the truth. As the person in charge, I felt it necessary to use a more convenient method. And the investigation revealed it wasn't a betrayal by the undercover agent; it was just a chance encounter with that group of drug dealers," Li Qishan continued to argue.

He believed his orders were correct.



Chapter 283 Statistical Analysis of Losses: Creating a Historic Era of Horrific Losses


Minister Tan's gaze towards Li Qishan was icy, then his voice grew increasingly loud: "Before coming here, I thoroughly understood the causes and consequences of this mission. Although I know that now is not the time to hold anyone accountable, the problem is so egregious that I must say this: this Li Qishan, regardless of whose side he's on, who recommended him, or what he's done in the past, has dragged all the comrades in Yunhai into an abyss. Because of his impulsiveness, we have lost countless comrades, causing serious losses to the country and the people."

"Furthermore, he has severely damaged the reputation of our motherland. His actions are exceptionally heinous, and the consequences are extremely serious. Behind me are comrades from the Beijing Municipal Commission for Discipline Inspection. Before coming here, they had already decided that this operation would pursue Li Qishan to the end, whether he is alive or dead."

Minister Tan's voice was extremely loud.

But his voice instantly brought a deathly silence to the scene.

Everyone's heart almost involuntarily stopped beating.

Because, at this moment, everyone understood that Minister Tan was truly enraged.

As Minister Tan finished speaking, four or five men in suits with badges on their chests stepped out from behind him.

They approached Li Qishan.

Li Qishan's mind went blank, completely dazed.

"Leader," Li Qishan struggled.

But he underestimated the devastating consequences of this operation.

Even Zhou Zhao was sweating profusely.

"Comrade Jiang, we've learned the details of the mission. If it weren't for your timely intervention, we don't know how much damage it would have caused to the country and the people."

At this moment,

Minister Tan, after Li Qishan was taken away, went directly to Jiang Tian.

Jiang Tian hadn't spoken since arriving, because he knew Gao Yangyang would be alright.

Jiang Tian had already thoroughly learned the details of the mission through Gao Yangyang.

The situation was already dire; the mountains were teeming with drug dealers, and they were already within the encirclement.

Upon entering the Yun family's territory, they were immediately detected by the Yun family. Unfortunately, Li Qishan was still hoping to deliver a perfect performance.

Seeing this, Jiang Tian understood.

Li Qishan treated this mission as a gamble. If successful, he could reap the rewards from Jiang Tian.

He would become a hero in Yunhai, but the final result was failure.

"Look, isn't that Officer Jiang who was hospitalized with us before?"

In the neat ranks, Jiang Tian stood before Minister Tan. This was the first time the two had faced each other.

Minister Tan had heard Jiang Tian's name countless times.

The creator of miracles.

Based on past events, Jiang Tian even gave people an unprecedented sense of security, as if there was no problem that Jiang Tian couldn't solve.

Strategic wisdom, personal combat skills—all-around, almost omnipotent.

This was the greatest feeling that Jiang Tian brought to people.

At that moment, a man in his thirties was having his wound treated. He was a member of the Second Squad of the Narcotics Control Detachment, and he had been shot in the arm. A young nurse was assisting Director Liao in treating his wound.

When Jiang Tian stepped out of the line, the young nurse pointed at him in surprise.

Liao Ying also turned to look, and sure enough, she saw him.

Jiang Tian, ​​after the battle, had his arm re-slinged around his neck.

Actually, although Jiang Tian's injury was healing quickly according to the blue indicator, the frequent shooting had caused the wound to reopen and worsen slightly.

On the other hand, keeping it slinged around his neck helped with recovery, which Jiang Tian could clearly feel, so he hadn't removed it.

"He also participated in this dangerous mission?" Liao Ying looked at Jiang Tian with surprise.

"He hasn't been to the hospital for several days, except for changing his dressing. With such a serious gunshot wound, how could he still be on a mission?" the young nurse frowned.

“We need to report this to the police station. Violent actions can easily worsen wounds, and if it gets infected, amputation might be necessary,” Liao Ying said, her anger rising.

The anti-drug squad member whose wound was being bandaged, hearing their conversation, quickly spoke up: “Doctor, if it weren’t for Team Leader Jiang, we might have lost at least a hundred or two more brothers in this mission. So Team Leader Jiang had no choice but to act.”

What?

Liao Ying’s eyes widened as she looked at the injured team member: “Why are you calling him team leader?”

She could tell this team member was quite old; some people even called him captain. He was definitely an old man. Wasn’t Jiang Tian only in his early twenties when he was admitted to the hospital?

Back then at the hospital, people seemed to call Jiang Tian captain!

How did he become team leader?

"Oh, Team Leader Jiang used to be a squad leader in our anti-drug squad. Yesterday, he was promoted again, transferred to the special emergency team as its leader. If you have any leads on drug dealers, you can't go wrong with Officer Jiang. In this world, there's no drug dealer that Team Leader Jiang can't handle." The team member lying on the ground spoke of Jiang Tian with eyes full of admiration.

Because he was one of the last hundred or so survivors from Li Qishan's group, he knew clearly that if it weren't for Jiang Tian, ​​he would probably be dead by now.

His body would probably be cold by now.

How could he be enjoying the glory of success like this?

A strange light flashed in Liao Ying's eyes. That scumbag, could he really be this capable?

Especially after learning that Jiang Tian had burned down 100,000 acres of poppy fields, this team member was completely conquered by Jiang Tian.

And he wasn't an isolated case.

Previously, some members of the brigade were resentful of Jiang Tian's rapid and unconventional promotions. While he had merit, they felt his promotions were too swift, and given his young age—some forty-year-olds were still working at the grassroots level—they naturally resented Jiang Tian's rapid advancement at just twenty.

But after today's events, these people finally understood.

Because in the final battle, Jiang Tian truly was like a god of death.

The issues that followed became extremely complex.

This incident had escalated far too much.

The scale was enormous; no one dared to conceal it. Nearly 350 people were killed or missing—more than 350 comrades!

This was a case of unprecedented severity in decades, its nature utterly heinous.

Meanwhile, in Kyoto, urgent negotiations were underway with the Third Special District, dispatching a professional rescue team to join forces with the Third Special District's personnel in searching for those missing in the mountains.

In addition, a large number of Yun family drug dealers had been arrested, and these also required negotiation.

The Third Special District, in particular, wanted to detain the Yun family drug dealers there for interrogation. However, through various negotiations, an agreement was ultimately reached: after the interrogation in Yunhai was completed, the case thoroughly processed, and all criminal activities were tallied, the drugs would be sent to the Third Special District for trial.



Chapter 284 Glory Bestowed Upon Him, News Broadcast Countdown


Of course, the number of dead and injured was around 80%.

After Jiang Tian burned down the poppy fields, he went on a killing spree, kneeling on the ground and calling out names one by one.

This completely shattered the mentality of these drug dealers.

Even under these circumstances, nearly two hundred more, including henchmen from the Yun family and other major criminal groups, as well as their minor leaders, were almost all arrested. The subsequent interrogation work was an enormous undertaking.

In addition, Yunhai itself was handling a large number of cases. To make matters worse, they split up and operated in small teams of three to five armed police officers, conducting a frenzied roundup in Yunhai.

What, you talk about evidence?

For drug dealers, little evidence is needed. As long as their identities are confirmed and there are witnesses, a little physical evidence is enough to convict them and then proceed with further interrogation.

In short, the already busy Yunhai anti-drug squad was completely overwhelmed this time.

Later, the anti-drug squad even needed significant manpower from the criminal investigation squad to assist, along with a large number of interrogation experts from the provincial department and various branch bureaus, to conduct swift trials.

As for Jiang Tian, ​​he also formally met with Minister Tan at the municipal bureau.

During this time, Minister Tan made no secret of his admiration for Jiang Tian.

"I've remembered your contribution this time. Many people opposed nominating you for a first-class hero award halfway through, including myself. But it seems Old Gao's judgment was as sharp as ever. You didn't disappoint Old Gao, nor did you disappoint headquarters."

Jiang Tian rushed back to the municipal bureau and worked until after 9 a.m., finally giving a detailed mission report on how he infiltrated the previous night and the cause and effect of his actions.

Then...

Jiang Tian was rushed to the hospital.

The main issue was that Yunhai was facing numerous problems at this time. Even a major case at the provincial level had been temporarily put aside by Shen Han, who was handling the aftermath.

Minister Tan only briefly spoke with Jiang Tian at the municipal bureau before hurriedly taking a connecting flight back to Beijing.

The events that had occurred here were far too significant, requiring a report to higher authorities. This was mainly due to the sheer scale of the issues involved, the need for coordination with overseas partners, and the arrangements for subsequent compensation.

The handling of the casualties and missing persons also required headquarters intervention.

But without a doubt, Jiang Tian's contribution was undeniable.

Around 9 PM, Jiang Tian sat in the ambulance, looking at Liao Ying and the nurse opposite him. Beside him sat Zhao Wei.

Zhao Wei had also sustained some injuries.

"I thought it was nothing serious, but I didn't expect my ankle to swell up so much after resting," Zhao Wei said, looking at his ankle.

That's right.

After spraining his ankle, Zhao Wei didn't feel anything at the time, but now, walking is painful and he's sweating profusely.

"Be more careful next time," Jiang Tian said irritably.

Zhao Wei, however, couldn't wait to ask, "Brother Jiang, do you think there will be a collective merit award this time?"

"The second batch of rescuers will definitely get a collective first-class merit award. As for you in the first batch, that depends on how the higher-ups handle it," Jiang Tian thought for a moment and then shook his head with a smile.

"Ah?" Zhao Wei looked dejected.

But the next moment, Zhao Wei's face stiffened.

Because Director Liao, sitting opposite Jiang Tian, ​​had already unwrapped the bandage on Jiang Tian's arm. The bandage was soaked with blood, and even the flesh was still sticking to it after it was unwrapped.

Liao Ying carefully tore off the bandage, looked at the torn flesh, and then at Jiang Tian's uneasy expression.

Then, Liao Ying carefully examined the wound and breathed a sigh of relief, "Luckily, it hasn't become infected and festered. Otherwise, we would have had to cut off the infected flesh. Officer Jiang, no matter what, you must stay in the hospital for treatment until your injury heals."

"No," Jiang Tian shook his head resolutely.

He had just glanced at his profile and saw that his sin value had even surpassed the 200,000 mark.

This time, Jiang Tian had gained a huge advantage.

The terrifying sin value allowed Jiang Tian to upgrade two red attributes, directly upgrading them to gold.

If he upgraded the rapid recovery attribute to gold, Jiang Tian felt that his injuries would be almost completely healed in two or three days. If he were discovered in the hospital, he'd be dissected.

“Officer Jiang, please don’t be so reckless,” Liao Ying was almost speechless with anger.

“There are too many criminals in the team right now who need to be dealt with and tried. This is not only for the country, but also for countless people who are waiting for criminals to be brought to justice. As the leader of the anti-drug squad, how can I stay in the hospital?” Jiang Tian said righteously.

Liao Ying was shocked by his words.

Her beautiful eyes focused on Jiang Tian.

It has to be said that Jiang Tian’s words were incredibly impactful.

In Liao Ying's memory, Jiang Tian had never had a good impression of her; a womanizer with several women around him.

And that was the first time she'd ever seen him like this.

Moreover, he ignored the doctor's advice, was irresponsible about his injuries, and refused to stay in the hospital despite being wounded.

Despite his young age, he had already been promoted several times without prior notice, yet Liao Ying's impression of him was still very poor, even to the point of publicly killing someone in the hospital.

This made Liao Ying's opinion of Jiang Tian even worse.

But today, he truly surprised Liao Ying. It turned out that he hadn't left the hospital for fun or anything else, but to fight a life-or-death battle with criminals despite his injuries.

It turned out that he was actually quite righteous.

"No matter what, you can't continue with dangerous missions this time. Look at your wound, how did you get it?" Liao Ying carefully treated his wound while chatting with Jiang Tian, ​​seemingly afraid that he would become violent due to pain. However, her worries were unnecessary; Jiang Tian's expression remained unchanged.

They stayed until the afternoon, even until the end of the workday.

Jiang Tian was finally forced to finish his IV drip.

Zhao Wei limped after Jiang Tian. Actually, the brand-new, barely used Phaeton that Jiang Tian had left at the hospital after their last departure was still there.

Regardless, it was a gift from the state, and Jiang Tian used it without a second thought.

Zhao Wei sat in the back seat, looking left and right. To an ordinary person, it would look like an ordinary car, but he was certain of how terrifying it was.

There's a saying: "It's not Mercedes or Land Rovers you should fear, but cars with big logos and letters," which perfectly describes this car.

"Captain Jiang, Qiu Ni called. She said you'll probably be on the news tonight, and she wants you to keep an eye out around 7 PM," Zhao Wei said impatiently.

This made Jiang Tian's heart skip a beat.

It's on the news!

And not just regionally, but personally. Jiang Tian felt a surge of excitement at the thought.

In his past life, he was still struggling in a ditch at this time, but in this life, he had already been promoted and received a raise, and was even about to be on the news.

Moreover, there was another reason: once Jiang Tian stepped forward, he would quickly become a representative figure of Yunhai, deeply ingrained in everyone's consciousness.

The peach he had nurtured for so long was finally about to be eaten by Jiang Tian. In the future, people like Li Qishan would have no chance of reaping the rewards.

"Captain Jiang, wait a minute, who's that up ahead...?"



Chapter 285 The First Contact in the Torrential Rain


Liao Ying stood by the bus stop near the hospital entrance, seemingly waiting for a bus.

Just then, dark clouds gathered, and a light drizzle began to fall. A cold wind blew, and Liao Ying couldn't help but hug her shoulders.

Liao Ying had already changed out of her doctor's white coat and into her everyday clothes. Her floral dress blew in the wind, and she shivered from the cold.

At that moment,

a low-key Volkswagen with lettering pulled up in front of Liao Ying. The window rolled down, and Jiang Tian smiled and waved with his injured hand.

For Jiang Tian, ​​this injury was nothing. In a few days, he felt his skill bonuses would be almost fully restored.

In fact, if he added the "Fast Recovery" skill to the gold skill level, even regenerating a severed finger would be possible.

Seeing Jiang Tian wave, Liao Ying, whose hand was still bandaged by her own hand, immediately said speechlessly, "Team Leader Jiang, that hand is injured; you can't move it carelessly."

"Get in the car, I'll take you home," Jiang Tian said without further ado.

To be honest, although Liao Ying was quite involved, she truly embodied the compassionate heart of a doctor for Jiang Tian.

Jiang Tian was also heading home; the sky was overcast and rain was imminent. Although he was busy at the police station, he definitely needed rest.

Even with Jiang Tian's strong physical constitution, mental fatigue was unavoidable.

He hadn't had a good night's sleep for many days.

"Too much trouble, no need, Team Leader Jiang," Liao Ying waved her hand.

But just then, a cold wind blew by, followed by a light, icy rain that pelted Liao Ying, making her shiver involuntarily.

“The rain is getting heavier. Don’t hesitate, get in the car. It’s on the way,” Jiang Tian invited with a smile.

Liao Ying hesitated for a moment, but finally opened the back door.

Once inside, Liao Ying was stunned by the surroundings. The interior was incredibly spacious, and the luxurious decor left her completely bewildered.

“Team Leader Jiang, the interior of this car is different from any other car I’ve seen of this brand!” Liao Ying said somewhat awkwardly.

She clearly sensed that this car was unusual. She had seen many cars of this brand, but this was the first time she had seen one so luxurious, even with high-tech interiors.

The exterior looked ordinary, but the interior was unimaginably luxurious.

“I’m not afraid of Mercedes or Land Rovers, but I’m afraid of Volkswagens with letters on them. This one costs over a million,” Zhao Wei boasted proudly from the passenger seat.

Zhao Wei knew this car because Fang Ming had mentioned at the Municipal Bureau that Jiang Tian had done their department a great favor, so he had specially allocated this car from the department.

Of course, this car wasn't a gift to Jiang Tian, ​​but rather intended for his official use.

The term was indefinite.

In essence, it was practically the same as giving it to Jiang Tian.

"Over a million, Team Leader Jiang, is your family that well-off?" Liao Ying's heart skipped a beat.

She even felt her feet twitching uncomfortably.

And this situation, however, led Liao Ying to a further change in her opinion of Jiang Tian.

A rich second-generation heir, yet he managed to join such a dangerous team, performing terrifying combat missions every day, and repeatedly achieving meritorious service. This fully demonstrates Jiang Tian's bravery and fearlessness.

Consider this: a privileged family background.

Yet Jiang Tian is willing to undertake such dangerous missions.

"Don't listen to his nonsense. I performed meritorious service on a mission, and the higher-ups temporarily gave me a spare vehicle to use as an official car," Jiang Tian explained.

Even with Jiang Tian's modest explanation, Liao Ying's impression of him improved even more.

"Even so, it must have been a very dangerous mission," Liao Ying asked.

Jiang Tian immediately fell silent.

The anti-drug squad has its confidentiality rules, but that department's confidentiality regulations are even more stringent.

Less important information leaks are one thing, but Jiang Tian is careful about what happens in that department.

Moreover, the impact of that mission across the Atlantic was terrifying.

The entire United States is practically insane.

A large-scale gunfight and fire broke out in the wealthy neighborhood, posing a terrifying challenge to the rich across the Atlantic.

Following this, countless wealthy individuals launched a frenzied protest the next day, signing petitions and issuing statements. Even the beautiful tycoon couldn't withstand the pressure and issued a statement indicating that she would deploy even stronger police protection.

If Jiang Tian's actions were exposed, his life would likely be anything but peaceful. It's estimated that the wealthy across the Atlantic would offer a terrifying bounty for his head.

Following Liao Ying's directions, Jiang Tian sped through the city streets.

Outside, a torrential downpour had begun.

However, being a luxury car, the soundproofing was perfect, and the interior was completely silent, although the windshield wipers were struggling to keep up.

The once clear sky had turned pitch black.

Thunder rumbled intermittently in the air.

As the car drove on, Jiang Tian was surprised to find that they were heading further and further out of the city.

Shortly afterward, the car entered an old, dilapidated residential area.

This completely astonished Jiang Tian.

In Jiang Tian's eyes, Liao Ying, so young—in fact, he felt she was only a few years older than him—was already the head of a department. Undoubtedly, she was a genius.

And such a genius couldn't be cultivated by an ordinary family.

"Director Liao, how come you live in this kind of environment?" Zhao Wei asked, his face full of astonishment.

"Surprised?" Liao Ying gave a wry smile from the back seat; this was also why she hadn't wanted to be driven.

However, Zhao Wei quickly recovered and tried to salvage the situation: "That's not what I meant. But, Director Liao, as the head of the department, doesn't the hospital provide accommodation?"

"There are staff rooms, but I don't have the money to buy one," Director Liao said, looking out the window.

Unconsciously, the car had stopped in front of a small alley. In the pitch black, walking alone would truly be frightening.

"It's too dangerous to live here," Jiang Tian said with some concern.

After all, Director Liao is so beautiful; it's easy for someone to be driven by lust or have ulterior motives. If they were to do something here, it would be very difficult for a young girl like Liao Ying to resist.

“There was no danger, I’m used to it now. Thank you, Team Leader Jiang. And don’t forget to come to the hospital for your dressing change tomorrow.” Liao Ying opened the car door.

“Let me give you a ride.” Jiang Tian grabbed an umbrella and went to Liao Ying’s side, holding it over her.

The two unconsciously drew closer.

Jiang Tian didn’t notice, but for Liao Ying, who had never been near a man before, her fair face flushed slightly.

But as the large raindrops began to fall, Liao Ying swallowed back her words of refusal.



Chapter 286 Investigation: A Dark and Windy Night of Murder


Jiang Tian appeared to be chatting with Liao Ying, but his eyes were actually observing his surroundings.

Because in his previous life, a fleeting news report depicted this very setting.

"A rainy night."

"Wait, doesn't that mean tonight?"

Jiang Tian suddenly remembered the news from his past life: Liao Ying, because she refused to give her younger brother money, was brutally murdered by him on a stormy night.

After Liao Ying's brother was arrested, investigation and interrogation revealed that he was demanding money because of drug addiction. Without money for his drugs, he had turned to his sister, but Liao Ying sensed something was wrong and refused to give him any.

So, after his drug cravings, he chose to brutally murder Liao Ying.

"Damn, I almost forgot about that." Jiang Tian was startled.

If he hadn't coincidentally bumped into Liao Ying at the hospital entrance, Jiang Tian would have completely forgotten about this incident, preoccupied with the various cases he'd been dealing with these past few days.

After all, Jiang Tian had only seen this incident in the news in his past life, and his connection to the two individuals was solely due to his extraordinary memory.

If they were just ordinary people, how could they possibly recall a casual case they'd seen in the news ten years ago, given the time gap of over a decade?

"Do you live alone?" Jiang Tian asked, holding his umbrella, then waved goodbye to Zhao Wei sitting in the car.

This prompted Zhao Wei in the car to instinctively give him a thumbs up, looking at Jiang Tian with adoration.

"Yes," Liao Ying replied in a barely audible voice, assuming Jiang Tian had other intentions.

"Aren't you going to invite me up for a drink?" Jiang Tian asked thoughtfully.

Buzz!!!

Liao Ying's face instantly turned red.

She was having impure thoughts, already wondering what kind of thing a scumbag like Jiang Tian might do to her if she invited him upstairs. If he did, should she resist or submit?

Liao Ying's mind was buzzing.

Before he could answer, Jiang Tian had already followed her into the dilapidated courtyard of a residential area, surrounded by darkness.

Then they went up a flight of stairs from the courtyard.

Jiang Tian didn't even wait for Liao Ying's reply before following her up the stairs.

Inside the house, there was only a living room and a bedroom. Although the house wasn't great, it was very clean and tidy.

"Have a seat, I'll get you some water." After entering the house, Liao Ying practically ran inside, leaving Jiang Tian alone on the sofa.

"What's the rush?"

Jiang Tian looked at Liao Ying's flustered state and couldn't help but mutter a slightly exasperated comment. He then walked to the window beside him.

Creak!!

The window was wooden, with a broken pane of glass. Opening it, one could see directly into the alley outside.

But Jiang Tian's gaze was fixed on one direction.

"He's in, he's in."

"Damn it, he dared to mess with my sister, I'll kill that son of a bitch."

"Achoo!!!"

Right at the corner of an alley, under the eaves, were two young men, both with dyed yellow hair and extremely unconventional hairstyles, and both wearing earrings.

At that moment, the blond man stared fiercely at the second floor, seemingly about to do something.

But the next instant, he was grabbed by the young man beside him.

“Liao Jiajun, don’t be impulsive. Even if we kill him, we still won’t have any money. Don’t forget what we do,” the young man advised.

Sure enough, as his companion spoke, Liao Jiajun put the dagger he had drawn back into his pocket.

“Look closely at the car at the alley entrance, it’s a Volkswagen with letters on the back!” the young man beside him exclaimed excitedly.

“So what if it has letters? Does that mean I can’t kill him?” Liao Jiajun retorted angrily.

“You forgot? I used to work at a karaoke bar. I saw a boss drive this car. The karaoke bar owner personally greeted me and told me that this wasn’t an ordinary Volkswagen; those with letters cost over a million. This guy is rich,” Yan Yan, his companion, said excitedly.

“Over a million?” Liao Jiajun swallowed hard.

“Listen to me,” Yan Yan planned, “we’ll break down the door with knives. You go up and yell at him, then demand money. If he doesn’t give us 100,000, we’ll accuse him of raping your sister, and we’ll even kill him. A rich man like him will definitely give us the money.”

Liao Jiajun’s face grew increasingly greedy as he listened: “500,000! These heartless rich people spend a million on a car. We’ll take it all anyway, at least 500,000.”

He was already thinking about how to spend the 500,000.

Yan Yan was startled, but upon reflection, he realized that 100,000 and 500,000 were essentially the same.

“Right, we’ll say he broke into our home and tried to rape your sister. That’s it! If he doesn’t give us the money, we’ll kill him and sue him!” Yan Yan was also excited.

So, soon, before their very eyes, the man inside the window slowly closed it, making the two even more excited.

To prevent the man from leaving, they even ran into the yard in the rain, then climbed to the second floor and squatted by the door.

They thought they had covered their tracks perfectly.

But they didn't realize that for Jiang Tian, ​​a mere twitch of his ears and the faint sound of footsteps mixed with the rain was as loud as a shout in his ear.

They came, just as he expected.

Jiang Tian's gaze fell thoughtfully. He watched as Liao Ying brought out a plate of fruit from the inner room and placed it on the table in front of him, constantly rubbing her already flushed face with her hands.

"Have some fruit, the tea's a bit hot," Liao Ying said, sitting a meter away from Jiang Tian, ​​her head bowed, seemingly lost in thought, appearing quite nervous.

This made Jiang Tian worry that Liao Ying might have caught a fever while waiting for the car, so he moved closer, touched her forehead with his hand, and asked in surprise, "Director Liao, are you running a fever? You're so hot!"

The next moment, almost visibly, steam was rising from Liao Ying's head.

"Team Leader Jiang, it's nothing, I'm fine, it's just a bit hot in the room." Liao Ying jumped up reflexively, forcing a laugh while frantically fanning herself.

Jiang Tian wasn't stupid; he finally understood Liao Ying's strange behavior, which only confused him more. When Liao Ying bandaged him at the hospital, there had been skin-to-skin contact, yet Liao Ying hadn't shown any change in expression.

She seemed completely unaffected.

How did things get like this?

Jiang Tian stood up, about to say something, when his phone rang.

Jiang Tian smiled apologetically, then walked to the window, opened it a crack, and asked, "How is it?"

Zhao Wei's voice came through: "Captain Jiang, I found out about Director Liao, whom you asked me to investigate when you got out of the car, but..."

"Stop beating around the bush, just say it," Jiang Tian said impatiently.

There were some things Jiang Tian didn't want to pry into about her family situation. Actually, when Jiang Tian thought of Director Liao before, he had planned to investigate Liao Ying's background and family situation.

He only had a general understanding from the news in his previous life. He was worried that directly asking Liao Ying might give her undue sympathy, so having Zhao Wei investigate through the Municipal Bureau was much simpler.

And sure enough, the information came back in just a few minutes.



Chapter 287 Are you out of your mind? You even dare to extort money from Team Leader Jiang?


"Captain Jiang, Director Liao's family is in a terrible situation. Years ago, her mother was a successful businesswoman, and the family was well-off. However, ten years ago, a major tragedy struck. Liao Ying's mother disappeared, and around that time, her father seemed to have become addicted to gambling, quickly bankrupting the family. Now, Director Liao's family still owes a large amount of debt."

"Liao Ying's mother disappeared?" Jiang Tian sensed something amiss.

"Captain Jiang, don't overthink it. The police investigated at the time, and it had nothing to do with Liao Ying's father. It seems the gambling addiction started after her mother's disappearance," Zhao Wei quickly explained.

"Hmm, anything else?" Jiang Tian still felt something was off.

Zhao Wei shifted in the car to make himself more comfortable and said, "Captain Jiang, Director Liao also has a younger brother. It's truly tragic. They have the same parents, and Director Liao is so outstanding, but her brother is a complete hoodlum. He dropped out of school at a young age to join the underworld, and ended up in juvenile detention twice for fighting. Even after his release, he doesn't have a proper job."

Jiang Tian's gaze softened, and a sudden wave of sympathy rose in his heart for Director Liao.

With a father and brother like that, no wonder they live in such a dilapidated place.

"Okay, call the city bureau and have all the team members come over," Jiang Tian said calmly.

"Yes, Captain Jiang."

Zhao Wei immediately followed Jiang Tian's orders and mobilized the team after hanging up the phone. This was something Jiang Tian was very satisfied with. Despite Zhao Wei's usual boisterous and loud manner, he rarely questioned the reasons or justifications for what Jiang Tian said.

As for why he called all the team members over, that was none of Zhao Wei's concern.

Having experienced so much, Jiang Tian had become accustomed to making all preparations in advance to avoid any misunderstandings after the operation.

After sitting down, Jiang Tian slowly ate a piece of fruit. After finishing, he subconsciously took out a cigarette, but after glancing at Liao Ying, he put it back in his pocket.

Since his rebirth, Jiang Tian had strong self-control and was basically not addicted to cigarettes. However, recently, the pressure had been too great, and he would unconsciously want to smoke a few cigarettes to relieve the stress.

Looking up at Liao Ying sitting beside him, Jiang Tian's eyes softened. He found it hard to imagine how Liao Ying had grown up to be so outstanding in such a family environment.

Then, Jiang Tian's sharp eyes noticed a black stain on Liao Ying's face. He reached out and wiped it away.

Liao Ying looked at Jiang Tian, ​​pursing her lips, but her face grew increasingly red. Finally, unable to bear the pressure, she closed her eyes, pretending not to see, deceiving herself.

Bang!!!

A loud sound suddenly rang out.

The already flimsy door was suddenly kicked open.

With the lock clattering to the ground, Liao Jiajun saw his sister with her eyes closed, and that scoundrel's hand was on his sister's face.

Liao Jiajun's head buzzed.

To him, what did it matter if the man was outstanding or not? He only knew that this sister was his cash cow.

As long as his sister didn't get married, all the money Liao Ying earned would be his.

"Get out of my way, get away from my sister, you son of a bitch, trying to sleep with her? I'll make sure you die without even knowing how!" Liao Jiajun roared furiously.

This voice startled Liao Ying, who had been sleeping with her eyes closed. Upon seeing Liao Jiajun, her face turned deathly pale.

She jumped to her feet with lightning speed, panicking, "Why are you here? Get out of here!"

She didn't want Jiang Tian to see her ugly younger brother.

"I'm broke," Liao Jiajun said nonchalantly.

Hearing this, Liao Ying's face turned cold: "I just gave you your living expenses for this month a few days ago. It's only been a few days, and I'm broke. Get out of here."

But Liao Jiajun didn't leave. Instead, he put one foot on the table and looked at Jiang Tian with malicious intent, saying, "You don't have any, but this pretty boy does. Kid, you broke into a private residence and tried to take advantage of my sister. I saw it all. Tell me, how do you want to resolve this?"

"Liao Jiajun, don't push your luck!" Liao Ying was almost furious. She grabbed Jiang Tian, ​​saying, "Team Leader Jiang, you should leave first. I'll explain later."

Jiang Tian simply smiled, then gently removed Liao Ying's hand and looked at Liao Jiajun, saying, "How much money do you want?"

"Team Leader Jiang," Liao Ying tried to say something, but Jiang Tian stopped her with a raised hand.

Liao Jiajun, seemingly surprised by Jiang Tian's cooperation, grinned greedily, "Five hundred thousand."

"I know your car is worth over a million, so five hundred thousand shouldn't be difficult. Give me five hundred thousand, and I'll pretend nothing happened today. Otherwise, I'll break your legs first, then sue you for trespassing and attempted rape of my sister." Liao Jiajun's face was filled with impatient greed.

His ugly face made Liao Ying stare at her brother in disbelief.

When did this happen?

Liao Ying swayed slightly, it seemed to have started a few months ago when her brother started going out frequently, suspected of drug use.

Could he really be using drugs?

Otherwise, how could he turn someone into such a hideous person?

"I'll stop him later, you go first," Liao Ying said, trembling uncontrollably.

Jiang Tian patted Liao Ying to comfort her.

"Take your pig-like hands off me," Liao Jiajun said fiercely.

Jiang Tian smiled slightly, not angry at all. He sat on the sofa, reached into his pocket, and slowly pulled out a bank card.

“But I have one million in this card, and you only want five hundred thousand. This is so troublesome. How about I find a bank and transfer it to you?” Jiang Tian said calmly, holding the bank card between two fingers.

*Buzz!!!*

How devastating were these words to Liao Jiajun and Yan Yan, two inexperienced young men?

A sucker.

Yes, that's right. In that instant, in their eyes, Jiang Tian became a sucker.

Especially Liao Jiajun, who grabbed Jiang Tian's bank card and pulled it away.

Liao Jiajun was beaming with excitement: “You're smart enough to know what's good for you. I'll give it to you after I've used up the five hundred thousand. I'll just take this card for now.”

“Achoo!!!”

As he spoke, Liao Jiajun yawned and sneezed.

Jiang Tian seemed unsurprised by Liao Jiajun's expression and smiled and nodded: "Use it as you like. I have more when you're done."

Liao Jiajun was filled with suspicion, but after Jiang Tian showed him the bank card balance and then gave him the PIN, Liao Jiajun eagerly grabbed Yan Yan and ran out the door.

As for Liao Ying?

Liao Jiajun had long forgotten about her.

“Team Leader Jiang, you, how could you give him so much money? He…he…” Liao Ying was practically jumping up and down in anxiety.

By the time she tried to chase after them, Liao Jiajun had already run off with his men.

Unfortunately, Jiang Tian didn't answer. He simply dialed Zhao Wei's number and said calmly, “Tell them to be careful, don't lose them.”

Zhao Wei hurriedly replied on the other end of the line, hung up, his heart pounding in his throat. He swallowed frantically, only one thought running through his mind: This world is crazy.

Someone has gone mad.

Someone actually extorted one million from Team Leader Jiang?

Is this world too crazy?

On the other end of the phone, Jiang Tian was met with Liao Ying's panicked eyes after hanging up: "Team Leader Jiang, what just happened?"



Chapter 288 The case gradually becomes clear, the special task force's first operation


“How could you give him a million? Team Leader Jiang, even if you have the money, you can't do this! I... I don't have the money to give you.” Director Liao was flustered and pale.

A million-dollar debt! As a medical worker, Director Liao's salary, including all commissions, even after working himself to the bone for a month, was roughly the same as Jiang Tian's current salary—a little over a thousand yuan.

Incomes are generally very low these days.

Even for medical workers, as a director, a monthly salary is less than two thousand yuan, and that's with commissions and benefits. A million—that's an astronomical figure.

And Liao Jiajun, how could he be so bold?

That's a million!

How dare he take it!

Director Liao's head was buzzing.

“Let's go, let's go quickly.”

Director Liao pulled Jiang Tian's hand and walked out.

“Don't rush, why are you in such a hurry?” Jiang Tian followed helplessly behind.

"I'll take you to get that money back. Even though you didn't say it, I know you risked your life for it. Those criminals get bounties, and your superiors get rewards too. A million—you must have risked your life to kill such a big criminal. I'll take you to get that money back." Liao Ying dragged Jiang Tian frantically out of the room without further ado.

"It's gone, it's gone. It's really nothing," Jiang Tian smiled helplessly.

A million wasn't a huge sum for him now. Besides the Huiteng, he also owned an office building in a prime location, its value beyond measure. The rent alone would cover it in less than a month.

Besides, Jiang Tian had many bank cards and even more money. He had rewards from recent arrests, drug dealer bounties, city bureau bonuses, and other payments from the provincial and headquarters departments.

In this way, Jiang Tian's publicly awarded wealth has already reached three to four million. After this mission, Jiang Tian will undoubtedly have even more, with bonuses pushing his assets past five million.

Now, Jiang Tian is also blessed by the God of Wealth, earning several thousand a day.

Of course, Liao Ying was anxious, and Jiang Tian didn't explain these things to her. He was simply pulled by Liao Ying, running out of the house in the raging wind and rain.

They didn't even have time to close the door.

In this era, although wages weren't high, housing prices were naturally low as well. One million was enough to buy a fairly nice house in Yunhai.

In fact, he didn't even need a million; just a few hundred thousand would have been enough for Liao Ying to buy a three-bedroom apartment with the hospital's employee housing benefits.

This amount of money was terrifying.

The rain was pouring down. Jiang Tian was pulled open the car door, and Liao Ying shoved him into the driver's seat. Liao Ying, soaked to the bone, hurriedly said from the back, "Let's go."

"Even if we searched, we wouldn't find them now. They've vanished without a trace," Jiang Tian said, his lips twitching.

In reality, Jiang Tian had already deployed his men.

Unfortunately, Liao Ying didn't know. She quickly said, "You don't know the place? I do. Go to Haihe Road."

"Oh, Haihe Road?" Jiang Tian was slightly surprised.

Zhao Wei also turned his gaze to Jiang Tian, ​​his eyes flickering slightly.

Because Zhao Wei knew his boss's plan.

Just now on the phone, Jiang Tian's orders had subtly implied that the other party was likely a drug user.

And without a doubt, drug users would be linked to a series of drug crimes and drug trafficking.

Zhao Wei's eyelids twitched involuntarily, and he subconsciously swallowed, but dared not say anything.

You see, Jiang Tian had just made a great contribution in Myanmar during the day and had only just returned to rest. In the time it took to meet a girl, was he about to uncover another drug trafficking lead?

Was he crazy?

Zhao Wei rolled down the car window, his hand trembling, carefully lit a cigarette, and took a deep drag to calm his agitation.

In fact, many people know that the criminal investigation team faces immense challenges, especially in the early days when almost 90% of the investigations relied on manpower to deduce the causes and consequences of cases. That was incredibly stressful, and smoking was often the only way to relieve that pressure.

But many people don't know that...

Excluding other areas, within the Yunhai region alone, the pressure on the Narcotics Control Detachment is countless times greater than that on the Criminal Investigation Detachment.

They are truly on edge at all times.

The Criminal Investigation Detachment only needs to focus on solving cases and apprehending criminals, but the Narcotics Control Detachment has many more considerations. They cannot reveal their identities, and when apprehending drug dealers, they must be constantly wary of their weapons, almost all of which are armed, and there's always the possibility of taking two people down with them—this is no joke.

Furthermore, if the Criminal Investigation Detachment wants to secure more cases, it needs to plan, conduct various surveillance operations, and accurately analyze the drug dealers' strength, determining the stage of the operation.

The issues they face are far more complex than those of the purely case-solving Criminal Investigation Detachment.

Of course, Criminal Investigation Detachment doesn't only solve cases; some other important cases also require its involvement in arrests.

"Why are you on Haihe Road?" Jiang Tian asked casually while driving.

“Because that’s Yan Yan’s house. If they get your money, they’ll definitely go to get it first, and then they might go to Yan Yan’s house,” Liao Ying said.

“Yan Yan, is he the young man your brother was with?” Jiang Tian asked casually.

However, he gave Zhao Wei a look, and Zhao Wei instantly understood, taking out his phone and rapidly sending messages.

Don’t be fooled by Jiang Tian’s usual veiled demeanor; it’s because he can easily resolve issues using this method, especially since it’s in a drug lord’s lair, requiring no surveillance.

The cases Jiang Tian has handled in the past are almost all unconventional.

The normal case process is like this: surveillance, surveillance of drug users, and then following the clues to arrest drug dealers.

Through the drug dealers, further investigation uncovers more problems.

In fact, this is why many anti-drug police officers face retaliation; a significant reason is that along this arrest route, some people become restless.

Because once a problem is uncovered, it's like pulling up a radish and bringing out the rest—it involves an organized, premeditated organization with extremely high combat capabilities.

The dangers faced by anti-narcotics police are countless times greater than those of ordinary units, which is why so many anti-narcotics officers sacrifice their lives even in peacetime.

Following Liao Ying's directions, Jiang Tian headed towards Haihe Road.

Just then, Jiang Tian's phone rang. He took it out and saw—it was Jiang Xiaoyu!



Chapter 289 Old Stories, New and Old Grudges


Jiang Tian, ​​driving, hurriedly answered the phone, "Xiaoyu, what's up?"

After a moment of silence on the other end, Jiang Xiaoyu's voice suddenly rang out, "Brother, when are you coming home? I miss you."

Hearing Jiang Xiaoyu's pitiful voice, Jiang Tian slapped his forehead.

Indeed.

Since his rebirth, Jiang Tian had focused all his attention on his career, unknowingly neglecting his family.

"Dad said you'd be home tonight, but it's so late already, why aren't you home yet?" Jiang Xiaoyu continued.

"I'm busy with some things outside, Xiaoyu. I'll hang up now, I'll be home later," Jiang Tian said, about to hang up.

“Brother, there’s something else. Remember Jiang Zihao from Uncle’s family? He got promoted and received a raise to become a deputy section chief. Uncle’s hosting a celebration tonight, with two tables, and he wants the whole family to come. He just called to urge us to come. Dad’s working overtime, but he insists you come.” Jiang Xiaoyu seemed to suddenly remember why she’d called Jiang Tian.

“Jiang Zihao?”

Jiang Tian’s gaze darkened at that name. How could he not know or be familiar with that name?

To be honest…

After his rebirth, Jiang Tian had almost completely cut off contact with his family’s connections because he had seen through them in his previous life.

Back then, when Jiang Tian’s mother was seriously ill and needed an astronomical sum for surgery, his father, with his white hair, went with him to his uncle’s house to borrow money.

Because Jiang Peng, although an official, was never corrupt, and his salary was very low, the family had practically no money. After all the troubles that had happened, Wang Yifei had given all the family’s money to her parents’ hometown.

This led to Jiang Peng and Jiang Tian being unable to get their money back and, in desperation, turning to their uncle's family for a loan. The scene at their uncle's house remains vivid in Jiang Tian's memory.

Upon knocking on the door, they didn't even see their uncle, but only Jiang Zihao, who stood arrogantly at the door, a newly promoted and high-paid young man, coldly sneering and rudely dismissing them. At that moment, they felt like stray dogs.

Of course, it was within Jiang Tian's rights not to help, and he didn't harbor much resentment.

What Jiang Tian truly couldn't accept was...

Later, a baseless accusation was leveled against Jiang Peng—the most damaging accusation against him. His uncle only needed to come forward and testify to clear him of any wrongdoing.

But at that time, his uncle hastily distanced himself from the situation, plunging Jiang Peng into an abyss. Jiang Peng, who could have easily landed a sinecure, was instead placed under investigation, expelled from the Party, and sentenced to probation.

“Brother, Mom said you have to come no matter what you’re doing,” Jiang Xiaoyu continued.

Jiang Tian sighed.

Actually, Jiang Tian only heard about the celebration banquet after it was over in his previous life; he hadn’t attended at all.

Because in his previous life, his family had fallen on hard times.

And his uncle’s family, especially his uncle, was now the secretary of a county in Zhen City, a genuine department-level cadre.

Back then, they couldn’t wait to sever ties with Jiang Tian’s family, naturally avoiding them as much as possible.

But his uncle’s family had completely forgotten.

Years ago, his uncle's family went bankrupt and nearly fell into poverty, owing tens of thousands of yuan in high-interest loans. Jiang Peng used his newly received merit award to pay it off. Although his uncle eventually repaid the money, he didn't give them a penny more.

Especially after the bankruptcy, it was Jiang Peng who ran around, pulling strings and using connections to get his uncle back into the system.

Of course, these things happened when Jiang Tian was very young, decades ago, and Jiang Peng had only recently joined the system.

If it weren't for the subsequent crisis in Jiang Tian's family, and Jiang Peng standing at his uncle's door revealing these old stories in an attempt to borrow money to treat Jiang Tian's mother, Jiang Tian probably would never have known about these things.

"I understand, I'll be there later," Jiang Tian said casually.

Knowing his uncle as he did, he knew that if Jiang Peng didn't go, he wouldn't give up easily.

In the end, he'd probably bring a group of people straight to his house.

"I heard your uncle's son has a girlfriend, I don't know if they're engaged or not, she works at the same company. Her family is well-off, apparently she's the daughter of a leader in Zhen City. Brother, you need to work harder too!" Jiang Xiaoyu said with a sigh.

Jiang Tian hung up the phone without saying a word. In his previous life, Jiang Tian naturally knew about Jiang Zihao's girlfriend—a leader in Zhen City. Because of this connection, his uncle's career was poised for further advancement in the next few years.

However, this had nothing to do with Jiang Tian.

Although Jiang Tian wanted to sever ties completely, to Jiang Tian, ​​she was his uncle, and to his father, Jiang Peng, she was his older brother. From his father's perspective, Jiang Tian didn't want to put Jiang Peng in a difficult position.

Especially since his uncle's family had become a bit arrogant at this time, though they hadn't completely offended them yet.

"We still need to find a way to sever ties quickly. My uncle's family and the leader they rely on have embezzled a lot of money. If we don't cut ties, even a slight involvement will be enough to finish off Dad."

Jiang Tian knew his uncle all too well. In his previous life, his uncle was too arrogant, and within a few years, things went wrong. The consequences were even worse than theirs; his uncle was even sentenced to death for his heinous crimes.

Getting involved even slightly would not only stain Jiang Peng's reputation, but also Jiang Tian's, with a terrible political stain.

After all, in the eyes of the higher-ups, your close uncle has committed so many crimes. As a family member, can you really claim innocence?

Even if you are truly innocent, why would a leader risk their own safety to promote you?

Jiang Tian pondered these thoughts.

The car had arrived at Haihe Road.

This was near the Yunhai Third Ring Road, not the suburbs, but not in the core area either; it was just on the outskirts. Of course, the environment was a million times better than where Liao Ying lived.

"This is it," Liao Ying said, pointing to a villa complex.

Jiang Tian's car gradually pulled up.

Unexpectedly, the security here was quite good; vehicles not belonging to the complex seemed unable to enter, blocked by barriers.

Jiang Tian stopped the car in front of the security booth and rolled down the window.

"What are you doing here?" The security guard looked to be only in his early twenties.

The quality is better than expected!

Jiang Tian thought to himself, then pulled a notebook out of his pocket. However, upon closer inspection, he realized it was red and had taken the wrong one. He quickly put it back and then pulled out another notebook.

Liao Ying, sitting in the back seat, didn't notice and had no idea what this meant.

But Zhao Wei, who had come from Kyoto and was well-traveled, saw his pupils contract and gasped repeatedly.

Holy crap!!!

Zhao Wei's heart pounded wildly.

The muscles in his face trembled involuntarily.

Others might not know, but he certainly did, especially after catching a glimpse of the words "National Security" out of the corner of his eye. This made Zhao Wei swallow hard.

"From the Municipal Bureau, there's a case to investigate," Jiang Tian said, directly producing his identification.

Upon hearing "Municipal Bureau," the young man instinctively saluted, quickly took the identification, glanced at it carefully, and then opened it to look at the photo.

"Jiang Tian?"



Chapter 290 Old Friends Reunited: I'm a Police Inspector, You're a Security Guard


Through the rain, the security guard finally saw Jiang Tian's clear face in the driver's seat and compared it to his ID photo.

"Holy crap, Jiang Tian, ​​is that really you?" the guard exclaimed, bewildered.

"Me?"

Jiang Tian was slightly surprised and looked the guard over carefully.

"Holy crap, it's me, Ouyang Feng!" the young man exclaimed excitedly, pointing at himself.

Ouyang Feng?

Jiang Tian was stunned. After a closer look, he finally recognized him.

Good heavens.

That's right, this guy was not only Jiang Tian's high school classmate, but he had even bullied Jiang Tian. Back then, relying on his connections with some thugs, he acted like a big shot at school. In high school, besides Zhao Kai's gang, this guy was the most arrogant.

But unfortunately, Jiang Tian's family was wealthy; his father was rich. This guy's family was poor, so he worked as a lackey for thugs.

And now, who would have thought!

"You're a security guard now? I didn't see you at the class reunion?" Jiang Tian asked in surprise.

"Ahem..."

Ouyang Feng coughed awkwardly. Jiang Tian instantly understood. Everyone knew these kinds of gatherings were for showing off. If a security guard like him went up there, without the money to flaunt his status, and his identity was exposed, he'd be truly finished.

So, he simply didn't attend and muted the group chat.

"I didn't expect you to not only get into the police academy, but are you doing an internship now?" Ouyang Feng looked at Jiang Tian driving, a hint of envy flashing in his eyes.

If he had studied hard back then, would things be different now?

Would he not be working as a security guard here, but instead, like Jiang Tian, ​​driving around in a car, proudly displaying his badge while investigating cases?

"Yeah, more or less," Jiang Tian replied casually without arguing.

After the initial novelty wore off, Jiang Tian no longer even had the desire to show off.

"Sigh, if I perform well and get lucky, I'll become a permanent employee after I get promoted, unlike me..."

Ouyang Feng sighed, about to close his badge, but out of the corner of his eye, he vaguely saw the words "Team Leader."

He glanced at it hurriedly.

Buzz!!!

That's right.

Head of the Special Operations Team, Narcotics Control Detachment, Yunhai City Public Security Bureau!

Rank: Third-Level Police Inspector!

Buzz!!!

Looking at the simple title, Ouyang Feng's mind went blank.

What is this?

"Finished looking?" Jiang Tian held out his hand.

"Oh, oh, oh, finished looking, finished looking." Ouyang Feng, still dazed, returned the document.

As Jiang Tian's car gradually entered the residential area.

Ouyang Feng watched the car's rear end disappear into the rain, finally coming back to his senses.

He looked down at his security guard uniform.

Then he looked at Jiang Tian, ​​who had already left.

A self-deprecating smile appeared on Ouyang Feng's face.

In school, Jiang Tian was an ordinary, unremarkable person. His only redeeming quality was that after being humiliated by the principal and becoming the object of ridicule, he worked diligently and got into police academy.

Back then, Ouyang Feng mocked Jiang Tian, ​​saying that last-minute cramming was useless.

Now, he finally realized that if he had worked just a little harder back then, and gotten into a good university like Jiang Tian, ​​would everything have been different?

But there's no going back.

As for Jiang Tian, ​​he stormed straight to his apartment building.

Liao Ying hurriedly tried to get out of the car, but Jiang Tian grabbed her arm.

Facing Liao Ying's questioning gaze,

Jiang Tian pointed to an ordinary Volkswagen Santana in the parking space in front of the apartment building.

Zhao Wei instantly straightened up, excitedly looking at the Santana. He had absolute faith in Jiang Tian's intuition, even blind confidence in it.

Even within the city police station, those who disliked Jiang Tian had to admit that his intuition for criminals was truly terrifying.

Terrifying to the point of being almost mythical.

"Is there something wrong with that car?" Liao Ying's lips paled.

"That's right, the car window was cracked open for ventilation, and smoke was blowing out, so there's clearly someone in the car. It's five or six o'clock, the time when people usually leave work. Normally, it's possible someone was relieving stress, but if you look closely, there's smoke coming out of the passenger seat too. It's clearly not a normal person leaving get off work. Being in the car in this heavy rain is suspicious, especially since the car subconsciously closed the window when I drove by. They're obviously hiding something."

This wasn't just Jiang Tian making things up.

With Jiang Tian's "King of Criminal Investigation" attribute, even without the "Judgment Eye" and scanning radar, he could observe these issues.

The other two attributes only helped Jiang Tian further confirm the other party's identity.

"Boss, is it time to move?" Zhao Wei asked impatiently.

"Don't rush. We need to catch them at the scene. What if we don't have evidence?" Jiang Tian rolled his eyes.

It's only a little later, but it allows us to gather evidence.

Liao Ying clearly heard their conversation.

This plunged Liao Ying into utter panic and confusion.

Panic stemmed from the possibility that these people were criminals, and further, she wondered what their purpose was here, and even whether her brother might be among them.

Confusion arose from the fact that Jiang Tian seemed to have planned this all along.

Could it be that she was merely bait for Jiang Tian?

He simply didn't give her a chance to think deeply.
