Inside the black Volkswagen Santana, two car doors slammed shut, and two men, soaked by the rain, rushed towards the apartment building.

After getting out of the car, the two men acted even more furtively.

Because Jiang Tian had turned into the next apartment building, he hadn't attracted any attention.

"Move!"

Jiang Tian pushed open the car door.

As Jiang Tian finished speaking,

Zhao Wei simultaneously issued the order over the walkie-talkie.

Zhao Wei's foot injury wasn't serious, but to prevent further damage, walking was discouraged. In other words, Zhao Wei could only watch helplessly, but he knew this would be another huge achievement.

To feel involved, Zhao Wei was practically frantically trying to impress.

And indeed.

Zhao Wei's arrangement was quite good, perhaps because he was considered very capable by the people in that department.

The moment Jiang Tian entered the apartment building, Liao Ying, who was being led ahead by him, felt a dark shadow flash past behind her. The instant she turned around,

four figures appeared behind Jiang Tian.

Without a doubt, all four, except for Song Qiuwan with a complicated expression, were filled with excitement and fervor.

"This is our special operations team's first operation. Understand my orders, everyone? Make sure everything goes perfectly."



Chapter 291: He's beyond saving, just execute him.


"When?" Liao Ying stared in astonishment at the four figures behind her.

When did they appear?

If Jiang Tian hadn't turned and waved at them, Liao Ying would have almost thought they were ghosts.

Besides Zhao Wei, it has to be said that that department had indeed brought Jiang Tian a significant boost, and most importantly, the Yunhai Municipal Bureau was currently severely understaffed.

Although it seemed that Yunhai had accumulated many merits and that reaping them was easy, the most important thing was to look at the casualty rate.

Recently, there had been many merits.

In fact, in the last few days alone, the Yunhai Municipal Bureau had reaped at least two collective merits, with everyone receiving substantial bonuses and honors. Even a third of the staff already had individual merits.

These merits were all recently acquired.

Many people were envious, but the Yunhai Municipal Bureau was still severely understaffed. Why?

Because of the terrifying casualty rate.

Currently, the casualty rate in the Yunhai Municipal Bureau had reached over 30%.

Yes, you read that right. Over 30% of the anti-drug squad members have been killed.

This number and casualty rate are terrifying. In other words, several operations so far have already resulted in a staggering one-third of the casualties.

This is a devastating blow to Yunhai, but at the same time, it's an even more frightening statistic for many aspiring members of the anti-drug squad.

Because, among all the detachments, only the anti-drug detachment suffered far more casualties than the others. While the rewards were much harder to obtain, the casualty rate was also terrifyingly high.

This resulted in a situation where, although there were opportunities to gain merit, many people lacked the courage to join. Even those who did have the courage, especially the newcomers, were simply not suitable for Jiang Tian.

Jiang Tian simply didn't have the time or energy to train them.

Consider the missions Jiang Tian was involved in; even Zhao Wei and Li Zijin could only provide logistical support. Ordinary newcomers couldn't even offer much help, at least not in any way.

What Jiang Tian needed now were helpers who could significantly assist him.

And at this time...

It must be said...

After Jiang Tian joined that department and even replaced Du Qing, he naturally took over Du Qing's personnel.

At this time, joining that department didn't even affect Jiang Tian's usual identity or actions; he simply had to report for duty when the department had a mission.

The convenience brought by his new position.

What surprised Jiang Tian even more was that Fang Ming had prepared everything in advance for him.

He directly transferred those people's files and identities to the anti-drug squad. How he did it was unknown, but it conveniently filled the vacancies for Jiang Tian's team members.

In other words, Jiang Tian's emergency response team now possessed a certain level of combat capability.

"What's going on?" Liao Ying asked, her face pale, watching them cautiously advance with guns in their hands.

In the time and effort required to apprehend drug dealers, normal operations, especially when there might be a drug transaction site, typically involve at least a dozen or twenty people. If the enemy has a large number of people, the maximum number could be countless.

However, the Municipal Public Security Bureau was quite busy, and Jiang Tian didn't have time to request their support.

"Is your brother involved in drug use?" Jiang Tian asked Liao Ying with a serious look in his eyes.

Liao Ying's whole body tensed.

Jiang Tian sighed inwardly. Sure enough.

He knew it; Liao Ying had probably noticed something, but hadn't confirmed it yet.

"Team Leader Jiang, I've only had some suspicions all along. Have you discovered something?" Liao Ying asked hurriedly.

Jiang Tian explained, "You're just an ordinary person and don't understand. When your brother came in, he was yawning frantically, and his emotions were unusually volatile, even bordering on delirious. It's obvious he's having a drug relapse. The worse news is, his behavior doesn't even suggest he just started using drugs; rather, it seems he's been using them for some time, and his addiction is quite severe."

That's right.

Jiang Tian, ​​from both his past and present lives, had a thorough understanding of drug users within the anti-drug system.

This stuff, once you encounter it, a person is completely finished.

Simply put, if you've already gotten involved and realized your mistake, and then resolutely cut off your hands, there's still a chance to save yourself.

But if some time has passed...

At this point, the drugs have already destroyed your bodily functions.

The torment is both mental and physical.

At this point, if you still want to save yourself, it's too late.

*Buzz!*

Sure enough, as Jiang Tian finished speaking, Liao Ying's face turned deathly pale.

The next moment, Liao Ying knelt before Jiang Tian, ​​tears streaming down her face, pleading, "Team Leader Jiang, please save him! I beg you!"

In the hallway, Jiang Tian looked at Liao Ying kneeling on the ground and sighed, "I'm sorry, I lied to you earlier. After seeing your brother, I investigated your family situation. The information mentioned that you relied entirely on student loans to complete your university education, which are still not paid off. Your father is a gambler, and your brother is relentlessly pursuing you for money, like a death knell. And yet you still beg me to save your brother?"

Liao Ying nodded repeatedly, saying, "Team Leader Jiang, no matter what, he's still my brother. If he becomes something worthless because of drugs, I'd rather he die here; that would be a relief. Team Leader Jiang, please don't let him get involved with that kind of thing again."

"Get up. I'll do my best," Jiang Tian nodded, but didn't give a definite answer.

Under Jiang Tian's watchful gaze, Li Zijin helped Liao Ying aside and comforted her, "Director Liao, don't worry. We have a professional procedure for dealing with these people. Once apprehended, they'll be sent to a drug rehabilitation center for about a year and a half of compulsory treatment, after which we'll give them hope for a new life."

"Thank you, thank you all," Liao Ying repeatedly expressed her gratitude.

At this moment, Liao Ying was also filled with immense remorse.

In fact, she hadn't noticed anything amiss at first. Perhaps it was only recently, as her addiction deepened and her craving for drugs increased, that Liao Ying realized something was wrong with her brother.

He frequently demanded money, and his attitude became increasingly vicious, ferocious, and terrifying. There was even a time when Liao Ying felt that if she didn't give him money, her brother might kill her.

If he couldn't change his ways in the future, Liao Ying couldn't believe what her younger brother would become.

Liao Ying leaned against the wall in a daze, her eyes rolling back. A gambling addict father, a drug-addicted brother.

Liao Ying felt her consciousness fading.



Chapter 292 A Beast Completely Without Reason and insane


Of course, Jiang Tian had no interest in what Liao Ying was thinking. Guided by Liao Ying, he and his men had arrived at the door, and Li Zijin helped and escorted Liao Ying to a safe spot down the stairwell.

Jiang Tian stood by the door, leaning against the wall, and nodded to Lü Chun, who was standing behind the door.

Lü Chun immediately understood.

In fact, for these people in the special department, even Song Qiuwan possessed terrifying skills. Jiang Tian later learned that these people had undergone special training in secret locations, making their skills far surpass those of ordinary people.

For them, arresting ordinary drug users and criminals was simply overkill.

Bang bang!!!

Lü Chun knocked twice on the door, then stood quietly with her pistol in both hands, showing no nervousness whatsoever in this small-scale situation.

Jiang Tian also held a gun, watching the red dot approach on the scanning radar.

Even without the scanning radar, Jiang Tian could hear the approaching sounds coming from inside the door.

“Who’s there?” a voice rang out.

Jiang Tian recognized the voice; it was Yan Yan's voice, the one who had been with Liao Ying’s younger brother.

“It’s the property management. Your downstairs neighbors reported a leak in your bathroom, so they sent us to check it out,” Jiang Tian said.

“Wait a moment.”

After Yan Yan finished speaking, a moment later, the door clicked open.

Meanwhile, a different scene unfolded inside the room.

In the bedroom.

Liao Jiajun took a deep breath, his face full of satisfaction, then his eyes lit up with excitement.

“Good stuff.” Liao Jiajun took a sniff and swallowed hard.

The two people sitting opposite Liao Jiajun were the same people Jiang Tian had seen getting out of the Volkswagen Santana at the entrance of the building.

Looking at Liao Jiajun, the two men exchanged a glance and smiled, saying, "Good quality at a low price. Our quality is guaranteed, and we wouldn't cheat you; after all, it would ruin our reputation."

Liao Jiajun seemed very satisfied. He then took out three stacks of money from a drawer, a total of thirty thousand yuan.

"This is the goods you ordered. Check if it's correct." The two men exchanged another glance and tossed him a small bag.

Liao Jiajun eagerly took it, unlocked the chain, and saw that it was full of white powder.

Liao Jiajun's eyes were filled with greed.

"Yes, yes, yes, absolutely correct." Liao Jiajun nodded frantically.

The two men didn't ask Liao Jiajun where the money came from. They wouldn't care where the money came from; they were only responsible for sales. As long as the money was there, even if it was obtained by robbing a bank, it was none of their business.

"Since it's over, we'll be leaving now." The two men who came to make the transaction stood up and took their leave.

Liao Jiajun eagerly stood up to see him off, yawning incessantly, tears and snot streaming down his face.

Yes, his craving was kicking in.

Especially since the item was so close.

But that didn't matter.

"Do you have anything even more luxurious?" Liao Jiajun asked, wiping his tears and snot.

The two men at the door stopped abruptly, then turned to Liao Jiajun, their eyes bright. "Yes, our boss recently developed a new, top-of-the-line formula, absolutely the best on the market, but it's three times more expensive than the ordinary ones."

"Go on, bring it next time." Liao Jiajun, now rich, didn't care.

He hadn't thought about how long a million would last. If it ran out, he could just go back and extort more money from that pretty boy.

Driving a car worth over a million, he had plenty of money.

"I don't lack money, I have plenty of idiots willing to give me money."

"I'll bring it for you to try tomorrow, everyone who's used it says it's great." The two salesmen didn't think much of it.

"Okay, okay, you guys go now." Liao Jiajun's hands trembled as he pinched out some powder, his tools already prepared.

With money, they would do anything.

Whether it was obtained legitimately or not was none of his concern.

Click!!!

As they spoke,

they opened the bedroom door.

Because the property management staff had just come to repair the leak, they didn't think much of it.

It's pouring rain and wind outside; leaks in the complex are quite normal, right? At most, it's just that this property management staff is particularly diligent.

They didn't think it was such a coincidence in this weather.

So.

The moment the door was opened.

"Don't move! Everyone, don't move! Drop your weapons!"

Bang!!!

A loud gunshot suddenly rang out, and Liao Jiajun, who was about to enjoy himself, almost wet himself in fright.

He opened his eyes and looked.

He saw the two men who had just sold him drugs at the door, holding pistols and seemingly about to resist violently, but the next moment, blood spurted from their arms, and then the two pistols clattered to the ground.

That's right.

This is the most dangerous part of drug enforcement.

Drug trafficking—what kind of behavior is this? A deadly crime. Simply put, if caught, fifty grams is enough to get you executed.

In other words, even if 20 grams of flour were mixed into those 50 grams, it would still be punished as if it were the 50 grams, which carries the maximum penalty of death.

Such terrifying risks naturally lead them to carry dangerous weapons.

This is countless times more dangerous than committing a criminal offense.

This is also what makes anti-drug police so admirable.

When arresting criminals, they could encounter gunfire from drug dealers at any moment. No one could be sure that they wouldn't be accidentally shot in a vital area by a resisting drug dealer in the next instant.

Two drug dealers clutched their hands and howled in pain.

But before they could even utter a sound, the next moment, several men following Jiang Tian rushed out, kicked the two men to the ground, and handcuffed their hands together in less than a second.

Simple.

Jiang Tian held a pistol in his hand and then pointed it at Liao Jiajun.

At this moment, Liao Jiajun lay on the bed, his bewildered face filled with confusion.

Seeing the dense mass of gun barrels and several people pointing their weapons at him, Liao Jiajun instinctively raised his hands.

He glanced blankly at Jiang Tian, ​​then at Yan Yan.

The next moment.

Almost without a word, Liao Jiajun exhaled sharply and swiftly grabbed a straw.

Jiang Tian, ​​witnessing this, couldn't help but shake his head.

This is the terrifying aspect of being addicted to that stuff.

Even when facing death, the thought isn't to cover one's head with hands, but to inhale a mouthful.

Even if the price of that mouthful is death, it won't stop them.

This is truly being addicted to something that can ruin a life.

So, as a righteous policeman, how could Jiang Tian stand by and watch someone inhale that stuff right in front of him?

Bang!!!

No matter how fast Liao Jiajun is, he can't be faster than Jiang Tian. The instant Liao Jiajun made his move, Jiang Tian kicked over the table holding the tools, scattering all the powder onto the ground.

But Liao Jiajun knelt on the ground almost without thinking, trying to inhale it frantically.

He's gone mad.

Liao Jiajun has gone mad, completely mad, even lost his mind. His eyes are bloodshot, his nose and eyes are covered in snot and tears, but at this moment, all he sees is the powder on the ground.

Jiang Tian shook his head at this sight; he was truly pathetic and pitiful.

Such a person does not deserve any sympathy or pity. Jiang Tian kicked Liao Jiajun aside with a bang, but Liao Jiajun still crawled over like a dog.



Chapter 293 Killing someone by destroying their spirit, utter madness


Then, after being kicked away by Jiang Tian, ​​Liao Jiajun finally went completely mad. He tried to get up from the ground, but Jiang Tian stepped on him, finally bringing him under control.

Then, Liao Jiajun stared bloodshot at the powder on the table. It was all gone, not a single drop left.

Only after the thing that had driven him mad disappeared did Liao Jiajun seem to regain a little sanity.

His vision blurred as he looked at the person controlling him.

"It's you, it's you, it's you..." Seeing Jiang Tian's face clearly, Liao Jiajun became even more frenzied, screaming and shouting as if he wanted to fight Jiang Tian to the death.

He was panting heavily, snot and tears streaming down his face.

"Let me go! Let me go now, or I'll kill you! Yes, I'll kill you! If you don't let me go, I'll kill you!" Liao Jiajun suddenly pulled a knife from his pocket.

Snap!!!

The next moment, Jiang Tian moved even faster, pulling out a black police baton from his person.

That's how it is.

For Jiang Tian, ​​firearms might be more convenient abroad, but in China, nothing is more convenient than a police baton.

Liao Jiajun had barely reached out when Jiang Tian slammed the baton into his arm, breaking it instantly.

The excruciating pain caused Liao Jiajun to scream in agony, which also brought him back to his senses.

"Who are you? Whoever you are, please, let me go, let me take one more hit, just one more," Liao Jiajun pleaded. "I only spent 30,000 yuan, all the money and cards are still here, I'll give it all back, I just beg you, give me one more hit."

Pathetic.

Jiang Tian shook his head slightly.

This is what they call "the pitiful often have something hateful about them."

Jiang Tian naturally couldn't pity such a person, because such a person could no longer be called human.

Seeing Liao Jiajun's frenzied state, Jiang Tian finally understood why Liao Ying could kill him without money.

Because such people, though wearing human skin, could no longer be called human; they had become irrational beasts.

*Click!*

The next moment, Lü Chun stepped forward, took out handcuffs, and cuffed Liao Jiajun's hands, forcing him to lie face down on the ground.

Then Jiang Tian slowly squatted down in front of him, took out an ID card from his pocket, waved it in Liao Jiajun's face, slapped it twice, and then opened the card.

"Jiang Tian, ​​leader of the Yunhai Anti-Drug Squad's Emergency Response Team, is now formally arresting you for drug use and extortion of a large sum of money," Jiang Tian said.

To be fair, Liao Jiajun was quite good-looking.

Fair skin, tall and slender.

It's such a pity that the non-mainstream style ruined such a good foundation. Jiang Tian couldn't help but find it hard to imagine how beautiful Liao Ying and Liao Jiajun's mothers must have been.

What a pity!

Liao Ying relied on herself to get to where she is now, while Liao Jiajun has become this utterly worthless person.

So...

Liao Jiajun looked at the identification, then at Jiang Tian's pretty-boy appearance, his head buzzing.

A policeman?

This rich, idiot is actually from the anti-drug squad?

"A policeman?"

No.

Liao Jiajun, despite the excruciating pain, regained his senses. His intelligence returned, and he quickly analyzed the situation.

This wasn't some fool with too much money; this was a deliberate trap.

Jiang Tian patted Liao Jiajun's face and said, "Kid, you've figured it out. That's right, I set you up from the beginning. By the way, your sister doesn't want you to get out. Stay in there and make the best of it. Don't worry, even though you'll never get out, I'll take care of you. And of course, I want to thank you for sacrificing yourself and making a significant contribution to me."

"I'll kill you! I'll kill you, you bastard!" Liao Jiajun completely lost his mind.

He struggled frantically, seemingly forgetting even the pain of his broken arm, unable to keep up with the agony within him.

After saying this, Jiang Tian wasn't particularly happy.

This is what it means to see the whole picture from a single glimpse.

Although Jiang Tian had recently dismantled many drug lords at the top, drug crimes in Yunhai hadn't decreased.

There's a saying that goes, "When the profit reaches 300%, some people will risk their lives to do it," and the same principle applies to the saying "The bigger the storm, the more expensive the fish."

"Team leader, the count is complete. It's about 300 grams, a huge amount," Li Zijin said, approaching them.

When they rushed in earlier...

Seeing there was no danger, Li Zijin followed them in to immediately assist with processing all the crime records at the scene.

Especially in determining the quantity and scale of the crime.

"Thirty thousand yuan, and only a mere three hundred grams."

Jiang Tian was stunned. He finally understood why, even though Yunhai was practically being raided, so many people were still recklessly rushing in.

Because the price was terrifyingly equivalent to the price of gold.

Of course, that wasn't the most important thing. The most important thing was these two drug dealers.

Jiang Tian's face lit up.

Don't underestimate these two simple individuals; in the long run, they'll uncover a whole host of other criminals. Right now, they're trading 300 grams, but what's next?

What happens when you trace it layer by layer?

The most crucial element in apprehending these drug traffickers is finding a breakthrough. That's the key. Yunhai's previous lack of progress stemmed from their inability to find a breakthrough. By starting with a single point and delving deeper, the investigation will uncover increasingly larger criminal issues.

Jiang Tian immediately called Song Liguo.

At this time...

Song Liguo and Jiang Peng were both working overtime at the Municipal Public Security Bureau. Song Liguo had just completed a mission in Myanmar and could have gone home to rest, but he couldn't. The Municipal Bureau was too busy; he didn't even have time for a check-up.

For example, right now, Song Liguo and Gao Xiaolong were standing behind the one-way glass partition next to the interrogation room.

Inside the interrogation room sat the second young lady of the Yun family.

The most important person in the Yun family, without exception.

"There are no valuable leads," Gao Xiaolong said, his face grim.

Such a large-scale operation was ultimately thwarted by the Yun family's patriarch and their eldest daughter, who both managed to escape.

“Although we found a large amount of criminal evidence in the drug manufacturing and parts factories, none of it proves a direct connection to this second young lady. Furthermore, much of the Yun family's connection to Yunhai and its domestic networks is in the hands of their patriarch and eldest daughter.” Song Liguo's face was extremely grim.

So many people had been sacrificed, and such a huge commotion had been caused.

Although many were killed, this result was already a disgrace to them.

“Relax, take it slow, there will be a result eventually.” Gao Xiaolong didn't blame him.

But the next moment, Song Liguo's phone rang. A moment later, Song Liguo's eyelids twitched: “What did you say? You caught someone at a drug trafficking scene again?”



Chapter 294: Rising to the Top with Chief Gao's Approval


In Yunhai, a large number of drug traffickers have been arrested. They will all face interrogation and have their charges confirmed.

Sixty percent will be sentenced to death with a two-year reprieve or life imprisonment, while the best outcome for the remaining forty percent will likely be a death sentence.

Overall, a large number of drug traffickers, both large and small, have been arrested in Yunhai recently.

The city police are very busy, but even busier is dealing with the aftermath of the operation in Myanmar. This includes notifying the families of the deceased and providing compensation, among other things.

In any case, these are numerous and serious problems.

However, the morale of Yunhai as a whole, which had been high just moments before, has been dampened again by this operation in Myanmar.

What is Yunhai lacking now?

A new case is missing.

A new lead is needed.

Although the lead on the Yun family in Myanmar has gone cold, the fight against drugs hasn't stopped; even more important and severe challenges await.

Song Liguo dared not delay in the slightest.

But for now...

Those who needed to be rounded up have been mostly arrested, and those who could be interrogated have been interrogated.

The people arrested from the Yun family have also been interrogated, but the results haven't been very good. The second young lady of the Yun family, to put it bluntly, is a complete idiot; she knows almost nothing about the family's situation or affairs.

All the matters she handled and was involved in overseas were charitable acts, so Miss Yun Er needs to be released.

This is what's most distressing, but the key is that she's truly innocent.

Currently, Yunhai desperately needs a breakthrough.

Just now, Gao Xiaolong had a heavy discussion about this issue with Song Liguo and Jiang Peng. The main issue is that everything was progressing smoothly in Yunhai, but an operation in Myanmar dealt a huge blow to the overall image of Yunhai's government.

Now, there's an urgent need for a new case, a new operation, to deter criminal groups.

And now, it's like a godsend, a timely help.

With no breakthrough in the Yun family's line in the short term, Yunhai's anti-drug squad is in a somewhat awkward position.

The necessary arrests have been made in emergency operations, and the interrogations are almost complete.

Besides arresting the major drug lords, most of the smaller drug dealers have also been apprehended. At least, those identified through interrogation within the intelligence system have been mostly caught.

With the Yun family's access cut off, it's easy to feel lost now, as if we've returned to square one.

Song Liguo was slightly troubled, because one wrong move could easily send Yunhai back to its previous predicament.

Who knew that such a pleasant surprise would occur at this moment?

It's you again!

Song Liguo's heart pounded: "Are you sure?"

"What's there to be sure about? I've already apprehended the drug dealers. There are also drug users on site; they're having a really bad withdrawal. I also called people from the rehab center to come along."

On the other end of the phone, Jiang Tian held the phone, his gaze fixed on Liao Jiajun, handcuffed and even being controlled by Lü Chun who was straddling him.

He'd gone mad.

Liao Jiajun was completely insane, snot and tears streaming down his face. One moment he was crying and pleading, the next he was furiously pounding his head against the ground, seemingly trying to kill himself.

Even Jiang Tian's eyelids twitched at the sight of this madness.

To be honest, Jiang Tian had dealt with drugs before, but every time he witnessed such a scene, it still made him uneasy.

"Drugs, this kind of thing, easily destroys all reason," Jiang Tian shook his head. Once you're hooked, let alone an ordinary person.

Even Jiang Tian felt that with his willpower, he would have a hard time resisting. He was confident he could operate without anesthesia and remain silent, but he couldn't guarantee he could maintain his sanity after becoming addicted.

This was countless times more terrifying than deadly poison.

"Okay, I'll go right away, no, I'll go there myself right now."

Song Liguo said excitedly.

Once it was confirmed that the second young lady of the Yun family was innocent, there was no point in continuing the interrogation.

The leads in the case had almost completely dried up, and the second young lady had no idea where the eldest young lady and the head of the Yun family had gone.

Therefore, Song Liguo completely lost interest in the second young lady of the Yun family.

Although the Yun family's leads had run dry, Song Liguo's ambition was rekindled; he felt the flame of his fighting spirit rise again. Even without the Yun family's mainland leads, a new drug trafficking lead had emerged.

Instantly, the confusion before him vanished.

After hanging up the phone, Song Liguo hurriedly arranged his men.

Meanwhile, Gao Xiaolong, in the interrogation room, remained very calm.

Upon hearing that Jiang Tian had found the drug trafficker lead, his eyes only flickered slightly before his composure returned instantly.

"Jiang Peng, you may be a bit useless, but you've raised a fine son!"

Gao Xiaolong glanced at Jiang Peng with a slight sigh. His words almost made Jiang Peng spit out his drink. For a moment, he couldn't even tell if Gao Xiaolong was insulting or praising him.

But in the end, Jiang Peng just smiled obsequiously.

No, even his face was full of smiles.

Why did these seemingly insulting words make Jiang Peng so unusually excited?

Because you can't take a leader's words literally. On the contrary, these seemingly insulting words, coming from Gao Xiaolong, genuinely meant he considered Jiang Peng one of his own.

And you see, Jiang Peng is currently at a crucial juncture. Normally, based on Jiang Peng's qualifications, he wouldn't be qualified to take the position in the political and legal system.

But with the support of headquarters, especially Gao Xiaolong's support, this matter is practically guaranteed.

Before this, although Gao Xiaolong highly valued and cultivated Jiang Tian, ​​he had never expressed any opinion about Jiang Peng.

This statement now signifies a complete acceptance of Jiang Peng.

Sure enough, the next moment, Gao Xiaolong said with a deep gaze, "Xiao Jiang is a capable genius. Such a genius has limitless potential. He possesses abilities that no one else has. Xiao Jiang will definitely go to headquarters in the future. Therefore, although I will support you in becoming the top political and legal official in Yunhai, you must understand that it's not for yourself, but for Comrade Xiao Jiang."

Without Leader Jing, is Jiang Peng guaranteed success?

It's not that simple.

Because even without Leader Jing, there are countless competitors. Until the very last moment, within the system, no one knows who will have the last laugh.

"Actually, what you don't know is that Yun Province is planning to parachute in a new top political and legal official to take over. But don't worry, the Jing Feifan incident represents one thing: how much trouble it can cause if you can't be a good partner. After becoming the top political and legal official, protect Xiao Jiang well."

Gao Xiaolong spoke slowly.



Chapter 295 The dust has settled; three generations of poor farmers have finally made something of themselves.


But every word struck Jiang Peng like a bolt from the blue.

That's right.

Jiang Peng was unaware of this news, and even the entire Yunhai region was unaware.

In that instant, Jiang Peng broke out in a cold sweat.

Yes, although he was still trying his best, he knew that's how it was here. Many people were highly regarded, but in the end, it was common for someone to be parachuted in to fill their position; it happened almost every day.

If it weren't for Gao Xiaolong, he might have been on the verge of promotion, and everyone was congratulating him, when suddenly a top official in the political and legal system would be parachuted in.

Hiss!!!

Jiang Peng felt a chill run down his spine; he couldn't bear to think about it.

"To be honest, your abilities are mediocre. Of course, that could be a strength, but what I still can't understand is how someone as mediocre as you could have such an outstanding son as Xiao Jiang. Sigh, I just can't understand it."

Gao Xiaolong, hands behind his back, seemed to think of a close relative in that instant, and with a grim expression, he muttered to himself as he left.

Leaving Jiang Peng standing there, stunned.

Jiang Peng even took deep breaths repeatedly,

trying hard to suppress the excitement within him.

How could he possibly stay calm!

Who could remain calm?

You have to understand, just before this, even not long ago, Jiang Peng was constantly teetering on the brink of being brought down.

Let alone going any further.

He was even struggling to hold onto his position as bureau chief; at that time, Jiang Peng was even considering giving up and resigning himself to his fate.

But then…

In a short period of time, Jiang Peng not only turned the tide against all odds, but he was about to take another step forward.

Born into a peasant family, Jiang Peng was now about to become the top political and legal official in Yunhai, the provincial capital.

It's important to understand that political and legal work is no longer limited to the police station, but encompasses the entire public security, procuratorate, and judicial system, including crucial responsibilities such as maintaining social stability.

This also means that Jiang Peng has taken a giant leap from the single police system to a much broader future.

Don't be fooled by the seemingly small increase in rank.

This signifies that Jiang Peng's future political path has been completely paved.

It's like a dream.

It truly feels like a dream.

After Jing Feifan's downfall, his opportunities increased, but not as much as he had imagined. On one hand, he lacked the experience of leading the public security, procuratorate, and judicial systems; on the other hand, his qualifications were too shallow.

Therefore, Jiang Peng understood perfectly why he had received support from headquarters, and even why Gao Xiaolong had personally intervened. He understood the reasons behind it all too well.

Do I even have that kind of face? I'm nothing!

In the end, isn't it all because I have a son?

It's all because of Jiang Tian!

This made Jiang Peng blush slightly. He really hadn't expected that not long ago, he had made an exception for the only time, dragging Jiang Tian into the Municipal Bureau and even using his connections to get Jiang Tian into the team, wanting to train him.

Surprisingly, he lived up to expectations and even got promoted.

For a moment, he was overwhelmed with mixed feelings, unsure whether to be happy or sad.

His son was so outstanding, yet his abilities seemed excessively mediocre.

"Something's not right."

Jiang Peng suddenly paused, watching Gao Xiaolong's departing steps.

That's right.

A leader like Gao Xiaolong couldn't possibly be blind to his own abilities.

Promoting someone to a high position when their abilities are insufficient can sometimes be detrimental.

Jiang Peng was already struggling as a bureau chief; he couldn't solve many problems. Leading the public security and judicial system, while difficult for him given his abilities, was still manageable with effort.

Even if mistakes were made, the Party Secretary would be watching.

But Jiang Peng knew his abilities were probably limited, a fact he discerned from Gao Xiaolong's tone.

The unspoken meaning was clear: he was to protect Jiang Tian in this position, ensuring a smooth transition from Yunhai to headquarters.

And Jiang Peng's role was to protect him.

Thinking this through, Jiang Peng understood. Considering Gao Xiaolong's personality—intolerant of incompetence—personally supporting a useless person was more unbearable for Gao Xiaolong than eating shit.

In fact, Jiang Peng wasn't wrong.

Gao Xiaolong felt utterly miserable, like he'd eaten shit, but there was nothing he could do; after all, this was Jiang Tian's father.

So Jiang Peng wasn't wrong at all. After Gao Xiaolong left the small, dark interrogation room, he went straight to the office area, took out his phone, and answered a call.

"Old Gao, we will definitely implement headquarters' suggestion. Don't worry about it. Besides, we all recognize Comrade Jiang Peng's abilities. But what about the original personnel?" Old Mu's voice came through the phone.

"The security situation in Zhen City still has many problems. He may be senior, but sometimes you can't place too much emphasis on seniority. Beijing has repeatedly emphasized the need for younger cadres and to avoid retaining old ideas. My opinion, and also headquarters' opinion, is that as compensation, he should replace Comrade Jiang Peng as bureau chief. After all, in terms of merit and achievements, he's far inferior to Comrade Jiang Peng." Gao Xiaolong deliberately emphasized the headquarters' opinion.

Old Mu smiled wryly, a look of helplessness on his face. "I understand, but this person's background is a bit unusual. I'm worried about what kind of emotional reaction he might have."

"What emotional reaction? If he dares to have emotional reactions while working, I'm ready to fire him completely. Jing Feifan and Li Qishan are prime examples," Gao Xiaolong said loudly and bluntly.

After saying that, he hung up the phone. On the other end of the line...

Old Mu wasn't alone in his office. Sitting opposite him was another leader, his face showing embarrassment and displeasure.

"After all, this is the political and legal system. We in Yunnan Province will definitely have to carefully consider the headquarters' opinion. So, regarding the personnel selection for the Space Department, let's replace Comrade Jiang Peng. Seniority doesn't represent everything, right? Right now, performance is the top priority," Old Mu said casually as he made tea.

The leader opposite him nodded with a complicated expression. Given Old Mu's attitude and the headquarters' attitude, even as the leader of Yunnan Province's political and legal system, he could only feel powerless.

Perhaps if he forcefully supports it and completely breaks off relations, he might succeed.

But the outcome would be something absolutely no one could bear.

He could forget about continuing to work in the industry afterward.

“I understand. After all, Comrade Jiang Peng's achievements are undeniable. I will work with Director Dai of Zhen City to reassure him about this matter,” the political and legal leader nodded slightly and sighed.

After watching the political and legal leader leave with a smile, Old Mu took out his phone and called Gao Xiaolong back, saying, “It went smoothly, it's resolved.”

“Old Mu, you've helped me again,” Gao Xiaolong said.

After a brief silence, Old Mu continued, “Now I happen to have something I need your help with.”

“I told you, you old rascal, but you know me, don't ask for anything that goes against your principles,” Gao Xiaolong said directly.



Chapter 296 Are you crazy? A high-ranking provincial leader actually asked you to have dinner?


Old Mu took a sip of tea, then stood up and sighed, "Help me arrange to meet Xiao Jiang. You know there's a misunderstanding between Xiao Jiang and me, so I want to clarify it. Also, that girl Mu Ya is losing her appetite and looks terrible. I don't know what's so charming about that brat Jiang Tian. I have no choice, please help me arrange to meet her."

At the Municipal Public Security Bureau, Gao Xiaolong scratched his head. He naturally knew the cause and effect of the conflict between Old Mu's family and Jiang Tian, ​​and had even been present at the scene, but only about what happened within the Municipal Public Security Bureau. He hadn't paid attention to what happened afterward.

He thought the matter was over.

"What, wasn't it resolved back then?" Gao Xiaolong asked in surprise.

"It's a long story, but tell him that Uncle Mu is divorced. I'll treat him to dinner tonight," Old Mu said directly.

Hiss!!!

Gao Xiaolong's head was buzzing.

Who is Old Mu?

Keep in mind, he was only slightly lower in rank than Minister Tan.

But it's no exaggeration to say he was a terrifyingly powerful regional official. And yet, this very official actually lowered himself to apologize?

Was the world too crazy, or was he dreaming? At this moment, even the well-informed Gao Xiaolong was completely bewildered.

Gao Xiaolong quickly replied, "Uh-oh, okay, I'll pass it on, but whether he'll agree or not, I'm not so sure."

At this very moment.

If anyone knew about their conversation, their eyes would probably pop out of their sockets.

These two terrifying regional officials.

Are they actually going to invite someone to dinner as an apology and worrying about whether the other person will come?

"Try your best. I'll host a dinner for him at the Lihua Hotel tonight. If that kid dares not to come, see how I teach him a lesson." Old Mu hung up the phone menacingly.

Gao Xiaolong stared at the disconnected phone, his lips twitching involuntarily.

However, Gao Xiaolong immediately dialed Jiang Tian's number.

As for Jiang Tian at that moment...

After informing Song Liguo, he drove away alone. All the drug dealers were already under control; Jiang Tian wouldn't be in any trouble staying.

Even if they needed to interrogate him, it wouldn't be a quick process.

Besides, Jiang Tian had another matter to attend to. Unexpectedly, Jiang Tian started the car from the driver's seat.

As for Zhao Wei in the passenger seat, Jiang Tian had already kicked him out and put him in the official car belonging to Lü Chun and his team, which was parked nearby.

"Chief Gao."

Jiang Tian started the car, drove away from the residential area, and answered Gao Xiaolong's call.

Gao Xiaolong didn't beat around the bush and directly mentioned that Elder Mu had invited him to dinner.

This made Jiang Tian frown.

Upon hearing the name "Old Master Mu," Jiang Tian instinctively thought of Liang Ru and Mu Ya. Reason told him that Liang Ru was Liang Ru and didn't represent Mu Ya.

However, even though Jiang Tian knew Mu Ya's final choice was correct, he still felt a pang of sadness, leaving him unsure how to handle their relationship.

"I won't go, I won't see her," Jiang Tian decided, choosing to avoid the trouble altogether.

It wasn't that Jiang Tian was angry with Mu Ya, but rather that he didn't know how he would deal with Liang Ru if he were with Mu Ya. Besides, Jiang Tian couldn't stand being wronged. If they were married and living together day and night, having a mother-in-law like that would likely cause chaos in the whole family. His past life had taught him a painful lesson; he couldn't repeat it in this life.

"If you're worried about Liang Ru, Old Mu asked me to tell you that Liang Ru has been divorced. Some things, I think, need to be discussed clearly, don't you think? Ambiguity is always a problem," Gao Xiaolong advised.

Actually, Gao Xiaolong didn't know about the relationship between Gao Yangyang and Jiang Tian. His original intention was still to matchmake Jiang Tian and Mu Ya.

Jiang Tian naturally understood, so his lips twitched.

He didn't know what Gao Xiaolong's expression would be if he knew the truth.

"But..." Jiang Tian thought of his uncle.

"No buts, it's settled. Old Mu's secretary has already booked the Lihua Hotel, and Old Mu is waiting. Go quickly; his secretary will naturally greet you when you arrive." Gao Xiaolong hung up the phone after saying this.

Jiang Tian, ​​however, looked puzzled. He hadn't expected such a coincidence.

His uncle was also hosting a banquet at the Lihua Hotel today.

Of course.

Why would his uncle, an official in Zhen City, choose the Lihua Hotel? It was all about prestige.

The Lihua Hotel was the most popular hotel in Yunnan Province this year, bar none. Its luxurious decor rivaled that of a five-star hotel, and it had become incredibly popular. Many wealthy people considered the Lihua Hotel their first choice, vying for it.

In this context, it represented status.

On the other hand, it was also because of his eldest cousin, Jiang Zihao.

The pride of his uncle's family.

Because his cousin, Jiang Zihao, worked in the provincial party committee. Yes, you could say that ever since Jiang Zihao joined the provincial party committee, his uncle's family had become completely arrogant.

They'd become arrogant to the point of losing all sense of propriety.

Even though Jiang Zihao had only recently been promoted to deputy section chief, there's a saying: "Even a seventh-rank official is subject to the prime minister's gate." Working in the provincial party committee, even as a lowly deputy section chief, was something that even some department-level leaders in remote areas would envy.

"But a deputy section chief hosting a banquet at the Lihua Grand Hotel—those who know him might think he's a deputy section chief, but those who don't might think he's saving money."

Jiang Tian sneered.

Although Jiang Tian had never been there, he had heard plenty of rumors about the Lihua Grand Hotel when he was down on his luck in his previous life.

There were even rumors that a single meal cost tens of thousands.

In this era, that was absolutely synonymous with luxury.

Jiang Tian instantly made up his mind: the most urgent thing was to sever ties with his uncle's family as soon as possible.

But this matter can't be rushed.

Meanwhile, at this very moment...

Inside the Lihua Hotel, Jiang Tian was unaware that a farce was unfolding.

"Sister-in-law, I'm not saying this to be mean, but don't you have any men in the house? Why are you two women coming over?"

"Jiang Peng is doing well now. He didn't even come to celebrate his nephew's promotion. I know he's jealous of Zihao's excellence since childhood, but this behavior is so unbecoming. Didn't he see that there are provincial leaders here? Isn't this ridiculous?"



Chapter 297 Turning Black into White, Distorting Right and Wrong


Outside the luxurious private room, Zheng Fang stood anxiously with Jiang Xiaoyu at the door.

"Hasn't your brother arrived yet?" Zheng Fang asked, glancing at the time.

"I just called him, and he said he's on his way," Jiang Xiaoyu replied, rubbing her forehead.

The two families were very close. Jiang Xiaoyu and Zheng Fang both understood that Jiang Yong was Jiang Peng's older brother, but the crucial point was that Jiang Peng was genuinely tied up. The aftermath at the Municipal Public Security Bureau was too important; even if something major happened at home, Jiang Peng would have to work overtime at the bureau.

As relatives, if Jiang Xiaoyu and Zheng Fang didn't come, they might face difficulties from Jiang Yong's family.

However, Zheng Fang and Jiang Xiaoyu were helpless.

The head of the family, Jiang Peng, couldn't make it, and now even Jiang Tian hadn't arrived. With time running out, the mother and daughter had no choice but to grit their teeth and enter the private room.

Actually, their plan was sound. The enormous table, large enough to seat twenty or thirty people, exuded high-end luxury.

The room was now completely full.

Jiang Tian's uncle, Jiang Yong, wasn't seated in the main seat; in fact, he sat to the side, acting as the host.

He was frequently and amicably chatting with a man in his thirties sitting at the head of the table.

However, it was obvious that the man kept checking his time, appearing somewhat indifferent. Crucially, Jiang Yong showed no annoyance whatsoever; in fact, he was even more attentive.

"I have important business at eight o'clock. What, are you missing?" The young man at the head of the table frowned slightly.

Speaking of this, Jiang Yong's anger flared, but he sighed and said, "This isn't my younger brother. He's the director of the Yunhai City Public Security Bureau. I don't know if he's too busy, but he hasn't even had his family come. Director Zeng, please don't take it to heart. He's just gotten a little capable and then got arrogant. What can I, as his older brother, do? He's my own brother after all. He looks down on people just because he has a little power. I've told him so many times, but he just won't listen."

Jiang Yong was even resorting to smearing Jiang Peng to try and build a closer relationship with the young man.

He had no choice but to try!

Jiang Yong knew very well that if this person weren't his future daughter-in-law, why would she come here to give him this face?

Because this person was not only Jiang Zihao's superior,

but also the deputy director of the provincial organization department's office.

Although her rank was deputy division chief, even half a rank lower than Jiang Yong's, what good would it do?

One was a leader of an unknown small county, while the other was in charge of all promotions and advancements across the province, demonstrating the importance and authority of the organization department.

If Jiang Zihao could establish this connection, his future would be smooth sailing.

Director Zeng's expression softened slightly, and he patted Jiang Yong's shoulder sympathetically, saying, "Brother Jiang, you're a bit older than me, yet you call me 'Dad.' Logically speaking, I shouldn't have come to your family banquet. I didn't know much about you before, but now I see you have a good character. One day, someone like him will suffer. Don't get too involved and end up dragging yourself down with him. I'm only speaking frankly to you for Yuanyuan's sake."

"Yes, I understand."

After seeing that he had established a good relationship with Director Zeng, Jiang Yong immediately clapped his hands, signaling the waiter to start serving the dishes. He then addressed the others, saying, "No more waiting, no more waiting. Director Jiang has a lot of influence; he might look down on me, his older brother. We can't just fawn over him. The person next to me is the director of the provincial organization department's office. The main thing is to entertain Director Zeng well, right everyone?"

As Jiang Yong finished speaking, the group of relatives and friends at the table finally relaxed and began to launch into a barrage of criticism against Jiang Peng.

"That's right. I heard Jiang Peng got to where he is now only because Jiang Yong spent money and connections to help him become a bureau chief. And now, after rising to power, he looks down on his elder brother."

"What a pity for you, Brother Jiang. Having a brother like that is a waste of your good intentions."

"Brother Jiang is still Brother Jiang. I've always seen Jiang Peng as cold-hearted and ungrateful."

The saying "He who eats another's bread sings his song" perfectly describes this situation. The aunts and uncles present had never seen so many high-ranking officials before.

Besides, they weren't fools.

Everyone understands what the Organization Department is, but it's responsible for promotions and assessments across the province, making it undoubtedly one of the most authoritative departments.

In ancient times, it would be the Ministry of Personnel.

Moreover, seeing Jiang Yong flaunting his position so ostentatiously, they all understood that besides Jiang Zihao's promotion, Jiang Yong was beaming with pride and might even be promoted further.

And what would that further promotion be?

Directly becoming the executive vice mayor?

To these relatives, Jiang Peng's position might not be low, but so what?

They hadn't seen how generous Jiang Yong was, and how much help he had given them. Before, when they tried to use Jiang Peng's connections, he wouldn't even acknowledge them, refusing any gifts.

With Jiang Yong leading the way, a group of distant relatives instantly launched into a barrage of criticism against Jiang Peng.

"Jiang Peng became a high-ranking official, and now everyone back home has forgotten about him. They won't even let us in with gifts, they're so arrogant. If you try to get your kids into school through connections, they won't even acknowledge you."

"They're brothers, how can there be such a huge difference?"

"Exactly! Back when Yong was in business, he made so much money, and he used it all to help Jiang Peng. If Yong hadn't gone bankrupt trying to pull strings, Jiang Peng wouldn't be where he is today. It's such a waste for Yong to have a brother like that."

"Not only is he useless, but even his son is inferior. Yong's son, Zihao, graduated from a prestigious university and even got a direct selection for a government position. I heard his good-for-nothing son went to police academy, and I don't even know how he got there. Without connections, Fan is just a menial laborer now."

Everyone started criticizing him furiously.

Distorting the facts?

No, no, no, it's a result Jiang Yong deliberately created.

It's a good thing Jiang Tian wasn't here; if he were, he probably would have flipped the table.

Jiang Peng orchestrated all that nonsense about making money in business. If it weren't for Jiang Peng back then, Jiang Yong would probably be back in his village or working as a migrant worker by now. Would he be where he is today?

He went bankrupt in his own business, and this is the kind of story that's circulating about him.

Of course, this is also why Jiang Peng rarely gets involved with these distant relatives. Jiang Tian, ​​naturally, fully supports his father's actions.

Don't be fooled by these people's fierce criticism of Jiang Peng; they're all quite pragmatic. If Jiang Peng were to host a dinner party, he could easily change their tune.



Chapter 298 One sentence shocked the entire audience: My brother is a police inspector.


This is also why Jiang Tian, ​​regardless of his past or present life, rarely had any involvement with these relatives.

And as everyone expected, Jiang Yong's face showed a satisfied smile.

Especially when people mentioned comparing themselves to Jiang Tian, ​​Jiang Zihao's face revealed a contemptuous smile.

Because from childhood, whether in school or in anything else, Jiang Tian and he were two extremes.

He didn't do well in school.

He wasn't as good as him in anything else.

Jiang Zihao received awards left and right from a young age, and was always the "model child."

Jiang Tian was like a mirror reflecting his opposite.

So Jiang Zihao stood up, and very elegantly served wine to everyone, naturally receiving a chorus of praise.

And just then.

The door was pushed open, and Zheng Fang walked in with Jiang Xiaoyu.

Actually, Jiang Xiaoyu didn't want to come. She didn't have a good impression of her uncle's family. Not only her, but Jiang Tian had also rarely interacted with his uncle's family since childhood.

From childhood, whenever they met, her uncle's family always used Jiang Tian to highlight Jiang Zihao's excellence, relentlessly belittling and insulting Jiang Tian.

The key point is that back then, Jiang Zihao was indeed showered with awards, while Jiang Tian was in a terrible state, consistently at the bottom of his class. Even in high school, Jiang Tian's grades were actually quite poor. If it weren't for his six months of relentless, all-night study in his final year, Jiang Tian wouldn't have even been able to get into police academy.

Later, her uncle's family moved to Zhen City, so the two families saw each other less often.

Now, the two families haven't met for over a year, only exchanging brief phone calls, usually between Jiang Peng and Jiang Yong.

Jiang Zihao never had any involvement with Jiang Tian and his sister among the younger generation.

However, when the two reached the door and heard what these relatives were saying, Jiang Xiaoyu's face turned bright red with anger, and her hands became ice-cold.

Zheng Fang seemed to sense Jiang Xiaoyu's emotions and gently comforted her, holding her hand.

To Zheng Fang's surprise, they didn't say anything.

The extended family members who had been gossiping about them were caught red-handed by the sudden appearance of the two women, looking around awkwardly and pretending not to see them.

However, Jiang Yong showed absolutely no remorse. Instead of feeling ashamed, he stood up and insulted Zheng Fang and Jiang Xiaoyu, saying that Jiang Peng looked down on them.

This infuriated Zheng Fang, who wanted to turn around and leave immediately.

Although Zheng Fang was usually low-key, she wasn't one to be bullied.

However, considering the relationship between Jiang Yong and Jiang Peng—after all, they were brothers and would inevitably run into each other—Zheng Fang could only force a smile to clear up the misunderstanding: "Brother, you've misunderstood. Yunhai has been in a lot of turmoil lately. Jiang Peng, as the bureau chief, has been working overtime day and night, his hair has turned completely white from worry, and he hasn't been home for three days."

"Yunhai is in such chaos, Jiang Peng bears the main responsibility. Who in the country doesn't know Yunhai is known as the 'Little Gotham City of Sin'? Ultimately, it's because the Municipal Bureau failed to do what it was supposed to do. If you're not capable, don't try to climb the ranks. Give positions to capable people; it's irresponsible to those who can't." Jiang Yong showed no mercy and left no room for retreat.

These words made Zheng Fang's heart sink.

Because...

He sensed that the visitor was up to no good.

He even smelled a hint of a trap.

This made Zheng Fang frown. "Since our eldest brother doesn't welcome us, then we won't bother him any longer."

Jiang Yong's expression instantly changed, and he slammed his hand on the table, seemingly angry. "Then I'll call and ask Jiang Peng what he's up to. Does he even respect me as his eldest brother? This is such an important celebration banquet for Zihao, and not only does he not come, but he sends you all here to slap me, his eldest brother, in the face! Does he even respect me as his eldest brother?"

Upon hearing this, Zheng Fang's expression also darkened.

Zheng Fang knew this eldest brother all too well.

They hadn't been in contact for over a year because they had realized early on that this eldest brother was not someone they could get along with.

He spent his days studying crooked and wicked methods; sooner or later he'd be in jail.

Furthermore, as the eldest brother, he often tried to get Jiang Peng to do things against the rules, which the straightforward Jiang Peng simply couldn't tolerate, hence the gradual reduction in contact.

Jiang Yong had actually boasted in Zhen City that he had a brother, the bureau chief Yunhai, who could solve all sorts of problems, etc. In the end, Jiang Peng refused to do so, which completely offended Jiang Yong, causing Jiang Yong to harbor a deep-seated prejudice against them.

Zheng Fang never expected things to turn out this way.

If she had known, Zheng Fang regretted it somewhat; she should have stopped Jiang Peng from bringing Jiang Yong into the system in the first place.

“Yes, Second Aunt, you don’t have a problem with me, do you?” Jiang Zihao stood up, his smile carrying a hint of sarcasm.

“If Second Aunt doesn’t have a problem with me, then come and sit down. By the way, Second Uncle isn’t here. Where’s Jiang Tian? Second Uncle is the bureau chief, he’s extremely busy. Jiang Tian is just a junior intern; he can’t possibly be working overtime too, can he?”

Jiang Zihao, like a smiling tiger, invited her with a smile, but his words were laced with barbs and difficulties.

This made Zheng Fang’s heart sink.

“My brother got promoted a long time ago, he’s a Level 3 Police Inspector now,” Jiang Xiaoyu couldn’t help but say.

“Pfft!!!”

Jiang Zihao, who was drinking water, instantly spat it out.

The other relatives didn’t react immediately, but as Jiang Zihao spat out water, even Jiang Yong and Director Zeng almost burst out laughing.

Director Zeng, in particular, coughed twice, his face strange, and said, “That Jiang Tian, ​​he shouldn’t be older than Zihao, right?”

Jiang Yong nodded: “He’s a senior in college and hasn’t graduated yet.”

“Cough cough!!!”

Chief Zeng coughed twice frantically: "Young lady, you seem to have some misunderstanding about police ranks. What you're referring to as 'meritorious service promotion' should be Third-Class Superintendent. Like the civil service system, promotions are every three years. You need at least three more years to reach Second-Class Superintendent, then another three years to First-Class Superintendent. Only after a very successful and outstanding achievement can you have a chance to become a Third-Class Superintendent by the time you turn thirty. Don't get it wrong next time."

"Young and ignorant, Xiaoyu, don't make a fool of yourself in front of outsiders," Jiang Yong said, his brow twitching.

"Alright, Xiaoyu, stop talking nonsense," Zheng Fang hurriedly pulled Jiang Xiaoyu back from explaining further.

This left Jiang Xiaoyu seething with anger.

Because she clearly remembered that her older brother was a Third-Class Superintendent; he wouldn't lie to her.



Chapter 299 Furious: You bullied my sister? You're fired!


"I bet 'getting a permanent position' is just a joke. The probationary period alone takes two or three years. Do you know what a police inspector is? That's equivalent to a section chief! I graduated from a top university and was selected to work in the provincial capital. I've had a smooth career for over ten years, and I'm only just about to become a deputy section chief. A police inspector is already a section chief! Hahaha..." Jiang Zihao burst into laughter.

"My brother wouldn't lie," Jiang Xiaoyu said angrily.

From the moment she entered, she could tell these people were targeting them.

Jiang Peng's rank was lower than Jiang Yong's, so they could only attack him from other angles. Jiang Tian was being relentlessly criticized, and Jiang Xiaoyu couldn't stand it anymore.

Because she knew her brother had made many contributions.

In her mind, her brother never lied; he was omnipotent.

Jiang Zihao: "Hahaha, a police inspector, a section chief?"

Even Director Zeng laughed: "Brother Jiang, your niece is adorably foolish!"

This seemed to make Jiang Yong feel he had lost face, and he couldn't help but coldly reprimand her: "Jiang Xiaoyu, you're a top student, how can you be so foolish? Don't say such embarrassing and unrealistic things."

Jiang Xiaoyu's eyes were red.

These people were bullying them.

Jiang Xiaoyu hated these people.

She could even sense that even her uncle looked down on them from the bottom of his heart.

And their appearance completely chilled Zheng Fang's heart.

The last glimmer of hope for family affection deep in her heart shattered completely.

Zheng Fang grabbed Jiang Xiaoyu's hand to comfort her, stood up, and wanted to pull Jiang Xiaoyu away.

She made up her mind that no matter what happened in the future, she would never contact them again, severing all ties completely.

But the next moment, before Zheng Fang could act, a voice suddenly rang out from the doorway.

“Is it funny?”

A cold, questioning voice rang out.

This instantly silenced the laughter in the room.

Then everyone turned to look at the doorway.

It turned out that Jiang Tian had appeared there sometime earlier.

Actually, Jiang Tian had already arrived downstairs at the hotel, but he had run into Old Master Mu and his secretary, who had invited him to dinner.

As for Mu Feng and his daughter Mu Ya, they hadn't arrived yet, as their home was a bit far away; they would be there soon.

Downstairs at the hotel, Jiang Tian exchanged a few words with Old Master Mu.

After seeing Old Master Mu to his private room, Jiang Tian remembered that he had an important matter to attend to, so he hurriedly said goodbye to Old Master Mu and his secretary.

He had just arrived at the door when he heard Jiang Xiaoyu's words, which warmed his heart for a moment. But the next moment, he heard his sister being viciously mocked and even scolded by Jiang Yong.

His most beloved sister was being called a disgrace?

Who does Jiang Yong think he is?

Without Jiang Peng bringing him into the system, without Jiang Peng giving him his resources, could he have climbed so quickly after starting out?

Without Jiang Peng, Jiang Yong, who is now a high-ranking official, would probably still be a migrant worker somewhere.

And now he's sitting here, scolding and insulting his own sister?

Jiang Tian, ​​who had initially intended to plan slowly, or even just deal with things temporarily, instantly exploded with his short temper.

Sure enough.

The moment he uttered a sound,

he immediately attracted everyone's attention.

To be honest, after looking around, Jiang Tian realized, very well, he knew all these distant relatives.

In his previous life, when his mother was seriously ill and his father was expelled from the Party, he had witnessed the ugly faces of these relatives firsthand. It perfectly illustrated the saying: when you have money and power, these people fawn over you. Even if Jiang Yong spoke harshly, they wouldn't dare completely offend Jiang Peng.

But what about after they lost power?

Every single one of them, almost all of them, kicked him when he was down. Forget about helping them; for example, when his great-aunt's cousin lent them five thousand yuan for his elderly relative's medical treatment, Jiang Peng gave it to them without hesitation.

As a result, when Zheng Fang was seriously ill from worry in prison, she borrowed money everywhere for medical treatment. This family not only refused to lend them money, but they also refused to repay the five thousand yuan they had lent them before.

Jiang Tian didn't even dare to look at them for long, because he was afraid he would impulsively flip the table and kill them.

"Oh, Jiang Tian, ​​you're finally here. Did you hear what your sister just said? It seems your sister has many misunderstandings about you. Why don't you explain it to her?" Jiang Zihao stood up with a smile on his face.

It seemed like he was acting out of kindness, but Jiang Zihao was insidiously bringing up the old story again. If it wasn't true, he wanted to embarrass Jiang Tian's sister in front of everyone again.

But the next moment, Jiang Tian said something shocking: "Who said my sister was wrong?"

Buzz!!!

Everyone present was stunned. Even Director Zeng, who was sipping tea and watching the commotion, froze for a moment, staring at Jiang Tian with a look of astonishment.

"Xiao Jiang, your sister is foolish. You yourself are making a fool of yourself in the police system. Director Zeng is right here. Listen to me, stop making these jokes. Learning from your brother Zihao is far better than anything else. Stop acting so irresponsibly. Your brother Zihao is already thirty and about to be promoted to deputy section chief. Did you hear what he said? Do you think you can get promoted by daydreaming all day?" Jiang Yong seemed to be earnestly admonishing him.

Jiang Zihao humbly replied, "There are many hidden talents in the province. Even I know I'm not capable enough. I was promoted thanks to the organization's support and the leadership's trust. If I have the opportunity, I really want to continue gaining experience as a staff member."

See? See? The contrast is obvious.

"Zihao is too modest."

"Modesty is fine, but excessive modesty is also wrong. Zihao is so outstanding, I think the organization's promotion of you is very reasonable!"

"Yes, the promotion list has already been released. Even if you don't want to be promoted, it's too late."

Even Director Zeng said with some approval, "Zihao, you're being too modest. The list has already been released, and your promotion has been approved. Even if I wanted to stop the promotion, I couldn't. Not even my superiors could do that. So, Zihao, work harder in the future."

But before Director Zeng could finish speaking, a soft voice rang out.

After all, this was about changing the list.

This list had already passed through layers of review.

Even his superiors couldn't do that.

Director Zeng was equally unabashed.

However, to everyone's surprise, a soft voice suddenly rang out amidst the flattery.

"Brother Zihao, if you didn't want to do it, you should have said so earlier. I can totally help you. You guys always say our family won't help you, right? Today I'll personally help you. Don't worry, I'll handle this for you, I'll make sure you can't get promoted."



Chapter 300 Expanding His Network, Yunhai Walks Sideways


"You help me? Hahahaha, Xiao Jiang, stop joking, hahaha..." Jiang Zihao, picking at his ear, almost thought he'd misheard, then burst out laughing.

He wasn't just being modest in front of Director Zeng, but Jiang Tian actually took it seriously. Besides, even if he didn't want to be promoted, could a mere policeman like him possibly ruin things?

Does he even know where this order came from?

Does he know the promotion list has already been approved, and even Director Zeng can't remove it?

His intention in saying that, and Jiang Yong's intention in saying it, was to set up a conversation where they could subtly suggest that Zihao wasn't capable enough and ask Director Zeng to promote and mentor him, thus naturally establishing a connection and common ground.

But unexpectedly, Jiang Tian interrupted them halfway through, and not only that, Jiang Tian's words were also endearingly foolish.

"Xiao Jiang, you're in the police system now. I don't know if you've been officially promoted, but you should have been exposed to the provincial decisions. Even Director Zeng can't change them. Don't joke around. Aren't you just making a fool of yourself in front of so many people?"

Almost instantly.

Jiang Yong's face turned cold. After reprimanding Jiang Tian, ​​he turned his attention to Zheng Fang, saying, "Sister-in-law, how did you raise your children? If they're incompetent, that's their problem. But raising two children to be so ignorant is a serious problem for you parents. Take them home and discipline them properly. Don't bring them out here to embarrass yourselves and become a laughingstock."

"Yes, yes, even if they're not as outstanding as Zihao, they can't be this stupid!"

"Even if I'm not in the system, I know what's going on here. This Xiao Jiang is simply too ridiculous."

"Don't think he can be so foolish as to take someone's politeness seriously. And this lowly intern policeman can change the province's decision? Hahaha!"

"So stupid!"

"Yeah, like begets like, and that saying is so true. Both kids are so stupid, I wonder what their parents are like."

"They're cousins, but their fates are so different. Look at Zihao, how humble and sensible he is, and how excellent he is at everything he does. As cousins, with some blood relation, there's such a huge contrast, how can he be so stupid?"

At that moment, with Jiang Yong's words, the sounds of criticism and condemnation exploded.

After everyone reacted, they mocked Jiang Tian, ​​pointing fingers at him.

A joke.

Although Jiang Tian's family had connections, and Jiang Peng was the bureau chief, Jiang Peng had said he wouldn't do anything for anyone, and no one should come to him, because he wouldn't help.

In contrast, Jiang Yong not only helped them whenever he could, but also promoted them, and there was even a director from the provincial organization department present.

Compared to him, they naturally knew who to curry favor with.

These words made Zheng Fang turn pale with anger, and Jiang Xiaoyu was also furious.

But Jiang Tian remained completely unperturbed.

Since Jiang Zihao didn't want a promotion, Jiang Tian wasn't worried about helping him out. A promotion was out of the question, but a demotion was a request Jiang Tian had never heard before.

From the moment he entered, he relentlessly insulted them, using the insult to his family to curry favor with this so-called director.

He sat in the main seat, acting all high and mighty.

In the eyes of ordinary people, even those of higher rank like Jiang Peng and Jiang Yong, the Director of the Organization Department was superior.

Because even a minor official has power.

It's like a small section chief in the capital city managing national affairs, or even a high-ranking provincial leader who has to queue up for an appointment to discuss local development.

While Director Zeng wasn't from the capital and didn't have that much influence,

even at the provincial level, he was far beyond the reach of a mere county magistrate.

Of course, while this was incredible to ordinary people, it meant nothing to Jiang Tian.

So Jiang Tian calmly said, "It's nothing, a small matter. It's just helping to cancel your promotion. It's rare for Brother Zihao to have this kind of awareness; how could I, as his younger brother, not lend a hand? I can solve it for you with a simple phone call."

A flicker of panic crossed Jiang Yong's face.

Because he was afraid Jiang Tian might actually do that. Could it be that he had misjudged Jiang Tian—wasn't just a good-for-nothing, but actually knew some powerful figure behind the scenes?

But the next moment, Director Zeng abruptly stood up and said, "What arrogance! You're like a frog in a well, thinking you can get Zihao's promotion canceled just because you know a couple of people with some connections. Even I can't stop Zihao's promotion now. It's what everyone wants. So young man, being foolish isn't your fault, but showing your foolishness is."

As soon as Director Zeng stood up, the last flicker of worry in Jiang Yong's heart vanished. He stood up and smiled humbly, "Director Zeng, you're joking. Little Jiang, if you're so capable, then tell Zihao to cancel his promotion immediately. Zihao is a pragmatic person and needs more training. The organization wants to give him more responsibility, and since the organization needs him, Zihao can only try his best under Director Zeng's guidance and support. If you can stop it, I'll really thank you."

Jiang Zihao also retorted with a mocking smile, "Little Jiang, learn your lesson. Don't say things so carelessly in the future."

To be honest.

This was the first time Jiang Tian had heard of such a request, and without hesitation, he took out his phone.

Actually, Jiang Tian's first thought was to call Sun Feng, because Sun Feng was Zhou Zhao's secretary.

Although Zhou Zhao was the Party Secretary of Yunhai, it's important to remember that his position in Yunhai was high-ranking. Because it was the provincial capital, Zhou Zhao was both the top leader of Yunhai and a member of the Standing Committee of the Yunnan Provincial Committee.

Normally, Zhou Zhao worked in the Yunhai Municipal Government, but he also had an office in the provincial government.

In other words, Zhou Zhao's position was exactly the same as that of Da Kang in the novel Jiang Tian had read about in his previous life.

This matter could be resolved by contacting Secretary Sun Feng.

However, Jiang Tian suddenly remembered the phone number of Old Master Mu's secretary, which he had just saved on his phone that day.

Although Elder Mu was in charge of law enforcement, in terms of influence within the province, Zhou Zhao was far inferior to him.

Jiang Tian and Mu Ya were still considering their relationship.

But now that Elder Mu had personally intervened, Jiang Tian knew that Elder Mu had shown sincerity, and continuing to pursue the matter would be in his own fault.

Liang Ru had already been divorced, so Jiang Tian wasn't particularly concerned about the minor misunderstanding with Mu Ya.

In that case, Jiang Tian would naturally make use of Elder Mu's connections and influence; having that connection was far better than relying on Sun Feng.

"Hello, Secretary Cheng."



Chapter 301: Changing Decision in Three Minutes, Getn a Beating?


Jiang Tian glanced at the filthy face of the man at the table, smiled indifferently. Since they had this request, Jiang Tian would naturally fulfill it.

Meanwhile,

In a more upscale private room on the upper floor of the grand hotel, Secretary Cheng stood at the door, essentially acting as a waiter.

During their initial meeting, Secretary Cheng had deliberately tried to cultivate a relationship with Jiang Tian, ​​hoping to befriend him.

Because Secretary Cheng followed Elder Mu, he knew a great deal about Elder Mu's affairs, including matters concerning the Municipal Bureau.

He understood all too well how terrifying and brilliant Jiang Tian's future prospects were.

Even during a conversation between Elder Mu and Gao Xiaolong, when Secretary Cheng happened to go in to deliver water, he overheard them discussing how a certain bigwig in Beijing had taken notice of Jiang Tian and was likely to personally meet with him soon.

Hiss!!!

Who could resist such a thing?

Such a promising man was someone Secretary Cheng naturally wanted to befriend as quickly as possible.

He had assumed Jiang Tian would soon forget him, but to his utter surprise, Jiang Tian called so quickly.

“Brother Jiang, what’s up?” Secretary Cheng asked softly into the phone.

“Have Mu Ya and the others gone?” Jiang Tian inquired first.

“They’re already in the private room. Brother Jiang, when are you coming? Has the matter been resolved? If anything happens in Yunnan Province, just let me know, I’ll help you,” Secretary Cheng said.

Because Jiang Tian had just said that an acquaintance was downstairs and he had some urgent business to attend to, but would be back soon.

Therefore, Secretary Cheng naturally inquired, and deliberately tried to befriend him with utmost sincerity.

As he spoke, Secretary Cheng cautiously glanced inside the room. Inside, Old Master Mu sat in the main seat, eyes closed, resting.

Below him, Mu Feng and Mu Ya sat. Mu Ya kept her head down and remained silent, while Mu Feng seemed somewhat restless.

Seeing this scene, Secretary Cheng couldn't help but sigh inwardly.

This was a big shot! When had Old Master Mu ever treated someone to such an invitation and apologized to anyone? Secretary Cheng hadn't even dared to dream of such a thing in the past.

Therefore, Secretary Cheng's resolve to befriend Jiang Tian only strengthened. He was capable and had a bright future, and crucially, Jiang Tian had a terrifying backer.

Even with Old Master Mu only having one son and granddaughter.

Mu Feng was a businessman, and Mu Ya was a journalism major. If Jiang Tian and Mu Ya married, it was conceivable that Jiang Tian would inherit almost all of Old Master Mu's political legacy.

Hiss!!!

Thinking about this, Secretary Cheng felt a slight toothache.

"Speaking of which, there really is something I need Brother Cheng's help with," Jiang Tian said calmly.

If Secretary Cheng had hesitated even slightly, Jiang Tian wouldn't have continued the conversation. But Secretary Cheng was shrewd; without a second's hesitation, she immediately said, "Brother Jiang, don't be so polite. Your problems are my problems. I'd even like to make time to become sworn brothers with you, like brothers. Just tell me what you need, and I'll do what I can. Even if it's not possible, I'll find a way."

Secretary Cheng's attitude made Jiang Tian slightly take notice.

"Being sworn brothers is one thing, but the most crucial thing in this business is to be careful at every turn, otherwise you might get dragged down with you."

"Well, I have a cousin who works in the provincial organization department, and he's about to be promoted," Jiang Tian said casually.

"Good, that's a small matter. Brother Jiang, what's his name? Leave this to me. By the way, what's his position?" Secretary Cheng paused slightly to give himself some leeway.

"Just a junior staff member being promoted to deputy section chief," Jiang Tian said.

Secretary Cheng immediately breathed a sigh of relief: "A small matter, haha, a small matter. Although the recent selection list has already been submitted, Brother Jiang, don't worry, I'll handle it for you. I know the leaders in the organization department very well; we even had dinner together yesterday. What's his name? I'll add him in."
