Secretary Cheng naturally assumed this was a favor for someone he knew, but it wasn't a big deal; it was just a junior staff member, so it wouldn't take him much effort.

Jiang Tian immediately followed up, "His name is Jiang Zihao, a clerk in the Organization Department. But Secretary Cheng, you've misunderstood. He was already on the promotion list. I'm not here because of the promotion, but because my cousin told me he's not capable enough to handle the organization's responsibilities. He asked if I could cancel his promotion and let him gain more experience at the grassroots level. I was moved by his sincerity, so I made an exception and asked you for help, Secretary Cheng."

*Buzz!*

Secretary Cheng was completely bewildered, but as a secretary, her most important skill was reading between the lines. Almost instantly, she guessed the situation perfectly.

Now that she understood, Secretary Cheng's eyes lit up. "No problem, no problem! I'll call the leaders in the Organization Department right now, Brother Jiang. I can guarantee you, he'll receive the call canceling the promotion in three minutes."

Jiang Tian smiled upon hearing this.

It would probably be quite troublesome to pull strings for a promotion.

However, not wanting a promotion and preferring to hone one's skills at the grassroots level is truly a simple matter of choice; one person, one position.

Countless people are eagerly awaiting promotions. With so many talented individuals, even if you're not promoted, more will take your place.

"Brother Jiang, tell me your address, and I'll come pick you up." Secretary Cheng was meticulous in her work, even going so far as to support Jiang Tian.

Jiang Tian hesitated for a moment, but finally gave her the address.

Actually, Jiang Tian hadn't expected such a coincidence.

He hadn't planned for the two families to meet so soon.

But now, since they were already here, and Zheng Fang and Jiang Xiaoyu were both here, he couldn't very well send them back in the pouring rain while he ate here.

So he would definitely take them upstairs later.

Thinking this, Jiang Tian's face stiffened. Good heavens, this... wasn't this a meeting between in-laws?

Hiss!!!

Jiang Tian's eyelids twitched violently.

To those present, this scene appeared to be Jiang Tian's guilty conscience.

"What's wrong, Xiao Jiang? Can't you even do this little thing I asked you to do?"

Jiang Zihao, now much more composed, stood up and asked Jiang Tian with a broad smile.

“Xiao Jiang, that’s a provincial decision, something you can’t change. Even Director Zeng doesn’t dare say a word here. Do you think you have more power than Director Zeng? It’s not like your uncle is teaching you, but with your stupid brain, once you’re in the system, don’t you know to keep your mouth shut? How have I always told you? Don’t strive to be as outstanding as your brother Zihao; being half as good is enough…” Jiang Yong stood up, his face ostensibly full of concern for Jiang Tian.

But Jiang Tian had never forgotten that.

What kind of bullshit education is that?



Chapter 302 One phone call, and Brother Zihao completely broke down.


Jiang Tian vividly remembered the days when they used to keep in frequent contact. Every time they met, Jiang Tian became the object of his uncle's boasting about his son.

From comparing his son's academic performance to his character and conduct, Jiang Tian was belittled in every way.

Now, he openly and subtly belittled Jiang Tian, ​​even treating him like a joke.

In the past, Jiang Peng treated him like a brother, genuinely caring for him.

He especially wanted to maintain that relationship.

Jiang Peng's thinking had always been rooted in the values ​​of a large family.

Later, Jiang Peng realized that this older brother didn't even consider his family, especially Jiang Tian, ​​as human beings, and gradually, Jiang Peng distanced himself from his uncle.

"Uncle, so many years have passed, and you haven't changed a bit," Jiang Tian sighed.

They were all family, and if it weren't for the final outcome, Jiang Tian truly didn't want things to turn out this badly.

It's just that his uncle's actions went too far.

Jiang Tian had no choice but to go even further.

“Nephew, what do you mean by that? Are you implying that your uncle is wrong? Is this how you treat your elders? Not only are you stupid, you don't even know how to respect your elders! How did your father raise you? You've become such a bastard! We have distinguished guests here, and I don't want to argue with you. You've been standing here for so long, and you still haven't embarrassed yourself enough? What are you doing standing there?” Jiang Yong didn't want to say anything more to Jiang Tian, ​​slamming his hand on the table, his face filled with undisguised disgust.

Jiang Tian didn't know where he or his family had offended Jiang Yong to warrant such a hostile attitude.

Jiang Tian couldn't understand it before, but now he did. With the "King of Criminal Investigation" entry, Jiang Tian could easily deduce that Jiang Yong was a typical example of someone with a weak and fragile ego at play.

As the eldest brother in the family, he was always overshadowed by his younger brother.

Finally, he even had to rely on his brother's help to get into the system. For Jiang Yong, this was an insult, a personal humiliation.

His pride was severely wounded, so instead of being grateful to Jiang Peng, he even harbored resentment towards his younger brother's family. He then sought solace in Jiang Peng, relentlessly comparing his son's achievements to Jiang Tian's.

This is a direct manifestation of a personality trait in psychology.

“Xiao Jiang, don't upset your uncle. Everyone was just joking. Please take a seat.”

“Hahaha, your uncle and Zihao were just joking, and you actually took it seriously.”

“Yeah, do you really think you can get your brother Zihao to cancel his promotion and overturn the provincial decision?”

“Who says I can’t?”

Just as the various relatives were trying to smooth things over, Jiang Tian's words instantly created an oppressive atmosphere.

Even Director Zeng looked at Jiang Tian, ​​his brow furrowing slightly. This young man was somewhat ungrateful.

“Fine, I'll watch from here. If you can get Zihao's promotion canceled, I'll take your surname from now on,” Director Zeng said, slightly annoyed.

"Jiang Tian, ​​you good-for-nothing! You've angered Director Zeng! Apologize now!" Jiang Zihao suddenly stood up and roared.

Just then,

Director Zeng's phone rang.

Director Zeng glanced at his phone and said to Jiang Yong, "Brother Jiang, I need to take this call. It's our Organization Minister."

"The Minister's call? Answer it right away!" Jiang Yong's heart skipped a beat.

The provincial Organization Minister—to him, that was a huge deal!

"I guess it's about confirming promotions," Director Zeng said casually, completely ignoring Jiang Tian.

Knowing that Jiang Tian was just a recent graduate, perhaps even a junior police officer who hadn't yet been officially promoted, Director Zeng had lost all interest in even glancing at him.

However, while answering the phone, Director Zeng said to Jiang Tian, ​​"Kid, this is our Organization Minister's call. He's probably calling to verify our promotion list. These promotions can't be changed unless the Minister himself approves. Seeing that you're new to the system, I'm already letting you know a thing or two, you're so clueless."

After saying this, he completely ignored Jiang Tian and answered the phone, his expression instantly becoming cautious. "Minister, it's so late, haven't you rested yet?"

Director Zeng even put it on speakerphone to confirm its authenticity.

The Minister's voice quickly came through: "Enough with the other stuff, what's the deal with that Jiang Zihao in your department?"

Buzz!!!

Director Zeng's head exploded.

Jiang Yong, standing beside him, also felt a chill.

Jiang Zihao, who had stood up and mocked Jiang Tian, ​​also froze.

The extended family didn't dare utter a sound.

"Minister, what...what do you mean?" Director Zeng asked, bewildered.

The minister, without any preamble, stated directly: "Regarding Jiang Zihao in your department, I heard he feels his abilities are insufficient. Therefore, I have decided to temporarily cancel his promotion. Since he feels his abilities are lacking, let him hone his skills at the grassroots level. He shouldn't stay in the Organization Department. Fortunately, there's a recent plan for a batch of cadres to go to the countryside for training. Since he wants to hone his skills, let him go to the countryside."

*Buzz!*

As soon as the minister finished speaking,

everyone present was stunned.

Director Zeng jumped to his feet: "Minister, you...you're not joking, are you?"

"What joke? Others can go to the countryside, why can't he? He himself feels his abilities are insufficient. Promoting him would be irresponsible towards the people. It's rare to find Comrade Zihao with such high awareness; we must support him. After all, whether you're a leader or a janitor, it's all about serving the people."

After speaking, the minister didn't even give Director Zeng a chance to speak before hanging up the phone abruptly.

Director Zeng, holding his phone, stared in disbelief.

As for Jiang Zihao, his face was completely dumbfounded.

Jiang Yong jumped to his feet, glaring at Jiang Tian with furious rage: "You little beast, what have you done?"

He's gone mad.

Completely mad.

From a promising cadre in the provincial organization department—you know, the provincial level is half a rank higher, and promotion within the government, even a transfer to a rural area, is still a half-rank or a full rank improvement.

Many promotion opportunities, little competition.

And in the countryside?

A clerk sent to the countryside?

It's safe to say that, barring any unforeseen circumstances, Jiang Zihao's future is completely ruined.

Unless Jiang Zihao, like Jiang Tian, ​​receives some kind of cheat code along the way, he'll just have to experience it for himself.

He'll get a taste of the hardships of working at the grassroots level.

Mainly, Jiang Tian hadn't expected Secretary Cheng to be so reliable, not only reliable, but also doing a fantastic job, going straight to the countryside.

"Who did you just call?" Even Director Zeng, if he was slow-witted, realized it was the call Jiang Tian had just made.

The next moment, click! The door opened.

Accompanied by this, Secretary Cheng's face appeared before Director Zeng.

"Give me..."

Boom!!!



Chapter 303 A Sensation: Old Master Mu Actually Invited You, a Useless Trash, to Dinner


As the door opened, Secretary Cheng stepped into the room from outside.

Before anyone could react, she was beside Jiang Tian.

At the same time,

Director Zeng, who had just stood up, his face grim, feeling humiliated and about to berate Jiang Tian, ​​froze in place.

It wouldn't matter if others didn't know.

The key point was...

How could Director Zeng not recognize Secretary Cheng? The secretary of the top official in the provincial political and legal system.

In fact, someone of Director Zeng's rank wouldn't even bother with him.

What? You say the secretary's position is too high? Actually, their positions are similar, but their career prospects and futures are on completely different levels.

A secretary to a high-ranking leader is not on the same level as a junior director in a department like his.

Like the level Secretary Cheng interacts with, it's basically at least the level of the Organization Department head.

"Who are you?" Jiang Yong asked, utterly astonished.

But at this moment, Director Zeng suddenly spoke up, "Secretary Cheng, what brings you here?"

"Secretary Cheng?" Jiang Yong's mind went blank.

A secretary.

Is that something just anyone can do?

And since she's a secretary, what level of leadership, what department, does she serve?

As a leader within the system, how could Jiang Yong not know?

Jiang Zihao's face turned deathly pale.

His mind was spinning. Wasn't Jiang Tian just a lowly policeman, a junior intern?

Why would he know Secretary Cheng?

Jiang Zihao, who worked in the provincial organization department, although Secretary Cheng didn't know him, had seen her in his department before, but only from afar, because at that time, Secretary Cheng was meeting and chatting with their department head.

He wasn't even qualified to serve her tea or water.

Unexpectedly, in the next moment, Secretary Cheng calmly said, "Today, Secretary Mu invited Brother Jiang to dinner at this hotel. Who knew someone would ask Brother Jiang for a favor? I happen to be free, so I'll do Brother Jiang a small favor."

Boom!!!

These words were like a bolt from the blue, instantly exploding in everyone's minds.

Secretary Mu?

Director Zeng stared at Jiang Tian in utter shock.

That secretary?

Jiang Yong plopped down on his chair, while Jiang Zihao's eyes were practically red with rage. Why? Why did Jiang Tian, ​​this good-for-nothing, get invited?

Jiang Tian, ​​who had always been inferior to him in everything, why was he invited by Secretary Mu?

The Jiang Yong father and son, along with Director Zeng, were all people within the system. Even if they hadn't met Secretary Mu, they had heard of him.

He was the true head of Yunhai's political and legal system!

To put it bluntly, the license plate number was practically 003.

Someone of that caliber actually invited Jiang Tian to dinner?

That means...

Jiang Tian wasn't just bragging; they genuinely could do it.

Jiang Yong abruptly stood up and blurted out, "Secretary Cheng, I'm Xiao Jiang's uncle. I was just joking; it was a misunderstanding."

Secretary Cheng's face instantly lit up with a smile, but it was tinged with sarcasm.

Jiang Tian's smile was similar.

Now he's the uncle again.

I hadn't heard them mention Uncle before, but now they're starting to talk about him.

Especially Secretary Cheng. Every secretary is incredibly shrewd and capable. They serve their superiors and manage relationships across all levels. What the leader can easily handle, the secretary handles; what the leader can't handle, the secretary still has to handle.

Secretary Cheng knew perfectly well what had happened.

"Whether it's a joke or not, that's none of my business. Brother Jiang asked me for help; after all, it's rare to find a comrade with such high awareness, how could we not be obliged?" Secretary Cheng said with a faint smile.

"No, no, no, Secretary Cheng, it was just a joke, just a joke with my cousin." Jiang Zihao was almost crying.

Damn it.

From a promising political star, just about to muster his courage and make a big splash.

But then he turned around and found himself gone.

Gone with his promotion.

Even his job in the province was gone; he was sent directly to the countryside, demoted to the grassroots level. What was this all about?

To put it nicely, high awareness.

To put it bluntly, he's already marginalized.

Unless he gets some stroke of luck, or truly achieves great success and political accomplishments, Jiang Zihao's life is practically half ruined.

Of course, that's assuming no one mentors him.

"I'm telling you, comrade, your awareness is seriously questionable. Can you joke about something like this? Can you joke about the Party and the people? This was just a small test from the Party, and you went back on your word. I thought you were a promising young man, but now it seems you should go to the countryside for some training."

Secretary Cheng immediately lectured Jiang Zihao with a righteous expression.

This completely bewildered Jiang Zihao.

But no matter how bewildered Jiang Zihao was, Jiang Tian had no desire to continue.

"Mom, sister, let's go. Secretary Mu is still waiting. He just came here to call you up," Jiang Tian said to Zheng Fang.

"Me, us?" Zheng Fang was startled.

Jiang Xiaoyu, her face beaming with excitement and joy, looked at Jiang Tian with eyes practically sparkling with adoration.

Yes, it had to be her omnipotent older brother!

"That's right, Secretary Mu just heard that you, Madam, are having dinner downstairs, and he specially invited you and Brother Jiang to come up. You know, Secretary Mu is very curious about what kind of family could raise such an outstanding person as Brother Jiang, and he specifically wanted to learn from you." Secretary Cheng spoke very pleasantly.

But these words instantly slapped everyone present in the face.

Just now, everyone had been attacking Zheng Fang, mocking her children as stupid and useless.

What kind of education could produce such useless children?

They had utterly belittled Jiang Xiaoyu and Jiang Tian.

But now?

In the blink of an eye.

Secretary Cheng arrived, instantly slapping everyone in the face.

Secretary Mu personally sought advice on how to cultivate such outstanding talent.

Jiang Yong's face flushed red and then turned pale.

For someone as proud as Jiang Yong, this was worse than death.

Because this was the only thing he could boast about—his son, Zihao, who was countless times more outstanding than Jiang Tian. This was the only source of his sense of self-worth he could find.

Even with a lower position than Jiang Peng, as the eldest brother, this was the only sense of self-worth he could find.

But now, he was being utterly crushed.

Especially remembering what he had just said, Jiang Yong's face was almost burning red.



Chapter 304 News Broadcast: The Six-King of Merit is Just the Beginning


What Jiang Yong found most unacceptable was that Jiang Tian hadn't uttered a single word throughout, yet this disregard dealt a severe blow to Jiang Yong's pride.

After Secretary Cheng and Jiang Tian left with Jiang Xiaoyu and Zheng Fang,

the scene remained completely silent. No one dared to speak.

The scene was utterly silent.

Because just moments before, everyone had been hurling insults and accusations at Jiang Tian and his group.

And now, the backlash had come so quickly.

"Um, I just remembered, I have something to attend to, so I won't stay here any longer. You all eat."

Just then, Director Zeng abruptly stood up, hurriedly said this, and then rushed away.

Jiang Yong's face darkened even more.

But this was still not the end.

Jiang Zihao was accompanied by a woman, a young woman, probably around twenty-six or twenty-seven years old. While not exceptionally beautiful, she had a certain charm that grew on you, and she hadn't uttered a single word throughout.

Even as others mocked and ridiculed Jiang Tian and Zheng Fang, this woman remained silent.

That's right.

This is Yuan Yuan, Jiang Zihao's girlfriend, or rather, his soon-to-be fiancée.

She possesses a refined and elegant demeanor, typical of a woman working within the system.

But now, Yuan Yuan's eyes flickered.

Her mind was racing.

"This good-for-nothing, how does he...how does he even deserve this?"

Jiang Zihao was unwilling to accept reality, because in his mind, Jiang Tian was inferior to him in every way.

Then he suddenly discovered that Jiang Tian had such a powerful figure by his side.

He even knew Secretary Cheng, and Secretary Mu had treated him to dinner. This was simply unacceptable to Jiang Zihao.

But he had met Secretary Cheng in his department.

Therefore, Secretary Cheng couldn't possibly be lying to him about this.

But this was also what hurt him the most.

"Yeah, he's just a lowly trainee police officer. Why would he know people like Secretary Cheng and Secretary Mu?" Jiang Yong's face was ashen.

Some people are like that. Not everyone is a sycophant. Psychologically speaking, some people have extremely strong self-esteem.

Even if you're successful, your family or friends won't be happy; they'll be filled with resentment.

"Didn't you all hear that he never said he was a trainee? He said from the beginning that he was a police inspector. 'Trainee' is just your wishful thinking," Yuan Yuan said, standing up.

Sure enough.

With Yuan Yuan's words, everyone's faces were filled with astonishment and disbelief.

"Yuan Yuan, you're actually defending that good-for-nothing! He hasn't even graduated yet!" Jiang Zihao said angrily.

Yuanyuan, however, showed no anger: "This is just wishful thinking on your part. If you can't do it yourself, why do you assume others can't? The facts are right in front of you, yet you're still deceiving yourselves. Actually, you should thank me for coming today, for letting me see your true colors. Pathetic and pathetic. Let's end this now."

Buzz!!!

Misfortunes never come singly.

Jiang Zihao was completely dumbfounded.

End?

End with Yuanyuan?

Of course.

That's why the private rooms here are so high-end. They even have large TVs inside—though they're bulky, but for this era, they were considered very impressive and high-class.

And this happened to be the time when the news was broadcast.

As Yuanyuan reached the door, the news broadcast started on the TV.

Answerer: [In this peaceful society, there are always some great people who bear heavy burdens. Who says only those standing in the light are heroes? Some heroes never show their faces, yet they bear heavy burdens in the darkness. They disregard their own lives, they fight criminals to the end. In a peaceful society, they fight in the shadows, using their flesh and blood to protect the lives and property of the Party and the people.]

[Drugs, a crime that is common yet difficult to eradicate. Today, let's uncover a great yet low-profile profession: the anti-drug police.]

The news broadcast was accompanied by the anchor's impassioned voice.

Immediately afterward, the television screen displayed a series of thrilling, yet exhilarating, shouts and gunshots.

Scenes flashed rapidly across the dense forest, the city, the arrest scene, and the battleground.

This caused Yuanyuan, who was quickly walking away, to stop in her tracks.

Because she remembered something.

Back then, Zihao, to show off, had specifically mentioned that his cousin, Jiang Tian, ​​had become an intern in the anti-drug police force. He said that such a good-for-nothing, in such an important profession, must have fared terribly.

And Yuanyuan now had no doubt about Inspector Jiang Tian.

If he was an inspector...

How much merit and what great deeds would he have accomplished?

In other words, the answer might lie in this news broadcast.

Sure enough, the next moment, the anchor's impassioned voice rang out again on the television.

[Having seen the images just now, you should now have a basic understanding of the daily life of a narcotics officer. Perhaps you're curious about the kind of people who become narcotics officers. Today, we've found a highly decorated narcotics officer who has single-handedly killed hundreds of drug dealers. Let's see what his life motto is.]

The scene shifts, and on the television screen, a figure appears, its features blurred.

However, the general build and shape are still discernible.

That's right, this is the scene from when Jiang Tian was interviewed.

To be honest, if he were in any other profession, or even a different police force, Jiang Tian could easily appear openly in the public eye.

However, this isn't necessarily a good thing.

Within the system, moderation is valued—not in actions, but in character.

Look around, which high-ranking official or wealthy person spends their days cultivating their own power? Not a single one.

Being too famous isn't a good thing either.

Conversely, his high-profile actions and low-profile personal life perfectly align with Jiang Tian's future plans.

Just as in the initial interview, Zhao Cainan's interview with Jiang Tian was broadcast almost entirely unedited.

This was mainly because Jiang Tian's words were so positive.

So inspiring.

Whether it's their ideological awareness, their words, or their actions, it's all the same.

That's why the entire news report devoted almost four minutes to an interview with Jiang Tian.

"Six Merits King, a very nice name, but I think it's just a temporary honor for you, because your seventh First-Class Merit shouldn't be too far off."

These were Zhao Cainan's last words, her final summary.



Chapter 305 Misfortunes never come singly, utter collapse


Jiang Tian on the screen didn't speak, and his face was so blurry that even his expression was indistinct.

Was it him?

Yuan Yuan stood in front of the television, staring blankly.

Her subconscious intuition told her it was very likely Jiang Tian.

Because, without such great achievements, how could he become a police inspector before even graduating?

Is he an idiot?

Does Jiang Xiaoyu look like an idiot?

What a joke! Jiang Xiaoyu is a student at a top university, Yunnan University!

Therefore, Jiang Xiaoyu couldn't have fabricated this.

The more Yuan Yuan thought about it, the more shaken she became.

And in comparison, if all of this was true, then Jiang Zihao's actions were undoubtedly clownish.

Ridiculous.

Such a close relationship, and instead of showing appreciation, he was constantly belittling and targeting her.

Serves him right.

“Yuanyuan, are you having second thoughts? Don’t worry, I was just joking. I’ll come back. I won’t go to the countryside.” Jiang Zihao ran up to stop her.

Yuanyuan, however, gave a disdainful smile, even adding insult to injury: “No, I was just slightly saddened. Such a close relationship, yet you didn’t cherish it. You and your father turned into enemies. Your going to the countryside is entirely your own fault. From this moment on, I officially declare that we’re breaking up. Goodbye.”

After saying this, Yuanyuan left without looking back.

As for Jiang Tian, ​​Yuanyuan was self-aware enough to know that he was simply beyond her reach. Such an outstanding individual was not worthy of her acquaintance.

From the very beginning, they were not on the same page. Yuanyuan was very rational and self-aware.

After Yuanyuan left,

Jiang Zihao completely broke down.

His mental state completely exploded.

He collapsed.

What was supposed to be a double blessing in both career and love has suddenly turned into a double blow.

The future seems bleak and uncertain.

“Dad,” Jiang Zihao burst into tears.

Jiang Yong, already depressed, couldn't hold back for the first time in his life upon seeing Jiang Zihao's state and slapped him across the face: “What are you crying for?”

The slap stunned Jiang Zihao.

Jiang Yong also paused for a moment, then his tone softened as he said, “Don't worry, I'll call your second uncle. That beast Jiang Tian is heartless, but if your second uncle still wants to maintain this family bond, if he doesn't want to bear the infamy, he won't act recklessly.”

After speaking, Jiang Yong took out his phone and dialed Jiang Peng's number.

Unfortunately, Jiang Peng was too busy to answer the phone.

At this time, even with the torrential rain, families of fallen heroes continued to arrive at the Municipal Public Security Bureau to claim their loved ones. Jiang Peng, as the bureau chief, personally met with and comforted each one.

Meanwhile,

On the top floor.

Zheng Fang and Jiang Xiaoyu, following behind Jiang Tian, ​​had arrived at the door of the private room.

Jiang Xiaoyu didn't seem to feel anything yet.

But Zheng Fang, working at the Finance Bureau and within the system, was so nervous about meeting Secretary Mu that she didn't even dare to step inside.

Most importantly, Secretary Mu actually invited her son to dinner! Even now, Zheng Fang still felt like she was dreaming; her head was spinning.

“Mom, don’t be nervous. Secretary Mu won’t eat you,” Jiang Tian comforted her helplessly.

“Why did Secretary Mu want to see you and invite you to dinner?” Zheng Fang wiped the sweat from her brow.

“Nothing, just an apology,” Jiang Tian explained.

“An apology?”

“Secretary Mu is apologizing to you?” Zheng Fang exclaimed.

If it weren’t for the fact that this was her own son, she would have slapped him.

“Stop talking nonsense. What is it about?” Zheng Fang asked seriously, because she really needed to be prepared.

Jiang Tian’s eyelids twitched. There was no way around it; in this era, nobody believed the truth.

Jiang Tian thought for a moment and said, “Probably... Secretary Mu thinks I’m too outstanding, so he wants to introduce his granddaughter to me.”

Hmm?

Zheng Fang’s eyes lit up.

"That's more like it."

After all, Jiang Tian was quite handsome. As for his outstanding abilities, Zheng Fang didn't pursue the matter further; after all, the child was grown up, and being exonerated as a child didn't mean he would remain ordinary as an adult.

"Secretary Cheng, thank you for today. I'll treat you to dinner another day," Jiang Tian said politely to Secretary Cheng.

"Thank you too much. Brother Jiang, please come in. Secretary Mu is waiting," Secretary Cheng said with a smile.

Jiang Tian nodded and led Zheng Fang into the private room.

To be honest,

today's meeting with Secretary Mu was unexpected for Jiang Tian. Jiang Tian bringing his family completely dispelled the grudges that had been weighing on Old Master Mu's mind.

No matter what, Old Master Mu was a figure in the province.

Humblely sending someone to apologize wouldn't sound good, and if word got out, it would certainly damage Old Master Mu's reputation.

Therefore, when Jiang Tian arrived with his family,

Old Master Mu's expression instantly brightened with joy.

Because this also signifies a meeting between the two families!

Old Master Mu hadn't expected Jiang Tian to be so thorough in his work.

As for Jiang Tian, ​​not much time had passed since their last meeting, and there was no awkwardness or distance between them.

Since they were already quite familiar with each other, even though Jiang Tian hadn't contacted Mu Ya during this period, seeing her haggard appearance still softened his heart.

Under the table, he took Mu Ya's small hand.

Ultimately,

Mu Ya was Jiang Tian's deepest, most cherished memory.

Whether in his past life or this one.

In this life, although he stole Xiao Bo's girlfriend to get revenge, ultimately, the influence Mu Ya had on Jiang Tian in his past life was still too great.

Sure enough, as their hands were joined,

Mu Ya's face flushed slightly.

The conflict seemed to vanish instantly.

But the next moment, while Old Master Mu and Zheng Fang were talking familiarly...

Mu Ya whispered, "That Xiao Bo keeps calling me lately, saying he wants to meet me."

"Xiao Bo?" Jiang Tian's face darkened.

Mu Ya glanced at Jiang Tian cautiously and said, "I blocked him a long time ago, but he's been calling me from several different numbers."

That bastard still dares to haunt me?

Jiang Tian's brow twitched.

"He's asking for it."

"He said he'd keep harassing me with phone calls if I didn't see him, and even threatened to commit suicide," Mu Ya continued.

Jiang Tian's eyes flashed coldly.

Actually, Jiang Tian hadn't been paying much attention to Xiao Bo lately.

Because he couldn't stay at the Municipal Public Security Bureau anymore; recently, Xiao Bo hadn't even gone to the bureau.

Although Xiao Bo hadn't been officially fired, it was practically the same as being fired; he was currently suspended from duty at home.

"It's okay, leave these things to me. Next time he calls you, tell me, and I'll go see what he's up to."



Chapter 306 A Single Thought Makes a World of Difference


Jiang Tian hadn't paid much attention to Xiao Bo lately.

However, for Jiang Tian now, crushing Xiao Bo would be incredibly easy. But the more he experienced, the more dire Xiao Bo's current state became—it was actually far simpler than killing him.

Think about it.

Xiao Bo's family had gone bankrupt to marry him to Wang Yifei.

And Jiang Tian knew exactly what kind of woman Wang Yifei was. She could betray him in his past life, and she could betray Xiao Bo in this one too.

Even if he married her, who could guarantee Wang Yifei wouldn't do something?

Jiang Tian had actually been more interested in seeing what would happen if Xiao Bo and Wang Yifei, this pair of scoundrels, were paired together.

Would Wang Yifei fall for someone more promising and with a brighter future?

And perhaps lead Xiao Bo down the same path she had in her past life.

But now, Xiao Bo dared to court death.

Of course, Jiang Tian and Mu Ya might indeed have some conflicts, but even if they did, they weren't something Xiao Bo could touch.

"Besides, Xiao Bo seems to know what happened between us," Mu Ya continued.

Jiang Tian looked thoughtful, considering the situation carefully. After making sure no one in the police force could be informing Xiao Bo—or perhaps even if they were, Jiang Tian hadn't discovered it, but it didn't matter.

"Next time you need to contact me, just call me directly," Jiang Tian said.

Meanwhile,

Jiang Tian's mother, Zheng Fang, also relaxed under Old Mu's deliberate guidance.

Who was Old Mu? He was a true veteran, able to find common ground with anyone in just a few words.

Plus, Jiang Xiaoyu sat on the other side of Mu Ya, occasionally glancing at her curiously, her big eyes darting around, seemingly lost in thought.

In a short time, Old Mu quickly became acquainted with Zheng Fang, saying, "Actually, as the top official of Yunnan Province, Yunhai has always been known as the crime capital. Comrade Jiang has recently made outstanding contributions to Yunhai's fight against crime. I've long wanted to know what kind of family could raise such an outstanding talent."

These words instantly made Zheng Fang beam with joy.

In truth, Zheng Fang was just an ordinary woman. Aside from being somewhat pretty, she was normally just an average woman.

Her only advantage was that, as a leader in the Finance Bureau, she had more experience than some people; her intelligence was merely above average.

Fortunately, she had Jiang Peng supporting her.

Otherwise, she wouldn't have reached her current position.

Old Mu seemed to have grasped Zheng Fang's essence in just a few words, and his face showed great satisfaction. This was good.

Because if someone was too shrewd, he would worry about having a too-shrewd mother-in-law; his granddaughter would suffer greatly if she married into such a family.

Fortunately...

Grandpa Mu didn't delve into any deeper topics.

After dinner, the conversation went well.

Grandpa Mu, being very understanding, simply mentioned that Jiang Tian and Mu Ya were dating, and that the two families should get to know each other.

Actually, Jiang Tian wasn't keen on dating too early.

"Grandpa, you should go back now. Jiang Tian and I are going out for a walk," Mu Ya waved to Grandpa Mu as the meal ended.

His face beamed with a radiant smile.

His pale complexion seemed to have regained some color after the meal.

This made Jiang Tian sigh slightly.

"We'll be going now," Old Master Mu said with a slight smile.

As for Mu Feng, he hadn't spoken a word during the meal, but just before leaving, he quietly pulled Jiang Tian aside.

"Brother Jiang, could you put in a good word for me with President Qin?" After not seeing him for several days, Mu Feng seemed to have aged more than ten years, his hair turning much whiter.

Actually, Jiang Tian didn't know.

During this time, the Mu family was also experiencing tremendous difficulties.

After Qin Shiyu left, she completely abandoned the Yunhai Investment project.

This left Mu Feng in a state of utter chaos.

Now, Mu Feng's company was on the verge of bankruptcy. As for loans, even with Mu Feng's connections, it would be difficult to get any more money.

Especially since a loan of 200 million yuan was about to mature.

If Qin Shiyu didn't inject investment soon, the new project would fail, the loan would mature, and the company would be able to declare complete bankruptcy.

"I'll give it a try," Jiang Tian said, not giving a definite answer, but leaving some room for negotiation.

As long as Liang Ru wasn't involved, everything was fine.

Jiang Tian truly hated Liang Ru.

She looked down on him.

"Anyway, Uncle thanks you in advance," Mu Feng said gratefully.

Jiang Tian nodded slightly.

Then Jiang Tian went downstairs. His family was well-off; Jiang Peng had a company car, and they also had a Volkswagen Santana, which Zheng Fang used for leisure.

So, as Zheng Fang was about to leave, Jiang Tian leaned out the window to say goodbye. In an instant, Zheng Fang grabbed his ear and said, "Kid, what's going on? How did you manage to win over Secretary Mu's granddaughter?"

"Mom, your son is so handsome, isn't this normal?" Jiang Tian grinned.

"I see the girl is completely devoted to you. Don't mess around outside, or I won't let you off the hook," Zheng Fang threatened fiercely.

"I understand, Mom," Jiang Tian nodded frantically.

Thinking to himself, he figured he hadn't really messed around.

After all, he hadn't done it intentionally with Gao Yangyang.

As for the others, it was all just platonic relationships; he hadn't had any deep connections with other women.

"You know what you're doing. Now that you're capable, Mom can't control you anymore. Since you made Mom proud today, I won't say anything. Anyway, you're all grown up now," Zheng Fang said, then drove away from the hotel with her head held high.

She was so proud of him.

Thinking about how Jiang Yong had been thoroughly humiliated today, she felt so triumphant!

She had wanted this day for so long, and now it had finally come true.

So many years.

Back then, Jiang Peng had always yielded to Jiang Yong to maintain their relationship, even having to protect Jiang Yong's fragile and humble self-esteem, carefully helping him while simultaneously comforting him.

Zheng Fang had complained back then, "This isn't like taking care of an older brother; it's harder than taking care of a son."

The worst part was, the other party didn't appreciate it.

Now look at him.

He's grown up, and the first thing he did was come back to them, wanting to humiliate them.

And unexpectedly, Jiang Tian proved him right.

It's just that he went a bit too far in the end.

Zheng Fang was worried that Jiang Yong might resort to desperate measures, after all, Jiang Tian had crushed all of Jiang Zihao's hopes.

He had directly ruined Jiang Zihao's future.

Given Jiang Yong's narrow-minded nature, he might do something desperate. Zheng Fang wanted to go home and discuss countermeasures with Jiang Peng.

So, although Zheng Fang left...

Jiang Tian turned to look at Jiang Xiaoyu and Mu Ya, who were already embracing each other intimately. His lips twitched slightly.

"Sister-in-law, you also graduated from Yunnan University! We're so lucky! I studied at Yunnan University too, hehe. Sister-in-law and senior, it seems my older brother is the dumbest of the three of us." Jiang Xiaoyu chuckled foolishly.

Upon hearing "sister-in-law," Mu Ya's face flushed uncontrollably: "No, no."

"Don't you want to be my sister-in-law?" Jiang Xiaoyu asked suspiciously.

"No, no, no." Mu Ya's head was already steaming.

Fortunately, Jiang Tian had already walked up and slapped Jiang Xiaoyu on the head: "You talk too much."

Although he said that, Jiang Tian wasn't actually angry.

Thinking back to his past life, Wang Yifei's terrible attitude, and even Jiang Xiaoyu's murder at school because of Wang Yifei.

And now, Mu Ya and Jiang Xiaoyu were getting along harmoniously.

Jiang Tian felt as if he were in a dream.



Chapter 307 The sky has fallen, Elder Mu is in trouble.


In her past life, when had she ever imagined such a scene?

And who could have imagined such a heartwarming moment?

“Brother, where are we going next?” Jiang Xiaoyu asked excitedly.

Jiang Tian looked at Mu Ya.

Mu Ya thought for a moment and said, “How about we go shopping?”

“Okay, okay!” Jiang Xiaoyu cheered.

Jiang Tian didn’t refuse and went straight to his Phaeton, which left Jiang Xiaoyu completely bewildered.

“Brother, did you embezzle?” Jiang Xiaoyu asked cautiously.

“Shut up.” If Jiang Tian hadn’t considered Jiang Xiaoyu’s potential to become a star, he would have slapped her long ago.

Throughout the journey, thanks to the lively atmosphere created by the clever Jiang Xiaoyu, Mu Ya’s smile never faded.

However, playfulness aside, business still needed to be dealt with.

After getting in the car, Jiang Tian received a call from Zhao Wei.

"Boss, the interrogation results are in!" Zhao Wei said impatiently.

This made Jiang Tian's eyes light up.

He hadn't expected Zhao Wei to act so quickly.

He knew who Zhao Wei was talking about. That's right, it was Director Liao's younger brother, Liao Jiajun, along with Yan Yan and the two small-time drug dealers who sold them drugs.

"According to the interrogation, Liao Jiajun was introduced to drugs by Yan Yan a year ago, and the two of them have been using them for a full year. As for the two small-time drug dealers, according to the interrogation, they contacted their supplier through a website to place orders, and the supplier delivered the drugs directly to their door," Zhao Wei explained.

"Good, dig deeper. I'll be back later." Jiang Tian's eyes lit up.

After hanging up the phone, he noticed that Mu Ya and Jiang Xiaoyu were looking at him.

"Brother, you haven't been home for days?" Jiang Xiaoyu pouted.

"It's okay, I'll stay with you guys first," Jiang Tian comforted her.

But just as Jiang Tian finished speaking...

Mu Ya's phone suddenly rang.

Jiang Tian didn't ask who it was. After all, despite the previous misunderstanding, he had complete confidence in Mu Ya's character.

Especially regarding their relationship.

It was certain that Mu Ya would never have another man besides Jiang Tian.

However, though Jiang Tian didn't notice, the next moment, Mu Ya exclaimed in shock.

"What did you say?"

Mu Ya was speechless.

"My grandfather was in a car accident?"

Buzz!!!

Jiang Tian's mind went blank.

The sky had fallen!

Old Master Mu was in trouble; the sky had fallen.

Jiang Tian gasped frantically.

Sure enough, as soon as Mu Ya hung up the phone, tears streamed down her face uncontrollably: "Quick, to Yanshan Road, my grandfather was in a car accident."

"Don't panic, there aren't many large trucks in the city," Jiang Tian said reassuringly.

But the previously leisurely Phaeton suddenly found its tires smoking.

The exhaust pipes emitted a terrifying, buzzing roar, and the car shot forward like an arrow.

The Phaeton was equipped with a genuine V8 engine, and almost in the blink of an eye, Jiang Tian had driven it to 200 km/h.

It was nothing short of a coincidence.

Jiang Tian had lived in Yunhai his entire previous life, though he worked in a remote area. His ancestors had always lived in Yunhai, and he knew the roads there like the back of his hand.

"Yanshan Road is just ahead."

Jiang Tian spoke calmly.

At the same time, his mind was racing.

For Jiang Tian, ​​what impact would this car accident, happening right after Yue had dinner, have on him?

Of course, even if it didn't affect Jiang Tian much,

the fact that Elder Mu, as the top official in Yunnan Province's political and legal system, had an accident in Yunhai signifies something. It represents the problems and situation in the entire Yunhai area.

Regardless of whether there's someone behind it,

even if there's no conspiracy and it's just a normal car accident, it's still a major blow to Yunhai and the Yunhai Municipal Public Security Bureau.

Because the traffic police in the traffic police detachment are still a police force, under the jurisdiction of the municipal bureau.

This, to some extent, reflects the municipal bureau's lax jurisdiction.

Of course, this has no impact on Jiang Tian, ​​but it will severely affect Jiang Peng.

If something really happens to Elder Mu...

Jiang Peng would be incredibly grateful if he wasn't immediately suspended, let alone promoted.

If he's lucky, he'll be treated as a responsible party.

An earthquake struck.

This was a truly massive earthquake.

Jiang Tian didn't hesitate at all; he ran there almost at full speed.

Of course...

That's why the luckiest thing was that Jiang Tian's direction of departure was exactly the same as Elder Mu's.

Elder Mu left first, and Jiang Tian left a few minutes later.

Those few minutes roughly corresponded to a difference of a few intersections.

In other words, the accident happened just now?

Jiang Tian's gaze turned serious.

Something's not right.

With the reasoning logic of a master detective, Jiang Tian could no longer rely on normal logic.

He could pick up on even the slightest, most subtle clue.

And now, based on the information he received, Jiang Tian had quickly deduced what was unusual.

Because, judging from the time difference, the first thing to do after the car accident should have been to Mu Ya.

Then why not to the police, but to Mu Ya?

It's common sense that after a car accident, whether serious or minor, the first thing to do is call the police.

Secretary Cheng didn't immediately call the traffic police; instead, she called Mu Ya. Even if the accident was serious, Secretary Cheng wouldn't make such a common-sense mistake.

Something was amiss.

Of course, Jiang Tian wasn't suspicious of Secretary Cheng, but the unusual fact that she hurriedly called Mu Ya and then called the police first indicated a very urgent problem at the scene.

What problem could have prompted such a rush to make the calls?

Two or three traffic lights were nothing more than a blink of an eye for Jiang Tian.

Especially because of the urgency, Jiang Tian ran a red light.

A car approached from the side, and the other driver was clearly startled. Jiang Tian floored the accelerator, accelerating rapidly just before the impact.

And the instant Jiang Tian arrived at the accident scene, he witnessed something that was both unacceptable and shocking.



Chapter 308 Braving the Rapids, the entry explodes, attracting worldwide attention.


Before him lay the cross-river bridge, the Yanshan Road Bridge, approximately 300 meters long, with barriers over a meter high on both sides of the road.

Jiang Tian was moving very fast.

Even after Jiang Tian arrived, he could still see smoke billowing from the car after the impact.

This proved the accident had occurred very briefly.

However, what truly shocked Jiang Tian wasn't just that, but the fact that Elder Mu's car was now suspended on the edge of the bridge.

Most importantly, another car was pinned beneath it, clearly the vehicle from the accident.

The most terrifying part was that the tires of the car that was pinning Elder Mu's car were still burning fiercely on the ground.

Jiang Tian's pupils contracted.

He immediately realized that this was clearly premeditated.

Thinking this, Jiang Tian's head felt like it was about to explode, and goosebumps rose all over his body.

A premeditated murder of Old Master Mu?

Who?

Who has such audacity?

Are they insane?

This can't be insane, can it?

But whoever it was, Jiang Tian reacted instantly. Without even stopping, he drove straight towards the car beneath Old Master Mu's vehicle.

The speed was incredible.

But by then, it was clearly too late.

With a cracking sound from the guardrail, when Jiang Tian's car was still more than ten meters from Old Master Mu's car, both vehicles plunged into the river.

The rushing river current swept past.

With two splashes, both cars plunged into the water.

Jiang Tian got out of the car immediately.

Mu Ya's legs went weak the moment she opened the car door to get out. Fortunately, Jiang Xiaoyu was there to support her.

"Quick, call the Municipal Bureau." Jiang Tian was worried that Secretary Cheng wouldn't have time to make the call.

Jiang Tian turned to Jiang Xiaoyu and gave her some instructions.

Jiang Xiaoyu, completely bewildered and flustered at the scene, hurriedly pulled out her phone upon hearing this.

Fortunately, Jiang Tian's family had always been well-off.

Jiang Xiaoyu had been given a cell phone after starting university.

It came in handy at this moment.

Actually, while Jiang Xiaoyu was making the call, the city police station was in complete chaos.

All the pursuit of the criminals and the interrogations were halted.

All personnel in the city police station, including those in the branch offices, had been mobilized, so when Jiang Xiaoyu called, there was no answer.

Jiang Tian breathed a slight sigh of relief, because this meant that the city police had arrived.

Or rather, it was utter chaos. Jiang Tian could already imagine the state of the city police station at this moment.

Old Master Mu!

If something happened to Old Master Mu...

For the entire Yunhai region, no, for the entire Yunnan province, it was countless times more terrifying than an 18-magnitude earthquake.

The impact it caused was no less than that of Gao Xiaolong's accident.

Because it was so egregious.

This wasn't a normal car accident; it was more like a murder. More than a car accident, Jiang Tian clearly noticed that the driver was deliberately, even intentionally, pressing the accelerator.

Even if Jiang Tian had fired his gun, it would have been too late.

Below the bridge flowed the mighty river, its waters rushing past.

At this very moment.

At this exact time.

It was exactly 8 or 9 o'clock, the peak time for traffic congestion. As a result, many vehicles had already stopped near the accident site, and people had rushed to the bridge to watch.

Many people were frantically calling the police, and others were filming.

In short, there were many onlookers.

Of course, while not far from the city police station, it was still a few kilometers away. Furthermore, Yunhai's economic development had always been quite good. Although traffic wasn't as congested as in later first-tier cities, there was still considerable traffic at this time.

So, by the time the city police arrived,

it would probably be pretty much over.

"We can't wait any longer," Jiang Tian said, immediately taking off his coat.

Because at this moment, using the dim light from the bridge, Jiang Tian could see that both cars were being carried downstream by the raging river, and most frighteningly, both cars were gradually sinking into the water.

Given the turbulent river, the season, and the undercurrents,

Jiang Tian took a deep breath.

The most terrifying aspect of rivers like this is never the surface, but the terrifying undercurrents hidden beneath.

How terrifying are these undercurrents?

Even someone wearing a life jacket would be almost certainly killed if they went in.

And they would be swept away by the current, leaving no trace, not even a corpse.

In other words, this was definitely premeditated, and planned for a long time.

Jiang Tian's combat prowess is almost invincible on land, but that doesn't mean he's invincible in water.

Especially since Jiang Tian, ​​while not a complete landlubber, only knows a little swimming, going into this river would be suicide.

"Jiang Tian, ​​what are you doing?" Mu Ya grabbed Jiang Tian.

"It's too late, I have to go in and save them first." Jiang Tian said anxiously.

At the same time, he searched for the term "swimming" among the many entries.

After all, Jiang Tian had killed so many criminals, and he had actually collected various kinds of minor affixes, at least in different areas, without using them.

None of them had been synthesized.

This was because, in an emergency, he could quickly use the hidden, pre-prepared sin points to level up rapidly. This was a backup plan Jiang Tian had kept for himself after he had almost no weaknesses.

Now, it was coming in handy.

After finding the swimming affix, Jiang Tian began frantically stacking and upgrading it.

“No, you can’t go down. Do you know how many hidden currents are down there? If you go down, you won’t be able to come back up. My family is about to die. If you die too, I really don’t have the courage to live.” Mu Ya hugged Jiang Tian tightly from behind, refusing to let go.

This, however, slightly eased Jiang Tian’s mood.

Because it meant that Jiang Tian’s position in Mu Ya’s heart was irreplaceable, even by family.

As a family member, it might be hard to accept, but as the beneficiary, how could Jiang Tian not feel warmth?

If she were the heartless type, especially someone like Wang Yifei, she probably would have already pushed Jiang Tian inside.

"It's okay, I'm confident," Jiang Tian reassured her.

"No, you can't go down."

Mu Ya's attitude was extremely resolute, even though her own grandfather was riding in the sinking car.

Even though her father was also in the car.

It wasn't that Mu Ya was heartless; she wouldn't have given up if she could have saved him. But the number of people who drowned in this river every year was well-documented.

Because two rivers converged upstream, this area was practically a no-man's land, filled with countless hidden currents.

This made Jiang Tian's lips curl into a smile. "It's alright, your words are enough."

Jiang Tian turned and kissed Mu Ya's forehead. Although Jiang Tian himself wasn't entirely confident, if he didn't act soon, Old Master Mu would truly be gone.



Chapter 309 A Plot to Attract Attention? Did He Really Jump?


"Young man, are you crazy?"

"Yeah, more than ten people drowned here last year, and they never even recovered their bodies."

"If you go down, the body will be swept away by the current somewhere. Young man, you're just throwing your life away!"

"Don't even mention you, even professional swimmers wouldn't make it back up."

"Last year, there was someone who challenged himself to cross the river. He'd barely swum a few dozen meters when an undercurrent swept him to the bottom. They didn't even know where his body was. The search team searched for half a month, and they still haven't found it."

Jiang Tian's actions instantly attracted a crowd of onlookers.

People at the scene stood up to dissuade him.

Playing around is one thing, but this section is no joke. Because of the confluence of upstream rivers, this section becomes almost a death trap.

When the bridge was built, this very issue greatly increased the difficulty of construction.

Therefore, in the eyes of the onlookers, Jiang Tian's actions were undoubtedly suicidal.

Of course, Jiang Tian wouldn't listen to any of their advice.

If he didn't have enough sin points to upgrade his swimming skill, Jiang Tian would have been forced to stand by and watch. But now, to be on the safe side,

he directly upgraded his swimming skill from a white attribute to a gold attribute along the way.

This reduced Jiang Tian's sin points from over 200,000 to only 120,000-130,000.

Of course, compared to saving Elder Mu, this expenditure was a huge gain for Jiang Tian.

Because these 100,000 sin points not only gave Jiang Tian a gold attribute for swimming, but could also directly bring him terrifying political resources.

That's one way to put it.

Even though Jiang Tian had a relationship with Mu Ya, for someone like Elder Mu, it was only out of respect for Mu Ya; in reality, there wasn't much of a connection between them.

When Jiang Tian does something, or needs help, it's perfectly reasonable for Elder Mu to stand idly by. It's entirely up to him; not helping is his right, and helping is Jiang Tian's way of doing a favor.

However, if Jiang Tian could save Elder Mu, it would be a different story—a life-or-death rescue.

It could even be compared to Gao Xiaolong.

After saving Gao Xiaolong, what did Jiang Tian gain directly? A vast network of connections.

Of course, he wouldn't use this connection in ordinary times, and Jiang Tian wouldn't easily use it, but on another level, the hidden gains are absolutely terrifying.

Because it could provide Jiang Tian with the fair environment he rightfully needs.

Gao Xiaolong alone is enough for Jiang Tian to act with impunity.

Now, with Elder Mu added to the mix...

What if Elder Mu were included?

The benefits this network will bring to Jiang Tian are unimaginable. While saying he can roam freely in Yunhai might be an exaggeration, the convenience this network will provide for Jiang Tian in everything he does, whether investigating cases or otherwise, is unimaginable.

Of course, putting all that aside...

Even if an innocent person fell in, given Jiang Tian's status and position, he couldn't simply stand by and watch someone die when he had the ability to do so.

"Just putting on an act, this kid's all show. You actually believe he'd dare jump?"

Just then, an untimely voice rang out.

It sounded particularly jarring in the environment.

Everyone's gaze turned to the speaker.

The next moment, a man in a suit leaned against the driver's side of a Mercedes-Benz, looking at Jiang Tian with a knowing, mocking expression.

In the passenger seat sat a woman in a yellow wool coat, wearing a mask and hat, her expression unreadable.

However, her brow was slightly furrowed, and she remained silent as she watched the scene unfold.

"See that? The kind of show-offs I despise most are like this. Everyone knows jumping is suicide, someone would definitely try to stop him. This guy is definitely exploiting the crowd's psychology," the young man deliberately provoked with sarcasm.

The unsuspecting onlookers were indeed stirred up.

"Young man, it's going too far to exploit our sympathy for attention!"

"Yeah, we were wrong about you. We really thought you were the kind of person who would sacrifice yourself for others, but it turns out you're just using our sympathy for attention."

"It's outrageous! They're already in such a terrible situation, and you're just making things worse for them. How can you be so shameless?"

Instigated by the young man, many uninformed people continued to criticize Jiang Tian.

What could Jiang Tian do?

He could only conclude that there were bad people among the crowd.

They were sowing discord.

Especially after Jiang Tian glanced at the license plate number, he secretly memorized it.

If it were any other time, Jiang Tian would definitely have gone up and taught them a lesson, but now it was almost too late. If he didn't go down now, he wouldn't be able to save the person.

Of course, it was precisely because of the Mercedes-Benz driver's words that the people who had just been so moved by Jiang Tian now felt just as much hatred.

"Those who exploit sympathy are the most despicable."

The masked woman accompanying the troublemaker, a Mercedes-Benz owner, looked at Jiang Tian with increasing indignation and said, "What you're doing is shameful. They're already in such a state, and you're still using their sympathy to manipulate everyone. Shameless!"

"Hmm."

"Fine."

Jiang Tian glanced at them, too lazy to even acknowledge them.

Arguing with them was pointless.

There was no need to reply.

"Why aren't you saying anything? Are you too scared to speak? I knew it, haha, you're definitely too ashamed to speak," the Mercedes-Benz owner sneered.

He continued to press his advantage.

Using everyone's sympathy to showcase his cleverness.

Especially his gaze, intentionally or unintentionally, drifted to the woman beside him, a hint of warmth in his eyes.

Because this woman was his childhood sweetheart.

They hadn't been in contact for a long time after graduating high school, and now that she was finally back, he was determined to make a good impression.

"Su Tong, do you see now? Society is a treacherous place. Men like this, hah, are completely untrustworthy," the sneer of the tiger-headed man said.

The woman called Su Tong seemed to grit her teeth as she said, "Yes, I never expected there to be such a shameless man."

"Does he have no compassion at all?!"

"How could this person be like this!"

When Su Tong first saw Jiang Tian's state, she rushed down to stop him, initially moved, never expecting such a kind man to exist, even so selfless, daring to jump.

But she never imagined it was all a conspiracy.

It's shameful.

He's already in such a terrible situation, and you use him to gain sympathy and attention.

It's disgusting.

Su Tong grew angrier the more she thought about it.

Even her gaze towards her childhood friend softened slightly: "Looks like you're not without ability to start a business in Yunhai. You're even driving a Mercedes-Benz. Back in school, who would have thought someone like you, a bottom-of-the-class student, would be doing so well?"

"Of course," Ye Xuan said proudly, head held high. "Someone like that is most afraid of dying. Don't even mention now, even if several people pushed him, he probably couldn't fall. Heh..."

However...

As Ye Xuan's sarcastic voice rang out, before his words had even finished...

At that very moment.

Thump!!!



Chapter 310: Another Golden Entry Appears, Crossing the Ocean


A splash echoed in the water.

Ye Xuan, who had just been sneering, froze, staring at the scene before him, seemingly searching for the person who had just jumped.

At the same time,

Ye Xuan's face was also filled with confusion.

No.

Jumped?

Ye Xuan's head was buzzing.

Really jumped?

Ye Xuan couldn't believe it.

In truth, Ye Xuan was very pragmatic.

He knew clearly what he had relied on to get to where he was now after building his business step by step.

It was his intelligence, and his deep understanding of human nature.

In this world, there are no truly selfless people.

Ultimately, it's all about self-preservation.

Ye Xuan was no exception.

He didn't believe that anyone in this world could be so noble as to sacrifice themselves to save others.

Death means the end.

No one is that foolish.

So, seeing Jiang Tian's appearance, he was very confident.

But now, all his confidence turned into a series of slaps in the face, relentlessly striking him.

Even during this time, Jiang Tian didn't respond at all.

Jiang Tian ignored Ye Xuan's mockery like a clown, and besides, even if he wanted to argue, it was too late now.

So, in the end, Jiang Tian simply turned to Mu Ya and said, "No matter what, we have to try. After all, he's your grandfather."

Of course.

Even if the person who fell wasn't Mu Ya's grandfather, Jiang Tian would still help. He wouldn't lay a hand on anyone except his enemies. Today, even an ordinary stranger, if capable, would jump without hesitation.

If the chances of survival were greater than 50%, Jiang Tian simply couldn't ignore it.

This could be said to show that Jiang Tian was sometimes selfish, something he wouldn't even deny himself.

But at this moment...

Jiang Tian's bottom line, however, remained unchanged.

After speaking, Jiang Tian pried open Mu Ya's tightly clasped hands, then leaped into the surging river.

In the pitch-black night, lying on the bridge's edge felt like being in an abyss.

The bridge was incredibly high; Jiang Tian estimated it to be close to twenty or thirty meters.

This was because Yunhai—no, the entire Yun Province region—was predominantly mountainous, making the river flow more like a canyon.

At this height...

Even a professional athlete might not be able to withstand it, let alone Jiang Tian.

After all, at a certain height, the water surface is essentially like concrete.

This height was a distance that posed a life-threatening risk, but not without a chance of survival. If the distance were fifty or sixty meters, Jiang Tian wouldn't even glance at it.

Because the vehicles had fallen from a height of fifty or sixty meters, the impact had made the water surface almost as hard as concrete.

Given this opportunity, Jiang Tian was even less likely to give it up.

So, in the blink of an eye,

Jiang Tian was incredibly fast.

What seemed like a long time had actually only just come to a stop; the two vehicles were now just floating steadily on the surface, drifting downstream with the current.

Jiang Tian took a running start and jumped about four or five meters.

As he landed, only the screen visible to him displayed:

【Swimming (White) - God of the Ocean (Gold)】(Wearing this attribute, while it won't allow you to breathe like a fish in the ocean, if your other bodily functions are sufficient, it will allow you to cross the Pacific Ocean and participate in all water-related competitions. I believe no one in this world can surpass you.)

Just in case, Jiang Tian even maxed out the attribute.

After all, the water was terrifying.

Whether before or after his rebirth, Jiang Tian had always been relatively unfamiliar with water, and had never even swum in a reservoir or any other body of water.

He also lacked professional training.

In short, Jiang Tian had a fear of water.

But at this moment.

The moment the attribute was equipped.

Jiang Tian plunged backwards towards the river, looking down at the surging waters with a sense of familiarity.

Yes, the explanation in the entry was correct.

It was incredibly awesome.

In other words, Jiang Tian felt that, combined with the entry about extraordinary humans, this perfectly matched the explanation—he could even cross the Pacific Ocean with his bare hands now?

It sounded a bit far-fetched, but Jiang Tian didn't dwell on it. He simply adjusted his body slightly, instantly assuming the perfect diving posture.

Then, the next moment.

Upon hitting the water, Jiang Tian didn't even create a ripple. He disappeared into the river so easily, as if without even a wave.

Hiss!!!

At that moment.

Everyone on the shore felt their scalps tingle.

Their eyes widened.

Especially those who had just spoken rudely to Jiang Tian were now filled with shame.

Besides shame, they kept shouting.

"Young man, you're foolish!"

"Damn it, young man, were you really going to jump?!"

"Are you crazy? Look, look! He's gone without even a splash! So impulsive!"

The onlookers stared wide-eyed, frantically searching the river.

Su Tong, who had just mocked Jiang Tian, ​​her face behind her mask was almost unrecognizable.

She was very compassionate.

The kind of person who would help even stray cats and dogs she encountered on the street.

To put it bluntly, she had an overflowing sense of compassion, a typical saintly heart.

Therefore,

the shock of this scene was especially immense and unbearable for Su Tong.

"No, it can't be."

Su Tong's pupils contracted continuously.

The others only felt ashamed.

But for Su Tong, having just mocked a hero who risked his own life for others was more painful than death.

If anything were to happen to Jiang Tian, ​​she would live in his shadow for the rest of her life.

No, even if Jiang Tian survived, she would suffer for the rest of her life because of what she had just said.

And the cause of all this was this bastard in front of her.

"You heartless beast! How could you wrong such a hero? Are you even human?"

"That's right, it's this beast! Beat him up!"

"Wheelchair! What's wrong with the Wheelchair? Smash his car too!"

"Yeah, to make us misunderstand such a hero like this, he deserves to die! He should be thrown down the road with us!"

The onlookers were also enraged.

They hurled accusations at Ye Xuan.



Chapter 311 The scene of the complete explosion, and the impending massive earthquake.


This is their most innocent and kind side: once they realize their mistakes, they immediately correct them and make amends for what they've done.

"No, it wasn't me, don't come any closer!" Ye Xuan's legs went weak.

Bang!!!

Just then, a figure appeared behind him, holding a brick, and slammed it into the back of his head.

Blood splattered.

Ye Xuan, surprisingly resilient, didn't even lose consciousness after being hit by the brick.

He swayed a couple of times, then hurriedly tried to get away from them.

But how could he keep up with the angry mob? Several burly men slapped and kicked Ye Xuan.

The onlookers pointed and whispered.

Su Tong, in the passenger seat, initially wanted to stop Ye Xuan from being beaten, but in the end, she watched helplessly as it unfolded.

Of course, neither Mu Ya nor Jiang Xiaoyu participated in the chaos and fighting behind them. Jiang Xiaoyu, let alone helping Mu Ya, was completely exhausted and collapsed on the bridge after Jiang Tianyi jumped in without hesitation.

Looking at the silent river below.

Meanwhile, in the crowd, a woman was filming the scene with a camera.

"Stop!"

"Everyone stop!"

"What are you doing? What are you all doing?"

"The police are here!"

Just then, a commotion erupted.

A moment later.

The wailing of police sirens blared as they arrived.

Of course, the road was already very congested; even the police cars could only stop beside the bridge, and then the officers had to run frantically for that distance.

Despite his age, Gao Xiaolong was still incredibly strong and agile.

His speed was phenomenal; he was practically in the lead.

Beside Gao Xiaolong, a young man ran alongside him.

Behind them,

almost the entire city police force was mobilized in large numbers.

This includes the traffic police brigade, and so on.

In short, you could say everyone who could come came.

Do you know what it means for the sky to fall?

This is what it means for the sky to fall.

If something happens to Old Mu...

The consequences would be terrifying. In my past life, the aftermath of Gao Xiaolong's incident served as a stark warning.

No, in some ways, the impact would be even more severe than Gao Xiaolong's.

Because that was, after all, an accidental drug dealer conflict. But with Old Mu involved, it wasn't just a simple car accident; it even involved murder.

Good heavens, the uproar caused was unimaginable, and the sheer terror of the implications was beyond comprehension.

Jiang Peng was the first to be affected.

Jiang Peng was truly silent and dumbfounded.

Why now of all times? Regardless of the reason, he couldn't escape responsibility for this.

Want a promotion?

Want a raise?

That was a pipe dream. He'd be completely wiped out. He'd be lucky to escape with his position.

Jiang Peng was absolutely well aware of this.

Let alone him, even Shen Han from the provincial department would likely suffer significant repercussions; this is a terrifying, systemic impact.

Those directly responsible, particularly within the traffic police, will probably undergo a complete overhaul from top to bottom.

Who can withstand this?

Nobody!

Behind Gao Xiaolong, traffic police, armed police, criminal investigators, and almost everyone arrived in quick succession.

“Hurry, hurry, speed things up!” Gao Xiaolong roared.

This wasn't just because of Old Master Mu's status, but also because the relationship between Old Master Mu and Gao Xiaolong was incredibly strong.

How strong?

They had known each other for nearly forty years.

And their interactions were perfectly compatible, their relationship only growing stronger. Even after they scattered to different parts of the country, they still frequently met up for drinks and chats—a tradition they never missed.

Whether from a personal or public perspective, nothing could be wrong with Elder Mu. Any incident involving Elder Mu would be a heavy blow to Gao Xiaolong.

"Chief Gao, this should just be a regular car accident. Elder Mu will be alright."

Shen Han followed behind, panting.

Actually, it was only because they were part of the police system that they received the news and acted the fastest. Any slower, and even provincial and municipal leaders would have all arrived by now.

Gao Xiaolong didn't stop.

After all, the bridge was over 300 meters long, and the middle section was completely blocked.

Vehicles on both sides were unable to pass, and the traffic jam was getting worse.

Gao Xiaolong, however, looked grave.

Because the Municipal Public Security Bureau had reacted immediately after receiving Secretary Cheng's emergency call.

"Secretary Cheng's call was suspicious; it didn't seem like a regular car accident," Gao Xiaolong said grimly.

*Buzz!!*

Shen Han, running frantically behind, was in a daze.

Not a regular car accident?

Murder?

Murder of Elder Mu?

Are they crazy?

Who would dare do this?

In the entire Yunhai, who would dare do this? Is this some kind of madness?

"Impossible," Shen Han had never considered this.

"I hope I'm just overthinking it, but clearly, Secretary Cheng didn't have time to say anything more."

Gao Xiaolong was already in the car, listening to a detailed report from his subordinates.

At the time, Secretary Cheng only hurriedly stated that Elder Mu had been in a car accident and his location before abruptly hanging up the phone.

Whatever happened, it could have caused Secretary Cheng to hang up the phone without even having time to explain clearly.

None of this is normal.

Something's not right.

Gao Xiaolong also hoped he was just overthinking things.

Upon arriving,

seeing the chaotic scene, Gao Xiaolong's heart skipped a beat.

Because, he couldn't see Old Master Mu's car at all.

"It's gone, Old Master Mu's car has gone down."

Just then, Jiang Peng, who had already understood the situation, came to Gao Xiaolong's side, his face pale, and pointed to the broken guardrail.

Buzz!!!



Chapter 312 is a complete disaster; a series of bolts from the blue have overwhelmed the commander.


But just as Jiang Peng finished speaking,

Mu Ya and Jiang Xiaoyu, supporting each other, were both pale-faced, their legs clearly weak even when walking.

That's why.

Mu Ya was usually very resilient; under normal circumstances, her mental state and resilience wouldn't allow her to experience such a significant emotional fluctuation.

But this was completely different.

Her grandfather and father were in trouble, and now, even if Jiang Tian had jumped, Mu Ya, no matter how strong her mental fortitude, couldn't remain calm at this moment.

"Xiaoya, don't worry, we will do everything we can to search for your grandfather," Gao Xiaolong comforted her, his voice heavy with worry.

However, seeing Jiang Xiaoyu at the scene made Gao Xiaolong frown slightly. Any stranger, especially in a situation involving the possibility of Mu's grandfather's murder, should be treated with caution.

And that was exactly the case now.

Very cautious.

"Who is this?" Gao Xiaolong asked, frowning.

As he asked, Gao Xiaolong's heart grew heavier as he approached the bridge, watching the car drift further and further away below.

It was obvious.

It was moving incredibly fast—no, or rather, the river appeared calm, but the car was drifting at an alarming rate.

In just a few minutes, it had already drifted hundreds of meters.

This proved the river's current was terrifyingly strong, far more complex than it appeared on the surface. The most terrifying aspect was that the car's roof was gradually being submerged.

That meant that soon, both cars might be submerged.

This…

Gao Xiaolong's heart sank even further.

They had arrived, but the rescue team hadn't. Even if more people came, they couldn't get down; going in would be suicide.

It's over.

They simply couldn't catch up.

Even Gao Xiaolong was in this state, let alone everyone else. At this moment, they were all frantically searching like headless flies, clearly anxious, but utterly helpless.

“Dad, quick, save her!” Jiang Xiaoyu was almost fainting.

Because for Jiang Xiaoyu, the closest person was her older brother, Jiang Tian.

The two had been the best of friends since childhood; in fact, Jiang Tian's position in Jiang Xiaoyu's heart even surpassed that of their parents.

Her own brother, whom she had grown up with and who had accompanied her through her childhood!

It was a testament to Jiang Xiaoyu's strong mental fortitude that she hadn't fainted yet.

“We know, but what are you doing here?” Jiang Peng was working overtime and genuinely didn't know the specifics of what had happened that evening.

Gao Xiaolong felt a sense of relief. It was an acquaintance, and Jiang Peng's daughter at that; it seemed there wouldn't be any problems.

"Brother went down, went down to rescue people, Dad," Jiang Xiaoyu said quickly.

*Whoosh!*

Jiang Peng stumbled.

Along with Jiang Peng's shock,

Gao Xiaolong, who had been silent the whole time, didn't react at first.

"My own brother?"

Luckily, my brother was nearby, so he rushed over immediately, but it was too late. The driver did it on purpose; he accelerated recklessly, aiming for Grandpa Mu's car to plunge into the Yanjiang River. My brother jumped in to save him."

Buzz!!

And sure enough...

They were insane.

Jiang Tian had actually gone down.

Either piece of news would be a bolt from the blue for them, let alone two.

Yes.

Just like a car that had fallen into the river.

In just a few minutes, it had drifted hundreds of meters away.

Add to that the countless undercurrents here, and even a body wouldn't be found.

Countless undercurrents.

the Municipal Police Station was only a few minutes late because they were so close to Yanjiang.

Chapter 313 My identity is exposed, my beliefs crumble, I deserve to die!

Half an hour.

completely stunned Su Tong.

Chapter 314: Bad News Keeps Coming, Will We All Be Buried Along With Them?


Every word, like a thin veil, chipped away at Su Tong's spirit.

Her kindness, her compassion, her moral compass—one could say her sympathy was overflowing.

But...

It cannot be denied...

When she not only wronged one hero, but wronged him a second time...

Indirectly causing the hero's sacrifice, the pain would be unbearable for an ordinary person.

For her, it was like a demon, instantly appearing deep within her heart and completely locking her away.

If Jiang Tian were to sacrifice himself...

Su Tong would live the rest of her life in pain and regret.

"Arrest him." Jiang Peng remained silent, waving his hand, not wanting to say anything more.

Because there was no need to say anything.

Even Jiang Peng thought that Li Zijin had spoken too much, revealing confidential information, but now that Jiang Tian was probably 80% dead, there was no need to hide it anymore.

Perhaps...

"Finally, we can completely expose the past and let everyone know that Yunhai has such a silently fighting hero."

Jiang Peng muttered to himself.

Actually.

It's already blown up today.

Even at the police station, Jiang Peng made a point of watching Jiang Tian's interview—yes, the one on the news channel.

He had to admit, although the face was blurred, Jiang Peng knew it was his son. The words he spoke directly moved and shocked Jiang Peng.

It seemed this was the first time he truly understood his son.

Especially those words.

Gao Xiaolong watched with a gratified smile.

In fact, with Jiang Peng beside him, Gao Xiaolong received a phone call, seemingly from a big shot in Kyoto, who emphasized again that Jiang Tian must finish his current tasks and find time to go to Kyoto so he could have a serious talk with him.

It was so moving.

Jiang Peng hadn't even had time to feel gratified yet.

At this very moment, he heard such devastating news.

It was like a bolt from the blue.

"Wait."

Just as the two officers were restraining Su Tong and the other woman, preparing to take them to the city police station, Gao Xiaolong seemed to have seen something and stopped them.

"Chief Gao, you heard everything, didn't you?" Jiang Peng sighed.

That's right, Gao Xiaolong had been there too, hearing Li Zijin's report. He couldn't believe Gao Xiaolong wasn't angry.

Of course, it would be a lie to say Gao Xiaolong wasn't angry.

You can insult anyone, but you absolutely cannot insult Jiang Tian, ​​especially not someone living in Yunhai.

But now.

"Su Tong?" Gao Xiaolong asked tentatively.

And indeed.

At this very moment.

Su Tong, still immersed in pain and regret, looked up, her eyes already red and swollen.

Of course, even though she was wearing a mask, Gao Xiaolong recognized Su Tong immediately by her voice and the expression in her eyes.

And sure enough.
