Su Tong looked at Gao Xiaolong with surprise: "Grandpa Gao?"

"It's you."

Gao Xiaolong's face was grim, even dark. "Su Tong, I never imagined you would do something like this. You've disappointed me terribly. Do you know how furious your family would be if they knew? That girl was right. The people you provoked and provoke are heroes of Yunhai, and undisputed heroes of the entire civil service system. Yet you insulted and provoked them like this! Ugh!!!"

*Buzz!*

This was further confirmed by Gao Xiaolong.

At this moment.

Su Tong wanted to die.

She didn't want to live anymore.

Her eyes were bloodshot, and she wanted to end it all.

What had she done? What had she done?

Although she hadn't provoked them, she had spoken accusingly and provocatively, making her undoubtedly an accomplice.

"Grandpa Gao, I didn't know. I was confused back then." Su Tong even truly wanted to die with them.

That's right.

It was that serious.

It wasn't that Su Tong was overly sensitive.

It was simply...

She couldn't bear to see herself in such a despicable state.

She had actually insulted a hero, ultimately leading to his sacrifice.

What? You say he's still alive?

Is that possible?

He's been underwater for so long and hasn't surfaced yet.

Is that even possible?

It's simply impossible.

So, she's the one who harmed that hero?

Thinking of Jiang Tian's resolute jump into the river, without the slightest hesitation, Su Tong's fists clenched tightly, trembling.

What had she done? What utterly heinous thing had she done?

The one who deserved to die the most was Ye Xuan, but as an accomplice, she deserved to die too!

How could she ever face this world again?

Su Tong's eyes were bloodshot. Looking at the surrounding police officers, and then at her, all with faces full of resentment, even hatred, made Su Tong's heart break even more.

No, it should be said that she had no face to face him.

“I…I will make it up to him,” Su Tong said.

“Make it up to him? What will you use to make it up to him?” Li Zijin said angrily.

Even if you risked your life, could you bring Team Leader Jiang back to life?

“Sir, there’s a situation!” Just then, a loud voice shouted.

The man holding the high-powered flashlight looked excited.

Gao Xiaolong instantly snapped to attention and rushed towards the riverbank.

Jiang Peng ran after him as well.

But just as they turned around,

a shadow moved even faster.

While everyone’s attention shifted, Su Tong pushed Li Zijin aside.

After Gao Xiaolong recognized Su Tong, the two officers instinctively released her, but this gave Su Tong an opportunity.

She pushed Li Zijin aside and jumped off the bridge.

*Whoosh!!!*

Sitting on the ground, Li Zijin stared blankly at the scene, her mind completely blank.

No.

I was just saying...

It shouldn't be that bad, right?

A loud splash followed.

Gao Xiaolong and Jiang Peng had only run halfway.

Ye Xuan's voice broke out in despair: "Boss, save her! Quick, save her! Su Tong, she jumped in!"

That's right.

A pang of moral condemnation, and a terrifying sense of guilt.

This had completely broken Su Tong, to the point that she lost the will to live, and even the courage to do so after wrongly accusing a hero of death.

They are still able to live safely and unharmed.

No.

For her, every second of living after Jiang Tian's sacrifice was excruciating, worse than death.

So, in the end, Su Tong simply jumped down after him, sacrificing her own life.

This completely baffled Gao Xiaolong.

As he turned his head,

Su Tong had already plummeted.

A drop of nearly twenty meters—not to mention the dangers of the water, even if an ordinary person jumped in, while it wouldn't be as hard as concrete, it would still be easy to knock them unconscious.

In other words, an ordinary person falling in might not even have a chance to struggle; they would instantly lose consciousness, be swept away by the current, and ultimately be left without a trace.



Chapter 315 The Only Man to Emerge from the Forbidden Zone of Life


If she were an ordinary person, it wouldn't be a problem, but the key is that Su Tong isn't an ordinary person!

"Chief Gao, this woman?" Jiang Peng exclaimed in astonishment.

His heart skipped a beat.

He shared the same thought as Chief Gao: if she were an ordinary person, this matter could be resolved without much impact, just adding some trouble for them.

But Chief Gao's attitude just now clearly indicated she wasn't an ordinary person!

"What do you think of Su Zhenxing's daughter from Donghai? And she's been lost for many years, just recently reunited with her family," Gao Xiaolong said expressionlessly.

Buzz!!!

Fine, fine.

Is this how you play the game?

Are you determined to make me, Jiang Peng, die without a burial place?

At this moment, Jiang Peng even had the urge to jump. Die, just die like this, and it will all be over. Because he had no idea that if something else happened to Su Tong, he, as the bureau chief, wouldn't be promoted, he'd probably be completely stripped of his position.

Who is Su Zhenxing?

Actually, this name is quite famous.

He's the head of the renowned Tianxing Group. Over twenty years ago, he rose from humble beginnings in a mountain village, gradually building his fortune through real estate development and construction.

Ultimately, he became a near-dominant player in the domestic real estate industry.

His assets have reached an immeasurable and terrifying level.

However, Su Zhenxing has only had one wife and has had no children for decades, a fact that once sparked heated debate: who would inherit such a vast fortune?

Unexpectedly, not long ago, a shocking piece of news broke.

Su Zhenxing had found his long-lost daughter.

Whoever marries Su Tong will almost instantly become the heir to a fortune of hundreds of millions.

Jiang Peng knows this because Su Zhenxing had previously posted missing person notices on news programs.

Later, he even publicly stated that he had found his daughter.

This incident caused quite a stir in the police station, especially among the men. Who wouldn't want to marry Su Zhenxing's daughter?

"The sky has fallen," Jiang Peng swallowed hard.

After all, this was the largest construction group in the country.

Not only were their assets countless, but more importantly, their network of connections was unimaginable.

And now, his daughter, whom he had finally managed to get back, was dead here.

Hiss!!!

"Chief Gao..." Jiang Peng's face showed a smile more like a grimace.

Gao Xiaolong didn't answer, because he had already run to the shore. Just moments before, the officers with spotlights on the bridge had cried out in alarm, only to be interrupted halfway by Su Tong jumping in.

This had even stunned them for a moment, leaving them unsure of what to do.

But there was no time to think further. They immediately rushed to the spotlights, hoping for some good news.

As for what kind of good news?

"Well, they don't know themselves."

It seems that whatever the news, it's not good news for them right now.

"What did you find?" Gao Xiaolong ran over and asked loudly.

Meanwhile, the police officers holding spotlights hurriedly aimed them at the river surface. Two whirlpools were constantly appearing in the murky water, looking extremely terrifying.

Don't underestimate those seemingly small, harmless whirlpools, because beneath them might lie terrifying underwater tornadoes, capable of sucking anything in their path.

In other words, these small whirlpools might be like hidden, seemingly harmless, but actually incredibly deadly undercurrents.

"Isn't that a whirlpool? What's there?" Gao Xiaolong frowned after examining the area carefully.

"No, I just saw someone pop up!" the officer shouted.

*Whoosh!*

Gao Xiaolong and Jiang Peng both tensed, their breathing quickening.

"Are you sure you didn't see wrong?" Jiang Peng shouted urgently.

"Chief, I didn't see wrong. I just saw a head pop up, absolutely no mistake, but then it went back down," the officer said cautiously.

Huh?

It came out, then went back down?

What is that thing?

While they watched, Shen Han also walked to the riverbank.

Director Shen was also feeling quite melancholy at this moment!

After Su Tong jumped in, Director Shen's mind went blank.

Especially after hearing Gao Xiaolong's words, he was completely dumbfounded.

What does that mean?

Look at who's on the river?

Su Zhenxing's daughter, Su Tong.

Jiang Tian, ​​the Sixth Merit King, goes without saying.

And Old Master Mu.

The lowest-ranking one, well, Secretary Cheng.

No, there's another murderer, but this murderer is also crucial.

Oh, no, there's also Mu Feng.

Apart from Mu Sheng, whose sacrifice wouldn't cause too much chaos, the sacrifice of anyone else at this time would be an absolute cataclysmic earthquake.

After hearing the police officer's words, Director Shen immediately observed the situation, anxiously hoping for a turning point.

What, you said the rescue team?

There's still half an hour.

Half an hour later, what were the rescue teams doing? Retrieving bodies?

It's fair to say that the entire river, even the armed police, had blocked off the bridge and rushed down to the riverbanks.

But was it any use?

They stood on the bridge, on both sides of the Yanjiang River, completely helpless. They could only watch them drown; not a single person could get down.

Some even went down with ropes, but as soon as they entered, even life jackets were useless—they sank instantly. Luckily, several people on the shore held onto the ropes; otherwise, they would have been swept away by the current.

Danger.

Because of the recent heavy rains, the Yanjiang River was now at its most dangerous level; falling in was almost certainly fatal.

Even with the rescue teams on site, whether they could pull anyone up was uncertain.

Could there really be any chance of a breakthrough at this point?

Director Shen's heart was heavy!

If these people run into serious trouble, do you really think only Yunhai or Jiang Peng will bear all the responsibility? That's a joke. Director Shen is also primarily responsible, and when he's punished, he'll undoubtedly be one of those responsible as well.

Then...

Just as Shen Han was gradually despairing, even almost accepting his fate...

In the murky river water, at the center of a small whirlpool, a small dark shadow suddenly appeared in the light.

Then, almost without pause, the small dark shadow bulged from the river's surface in an instant.

Finally, it gradually solidified into a head emerging from the water.

"It's a person! It's a person! It's appeared!" Shen Han roared, his hands gripping the railing with bulging veins.

Accompanying Shen Han's roar,

sure enough,

the scene erupted completely.

Jiang Peng and Gao Xiaolong were also utterly shocked.

Because they had just witnessed it firsthand. When they arrived, they found ropes and life jackets, and organized skilled swimmers to try.

However, the moment they went in, they were swept away by the undercurrent. They couldn't even hold on for a few seconds. If it weren't for the rope, one more person would have perished. It was unimaginable; this was practically a death trap.

But what did they see now?

In this death trap.

The only man to surface.



Chapter 316 A Miracle Occurs, Creating a Legend Once Again


"This section of the Yanjiang River, after the confluence of two rivers upstream, has become treacherous with undercurrents for dozens of miles downstream, including this stretch. Due to the current speed and other factors, this area is already countless times more dangerous than other bodies of water, with countless times more undercurrents. Even a feather would be sucked to the bottom. Now, with the recent heavy rains, mudflows have mixed in, causing the river to overflow, making it extremely difficult for rescue teams to go down."

Jiang Peng had personally investigated the specific situation, and it was precisely because he understood it that the despair was so palpable.

Under these circumstances, how could anyone possibly survive?

Don't be ridiculous.

Retrieving a body would be considered lucky.

Unlucky, there would be no trace of the remains; the body would be nowhere to be found at the bottom of the river, or perhaps swept away by the undercurrents. The Yanjiang River flows too fast; the body might be gone by the end of the day.

As for someone?

A person emerged from the water?

Isn't that absurd?

Jiang Peng simply didn't believe it.

However, Shen Han was trembling with excitement at this moment.

Because...

This wasn't just about Jiang Peng's promotion; it was about his own situation.

That's right, in the final report on handling the problem, Jiang Peng was listed first among those responsible, and Shen Han was also among them, inescapably implicated.

One Elder Mu was already overwhelming, and then there was Jiang Tian, ​​the future of the police system, the peacemaker, the Six Merits King, who was being watched by high-ranking officials.

This was even more unbearable.

Following closely behind, even Su Zhenxing's daughter had jumped in.

Good heavens.

It's all over.

He was almost in despair.

At this moment, seeing hope, how could Shen Han not tremble with excitement?

At this very moment, as Shen Han trembled, the head of the figure that had surfaced finally completely broke the surface.

Although it had escaped the water's surface, it was still clearly bobbing up and down, sometimes even being dragged underwater by whirlpools.

But the next moment, it surged back up.

Using the momentum, it swiftly and violently broke free from the whirlpool, the force of its leaps and the slapping of the water's surface like a bomb, creating a tremendous commotion.

The figure's face was finally facing the bridge.

This allowed Shen Han to clearly see the other person's face. Although water was splashing everywhere and hair was plastered to their forehead, thankfully, the person's hair wasn't long.

The face was clearly displayed before Shen Han, causing Shen Han to be utterly overwhelmed with surprise.

"Jiang...Jiang Tian, ​​damn it, Jiang Tian, ​​that's Jiang Tian!" Shen Han roared.

Seeing Jiang Tian, ​​Shen Han seemed to see his impending fate gradually changing.

He was still Director Shen.

He continued to safeguard the lives and property of the people of Yunnan Province.

This wasn't because he coveted power; it was simply that Jiang Tian's survival gave him the opportunity to continue serving the people.

Director Shen's eyes welled with tears.

A ray of hope beckoned to him.

A visible reshuffling of officialdom seemed to be receding into the distance.

As Director Shen shouted, Jiang Peng and Gao Xiaolong didn't look at him.

Like Director Shen, their eyes were fixed on the river's surface; it was impossible for them not to notice the splash.

"Still alive! Still alive! Damn it, I knew it! This guy was a menace, dying so easily!" Gao Xiaolong laughed, his voice trembling.

Shock.

In truth, their shock was genuine.

During this time, they all knew that this stretch of the Yanjiang River, dozens of miles long and nearly 300 meters wide, was practically a death trap.

Almost no one who went in survived.

You could say that even with a rescue team by your side, if you fell in, they wouldn't be able to pull you out.

Because the Yanjiang River's currents were too complex, unimaginably complex. The undercurrents were intricate and unpredictable; no one could possibly foresee where you'd be swept away.

Especially now, after the torrential downpour.

Mu Ya and Jiang Xiaoyu, hearing the commotion, ran over as expected. Seeing Jiang Tian surface, well, their despair was finally broken.

But they were also bewildered!

These people didn't know.

But they did!

Before the police arrived, Jiang Tian had already jumped in several minutes ago, silently, causing almost no ripples on the water.

Then came the police and spent nearly ten minutes investigating.

In these complex water conditions, ten minutes of silence was enough to drive anyone to despair.

But now...

"I knew it, he's a man who likes to create miracles," Mu Ya said, tears of joy streaming down her face.

"Don't get too excited yet. The rescue team hasn't arrived. There's still danger. Xiao Jiang jumped in and hasn't come out for so long; he must have been swept away by the undercurrent. He was just caught in it too, especially now that it's rained heavily. There are also mudslides and flash floods inside, making the danger exponentially greater. Urge the rescue team to hurry up," Gao Xiaolong said in a deep voice.

This interrupted everyone's jubilation.

That's right!

They seemed to have celebrated too soon.

Jiang Tian jumped in and disappeared without a trace. Was he playing hide-and-seek with them? That's ridiculous.

He was clearly trapped.

Therefore.

The danger is still there.

Actually, they were right.

Jiang Tian, ​​in the river, wiped the water droplets from his forehead. Even he couldn't tell if it was cold sweat or river water.

In any case, Jiang Tian himself was still shaken.

After jumping in, Jiang Tian tried to swim back up, but suddenly, he felt a terrifying force. What did it feel like?

It was like a non-swimmer standing at chest level, involuntarily being pulled into deeper water.

An irresistible force swept Jiang Tian directly to the bottom of the river. Even now, his hands are still covered in mud from the riverbed.

This area was dozens of meters deep, yet it instantly dragged Jiang Tian down, pulled by various forces.

Jiang Tian really struggled, finally managing to break free from the current and scramble to the surface using both hands and feet.

If Jiang Tian hadn't activated his golden talent, the God of the Ocean, he probably would have perished here.

Human strength has its limits.

Jiang Tian was genuinely shaken. Humans are so insignificant in the face of nature.

Of course, Jiang Tian was also quite lucky. After floating up, he was only a few dozen meters away from the two sinking cars—a stroke of good fortune amidst misfortune.



Chapter 317 Gathering, Attention, Moving Forward Under Heavy Burdens


Of course, even more unfortunately, at this moment, only half of the car's rear end was above water.

The situation inside the car was still unknown.

Jiang Tian didn't hesitate for a moment. Without resting, he increased his speed further, swimming with incredible speed, like an arrow released from a bow. Jiang Tian's strength was already terrifying.

Moreover, his swimming form was almost unbelievably perfect, faster and more graceful than a professional athlete, fully demonstrating a kind of violent aesthetics.

Jiang Tian spread his arms, and after each dip in the water, he instantly leaped several meters.

Almost in the blink of an eye, Jiang Tian didn't even look at the thugs' car.

What happened to the thugs didn't matter.

Even Secretary Cheng and the others didn't matter, but Old Master Mu absolutely couldn't be harmed.

Fortunately, he was lucky.

Jiang Tian smashed the car window with his fist, and as water rushed in,

Jiang Tian saw Old Master Mu sitting in the back seat, the water already up to his neck.

Secretary Cheng was also in the back seat, her face pressed against the roof.

However, it was obvious that Secretary Cheng was exhausted.

No, because after careful observation, Jiang Tian realized that the water level was too high, and there was no room for two people. So, at the last moment, Secretary Cheng gave the opportunity to Old Master Mu. He himself was gradually losing strength, and his raised hand was slowly falling.

"Very good, very good."

Jiang Tian looked at her with admiration.

To be honest, Jiang Tian could already imagine that if Secretary Cheng survived, she would undoubtedly become one of Old Master Mu's most trusted confidants.

Of course, this had nothing to do with Jiang Tian himself, because he admired Secretary Cheng; she was someone who truly risked her life for her leader in the final moments.

What leader wouldn't like someone like that?

While he was thinking this, Jiang Tian's actions were swift and decisive. Almost instantly, he grabbed Old Master Mu and Secretary Cheng, who was on the verge of unconsciousness.

As for the driver's seat...

Jiang Tian glanced at it and sighed deeply. It was hopeless; there was no saving him.

Because there was a clear gash on his forehead, he was probably stunned by the airbags when the car crashed, and drowned instantly upon entering the water.

As for Mu Feng in the passenger seat, it was unclear whether he was dead or alive. Bubbles occasionally rose from his nose in the water, and he remained motionless with his head down.

Well, he's practically his future father-in-law, after all.

Jiang Tian didn't hesitate. After making an immediate assessment, he grabbed one person with each hand, and finally unbuckled the passenger seatbelt. Just as the car was about to sink completely, Jiang Tian dragged the three of them to the surface.

Secretary Cheng was semi-conscious, like Mu Feng, barely aware of anything. Jiang Tian, ​​holding onto their clothes, half-submerged in the water, occasionally swallowing mouthfuls of water.

There was no other way.

Jiang Tian was having a tough time; he didn't have three heads and six arms.

It was fortunate that he was dragging three people at once; try it on someone else.

As for Old Master Mu...

At this moment, it must be said that his condition was relatively stable, because Jiang Tian had the strength to manage with one hand, so he hadn't swallowed much water.

He remained silent as Jiang Tian dragged him to the shore.

A slight smile appeared on his face when he saw Jiang Tian appear to rescue them, but he remained silent afterward.

For Old Master Mu, this was a disgrace, a profound one.

It wasn't just a disgrace; what chilled him to the bone, and what enraged him, was who?

So utterly depraved.

He'd been murdered. Old Master Mu's mind raced.

But outwardly, he didn't show it. He sighed and glanced at Secretary Cheng, saying, "I was puzzled when you were the first one I called, but now I understand."

"Secretary Cheng's assessment of the situation is quite good," Jiang Tian said, his nerves on edge.

Because it wasn't safe yet, in fact, it was even more dangerous than before. Jiang Tian had to assess the undercurrents.

Feeling the current, Jiang Tian was now dragging one person with one hand and two others in a near-dead state with the other. His hands were essentially bound, and he was only propelled by the buoyancy of his legs.

If they were swept away by the current, all three except Jiang Tian would die.

Fortunately, Jiang Tian's golden affix to the God of the Ocean skill even allowed him to avoid some undercurrents in advance.

Jiang Tian proceeded cautiously. He was now in the very center of the Yanjiang River, more than a hundred meters from the shore on either side.

"Little Jiang, if there's any danger later, just put the three of us down," Old Mu said, finally recovering from his oxygen deprivation after a short while of heavy breathing.

However, he clearly understood his predicament.

"I would have done it even if you hadn't said anything," Jiang Tian replied without change in expression.

High-sounding words were unnecessary at this moment.

From this day forward, he was Old Mu's greatest savior.

Even if Old Mu didn't admit it, everyone in Yunhai, no, in the entire Yun Province, knew, and that was enough.

As for anything else, it didn't matter.

This would allow Jiang Tian to avoid many unknown risks in advance. For example, if Jing Feifan had had the connection with Elder Mu to the contrary from the beginning, would he have dared to do this?

If they really got caught in the undercurrents, Jiang Tian wouldn't be dragged down with them; that would be foolish.

"I knew you wouldn't be that brainless," Elder Mu laughed.

However, at this moment, Jiang Tian could still see that Elder Mu was clearly tense.

That's right.

Who would want to die if they could live?

He hadn't yet seen his granddaughter, Mu Ya, get married.

He still had many things left to do.

He still had many reforms to begin.

And his greatest enemy hadn't been dealt with.

And at this moment.

After Jiang Tian dragged out Elder Mu, Secretary Cheng, and Mu Feng.

The people on the shore went crazy.

That's right, crazy.

Jiang Tian, ​​alone, was dragging three people along in the raging river, a sight that made many people think they were dreaming.

Is he crazy?

This is unbelievable! One person saved three people at once?

They'd seen many people rescued from drowning.

But this was the first time they'd seen someone dragging three at once.

Of course, the most terrifying thing was that it happened in such a turbulent river.

Gao Xiaolong led his men, frantically running towards the riverbank in the direction Jiang Tian was swimming.

People like Director Shen and Jiang Peng, along with numerous police officers, followed closely behind.

Besides them.

At this moment, many leaders who had received the news also arrived, running towards the riverbank after Gao Xiaolong.

What does it mean to weep with joy?

This is it.

They thought everyone was dead, but not only did Jiang Tian survive, but Old Mu was also saved.

This, this... a hero reborn in the eyes of many leaders, like Director Shen.



Chapter 318 Shakes the Entire Province; The Incident Grows Larger and Larger


To be honest, Jiang Tian was struggling to swim.

The Yanjiang River is constantly flowing, and although it doesn't look particularly turbulent, its current is actually terrifyingly fast.

Jiang Tian had to dodge the undercurrents while simultaneously fighting off the river's powerful current. Of course, this wouldn't be a problem for Jiang Tian himself, but don't forget, he was carrying three people!

Except for the driver.

In this situation, Jiang Tian was powerless.

Even if the driver might survive, if Jiang Tian was carrying another person, the risk of them all drowning would increase significantly.

In short, Jiang Tian couldn't turn back.

It was exhausting!

However, overall, it was still not bad.

The result was good.

Jiang Tian swam at incredible speed in the river. The God of the Ocean had given Jiang Tian terrifying swimming skills; he could even sense the current's speed and pinpoint the location of undercurrents to avoid them in advance.

Adding to Jiang Tian's own extraordinary human attribute, without either of these attributes, Jiang Tian's feat today would have been nothing more than a sacrifice.

And then.

The mighty Sixth-Rank King, no, the one who might soon become a Seventh-Rank King, might very well have drowned here.

It was truly a case of capsizing in a ditch.

The process was dangerous and terrifying, but the result was relatively good.

What puzzled Jiang Tian was that when he emerged from the water, something seemed to have fallen in, but it didn't matter; it was probably just rescuers on the shore hurriedly throwing some rescue equipment down.

After all, he had already wasted a lot of time.

Swept in by the undercurrent, he was directly pulled to the bottom of the river. It took Jiang Tian nearly ten minutes to barely break free. If it had been ten minutes later, Jiang Tian estimated that he could have retrieved a few bodies.

They're here.

They've finally arrived.

When Jiang Tian dragged three people about ten meters from the riverbank...

"Rope! Rope! Throw the rope!" Gao Xiaolong roared.

As for throwing life jackets, forget about that now.

Good heavens.

Jiang Tian was really in a bind now, dragging two completely unconscious people with one arm. Yes, Secretary Cheng and Mu Feng were truly ashen-faced and almost unconscious; it was unclear whether they were alive or dead.

Jiang Tian couldn't afford to keep them all on the surface; he could only struggle to swim, barely managing to keep them from drowning.

As for whether they could be saved in the end, Jiang Tian himself didn't know.

Most importantly, Jiang Tian was very rational, keeping Old Mu's nose above water. To do this, Jiang Tian himself spent most of his time with his nose and mouth submerged.

Dragging three people was simply too much even for Jiang Tian, ​​with his Ocean God's aura.

Ultimately, this only gave Jiang Tian the world's top-tier, most perfect swimming skills and an extraordinary sensitivity to water.

Even in the Atlantic Ocean, accurately determining direction and finding the way home from hundreds of miles away, but now, carrying three people across the Yanjiang River—this is truly a miracle.

Who doesn't know the danger and terror of the Yanjiang River?

It's infamous, practically legendary!

Last year, so many people drowned in the Yanjiang River. One person, disbelieving the river's danger, even dared to challenge it, and ultimately, their body was never found.

But Jiang Tian?

This is more than just creating a miracle.

The Yanjiang River is inherently dangerous, but the recent heavy rains have amplified its peril many times over.

For ordinary people, going down into the river is certain death.

But Jiang Tian not only went down, he even dragged three people with him. He really only had one chance. With the car completely flooded, a second trip, including the time spent searching, would likely only yield bodies.

So, despite the danger, Jiang Tian took a gamble.

And he succeeded.

Jiang Tian's success

completely shocked everyone on the riverbank.

Who wouldn't be shocked?

In that short stretch of just over ten meters, without the rescue team arriving, even those wearing life jackets and secured by ropes couldn't get into the water.

The previous incident served as a stark warning; those who went in nearly drowned.

At that moment, the entire Yanjiang River felt like a no-man's land, a place where anyone who went in would die.

Jiang Tian managed to pull three people back.

Gao Xiaolong's scalp tingled, his face flushed, his head practically exploding, as he roared at the top of his lungs, yelling for the rope to be thrown.

Life jackets were out of the question; Jiang Tian couldn't wear them and didn't need them now.

But the rope could be thrown to Elder Mu, to the conscious Elder Mu.

As Gao Xiaolong roared,

those behind him hurriedly reacted.

At that moment, more than a dozen spotlights converged on the shore, where the figures of people were densely packed.

It was even a bit crowded, but no matter how crowded it was, apart from a few armed police officers in life jackets, tied with ropes, who were close to the shore, everyone else could only stand five or six meters away. They couldn't get close, because if they fell in, it would be a certain death.

"Got it!"

One of the armed police officers pulled the rope with a look of surprise. The other end of the rope was perfectly looped around Old Mu's waist.

This also allowed Gao Xiaolong on the shore to breathe a sigh of relief.

That's how it is.

When you've prepared for the worst, even if the final outcome is bad, from a human perspective, there's still a feeling of having lost something and then regaining it.

"A miracle, this is simply a miracle."

"I've worked in Yunhai for so long, and I've never heard of or seen anyone manage to cross the Yanjiang River, let alone with three people in tow."

"More than that, this is the Yanjiang River after a torrential downpour. Even on the surface of the water, I've never seen anyone manage to save three people at once. Even a world swimming champion might not be able to do it."

"A world swimming champion might be able to drag three unconscious, non-struggling people, but absolutely not on the Yanjiang River."

A sensation ensued.

The people on the shore were in an uproar.

Even Jiang Peng could only stand on the edge at this point.

Why?

Because there were too many people there.

Almost all the leaders of Yunhai were present.

What? You dare say you weren't here? Elder Mu, the top official in charge of law enforcement in Yun Province, is in such great danger, his life hanging by a thread, and you still haven't come? Do you have some kind of problem?

Or rather, what could be more serious than this?

The leaders of Yunnan Province.

While Jiang Tian was rescuing people, they arrived one after another.

When Jiang Tian finally reached the shore, he looked up and saw countless figures, blurry indistinct, but without a doubt, almost all of them were wearing suits or executive jackets.

The next moment, with a splash, Old Mu was pulled ashore first. A cold wind blew past, and in a daze, Old Mu looked at the scene before him, a survivor.

Jiang Tian had snatched him back from the jaws of death.



Chapter 319 First-Class Merit Citation: Joint Petition to Apply for First-Class Merit Citation for Jiang Tian


So, in the end, it was Jiang Tian who saved him!

Old Mu took a deep breath. Although he was soaked, he was still sweating profusely.

This was the first time in his life he had experienced the feeling of teetering on the edge of death.

He was only one step away from death.

At that moment, in the rush, Director Shen, with his keen eye, rushed forward, carrying a thick coat and draping it over Old Mu.

Besides that,

Ambulances from medical personnel were frantically rushing over from afar.

That's why they say that.

Who said they were slow in their work? Jiang Tian was on the shore, and the once spacious shoreline now seemed incredibly crowded.

The medical personnel had only just arrived, but in an emergency, government officials arrived faster than them.

"Old leader, are you alright?" Director Shen's face was full of relief.

No other reason than:

He saved my life!

Not only in terms of saving the lives of those responsible, but also politically, Director Shen had always stood on the same side as Elder Mu.

If Elder Mu were to get into trouble, Shen Han would be truly finished.

“Xiao Jiang, quickly get Xiao Jiang and the others up!” Elder Mu, without time to exchange pleasantries with the other leaders, hurriedly shouted to the armed police at the scene.

Upon hearing Elder Mu's voice, everyone gathered back on the shore.

At that moment, Jiang Tian was dragging two people ashore.

For Jiang Tian, ​​it was only because of the torrential rain that had just hit Yanjiang that day that the situation was unusual; otherwise, he wouldn't have been delayed for so long. Trapped underwater for over ten minutes, if someone else had jumped in like Jiang Tian, ​​they would have been swept to the bottom and searched everywhere; their body would have been impossible to find in a short time.

"Little Jiang, what a hero!"

"What? Little Jiang? Could he be that anti-drug hero?"

"No wonder! When I first arrived, I thought some hero had created a miracle. I've heard so much about the Six Merits King."

"Wow, I never imagined! Comrade Little Jiang, you were so brave and invincible fighting criminals on the battlefield, and your underwater skills are unmatched."

"A commendation is due! A commendation is absolutely necessary! Comrade Little Jiang has saved the Party and the country from immeasurable losses; he must be awarded a first-class merit."

The moment Jiang Tian stepped ashore, he instantly became the center of attention.

While this battle didn't have the same massive or terrifying impact as fighting hundreds of drug dealers, the final result was absolutely no less than, and perhaps even surpassed, the capture of an entire terrifying criminal syndicate.

Old Mu!

He saved Old Mu.

Even more so, he single-handedly held off three others in the Yanjiang River.

"Not to mention rescuing three people, even jumping off the Yanjiang Bridge—besides Xiao Jiang, who else would dare?" Old Mu praised Jiang Tian at this moment.

Old Mu already remembered this favor.

In that dangerous and terrifying moment, who else but Jiang Tian?

Mu Ya and Jiang Xiaoyu were already at the back of the crowd; the two girls were now trembling with fear.

After seeing Jiang Tian rescue the people and bring them ashore, they almost burst into tears of joy, embracing each other and crying.

Especially for Mu Ya, it wasn't just the loss of her grandfather and father that meant losing Jiang Tian; she was even about to lose him as well.

Her mother had just been divorced.

This meant Mu Ya was about to lose everything.

She would have no family left.

But now.

A miracle happened.

A miracle smiled upon them.

Her father, grandfather, and Jiang Tian were all back.

The feeling of going from hell to heaven was indescribable.

"They're back! I knew it! Brother could do it!" Jiang Xiaoyu exclaimed joyfully, wanting to greet Jiang Tian.

Mu Ya followed closely behind.

But just as they stood up, Old Master Mu's words stunned them instantly.

Because they had been so focused on Jiang Tian and Old Master Mu, their minds were taut, and they seemed to have forgotten something else.

But Old Master Mu's words instantly brought them back to their senses.

Jiang Xiaoyu and Mu Ya came to their senses.

Jiang Peng, Director Shen, and Gao Xiaolong's expressions of surprise instantly froze. No wonder, Mu Ya and Jiang Xiaoyu had thought of it; how could they have forgotten?

They were simply overwhelmed with joy and forgot immediately.

"Chief Gao, it's a pity. Due to the rush and the special circumstances, we couldn't rescue the driver and the other perpetrator in time," Jiang Tian sighed softly.

Of course, Jiang Tian didn't consider these two people unimportant; it's just that at the time, his only immediate priority was to rescue as many as possible, with the most crucial personnel being the top priority.

Besides,

whether these people could swim or not was irrelevant. In the Yanjiang River, swimming skills were useless.

The currents were complex and treacherous, the river's current was extremely fast, and there were even muddy mudslides.

That's the gist of it.

Fortunately, they were both in the car, and Jiang Tian managed to pull them out immediately.

If a person had fallen in, given the complex currents and terrifying speed, they might have been swept away by an undercurrent for miles in an instant.

Like Jiang Tian, ​​who jumped off the Yanjiang Bridge and was swept to the bottom of the river by an undercurrent, only to surface a few dozen meters from the car after struggling to stay afloat.

In other words, he was swept nearly a thousand meters.

The level of danger was evident.

Even with all his credentials, Jiang Tian was unwilling to go down. If there were any legitimate rescues needed, that was one thing, but the murderer and the driver were likely doomed. Even if they went back, the car was nowhere to be found, and the search would be difficult. They could only send the salvage team up.

"No, Xiao Jiang, actually..." Gao Xiaolong swallowed hard.

Actually, Jiang Peng's expression wasn't good either.

If only he hadn't been so sarcastic!

So be it if Jiang Tian is insulted.

Why not let your son suffer a little? Why insist on revealing Jiang Tian's identity?

As a result, a young girl jumped in.

She was so humiliated and couldn't face herself; overwhelmed with shame, she jumped into the river.

This made Jiang Peng realize that the young girl wasn't malicious, nor was she an accomplice; the only one with malicious intent was her companion.

“Xiao Jiang…” Gao Xiaolong stammered.

Actually, it wasn't that they wanted Gao Xiaolong to go down. They understood the dangers of the Yanjiang River. Jiang Tian had created a miracle; he was willing, and they didn't even want him to risk his life a second time.

The hesitation stemmed from their inability to articulate the situation.

Could they say, “You just went down to save someone, risking your life to pull them ashore, and then a young girl, in a moment of despair, jumps in?”

Just as Jiang Tian was filled with confusion,

a hurried voice rang out.

“Su Tong, Su Tong…”

Accompanied by the voice, a figure rushed down from the shore.

Jiang Tian squinted.

Behind the man who shouted, several men in jackets and suits followed closely.



Chapter 320 Su Zhenxing Collapses, Where is the Rescue Team?


Just as the man's voice rang out, armed police stepped in to stop them; after all, unauthorized personnel were not allowed to approach at this time.

The next moment, the man's voice rang out: "I am Su Zhenxing, chairman of Tianxing Group. My daughter has fallen into the water! Please save my daughter!"

Hiss!!!

Good heavens.

Jiang Tian's eyes gradually blurred.

Su Zhenxing?

Tianxing Group?

How could Jiang Tian not recognize these two names?

It was around 2003.

Actually, at this time, the real estate industry had not yet experienced its complete boom. The real boom in the real estate industry would begin after 2008.

Of course, there were already some signs, but many people had no idea how terrifyingly high housing prices would rise across the country after 2008.

And along with the nationwide rise in housing prices,

related practitioners and companies naturally followed suit.

Among all the real estate companies in the country, Tianxing Group stands out as the undisputed leader, far surpassing all other conglomerates.

This is a colossal and terrifying conglomerate.

It has been engaged in massive development and construction across the country, its scale expanding exponentially, even reaching a peak where its market value increased tenfold in a single year.

Before Jiang Tian's transmigration, although the real estate industry was gradually entering its twilight years, Tianxing Group possessed an incredibly formidable vision.

It had already entered the new energy industry ahead of time, completing its industrial transformation ahead of schedule. Before the industry's decline, it sold off a large number of properties, allowing the group to not only land smoothly but also reach new heights.

Before Jiang Tian's transmigration, it had already become a terrifyingly powerful corporation.

Of course, Jiang Tian's understanding was only superficial. Given his previous life's status and position, he was not qualified to understand the deeper layers.

Therefore, this is the reason for Jiang Tian's bewilderment.

If he remembered correctly, there was a rumor that the founder of Tianxing Group had a daughter who had been lost and found. However, shortly after her marriage, her husband secretly injected her with drugs. What happened to her in the end was something Jiang Tian couldn't know.

These thoughts only briefly swirled in Jiang Tian's mind before he turned to look at Gao Xiaolong and Director Shen with a bewildered expression.

"Someone else fell into the water?" Jiang Tian asked, slightly confused.

Gao Xiaolong and Director Shen didn't speak. Jiang Peng stepped forward, giving Jiang Tian an awkward look, and said, "After you fell in, I learned what happened. I know some ill-intentioned people criticized and insulted you, so I briefly mentioned your honors. As a result, a young girl couldn't think straight and jumped in. Later, through Chief Gao, I learned that it was Su Zhenxing's daughter, Su Tong, from Tianxing Group. Nobody expected that the young girl would do something like this."

Pfft!!

What the hell?

Jiang Tian coughed violently and spat out some water.

That's why I say it.

Just as the skill was being equipped, a mudslide swept over Jiang Tian. Before he could even adjust to the skill, he gulped down several mouthfuls of water, and his stomach was still bloated.

"Is Xiao Jiang alright?"

Director Shen rushed to Jiang Tian's side to help him up, his expression somewhat complicated.

Because he knew that from today onwards, no one could stop Comrade Jiang's promotion.

"It's alright, it's alright for now," Jiang Tian's lips twitched.

This problem wasn't actually that serious. After all, the young girl had been the first to speak rudely, insulting Jiang Tian, ​​the Sixth-Rank King. Although it hadn't caused serious consequences, she had still made a mistake first. Jiang Peng hadn't said anything particularly harsh, only mentioning Jiang Tian's honor.

The young girl herself couldn't take it and jumped.

To be honest, Jiang Tian had a good memory. He quickly scanned his surroundings, and the people on the bridge he remembered matched the young girl—she wasn't very old.

Jiang Tian was certain of his target; it was probably the woman who got out of the passenger seat of the Mercedes-Benz.

However, Jiang Tian was slightly shocked.

He never expected that the woman, completely covered up and wearing a mask, was actually Su Zhenxing's daughter, with such a terrifying background, and, more importantly, such a weak mental fortitude.

Regardless, Jiang Tian, ​​though unwilling to stand idly by, looked at the surging river. The two cars that had been floating just moments before had sunk to the bottom, their whereabouts unknown, after several people had been rescued.

And the direction Su Tong had jumped in...

While Jiang Tian was lost in thought, Su Zhenxing, accompanied by armed police, was anxiously searching for people on the riverbank, frantically dialing for rescue teams.

At that moment, Jiang Tian saw the helplessness of an elderly father.

Yes, no matter how successful or wealthy one is outside, at this moment, looking at the surging river with their children's fate unknown, that helpless anxiety is something everyone can feel.

"Where's the rescue team? Where's the rescue team? Where's the rescue team? Please save my daughter!"

Su Zhenxing's voice trembled, and the group of people following behind him finally ran down the river.

When the group came down, the leaders initially didn't react, but upon closer inspection of the leader, everyone shuddered.

"Provincial Governor Huai!!!"

"Provincial Governor Huai, you're here?"

"Old Huai, what are you doing here?"

At this moment, Old Mu looked at the leader in astonishment.

That's right.

Old Huai.

Although they were at the same administrative level, he was a higher-ranking leader in terms of management!

Old Huai seemed to have already learned about the situation. Seeing that Old Mu was alright, he instantly breathed a sigh of relief and said, "Old Mu, it's good that you're alright. Recently, our Yun Province has been attracting investment, preparing to invite Tianxing Group to build a large commercial complex to alleviate the pressure on the city. On the other hand, it's also for local development. President Su came all this way, but his daughter was taken away by a friend. No one expected that President Su's daughter would fall into the water. What exactly happened?"

Although he knew some things, he didn't know much.

But now, Old Huai knew this wasn't the time to talk about that, so he quickly changed the subject, asking, "How did you get up here? Where's your rescue team? Where did they go?"

"Rescue team?"

This question immediately silenced Old Mu.

"That's right, where's the rescue team?"



Chapter 321: A Second Dive into the Water, Everyone Was Moved to Tears, Hero


Meanwhile, Jiang Tian's expression grew increasingly strange. Jiang Peng was beside him, explaining what had just happened. Furthermore, after Su Tong jumped in, Jiang Tian immediately rescued her, though he hadn't learned much more.

However, through Jiang Peng's explanation and Jiang Tian's understanding of the whole situation, he roughly figured out what had occurred.

The girl hadn't said anything particularly offensive.

She was mainly misunderstood. If the person with malicious intent was someone who initially stirred up trouble for those they disliked, the man who probably had psychological issues.

That is, the man who was with Su Tong.

If anyone was really going to jump into the water, it shouldn't have been Su Tong, but the man driving the Mercedes-Benz.

Of course.

After clarifying the situation, Jiang Tian faced a most serious problem:

Rescuing her.

Regardless of Su Tong's background, even if she were just an ordinary citizen, someone who, after realizing her mistake, was so ashamed as to jump into the river, while perhaps a little dim-witted, clearly wasn't inherently bad.

Jiang Tian couldn't simply ignore it.

Therefore, Jiang Tian continued almost without pause.

Meanwhile,

Huai Sheng also learned what had happened.

It turned out that Huai Sheng and Su Zhenxing had been having dinner together to discuss cooperation, and during this dinner, Su Zhenxing's daughter, Su Tong, left first.

Huai Sheng had even seen her off with a smile, reminding her to be careful, and had personally watched as the young man with the tiger-headed scooter picked her up.

At that time, Huai Sheng had even asked who the young man was.

Su Zhenxing didn't hide anything, because he genuinely felt guilty towards his daughter, who had been lost for many years and was finally found again.

Even after being recognized by the family, his daughter showed no arrogance and never mentioned her identity when out in public.

However, Su Zhenxing had specifically investigated Su Tong's past.

For example, there was Ye Xuan. After graduating high school at a young age, although he only attended an ordinary second-tier university, his quick wit allowed him to start businesses during summer vacations. During university, his assets steadily increased, reaching nearly ten million by graduation.

This was absolutely outstanding among young people, and most importantly, he seemed to be his daughter's childhood sweetheart.

Because of this, Su Zhenxing felt relatively comfortable letting her go on the trip.

But unexpectedly, she fell into the river halfway through.

Su Zhenxing's eyes reddened.

There was no time to understand what was going on.

They rushed over, but now they knew their daughter was in the river.

Su Zhenxing could only watch helplessly, because when he came down, he had personally witnessed life jackets thrown in being instantly swept to the bottom.

However, they were too late to witness Jiang Tian's rescue.

"We were pulled out of the water by Comrade Xiao Jiang," Old Mu sighed.

Director Shen hurriedly added, "The rescue team will probably arrive in another twenty minutes."

Buzz!!!

These words were heard.

Su Zhenxing and Huai Sheng were both stunned.

They were well aware of the dangers of the Yanjiang River; everyone knew the terror of this forbidden zone.

Su Zhenxing was unaware, but Huai Sheng had told him about the dangerous situation at Yanjiang on the way.

“That’s right, one person carrying three—it’s practically a miracle.”

“It was incredibly dangerous. Xiao Jiang went down and was immediately sucked to the bottom. It took him over ten minutes to surface; he almost drowned.”

“The currents here are too complex. We must strengthen safety measures around Yanjiang when we get back, especially the railings of the Yanjiang Bridge. We need to reinforce and stabilize them further.”

“Old Mu was able to get up thanks to Xiao Jiang!”

Before Old Mu could speak, the leaders standing nearby all chimed in.

They were particularly astonished when they mentioned Jiang Tian.

This immediately drew Su Zhenxing and Huai Sheng's attention to Jiang Tian.

Su Zhenxing, in particular, couldn't believe his eyes: “You rescued him all by yourself?”

Jiang Tian remained silent, frowning.

In reality, quite a bit of time had already been wasted.

Of course, even if Jiang Tian had discovered it at the beginning, it wouldn't have mattered, because Jiang Tian needed to rescue Elder Mu immediately.

Therefore, Su Tong naturally still had no chance.

In such a long time, no one knew where she was.

"Save my daughter."

Just then, Su Zhenxing came to Jiang Tian and knelt down on the ground.

Regardless of how Jiang Tian did it.

Regardless of how formidable Jiang Tian's swimming skills were, at this moment, Su Zhenxing was no longer a wealthy man, nor was he the powerful CEO Su who dominated the business world; he was simply a father.

This deeply moved Jiang Tian.

For no other reason than this.

As a wealthy man, if Su Zhenxing had directly offered Jiang Tian ten million or a hundred million to save his daughter, using money to get Jiang Tian to swim, Jiang Tian wouldn't have been willing, and even if he had gone in, he would have looked down on him.

But Su Zhenxing, at this moment, was kneeling before him.

"I'll try my best," Jiang Tian's lips moved slightly.

This caused Su Zhenxing's eyes to light up instantly.

After understanding the whole situation, Su Zhenxing's legs had gone weak, especially after learning about the danger in Yanjiang; his mind was even buzzing.

He had actually given up hope.

From initially rescuing his daughter, to now, finding her body.

The hope was that the salvage team would arrive quickly and retrieve his daughter's body.

But now...

Su Zhenxing's eyes shone brightly; he finally saw hope.

"Young man, I beg you. Whether Su Tong is dead or alive, you are a benefactor to our Su family. If there is any danger, please take care of yourself," Su Zhenxing said solemnly.

Jiang Tian didn't help him up.

At this point, Jiang Tian himself lacked confidence.

Even without Su Tong, Jiang Tian planned to go down a second time to see if he could find the killer's car and bring the killer back up.

However, for now, it seems that Elder Mu's case can be put on hold for a while and investigated slowly. The most important thing now is to save lives.

"Brother."

Splash!!!

Saving lives is paramount.

Mu Ya and Jiang Xiaoyu had just squeezed through the crowd when all they could see was Jiang Tian diving straight into the river.

In reality,

this wasn't just a matter of slim hope.

it was practically hopeless.

The river flowed swiftly, without a trace of a body. How could one person possibly search such a vast river as Yanjiang?

Even if a rescue team came,

the most they could probably do was retrieve the body by boat using grappling hooks.

But Jiang Tian knew that such a method would likely cause Su Zhenxing to faint from grief.

Of course, Jiang Tian didn't have time to think about that.

Without the three burdens, Jiang Tian felt as if he had become one with the water.

He plunged into the middle of Yanjiang.

The hope was slim, but not entirely hopeless.

Jiang Tian observed the current; the undercurrents were ever-changing, but ultimately, in the short term, they wouldn't be too different.

If Su Tong had jumped in after him, and from the same spot, she would most likely have been swept away by the same undercurrent that had carried Jiang Tian away.



Chapter 322 A Glimmer of Hope in Despair: He is a Great Hero


The entry for "God of the Ocean," a golden entry, while not entirely conceptual, allowed Jiang Tian to accurately analyze and determine the approximate location.

Jiang Tian's return journey took approximately two minutes.

In other words, Jiang Tian was swept up in the current for a full ten minutes, if he were in the same spot.

Without time to catch his breath, Jiang Tian plunged into the water, heading in the direction he had just emerged from.

That's right, Jiang Tian judged that, without any struggle, he would have been about two or three hundred meters ahead of the car, considering the speed of the current combined with the river's speed.

And considering Jiang Tian's chasing speed, the distance wouldn't exceed three hundred meters.

The moment Jiang Tian almost instantly plunged into the water, the leaders on the shore were stunned.

A hero.

This is a hero.

Put yourself in his shoes.

The first time was already fraught with danger, a miracle achieved through near-death experiences; would there be a second time?

Especially since Su Tong might have had a chance if she were still in the car, floating on the surface, but after going in, not a single bubble appeared.

Where could they find her in the vast Yanjiang River?

Especially given the perilous conditions of the Yanjiang River.

But Jiang Tian not only went down, he didn't even hesitate for a moment.

This…

Putting yourself in his shoes, who could do that?

Huai Sheng, who had just arrived, was deeply moved by this scene.

"Our Yun Province has produced a great hero," Huai Sheng said.

Indeed.

Jiang Tian's actions this time, unknowingly and thoroughly, sent a huge shockwave through the entire Yun Province, from top to bottom.

Everyone knew how dangerous the Yanjiang River was, but Jiang Tian went down anyway, and not only that, he went down a second time.

He created a miracle the first time, and the second time…

Okay, it doesn't matter if he couldn't be saved, but Jiang Tian's attitude shocked everyone.

The speed was so fast that even Elder Mu and Jiang Peng, who tried to stop him, didn't have time to reach out before Jiang Tian plunged into the water.

"This..." Jiang Peng said, his face full of worry.

"Little Jiang was able to drag three people ashore by himself. If he has no burdens, Yanjiang shouldn't pose any danger to him," Director Shen reassured him.

"However, the longer he stays in the water, the more terrifying the physical exhaustion becomes. Little Jiang just spent ten minutes diving at the bottom of the river before coming up, and he even rescued the three of us without resting at all," Elder Mu said gravely.

At this moment.

Secretary Cheng and Mu Feng, their faces ashen, saw the medical personnel finally arrive.

Upon seeing him, they immediately began first aid, performing CPR.

Fortunately.

Jiang Tian's treatment was timely, and the medical personnel arrived relatively quickly.

Both of them were vomiting water.

Mu Feng and Secretary Cheng gradually regained some breathing.

Everyone present breathed a sigh of relief.

After Jiang Tian entered the water, the others didn't rest either. Spotlights were shone on the river's surface, searching relentlessly.

They left no stone unturned.

But unfortunately, even with spotlights shining from both banks, there was still nothing suspicious on the water's surface.

Especially after the recent torrential rain, the murky river water made Jiang Tian's search and rescue operation infinitely more difficult.

Jiang Tian dove in, and half a minute later, he had reached the designated location.

It was about two hundred meters downstream from the car.

"If the judgment is right, it's right; if it's wrong, there's no chance left."

Jiang Tian's gaze sharpened.

A few minutes meant nothing to Jiang Tian.

However, for an ordinary person, it was enough to be fatal.

If the judgment was wrong, Jiang Tian's chances of finding the car would be slim. As time passed, the possible directions would increase, making the search wider and more difficult.

Most importantly, time was running out.

Or rather, Su Tong's time was running out.

Even if she were rescued, it would likely be a corpse.

Jiang Tian took a deep breath at the surface, then plunged into the water.

Just then, Jiang Tian felt a suction force from an undercurrent beneath his feet.

This left Jiang Tian with no resistance; drawn by the suction, he instantly sank to the bottom of the river.

That's why it's said.

Although the surface water was murky, it became increasingly dark the deeper he went, blurring his vision completely.

Jiang Tian could only rely on his senses to feel his way.

When Jiang Tian's hand suddenly touched something different, he felt a surge of joy.

At the same time.

Just as Jiang Tian touched it.

Lying at the bottom of the river, surrounded by darkness, Su Tong quietly awaited death.

She truly awaited death.

"One life for another, I don't owe him anything anymore, right?"

Su Tong lay at the bottom of the river, her face upward, not contorted in pain, her mouth slightly open. The mask she wore prevented her from choking on too much water initially, which actually gave Jiang Tian time to rescue her.

However, severe oxygen deprivation and the inability to breathe caused Su Tong's vision to become increasingly blurry.

Her consciousness grew increasingly faint.

A bitter smile gradually crept onto her lips.

She didn't know why she had been so foolish.

Foolish enough to believe Ye Xuan, to have misunderstood such a hero.

A recipient of multiple First-Class Merits!

"He must be a great hero."

"A great hero who saved so many people, it's just a pity he'll probably never know what he did."

Her consciousness grew increasingly blurred, and unexpectedly, Su Tong's mind drifted to Jiang Tian's resolute figure.

But the next moment.

Su Tong suddenly felt her arm being pulled.

Before Su Tong could even react, her mask had been pulled down.

This instantly brought Su Tong, who was already fading from consciousness, back to a slightly clearer state.

The next moment, a large amount of water was poured into her mouth.

Before Su Tong could react,

she felt her nose being suddenly pinched.

Su Tong's pupils contracted.

A ghost? Was a water ghost pinching her nose?

Before Su Tong could even think, a large amount of oxygen was poured into her mouth.

Su Tong, in her near-death despair, frantically tried to inhale the oxygen.

Good.

Jiang Tian breathed a sigh of relief.

There was a reaction.

Yes, she was alive.

Jiang Tian then quickly surfaced.

Actually, Jiang Tian had been feeling guilty just now, because many people drowned in the Yanjiang River every year, and countless bodies were never recovered. Over the years, who knew how many remained?

I heard there were two more suicide attempts by jumping into the water this spring.

What if I pull the wrong person out, or someone who's been dead for who knows how long, and then I have to give them oxygen? Ugh, Jiang Tian just couldn't bear to think about that.



Chapter 323 Safe Landing, A Journey to Heaven


As the frantic grip in his arms gradually subsided, Jiang Tian roughly understood that Su Tong had regained her senses.

When a normal person falls into the water, they become extremely frantic in the face of life and death.

Therefore, most water rescues actually occur from behind.

Su Tong may have initially faced death calmly, but in the face of imminent death, when a lifeline is lost, instinct kicks in, and she frantically searches for a way to survive.

And Jiang Tian wasn't wrong.

After taking several deep breaths of oxygen, Su Tong regained consciousness and her senses; the suffocating feeling from severe oxygen deprivation dissipated.

What followed was confusion.

Was she rescued?

Who saved her?

Isn't Yanjiang a place of certain death, a forbidden zone?

In this situation, who could have saved her?

She was rescued, but what about that hero?

But before she could think any further,

with a splash, Jiang Tian pulled Su Tong to the surface.

The next moment.

Su Tong finally saw the blurry scene before her.

On the dark river surface, Su Tong looked at the face so close to hers.

Looking at that familiar face.

Su Tong's mind went blank.

It was him.

Why him?

Su Tong's scalp tingled.

Why didn't he let her die?!

For a young girl with a fragile heart, a low tolerance for hardship, and an abundance of compassion.

Do you know how much guilt this would cause?

Do you know how tormenting this guilt is?

Yes, it will torment her for the rest of her life!

Insulting the hero had already tormented her to the point of wishing she were dead; misunderstanding the hero had made her want to pay with her life.

But now, the hero had saved her, disregarding past grievances.

Why did Heaven punish her like this?

Why did it make her do such a thing, and then have a hero save her?

Was she really going to let her live with guilt for the rest of her life, a life worse than death?

*Whoosh!*

Su Tong's face was ashen.

She didn't even know how to face Jiang Tian.

"Are you alright?" Jiang Tian asked.

At this moment, Jiang Tian was indeed full of energy, showing no signs of fatigue.

With the two golden attributes of Ocean God and Extraordinary Human, let alone saving a few people and crossing the Pacific Ocean, well, that was a bit difficult, but with enough food, Jiang Tian could actually do it.

"Thank you..."

Su Tong lowered her head, too ashamed to face Jiang Tian. She dared not look at his face; her face, as pale as paper, was now completely flushed.

The shame made her want to die.

She was already so ashamed she didn't want to live anymore, and now, good heavens, Jiang Tian had saved her again.

Su Tong exploded.

She exploded with shame.

Why not let her die?!

She really doesn't want to live anymore!

Of course, Jiang Tian didn't think that far ahead.

After all, the situation was urgent. They were currently in the middle of the river, not completely out of danger, especially in this area. Even though they were floating still, they were constantly being moved by the current, and downstream, there was a whirlpool.

It looked harmless, but Jiang Tian knew that while the whirlpool appeared small on the surface, it was getting bigger and bigger inside.

A piece of wood placed on it would be sucked in instantly.

This was also the most dangerous place.

Jiang Tian didn't care what Su Tong was thinking. He grabbed her with one arm and sped towards the shore at breakneck speed.

Su Tong, pressed against him, had her eyes closed.

She was too ashamed to face Jiang Tian.

She was even more ashamed to face anyone.

As for her recent transgression...

Su Tong knew the situation was urgent. Although she felt a pang of guilt—her body was gone—that pain vanished instantly in the face of overwhelming remorse.

Now, only one thing occupied her mind: how could she repay Jiang Tian's life-saving grace and explain her misunderstanding of him?

Of course.

As Jiang Tian surfaced,

and began to swim, the lights quickly focused on him.

Because after Jiang Tian entered the water, he had disappeared in a flash.

The hearts of those on the shore sank.

No way.

They couldn't save him, could Jiang Tian be dead too?

Thinking this, everyone's hearts sank even deeper.

They didn't know how long they waited, but for everyone, each day felt like an eternity.

Especially some of them, their mouths were now dry.

Until a spotlight swept across them.

Then everyone suddenly saw.

Jiang Tian was already four or five hundred meters away from them.

Even the spotlight could only make out a blurry shadow.

Su Zhenxing's heart sank.

He'd plunged hundreds of meters away in one go.

He must have encountered an undercurrent!

The level of danger was enough to make his scalp tingle.

"They're up!"

"Holy crap, they're up! They're up!"

"Two people, that's right."

"A miracle, this is a miracle!"

When a chorus of shocked exclamations rang out, Su Zhenxing finally saw clearly.

Especially the armed police, who immediately rushed towards the shore on Jiang Tian's side.

They held ropes, ready to rescue at any moment.

The medical staff, who had just managed to pull Secretary Cheng and Mu Feng out of danger, also hurriedly got up upon hearing the news.

Sometimes, fate is just that ingenious.

Liao Ying was wearing a white coat.

As the head doctor of the emergency department.

After Jiang Tian left, Liao Ying received a call to work overtime. A significant car accident had occurred on the outer ring expressway due to heavy rain obstructing visibility. Liao Ying didn't even have time to check on her younger brother, Liao Jiajun, before rushing back to the hospital.

But before she was even halfway through her shift,

she received another emergency call.

And it was a very important person. Liao Ying dropped her hospital work and rushed over.

She was very busy.

But that's how the emergency room is; Liao Ying was used to it.

However, what Liao Ying never expected was...

She had anticipated that the person would be very important, but she never imagined it would be this important.

How terrifying?

Nearly twenty leaders, exuding an air of oppression, stood on the riverbank.

Liao Ying didn't know who these leaders were.

But she knew that the person who had fallen into the water was definitely not an ordinary person.

"Quick, she's unconscious."

Jiang Tian struggled to drag Su Tong ashore.

To be honest, she was fine when they came out; Jiang Tian didn't know why Su Tong had fainted halfway there, and she hadn't drowned!

Seeing that the medical staff had arrived, Jiang Tian put Su Tong on the shore.

"Pfft, Team Leader Jiang?" Liao Ying was dumbfounded.

How could we run into each other like this?

"Director Liao?" Jiang Tian smiled slightly: "What a coincidence!"



Chapter 324 Enraged, yet still shameless, he brutally beat Ye Xuan.


"Let's save her first."

Jiang Tian's lips curled into a smile.

He had just remembered something—a news story from his past life, even if it was blurry.

But the word "drug dealer" still resonated with him.

Jiang Tian couldn't investigate the internal issues anymore, but he could roughly deduce what was going on.

Wiping his face, Jiang Tian watched as Su Tong was being carried into the ambulance.

He knew Su Tong was no longer in mortal danger.

Su Zhenxing, on the other hand, was profusely thanking Jiang Tian.

"This is my business card, Officer Jiang. I just learned that you're also in the police system. Whatever your needs are in the future, whatever you require of me, Su Zhenxing, just say the word, and I'll definitely help you."

Su Zhenxing handed Jiang Tian a business card, which Jiang Tian accepted with a slight nod, but something was swirling in his mind.

He had a premonition that he was getting closer and closer to another drug trafficking case.

"By the way, who was that man with Miss Su just now?" Jiang Tian asked.

Su Zhenxing's face instantly filled with anger. "Officer Jiang, that bastard is my daughter's former friend."

Su Zhenxing had just glanced at Su Tong and, finding her alright, had breathed a sigh of relief. However, even though he was worried about his daughter, Su Zhenxing hadn't forgotten his greatest benefactor.

This was the man who had saved his daughter.

To him, he was a savior.

Su Zhenxing wasn't an ungrateful person.

Mentioning Ye Xuan completely enraged Su Zhenxing. After understanding what had happened, he realized that this had nothing to do with Jiang Peng's family.

Because if Ye Xuan hadn't been malicious, none of this would have happened.

Jiang Tian, ​​having heard everything from Su Zhenxing, roughly understood the situation.

"So, in just a few years, he amassed a fortune of tens of millions?" Jiang Tian's brow twitched; he instantly sensed something was wrong.

Tens of millions.

In this era, it is indeed possible.

After all, in this primitive age, opportunities abound; seizing just one can easily lead to wealth.

However,

Don't overlook the crucial issue.

How could someone who just graduated high school, starting with capital and building a large empire in just a few years, be so reckless?

Of course, Jiang Tian doesn't have concrete evidence yet, but he roughly understands what happened.

He guesses that Su Tong was eventually won over by Ye Xuan through some unknown means, and then, for some unknown reason, Ye Xuan tried to drag Su Tong down by secretly injecting her with drugs.

And in the end, it was discovered and exposed.

Jiang Tian stroked his chin, deep in thought.

"Brother, are you alright?"

Just then, Jiang Xiaoyu rushed over, wailing, "Waaah, brother, I almost thought you were going to die here! If you died, what would I do? How could I live?"

"If your brother dies, no one will give you pocket money anymore," Jiang Tian couldn't help but roll his eyes.

However, at this moment, Jiang Tian's gaze fell on Mu Ya.

It has to be said...

Although he didn't gain any crime points today, the political gains for Jiang Tian were undoubtedly immeasurable.

Having accomplished this today,

in the future, not only domestically, but even in Yunnan Province, Jiang Tian could practically do whatever he wanted.

As long as he didn't go too far, Jiang Tian would face virtually no obstacles in the future; in fact, he might even rise to unimaginable heights.

Up to now.

A slight smile appeared on Jiang Tian's face.

The sense of urgency he felt after his rebirth.

Up to his current achievements, his accomplishments had forged Jiang Tian's invincible reputation.

But connections were the most important foundation Jiang Tian had gained.

Now, Jiang Tian truly possessed the capital to fight his way through within the system.

"Boss, what about that Ye Xuan?"

The crowd gradually dispersed, but without a doubt, they all shook hands with Jiang Tian very warmly before leaving.

Especially since the miracle Jiang Tian had created, many leaders were eager to meet him.

In this regard, it's safe to say that anyone can escape unforeseen circumstances in the future.

"Having more friends means having more options," they understood this better than anyone.

Jiang Tian greeted everyone with a smile.

After the leaders dispersed, even Director Shen accompanied Elder Mu away, Jiang Tian returned to the bridge.

Of course, Jiang Peng didn't leave. The salvage team arrived shortly afterward to retrieve the two sunken vehicles, while Jiang Peng, along with numerous armed police, remained to cordon off the scene.

Jiang Tian, ​​however, had already returned to Yanjiang Bridge with his men.

"Officer Jiang, I didn't know, I really didn't know it was you!"

At this moment, Ye Xuan, his hands cuffed, saw Jiang Tian and instantly broke free from the two armed police officers holding him, shouting repeatedly at Jiang Tian that it was a misunderstanding.

He was stunned.

Ye Xuan was absolutely freaked out!

He even wanted to slap himself. How could he have been so pathetic? Why did he say those things?

So close.

Just a little bit more.

Thinking about what might happen to Su Tong, Ye Xuan felt like killing himself.

But thankfully, she was alright.

Although Su Tong was frightened, thankfully she was unharmed. This made Ye Xuan incredibly grateful to Jiang Tian; thankfully, the kid was awesome.

"Let him come over," Jiang Tian waved his hand.

That's how it is.

With Ye Xuan's physique, trying to shake off two armed police officers was pure nonsense. The moment he broke free, the two officers were already grappling him from behind, almost making him faint from the pain.

Hearing Jiang Tian's words, the two officers released Ye Xuan.

And Ye Xuan didn't disappoint, instantly getting up and coming to Jiang Tian. However, he quickly noticed Mu Ya and Jiang Xiaoyu beside Jiang Tian and couldn't help but glance at them a few more times.

After all.

They were too beautiful.

Although Su Tong was also very beautiful, even neck and neck, everyone has their own unique charm.

“Officer Jiang, please unlock my handcuffs. Also, thank you for saving Su Tong, oh, the one who just jumped into the water. Don’t worry, I’ll definitely send your police station a banner.” Ye Xuan showed off his handcuffs with his hands behind his back.

Jiang Tian’s expression gradually turned cold.

Because from beginning to end, he hadn’t seen any remorse on Ye Xuan’s face, nor had he heard any apology.

“Officer Jiang, you're a policeman, you should have said so earlier! This is a misunderstanding. It's a misunderstanding, and thankfully Su Tong is alright. If something had happened to her, the entire Yunhai would be finished,” Ye Xuan kept saying.

Yes.

If something had happened to Su Tong…

It would have been a truly serious problem.

Don't underestimate Su Zhenxing just because he's a businessman. In the traditional social hierarchy of scholars, farmers, artisans, and merchants, the impact of reaching the pinnacle of any industry is unimaginable to many.

So Jiang Tian's lips curled into a smile, and he looked into Ye Xuan's eyes, saying, “Look at me.”

Ye Xuan, puzzled, slightly raised his head.

The next moment…

*Slap!*



Chapter 325 A dazzling red light, another great achievement?


A resounding slap echoed.

Just as Ye Xuan's eyes were filled with excitement and a touch of confusion, a sharp pain shot across his face.

Ye Xuan was dumbfounded.

Just moments ago, Ye Xuan had experienced the thrill of going from heaven to hell and back again.

Why?

It's a joke.

He's clinging to a powerful figure!

Because Ye Xuan never imagined that his childhood sweetheart was actually Su Zhenxing's long-lost only daughter. What did that mean? Her future inheritance of billions would be hers.

Although he himself had tens of millions in assets and seemed successful, he knew his own family's situation best. Ye Xuan understood the truth.

Moreover, with the passage of time and the country's rapid development, the Tianxing Group's assets would continue to expand at an alarming rate.

Ye Xuan couldn't even imagine the terrifying power and wealth the Tianxing Group would unleash in the future. But all of this would be his in the future. How could Ye Xuan not be excitedly and frantically want to cling to a powerful figure?

Especially after learning of Su Tong's return.

Ye Xuan shamelessly inquired everywhere for her contact information. Leveraging their childhood friendship and classmate status, he successfully gained Su Tong's trust and arranged a meeting.

With Ye Xuan's confidence, he was practically certain of success.

However, what happened tonight was full of twists and turns, but thankfully, the final outcome was good; Su Tong was rescued.

This meant he could continue his pursuit of Su Tong.

But he never expected this.

"You hit me?" Ye Xuan covered his face.

Thump!!!

Jiang Tian kicked Ye Xuan in the stomach, making him gag and vomit.

"Hitting you, hitting you is too lenient." Jiang Tian's face remained unchanged. He had never seen such a shameless person.

Jiang Tian hadn't forgotten a single word they said on the bridge. Did they really think he was the type to suffer injustice?

Su Tong used her life to atone for her mistake.

And Ye Xuan, the instigator.

"I just misunderstood you, and you...you even hit me! The misunderstanding is cleared up, how can you be so petty? As a police officer, I'm reporting you for using your position for personal revenge." Ye Xuan couldn't even clutch his stomach because his hands were locked behind his back. He could only kneel on the ground, his body prostrate, his voice trembling as he spoke, seemingly unable to believe it.

Petty?

Personal revenge?

Jiang Tian's smile grew even colder.

A gigolo, and such a shameless gigolo at that, most importantly, with ulterior motives.

"Report me? We'll talk about that after you get out of prison. Right now, you're under criminal detention for suspected disorderly conduct that caused serious consequences." Jiang Tian said expressionlessly.

If it weren't for Ye Xuan's instigation, Su Tong wouldn't have jumped. If she hadn't jumped, she wouldn't have faced such a near-death experience. If it weren't for Jiang Tian, ​​she might have died at the bottom of the river.

Because Ye Xuan's words of provocation and insults against heroes led to serious consequences and even extremely negative impacts, he fully met all the criteria for the maximum penalty.

Jiang Tian wasn't really settling a personal score.

Buzz!!!

Ye Xuan's mind exploded.

He never dreamed this would happen.

"Criminal detention?" Ye Xuan's eyes widened.

Having done business outside, he clearly understood what "criminal detention" meant.

"I'm going to report you! You're using your position for personal revenge! I'm going to report you! Let me go! I only said a few words of questioning, and you've already abused your power! I'm going to report you!" Ye Xuan struggled frantically and shouted.

No.

Suddenly, Ye Xuan realized something and remembered his relationship with Su Tong. His expression changed drastically: "Don't try to scare me. Su Tong and I are childhood friends. Do you dare use your position for personal revenge?"

That's right.
