He suddenly remembered that Su Zhenxing had specifically sought him out to thank him for taking care of Su Tong when they were children.

That's right.

Back when Su Tong was in school, Ye Xuan had been her devoted follower.

Of course, their relationship had always been good. Thinking of this, Ye Xuan straightened his back. A small-time policeman like him—would he dare offend Su Zhenxing?

Thinking this, Ye Xuan felt incredibly confident.

Jiang Tian almost burst out laughing.

Just now, down below, Su Zhenxing had been gritting his teeth, practically wanting to kill this guy.

He hadn't said anything in front of him earlier, probably because he was busy with Su Tong's affairs; otherwise, Su Zhenxing would have demanded the police take the maximum penalty.

Pleading for leniency?

What a joke.

Not getting anyone to give you a harsher punishment is already the greatest reward you can receive.

Because ultimately, this guy was the real culprit. If he hadn't had bad intentions, none of this would have happened. Of course, Jiang Tian was too lazy to argue about that.

He waved his hand, signaling two armed police officers to quickly take the guy to the city police station.

Jiang Tian suspected this guy might be involved in drug crimes, but there was no evidence now. However, he could look for it later. The evidence needed for drug crimes is different from that required for criminal offenses; the enforcement power for drug crimes is much greater.

"Too shameless!" Mu Ya said through gritted teeth.

She had never seen such a shameless person before—no, there was another one: Xiao Bo.

“I’ve never seen such a shameless bastard.” Jiang Xiaoyu nodded repeatedly.

“Aren’t you sure you shouldn’t take him to the hospital?” Mu Ya looked at Jiang Tian worriedly.

“No need, but you’d better go and take care of him.”

Jiang Tian looked at Mu Ya with a helpless expression.

He could already imagine Old Master Mu and Mu Feng in the hospital, with no one to take care of them, and even Secretary Cheng was unconscious.

“Zijin, go investigate Ye Xuan. I feel something’s off about him.”

Jiang Tian went to the car, called Li Zijin over, and took his police uniform from the trunk.

It was a newly issued uniform; after all, he had been promoted, receiving not only a medal but also a new uniform. Of course, the style hadn’t changed.

“I know.” Li Zijin’s spirits lifted.

Jiang Tian’s intuition and ability to find drug dealers were undeniable.

If Jiang Tian said there was a problem, then there definitely was.

"By the way, be careful during the investigation, don't expose yourself," Jiang Tian said, remembering something.

That's right.

Jiang Tian had just used his scanning radar and Judgment Eye to take a look; yes, it was the person he had hired.

A brilliant red glow.

After Li Zijin left, Jiang Tian returned to his car and changed into a clean police uniform.

As for Jiang Xiaoyu, Jiang Tian also asked Song Qiuwan to take her home.

That's right.

Since joining the police force, Song Qiuwan had been intentionally or unintentionally avoiding Jiang Tian.

Jiang Tian, ​​however, didn't seek trouble.

Sometimes, once a rift appears, it's there forever, and it may be difficult to mend it for a lifetime.

As far as Jiang Tian was concerned, he had a clear conscience.

"I'll get you home safely," Song Qiuwan said, driving away in Jiang Tian's car.

As for Jiang Tian himself, he naturally stayed behind.

After all, for Jiang Tian, ​​there was still one most important case to investigate: who did it.



Chapter 326 The Great Merit Has Arrived, Oh No, the Great Merit is Uncle Three?


In full view of everyone.

That was definitely not an accidental car accident. Who would crash their car and then frantically accelerate? Especially Jiang Tian, ​​who clearly saw the red glow emanating from the other party upon arrival. Without a doubt, it was a criminal.

And this matter...

Without a doubt...

was a major earthquake.

The murder of Elder Mu.

And a murder in public.

How serious was this?

Jiang Tian himself couldn't help but click his tongue in astonishment.

“According to the forensic investigation at the scene, the driver did not fall into a coma after the crash. In other words, this accident is basically confirmed as murder.”

Miss Qiu, with dead fish eyes, wore gloves and her eyes slightly narrowed.

“Are you sure?” Jiang Tian frowned.

“It's basically certain. This was an organized and premeditated act. The driver has completely lost vital signs, but it's clear that he struggled in the water before death. On the bridge, it can be confirmed that he was conscious.” Miss Qiu straightened her back and nodded very confidently.

Boom!!!

At this moment.

Jiang Peng, who had been standing there the whole time, couldn't help but gasp sharply.

Speculation was one thing.

But when it was finally confirmed, it was another matter entirely.

Even Jiang Tian couldn't quite believe it.

He knew that in his previous life, after Jiang Peng left his post, a bureau chief was parachuted in, full of enthusiasm for reform, only to die in a car accident a few days later.

And now.

Jiang Peng was fine now.

But the one who was in the car accident was Old Mu.

Hiss!!!

Jiang Tian's eyes flickered.

Clearly, as a top leader, there was no positive interaction between him and those at the bottom.

"Such a hasty murder proves one thing: Old Mu has taken away someone's interests, or rather, blocked someone's path," Jiang Tian frowned.

"If that's the case, I just remembered something,"

Jiang Peng turned and said.

The driver's identity has been investigated, and as expected, there's basically nothing to be found—a typical case of late-stage cancer.

He was bribed; you can't extract anything of value from a driver like that.

"What's the matter?" Jiang Tian asked.

Although this was the Criminal Investigation Division's job, Jiang Tian decided to follow up anyway.

"Old Mu recently wanted to carry out a comprehensive reform of the Yunnan Province police system, from top to bottom, even in conjunction with the Discipline Inspection Commission, deciding to conduct a thorough investigation," Jiang Peng said directly.

Okay.

Jiang Tian was basically certain.

Someone was getting restless, but what Jiang Tian found amusing was that the more they tried to cover something up, the more they revealed.

Most importantly, even if Old Mu was dealt with, didn't they know that if a new leader were parachuted in, this plan would definitely still be implemented?

"These people are simply insane," Jiang Tian shook his head.

"Haven't they always been like this? They've just been a little more discreet lately, but they couldn't hold back any longer," Jiang Peng said calmly.

What's this?

Jiang Peng had seen far more outrageous and arrogant things than this many times.

In fact, he had been threatened by criminal gangs more than once or twice.

Hearing Jiang Peng's words, Jiang Tian realized what was happening.

That's right.

The criminal underworld in Yunhai has never truly disappeared. Recently, perhaps due to the crackdown and Jiang Tian's extensive suppression, many criminal gangs seem to have vanished.

But this is merely a temporary respite; they've simply become more low-key.

They can't hold back any longer.

"Regardless, we're only responsible for investigating cases, not for anti-corruption," Jiang Peng said in a relatively good mood.

Thanks to Jiang Tian's excellent performance,

his promotion is practically guaranteed.

As for the case itself, Jiang Peng's expression suggested that once he's promoted, he'd leave it to the next bureau chief.

Jiang Tian didn't linger. After gaining a general understanding of the case, he found a car and returned home.

He'd leave this troublesome issue to Miss Qiu.

The next few days, unexpectedly, even to Jiang Tian's surprise, saw Yun Province appear relatively calm.

But Jiang Tian knew that a terrifying power struggle had likely begun at the top.

It was clear that Elder Mu was likely in the lead, as he was obviously in a good mood, having invited Jiang Tian to dinner several times, which Jiang Tian hadn't refused. His relationship with Mu Ya was also rapidly warming up.

Jiang Tian rested briefly. As for Ye Xuan, he was temporarily left out at the city police station, even having a private room prepared for him.

Of course, it wasn't that Jiang Tian didn't want to solve and investigate; another problem lay before him.

*Creak!!*

In a small, dilapidated house in a village within the city, Jiang Tian pushed open the half-ruined door. Entering, he couldn't help but wrinkle his nose at the musty stench emanating from the room.

After his physical enhancement, Jiang Tian's sense of smell and touch were now frighteningly acute.

But the more acute they were, the more torment he suffered.

The acrid smell in his nose made Jiang Tian's features contort.

"Team Leader."

"Boss."

Jiang Tian entered.

Lu Chun and Zhao Wei stood up.

"Any findings?" Jiang Tian asked.

"No, they didn't come," Lu Chun shook his head.

"Boss, we weren't tricked by those two bastards, were we?" Zhao Wei gritted his teeth.

Zhao Wei was incredibly resilient.

After spraining his ankle and resting for two days, he was completely recovered and eagerly requested to join the operation.

"The information can't be wrong. Since they confessed, there can't be any problems," Jiang Tian said in a deep voice.

That's right.

This was the information obtained after interrogating Liao Jiajun and Yan Yan, two drug users, and the person who sold them the drugs.

What surprised them was...

Their purchasing channel was online. After purchasing, they would send a delivery address, and the other party would find a way to deliver the drugs to their door.

Hiss!!!

When Jiang Tian found out, he was utterly astonished.

With a mind that's years ahead of Old Ma's, why choose to commit crimes?

And it's a business that will get you executed.

However, the delivery time is uncertain.

"Boss, we placed the order three days ago. Were we tricked?" Zhao Wei's face was ashen.

To avoid arousing suspicion, they'd been holed up in this stinking place for three days.

"They're here."

Jiang Tian suddenly spoke.

As Jiang Tian's words fell,

an electric scooter pulled up on the road outside, and a man got off.

"Target spotted." Li Zijin's voice suddenly came through the earpiece.

Sure enough.

The next moment, the man on the electric scooter took a lunchbox from the back seat, went to the door, and knocked.

But what he faced...

The door opened, and before Jiang Tian even needed to lift a finger, Zhao Wei and Lü Chun pounced.

They subdued him instantly, and Jiang Tian walked over and took off his helmet.

"Third Uncle?"



Chapter 327 No matter how you change, it's all just a thin veil.


The first thing that came into view was an honest, kind-looking man, pinned to the ground and looking somewhat flustered. Upon seeing Jiang Tian, ​​he buried his head even deeper in the ground.

But hiding was useless; Jiang Tian had seen him.

No.

What's going on?

Jiang Tian looked confused. Third Uncle.

That's right.

It was Jiang Tian's third uncle, no mistake, his biological third uncle.

Jiang Tian's father had three brothers, an older sister, and a younger sister, but they had all married far away, and apart from holidays, they rarely had any contact.

The only person Jiang Tian still held dear was this third uncle.

Unlike his eldest uncle, who was insatiable and ungrateful, his third uncle was an honest, simple farmer.

Even Jiang Peng had once offered to help his third uncle, but he had always refused.

Because of his third uncle's personality, their relationship had actually always been quite good.

The difference between him and his eldest uncle was like night and day.

But similarly, while his eldest uncle was deeply hurt and repaid Jiang Peng's kindness with enmity, he treated his third uncle utterly inhumanely.

He treated him like a poor relative.

This was precisely why Jiang Peng decided to distance himself.

In his past life, Jiang Peng remembered his third uncle's family as honest and law-abiding.

They only had one daughter, Jiang Tian's cousin, who was eighteen years old and had been admitted to a good university.

Of course, perhaps influenced by his eldest uncle, Jiang Tian didn't have a good impression of his third uncle's family in his past life; in fact, he felt extreme disgust.

They treated him like a poor relative, with a very poor attitude.

Even recently, before Jiang Tian's rebirth, when his cousin wanted to use his family to attend high school, Jiang Tian firmly refused.

But as the saying goes, fortunes change.

When Jiang Tian's family fell on hard times, his cousin was admitted to Yunhai University of Political Science and Law. Most importantly, she became a student of a highly respected professor at the university. That professor, however, experienced a resurgence in his career, entering politics and rising rapidly from the department level to the provincial level within a few years.

He had countless students, and his cousin's career skyrocketed along with him.

Before Jiang Tian's transmigration, his cousin already held a higher position than Xiao Bo.

Of course, Jiang Tian's success wasn't solely due to this connection.

Now, Jiang Tian no longer cared about such things. He had no shortage of connections, and most importantly, he had indeed been somewhat of a scoundrel in his previous life.

In a daze, Jiang Tian thought about many things, but his first instinct was to tell Lü Chun and the others to let go: "Third Uncle, what are you doing here? What happened?"

Jiang Tian helped his third uncle, Jiang Hao, to his feet.

Jiang Hao looked at Jiang Tian, ​​his face filled with fear and apprehension.

Jiang Tian sighed deeply, wishing he could slap himself. He had truly been a scoundrel in his past life; he shouldn't have associated with his eldest uncle's family, but his third uncle's family was genuinely good.

"Nephew, what are you doing here?" Jiang Hao asked, his face full of astonishment.

"Third Uncle, I'm apprehending a criminal. Who sent you to deliver this?"

As Jiang Tian spoke, he kept his scanning radar on, his eyes also fixed on his third uncle. Appearances can be deceiving.

In his past life, he had been somewhat of a scoundrel, but he couldn't be sure if his third uncle was truly guilty.

"At the intersection outside, they said they'd give me a hundred yuan for delivering a meal here, and I agreed without a second thought," Jiang Hao said.

[Jiang Hao]

[Sin Value: 10]

Jiang Tian's gaze sharpened.

That's right.

He has sin value.

But Jiang Tian didn't believe for a second that his honest-looking third uncle was really a drug dealer.

Moreover, sin value harvesting isn't really about ordinary crimes; it's mostly about drug dealers. Drug dealers generate the most sin value, a discovery Jiang Tian had made recently.

"Nephew, what... what happened?" Jiang Hao asked慌张地.

Jiang Tian stroked his chin. Clearly, this sin value appeared after his third uncle's operation, related to drug-related crimes. Whether he was innocent or not, Jiang Tian couldn't be sure; after all, ten sin value points wasn't insignificant.

On the other hand, Jiang Tian recently learned that his third uncle, though living in Yunhai as a caregiver for his cousin's studies, had been diligently working in a factory to earn money for her tuition.

"Third Uncle, do you know what this is?" Jiang Tian asked.

"I...I don't know, nephew, what's going on?" Jiang Hao shook his head frantically.

Jiang Tian squinted and opened the lunchbox inside the plastic bag. Inside, he saw small, transparent, sealed bags containing packets of white powder.

Jiang Tian roughly estimated it to be close to 500 grams.

Whoosh!!!

Jiang Hao's face turned pale.

"Third Uncle, do you know what this is? Fifty grams is enough for a death sentence," Jiang Tian said.

Jiang Hao slumped to the ground with a thud: "Nephew, I don't know, I really don't know!"

Even an old farmer, an honest and simple man, would know what this was.

Jiang Tian didn't frown deeply.

Because drug crimes are strictly controlled, even unknowingly transporting drugs for excessive rewards can be considered a crime.

“Third Uncle, are you confused?” Jiang Tian’s face was grim.

However, the law is impartial. Even though his third uncle was the pillar of the family, Jiang Tian couldn’t bend the law for personal gain at this moment.

“Nephew, I don’t know, I really don’t know! It’s that guy outside… Nephew, I know you’re a policeman, but I really don’t know anything about this. Your sister is waiting for me to earn money for her tuition! Catch those bastards!” Jiang Hao panicked completely.

Just as Jiang Tian was about to say something…

The next moment…

The scanning radar within a kilometer radius suddenly flashed red.

Jiang Tian had actually been using the scanning radar the whole time.

“It’s here.” Jiang Tian quickly stood up.

If they couldn’t find the person behind it all, even if his third uncle wasn’t implicated, he would definitely be severely punished in the end. But if they could catch the drug dealer behind it all and prove his third uncle’s innocence, although he would still be arrested, he could be released after a period of detention.

The next moment, Jiang Tian sped out of the house like a sports car.

Originally, Jiang Tian's plan was to take his team members on this mission to hone their skills.

However, now he had no choice but to act.

This rented house was located in a hutong (alleyway), a winding alleyway typical of urban villages, requiring four or five consecutive turns to reach the main road.

Jiang Tian didn't know the other party's criminal plan, but that didn't stop him from making the arrest.



Chapter 328 Organizational Meeting: Jiang Peng Gets a Promotion


Just a slight approach, and two red dots appeared on Jiang Tian's scanning radar.

But they only hovered on the outskirts, not approaching the alley.

Jiang Tian walked calmly along the main road. Seeing Jiang Tian appear, the two people walking side-by-side seemed to sense something and immediately turned to leave.

"Stop!" Jiang Tian shouted.

The couple, both young, appearing to be around twenty-seven or twenty-eight years old, didn't slow down but instead sped up.

Thump thump thump!!!

At this moment, Song Qiuwan and Chu Jing, who had been lying in wait behind Jiang Tian, ​​were incredibly fast, quickly catching up.

However, Jiang Tian raised his hand to stop them.

The next moment.

Bang!!!

Jiang Tian pulled out his pistol, shifted the pin, and fired a shot at them. "Take one more step, and you'll be shot."

The man and woman seemed not to hear him, continuing to run frantically, because there was an alley less than ten meters ahead of them.

"Looks like a big fish."

Jiang Tian sneered, then raised his hand and fired a shot into their legs.

However, Jiang Tian was skilled; a serious injury would hinder interrogation. He only shot flesh in the shin, posing no danger to their lives. A bandage wouldn't interfere with the interrogation.

"We almost arrested the wrong person. These drug dealers are too cunning." At this moment, Zhao Wei ran out, his face grim, complaining.

In the end, he hadn't been in the police force long enough, and hadn't been a narcotics officer long enough.

These drug dealers were all incredibly cunning; no normal person would suspect they had a backup plan.

A major success almost slipped away.

"Alright, arrest them all and send them back to the city station for interrogation," Jiang Tian said.

“That…that guy just now?” Zhao Wei cautiously looked at Jiang Tian.

After all, he had just learned that the man was Jiang Tian's third uncle. In Zhao Wei's view, he was just someone who had been unknowingly used, and releasing him now wouldn't matter; it wouldn't affect anything.

“Arrest him too,” Jiang Tian said impartially.

Jiang Tian couldn't release him now while he hadn't been cleared of suspicion. Most importantly, Jiang Peng had indeed broken the law.

“Yes, sir!!!”

Zhao Wei's whole body trembled, and he quickly saluted.

At that moment,

the light emanating from Jiang Tian, ​​refracted by the sun, was dazzling.

It was so radiant.

The admiration Zhao Wei felt was almost indescribable.

Jiang Tian, ​​however, paid no attention to what Zhao Wei was thinking, instead focusing on the case.

Clearly, this was no ordinary case.

Jiang Tian, ​​possessing the skills of a master detective, had already mentally constructed the entire case. Was his third uncle truly innocent? According to the king of detectives' analysis, he was nine-tenths innocent, merely being used.

However, a normal small-scale drug crime wouldn't involve such meticulous, repeated probing.

Clearly, this only occurs when something reaches a significant scale.

This case couldn't be small.

Lost in thought, Jiang Tian detained his third uncle, watching the vehicle slowly enter the city police station.

Beside Jiang Tian, ​​Jiang Hao's face grew increasingly pale.

For an honest, simple man, entering this place and about to face trial was a terrifying prospect beyond words.

“Uncle, don’t be nervous. I know you’re innocent. Don’t worry, you won’t be here long; you’ll be out soon,” Jiang Tian comforted him.

“Nephew, Uncle… Uncle…” Jiang Hao’s voice trembled, already breaking into tears, “Uncle, I don’t know what I did wrong, but… it won’t affect your sister, will it?”

Yes.

A little girl with pigtails, staring at him curiously with wide eyes, appeared in Jiang Tian’s mind.

That was a memory from when Jiang Tian was very young, returning to his hometown.

Thinking back, it had been many years since he’d seen her.

While thinking about this, Jiang Tian’s phone rang. He took it out and saw that it was Jiang Peng.

Jiang Tian glanced at Jiang Hao; Jiang Hao had clearly seen the caller ID, and his eyes flashed with pleading. Jiang Tian sighed. As the bureau chief, Jiang Peng couldn’t hide it from him any longer; he would find out sooner or later.

"Dad," Jiang Tian said.

"Son, I have good news for you. The Organization Department has spoken with me, and it's basically confirmed that I'll be appointed as the Secretary of the Political and Legal Affairs Commission in Yunhai." Jiang Peng said happily.

Jiang Tian's eyes also lit up.

His heart began to pound uncontrollably.

Secretary of the Political and Legal Affairs Commission.

The Secretary of the Political and Legal Affairs Commission in Yunhai.

For Jiang Peng, this was a terrifying leap forward. He was already, without exaggeration, the third-highest-ranking official in Yunhai.

If things had proceeded as planned, Jiang Peng, now forty-seven, would very likely have risen to the position of vice-provincial governor before retirement.

Think about what Jiang Peng's fate was in his previous life?

At this time, he was drowning his sorrows in alcohol, his hair already white overnight, desperately seeking connections and favors, even being treated like a plague and avoided at all costs, all to rescue his mother who had been placed under investigation.

Oh no, even his father, Jiang Peng, had only just been released from investigation, tortured beyond recognition by Jing Feifan.

Jiang Tian, ​​because of Wang Yifei, was living a life worse than death.

Now?

Everything has changed. Jiang Peng not only didn't step down, but he's actually been promoted.

"Great news! Congratulations!" Jiang Tian said.

"Why do you sound a little unhappy?" Jiang Peng asked, somewhat confused.

"Happy, very happy! Dad, you must have misheard me," Jiang Tian quickly explained.

Jiang Peng didn't delve into it and continued, "Your mother told me everything that happened yesterday. Sigh, your uncle is just like that, there's nothing we can do. Since he's been promoted, I'm planning to invite your third uncle to dinner. Your cousin is a senior in high school this year; you two might not even recognize each other anymore, so it'll be good for you to get to know each other better."

Hiss!!!

Jiang Tian looked at his third uncle beside him and couldn't help but gasp.

“What’s wrong, son? We can’t be like your uncle, forgetting our roots. Your third uncle is an honest man; he’s never asked us for anything, and we’ve never helped him. Don’t let your uncle influence you to think of us as poor relatives.” Jiang Peng thought Jiang Tian was unwilling, or that, like before, his tone immediately became more forceful.

“Don’t worry, Dad, he’s my beloved uncle. How could I possibly have any prejudice against him?” Jiang Tian quickly retorted.

"Good, good, son, you've really matured. Hurry up and finish the case you're handling. Call your third uncle tonight." Jiang Peng hung up the phone happily.

Jiang Tian, ​​in the car, fell silent. Suddenly, something occurred to him: Old Master Mu's accident, followed by his third uncle's coincidences—had he been framed?

Thinking this, Jiang Tian's heart sank, and a cold glint flashed in his eyes.



Chapter 329 Poor cousin, your brother is here to make it up to you.


Of course, Jiang Peng couldn't make a call now, because Jiang Hao's phone was switched off.

Meanwhile, in the city bureau office, Jiang Peng, holding his phone, smiled broadly.

"Good, good, good."

You see, back when Jiang Tian's cousin, Jiang Peng's niece, had just been admitted to Yunhai's top high school, Jiang Hao brought a lot of things, humbly requesting to stay temporarily because the dormitories at the top high school were being renovated and there was no accommodation available for this year's students.

As a result, Jiang Tian, ​​influenced by Jiang Yong, thought it was a poor relative visiting and threw the things out, enraging Jiang Peng.

Afterwards, the third brother moved to the city to accompany her, and he stayed for almost three years.

And after that day, there was almost no contact for three years.

Also, on that day, Jiang Tian suffered his first beating.

He still has some psychological trauma from it.

Now, looking back, he has indeed grown up.

Little Jiang has grown up, and most importantly, he hasn't been influenced by Jiang Yong.

Severing ties with Jiang Yong was fine, but Jiang Peng still felt he had wronged his third brother financially.

Because the family was poor, there were three brothers, and only Jiang Yong and Jiang Peng went to school.

When the third brother wanted to study, the family had no money left. To this day, he still felt he had let his brother down.

Being able to make amends naturally filled Jiang Peng with immense joy.

As Jiang Peng stretched and walked to the office building entrance, breathing in the fresh air, his heart was filled with emotion.

Because he was about to leave this place.

His family was gradually getting better, and he, Jiang Peng, would take another significant step forward in his career.

Just then, Jiang Tian approached with his men.

Jiang Peng, initially pleased, raised his hand to greet them, but quickly his smile froze as he looked at Jiang Hao behind Jiang Tian, ​​and the handcuffs in Jiang Hao's hands.

Jiang Peng's face gradually darkened.

His whole body trembled uncontrollably.

"You little brat, I admit you're capable, you're talented, but if you don't want to contact your third uncle, you don't have to use this method. I knew it, you heartless bastard!" Jiang Peng's head exploded.

He couldn't help it; this was his brother he owed!

For so many years, he had never made up for anything, and now, he was being framed by his own son.

As for Jiang Hao committing a crime, how could that be? Jiang Peng refused to believe that his honest and simple third brother would do such a thing.

His blood boiled, and Jiang Peng charged forward.

But the next moment, Zhao Wei, who knew the whole story, rushed forward: "Uncle, calm down."

Jiang Peng took a deep breath, realizing he had lost his temper, and said to Jiang Tian grimly, "What did your third uncle do that you arrested him for?"

Zhao Wei took out some heroin: "It's this, over 500 grams of heroin, all given to us by this uncle at the arrest scene."

Uh!!!

Jiang Peng looked astonished.

Actually, it wasn't that Jiang Peng was overthinking things.

Jiang Tian was in charge of the most dangerous and heinous crimes within the entire criminal organization: drug dealers.

Therefore, the people Jiang Tian arrested were basically the most vicious drug dealers.

No matter how you look at it, Jiang Hao couldn't possibly be connected to Jiang Tian. Aside from settling a personal score, which was why Jiang Peng was initially furious, it was also a way to mask his inner panic and shock.

Because no matter how he thought about it, he didn't know what he would do if Jiang Hao committed a crime.

Hearing this, Jiang Peng's face turned pale. Five hundred grams—enough to be executed ten times over. Even as the bureau chief, he couldn't do anything.

Third brother, you're confused!

"Of course, this uncle didn't know anything. Those people disguised heroin as a lunchbox and gave you a couple hundred yuan as a favor, asking you to deliver it," Zhao Wei continued.

Bang!!!

Jiang Peng slapped Zhao Wei on the head, his face full of shock and anger. "Can't you finish your sentence? Don't you know that leaving things unsaid can be terrifying?"

Jiang Peng clutched his chest; damn it, he was about to have a heart attack.

Without that last sentence, Jiang Hao would have been executed ten times over. But the next sentence meant nothing.

The difference between the two.

Jiang Peng hadn't killed Zhao Wei; it was only because Jiang Peng had recovered.

“Take them in,” Jiang Tian finally spoke, having remained silent until now.

“Yes, sir!!!” Zhao Wei, clutching his head, hurriedly led the men inside.

Ultimately, Song Qiuwan and her team weren't professional anti-drug squads. In fact, the most experienced member was Li Zijin.

After they left,

Jiang Tian and Jiang Peng arrived at the office.

This wasn't Jiang Tian's first time here. As the bureau chief's office, it was about 100 square meters. It wasn't that it couldn't be too big, but there were specific standards.

A little more or less wouldn't do.

But the view was excellent, with large floor-to-ceiling windows offering a view of the high-rise buildings under construction everywhere outside.

In the early stages of this rapid development, Yunhai had undoubtedly caught up with the times.

But without Jiang Tian's appearance, Gao Xiaolong's sacrifice, and the subsequent unexpected death of the bureau chief, Yunhai wouldn't have stagnated for over a decade.

"What exactly happened?" Jiang Peng asked, frowning.

Jiang Tian shook his head at his father. "I feel something's not right."

The case of Elder Mu is currently being handled by the criminal investigation team, with the disciplinary committee also involved. A predictable political earthquake is unfolding.

But what if the direction is wrong?

Jiang Tian couldn't help but suspect that Elder Mu had encountered problems after having dinner with him, and today his third uncle was involved in the case.

Behind all this, Jiang Tian was now unsure whether anyone was involved.

"Son, you know your third uncle. He wouldn't do such things. Whether he was being used or it was just a coincidence, we must investigate thoroughly." Jiang Peng's expression grew serious.

Jiang Tian nodded.

“Besides, you know about your third aunt’s accident years ago. Your third uncle is working while also earning tuition for your cousin and renting a place to take care of her. He definitely can’t leave temporarily, so it would be best if your cousin were brought home to be cared for,” Jiang Peng said in a consultative tone.

There was no other way.

The child is grown up.

Compared to Jiang Tian’s achievements, Jiang Peng understood that his own promotion was also due to his son.

“The bureau has a lot of cases, so you rarely get to go home. High school is demanding; you don't get out until after 10 PM and leave again at 5 or 6 AM, which clashes with your schedule. Except for Saturdays and Sundays, you two rarely see each other,” Jiang Peng explained quickly, seemingly afraid Jiang Tian would refuse.

This left Jiang Tian with a somewhat helpless expression.

He hadn't said he disagreed!

Besides, this incident meant one thing: his third uncle might be being targeted by forces wanting to harm Jiang Tian.

Don't think they're overthinking it; Jiang Tian's actions have offended far too many criminal groups, and those people are capable of anything.

If his cousin were alone, being targeted would be unimaginable.

“Dad, you underestimate her. Let her stay in my room,” Jiang Tian said directly.

“No need, your sister's room is fine,” Jiang Peng said hastily.

“It’s alright, I don’t go back often either. I often work overtime late into the night, and there’s a sofa when I do go back.” Jiang Tian’s family lives in a three-bedroom apartment, and he doesn’t go back frequently. Besides, Jiang Tian actually feels a little guilty towards his cousin.

A smile finally appeared on Jiang Peng’s face.

But the next moment, Jiang Peng’s landline rang. Jiang Peng picked it up immediately, and his smile vanished after a few moments.

After hanging up, Jiang Peng looked at Jiang Tian and slowly said:

“Your uncle has been placed under investigation.”



Chapter 330 The mastermind behind it all, wants to mess with me?


"Shuanggui?"

Jiang Tian's expression froze completely.

This forced Jiang Tian to make connections. Everything seemed unrelated, yet they had all happened so coincidentally within a few days.

Even Jiang Tian himself wouldn't believe there wasn't something fishy going on.

"Where did you get this information? Are you sure?" Jiang Tian asked.

Jiang Peng, seeing Jiang Tian's calm demeanor, couldn't help but nod slightly, a relieved smile appearing on his face.

Jiang Peng was self-aware; he knew his own abilities and was simply not cut out to be a leader.

In fact, with his abilities, at best he could be a secretary in the political and legal affairs department in Yunhai.

If he were to engage in a real, bloodless power struggle within the system, he would be played to death without even realizing it. For example, taking on this so-called bureau chief position was a trap from the start, a huge trap.

If he did things normally, the higher-ups wouldn't be satisfied and would take action, but there would be no leads. Even if he did find some leads, they would be from criminal groups targeting him.

And if he just lay low, he was even worse off.

A high-ranking official came down directly from above and started targeting you.

Many people understood the situation in Yunhai. That's why Jing Feifan, who initially had a strong chance of becoming the Yunhai Bureau Chief, ultimately gave it up – for this very reason.

Jiang Tian, ​​on the other hand, was pushed into the position. If it weren't for Jiang Tian, ​​considering his tragic end in his previous life, it's clear this wasn't a position for just anyone. Without sufficient ability, the final outcome would be bleak.

Jiang Peng also understood his own limitations. The position of Secretary of the Political and Legal Affairs Commission in Yunhai might be the starting point of his career, with the potential for further advancement.

However, his abilities also limited him. Without the corresponding skills, reaching a high position wasn't necessarily a good thing.

Jiang Peng also understood that without Jiang Tian, ​​he wouldn't be able to take another step forward, let alone cross that crucial threshold in his life; he might not even be able to keep his position as Bureau Chief.

And what he needed to do after taking the position was simple: protect and support Jiang Tian.

The true protagonist, the future, is Jiang Tian.

Witnessing his son's growth step by step, Jiang Peng felt a mix of joy and complexity. Compared to Jiang Tian's calm demeanor, his own composure was even inferior.

"I've offended far too many people during this time. As the saying goes, 'the tallest tree catches the most wind.' While I've gained a lot, I've also taken on corresponding risks. Many people are watching us, openly and secretly. Your promotion has likely displeased many, both overtly and covertly. So, they're getting restless and have made their move." Jiang Tian's lips curled into a cold smile.

Hiss!!!

Jiang Peng gasped. "Do you know who it is?"

"No idea yet." Jiang Tian shook his head.

"You mean, this is targeting you?" Jiang Peng was startled.

"I didn't say that, it's just a guess." Jiang Tian shook his head.

But it was too much of a coincidence. His third uncle and eldest uncle had both met with misfortune on the same day. Although, for Jiang Tian, ​​his eldest uncle's misfortune was actually a welcome development.

Especially since even Elder Mu was being murdered.

“There’s something else I need to tell you. Regarding Elder Mu’s case, a joint investigation team has been formed, comprised of personnel from the provincial and municipal criminal investigation divisions, as well as investigators from the Kyoto headquarters. We expect to have leads soon,” Jiang Peng said.

Jiang Tian had known about this for a while, but he hadn’t expected the Kyoto team to get involved.

Actually, Jiang Tian hadn’t found any leads in Elder Mu’s case either.

However, if he were to investigate, Jiang Tian was confident he could solve the case within a few days. Unfortunately, as a member of the anti-drug squad, his superiors didn’t trust his investigative abilities and hadn’t allowed him to participate.

However, Jiang Tian currently had many cases on his plate and didn’t have the time to get involved.

“Tell me in detail, what’s wrong with Uncle?” Jiang Tian hadn’t expected that his uncle, who had been so sarcastic and cynical towards him just a few days ago, would suddenly fall from grace.

Now, Jiang Tian mainly wanted to know who had done it.

“It was the Discipline Inspection Commission of Zhen City next door that took action. I heard the Zhen City Party Committee received an anonymous tip and detained your uncle overnight,” Jiang Peng sighed.

Jiang Tian frowned. He suddenly thought of Secretary Cheng.

He had indeed asked Secretary Cheng for help, and she had directly taken Jiang Zihao away.

“Old Mu personally called to explain. It's just children fighting; it shouldn't affect adults. It has nothing to do with Secretary Cheng. By the time the province found out, your uncle had already been placed under investigation,” Jiang Peng said.

“Was that Old Mu’s call just now?”

Jiang Tian’s heart skipped a beat. According to the current intelligence, this was clearly premeditated.

If his uncle had really committed a crime, he deserved to die.

However, the way he was being targeted still made Jiang Tian feel uneasy.

Clearly, Old Mu was also afraid they would misunderstand.

But…

Jiang Tian frowned deeply. Jiang Yong was clearly a key leader in the county, yet at this crucial moment…

Just then, Jiang Tian suddenly felt a chill.

He realized that if he released his third uncle,

he would immediately face terrifying pressure. No, not only himself, but the enemy might even target Jiang Peng.

Jiang Tian had thought of this, and Jiang Peng obviously had too.

Of course, Jiang Tian himself wouldn't face any problems now, at most he'd have to reflect on his actions, but Jiang Peng would be implicated, possibly even eliminated because of this.

And indeed.

Jiang Tian's heart pounded. Within the body, once you reach a certain level, every step you take becomes a terrifying trap.

But…

Jiang Tian's expression gradually turned cold. Those people probably hadn't expected that he had arrested the very person behind his third uncle's actions.

"Investigate your third uncle's case thoroughly. As for your eldest uncle, the evidence is complete; there's no way he can overturn it," Jiang Peng said, rubbing his temples.

This problem arose at a crucial juncture in his promotion. If it weren't already a done deal, Jiang Peng might have been implicated because of his elder brother's issues.

There were even whispers of this happening at the provincial level.

But unfortunately, Elder Mu wielded immense power over leadership appointments in the political and legal system.

Jiang Tian pondered this as he headed towards the interrogation room.

He vaguely sensed that an invisible net was about to engulf them.

However…

Jiang Tian's expression grew increasingly cold. Since those people were plotting something, then Jiang Tian would completely tear apart their shattered network and confront them head-on.

"A criminal organization, huh?"

Jiang Tian's heart turned icy.

Countless precedents had shown one thing: being targeted by a criminal organization meant no good end.

The criminal organization's target might even have already set its sights on his cousin.

Then Jiang Tian truly didn't mind a head-on confrontation to see who was tougher.

Click!!!

The interrogation room door was pushed open. The man and woman, both around twenty-seven or twenty-eight years old, were being held in a separate interrogation room and interrogated alone. The woman was being interrogated by Li Zijin and Song Qiuwan.

It must be said that Song Qiuwan's abilities were indeed remarkable.

Psychology, interrogation skills, and even her own physical abilities—she was practically an all-rounder. This was clearly not something that could be achieved overnight. In other words, Song Qiuwan had likely been exposed to these things from a young age.

It's laughable that Jiang Tian only just realized this now.

If Song Qiuwan weren't so capable, she wouldn't have needed that department to recruit her personally.

"How's the interrogation going?"



Chapter 331 You've gone mad! You're such a good guesser, are you the king of guessing games?


“Wen Qing, female, 28 years old, a local of Yunhai. She was previously detained for participating in a group fight. She has no family. According to her, she was an orphan who grew up in an orphanage. Later, she was taken in by adoptive parents, but they died in an accident before she reached adulthood. Now she has no relatives. That man is her boyfriend.”

Jiang Tian held the woman's simple information, the information he had obtained through interrogation.

It was somewhat valuable; at least he had basic information, including her past experiences in the orphanage and her adoptive parents' upbringing. These could be crucial clues at a critical moment.

“That's all we can get from her so far. She's unwilling to say anything else,” Li Zijin complained.

Jiang Tian looked up and saw the woman, presumably Wen Qing, holding a cigarette and puffing away.

“Who gave her that?” Jiang Tian asked.

“She came in saying she wouldn't say a word if she wasn't given a cigarette. After being given one, this is all she's said,” Li Zijin explained.

Jiang Tian asked, "Have you investigated?"

"Yes, I have. They didn't lie, but they denied everything else, especially the drug trafficking allegation," Li Zijin said, her face full of worry.

Jiang Tian understood. That's why they say the pressure of handling cases is immense. Many veteran officers are heavy smokers, especially in high-pressure departments.

If criminal investigators are all heavy smokers, imagine the even greater and more terrifying pressure faced by drug enforcement officers.

Investigating a case requires meticulous logical deduction, piecing together countless details, and outsmarting all sorts of criminals. Many veteran officers aren't even thirty yet, but their hair is already half-gray.

Even the innate talent of the "King of Criminal Investigation" requires enough detail to deduce the true answer, but that was in the past. For Jiang Tian, ​​that was simply not an option.

The next moment, Jiang Tian's eyes flashed, a faint blue light flickering within them.

[The Eye of Judgment]

[Name: Wen Liu, Wen Qing]

[Sin Value: 1020]

[Sin Value sufficient. Clue provided: On January 8th, 2003, an innocent civilian was murdered at the Jiang residence. The body was hidden in a concrete pillar of a bridge foundation in the XX area. The reason was that an innocent person accidentally stumbled upon her transaction, and she had been involved in multiple drug trafficking transactions.]

Wen Liu?

Jiang Tian's gaze suddenly fell on the woman.

Clearly, was the woman lying? No, she probably didn't. Perhaps Wen Qing was her usual name when she was in the underworld.

But Wen Liu—was it a former name, or did it have a special meaning? Did she have any siblings? If not, and was an orphan, this was a serious issue; she might have been mass-trained.

Thinking this, Jiang Tian's heart stirred.

He then walked up, leaned against the interrogation table, and met Wen Qing's gaze.

“Young man, you’re so close, you’ll scare her.” Wen Qing gave a seductive smile, then took a deep drag of her cigarette and blew it towards Jiang Tian’s face. Smoke swirled around him, but Jiang Tian remained unmoved.

“Little Six, I don’t see any fear in your eyes,” Jiang Tian sneered.

However, he was also relieved. After all, in a normal investigation, without catching her in the act, if the woman could withstand all interrogation and remain silent, Jiang Tian really wouldn’t be able to handle her.

At that point, he might even be caught red-handed by those watching him.

It wasn't until now that things had really gotten to this point.

Jiang Tian finally felt the pressure.

Within the Municipal Public Security Bureau, there were no longer any problems; everyone was united.

However, when investigating cases, being constantly watched and subjected to underhanded tactics is incredibly difficult to defend against. Even Jiang Tian felt himself sinking into a quagmire.

Of course, Jiang Tian himself wasn't particularly afraid, but when had he ever suffered such humiliation at being schemed against?

“Little Six, what Little Six?” Wen Qing asked, confused.

But Jiang Tian stared intently at Wen Qing's expression, a cold smile playing on his lips. With the title of “King of Criminal Investigation” attached to her, panic clearly flickered in Wen Qing's bewildered eyes.

"Looks like I guessed right, Little Six, oh no, your surname is correct, so you should be called Wen Liu," Jiang Tian said calmly.

Since she'd been tricked, Jiang Tian might as well flip the table.

Jiang Tian's words instantly caused Wen Qing's pupils to contract.

Not to mention Jiang Tian's keen observation, even Song Qiuwan noticed something amiss.

"I don't know what you're talking about. What Wen Liu? My name is Wen Qing. Handsome young man, you've got the wrong person," Wen Qing forced a smile, trying to maintain composure.

But unfortunately, Jiang Tian didn't answer Wen Qing. Instead, he continued, "Let me guess again. An orphan obviously wouldn't be named with a number, because how could an orphan have siblings? No siblings, yet you're the sixth child, and called Wen Liu—your adoptive parents died unexpectedly shortly after adopting you. Being called Wen Liu proves that you were secretly adopted by a powerful force."

Buzz!!!

Wen Qing stared wide-eyed at Jiang Tian, ​​feeling her pupils contract continuously.

A ghost.

"Is this person a ghost?"

Unfortunately, her reaction still didn't attract Jiang Tian's attention. Jiang Tian still looked at her calmly and smiled faintly, "Let me guess again."

"Stop guessing."

"I told you not to guess."

"Stop guessing."

Wen Qing's emotions instantly surged; she went crazy.

Wen Qing was really going crazy.

It's poisonous.

This man is definitely poisonous.

It's terrifying.

Are you even guessing?

How can you guess so well?

Are you the king of guessing games?

Inside the interrogation room, Song Qiuwan and Li Zijin, who had tried many methods but obtained almost no useful information, were completely dumbfounded.

Watching Jiang Tian drive the criminal crazy with this guessing game, they felt as if they had encountered a new world.

Even as Wen Qing went wild, she couldn't sway Jiang Tian. His voice remained calm as he said, "You're crazy. Let me guess again. You've probably been right. Based on this, everything I said before was correct. You were adopted by a powerful force. Why were you adopted? Obviously not for legitimate reasons. Considering today's case, that powerful force must be a major drug lord. You were under his control, he trained you, treated you like a suicide squad. Therefore, you've definitely committed more than one crime. I even suspect you've killed someone in the last month or two."

*Buzz!*

Wen Qing stared blankly at Jiang Tian.

The two women in the interrogation chairs burst out laughing, spitting out their water. They stared at Jiang Tian as if he were a ghost.

The criminal hadn't said anything, only a name. And you guessed everything?

You're only known for your outrageous criminal arrests; this guessing game is terrifying.

"You're shocked, which proves my guess was correct. Where is it, Wenhe Road?"

Wen Qing's expression relaxed.



Chapter 332 The First Person to Solve Cases by Guessing, Leader Huai is Shocked


The next moment, Jiang Tian shook his head: "Obviously not. In the city, sophisticated methods of disposing of bodies, besides tying them to stones and throwing them into water, involve burying them in cement. Let me think about the recent construction sites. The Jiang residence recently had an overpass built; that's a perfect place to store a body. Your expression changed; it seems I guessed right. But let me guess why you went there. Judging from today's events, an innocent passerby must have witnessed your transaction. So, in the last month or two, that is, January or February, you killed someone in the Jiang residence and conducted a transaction."

Wen Qing stared blankly at Jiang Tian, ​​her face completely dazed.

Is this a devil?

Jiang Tian turned to Li Zijin and said, "Take some men to the overpass at the Jiang residence; there should be a sealed body there."

Li Zijin was bewildered and somewhat skeptical, but given her trust in Jiang Tian, ​​she naturally wouldn't question him and immediately arranged for people to go and examine the body.

At this moment, Wen Qing had completely bowed her head.

She suspected this man was a devil, a devil with mind-reading abilities, so she decided to prevent him from seeing her expression and eyes. Unfortunately, the devil's voice continued to echo in her ears without ceasing.

“Let’s continue guessing the powerful force behind you,” Jiang Tian continued.

Pfft!!!

Song Qiuwan spat out her drink.

Because, at this moment, Wen Qing was clearly becoming agitated, her tone pleading: “Stop guessing, I beg you, please stop guessing, I beg you.”

“I’ll make a bold guess. Giving you the surname Wen means that her power has the surname Wen, at least someone important in training you. And the murder at the Jiang residence clearly indicates that that power is connected to the Jiang family. Therefore, I further boldly speculate that President Wen of the Wenye Group is the mastermind.” Jiang Tian concluded with his final deduction.

With the King of Criminal Investigation and Jiang Tian’s case hints, which were already so obvious, if Jiang Tian still couldn’t deduce it, he might as well just kill himself.

Wen Qing's expression, as expected, was completely blank at this moment.

Her eyes were lifeless.

Jiang Tian shook his head regretfully. This woman wasn't young anymore, and despite her special training from a young age, her mental fortitude was so weak; she couldn't handle even a small glimpse of his strength.

Actually, Jiang Tian didn't know.

It wasn't just Wen Qing, the person directly involved, who couldn't handle it; even as an observer, Song Qiuwan watched helplessly as Jiang Tian gradually drove Wen Qing to the brink of collapse.

The King of Guessing?

So good at guessing, did he start guessing from the womb?

Song Qiuwan thought to herself, feeling it was all absurd.

A name.

You bastard, you didn't even need interrogation, you directly guessed the crime, the forces behind it, and even the time of the crime?

Is this some kind of myth?

Yes.

Not only were Li Zijin and Song Qiuwan completely bewildered, but even the two elderly people silently watching in the small dark room next door were equally dumbfounded.

"Provincial Governor Huai, Xiao Jiang doesn't usually solve cases this ridiculously," Gao Xiaolong explained, his lips twitching.

Huai Sheng nodded, equally bewildered.

While this method of solving cases was somewhat novel, it was absurdly far-fetched—using a small piece of information from interrogating a criminal to guide the emergence of more questions and ultimately solve the problem.

But all of this hinges on the interrogated prisoner voluntarily revealing information.

Gao Xiaolong, despite his extensive experience, had never seen anyone guess everything from just a name.

If this were true...

Gao Xiaolong decided he'd eat shit while doing a handstand.

Sure enough, after Gao Xiaolong defended Jiang Tian, ​​Huai Sheng's gloomy expression gradually relaxed as he spoke: "I already told you, we received a report accusing Comrade Jiang of protecting relatives and friends, maliciously framing innocent people, and killing innocent people to claim credit. Moreover, this report appeared in my house. I naturally don't believe such petty slander, but some people in the province might. They wanted to see how the case progressed, but unexpectedly, Jiang solved the case in such a unique way."

Huai Sheng forced a smile towards the end.

His eyes inevitably revealed disappointment, immense disappointment.

The supposedly legendary Jiang Tian turned out to be nothing more than a charlatan. If his sole purpose was to swindle the drug dealer, there was no need to go to such lengths with such a ridiculous and detailed scheme.

How should I put it?

Was the criminal deliberately playing a joke on Jiang Tian, ​​teasing a fool?

Huai Sheng had serious doubts.

"However, the interval between the two events was less than ten minutes. If I said this wasn't meticulously planned, I wouldn't believe it myself. Those people are too arrogant," Huai Sheng sighed, his face grim.

Originally, Huai Sheng came here to discuss some issues with Jiang Tian.

Yunhai, the true evil, the true dark empire, is slowly revealing the tip of its iceberg.

Oh no, this tip has already been removed by Jiang Tian, ​​but the backlash from the iceberg is about to officially begin.

Those criminal groups are no longer able to sit still; they are trying to contain, even frantically targeting, Jiang Tian, ​​starting from every angle.

Huai Sheng realized this too, and came to the Municipal Public Security Bureau hoping to have a heart-to-heart talk with Jiang Tian and learn from his experience.

They wanted to quickly discuss what kind of assistance they needed from the provincial authorities.

But upon arriving, he witnessed Jiang Tian's performance.

Huai Sheng was dumbfounded.

Even though he wasn't from the public security, procuratorate, or judicial system, did they think he, Huai Sheng, didn't know anything about interrogation?

Even if you used deadly force, Huai Sheng could withstand it.

But what the hell was this?

You could use unrestricted hand-to-hand combat during the interrogation, and Huai Sheng wouldn't say anything.

Because he knew someone had made a move.

But he never dreamed of this!

"Time, place, method, process—they know every single name. If it were that easy, would Yunhai be in this state? Are there any criminals in the world that can't be caught?" Huai Sheng sighed.

After finishing speaking to himself, Huai Sheng walked towards the door.

"Wait, what if it's true?" Gao Xiaolong stopped Huai Sheng.

Although Gao Xiaolong felt it was absurd, what if it were true?

"To be honest, I've seen all kinds of interrogations, but this kind of farcical interrogation makes me very suspicious. This woman might actually be innocent. I'm starting to doubt Xiao Jiang's abilities!" Huai Sheng shook his head.

How could a criminal gang in Yunnan Province, or even just Yunhai alone, be so easily dismantled? Huai Province has already witnessed the terrifying methods they employ.



Chapter 333 Jiang's Crime-Solving Method: Dare to Try?


“The real criminal organization is gradually surfacing. I thought they couldn't sit still any longer and would make a move. As long as we withstand their counterattack during this period, they will face our Thunderfire. However, I have serious doubts about his capabilities!”

Huai Sheng's expression flickered with a sinister light.

Old Mu was in trouble. Although Jiang Tian rescued him in time, Old Mu's health was still compromised.

Afterwards, Old Mu was recuperating in the hospital. However, even while recuperating, Jiang Yong's incident instantly made Old Mu realize that a terrifying force seemed to be targeting Jiang Tian.

Jiang Yong was placed under investigation almost without any time to react.

Then, Jiang Tian's third uncle also ran into trouble in a short period of time.

It's foreseeable that at this critical juncture for Jiang Peng, if any link in the chain were to fail, Jiang Peng's promotion would instantly become a joke.

Huai Sheng clearly also realized the problem and its seriousness.

Or rather, Huai Sheng wanted Yunhai to grow and prosper even more than Elder Mu.

Huai Sheng had been in Yun Province for a very long time, and he knew better than anyone the terrifying nature of the local criminal groups.

They were incredibly cunning, and even more so, incredibly ruthless.

They were capable of anything; if anyone stood in their way, they would do anything, no matter how brutal, without any conscience.

Elder Mu's accident could even have been caused by these people; they were insane and terrifying.

There was even a strong possibility that foreign forces were behind it.

Originally, Huai Sheng had a good impression of Jiang Tian when he came to see him, and if Jiang Tian had acted normally, Huai Sheng wouldn't have shown any reaction.

But after arriving, Huai Sheng was dumbfounded.

Who solves cases like this?

Isn't this ridiculous?

"No, Huai Sheng, I think we should wait here a little longer and give Xiao Jiang some time. Someone has already gone, hasn't he?" Gao Xiaolong remembered something.

Past lessons made Gao Xiaolong hesitant to draw conclusions so quickly.

Even this investigative method had left him bewildered, but its application to Jiang Tian didn't seem impossible.

What if it were true?

Yes.

"What if? Heh, there's no 'what if.' Even though I don't come from the public security or judicial system, I'm very familiar with the procedures for interrogation and investigation. In all my years, I've never seen a case solved like this," Huai Sheng shook his head.

However, because it was a one-way mirror,

Huai Sheng could even see Jiang Tian inside the interrogation room, a faint smile on his lips, seemingly calm and collected.

But this made Huai Sheng's brow twitch.

His calm demeanor suddenly made Huai Sheng uneasy.

Of course, looking at Jiang Tian alone wasn't the problem.

The most crucial thing was the mental state of Wen Qing, the one who had been captured.

His eyes were fixed on Jiang Tian, ​​and the fear in them seemed genuine.

"Then, perhaps we should wait a little while?" This thought flashed through Huai Sheng's mind.

The Jiang residence wasn't actually far.

After Li Zijin arranged the personnel, professionals from the Municipal Public Security Bureau, equipped with advanced technology, immediately went to the overpass still under construction at the Jiang residence.

They also used state-of-the-art technology to scan and inspect the bridge pillars.

That's why...

Jiang Tian remained calm and collected.

Now, Jiang Tian had fully grasped the use of the Eye of Judgment: when his sin value reached over 1000, it could provide crucial intelligence.

Combined with Jiang Tian's own "King of Criminal Investigation" talent, the two combined were far more than just the sum of their parts.

"King of Criminal Investigation" not only represented reasoning ability but also interrogation and case-solving skills. Combined with meticulous analysis, it often revealed details that ordinary people missed.

This was practically giving Jiang Tian a god-like perspective.

Just like Wen Qing.

Her head was buzzing.

She felt a little dizzy.

She felt heavy-hearted.

Silence.

Deathly silence.

Jiang Tian didn't speak further, because Wen Qing's psychological defenses were gradually crumbling.

Looking at Jiang Tian, ​​her expression grew increasingly terrified, and her face grew paler and paler.

Some time passed.

Bang! Bang! Bang!

A sudden knocking broke the silence of the interrogation room.

The next moment, with a click, the interrogation room door opened.

Having uncovered the possible murder case, Zhao Wei entered with Miss Qiu behind him.

Since it was a search for the body in the cement pillar, Zhao Wei immediately contacted the Criminal Investigation Division, which naturally dispatched Miss Qiu to assist, bringing the most advanced equipment to examine the cement pillar.

"Team Leader Jiang," Miss Qiu said as she entered.

"Did you find anything?" Jiang Tian turned his head, a smirk playing on his lips.

With a few clues, he probed Wen Qing's reactions and expressions little by little, even learning almost everything about her. However, this was Jiang Tian's first time interrogating someone like this, and he wasn't sure how effective it would be.

"The investigation revealed human tissue inside the third pillar of the Jiangfu overpass. We're currently conducting explosives disposal," Miss Qiu announced.

"Thank you, Miss Qiu." Jiang Tian's expression completely relaxed.

Very good.

Even with some confidence, Jiang Tian still felt he was bluffing, given it was his first time using this method.

But thankfully,

his deduction was correct.

So Jiang Tian looked at Wen Qing: "Aren't you going to confess? Who adopted you? Who's the power behind you?"

At this moment, Wen Qing seemed to have finally realized what was happening. She lowered her head, remaining silent. From this moment on, she decided not to react to Jiang Tian's words, silently repeating in her mind, "I can't hear you, I can't hear you..."

And at this moment...

The situation inside the interrogation room completely shocked Huai Sheng, who was observing from the adjacent room.

"This kid, he guessed right?" Huai Sheng exclaimed, mouth agape.

Gao Xiaolong also smiled.

As expected, this guy really did have something up his sleeve; he always managed to surprise people.

"Calm down, Huai Sheng. Jiang Tian, ​​as a Six Merit King, has made history in recent decades, being the first person to achieve a Special Merit in peacetime. This is just a basic deduction for him, nothing to brag about." Gao Xiaolong couldn't help but puff out his chest; it felt good to show off.

Huai Sheng's performance reminded him of the first time he saw Jiang Tian try something, only to be repeatedly proven wrong.

"The criminal didn't say anything, just that his name was Wen Qing. He even knew the murder location, the body disposal location, and the time of the crime. Is this mind reading?"

"No, no, no, Jiang Tian is probably just guessing." Gao Xiaolong's smile was almost uncontrollable, his grin harder to suppress than an AK.

But Huai Sheng remained silent.

Because Jiang Tian started guessing again.

Jiang Tian circled around Wen Qing, stroking his chin with a slight frown: "Let's put aside this matter for now, whether it's related to Wenxing Group or not. But the fact that you've been involved in crime for so many years clearly indicates that you have a stable drug supply line behind you. In fact, there might even be a drug manufacturing plant in Yunhai behind you."



Chapter 334 The Heavenly Official's Attention, the Terrifying Determination of the Upper Class


“Analyzing your situation based on your identity, you probably don’t have many superiors. You likely report directly to someone behind the scenes. Let me guess, with the Wenxing Group’s vast wealth, they wouldn’t have time to meticulously instruct you on such petty tasks in a small-scale drug crime. However, whoever is directing you must be from the Wen family. Let me guess, it’s probably some seemingly ordinary, low-profile member of the Wen family, someone who’s dropped out of school and spends their days idly, probably in their twenties or thirties.”

Jiang Tian analyzed calmly.

In fact, this was a logical deduction.

However, for someone like Wen Qing, it was like a thin veil.

It seemed like a fantasy, but it was like impressionistic profiling. From the details of the crime, in future investigations, it could even sketch out the age and facial features of the perpetrator based on the modus operandi. Most terrifyingly, the similarity was very high, and it could even narrow down the suspects based on the analyzed characteristics.

“Zhao Wei, go and investigate all the members of the Wen family immediately,” Jiang Tian confidently told Zhao Wei.

"What? Are you right or wrong?"

"What a joke, does it even matter?"

Upon meeting, Jiang Tian cast a single, decisive glance, instantly revealing the other party's true colors.

Zhao Wei was highly efficient, handing over a complete set of documents to Jiang Tian in less than a minute.

Meanwhile, Wen Qing, sitting in her seat, was trembling uncontrollably. Even as she tried to brainwash herself into believing she couldn't hear him, Jiang Tian's voice, like a devil's whisper, kept echoing in her ears.

Finally, Wen Qing abruptly looked up.

Pretending not to hear was useless; her defenses had been completely exposed.

"This bastard, did he already know all along? Or did the person with her confess as soon as they came in, and he's just teasing me?"

"Yes."

"That's right."

"That bastard confessed everything without even a hint of resistance?"

"Otherwise, with such detailed information, deducing all this, wouldn't it be a lie?"

Of course, Jiang Tian had no time to listen to Wen Qing's inner monologue.

He already held the list of Wen family members from the Wenye Group in his hand.

"Boss, there are seven Wen family members aged twenty to thirty. Three of them have already entered the Wenye Group for internships and hold small shares. Three are still in school, and only one is currently unemployed after dropping out."

The list was placed in front of Jiang Tian.

Wen Lang.

The ID photo showed a man in his twenties with a slight smile. Judging by appearance alone, there was absolutely nothing wrong with him.

"Of all the Wen family members, only this one doesn't have shares, nor is he part of the Wenye Group," Zhao Wei added to Jiang Tian.

As he spoke, he couldn't hide his excitement.

Because this was the Wenye Group!

You must know.

The Wenye Group is deeply rooted throughout Yunnan Province, involved in all sorts of industries and aspects; it's a truly diversified enterprise.

According to statistics, the Wenye Group has twenty to thirty thousand employees.

This represents the impact on 20,000 to 30,000 families, nearly 100,000 people, which is already an extremely terrifying enterprise.

"You think we can't find out just because you don't say anything? Let me tell you the truth, we haven't even interrogated your companions. But, as they say, the net of justice is vast. Even if you don't confess anything, we can still find out. We exist solely to catch heartless criminals like you."

Jiang Tian placed Wen Lang's file in front of Wen Qing, pointing to the photo for her to see, without even giving her a chance to speak.

While Wen Qing was looking at it, Jiang Tian took in all of her movements.

"I don't know him," Wen Qing said with a cold smile.

But Jiang Tian didn't care, because he already knew the answer.

"Zhao Wei, take the men, move out."

Jiang Tian turned and left instantly.

Leaving Wen Qing bewildered in the wind.

Especially seeing the cold glint in Jiang Tian's eyes as he turned away, Wen Qing's face instantly turned pale.

Meanwhile, in the next room, Huai Sheng was even more astonished and uncertain.

Wenye Group.

This is no small group.

It's a crucial and diversified conglomerate in Yunnan Province, providing numerous jobs and contributing significantly to the province's GDP annually.

Huai Province found it hard to believe that the Wenye Group was involved in crime.

Especially since it was discovered so easily.

"Nothing is impossible. The power of a criminal organization might far exceed our imagination. Of course, if it weren't, Yunhai wouldn't have become like this after all these years." Gao Xiaolong stood with his hands behind his back, his eyes observing Wen Qing's movements.

Even if it was very discreet.

Gao Xiaolong had a background in criminal investigation, and Wen Qing's condition clearly indicated that Jiang Tian's guess was highly likely correct.

"Having evidence is fine, but without evidence, the pressure will be immense," Huai Province said.

“No matter what happens, whatever problems arise, I'll handle it. If I can't handle it, there's headquarters. If headquarters can't handle it, someone higher up will. But I can make it clear that the higher-ups have absolutely zero tolerance for the situation in Yunhai, especially after Lao Mu's incident. Several days have passed, and the joint investigation team hasn't made any progress. The higher-ups are already furious.”

Gao Xiaolong paused slightly at this point.

Cold sweat broke out on Huai Sheng's forehead.

The next moment, Gao Xiaolong continued, “The leadership said that at all costs, crime must be curbed.”

As for what might happen if it can't be curbed…

There are many possibilities.

Perhaps, the army will be deployed to seal the border; for example, Yunhai's development will come to a standstill; the higher-ups will be completely disillusioned, and so on.

There are far too many possibilities.

How could Huai Sheng not be sweating profusely?

He already sensed that a terrifying storm was brewing.

Lao Mu's incident, and the higher-ups' continued calm…

That was brewing something.

And if, this time, they can tear away just the tip of the iceberg of the criminal syndicate...

To bloodily expose the true face of this criminal organization...

For Yunhai, no, for Yun Province, it might truly be a lifesaver.

Of course.

At this moment, Jiang Tian is unaware that the power struggle above has already begun.

Zhao Wei is driving Jiang Tian's Phaeton.

That's why the Phaeton is so low-key.

Most people, upon seeing the Phaeton, only glance at it a couple of times, thinking, "This car is so much bigger than a regular Volkswagen, it must be more expensive."

But if you sit inside, someone in the know will feel breathless.

The interior is luxurious, yet simple and elegant.

To be honest, Jiang Tian was extremely satisfied with Fang Ming's arrangement.

If it were a sports car, it would be quite difficult for Jiang Tian in his current situation. The Phaeton's understated luxury perfectly suited his circumstances.

Jiang Tian sat in the back seat, his phone displaying an unknown number's call.



Chapter 335 Su Tong's Appointment, The Landlord's Foolish Son?


Jiang Tian's phone number was encrypted; even scam calls couldn't get through.

But when he answered, the voice on the other end sounded strangely familiar.

"Officer Jiang, this is Su Tong," a soft, weak voice said.

Su Tong?

Jiang Tian's heart skipped a beat.

While Jiang Tian wasn't certain whether the entire Wenye Group was involved in the crime, he was about 80% certain.

The most crucial question was...

If Wenxing Group were to collapse, who could take over? Without a smooth transition, even if Wenye Group was taken down, it would be the ordinary people who suffered.

If there was anyone with the most ability involved, it was undoubtedly Su Zhenxing.

As for Su Tong...

Jiang Tian had no objections. Aside from her seemingly dull mind, she had a strong moral compass.

In comparison, Ye Xuan was purely evil.

These past few days, Jiang Tian has been busy solving cases. After arresting Ye Xuan, he's been detained at the police station without being interrogated or dealt with.

"Miss Su, are you pleading for Ye Xuan?" Jiang Tian asked calmly.

He didn't have time to investigate, but there was no doubt that Ye Xuan was involved in something, definitely a crime. It's just that Jiang Tian didn't have time to deal with this kind of crime; he planned to handle it after finishing the cases at hand, so he temporarily put it on hold.

But if Su Tong really was pleading for Ye Xuan, then if Ye Xuan had a chance to get out in the future, whatever happened, it would be his own fault.

"No, no, Officer Jiang, you misunderstood me." Unexpectedly, Su Tong spoke hastily the next moment.

"I've been recuperating in the hospital these past few days, and thinking about it, I feel I owe you so much, Officer Jiang. You even put aside past grievances and saved me. Could I... could I treat you to a meal?" Su Tong said hurriedly.

"Okay, okay."

Jiang Tian instantly thought of a phrase.

The rich man's foolish son?

No wonder he was swindled out of his money and woman by Ye Xuan, and even secretly injected with that stuff in the end. His promising future turned into such a miserable state.

There's a reason for it.

Jiang Tian strongly suspected that if he hung up now and cut off all contact with Su Tong, this girl would truly live a life of pain and torment.

"I might not be free tonight," Jiang Tian said, checking the time; it was already very late.

"What about tomorrow?" Su Tong asked hurriedly.

"Even less tomorrow." If Jiang Tian were to take decisive action against the Wenye Group, such a behemoth would likely keep him very busy.

Su Tong's heart sank deeper and deeper.

Until...

Jiang Tian heard sobbing on the other end of the phone.

She was crying?

This silly girl, Su Tong, was crying?

Jiang Tian sighed helplessly, "It'll be past midnight. I'll treat you to a late-night snack; I'm working overtime."

"How late will you be working?" Su Tong's voice lit up with surprise.

"If you can wait, it'll be after midnight. If not, you should go to sleep first. I'll treat you when I have time," Jiang Tian said.

Meanwhile, at that moment...

Inside the hospital, Su Tong, accompanied by her father Su Zhenxing, was checking out of the hospital, her face increasingly shocked.

Working overtime until the early hours of the morning, and today, it even seemed to be Saturday.

But what about Jiang Tian?

Working until the early hours of Saturday nights—how arduous must their weekday jobs be?

At this moment.

Su Tong suddenly felt a surge of emotion. The peaceful society we enjoy today is thanks to heroes like Jiang Tian, ​​who silently bear the burden.

How many risks have they silently endured?

How many life-or-death crises have they faced?

They dedicate all their energy and time to fighting criminals.

How noble is that?

Thinking of this, Su Tong suddenly felt like crying.

"Thank you." A thousand words escaped Su Tong's lips as she uttered a simple "thank you," then hurriedly hung up the phone. She dared not speak further, for fear she would burst into tears.

Then, Su Tong forced back her tears and looked up at her father beside her.

"Dad, are the police very busy?" Su Tong asked.

Su Zhenxing thought for a moment: "It depends on the department. Some are very busy, under a lot of pressure, and often work overtime, like criminal investigators, who handle criminal cases. Others have fixed working hours, like beat cops."

Su Tong's eyes lit up: "Then, Officer Jiang, who saved me, must be a criminal investigator. He must be one of those super-successful cops who has solved many cases, like the super-successful cops on TV."

Su Zhenxing's expression was strange.

"What's wrong?" Su Tong asked, somewhat confused.

Su Zhenxing's tense face suddenly broke into a smile: "He's a narcotics officer, the most dangerous and busiest of all police branches. At least in Yunhai, narcotics officers have the highest level of danger, the highest risk factor in modern peaceful society."

Others might not know, but it was relatively easy for Su Zhenxing to find out Jiang Tian's identity.

Of course, he only knew Jiang Tian's profession; even Su Zhenxing couldn't find out any more details.

Sure enough.

Su Tong's eyes widened even more.

“When I was discussing cooperation with the leaders of Yunnan Province, they casually mentioned that Yunhai has made tremendous progress in drug control, with a significant decrease in crime. All of this was achieved in a short period, at the cost of the lives of dozens of anti-drug heroes. Officer Jiang has made a huge contribution and must be a key figure in this breakthrough in drug control.” Su Zhenxing patted Su Tong on the shoulder.

Compared to Ye Xuan, Su Zhenxing only respected his daughter, but he didn't have a good feeling about Ye Xuan.

He had a keen sense that something was off about Ye Xuan, but he couldn't quite put his finger on it.

In contrast, Jiang Tian was much to his liking.

Although they had little contact.

However, Su Zhenxing genuinely hoped his daughter could become friends with Jiang Tian.

Even to comfort his daughter,

Su Zhenxing booked a meal at a Michelin-starred restaurant.

And just after they arrived,

Su Zhenxing felt he saw a familiar car.

That's right.

A Phaeton.

Jiang Tian's car.

Although that night was rushed and the scene chaotic, Su Zhenxing was well aware of the situation, especially regarding Jiang Tian, ​​who jumped into the water. His car was still parked on the shore; he had specifically checked the license plate number.

"What a coincidence, it looks like Officer Jiang's car?" Su Zhenxing pointed to the car by the roadside. All the windows were sealed shut, and the dark tinted windows made it impossible to see if anyone was inside.



Chapter 336 A Celestial Being? Sorry, You're in Hell.


"Young Master Wen, please try this dish. Michelin-starred cuisine is quite good, Young Master Wen, please try it."

Inside a Michelin-starred restaurant.

In a private room, Jiang Zihao looked at a handsome young man with a fawning smile.

This young man wore a crisp white suit, and although he maintained a gentle smile, he exuded an aura of unapproachability.

Especially swayed by Jiang Zihao's obsequiousness, the young man merely nodded slightly, outwardly remaining calm, and said, "Unexpectedly, it seems your Jiang family has embezzled quite a bit. The country certainly didn't wrong you!"

It would have been better if he hadn't mentioned it; now that it was, Jiang Zihao instantly gritted his teeth.

It's over.

Because it's completely over.

Jiang Zihao's transfer order has been issued, and he will be departing for the countryside soon.

It's no wonder that the wealth gap in Yunnan Province is terrifying. In wealthy areas like Yunhai, the rich are incredibly wealthy, while the poor are incredibly poor.

Of course, Yunhai is the provincial capital, so even the poorest can find a way to make a living.

But other provinces are different.

Yunnan Province has had a very difficult time.

The entire province's resources have been focused on developing the provincial capital, with the idea that the wealthy would help the less fortunate. Meanwhile, other areas have practically stagnated.

Especially in many places, even primary school education is not widespread. In many areas, students have to carry their schoolbags, trekking over mountains every day to get to school.

In short, because of the backward environment in Yunnan Province, a breeding ground for crime has emerged.
