In fact, he looked at Jiang Tian with even greater satisfaction.

He had long heard stories of Jiang Tian's tireless work ethic.

He'd heard that at his busiest, he'd gone days and nights without sleep while apprehending major drug lords.

Young people should be like that—energetic.

Full of vigor.

And humble and polite.

Thinking this through, Huai Sheng had even higher hopes for Jiang Tian: "Comrade Jiang, don't be in such a hurry. President Wen is looking for you because he has something to discuss. It just so happens that I'm here too."

Jiang Tian suddenly realized, "Huai Sheng, you should have said so earlier! You're so busy, I was afraid of taking up your time. After all, the official business you're handling is for the people of Yunnan Province, for tomorrow's dawn."

Huai Sheng smiled.

At the same time, he blushed slightly, feeling a little embarrassed.

This young man.

Why is he being so nice?

Meanwhile, Huai Sheng was inwardly cursing. Which bastard spread rumors and assaulted a leader? Would Comrade Jiang Tian really do such things?

Nonsense.

Gao Xiaolong's expression also became increasingly interesting.

Others might not understand, but he did.

Something's not right.

When has this kid ever been so flattering?

Even when Boss Tan came, Jiang Tian just gave him a perfunctory response.

Something's not right.

Gao Xiaolong became wary; something was definitely amiss.

"Comrade Jiang, it's like this. I heard you arrested Wenlang today. Unfortunately, Wenlang is my son. It's my fault for not disciplining him properly; he's offended you greatly. If he's guilty, you can handle him as you see fit, sentence him accordingly. If he's innocent, I hope you'll release him. If you're still angry, you can teach him a lesson; I absolutely won't say a word." Wen Jijiang spoke with a practiced smile.

Jiang Tian narrowed his eyes.

That was a skillfully crafted statement.

The deeper meaning is that if there's evidence, handle it accordingly; but if there's no evidence, release him immediately.

If you're still not satisfied, beat him again, however you like.

The point is, don't detain him any longer.

Evidence?

Jiang Tian certainly has it.

Don't say there's no recent intelligence; even if Wen Lang plotted to kill Jiang Tian, ​​it was an attempted murder. Besides, there are witnesses. Jiang Zihao, practically useless, can be easily beaten up and he'll tell you everything.

On the other hand, Jiang Tian has already had the city police investigate this guy's drug manufacturing plant.

There's a saying:

Credit should be shared.

Jiang Tian can't be involved in every single detail.

Besides, if this case is solved, he's practically guaranteed a first-class merit, but a special-class merit is out of the question. Keeping it would be a waste, and it would give the leadership the impression of him hogging the credit. A lone wolf won't get anywhere.

Sharing some of the credit and work wouldn't harm Jiang Tian; on the contrary, it would make everyone in the Municipal Bureau extremely grateful.

So, releasing Wen Lang would be utterly absurd.

"Yes, Mr. Wen makes a good point," Jiang Tian nodded seriously.

Wen Jijiang sneered inwardly.

However, he understood.

The two leaders present were figures Jiang Tian couldn't possibly reach.

And his Wenye Group—what level of entity was it? It involved various industries, the livelihoods of tens of thousands of people. Any problem would have a chain reaction affecting hundreds of thousands, undoubtedly worsening the already dire economic situation in Yunnan Province.

Who would dare touch them?

Even Huai Province? Would they dare touch them?

That was Wen Jijiang's confidence.

"Comrade Jiang, then release Wen Lang. Don't worry, I'll teach him a lesson when we get back, and I guarantee he won't show his face in front of you again," Wen Jijiang continued.

This was already a show of weakness; in Wen Jijiang's view, he had already given Jiang Tian a great deal of face.

A lowly policeman like this, he could easily crush with a single kick.

"Sorry, I can't release him," Jiang Tian said with a smile.

"Comrade Jiang, we've already hired a lawyer. This is an innocent arrest, involving public beatings and even the use of firearms. Without evidence, even if this matter goes all the way to Beijing, we're in the right," Wen Jijiang said calmly.

This was utterly shameless.

Although Wen Jijiang wore a smile,

his eyes were growing increasingly cold.

He stared intently at Jiang Tian.

Gao Xiaolong and Huai Sheng frowned slightly.

But at this moment, they couldn't say anything.

In Wen Lang's case, they only saw Jiang Tian guess the Wenye Group during the interrogation of Wen Qing.

But to target a pillar industry based on mere guesswork.

Gao Xiaolong had complete confidence in Jiang Tian.

However, Huai Sheng couldn't let things go unchecked.

This concerned the overall economic development of the province's thirteen cities; it couldn't be treated recklessly.

Huai Sheng pondered for a moment, then looked at Jiang Tian and said, "Jiang, how far have you investigated Wen Lang's case gone? If there's nothing else, release him for now."

Wen Jijiang's smile gradually turned mocking.

No, not mocking.

It was condescending sarcasm.

So what if he's a lowly policeman with merit?

"Not even a lowly policeman."

"Going against the Wenye Group? You dare to challenge them?"

Though unspoken, Jiang Tian saw it in Wen Jijiang's eyes.

"Your superiors have already said so, Officer Jiang, release them." Wen Jijiang reached out and placed his hand on Jiang Tian's shoulder. His smile, though seemingly genuine, was far more infuriating than impotent rage.

Known.

Wen Jijiang's criminal activities.

Known, given the evidence.

What to do when someone tramples on your face like this?

Jiang Tian had only one answer.

Swish!!!

He drew his baton from behind and struck the condescending, mocking face.

Boom!!!

This blow.

Will ignite the entire Yunnan Province.

This blow, without a doubt, will also trigger a horrific bloodbath.



Chapter 348 Shocking: What if he's a major drug lord?


*Bang!*

A dull thud rang out.

A spurt of blood shot from Wen Jijiang's nose, his mouth stained with blood.

Jiang Tian, ​​baton in hand, a smirk playing on his lips, looked at Wen Jijiang as if he had just done something insignificant.

But then...

Huai Sheng jumped to his feet.

Chief Gao followed closely behind.

Wen Jijiang staggered, clutching his nose and mouth, his vision blurring, making him sway unsteadily.

*Buzz!*

Seeing this scene...

Huai Sheng was dumbfounded.

Wait, everything was fine just now, how did it suddenly turn into this?

What happened?

"Are you crazy? What are you doing?" Wen Jijiang's gold-rimmed glasses had already been smashed off and deformed.

He was completely stunned.

He'd been hit.

He'd been hit?

"Xiao Jiang, what are you doing?" Huai Sheng glared.

Looking at Jiang Tian, ​​Huai Sheng's face gradually flushed, and his anger flared.

Insolence.

He actually showed him no respect.

Actually, running into Wen Jijiang was just a coincidence.

However, the two were quite familiar with each other, especially at business conferences, where they had discussed matters many times and were very well acquainted.

Old friends meeting.

Plus, Wen Jijiang's message was that his son had been arrested, and by Jiang Tian no less.

Wen Jijiang was just observing the situation; he couldn't ignore it.

Because of the importance of Wenye Group, it touched upon the pillar industries of various sectors in Yunnan Province!

It was a behemoth belonging to Yunnan Province.

Wen Jijiang's identity was not only that of a businessman, but also that of a representative of Yunnan Province; his status and position were extremely prestigious.

And in comparison...

In truth, they hadn't felt anything amiss with Wen Jijiang's attitude towards Jiang Tian, ​​or rather, they were already used to it.

Those who dealt with Wen Jijiang were all high-ranking officials like him.

A mere police officer.

At best, a meritorious police officer.

But even so, what use was it?

Wen Jijiang believed he had already given Jiang Tian a lot of face, had been very considerate in his words.

He had already shown respect.

So what if Jiang Tian wasn't satisfied? Go beat his son up again.

Wen Jijiang believed he had already yielded a great deal.

But Jiang Tian didn't play by the rules.

A madman, he was a madman.

"Xiao Jiang, what are you doing?"

Huai Sheng rushed out, grabbed Jiang Tian's baton, and shouted angrily at him.

"Wenye Group has made outstanding contributions to Yunnan Province's GDP. They are renowned philanthropists and entrepreneurs, a true representative of Yunnan Province. How dare you strike President Wen with a police baton? Who gave you permission to do this? Are you insane?"

This blow...

If things go wrong, it could trigger a major earthquake throughout Yunnan Province.

Especially since much of Yunnan Province's development and planning relies on Wenye Group. If Wenye Group were to halt operations in a fit of anger...

The impact would be far beyond what can be described as affecting hundreds of thousands of people.

Do you know the magnitude of the impact this blow will have?

"Provincial Governor Huai, don't panic. I think Xiao Jiang must have a reason for acting this way." Gao Xiaolong, having already been repeatedly humiliated by Jiang Tian, ​​remained calm and didn't act impulsively like Provincial Governor Huai.

"No matter what happened, assaulting President Wen—do you know what kind of behavior that is, Jiang Tian? Have we been too lenient with you?" Huai Sheng was quite furious.

Fine, fine.

Before this.

He thought it was just a rumor.

That's right.

Before meeting him, Huai Sheng thought those rumors were false. In fact, after meeting him, seeing how eloquent and articulate Jiang Tian was, Huai Sheng had even admired him.

This young man is good; he'll definitely be a great person in the future.

But suddenly he went berserk.

He even dared to assault President Wen.

No matter what happened, at this time, assault is wrong.

Huai Sheng's eyes were filled with disappointment.

It wasn't that he was protecting President Wen or targeting Jiang Tian.

It's just that Jiang Tian's actions, no matter the reason, were wrong. Do you know how important Wenye Group is to Yun Province, how many aspects it involves?

Don't say there's no evidence. Even if there were evidence proving Wen Jijiang committed crimes inside, you can't be so impulsive. At the very least, you should have told him, notified him, and given Yunnan Province some time to prepare before you started planning your operation.

There are too many aspects to consider in this matter.

"Provincial Governor, Officer Jiang is furious. That brat Wen Lang must have completely enraged him. However, I hope the Municipal Bureau can give me an explanation and clarification for what happened today. Otherwise, all our future cooperation will unfortunately have to end." Wen Jijiang covered his face; his gold-rimmed glasses were broken, so his vision was slightly blurry, but he still immediately understood the situation.

His words were initially very firm.

(Vague and indistinct)

Wen Jijiang saw Jiang Tian looking into his eyes, and Wen Jijiang smiled.

No matter what happened.

Young man, you're too impulsive.

Don't you understand his importance to the entire Yunhai?

Or rather, his importance to the entire Yunnan Province.

He carried the weight of tens of thousands of people's livelihoods, plus their families—hundreds of thousands—on his shoulders, all depending on the Wenye Group.

Moreover, many development projects required the Wenye Group to lead them.

So no matter what happened, Wen Jijiang remained calm.

“Jiang Tian, ​​apologize to President Wen.” Huai Sheng sighed in disappointment, addressing Jiang Tian.

Too impulsive.

Jiang Tian, ​​in the end, was still too impulsive.

Sure enough, he couldn't change Yun Province.

It's true, he was just a young man in his twenties, easily angered. But the greater the expectation, the greater the disappointment; he hadn't expected the rumors to be true.

To dare to do this, assaulting a leader, it must be true.

“Little Jiang, Huai Sheng is here. You were a bit impulsive. President Wen is a renowned philanthropist. You might have misunderstood something. Besides, Wen Lang did something wrong; President Wen shouldn't have been involved.” Gao Xiaolong gave him a look.

If there was no evidence, quickly admit defeat and apologize.

If there was evidence, quickly speak up.

Wen Jijiang squinted, his face already swollen. Jiang Tian hadn't used much force with that baton.

However, for Jiang Tainan, it was more of an insult.

Because this bastard was a major drug lord entrenched in Yunnan Province!

And having entrenched itself for so many years, accumulating over ten thousand crime points, how many innocent victims have been there?

These are almost impossible to count.

“Apologize.” Huai Sheng’s voice gradually turned cold.

He hadn’t expected Jiang Tian to be so disregard for the bigger picture. Even if Jiang Tian and Wen Lang had a conflict, this was unacceptable, especially in front of him. Wasn’t this a slap in the face?

Wasn’t this disrespectful?

If word got out, would he still be able to work as a leader in Yunnan Province?

The more Huai Sheng thought about it, the worse his mood became.

But the next moment, Jiang Tian finally spoke: “Leader Huai, are you sure you want me to apologize?”

“That’s right.” Huai Sheng nodded without hesitation.

“Then, what if this person is a major drug lord?” Jiang Tian continued.

Buzz!!



Chapter 349 The sky has fallen, and the livelihoods of hundreds of thousands of people are at risk.


A drug lord?

Boom!!!

Huai Sheng's eyes widened.

Gao Xiaolong was equally astonished.

In truth, putting themselves in his shoes or using common sense, they hadn't deeply considered this question. The Wenye Group was a behemoth; why would such a behemoth get involved with something like that?

Drug lordship is inherently a highly profitable but dangerous profession.

The Wenye Group was enormous, a colossus with assets far exceeding billions.

The depths its reach were unimaginable.

It held a crucial and influential position throughout Yunnan Province. In other words, if the Wenye Group were to encounter problems, hundreds of thousands of people in Yunnan would be affected, instantly exacerbating the already impoverished province.

The consequences would be unbearable for anyone.

Even Huai Sheng couldn't bear it.

That's why Huai Sheng was so furious about Jiang Tian's actions.

Even if you have a thousand reasons,

that's not a reason to publicly assault President Wen. Even if Wen Jijiang might be involved in a crime, we need to proceed cautiously and absolutely cannot act rashly.

Jiang Tian's blow with the baton, without exaggeration, shook the very foundations of Yunnan Province, enough to cause a terrifying earthquake.

If Wenye Group were to expand out of the province and no longer be rooted in Yunnan, it would be equivalent to Yunnan losing a massive enterprise.

And countless people would lose their jobs.

It's just a pity.

Jiang Tian understands Huai Province's thinking, and he understands the thinking of these high-ranking leaders, but this is ultimately a limitation of this era, where everything is measured by GDP.

This has led these businessmen, especially the lawless ones, to be elevated too high, to the point that they forget their status and position, and even more so, the meaning of the saying "those who get rich first help those who get rich later."

Of course, we don't expect you to give back to society after you become wealthy.

What truly enraged Jiang Tian were these people. Instead of giving back to society, they committed serious crimes, and after being exposed, they dared to intimidate him.

Following Jiang Tian's words,

the entire room fell silent.

Because the limits of tolerance absolutely could not include drug lords.

All crimes give you time and opportunity to slowly lay the groundwork, but touching drugs is crossing a red line—a bottom line that no one can cross.

"Officer Jiang, have you gone to any lengths to slander me? I know you are a proud anti-drug officer of the Municipal Bureau, but that is absolutely no reason for you to slander me, and the Wenye Group is not someone you can slander."

Wen Jijiang's expression only paused slightly; his excellent composure allowed him to speak calmly.

However, the flickering in his eyes betrayed his inner turmoil.

Jiang Tian's lips curled into a sneer.

"Leader Huai, please step aside," Jiang Tian said with a smile.

Huai Sheng was still in shock and stunned, and subconsciously made way.

Jiang Tian took a step forward.

His baton flashed out of control, sweeping across Wen Jijiang's head.

"You dare!" Wen Jijiang's eyesight wasn't bad, though his vision was blurry from nearsightedness; his pupils contracted instinctively as he raised his hands to block.

Bang!!!

Crack!!!

A dull thud was followed by a crisp sound of bones breaking.

Wen Jijiang felt an excruciating pain shoot through his mind. The next moment, his mind went blank, and he was slammed onto the table, the table shattering into pieces.

Wen Jijiang lay writhing miserably among the broken pieces, clutching his arms, his mind nearly blank.

Buzz!!!

Huai Sheng watched this scene.

His head exploded.

He's gone mad.

He's rebelled.

He's turned against the Heavenly Gang.

"You're defying the heavens!

Are you trying to drag Yunnan Province down with you, with those two batons?

Are you going to drag the entire province down, making everyone starve because of your two batons?

Will the economic development from top to bottom suffer a major impact?

"This is outrageous!"

Huai Sheng roared in fury.

This is defying the heavens!

Gao Xiaolong swallowed hard, admitting that even he, who had seen many things, was somewhat dumbfounded by this scene.

He even doubted whether he could handle it.

Are you trying to kill the CEO of Wenye Group?

Others are one thing.

But Wenye Group is really important to Yunhai!

"You did that."

Gao Xiaolong's eyelids twitched.

He subtly glanced at Huai Sheng, and sure enough, Huai Sheng's face was now grim, his whole body trembling with rage.

The next moment.

Jiang Tian arrived beside Wen Jijiang.

He bent down, reached out, and in one swift motion, grabbed Wen Jijiang's shiny bald head, then dragged him abruptly from the shattered table to the open ground.

Wen Jijiang was now groaning in pain.

Too horrible.

Absolutely too horrible.

Gao Xiaolong's eyelids twitched; he couldn't bear to watch.

Huai Sheng pointed at Jiang Tian: "You, what are you going to do? Jiang Tian, ​​what exactly are you going to do? Are you going to kill Boss Wen?"

"No, no, no, it's precisely because of your guidance, Huai Sheng, that our Municipal Bureau can face crime with unwavering courage and sacrifice. Under your leadership, our Municipal Bureau has solved numerous major cases. No matter what kind of drug lord we face, we dare to take action; no matter their identity, we can act without hesitation. And all of this is because of your leadership, Huai Sheng." Jiang Tian stood at attention, his expression unusually serious.

Pfft!!!

Gao Xiaolong almost spat out his drink.

Huai Sheng's furious expression turned somewhat bewildered.

Looking at Jiang Tian, ​​his eyes were filled with complex emotions.

This bastard.

His words may sound nice, but are you trying to shift the blame?

"I wouldn't dare. You dared to kill someone in front of me, how could I be qualified to lead you?" Huai Sheng's expression was unpleasant, and his words clearly showed hostility.

Jiang Tian's actions had gone too far.

This had already caused Huai Sheng to harbor considerable resentment towards Jiang Tian.

Jiang Tian's eyes remained unchanged: "So, just because Wenye Group is extremely important to Yun Province, does that mean we should disregard their criminal activities, disregard their organized and premeditated malicious crimes, and allow them to harm the people?"

Hmm?

Huai Sheng's heart skipped a beat.

Crimes?

Organized?

Poisoning ordinary people?

Is the Wenye Group really that serious?

"What evidence do you have?" Huai Sheng's mood sank slightly.

Something's not right.

He sensed something was wrong.

"Evidence? Of course I have it," Jiang Tian sneered.

At this moment, Wen Jijiang, whose arm was bent at a grotesque and abnormal degree, was in excruciating pain, making his mind somewhat unclear.

But upon hearing about drug lords and evidence, Wen Jijiang's mind instantly sharpened, becoming much clearer.



Chapter 350 The leader's mentality collapsed. Damn it, this guy is a real drug lord.


"Provincial Governor Huai, look at your city police! Is this how they behave? Trying to kill me right in front of you? Is this the municipal police station, or is this the underworld?" Wen Jijiang, enduring excruciating pain, spoke with a fierce, preemptive roar.

"Have you committed any illegal or criminal activities, including drug-related offenses?" Huai Sheng inquired.

"No, Provincial Governor Huai. Why would I, with such vast assets as Wenye Group, do such a thing? It's utter nonsense," Wen Jijiang asserted.

Huai Sheng looked at Jiang Tian, ​​his face darkening. "Comrade Jiang, do you know what you've done? Do you have any evidence to prove that President Wen is indeed involved in drug-related crimes?"

Even Gao Xiaolong's expression changed slightly upon hearing this.

Because the meaning was clear.

Simply investigating whether someone in Wenye Group was involved in drug-related crimes, without proving a direct connection to Wen Jijiang, was useless.

Unless, he could produce direct evidence proving Wen Jijiang's drug offenses.

But...

Such a large corporation, such a powerful boss.

Even if they really did that, what evidence do you have that it wasn't your subordinates acting on their own?

This is a very serious problem.

“Evidence? I...of course I have it.”

Jiang Tian approached Wen Jijiang, who was lying on the ground, his eyes already bloodshot from the excruciating pain.

His eyes were fixed intently on Jiang Tian.

“Hahaha, what's the use of playing tricks? If you can't produce evidence, Huai Province, then I'm going to Beijing! I'm going to find the top leaders! I refuse to believe there's no place where justice can be served!” Wen Jijiang gritted his teeth.

At the same time, Wen Jijiang's heart was also pounding with rage.

A madman.

This is indeed a madman.

When Wen Lang made his move, Wen Jijiang hesitated, wondering whether he should also attack Jiang Tian.

However, in the end, Wen Lang persuaded Wen Jijiang.

Ultimately, no matter how many merits Jiang Tian accumulated, he was still just a low-ranking policeman—no, even a police inspector, in their eyes, just a low-ranking officer.

The leaders they interacted with daily were mostly at the provincial or ministerial level.

Jiang Tian's rank was far too low; they didn't even consider him a threat. However, Jiang Tian's actions...

resulted in significant revenue losses for them.

Because of Jiang Tian.

The hard-won stability in Yunhai, and even Yun Province, was shattered.

The balance between the criminal organization and the police system was disrupted.

Jiang Tian was like a sharp thorn.

piercing deep into the heart of the criminal organization, and the police system, smelling blood, pounced on him frantically.

The losses inflicted on them could not be compensated for by sheer numbers.

"I've read about the history of Wenye Group. Mr. Wen's father started from scratch twenty years ago, and in less than twenty years, the group has grown exponentially without ever experiencing any financial problems. Moreover, whenever there's a crisis, massive amounts of capital always flow in," Jiang Tian said calmly.

Before taking action against Wenye Group, Jiang Tian considered it for a moment.

The moment he entered, Jiang Tian had already made his decision.

Wenye Group's asset scale was indeed enormous.

He even planned to negotiate with Su Zhenxing first before considering taking action against Wenye Group.

However, he didn't expect the CEO of Wenye Group to jump out so quickly.

"This is your so-called evidence? If that's all, kid, you can wait for me to sue you," Wen Jijiang said with a mocking smile.

Huai Sheng's expression also changed slightly.

To be honest,

no one investigated Wenye Group's history of success.

A cat that catches mice is a good cat, regardless of its color.

While its history of success may not be entirely clean, it is definitely not an excuse, reason, or evidence to target Wenye Group.

"Jiang Tian, ​​do you have any evidence?" Huai Sheng asked, enunciating each word clearly.

Jiang Tian smiled and looked at Wen Jijiang, saying, "Don't rush, let me explain slowly. After discovering this problem, I immediately sensed something was amiss. Where did your company's money come from, and how did it come about? Of course, at the time, I was just curious."

"But subsequently, I conducted a detailed investigation and discovered that these funds all originated from overseas."

That's right.

Overseas funds.

This isn't just about drug trafficking.

It's also entangled with foreign forces.

What kind of behavior is this?

What exactly happened inside? What is Wenye Group trying to do?

Jiang Tian had no interest in knowing these things.

Wen Jijiang's expression visibly changed, showing some panic and a sense of being caught off guard.

"Later, I traced these overseas funds and found that some came from Myanmar, and some from Japan. Of course, the origin of your funds is none of my concern; these matters are handled by other departments."

Although Jiang Tian said this, remembering his other identity, he couldn't help but sigh bitterly.

He was in a difficult position too!

With a mountain of cases to solve, Jiang Tian didn't have the time to play cat-and-mouse games with Wen Jijiang.

Besides, Wen Jijiang had repeatedly attacked him, and Jiang Tian couldn't tolerate it any longer.

Fortunately,

Huai Sheng was here. If something went wrong, Jiang Tian could cover it up, and Huai Sheng would be prepared.

He was good at solving cases.

But as for the financial aftermath, he'd let Huai Sheng prepare in advance.

"However, this has piqued my interest in the Wenye Group. Just today, the young master of the Wenye Group contacted my cousin, trying to incite him to kill me. I decided I couldn't just sit and wait to die, so I did some research on the Wenye Group. Guess what I found?" Jiang Tian asked, looking at Wen Jijiang.

Wen Jijiang clenched his fists, and fine beads of sweat were clearly visible on his forehead, whether from pain or something else.

Those were cold sweats.

Wen Jijiang was panicking, already somewhat frightened.

Ring ring ring!!!

Just then, Jiang Tian's phone rang.

Jiang Tian took it out, saw it was Lü Chun's number, and answered without hesitation.

"Boss, the operation went smoothly," Lü Chun's voice came through.

"Have the witness been caught?" Jiang Tian glanced at Wen Jijiang, a cold smile creeping onto his face.

“We’ve arrested Wen Jijiang’s secretary at Wenye Group. He’s confessed to the crimes and provided evidence of Wen Jijiang’s crimes. The witnesses and I are almost at the city police station. It’s enough to convict Wen Jijiang of Wenye Group. Currently, he’s involved in drug trafficking, and the evidence we have so far reaches 300 kilograms.”

Lu Chun’s voice rang out, barely concealing his excitement.

That’s right.

Jiang Tian had already instructed Lu Chun to take action.

The hasty action, while the amount involved wasn’t as outrageous as initially imagined, only provided solid evidence; much more remained to be gathered.

As for Lu Chun, Jiang Tian had already sent him and his men to Wenye Group shortly after arresting Wen Lang.

Upon hearing this,

Huai Sheng’s head spun.

Damn it.

Something’s wrong. Wenye Group is really in trouble.

Chapter 351 caused a sensation. Had he broken a divine law?

Because without a doubt, they had performed a meritorious service, and this time it was a major one.

That's exactly it.

Jiang Tian, ​​seemingly arrogant and domineering, actually knew his limits.

Taking action without going through a meeting with higher-ups... were they sure there wouldn't be any major problems?

Good heavens.

Not exactly a protective umbrella.

Facilitating crime—no, it should be said, they themselves were committing crimes.

A big fish.

This isn't just a big fish!

Wei Xun and his second squad followed.

It could be said that

this secretary was incredibly important to them; even doubling the number of people wouldn't have been worthwhile.

Don't underestimate the loyalty within these criminal groups.

That's right. With the evidence Jia Yun left behind, if the Wenye Group found out, he'd be dead without a burial place, and none of his family members would escape. Exterminating his entire clan would be excessive, but even close relatives would likely be wiped out.

Jiang Ning had just visited his father.

Actually, the Municipal Public Security Bureau also had detention and residential facilities. Generally, people were only transferred to detention centers when they were deemed worthless or after interrogation.

Considering all these factors, Jiang Tian basically decided to keep his third uncle at the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

He was in a single room, and his mental state and other aspects were quite good.

Chapter 352 The truth comes out, the crimes are heinous, and he shall be executed.

Hmm?

Besides the logical reasoning skills he gained from being a criminal investigator, the King of Criminal Investigation had given Jiang Tian something even more important: psychology, an understanding of human nature!

In the entire room, the one who most wanted Wen Jijiang dead wasn't Jiang Tian, ​​but precisely Jia Yun!

"Jia Yun, speak directly. If Wenye Group has problems, as the governor of Yunnan Province, I will definitely deal with it strictly. If there are no problems, I will absolutely give you justice," Governor Huai said calmly.

Chapter 353 Jiang Tian's Amazing Plan: A Perfect Calculation

he had taken over the Wenye Group.

Jiang Tian.

This kid.

Chapter 354: The entire audience was moved to tears; not only had he rendered meritorious service, he had even considered human relationships.

"Impossible. We only just decided to take action against your third uncle, and we haven't even succeeded yet. Just getting your third uncle involved in drug trafficking is simply impossible."

How long has it been? A day?

No, it hasn't even been a day.

The decision to act was made in the afternoon, and by evening, their massive Wenye Group was sentenced to death in just a few hours?

In reality, the reason Wen Jijiang could act so brazenly, even audaciously, to come to the Municipal Bureau demanding his release was because of the importance of the Wenye Group.

Calling it the economic lifeline of a province might be an exaggeration, but the Wenye Group held an absolutely pivotal position in the entire Yun Province.

At this level, at this level, it's not something that can be moved so easily.

Even if action were to be taken, it would require countless meetings. He knew the efficiency of the system all too well. Even with a little evidence, the endless meetings from top to bottom would mean that by the time a decision was reached, the person in question would probably be on the other side of the Atlantic.

So he wasn't worried at all, because there was no indication of anything amiss. In fact, the province wanted their Wenye Group involved in an important project recently.

He had just had lunch with the leaders of the commerce department this afternoon.

If there had been any hint of trouble, he would definitely have known.

But now.

He didn't know.

Therefore.

“Yunhai is so big, with so much crime. Every day, drug deals happen in every corner of Yunhai. We have so many cases on our hands. Why do you think I would target you? Or rather, why would the anti-drug system, which just arrested so many criminals abroad, be targeting you? If you hadn't come forward yourselves, I might not have even bothered to target you.” Jiang Tian shook his head.

But sometimes, the truth is more hurtful than a lie.

"Just because you targeted your third uncle as revenge, you wiped out our entire Wenye Group in a few hours?

Our Wenye Group, built up over decades and three generations, was destroyed in a few hours?

'So that's how it is.'

Wen Jijiang seemed to age several years in an instant.

Don't even mention conclusive evidence; just proving he has this problem allows for unlimited detention and investigation.

Anti-drug work.

Doesn't need so many excuses and evidence.

"Jia Yun, when you were starving and your whole family was on the verge of death, I was the one who fed you, sponsored your schooling, and after graduation, I groomed you as my confidant, providing you with a life free from want. Is this how you repay me?"

Compared to his composure towards Jiang Tian, ​​Mr. Wen Jijiang became utterly deranged when facing Jia Yun.

Because he anticipated betrayal from everyone.

But he hadn't considered Jia Yun.

Why him?

"President Wen, I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I...I'm so sorry," Jia Yun repeatedly apologized.

But Wen Jijiang had gone mad, lunging at Jia Yun, trying to tear him apart.

Unfortunately, at this moment, Wen Jijiang had already had one hand crippled by Jiang Tian and had been brutally beaten. He was no match for Jia Yun. Jia Yun only needed to kick him once, and Wen Jijiang instantly knelt on the ground.

And Jia Yun's eyes were completely bloodshot.

"Do you think I want to break the law and get your charity? Do you think I don't know you've been controlling and coercing my family? Do you think I don't know about what you did with my wife? What did you force my wife to do? Wen Jijiang, you deserve to die! If you don't die, my family will!"

Jia Yun completely unleashed his fury.

This barrage of attacks caused Wen Jijiang's eyes to roll back repeatedly.

He almost fainted from exhaustion.

No.

Even if Jia Yun was gathering evidence, under normal circumstances, it wouldn't pose any threat to Wen Jijiang.

Only at this moment, when he was completely unprepared, could he deliver a fatal blow.

Was all of this Jiang Tian's scheme?

Wen Jijiang felt a chill run through him.

Because he suddenly realized it.

He had made a mistake in provoking Jiang Tian from the very beginning.

Take Blood Hand, for example. Because they attacked Jiang Tian's family, Jiang Tian ended up wiping out their entire family, storming their company, and single-handedly taking on the entire Blood Hand Group. All the key members were killed on the spot.

Because of that operation, coupled with Jiang Tian's recent frenzied crackdown on drug dealers,

this had created widespread fear among drug dealers throughout Yunhai.

If things continued like this, who knew who would be next? Therefore, besides the losses and significant impact on their profits and drug business, they needed to eliminate potential threats in advance.

At this point, Wen Jijiang considered the situation and, learning from past mistakes, decided to use Jiang Hao as a pretext to see Jiang Tian's choice.

Then, he would use Jiang Hao to attack Jiang Peng. No matter what, they couldn't let Jiang Tian continue to be so powerful, otherwise, Yunhai would have no good days ahead.

He needed to sabotage Jiang Peng's promotion first.

Then they targeted Jiang Tian's cousin, taking a step-by-step approach, first throwing Jiang Tian into disarray, then devising further plans to target him.

But they never dreamed that

they had just made their move against Jiang Tian's third uncle and cousin when Jiang Tian wiped them out, taking down the entire Wenye Group.

And the operation against the Wenye Group continues.

However.

At this moment.

After the initial excitement, Huai Sheng fell silent.

"Old Huai, what's wrong?" Gao Xiaolong turned and asked.

Well done, very well done.

He's grown too fast.

Gao Xiaolong had watched Jiang Tian go from initially eliminating drug dealers to now...

Even the action was incredibly swift.

In just a few hours, they were able to take down a terrifying drug lord like the Wenye Group.

Most commendable of all, Jiang Tian even knew how to share the credit.

He not only achieved a great feat, but he also considered social niceties.

Tears welled up.

Thinking about it, even Huai Sheng in the room was somewhat moved. This brat, why is he so outstanding?



Chapter 355 You are the sinners of the entire Yunnan Province


"Sigh, Wenye Group is a pillar industry of Yunnan Province. I never expected it to run into this problem. If anything happens to Wenye Group, it will be a huge blow to the entire Yunnan economy," Huai Sheng said bluntly, without hiding anything.

"That is indeed a problem."

Gao Xiaolong frowned deeply.

Having come from Kyoto to Yunhai, Gao Xiaolong had a thorough understanding of Yunhai's economic situation.

First of all.

Yunnan Province's economic environment is almost certainly among the worst in the country, if not the worst. Calling it a poor and desolate place wouldn't be an exaggeration.

The complex geographical environment has fostered a great deal of darkness.

Under these circumstances, Yunnan Province has worked hard for many years to build Yunhai into a first-tier city, hoping that the rich would help the poor.

Although Yunhai has developed, the economic environment of the surrounding cities hasn't changed much.

They are still very poor.

If Wenye Group collapses, it will undoubtedly be a huge blow to the entire Yunnan province.

"Provincial Governor Huai, haven't you considered what the higher-ups' attitude would be if we let various crime problems go unchecked and only focused on economic development?"

Just then, Jiang Tian slowly spoke.

Provincial Governor Huai looked sharply at Jiang Tian.

If Jiang Tian had said these words earlier, Provincial Governor Huai would undoubtedly have been annoyed, because Jiang Tian's position was too low; what did a lowly policeman know about economics?

But now, after what just happened, from the operation to its conclusion, Wen Jijiang was brought down in just a few hours, enough to attract Provincial Governor Huai's attention.

"Comrade Jiang, you even understand economics?" Provincial Governor Huai asked in surprise.

Jiang Tian smiled slightly.

To be honest, although Jiang Peng's appointment was almost a done deal, there were still some controversies.

At this point, if Provincial Governor Huai could support it, it would be a complete certainty.

“While I’m not very knowledgeable about economics, I know one thing: the higher-ups are furious about the criminal activity in Yunnan Province. They don’t want Yunnan to be stagnant; they want chaos. The more chaos, the more arrests and crackdowns, the better.”

Jiang Tian, ​​having transmigrated from the future, understands how dire Yunnan’s economic situation was back then.

Moreover, Yunhai’s rise to a first-tier city wasn’t solely due to Yunnan’s own development; it also required support from above.

But when crime permeated Yunhai…

Several bureau chiefs died suddenly in succession.

In Jiang Tian’s memory, not long after, the higher-ups directly deployed the military to completely control drug trafficking and other criminal activities.

Under these circumstances, naturally, the development of Yunnan Province, especially Yunhai as a first-tier city, came to a complete standstill.

“It shouldn’t be that serious, right?” Huai Sheng was still somewhat hesitant.

Just then…

Wei Xun’s phone suddenly rang.

Wei Xun glanced at it, hesitating slightly.

"Answer it," Gao Xiaolong said directly.

Wei Xun quickly nodded and then answered the call.

"Captain Wei, we found the drug lab of the Wenye Group according to the location given by Officer Jiang. It was hidden so well that if we hadn't called in the armed police for assistance, we might not have been able to find it today. It's deep in the mountains." An excited voice came from the phone.

Drug lab.

Jiang Tian has taken down more than just one or two.

"How's the situation?" Wei Xun asked excitedly.

"When we came in, these people weren't prepared at all. We arrested them directly. We seized two thousand kilograms of new-type drugs, as well as various drug-making equipment, etc. We captured twenty-five people alive, and killed eight. Unfortunately, about ten people were covering for one person, and we don't know who it was, but they escaped." An excited voice came from the phone.

Good heavens.

Twenty-five people, every single one of them, are guilty of capital offenses!

Not a single one can escape.

Most importantly, two thousand kilograms.

What a terrifying number.

"Great, great, great!" Wei Xun exclaimed excitedly.

His gaze, even towards Jiang Tian, ​​was filled with gratitude.

There was nothing else to it.

Although Jiang Tian had commanded the entire operation, and the credit undoubtedly went to him—something no one could take away—

then, in other words, the share of the credit was already more than enough for them.

But this team member's words...

instantly dealt a blow to Huai Sheng, whose resolve was still wavering.

"Two thousand kilograms seized in one go?" Huai Sheng exclaimed in disbelief.

What did two thousand kilograms mean?

Even at wholesale prices, one kilogram would sell for tens of thousands.

This batch of goods was worth at least twenty million.

And this is the lowest price; in reality, the selling price would be astronomical, many times higher.

"That's right, and according to our on-site interrogation, they confessed to Wen Lang, the young boss of the Wen family," the person on the phone said.

Hiss!!!

Damn it.

Really damn it!

Huai Sheng stared at Wen Jijiang with bloodshot eyes.

This has already proven one thing.

The Wen family, the Wenye Group, definitely has serious problems.

And if Jiang Tian hadn't taken action beforehand.

Under the Wen family's protection, as the Wenye Group grew larger and larger, how much illegal and criminal activity did they secretly engage in?

How much drugs did they sell?

"Your Wenye Group deserves to die!" Huai Sheng roared in fury.

Wen Jijiang lowered his head, seemingly having completely lost all his energy.

A stark contrast to the condescending attitude Jiang Tian had displayed when he entered.

After speaking, Huai Sheng's gaze instantly shifted to Jiang Tiandao: "Comrade Jiang, regarding the Wenye Group, investigate thoroughly, without any leniency. All those involved in illegal and criminal activities will be arrested and punished to the fullest extent of the law."

He was furious.

His voice trembled with rage.

Because this was only what was seized on the spot. Over the years, the Wen family had dominated Yunnan Province, selling an astronomical amount of drugs.

It was simply an astronomical figure.

And how many people had the Wenye Group harmed?

Probably.

While Yunnan Province was developing, the Wenye Group was already frantically exploiting the province with drugs.

"Besides, during our investigation, we discovered the presence of Japanese nationals among them," the voice on the phone continued.

Boom!!!

That's right.

Huai Sheng's expression went from furious to pale.

Because this meant not only heinous crimes, but also collusion with foreign forces while trafficking drugs.

It's important to understand that while the country has been developing, its control over foreign investment has always been extremely strict.

In other words, in this era, foreign investment is allowed, but certain pillar industries are simply off-limits.

This includes real estate, which is also subject to strict restrictions.

The Wenye Group actually colluded with foreign forces to harm fellow countrymen?

Damn it.

"Wen Jijiang, your Wenye Group is a sinner against the entire Yunnan Province."



Chapter 356 From now on, all the leaders of Yunhai will owe me a favor.


Huai Sheng roared through gritted teeth.

Wen Jijiang looked up, wanting to say something, but in the end, his lips moved, and he took a deep breath, looking at Jiang Tian.

Jia Yun's betrayal, the factory's discovery.

All of this happened in just a few hours.

"Take them away," Jiang Tian waved his hand.

Although Wei Xun's position was higher than Jiang Tian's.

To be precise, Jiang Tian was currently only a deputy section chief, at most a deputy captain in the city bureau, while Wei Xun was the captain of the second brigade, a section chief by rank.

Even in terms of police rank, Wei Xun was significantly higher than Jiang Tian.

But now.

After seeing Jiang Tian's gesture, Wei Xun acted without the slightest hesitation.

He directly had Wen Jijiang and Jia Yun taken away from the office.

As the office door closed,

Huai Sheng slumped onto the sofa, seemingly devoid of all strength.

He hadn't expected criminal organizations to have infiltrated to such a terrifying extent. Even the Wenye Group was rife with various crimes.

What about the other groups?

What about the other forces?

It was chilling to think about.

In fact, Jiang Tian hadn't expected that even a group of this size would be involved.

This only proved one thing: the drug trafficking problem in Yunhai had reached a point where it could no longer be ignored.

"Xiao Jiang, what you just said, that the higher-ups are paying attention and taking it very seriously, is that really true?" Huai Sheng asked with a complex expression.

Jiang Tian's brow twitched; he hadn't expected Huai Sheng to be unaware.

Oh no.

It should be said that Huai Sheng had always focused on addressing economic issues; crime was a matter of law and order.

Normally, the focus was on maintaining a stable environment and reasonably pursuing economic growth. Besides, given Huai Sheng's position, he wouldn't be concerned with such matters.

Of course, even if he were concerned, it might not have been taken seriously before, perhaps due to the prevailing mentality that "it doesn't matter whether a cat is black or white, as long as it catches mice, it's a good cat."

So, before Jiang Tian could speak, Gao Xiaolong had already shaken his head and spoken.

“The higher-ups have reached their limit with the security situation here. Xiao Jiang is right. We're not afraid of chaos now, nor are we afraid of any economic problems, because the higher-ups will back us up.” Gao Xiaolong stated directly.

Huai Sheng sat on the sofa, smoking two cigarettes.

Smoke filled the air, but Huai Sheng's gaze was complex.

He seemed to have been on the wrong path all along.

Compared to economic development, the losses incurred by the public due to various crimes were the greatest.

“Yes, in that case, Xiao Jiang, you can go ahead and do it with confidence. We'll cover for you if any problems arise, and make crime in Yunhai a thing of the past.” Huai Sheng seemed to have figured something out.

Cold sweat broke out on his forehead.

That's right, he had always been thinking about what would happen if the economy suffered, but he hadn't considered this.

It's a stagnant pool, yet beneath that stagnant water, terrifying crimes rage daily, and the number of people traveling to Kyoto to file complaints is almost overwhelmingly from Yunnan province.

Even if they appear peaceful, how could Kyoto not know?

Now, they're simply being given another chance to resolve the situation themselves.

The consequences of getting involved are unimaginable.

Thinking about it this way, even if there are some economic losses, compared to fighting crime, the latter seems to be the most important.

With this in mind, Huai Sheng mentally prepared himself.

He looked up at Jiang Tian, ​​about to say something, but Jiang Tian continued, "Besides, who says Huai Sheng's economy will have problems? This world will keep turning no matter who's gone. Even if the Wen family is gone, someone else will fill the void."

Huai Sheng looked at Jiang Tian with surprise.

"I've already made an appointment with President Su Zhenxing. If President Su is willing to take over the Wenye Group, I think Yun Sheng will not only suffer no losses, but may even rise to a higher level."

This is, after all, Jiang Tian's hometown. If possible, Jiang Tian genuinely wants to do something for his hometown, at least something within his power.

But these words...

Huai Sheng, who had already mentally prepared himself, suddenly looked at Jiang Tian.

"What did you say?" Huai Sheng's face was full of disbelief.

"President Su Zhenxing, I don't think he'll refuse. After all, Wenye Group is a sure-fire way to make money, and if Yunnan Province develops in the future, he'll earn even more," Jiang Tian continued.

Hiss!!!

Huai Sheng was completely shocked.

Jiang Tian's gaze was terrifying.

Too far-sighted.

Could it be...

Even the mess left after dismantling Wenye Group, you've already planned it all out?

If all of this was conceived and executed within a few hours, that's simply too terrifying.

This is practically monstrous.

"Alright, Comrade Jiang, don't worry, you've made a great contribution to Yunnan Province this time, the entire province will remember you." Without further ado, Huai Sheng spoke directly to Jiang Tian.

Then, he gave Jiang Tian his business card and contact information.

He earnestly urged him to persuade Su Zhenxing.

Because Jiang Tian and Su Zhenxing had connections with Su Tong, Jiang Tian believed that Jiang Tian's involvement would increase the chances of success, and naturally, Jiang Tian didn't refuse.

It was a win-win situation.

It must be said that

the impact of the Wenye Group on Yunnan Province was somewhat beyond Jiang Tian's expectations.

Especially this time, it had truly angered Huai Province.

Jiang Tian was in his office.

He watched helplessly as Huai Province, in the dark of evening, had his secretary call each member of the Standing Committee, demanding an immediate meeting that very night.

However, Jiang Tian also knew that Yunnan Province was now at a critical juncture.

The collapse of the Wenye Group would inevitably involve many problems.

Regarding the selection of a successor.

Su Zhenxing was naturally their top choice, but they also needed to consider having a backup plan if Su Zhenxing was unwilling to come.

"Next, I will convene a meeting and prepare for a province-wide crackdown on criminal activity. All those arrested will be punished to the fullest extent of the law; there will be no leniency," Huai Sheng declared, standing up.

Jiang Tian's brow furrowed, for he hadn't expected Huai Sheng to possess such terrifying resolve.

Was it because the Wenye Group had completely crossed Huai Sheng's bottom line?

Jiang Tian looked at the enraged Huai Sheng, lost in thought.

"Sparing no effort to eradicate all illegal and criminal activities." Huai Sheng stood up.

Of course, this was only Huai Sheng's initial plan.

After all, implementing a province-wide crackdown would involve many considerations, and it wouldn't be resolved quickly.

After Huai Sheng left,

Gao Xiaolong turned to Jiang Tian, ​​annoyed, and said, "You little brat, you've gotten too big for your britches! You even kept it from me, not a word from me."

That's right.

Gao Xiaolong had been at the Municipal Bureau.

But such a big matter, such a large-scale plan, and that bastard Jiang Tian hadn't even informed him once.

"I made this decision on the spur of the moment," Jiang Tian coughed.

"This time, I'm going to owe you another first-class merit."

Gao Xiaolong couldn't help but say quietly; this was the most important thing.

This...

Another huge achievement.

The most terrifying thing was that he had made all the top leaders of the entire Yun Province owe him a favor.

Who can withstand this?



Chapter 357 Are you crazy? One first-class merit a day!


After calculating,

Gao Xiaolong was shocked by what he found.

After calculating for a while, he realized Jiang Tian was accumulating almost one first-class merit per day.

Is this some kind of god?

"It's mainly good luck. I didn't expect these criminal groups to be so arrogant. I didn't provoke them; they just jumped out on their own," Jiang Tian said, spreading his hands.

Whether it was the Blood Hand or the Wenye Group, if they hadn't made the first move, Jiang Tian wouldn't have targeted them so easily.

It can only be said that they reaped what they sowed.

"Alright, now gather the men and get to work."

Gao Xiaolong's smile gradually faded.

Joking aside, when it came to work, Gao Xiaolong's true nature was fully revealed.

This kind of temperament...

Jiang Tian thought for a long time and finally used one word to describe it: ruthless.

Gao Xiaolong is truly ruthless and merciless when facing criminal forces; he shows no mercy to anyone.

It was only when Jiang Tian dealt with the Wenye Group that he acted swiftly, planning and executing his schemes quickly. The entire operation was essentially a done deal within hours. Most importantly, while some have been exposed, it's estimated that most of those involved will never discover the truth.

Take, for example, the two thousand kilograms of drugs.

These drug dealers are driven by profit.

Moreover, these drugs, in their possession, are incriminating evidence every moment. Logically, anything could be hoarded, but heroin is something that would never be hoarded.

Since two thousand kilograms of heroin were found stockpiled at the drug factory, it signifies one thing: a serious transaction has definitely taken place recently.

And this transaction…

Jiang Tian's eyes flickered.

This buyer, capable of consuming two thousand kilograms in one go—no, two thousand kilograms only refers to the amount already produced and stockpiled; there's more being produced—essentially brings the total to close to three thousand kilograms.

Anyone with the ability to consume nearly three thousand kilograms of heroin in one go cannot be an unknown figure in Yunhai, or even the entire Yun Province.

These are all major achievements.

In fact, whether Jiang Tian participates in the subsequent arrests or not is irrelevant.

Because he has already secured the credit.

The subsequent smaller fry are also a share of the credit.

However, it's clear that the entire Municipal Public Security Bureau is severely understaffed; in fact, some are doing the work of three people. If Jiang Tian were to leisurely stroll around, it wouldn't look good.

In the pitch-black night.

In the center of the Municipal Public Security Bureau compound, a flag flutters in the wind.

Below the flag.

Nearly three squads stand in neat rows.

Everyone stared intently ahead.

Their eyes shone with an unmistakable brilliance and excitement.

For without a doubt, this was another major operation.

Every operation carried inherent danger, but if safety were the priority, who would choose to join the anti-narcotics unit? Any other police system was safer than the anti-narcotics unit; choosing to join signified lofty ambitions.

Gao Xiaolong stood at the forefront, followed by Jiang Peng and Song Liguo.

However, Song Liguo's expression was grim.

Today, the Provincial Narcotics Control Division had arranged a province-wide meeting of all its narcotics control division chiefs, a targeted meeting addressing the escalating drug crime situation in Yun Province.

The meeting lasted all afternoon.

He had just driven back to the Municipal Bureau.

Good heavens.

Jiang Tian had completely turned things upside down.

He had wiped out the Wenye Group.

Heaven knows what Song Liguo's feelings were, having been completely unaware of this operation.

So much so that Song Liguo's gaze towards Jiang Tian carried a hint of resentment.

Meanwhile,

More than ten police cars were already parked outside the Municipal Bureau, their lights gleaming.

A major operation was about to unfold in Yunhai.

Fortunately.

Although the Wenye Group is a pillar industry of Yunnan Province, Yunhai is the provincial capital, and the group's headquarters are located there.

Therefore, roughly 70-80% of the key personnel are in Yunhai.

The remaining personnel...

We had just discussed whether to coordinate a province-wide operation, but ultimately abandoned the idea because this operation must be swift, accurate, and ruthless, leaving no time for anyone to react.

Once the operation expands to the entire province, there's a risk of information leaks.

That would significantly increase the difficulty of the operation.

"Comrades, the reason I've called you all here tonight is because we discovered a major incident today. The Wenye Group attempted to target Comrade Jiang Tian, ​​but he discovered the plot and uncovered evidence of their crimes. For years, they've been selling large quantities of drugs, harming the people. The Wenye Group's boss has confessed, but a large number of their members remain at large. Our operation now is to arrest all the criminals within the Wenye Group."

Gao Xiaolong announced loudly, his face grim.

Uncovering a massive criminal organization should have been a cause for celebration, but Gao Xiaolong felt no joy whatsoever.

Such a colossal enterprise and group.

An enterprise least likely to be involved in drug crimes, yet it turned out to be such a massive drug lord.

If the Wenye Group is like this,

then how many other forces in Yunnan Province have been innocent, given the province's long-standing, unregulated expansion and support?

"Today there's only one target. Anyone showing signs of resistance, I authorize you to kill them on the spot. Your own safety is paramount. Understand?" Gao Xiaolong roared.

That's right.

Before this.

Jiang Tian was fortunate not to have Gao Xiaolong involved. Everyone knew Gao Xiaolong's personality—he was inflexible.

If Gao Xiaolong had known immediately, he would have reported it to headquarters, directly to Beijing, while Yunnan Province was completely unaware.

The entire Yunnan Province, from top to bottom, would have been in dire straits.

In fact, that's why Huai Province kept saying that almost the entire Yunnan Province owed Jiang Tian a favor.

"Yes!!!"

Everyone shouted in unison on the parade ground.

"Everyone, proceed according to plan," Gao Xiaolong directly issued the order.

Then, he took Jiang Peng directly back to the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

Even Song Liguo had to personally participate in the arrests in this operation because it involved a large number of people.

Due to the extremely heinous nature of the case,

the scope of its impact was also extremely wide.

As for Jiang Tian, ​​the person in charge of the arrest was relatively simple: a vice president of the Wenye Group, a member of the Wen family, and a brother of Wen Jijiang.

On the surface, he appeared clean, but in reality, according to Jia Yun's account, this brother of Wen Jijiang had secretly been involved in large-scale human trafficking on multiple occasions.

He was also involved in drug trafficking, among other things.

He had even been responsible for more than one murder.



Chapter 358 My Second Brother Did So Much for Me


In short.

Jiang Tian understood Ren Daozhong's feelings, and this feeling, far from lessening with the cracking of the case and the capture of the drug lord, actually grew heavier.

"When I thought I had uncovered all the hidden evils, I ultimately discovered that what I had seen was only the tip of the iceberg."

Jiang Tian didn't act immediately because Jiang Ning still needed to be dealt with.

At this moment, Jiang Ning and Guo Na, the two young girls, were standing together in the third-floor corridor of the anti-drug office, next to the green plants. On the soil of those plants, several cigarette butts were about to go out, emitting intermittent smoke.

But at this moment, the two girls were clearly oblivious.

They were simply staring blankly down at the three squads standing under the flag.

One anti-drug squad.

And two assisting armed police squads.

The anti-drug squad alone had about seventy people.

The armed police squad numbered one hundred and fifty.

Of course, the number of armed police officers was only for assisting the anti-drug squad in handling cases.

There's a saying that goes, there's no ceiling to the number of people anti-drug police can call in; in other words, there's no limit.

Within the country, no matter how powerful you are, even an army can call in missile support.

That's the power of anti-drug efforts.

As time passed, soon, after the three squads began their synchronized movements,

Guo Na looked worried.

Because her father was also involved in the operation.

Facing vicious criminals, danger could arise at any moment.

"Jiang Ning, where you're staying now isn't safe anymore. Why don't you come stay at my place for a few days?" Guo Na invited Jiang Ning.

"No, that would be too much of a bother for you. Besides, the people who attacked us have already been caught. I'd better go back to my place," Jiang Ning said after a moment of silence, shaking his head.

Guo Na thought of that small, dark room.

She didn't know how Jiang Ning managed to stay there, and how his grades were still so good.

"Is that kind of house even habitable?"

"All sorts of people live there, and there are even prostitutes nearby. It's too unsafe. It's okay if your uncle is home, but it's too dangerous for a young girl like you," Guo Na kept inviting her.

But Jiang Ning had already experienced the coldness of human nature through Jiang Tian.

After being rejected twice by Jiang Tian, ​​she had become accustomed to self-reliance.

Even her cousin was like this; what about friends with no blood relation or family ties?

"Never mind, it's too late now. I... I'll go home first." Jiang Ning silently lowered her head and walked towards the stairs.

Guo Na stared blankly.

Just now, when Captain Guo left, he told her to wait at the Municipal Bureau for a while, and he would come back to take her home after he finished his mission.

But now it was getting late.

It was past eight o'clock.

"How are you going to get home?" Guo Na chased after her.

Jiang Ning clutched the crumpled change in the pocket of her faded school uniform, biting her lip. It was her allowance for two days.

It was so late; she undoubtedly needed to take a taxi home.

But just then...

Footsteps sounded.

The next moment, Jiang Ning seemed to bump into something, her head throbbing with pain.

Before she could cry out, she was pulled back, nearly falling to the ground.

"Little sister, didn't I tell you to wait inside? Why did you run out?"

Jiang Tian was speechless. Luckily, he hadn't left the operation directly to check on things.

As for his father, Jiang Peng, he was probably sweating bullets right now, planning the entire operation with Gao Xiaolong.

They were controlling the operation's target, and to maintain secrecy, they hadn't even informed the lower-level police stations.

"Second brother, it's getting late. I want to go home first," Jiang Ning said softly, her head down.

"Don't go back," Jiang Tian said directly.

"Second brother?" Jiang Ning looked up in disbelief.

"What are you thinking? Your place is too dangerous, and you've already been targeted by bad guys. Perfect, come stay at my house," Jiang Tian said with a sly smile.

"Go to your house?"

Jiang Ning shook her head frantically: "No need, second brother, you don't need to trouble yourself like that."

"That's an order. If you don't want to be captured and tortured by drug dealers, don't refuse," Jiang Tian said, deliberately putting on a cold face.

Jiang Ning lowered her head even further.

Jiang Tian turned to Song Qiuwan, who had followed him, and said, "Qiuwan, don't come with us for now. Take my second sister home, and also take Miss Guo Na back with you."

"Okay."

Song Qiuwan, who had been standing behind Jiang Tian, ​​nodded repeatedly.

Jiang Ning, watching Jiang Tian's retreating figure as he hurried downstairs without a pause after speaking, stared blankly, lost in thought.

"What are you looking at? Your second brother is leaving. I'll see you off," Song Qiuwan waved.

"Is my second brother...on a mission?" Jiang Ning asked, pursing her lips.

"Yes, your second brother is worried about your safety. Actually, this large-scale operation is also related to you," Song Qiuwan sighed softly.

One wrong step.

The relationship between the two sides finally cracked.

Recently, Song Qiuwan had been trying to mend the rift between her and Jiang Tian, ​​but Jiang Tian seemed to treat her entirely as a subordinate.

It was as if they had nothing more to do with each other.

At first, Song Qiuwan thought Jiang Tian was just being stubborn, and she angrily didn't respond.

But as time passed,

Jiang Tian not only didn't change at all, but he increasingly treated her like a familiar stranger—no, perhaps "unfamiliar subordinate and colleague" would be a more accurate description.

This made Song Qiuwan panic.

As time went on, she realized that the rift in her relationship with Jiang Tian was widening.

Jiang Ning's appearance gave Song Qiuwan some food for thought.

"Because of me?"

At this moment, Jiang Ning was completely dumbfounded.

Hundreds of police officers had been mobilized because of her.

Jiang Ning's little head was completely blank.

"That's right, your second brother has it tough too. Being a narcotics officer is very difficult. If you do something good, you're likely to be retaliated against by drug dealers. Narcotics officers are different from other police officers. Drug dealers are almost always organized and powerful. Once you take action, you'll face retaliation. Your second brother himself has been assassinated but failed. And your second brother's family members are also in danger. Not only are they in danger, but their family members who are in this line of work are also in danger. If those people can't deal with your second brother, they'll turn their attention to your second brother's relatives. Even for your own sake, you should go and stay at your second brother's house. It's safer there."

Song Qiuwan didn't hide anything and comforted Jiang Ning.

Song Qiuwan knew quite well what kind of person Jiang Tian had been before; to put it bluntly, he was a complete scoundrel.

Jiang Ning was such a good girl.

Yet Jiang Tian treated her like a poor relative.

"You mean, this time, it was also because of me that Second Brother went to catch those bad guys?" Jiang Ning's eyes seemed to light up.

"Not entirely, but more or less." Song Qiuwan nodded almost imperceptibly.

Jiang Ning nodded, tears welling in her eyes. At this moment, she felt as if struck by lightning. Second Brother had done so much for her. Had she misunderstood him before?

Of course.

Jiang Tian, ​​naturally, knew nothing of what had happened here.



Chapter 359 The sweeping operation that shook Yunnan Province, the collapse of a behemoth


His task for Song Qiuwan was to bring Jiang Ning there, no matter what, even if it meant kidnapping him.

The Wenye Group was such a powerful force that even a full-scale attack couldn't wipe them all out; there would always be survivors retaliating.

At that point, Jiang Tian's situation would be far more difficult than it was now.

Because this meant a complete declaration of war against all criminal organizations in Yunnan Province.

This time it was the Wenye Group; who would be next?

Nobody knew.

The retaliatory actions Jiang Tian would face would only increase.

But Jiang Tian understood that someone had to do these things.

If he didn't,

someone else would have to bear the consequences.

This operation was far different from the past.

The Wenye Group.

After all, it was a massive, pillar industry.

Almost all the people involved had legitimate identities on the surface.

Most importantly, they wielded enormous influence.

The Dongwenye Group represented an earthquake for the entire Yunnan Province.

This was actually the source of Wen Jijiang's initial confidence.

However, he never dreamed that Jiang Tian would act so quickly and swiftly.

He directly counterattacked and killed him.

The result was that most people were completely unresponsive.

After all, no one expected that the entire Wenye Group would collapse so suddenly and completely without any prior warning.

More importantly, there had been absolutely no prior indication from the provincial authorities or any other party, catching them completely off guard.

Inside a villa.

Wen Jijiang's brother, and one of the major shareholders of the Wenye Group,

Wen Yulin, also one of the heads of the criminal organization, stared blankly at his phone.

"Yulin, don't think too much. Your brother's phone probably just ran out of battery," a woman in a light nightgown comforted Wen Yulin beside him.

“Impossible. My brother’s backup phone is always on; there’s absolutely no way it would run out of power.” Wen Yulin shook his head, his face somewhat gloomy. “I have a bad feeling. Wen Lang being beaten and arrested in the street by that Jiang Tian is simply outrageous.”

“So what? At worst, we can just find a few people to kill him. No matter how arrogant he is, he’s still flesh and blood. One shot and his head will be blown off.” The wealthy woman comforted him from the side.

Wen Yulin’s expression gradually softened.

“Besides, after so many years of development, Wenye Group’s assets are worth far more than several billion. It concerns the livelihoods of hundreds of thousands of people. Don’t even mention the small municipal bureau; even the provincial government wouldn’t allow it. Even if they really wanted to take action, given their efficiency, we would have already run away. There’s not a single word from the province right now.” The wealthy woman continued to comfort him.

The worried expression on Wen Yulin’s face gradually dissipated.

But just then…

Wen Yulin’s phone suddenly rang.

“How’s the matter I asked you to keep an eye on?” Wen Yulin asked directly.

"Boss, the provincial government has urgently convened a standing committee meeting in Huai Province," the voice on the phone said rapidly.

Hiss!!!

"What did you say?" Wen Yulin jumped to his feet.

Like I said, no one is a fool to go to this lengths.

His older brother just went missing.

The provincial government convened an emergency standing committee meeting. He simply couldn't believe these two matters were unrelated.

After all, they were capable of holding a standing committee meeting overnight.

Could it be a small matter?

Hiss!!!

The more Wen Yulin thought about it, the more cold sweat broke out on his forehead.

"Dad, what happened?"

Just then.

On the villa's stairs, a pair of long legs appeared first, followed by a sleepy face, rubbing its eyes and blinking, looking at its father.

"Go back to sleep. Whatever happened, you must not know."

Wen Yulin shouted coldly.

"Dad," the girl said incredulously, because her father had never been so fierce before.

"Minmin, listen to your father. Go back to sleep. If anyone asks you anything, tell them everything you know. If you don't know anything, don't say anything," the woman said, standing up.

Wen Min was stunned.

"What are you standing there for? Go back to sleep!" Wen Yulin snapped.

Just as the words left his mouth,

Waaaaah!!!

A wailing sound of a police siren, like the sound of judgment, echoed in everyone's ears.

As time passed,

the siren grew louder and more piercing.

Sometimes, sirens aren't used during operations and missions.

But they are used during the final stages of a operation.

It also serves as a deterrent to criminals.

The piercing siren of a police car blared.

Wen Yulin slumped onto the sofa, feeling as if all his strength had vanished.

This day had finally come.

Meanwhile, Wen Yulin's phone rang incessantly, wave after wave.

He understood.

The Wenye Group had been taken down.

Wen Yulin's face was filled with bitterness.

The wealthy woman glanced at Wen Yulin, then quickly went to Wen Min's side.

As Wenmin's eyes glistened with tears, vaguely sensing something yet unwilling to confront it, the wealthy woman's trembling hand caressed Wenmin, her voice trembling as she said, "Minmin, listen to me. Wenye Group is going to collapse. But all your father's overseas investments over the years have yielded enormous profits. All those assets are now in your hands. Don't think about saving us anymore; it's useless. You won't have us by your side anymore. You must take good care of yourself, and be careful not to be deceived by men. Live a good life from now on."

As if giving her last instructions, the wealthy woman's voice trembled.

"Mom," Wenmin said, her eyes filled with tears.

Click!

The next moment.

The villa door was pushed open.

Wenmin's vision was blurred with tears.

She watched helplessly as the villa door was forcibly broken open, and a man, or perhaps a boy, with a familiar air about him, but now with a cold, expressionless face, led his entourage in step by step.

Behind him, dozens of police officers and armed police quickly rushed to various corners of the villa to stand guard, their automatic rifles pointed at her father.

"What a heartbreaking scene. If only he had known this would happen."

After a moment, the leading man shook his head with a sneer.

Pity?

Unless he's completely out of his mind.

Anyone who has seen the crimes Wen Yulin committed and the number of people he harmed knows that he deserved his fate.

"You know us so well, having arrested and killed so many major drug lords. Don't you know that sometimes, once you step into this line of work, you're no longer in control of your own destiny?" Wen Yulin said, his lips cracked and his voice hoarse.



Chapter 360 The End of a Tyrant's Life, a Sensation, News of a Special Merit


The tycoon's reign ended.

But for Jiang Tian, ​​there was no psychological burden.

"Every pitiful person has something hateful about them."

That's exactly it.

It wasn't just about selling large quantities of heroin; the Wenye Group's rise was accompanied by countless bloodshed and tragedies, harming countless ordinary people.

It wasn't just about drug crimes; most seriously, it involved connections with foreign forces. This had reached a point where Jiang Tian's other identity could be used to deal with it. He hadn't just crossed a red line; he'd laid it beneath his feet.

"Officer Jiang."

Just as Jiang Tian was about to approach, Wen Yulin suddenly called out Jiang Tian's name loudly, then stood up and said, "I know you. We've been watching you from the beginning. You're a hero of Yunhai."

"Thank you for the compliment."

Jiang Tian's expression remained unchanged. Praise from a drug kingpin?

Yes.

If Wen Jijiang was the ringleader, then according to Jiang Tian's investigation, the most important figure involved was actually Wen Yulin.

Wen Yulin held even more shares than Wen Jijiang.

Jiang Tian personally took charge, even bringing dozens of armed police officers to assist, precisely to prevent any unforeseen circumstances. However, the outcome was positive.

"Officer Jiang, I, Wen Yulin, admit defeat. Falling into your hands is deserved. I told you from the beginning not to lay a hand on me, but the second brother wouldn't listen." Wen Yulin seemed to be experiencing a lot of emotions.

Unfortunately, Jiang Tian didn't have much time to linger with Wen Yulin.

"You've finished speaking. Now you should come with us. After all, there are many people involved in the case within your Wenye Group, and we still need to thoroughly investigate your group. We have a lot of work to do and don't have time to listen to a tycoon's final lament." Jiang Tian's expression remained unwavering.

"Why didn't you think this way earlier?"

Not only did they harm so many people, but they even targeted him, and even his family.

Now, if it's not Wen Yulin who's falling, then it's Jiang Tian.

Jiang Tian had already failed once in his previous life, a failure that taught him a painful lesson.

Since he didn't want to fail, he would continue to win.

"Officer Jiang, I deserve my punishment, and Wenye Group also deserves its punishment, but my daughter Wen Min is innocent. She knew nothing about these things and wasn't involved. She just graduated this year."

Just graduated this year?

Jiang Tian frowned.

Jiang Tian glanced at the figure around the corner, then calmly turned back. "You'll have to explain these things to the Municipal Public Security Bureau. I'm just a team leader; I have no right to make any judgments."

"No, others may not have the right, but you do. You are the first person in peacetime to receive a Special Merit Citation, a pioneer in history, and a recipient of multiple First-Class Merits—a legend in Yunhai's police force," Wen Yulin said in a deep voice.

As Wen Yulin finished speaking,

at the corner of the stairs, surrounded by several automatic rifles, Wen Min, her face blank and bewildered, looked at Jiang Tian with a look of utter astonishment.

The phrase "a legend in the police force" was simply unbelievable to her.

Especially since Wen Min was already shocked and at a loss the moment she saw Jiang Tian.

Hearing the words "a legend in the police force" only deepened her bewilderment.

Why was she so shocked?

Although Jiang Tian didn't recognize her,

Wen Min recognized him.

High school, after all!

Yunhai only had a few top-tier high schools.

Plus, Jiang Tian and Wen Min were roughly the same age, naturally in the same grade, though not in the same class.

This was why Jiang Tian didn't recognize her and didn't know her at all.

Wen Min, on the other hand, recognized him.

This was naturally due to the principal's public humiliation of Jiang Tian in front of the entire school, publicly shaming him for a future destined for construction work.

They were told that if they didn't do well in school, they'd end up working as construction workers with Jiang Tian, ​​and so on.

Wen Min had vivid memories of this classmate back then. Years had passed, but Jiang Tian's appearance hadn't changed much, so she recognized him immediately.

But it was precisely because she recognized him that Wen Min found it unbelievable.

The Jiang Tian who had been dragged out by the principal as a model student for the whole school—a terrible example, of course—the Jiang Tian whose best friend had teased her about, saying he'd definitely be a good-for-nothing in the future.

Now, he was a legend in the police force?

"You flatter me. A legend in the police force? I've never heard of that. And you have absolutely no right to discuss any terms with me right now," Jiang Tian said expressionlessly.

Hearing the words "legend in the police force," he showed no emotional fluctuation.

This made Wen Yulin, who had been watching intently, gasp.

He had investigated Jiang Tian's background. Just over twenty years old, the same age as his daughter, yet he possessed such a terrifying temperament.

A young man, achieving such great merit, so brilliant—he must have risen to the heavens.

Despite such praise, Jiang Tian's expression remained unchanged—a testament to his remarkable composure and self-control.

It can only be said, he wasn't wronged.

Their defeat was entirely justified.

Wen Yulin shook his head and said, "I'll use a piece of information to exchange for your promise."

"Information?" Jiang Tian's heart skipped a beat.
