"That's right, very important information. If you succeed, you might even receive a Special Merit Citation." Wen Yulin continued.

Hmm?

Jiang Tian pondered slightly.

Special Merit Citation?

What kind of information could be related to a Special Merit Citation?

You see, Jiang Tian recently conducted a night raid covering hundreds of miles, traversing mountains and forests, rescuing so many comrades and killing so many people, yet he didn't receive a Special Merit Citation, only a nomination for First-Class Heroic Model, which was considered a solid achievement, and no one could say anything.

With good luck, he might even get a First-Class Merit Citation.

Of course, being named a model hero likely means losing the First-Class Merit Citation.

However, Jiang Tian does deserve credit for saving lives.

The Special-Class Merit Citation is incredibly important to Jiang Tian.

Of course, even if not for himself, even if it were solely for the sake of the people, he couldn't ignore it.

"You really trust me that much with my promise?" Jiang Tian asked.

Wen Yulin gave a bitter smile and said, “You understand, in our line of work, we offend many people, both openly and secretly. You anti-drug police are in danger, and none of us are safe either. Dealing with those desperate criminals, we might be betrayed and falsely accused. When they're powerful, they won't dare do anything, but once they lose power, if I die, so be it—I deserve it. But my daughter is innocent.”

“If that's the case, why don't you send your daughter abroad?”

Just then, Lü Chun, standing beside Jiang Tian, ​​couldn't help but show a curious expression.

The next moment…



Chapter 361 No one would believe it except you, the legend of the police force.


Lu Chun noticed that both Wen Yulin and Jiang Tian were looking at him like he was an idiot.

Lu Chun's expression stiffened.

"Officer, you're joking. Where could I possibly go? If I went abroad and got found, I'd probably be gone without a trace. Staying in the country is actually safer," Wen Yulin said with a bitter smile.

That's right.

If the simple-minded Wen Min went abroad, she'd probably be reduced to ashes.

If Wen Yulin managed to escape with her, with an old fox like him around, he could protect Wen Min.

However, without Wen Yulin, Wen Min would be like a walking treasure trove, like a baby walking through the market carrying a large sum of money.

Anyone who saw her would be tempted to take advantage.

Within the entire Wenye Group family business, if only Wen Min were out there, even a fool would know she was incredibly wealthy.

It's hard to believe that such a behemoth wouldn't leave any money for its family. From now on, countless eyes will be watching Wen Min.

They wanted to seize the enormous wealth that Wenye Group had left behind on Wen Min.

“I just want a promise: once we’re inside, will you, the god of the police force, protect my daughter’s safety?” Wen Yulin said, his eyes fixed on Jiang Tian.

Jiang Tian squinted, glancing at the long-legged Wen Min.

Then he looked at Wen Yulin again.

Indeed, Jiang Tian had already scanned the area with his Judgment Eye, and Wen Min was truly innocent.

If Wen Min was innocent,

at worst, Jiang Tian would just keep a closer eye on her and protect her in the future.

"Okay, I promise you, once you're inside, I will protect your daughter's safety. No one can harm your daughter." Jiang Tian nodded.

This was a clue to a huge case.

Protecting a defenseless young girl, especially one who was truly innocent, gave Jiang Tian no qualms.

"But, you trust me that much?" Jiang Tian asked, puzzled.

Even his sister and cousin had nearly been in danger.

Jiang Tian himself had been attacked with TNT by someone hired by Mr. Yun.

If it weren't for Jiang Tian's special abilities, he would have died countless times.

Therefore, Jiang Tian understood even more deeply...

"Hahahaha..."

Wen Yulin laughed heartily, "I would never believe what others say, but I wholeheartedly believe what you, Jiang Tian, ​​this legend of the police force, say. Most importantly, I believe you, and only you can protect my daughter."

Thump, thump, thump!!!

Accompanied by heavy footsteps,

Jiang Tian walked step by step to Wen Yulin's side, then stopped after a moment.

"It's truly my honor. Now, tell me your news."

Jiang Tian looked at Wen Yulin: "You've obtained my promise, but I hope your news is worthy of the price you've paid."

"Come closer," Wen Yulin said.

"Boss."

Lu Chun pulled Jiang Tian back, somewhat worried.

Mainly, if something happened to Jiang Tian here, or if Wen Yulin had some ulterior motive, Lu Chun would be truly dumbfounded.

After all, being caught off guard and losing one's life isn't impossible.

"It's alright."

Jiang Tian shook off Lü Chun. What a joke! To be defeated by Wen Yulin like this was a death sentence for Jiang Tian.

Jiang Tian's equipment stats were now all top-tier.

Except for the red stat "Martial Arts Master," attributes like Physique were all gold-tier.

And there was "Unfailing Accuracy."

Jiang Tian moved closer to Wen Yulin, who stared intently at him. Seeing Jiang Tian approach without any fear,

Wen Yulin sighed. He knew his defeat was justified, not at all.

The legend of the police force lived up to his name.

However, this also made him feel more at ease.

Wen Yulin didn't use any tricks, whispering something in Jiang Tian's ear.

At first, Jiang Tian's expression didn't change, but as time passed, his expression grew colder and colder.

He was increasingly shocked.

Jiang Tian, ​​having handled countless drug cases and experienced so much, could be considered a seasoned veteran, having seen it all.

However, the information he received from Wen Yulin completely shattered his understanding.

What surprised Jiang Tian even more was the sheer sophistication of the Wenye Group's drug trafficking network.

Without dismantling the Wenye Group, he only knew that the entire Yunhai, and even the entire Yunnan province, was practically infiltrated by criminal organizations.

By day, Yunhai is a bustling first-tier city.

By night.

This is Gotham.

In the pitch-black night, countless crimes occur every moment.

Especially with the number of missing immigrants increasing annually, the evil hasn't disappeared beneath the surface of increasing calm; their darkness is simply too vast.

Vast to the point of being terrifyingly all-encompassing.

They have practically created an entire network and structure of criminal organizations, with seamless cooperation and mutual concealment between various links and channels.

"Of course."

It's impossible to obtain evidence against those criminal groups from the Wenye Group alone.

Although their united front, like the eighteen warlords opposing Dong Zhuo, appears powerful,

the infighting behind the scenes is never lacking.

"So, does your Wenye Group have evidence of other groups' crimes?" Jiang Tian inquired.

"We have collected some, but unfortunately, not much. We all know what the other side is thinking, so we won't actively collect it. But we do know who they are; it just depends on whether you have the ability to act," Wen Yulin said.

"Don't you understand your current situation? With me here, the entire Yunhai will not tolerate any criminal forces," Jiang Tian grinned.

Click!!!

The next moment, Jiang Tian handcuffed Wen Yulin.

Then he handcuffed the lady.

Although Jiang Tian didn't interrogate her,

the lady did have criminal records.

Jiang Tian took a look with his Eye of Judgment and found the situation to be relatively good; the sin value wasn't high.

It was essentially a charge of failing to report a crime.

This charge, even without personally participating in profiting from directing others' actions, can only be considered a cover-up. Given the special nature of the Wenye Group, they might be able to get away with it under normal circumstances.

But now, they'll be in jail for at least a year and a half.

As for Wen Yulin, now that he's in, he'll never get out.

There's no possibility of a suspended death sentence. As a prime example, a bullet is inevitable, unless he continues to provide valuable intelligence and assist Yunhai in solving more cases, thus redeeming himself and perhaps receiving a suspended death sentence.

It can only be said that the charges are extremely serious.



Chapter 362 Ruthless and Insane, Their True Colors Completely Exposed, They Dare to Defy the Heavens


"Officer Jiang, I'm counting on you." The wealthy woman nodded to Jiang Tian.

Perhaps they were mentally prepared when they started this line of work.

"Boss, what about this person?" Lü Chun asked Jiang Tian.

Jiang Tian looked at Wen Min.

"This person is fine, don't handcuff him," Jiang Tian said.

The Eye of Judgment is absolutely reliable.

No one could possibly hide within the Eye of Judgment.

Jiang Tian also knew one thing.

That is, those successful criminals, even drug lords, like the Yun family in Myanmar,

always leave behind an innocent person.

These people, without a doubt, have clean backgrounds.

They have absolutely no involvement in the family's businesses, and possess all the clean wealth left by their families.

This money has been with these people from the beginning, and from start to finish, it has had absolutely no connection with crime; no investigation can prove it clean.

They've done too many bad things.

There's always a backup plan.

Jiang Tian was already used to it.

"Thank you," Wen Min said, looking at Jiang Tian with gratitude.

"I know you're innocent, but I hope you can learn from your parents' mistakes in the future," Jiang Tian said.

"Officer Jiang, please rest assured," Wen Min hurriedly replied.

Wen Min wasn't stupid.

After the series of events that had just occurred, Wen Min understood that her family's business likely had huge problems, and even involved horrific criminal activities.

Otherwise, why would she have brought Jiang Tian the news of a special merit award?

"However, you'll need to come back to the police station with us to give a statement. After you come back, I suggest you don't stay here anymore. Find a more secluded place, at least for now," Jiang Tian said.

Because of the collapse of the Wenye Group, the entire Yunhai, and even the entire Yun Province, will undoubtedly be plunged into chaos.

Just like if Jiang Tian had eliminated a major drug lord.

The price of heroin on the market would have risen.

Meanwhile, some areas have experienced shortages.

Drug addicts, after running out of supplies, are capable of anything, and dare to do anything.

Therefore, it's foreseeable that, not only the municipal bureau, but the entire police system will be extremely busy for the foreseeable future.

Jiang Tian certainly can't spare any time for Wen Min right now.

So, he wants Wen Min to find a safe and secluded place to hide first.

"From now on, if you want to be safe, don't trust anyone, don't trust anyone at all. Cut off all contact with the outside world until this case is over," Jiang Tian instructed after a moment's thought.

"Thank you. My family bought me a house outside. My dad said no one knows, so I'm going there," Wen Min said.

"It's up to you."

Jiang Tian nodded, then closed his eyes to rest.

At this moment.

Wen Min sat in the Volkswagen Santana police car, feeling somewhat uneasy.

After all, this was the first time she had ridden in a police car in her entire life, and the events of the day had left her physically and mentally exhausted.

"Boss, what news did Wen Yulin just say?"

As the driver, Lü Chun couldn't suppress his curiosity.

After all, he didn't know about anything else, but he clearly heard the words "Special Merit."

"Don't ask what you shouldn't ask."

Jiang Tian didn't even open his eyes, speaking expressionlessly.

Lü Chun, sitting in the driver's seat, scratched his head nervously.

After all, the Special Merit was for the leader; as a team member, it wouldn't be unreasonable for him to receive a First-Class Merit.

And for the team as a whole, a First-Class Merit wouldn't be unreasonable either.

Seeing Jiang Tian resting with his eyes closed, Lü Chun swallowed hard, but ultimately didn't ask.

Chu Jing, in the passenger seat, didn't ask, but the bright light in her eyes betrayed her inner turmoil.

Good heavens.

Who could resist?

How could anyone resist such a great achievement?

But they didn't know.

At this moment, Jiang Tian's mind was also racing.

Although outwardly calm, deep inside Jiang Tian was in turmoil.

Thinking of the news Wen Yulin had mentioned, a dark cloud settled over Jiang Tian's heart.

This is a serious problem.

Just as Jiang Tian was thinking this,

the car slammed on the brakes.

"What happened?" Jiang Tian opened his eyes.

He'd been too tired these past few days, and he'd been preoccupied with the news Wen Yulin had told him, so he hadn't noticed what was happening ahead.

"There's been a car accident up ahead, a traffic jam," Lü Chun said.

"A traffic jam?"

Jiang Tian's eyes snapped open.

He glanced over and, sure enough, right in front of their vehicle, a van that had darted out from a side road had collided with a car.

The front of both vehicles was almost completely destroyed; the impact was tremendous.

The two vehicles were now blocking the middle of the road.

And the van was overturned.

"I'll get out and check," Lü Chun said, opening the car door.

"Wait."

Jiang Tian grabbed Lü Chun's arm.

At this moment, by such coincidence, they collided.

The two cars collided, and not even beforehand.

But...

"This is bad."

Jiang Tian's expression changed abruptly.

"Boss, what's wrong?" Lü Chun also sensed something was amiss.

"Did the car carrying Wen Yulin just pass by?" Jiang Tian asked.

"We left last, they left first."

Lü Chun nodded blankly; he hadn't yet noticed anything wrong.

Jiang Tian's gaze darkened.

Sure enough.

This time.

They deployed a total of twelve vehicles.

The detainee was in a large nine-seater armed police vehicle, the kind with bars welded to the windows—everyone knows that's used for detaining people.

Jiang Tian, ​​because he was with Wen Jing, was a little late and followed at the very back of the convoy.

At this moment.

Although six or seven armed police vehicles were blocked ahead.

But there were still five or six armed police vehicles.

"These people are really courting death!"

Jiang Tian sneered.

Initially, Jiang Tian was somewhat skeptical of Wen Yulin's words; after all, the matter was too significant, and he was reluctant to believe it.

He only intended to investigate the truth later.

But he never expected this.

Those people acted so quickly and swiftly.

Even more unexpected was their madness.

They had even reached a point of lawlessness.

But.

This is the true criminal force.

Jiang Tian thought of his past life. Three consecutive bureau chiefs of Yunhai were all parachuted in. Each initially stabilized the situation and assessed the circumstances within ten to fifteen days, then launched swift action, each causing a huge sensation.

But then they all died suddenly.

Some died from sudden illness.

Others from car accidents.

The most outrageous was that some even committed suicide by jumping from buildings.

The crucial point is that their remains were even found in their rooms.

Just imagine how outrageous that could be.

And this actually happened in Jiang Tian's past life. Three consecutive bureau chiefs died violent deaths, enraging the capital.

The military completely sealed off all roads, large and small, along the border.

Internally, a ruthless crackdown was launched.

This is why Yunhai's economy stagnated for decades, ultimately declining further.

“Captain Wang, quick, turn back the way we came, there’s danger…” Jiang Tian dialed the armed police.

“Danger?”

Almost before Jiang Tian finished speaking…

Boom!!!

A terrifying, deafening roar suddenly resounded from afar.

In the darkness, the roar was especially loud and deafening.

Flames illuminated Jiang Tian's face.



Chapter 363 The Driver Blocking the Road? A Police Baton Saw the Too-Mother


Captain Wang was one of the armed police escorts.

Normally, such operations are conducted by the armed police, with Jiang Tian and his team primarily responsible for the arrests and command.

This is why they escorted Wen Yulin and his wife ahead of them from the beginning, leaving Jiang Tian and Wen Min only a traffic light or two late.

Mainly, Jiang Tian himself hadn't expected such a ruthless act; it went far beyond mere ruthlessness.

Who would have thought they'd dare use a bomb in a busy urban area?

Of course, this wasn't Jiang Tian underestimating them; it was mainly their arrogance and recklessness that Jiang Tian found unbelievable.

Are they crazy?

While there's a saying that goes, "Heaven will destroy those it desires, first it will drive them mad,"

however,

this kind of madness absolutely does not include daring to use such a large-scale bomb in the bustling downtown area of ​​a first-tier city.

This can no longer be described as mere criminal activity; it even qualifies as a terrorist attack.

This is a completely different concept from drug enforcement.

But…

“How is this possible?” Lü Chun stood beside Jiang Tian, ​​staring incredulously at the distant flames, her eyes filled with shock and disbelief.

They’ve gone mad.

“These people have completely lost their minds.” Jiang Tian’s expression remained unchanged.

Illuminated by the firelight, Jiang Tian’s face was cold and expressionless.

In his past life…

Think about it, could a few simple criminals possibly affect the development of a city? Impossible, utterly impossible.

And to bring a city’s economic development to a standstill, a large-scale deployment of the military for control would be necessary.

This speaks volumes.

It's not that these criminal groups are brainless; it's that they've been local tyrants in Yunhai, or rather, Yunnan Province, for far too long.

They've even begun to believe that no one can stop them.

In fact, this isn't the first time they've done something similar—like the bombing.

But this time, the nature of the attack is completely different. This isn't an arrest; it's a large-scale attack on the armed police.

"How many people are in the convoy?" Jiang Tian asked.

"Our armed police convoy has about thirty people, about the size of a battalion," Lü Chun replied after a moment's thought.

Thirty-odd people, seven or eight vehicles.

But the vehicle holding Wen Yulin and his wife was a clear target.

"Quickly, gather everyone! And arrest both drivers!"

Jiang Tian immediately gave the order.

In fact, during this period, the distance between the two sides had increased after the initial one or two traffic lights, but after the vehicles stopped.

The other side had deliberately positioned themselves at this point.

In other words, from the very beginning of the operation, the other side knew everything about them.

They dared to attack over thirty armed police officers.

But they had to stop Jiang Tian.

Why? Was it simply fear, or was there another reason?

Or perhaps, they were afraid that his presence would cause some kind of accident.

"Hurry, hurry, hurry!"

Jiang Tian didn't even need to do anything to the driver of the vehicle; these kinds of people are usually terminally ill or desperately need money.

These people all shared one characteristic: they came from remote areas and had never been exposed to the outside world.

While they might not be common elsewhere, in 2003, Yunnan Province was teeming with people from remote areas who had never experienced the outside world.

Besides that...

Jiang Tian's gaze fell into deep thought.

Generally, these kinds of people would obediently follow the instructions of criminals if given some money or something.

For example, they might pay you ten thousand or twenty thousand yuan to drive a car to a specific time and place to collide with another vehicle.

You wouldn't be held responsible for anything else, and so on.

However, legally, ignorance is no excuse.

When Jiang Tian arrived at the van, Lü Chun had already called for help, and together they moved the medium-sized van to the side of the road, at least enough for one vehicle to pass.

"What are you doing? What are you doing? Why did you touch my car? Who gave you permission to touch my car?"

The van driver, still in the driver's seat, continued to try and stop them.

Sure enough.

Jiang Tian glanced at him; there was no red aura emanating from the van driver, meaning he had never committed a crime.

He seemed like an innocent person.

However.

Jiang Tian walked over.

At this moment, the driver had gotten out of the van. He was a young man, probably in his thirties, and was preventing the others from moving the vehicle.

"Who told you to touch my car? Are you traffic police? If you're not, who told you to move it? Move the car back!"

The driver cursed and shoved Lü Chun.

This made Lü Chun look at Jiang Tian.

If this were before, Lü Chun would have already taken action, but now it was different. As a narcotics officer, he didn't have the same level of authority as before.

Of course, in this situation...

"Are you their leader? What are you doing? Let me tell you, even if you're police, you can't just move a car like that. I've been in an accident, and I need to wait for the traffic police."

Seeing Jiang Tian approach aggressively, the driver's tone gradually weakened, but he still steeled himself and blocked Jiang Tian's path, standing in front of him.

Swish!!!

Jiang Tian drew his baton.

"What do you want? I'm filming you!" the driver said, holding the camera up to Jiang Tian, ​​his forehead covered in cold sweat.

But the next moment.

Bang!!!

The driver's vision went black instantly, then he crashed to the ground with a thud. Before him, his great-grandmother, who had passed away many years ago, appeared.

The recording camera also fell to the ground.

In the moments before losing consciousness, the driver gradually heard Jiang Tian's voice.

"I believe you may not have been involved, but receiving excessive compensation for providing cover-ups and concealment for criminals, especially extremely egregious ones, and for obstructing official duties resulting in serious consequences, ultimately receiving a huge reward from the criminals."

The driver's heart skipped a beat.

Jiang Tian was right.

Or rather, for Jiang Tian, ​​who possessed the "King of Criminal Investigation" title and was thoroughly familiar with criminal psychology, this kind of criminal tactic was all too familiar.

Just as Jiang Tian thought, he was simply an ordinary person from a remote mountain village.

One day, a group of people approached him, offering him tens of thousands of yuan for a few simple tasks.

Tens of thousands of yuan was an enormous sum to him.

Even a few thousand yuan would be astronomical.

The task was so simple: just stop a group of people.

Simply drive out at a specific time, crash into another car, and block the road for a short time.

Such a simple task, and he was offered so much money? He agreed without a second thought.

But the end result was as it is now.

He thought nothing had happened.

He thought he hadn't committed any crime.

But the law doesn't allow ignorance to excuse wrongdoing.



Chapter 364 The Outsider Awakens, Watching the Turbulent Times Rise


“Just like when you were enforcing the law before, there's no need to hold back on the intensity of drug enforcement,” Jiang Tian said directly.

He understood Lü Chun's meaning.

After all, they were now in different clothes. Although they were still part of a government department, the uniforms they wore belonged to the police system. They were probably worried that if they made a mistake and were exposed, the consequences would be too severe.

But Jiang Tian could only say that they were overthinking it.

Normal methods were no longer enough to deal with these vicious criminals.

“Besides, after they made their move, they should have realized something.”

Jiang Tian sat in the police car, his gaze deep.

“What is it?” Chu Jing asked curiously.

“That is, normal drug enforcement requires evidence of the perpetrators being caught red-handed, but counter-terrorism only requires identification, not evidence.” Jiang Tian said.

That's right.

This was no longer a drug enforcement operation.

Jiang Tian simply couldn't understand how those people could be so audacious.

Using various methods of assassination and murder in the shadows was one thing.

They even dared to use such heinous methods so blatantly.

And it's foreseeable.

What terrifying storm of retribution awaits them next.

Police cars roared past.

Jiang Tian and his team only used two police cars for their anti-drug operation.

One officer remained to guard the unconscious driver. As for the other driver, Jiang Tian saw he had no criminal record, but he also detained him for now.

Both men might seem fine now, but they could both have issues. It's possible both drivers involved in the crash received bribes.

But if they profited from it,

they'll face life imprisonment.

If there wasn't a major impact, that's one thing.

But such a terrifying impact...

Such a severe impact, the minimum sentence would be at least ten years.

Even more serious, life imprisonment is entirely possible.

"These people have gone mad," Lü Chun said, driving towards the explosion site, completely ignoring traffic lights.

Actually, the distance from where they were stuck in traffic was at least three kilometers.

Three kilometers is already quite a distance.

Lu Chun drove at full speed, and it took him a few minutes to arrive.

Meanwhile, Jiang Tian was constantly dialing Captain Wang's number.

Captain Wang was the team leader this time.

However, after the explosion, he had been completely unreachable; no matter how many times he called, he couldn't get through.

"Faster."

Every second could bring a major change.

Jiang Tian didn't dare to delay for even a moment.

Just then, Jiang Tian's phone rang.

Jiang Tian took it out and glanced at it, discovering it was a phone number from Huai Province. He had saved Huai Province's phone number beforehand, so he recognized it immediately.

"Huai Province," Jiang Tian quickly said.

"Jiang Tian, ​​what exactly happened? I just held an executive meeting, and suddenly there's been a huge explosion in the city center! And according to those who reported it, there are already many police cars. I asked the city bureau, and they said it's in that area. Are you the only one there with your men right now?"

Provincial Office.

At this very moment.

A profound silence fell.

All those attending the meeting remained completely silent.

The spacious, solemnly decorated office contained over a dozen single sofas.

At this moment, the sofas were filled with people.

Even Shen Han, at this moment, could only sit halfway on the sofa, slightly hunched over as if listening intently.

At the head of the table sat Huai Sheng, holding a mobile phone and dialing Jiang Tian.

It was important to know that

After thoroughly understanding the criminal activities of the Wenye Group, Huai Sheng was extremely alarmed. Immediately, while ordering all personnel to make arrests, he convened an emergency meeting, summoning all the key provincial leaders for an important discussion.

The meeting's content concerned the future of Yun Province.

Currently, Yun Province's economic development was not optimistic. Although it seemed to have Yunhai as a pillar city serving as its face, the surrounding areas still struggled to drive development, especially Yunhai itself, whose development had gradually stagnated.

A crucial factor in this is the recent policy issued by Kyoto to vigorously promote the development of first- and second-tier cities, strongly support their economic growth, and encourage the "let the rich get richer" approach to help those less fortunate.

The strategy centers on first-tier cities, gradually driving the development of surrounding cities to achieve common prosperity.

To this end, Kyoto has provided substantial political and economic support to first-tier cities.

However, what is particularly noteworthy is that Yunhai is not included in this list of cities benefiting from the "let the rich get richer" economic development initiative.

Yunnan Province was already facing a dire situation, and then this problem with the Wenye Group emerged, immediately drawing opposition from several leaders.

They believed that at this crucial moment, with Yunhai Province working on its list of the wealthiest individuals, no problems could be tolerated; stability was paramount.

If the Wenye Group were to collapse, and a complete crackdown were launched against it, hundreds of thousands of people would lose their jobs, making it even harder to be selected for the national lottery.

Even in Kyoto, doubts might arise about their capabilities, potentially leading to a halt in their support and development of Yunhai.

Even if action is to be taken, it should be delayed, at least for a period of time, until Yunhai's inclusion on the list is definitively confirmed.

That's why it's said this way:

Those involved are often confused, while bystanders see things clearly.

After speaking with Jiang Tian and receiving confirmation from Gao Xiaolong, Huai Sheng was utterly shocked.

Previously, they instinctively covered everything up, regardless of the situation.

Everything else could be put on hold.

No matter what happened, stability and order were always the top priority.

They thought the reason he wasn't on the list was because of the chaos in Yunhai, but quite the opposite was true. It was precisely because Yunhai wasn't chaotic enough that criminals were rampant, and the number of victims was increasing, making the situation increasingly serious.

Ironically, they were going against the thinking of those above them.

The key point was that the higher-ups couldn't openly discuss this kind of issue.

Does this mean that the more trouble you cause, the more people you arrest, the bigger the trouble becomes, and naturally, the more support you'll receive?

But Jiang Tian's words jolted him awake, followed by Gao Xiaolong's question: "Do you expect Kyoto to allocate a huge amount of its economy to develop a Gotham City, only to let all the money go to the criminals, further strengthening their power?"



Chapter 365 The meeting erupted in chaos; who dared to be so ruthless?


That last sentence.

For Huai Sheng, it was the decisive blow.

Before this, everyone was against it, and Huai Sheng was still trying to figure out how to persuade them.

Then, Old Mu, who had almost fully recovered and was attending the meeting, received a call from the city's political and legal leadership.

It turned out that an emergency call had reached the city bureau.

The city bureau immediately reported it to Jiang Peng.

Jiang Peng was startled and utterly shocked. He quickly reported the situation and information to Old Mu.

Normally, this kind of thing should have been reported to the city's political and legal leadership, but unfortunately, those leaders had been transferred.

The position was now vacant, waiting for Jiang Peng to take over.

It was under these circumstances that Jiang Peng was able to call Old Mu immediately.

When Old Mu received the call, he emphasized that Jiang Tian was likely the one carrying out the operation in that area.

He suggested asking Jiang Tian.

As for Jiang Peng, he had no time for that now.

Reinforcements from the Armed Police were immediately dispatched.

The Municipal Public Security Bureau is now severely understaffed, largely due to the recent surge in cases, with one major case after another.

Not only the Narcotics Control Unit, but also other units and their personnel are almost entirely mobilized.

Not to mention the Municipal Public Security Bureau's manpower.

Even a significant portion of the Armed Police has been drawn from the force for this operation.

Now, even the Armed Police are facing a personnel shortage.

Jiang Peng, on one hand, quickly reported to his superiors.

On the other hand, he swiftly contacted Armed Police Officer Chen to urgently dispatch reinforcements to the explosion site.

However, currently,

all that is known is that a large explosion occurred; Jiang Peng himself doesn't know the specifics.

But this doesn't hinder him; Jiang Peng is currently bewildered.

He was about to be promoted.

Couldn't it have been delayed by a few days?

If it had been delayed a few days, allowing him to get promoted first, and then these problems would have arisen.

This was a major incident; one wrong move and Jiang Peng would bear significant responsibility.

Strict investigation could even lead to his dismissal as the city bureau chief.

How could he not panic?

Therefore, after Jiang Peng reported to Elder Mu, Elder Mu took it extremely seriously and immediately reported it to Huai Province.

This instantly interrupted the debate in the meeting.

After all, most people were still sticking to their opinions, believing that stability was paramount. However, they didn't know that Huai Province was sitting firmly on the sidelines, clearly aware of what had happened but not revealing it immediately.

Until Elder Mu's voice rang out, all the discussion ceased instantly, and all eyes turned to Huai Province.

Huai Province didn't delay either, immediately and extremely seriously calling Jiang Tian to find out the situation on the spot.

What? An explosion, why did it directly reach Huai Province, and why was the entire meeting room completely silent?

What a joke! The same explosion depends on where it happens.

In remote areas, no one might even notice.

In third- or fourth-tier cities, action would be taken immediately in the city center.

But in the provincial capital, you have to understand, the provincial government offices are located nearby!

"Explosion! What an explosion!"

"Hiss, I just heard a loud bang, but I didn't pay much attention. It turned out to be an explosion!"

"I heard it too."

"Are you crazy? Where did that explosion come from? Aren't hazardous materials not allowed in the city?"

A murmur of discussion rippled through the air.

At that moment, Huai Sheng also dialed Jiang Tian's number.

It was obvious.

Even they heard the explosion, so it probably wasn't too far away, maybe ten or twenty miles at most.

That's the real core urban area!

A huge explosion had occurred! Huai Sheng's heart skipped a beat.

He dialed Jiang Tian's number and put it on speakerphone so the leaders could hear the details, saving Huai Sheng the time of explaining.

"What caused the explosion? What are the casualties?"

In accidents, or rather, in situations where problems arise, regardless of the organization, the maximum number of casualties is always three.

In other words, incidents involving three or fewer people can be handled simply, but injuries or fatalities involving more than three people constitute a major accident, requiring immediate reporting to the provincial level and even the capital city for backup.

“I’m on my way to the scene. The casualty situation is still unclear,” Jiang Tian quickly replied.

He was very close.

Jiang Tian could already see some flames.

They were at the foot of a cliff.

That’s why it’s said.

Although the entire Yunhai area has been extensively developed,

some mountains within the city remain that are difficult to modify or where foundations cannot be driven. Some of these mountains have been turned into parks, while others, due to their historical significance, have been preserved as scenic areas.

The route of the accident was a ring road carved out of the mountain.

Of course, due to long-term development, it no longer holds many secrets. The mountain is mostly covered in forest, but it still has an eight-way road. Several residential complexes have been built along the mountainside, and they were traveling on the road behind these complexes.

The accident occurred here.

"It's still unclear. Investigate as quickly as possible to find out what caused the explosion, what the explosive was, and the extent of the casualties. Report back to me immediately after you've ascertained the situation," Huai Sheng said thoughtfully.

"Reporting to Huai Sheng, although I don't know the casualties, I know the explosion wasn't an accident, but a deliberate bomb," Jiang Tian said directly.

BOOM!!!

Huai Sheng was stunned.

All the leaders fell silent instantly.

"Nonsense! This is a busy area, who would dare do such a thing?" one leader said, his face darkening.

Unfortunately, Jiang Tian was too far away to hear clearly on the phone, but continued, "Furthermore, judging from the sound and power of the explosion, it wasn't ordinary explosives; it was likely a significant amount of TNT."

BOOM!!!

"Nonsense! TNT, please!"

"Who would dare to be so audacious?"

"That's right, isn't this suicide? Catch the killer immediately!"

"How could this be? This is the Municipal Public Security Bureau! Who would dare to do something like this? What are they after? Just to cause a massive explosion? Isn't this suicidal? How could anyone be so stupid? This is absolutely impossible."

It exploded.

Even the leader's conference room exploded.

Huai Sheng's mind went blank for a moment, then his blood boiled. Just as he was about to issue an order for a thorough investigation to Jiang Tian in a fit of rage, Jiang Tian's voice rang out again.

"Moreover, the TNT explosion was deliberate, not to cause chaos, but to attack us. Some comrades may have already been killed."

BOOM!!!

Accompanying these words,

all the leaders exploded with a deafening roar.

An attack on the armed police?

A TNT attack.

And it involved the armed police and the anti-drug squad.

Have they gone mad?

Who is it?



Chapter 366 The New Boss is Completely Bewildered


"I was just about to say that we just arrested Wen Yulin, a key suspect in the Wenye Group operation. The assailants attacked the vehicle carrying Wen Yulin and his wife, and we're currently en route to the scene."

Jiang Tian quickly revealed the part closest to the truth.

Unbeknownst to Jiang Tian, ​​at this very moment, in the dignified conference room, his voice was utterly silenced.

Silence.

Dead silence.

Everyone was stunned into disbelief by this news.

Gazes were fixed on the phone in Huai Sheng's hand—yes, it was on speakerphone.

So.

Jiang Tian's meaning was clear.

That was, they had launched an operation against the Wenye Group, but the final result was that after arresting a key suspect, someone had used a TNT bomb in the middle of the city.

This...

This is utter madness.

Everyone was dumbfounded.

If this isn't madness, what is?

"You mean, after you obtained evidence of Wenye Group's crimes, you took action, but while you were targeting Wenye Group, an unknown force launched a TNT attack?" Huai Sheng asked expressionlessly.

"That's right, that's roughly the situation."

Jiang Tian didn't hide anything and told him everything.

Huai Sheng's summary was very accurate.

However.

Everyone's expression changed.

Jiang Tian had no idea what kind of debate and argument had taken place in this office before.

In this era, where everything is based on the economy, those with the means want to develop a more effective economy, and those who should retire want a smooth retirement. Therefore, when Huai Sheng started targeting Wenye Group, almost everyone was against it.

Huai Sheng didn't reveal the evidence of Wenye Group's crimes.

That's why they found it hard to believe.

Just moments ago, they were debating whether or not to take action against Wenye Group, and now they had already turned around and taken action.

"What on earth is going on? They've already attacked the Wenye Group? Why didn't we know?"

At this moment, an old man frowned, unable to believe it.

"That's right, they've already attacked."

Faced with everyone's questioning gazes, Huai Sheng nodded slowly, his expression unchanged.

Boom!!!

Sure enough.

Everyone's eyes widened.

The mere fact that they attacked the Wenye Group was already unbelievable, but the fact that they were attacked by bombs afterward was even more unbelievable.

This...

Either of these was simply unimaginable to them.

"Why was it so sudden? Why was there no news before they attacked?" Just then, another person spoke up.

"Just now, didn't Huai Sheng already say so? And didn't he discuss it? But everyone's opinions were full of debate." Old Mu said calmly.

To this day,

the case regarding Old Mu's incident has not yielded any results.

The driver, who drowned, was the subject of a comprehensive investigation. However, regardless of the efforts of the joint investigation team from the provincial and municipal police departments, no further leads have been found.

In other words, the trail went cold with the driver.

Especially since they discovered that the driver was an orphan from a young age, with virtually no family or social circle.

This is the most difficult aspect to investigate.

But without a doubt, the person who took action possesses considerable power. If they could eliminate a few powerful figures, perhaps they could gather some clues.

Regarding the Wenye Group's actions, he, as the person in charge of law enforcement, certainly gave his full support.

"Old Mu, you're wrong. We just had a discussion, and we haven't reached a conclusion yet," a leader next to Old Mu said, frowning.

While the argument was raging,

Huai Sheng, sitting at the front, patted the small table next to the sofa, and the entire conference room fell silent again.

Huai Sheng remained calm and said, "Everyone's opinions are divided, but the battle within the police system has begun. Fighting criminals is like fighting a battlefield; opportunities are fleeting. By the time we reach a consensus, the criminals will probably be enjoying themselves in their villas across the Atlantic. This action was approved by me." Huai Sheng directly delivered the final conclusion.

"But doing so would be tantamount to wiping out years of economic hard work in Yun Province."

"Yes, we could have used much gentler methods!"

"This is a bit too sudden; at least let us know."

Several voices emerged from below after Huai Sheng spoke.

Huai Sheng's gaze swept over the speakers, his expression meaningful. After they had all finished speaking, Huai Sheng finally spoke: "You've all finished. Now, let me tell you. According to the investigation, Wenye Group started with drug trafficking, and eventually, they owned a large-scale drug manufacturing plant. Besides the drug plant and Wenye Group itself, they have close ties with foreign forces in Japan. During the raid, just recently, two thousand kilograms of heroin were found at the drug manufacturing plant. They were caught red-handed. Chairman Wen Jijiang confessed on the spot. Now, are you still saying we shouldn't touch Wenye Group?"

Huai Sheng's voice wasn't loud.

But every word he spoke carried a terrifying shock, sending chills down the spines of everyone present.

Each piece of evidence was enough to break their composure.

Some people even blushed, stammering as if trying to say something, only to find that anything they said would be superfluous.

"Moreover, the evidence against Wenye Group goes far beyond this. Economic development is necessary, but it absolutely cannot be based on sacrificing the social environment. Now, do you have any further questions?" Huai Sheng continued.

"So, this means Wenye Group has been apprehended? Then, what about the bomb?" A leader keenly sensed something was amiss.

"It's obvious that the Wenye Group has obtained evidence of wrongdoing by certain forces. They're afraid the Wenye Group will reveal it, so they're taking action against them. In other words, it's rotten. Our Yunhai is completely rotten, comrades! What we need now is radical reform to cleanse our system and move forward with a lighter burden. Otherwise, if everything is based on economic considerations, once the poison takes hold, we will all perish. We will all be sinners against Yun Province."

Huai Sheng slammed his fist on the table.

In the room, some people lowered their heads, their faces flushed with shame; others were filled with shock; and still others were bewildered, seemingly completely unaware.

A myriad of emotions were playing out in this small conference room.

But unfortunately, Huai Sheng didn't want to see their expressions.

At this moment, Huai Sheng's heart was heavy with anger.

He didn't understand many things because he had only been in Yun Province for two years.

He had only been transferred here not long before the New Year.



Chapter 367 A Frenzied Attack, Utter Madness


He was currently in the process of understanding Yunnan Province, but the impressions it left on him had thoroughly enraged him.

Before this, his understanding of Yunnan Province was that it was impoverished, yet superficially prosperous, occasionally experiencing chaos and problems, but never anything too serious.

But recently, things had been constantly shattering his worldview about Yunnan Province.

Did they really think Yunnan Province had only just begun to know about Jiang Tian? No, he had been following Jiang Tian from the moment he first appeared.

Even when Jiang Tian wiped out the first criminal gang and earned a first-class merit, he still felt that Yunnan Province wasn't as peaceful as it seemed on the surface.

The second first-class merit: Yunnan Province had some problems.

The third first-class merit: Yunnan Province had major problems.

The fourth first-class merit: Not only did Yunnan Province have major problems, but a dark cloud had descended upon him.

The fifth, the sixth...

Very well...

Now, even the Wenye Group has encountered such serious problems.

Who is innocent?

A slightly bewildered expression appeared on Huai Sheng's face.

If it weren't for Jiang Tian, ​​would these problems persist forever, continuing alongside Yunhai's development?

Then...

What about this conference room?

Are there really people who know absolutely nothing?

It's understandable that he, being new, wouldn't know.

But what about these people?

Of course, there was something else that chilled Huai Sheng to the bone.

That is, why were the two leaders from Yun Province transferred away, and now that he's been appointed to oversee the overall situation, why is the top position still vacant?

He suddenly understood what the higher-ups meant.

It was precisely because he knew that Huai Sheng felt a chill run down his spine.

The problem was enormous.

But, clearly, in a public place, even TNT was used to attack armed police; how brazen had the criminals become?

Regardless of their own motives, the emergence of this problem undoubtedly shifted everyone's focus.

"Investigate thoroughly, no compromise allowed."

"Absolutely lawless."

"This is insane! Such a heinous incident has been discovered in the provincial capital!"

Everyone was in shock.

The situation was clear.

But regardless of their own thoughts, no one could tolerate this.

Because if they dared to bomb the armed police today, what would they dare to do tomorrow...?

Huai Sheng pondered for a moment, then stood up, took his phone, and left the meeting room, making a call outside.

At the same time, Shen Han also stood up: "Leaders, I'm going to the scene immediately."

"Yes, this matter must be handled with utmost care. The site must be properly cordoned off to avoid causing panic among the public," Elder Mu instructed.

Clearly,

Shen Han was determined to go to the scene for such a serious matter.

The other leaders also had grim expressions.

This explosion was no less than an extreme provocation against them.

Of course, Jiang Tian was unaware of everything happening here.

After hanging up the phone, Jiang Tian had already arrived at the scene.

The fire was still burning.

When he was still a hundred or two meters away.

Bang bang bang!!!

Intense gunfire rang out continuously.

"So, they've started fighting?"

Jiang Tian's expression hardened. "Everyone, prepare to move!"

As he spoke, Jiang Tian's scanning radar continuously began scanning.

It's true what they say, watching a spectacle is human nature.

When they were about two hundred meters away, the car stopped. Why? Because the traffic was completely jammed.

"Good location. Although it's a main road, it's obvious that whether you go uphill on the left or downhill on the right, you can move out and retreat quickly." Lu Chun got out of the car and frantically pushed through the crowd.

That's right.

At this moment, just over two hundred meters away, besides the traffic jam, many people had gotten out of their cars to watch the commotion.

It's true what they say, ignorance is bliss.

If someone were accidentally hit by a stray bullet, they would die here, but clearly, these people didn't realize that.

And the fact that the armed police hadn't dispersed the crowd at this point was enough to show that the fighting was intense, and there was almost no time for evacuation.

"Damn it, are these people crazy?" Jiang Tian's eyes were icy.

"Quick, everyone get out of the way! Everyone, evacuate to the safe area immediately! Also, Chu Jing, you stay and evacuate the crowd." Jiang Tian turned and shouted to the crowd still watching and talking.

Jiang Tian pulled out his gun from his back waist. Just as he was about to leave, someone grabbed his hand. He turned around and saw...

It was Wen Min.

"Save my parents!" Wen Min's voice trembled. Although she appeared strong and composed, her lips were clearly shaking, and even the hand holding Jiang Tian's was noticeably cold.

"Try your best." Jiang Tian finished speaking and rushed forward with his pistol.

Chu Jing didn't dare to be careless either. After Jiang Tian left, Lü Chun followed closely behind him, but Chu Jing didn't dare to be careless either, because Wen Jing was with her.

Since those people had attacked Wen Yulin and his wife so ruthlessly, who could be sure they wouldn't target Wen Jing?

Jiang Tian, ​​however, didn't know this.

At this very moment, in the heart of the battlefield.

Captain Wang, who had just spoken to him, was now hiding behind a vehicle, enduring the pain.

Beside him lay the bodies of two recently fallen armed police soldiers.

Captain Wang himself was covered in blood.

“Captain, ten casualties,” a subordinate reported.

“Ten men! Damn it, these beasts!” Captain Wang roared in fury.

Has he gone mad?

Is he sure he hasn’t gone mad?

Such a heinous incident hasn’t happened in years.

And this isn’t overseas, it’s in the provincial capital.

This has happened.

Captain Wang’s eyes were bloodshot, his breathing rapid.

“The enemy chose a good position, they planted TNT along the road beforehand. Nobody expected them to be so ruthless,” the subordinate reporting the count.

Captain Wang peeked out.

The crackling of gunfire continued.

Just like Captain Wang's feelings, flames of anger were burning within him.

Absolutely audacious.

This is utter madness.

Of course, Captain Wang knew that at this moment, there was another crucial question.

"How is Wen Yulin?" Captain Wang asked through gritted teeth.

"We rescued him right after the explosion, but now it looks like he's in grave danger. Mr. and Mrs. Wen Yulin are both unconscious," a team member replied.

Actually, at the beginning, Captain Wang and his team were driving normally, with forty or fifty escorts protecting them from the front and back.

Or rather, it wasn't that Captain Wang was careless; rather, given the level of escort, no one expected an accident.

The result was unexpected: the other side used TNT.



Chapter 368 Arrival, a sheer veil, overwhelming the entire scene.


Fortunately.

The other side probably didn't have much explosives; the yield wasn't very high. After the explosion, they immediately began rescuing the people inside the burning transport vehicle.

If only the explosives had been a little more powerful.

Without a doubt.

The people inside the car had absolutely no chance of survival.

Even now, surviving wouldn't be easy.

Because the other side was using TNT, even being run over by a car wouldn't cause any problems, as long as the internal detonator wouldn't detonate.

The other side was focused on the transport vehicle.

Then, during the rescue, a dense barrage of gunfire suddenly erupted from both sides of the roundabout on the mountain.

In a moment of inattention, they lost more than ten men on the spot.

Actually, from the time Jiang Tian was blocked in the traffic,

then they chased after him, only about three minutes late, but those three minutes were enough for a lot to happen.

From the moment those unknown criminal groups attacked until now, less than two minutes had passed.

“Captain, they’re running!”

Just then, an armed police officer who was firing back shouted.

Captain Wang looked up and, sure enough, it was true.

The previously dense gunfire had suddenly become much less frequent.

And it sounded more like they were covering a retreat.

“Captain, should we chase after them?” someone asked, barely containing their anger.

Because from the moment these people attacked, before they could even react and retaliate, they had already retreated rapidly.

The speed was too fast.

The key was, they were too frustrated.

“No, protect Wen Yulin.” Although frustrated, Captain Wang spoke rationally.

He clearly understood that anyone could die now.

But Wen Yulin had to live, because Wen Yulin was definitely involved with this group.

As long as they're alive, they'll definitely be able to reveal their whereabouts.

But at this moment...

They don't know.

In the dense forest beside the road...

Jiang Tian has already located these people using scanning radar.

Each bright red dot is like a target to Jiang Tian.

Back then, when Gao Xiaolong was ambushed...

Jiang Tian, ​​with only one unerring aim, was able to wreak havoc in the darkness.

But now...

Jiang Tian's entries are practically overflowing; dealing with these people won't be difficult.

"If Captain Wang is in command, this is indeed impressive."

Jiang Tian, ​​hidden in the darkness, his bright eyes flashing, seems to be searching for the red-glowing criminals, and of course, he's also checking his entries.

[Spot on target (Gold)]

[Scanning radar (Gold)]

[Extraordinary human (Gold)]

[Martial arts master (Red)]

[King of criminal investigation (Red)]

[Photographic memory (Blue)]

Current sin value: 163,054

That's right.

This is Jiang Tian's current skill panel, practically bursting with golden light.

It's truly dazzling.

Of course, Jiang Tian originally had over 200,000 sin points. After upgrading to Ocean God, he spent over 100,000 sin points to save Su Tong.

He still had over 100,000 left, but then he captured Wen Jijiang and his gang.

This gave Jiang Tian a significant sin point reward.

Now he has over 160,000.

It's not a huge amount, but it's not a small amount either. However, Jiang Tian is quite envious of the next stage of the "stamp collecting" skill reward—the Rolls-Royce.

Although for now, it seems like he has almost enough; headquarters even owes him two first-class merits and one first-class hero.

But who would complain about having too many merits?

“If we catch these guys, it’ll probably be enough for a first-class merit award. No, if we can wipe out the entire network behind them, it’ll probably be enough for a special-class merit award.”

Jiang Tian recalled the words Wen Yulin had whispered to him before leaving.

But his actions were swift; he had already infiltrated the group of drug dealers hiding.

“Boss, what if these cops aren’t falling for it?” one of them asked.

“Wen Yulin should be dead. We can’t stay here any longer. Follow the planned route and retreat quickly. If we get caught, you all know what to do. Your families will be taken care of.”

The boss said worriedly.

The others didn’t speak, but they had already tacitly agreed.

The threat of family threats.

A common tactic of organized crime.

Jiang Tian’s gaze fell on the leader.

His expression was slightly surprised.

The attackers numbered around thirty, all hiding behind cover from the armed police on the road, while another twenty or so were positioned on the other side of the road.

In total, nearly fifty people were deployed in this single operation.

Jiang Tian fell into deep thought.

What kind of heinous crime could drive criminals to such madness, mobilizing so many people, disregarding the terrifying consequences, to kill Wen Yulin?

"No wonder it's news of a special merit award; it seems Wen Yulin was right."

Jiang Tian's eyes flickered, then he raised his pistol, aiming at the gang leader.

At that moment.

The gang leader seemed to have a keen intuition, abruptly turning his head to look behind him.

But in the next instant.

Bang bang bang!!!

A series of gunshots rang out.

The gunshots, due to the extremely high rate of fire, almost sounded like those of an automatic rifle.

Six bullets.

He was shot away almost instantly, a thick plume of black smoke slowly rising from the muzzle.

But the gunshot rang out.

A moment before the sound of the gunshot rang out, the boss had already fallen to the ground.

In the moments before losing consciousness.

The boss looked completely bewildered, seemingly not understanding what had happened.

But at this moment, it wasn't just the leader.

The leader's men, the ones in charge.

Those hiding behind the trees.

The six men almost directly exposed to Jiang Tian's position were all headshot by him from this angle.

*Snap!!!*

The next moment, Jiang Tian moved like a cheetah, disappearing in a flash. In less than a fraction of a second, his magazine had already fallen to the ground.

A new magazine was already loaded.

In less than a second, the spot where Jiang Tian had just stood was covered by a dense hail of bullets.

If it were nighttime even for a short while, Jiang Tian would be riddled with bullets.

Because of his skill and daring, Jiang Tian had gotten extremely close in order to finish the job quickly.

The moment the shots were fired, those people could react.

Perhaps, anyone except Jiang Tian would feel a chill run down their spine in this situation.

But unfortunately, Jiang Tian had already moved to a new position.

Bang bang bang!!!

Instant kill.



Chapter 369: Dozens of people could only bring me slight pleasure.


“Well, when you gaze into the abyss, the abyss also gazes into you.”

Jiang Tian touched the slightly hot barrel of his gun, having fired twelve rounds in quick succession; the chamber was almost bursting with fire.

He blew on it, his gaze fixed on the six people within his sight.

Twelve criminals, already dead.

As for survivors?

There wasn't much point. Jiang Tian had just glanced at their recent intelligence reports; four or five of them had appeared in recent reports, meaning their crime values ​​were all above one thousand.

For Jiang Tian, ​​this was more than enough information.

Besides, at this time...

There were too many people; at least the scene needed to be cleaned up.

“A ghost!”

“It’s a ghost!”

“Third brother.”

“Bastard.”

When his twelve companions fell to the ground in an instant, lifeless, everyone panicked almost instantly.

There were probably fewer than twenty people left.

These men had just been lying in ambush, concealed behind cover, intending to ambush the armed police ahead. In other words, the moment Jiang Tian arrived, their backs were all exposed to him.

In a sense, this was a huge waste of Jiang Tian's "crime points."

A heinous crime.

Added together, it would amount to at least ten or twenty thousand crime points.

Click!!!

Jiang Tian casually changed his bullets. The moment the empty magazine fell to the ground, Jiang Tian, ​​like a ghost, had already moved to a position more than twenty meters away, searching for a position where they could be seen with their backs turned.

Bang bang bang!!!

A continuous stream of gunfire rang out.

Like the scythe of death, it relentlessly reaped their lives.

With each round of gunfire, Jiang Tian would change positions, meaning these men could only chase after him.

The hail of bullets posed no threat to Jiang Tian; on the contrary, each shot he fired took down at least two or three men.

Sometimes, it even took four or five.

On this side of the road, there were fewer than thirty men; how could they withstand several waves of attacks from Jiang Tian?

After several rounds.

In a small clearing in the jungle, three lone criminals stood back to back, each staring in the direction they were facing.

At this moment, unsurprisingly, their legs were trembling uncontrollably, their faces deathly pale, staring ahead in terror.

"Who, who is it?" one of them shouted, his voice trembling.

Although he wanted to shout to bolster his morale, his trembling voice only seemed to further demoralize his men, making them appear to have completely lost the will to resist.

"Jiang Tian, ​​we know it's you, and only you," the other said, his voice trembling.

That's right.

When Jiang Tian's name was uttered, Jiang Tian, ​​standing on a tree with his gun pointed at the three men, showed no change in his gaze. Or rather, the fact that these people knew his name had no effect on his state of mind whatsoever.

What a joke.

From the attack he himself suffered, to the attack on his family.

In a sense, Jiang Tian was already in a semi-public state.

He couldn't hide it; there was no way he could hide it.

Jiang Tian's current First-Class Merit and Special-Class Merit were not actually awarded according to the standards.

Of all the publicly disclosed achievements, only the First-Class Merit made the news, and even then, it wasn't named. However, the surname Jiang was used, and everyone in the Yunhai criminal organization understood its significance.

They all knew who this Jiang represented.

On the other hand, internal problems also existed.

Occasionally, Jiang Tian used his scanning radar or Judgment Eye to search for internal issues.

Generally speaking, the influence of these criminal forces had been immense over the years.

Apart from the relatively clean municipal bureau, it was difficult to guarantee that the lower-level branches were problem-free. If there was even the slightest problem, Jiang Tian's information would be impossible to keep secret.

But now, Jiang Tian's information had leaked to this extent.

Even the lower-ranking officers knew?

Jiang Tian's eyes flickered. It seemed there was no need to remain anonymous anymore.

Of course, Jiang Tian didn't actually realize what he had done.

He had even killed hundreds of criminals.

Compared to the fact that his information had been leaked, Jiang Tian was practically a death knell to these criminals, a figure who struck fear into their hearts at the mere mention of his name.

“Why? Why were you so fast?”

“Damn it, weren’t we already cornered? Yes…”

“Surrender! We surrender! Don’t shoot! Don’t kill me!”

One of them, seemingly unable to bear the pressure any longer, threw his weapon to the ground, then raised his hands above his head, shouting in despair.

Following the first man’s surrender, the remaining two also dropped their weapons, covered their heads with their hands, and surrendered.

After all, who would choose death if they could live?

“Is this all the resistance they’ve got?”

Jiang Tian shook his head slightly.

His intuition told him that those he was arresting were mostly ruthless drug dealers, people who would rather die than submit, and would fight to the death for even the slightest chance.

Let alone getting them to confess honestly.

Even if they were given several powerful memory restoration spells and dragon fruit combos right away, it might not be enough, because they knew that even if they surrendered, they would certainly die.

Especially on the battlefield, these people had almost no concept of surrender.

Besides certain death.

Oh, and now it seems these people have no chance of escape at all.

Because the surrounding armed police are also tentatively approaching.

Jiang Tian's eyes held a hint of appreciation.

The others couldn't see clearly.

But Jiang Tian had a high vantage point, and his superhuman physique allowed him to see faces clearly from a hundred meters away, even in the darkness.

Captain Wang led his men closer slowly.

It must have been the sudden burst of gunfire in the jungle, and crucially, the gunfire wasn't coming from their location, leading Captain Wang to suspect something was wrong with these criminals, so he quietly approached.

But he didn't rush in recklessly. The previous ambush had caught them off guard, and he remained remarkably calm in the face of these ruthless criminals.

This pleased Jiang Tian quite a bit.

Captain Wang and Jiang Tian were already acquainted. During previous operations, it was Old Chen who led the team, but Captain Wang, one of Old Chen's subordinates, was particularly to Jiang Tian's liking.

Especially during operations, Captain Wang's performance perfectly suited Jiang Tian's tastes. He had his own ideas, and crucially, he knew how to cooperate with Jiang Tian, ​​following his orders without question.

Especially today.

These criminals had occupied advantageous positions for an ambush. If they were to rush in recklessly, a large number of drug dealers would instantly surround them from both sides.

The losses would be difficult to quantify.



Chapter 370 A Sudden Change in the Night: He Arrived, and the Whole Army Was Annihilated


Jiang Tian made his move, yet he also sensed the anomaly and led his men forward stealthily.

He was definitely a very competent partner.

"It's too quiet."

At this moment, cold sweat broke out on the forehead of the junior armed police officer beside Captain Wang.

The deathly silence made his heart race, fearing something might suddenly appear.

"They must have run away, but that's impossible. There were gunshots just now, how could they run so fast?" another junior armed police officer said, puzzled.

"It's too quiet, everyone be careful."

Captain Wang, panting, tried to calm himself down and led his men, in groups of three, slowly dispersed.

But the next moment, in the small open space in front of him...

Swish swish swish!!!

A series of clanging sounds of guns being raised rang out.

The next moment, a bright light shone, revealing three figures.

At this moment, all three were crouching, hands covering their heads.

"We're not fighting anymore! We're surrendering!"

Hearing the voice, the three men shouted to Captain Wang with faces full of joy.

A bullet could strike their heads at any moment; heaven knows that in just that short time, some of them felt like they were about to die of fright.

If they had arrived any later, if the person in the darkness hadn't spoken, they probably really would have died of fright right there.

Their joyful reaction stunned Captain Wang.

"What happened?" Captain Wang approached, somewhat bewildered.

"We just attacked you! Quick, grab us!" One of the criminals had completely broken down.

And...

Captain Wang's heart skipped a beat, initially thinking it was a trap.

The next second, dozens of their teammates were gone.

Captain Wang followed their gaze.

Buzz!

Corpses lay scattered everywhere.

At first, they could hold on.

But seeing their comrades fall one by one, these criminals completely broke down.

Even professional fighters struggle to survive with a casualty ratio of half, let alone this ragtag group of drug dealers.

Corpses lay scattered haphazardly everywhere.

There were bullets in every location.

Captain Wang's heart pounded.

"Captain, what happened?"

Just then, a junior armed police officer approached Captain Wang, his voice trembling with fear.

Xiao Liu was a member who had recently joined the team.

To be honest...

“Jiang Tian.”

“He’s here.”

The captain’s face was pale.

“He’s here?”

“Jiang Tian?”

Xiao Liu’s eyes widened.

But the captain looked around and found no one there.

Captain Wang looked around and immediately went to the three men: “Did any of you escape?”

“No, only the three of us are left. They’re all dead, all shot dead.”

The three men didn’t dare to hide anything and immediately spoke hurriedly and nervously.

Hmm?

This made Captain Wang frown slightly.

Just then.

The previously completely silent environment suddenly erupted in a burst of intense gunfire.

Captain Wang’s expression changed, and he looked sharply in one direction.

And just then, Captain Wang’s walkie-talkie suddenly crackled to life.

"Captain, Captain, there's intense gunfire coming from six o'clock!"

Hiss!!!

Captain Wang's eyes widened.

Where is six o'clock?

That's the other direction of the route that just attacked them, the direction up the mountain.

This mountain range is very long, and perhaps because of its length and dense forests, even with Yunhai's current development, its development remains incomplete.

Many problems remain. For example, these drug traffickers chose this location and planned their escape route.

According to their plan, they had three minutes to act, after which, regardless of success or failure, they would all flee.

However, now, in terms of technological level and other aspects, the situation is completely different from twenty years later; there's no comparison whatsoever. Under these circumstances,

these drug traffickers naturally had no scruples before their operation. They only needed three minutes to act and then escape, quickly dispersing in two directions along the mountain range.

Once they entered the mountain range, continuing the search would be much more difficult.

Because this mountain range even leads directly to Myanmar.

Of course, there are also checkpoints on the mountain.

But it's impossible to guard every checkpoint, which is why these people dared to be so audacious.

A total of nearly fifty people were deployed.

Thirty were in this direction; after Jiang Tian caught up, the remaining twenty were simply a matter of time for him to pursue.

In other words, if these drug dealers hadn't scattered and fled, Jiang Tian could have dealt with them in an even shorter time.

But now, it only took Jiang Tian a little more time, but the result remained the same.

Back then, Jiang Tian's unerring aim was enough to kill so many drug dealers; now, with explosive abilities, terrifying speed, and invincible stamina,

Jiang Tian now even possesses the speed of a cheetah and the stamina of a zebra.

Combined with his unerring aim, how could these people possibly escape?

Therefore.

The moment the gunshot rang out.

It was like the death knell.

"Hiss!!!"

Captain Wang's scalp tingled.

He stared in horror at the corpses strewn across the ground, and three drug dealers who, terrified, dared not flee even when no one was around.

Then came the sound of gunfire.

Captain Wang felt a chill run down his spine.

"Arrest them all first, we'll deal with the rest later. Also, call backup, get the paramedics and forensic team here."

Captain Wang hurriedly issued the orders and began the cleanup operation.

Having worked with Jiang Tian for so long and so many times, he always knew that Jiang Tian was all about killing and no one else's job.

No matter what happened, shoot the enemy first, then arrest the drug dealers, and leave the remaining small-time dealers behind.

This was also because the city bureau had been very busy lately.

"Who?"



Chapter 371 Exchange, Wen Min is shocked, you also know medicine?


"Who?"

Just then.

Lu Chun emerged from the jungle, his face full of frustration.

But he hadn't caught any.

Instead, after the gunfire, he waited and waited, but no one came. He thought he'd come back to check, and when he arrived, damn it, the battle was over.

"Officer Lu." Captain Wang's face visibly relaxed.

Seeing Lu Chun's expression, Captain Wang understood. After leaving some men to clean up the battlefield, he quickly led his men back to the road.

And at that moment.

A high-speed buzzing sound rose from the sky.

That's right.

This operation was truly appalling, so appalling that even the military was mobilized.

Helicopters arrived at the scene immediately to provide support.

In addition, special forces soldiers were on their way.

However, Captain Wang knew at this moment that these people were likely to have made a wasted trip.

Given the current situation,

the remaining drug dealers were probably already Jiang Tian's for the taking.

But why so fast?

Undoubtedly, the wounds on these people were inflicted by Jiang Tian, ​​but the most terrifying part was that the gunfire had only stopped for a few dozen seconds when they arrived.

Those few dozen seconds, plus the distance from their location to the road was nearly a hundred meters, and crossing the road and then reaching the other side was at least another hundred meters.

In a track and field race, two hundred meters would take a few dozen seconds.

But what kind of environment was this? Beyond the road was jungle, mountains, and a bumpy, uneven terrain; even track and field athletes would twist their ankles here.

This speed...

It was breathtaking.

"Insane!"

Captain Wang was filled with awe.

However, Captain Wang was already used to this kind of inhumanity.

From initial shock to gradual numbness.

When they reached the road.

At this moment.

What came into view.

Captain Wang saw a familiar figure.

Buzz!!!

Even the numb Captain Wang felt a chill run down his spine.

Because at this moment, Jiang Tian was squatting in front of a figure.
