In fact, with current medical conditions, the chances of survival were slim.

Most importantly, he was unconscious.

Thinking of this, Captain Wang's face was filled with guilt.

Because this was the very couple he was escorting, Wen Yulin and his wife, and yet this had happened halfway there. Captain Wang's face was a mixture of shame and embarrassment.

"Team Leader Jiang, this was my mistake. I didn't expect them to actually dare to do something like this, even using TNT." Captain Wang hesitated before walking to Jiang Tian's side.

Meanwhile, at this very moment...

Jiang Tian, ​​his gaze thoughtful, was also gripping a part of Wen Yulin's body.

As Jiang Tian took action,

Wen Min, standing beside him, already heartbroken from crying, was completely stunned.

Because as he acted, her father's complexion gradually stabilized.

His vital signs gradually stabilized?

Has he gone mad?

Is Officer Jiang a medical genius?

Actually, it's not that Jiang Tian is a medical genius.

Wen Yulin is too important.

For Jiang Tian, ​​or rather, for the entire city police bureau, Wen Yulin is a crucial figure.

Because in some ways, Wen Yulin represents someone who has completely exposed the tip of the iceberg of the Yunhai criminal syndicate, or rather, a member of their core.

As for Wen Jijiang...

After a brief interrogation, and even while taking action, Jiang Tian sensed that Wen Jijiang didn't know much.

At least, this guy, although outwardly successful,

was...

Jiang Tian keenly sensed that the real mastermind behind the Wenye Group was someone else entirely, and sure enough, he was right.

After arresting Wen Yulin, Jiang Tian was absolutely certain of this conclusion.

While it appeared that Wen Jijiang was the boss, in reality, the one truly orchestrating and controlling the Wenye Group from behind the scenes, even the one presiding over the crimes and the group's development, was Wen Yulin.

Wen Jijiang wasn't exactly incompetent.

However, he was probably just someone who profited immensely.

Otherwise, his crime score wouldn't be so low, only a few thousand, less than half of Wen Yulin's.

The fact that people didn't react when Wen Jijiang was arrested, but went completely berserk when Wen Yulin was arrested, further illustrates this.

Wen Yulin was definitely connected to a terrifying figure behind the scenes.

After dealing with the people immediately, Jiang Tian had Chu Jing bring Wen Min over first.

Whether for humanitarian reasons or to understand the case, it was necessary.

Meanwhile, Jiang Tian didn't stop his work.

Among the many criminals he killed was a descendant of a family of traditional Chinese medicine practitioners, someone with a basic understanding of TCM. He had picked up TCM entries, but Jiang Tian himself didn't lack sin points, so he didn't synthesize it.

Keeping it behind proved useful.

Ding!

Consuming 100 sin points, currently: Basic TCM Knowledge (White) - TCM Successor (Green) 'You have a thorough understanding of all aspects of TCM, comparable to a TCM doctor.'

Ding!

Consuming 1000...

Ding!

Consuming 10,000...

Basic TCM Knowledge (White) - Medical Master (Red)

[As a righteous anti-drug police officer, you often encounter situations where your teammates are injured. In the past, you always watched helplessly as your teammates died before your eyes. But now, your TCM level in this world is top-notch. Perhaps you can try to leave your mark on the history of TCM.]

Buzz! !!

Instantly, a terrifyingly vast amount of knowledge appeared in Jiang Tian's mind.

A thorough understanding of the various structures of the human body is fundamental. He is proficient in the correspondences between acupoints and other body parts, mastering the four diagnostic methods of observation, auscultation, and palpation. At a glance, he can diagnose illnesses instantly.

Most importantly, he also possesses various treatment methods.

Hiss!!!

Jiang Tian's eyes lit up.

Over ten thousand sin points, half of what he gained tonight, were used to casually add such a bizarre entry.

In other words, even if Jiang Tian were to repeat the same mistake in the future, he could still achieve great things with this skill.

As for Wen Yulin's physical condition, Jiang Tian glanced at it and roughly understood:

Massive intracranial hemorrhage.

Under normal circumstances, before even reaching the hospital, he would definitely be in a vegetative state.



Chapter 372 Liao Ying arrived, and she was stunned. Could this really be done?


Luckily, he was in a vegetative state; any later, and he wouldn't even have had a chance. The TNT explosion sent the car flying.

This was just a quick overview of Jiang Tian's injuries.

A more systematic examination hadn't been conducted yet, but it was certain that the brainstem hemorrhage was serious, though definitely not the most severe of all the injuries from the impact.

Look at the few armed police officers who were escorting him who died.

From what Jiang Tian could see, three of those dragged out were beyond saving, including Wen Yulin's wife, who showed no signs of life on the spot.

In other words, the person in the car who was perhaps the luckiest and least injured was on the verge of death. Even with Jiang Tian's current skills, he couldn't guarantee their survival, but for now, at least keeping Wen Yulin alive until the hospital arrived was enough.

Therefore.

When Captain Wang arrived, the scene that greeted him was Jiang Tian holding a set of silver needles.

Jiang Tian didn't have any silver needles.

They certainly didn't have any.

However, with so many onlookers, there were bound to be some.

Although they weren't professional acupuncture needles, just ordinary needles from the car's first-aid kit, it was somewhat enough for Jiang Tian.

"Team Leader Jiang, what is this?"

Captain Wang gasped, mainly because the scene was too horrific. Jiang Tian had inserted several needles into Wen Yulin's Tianling acupoint, from which a large amount of bright red blood gushed out.

Captain Wang turned pale at the sight.

If it weren't for the fact that Wen Yulin, who had just been ashen-faced and on the verge of death, now looked much better, he would have almost thought he was murdering someone.

"Brainstem hemorrhage. However, he's lucky to have survived. The specific injuries need to be examined in detail at the hospital, but even if he's saved, it's uncertain when he'll wake up," Jiang Tian shook his head.

"No, I mean, you... you know medicine?" Captain Wang asked, bewildered.

That's the most important thing, okay?

Moreover...

To be able to diagnose the injury so quickly, and to be able to boldly use medical skills, employing such terrifying methods for emergency treatment—most doctors certainly couldn't do that.

"Oh, I'm just interested in this. I've read some books on traditional Chinese medicine before," Jiang Tian said, standing up without changing his expression.

He then patted his blood-stained hands.

His hands were clean after the intense chase through the jungle with a gun, but after treating Wen Yulin, they were covered in blood.

"Team Leader Jiang, sob sob... thank you."

The one most devastated at this moment was undoubtedly Wen Min.

This girl was truly pitiful.

She lived a wealthy and comfortable life, with a happy family and a comfortable background—a perfectly good young lady.

But in the blink of an eye, overnight, everything changed.

A shocking piece of bad news struck: her parents were both high-ranking criminals.

And they had committed terrifying crimes, possibly even facing execution.

Before she could even process what was happening, she was blown up into the air by a group of mysterious people while being escorted.

The mother died instantly, and the father was now seriously injured and unconscious, his future uncertain.

To be honest, Jiang Tian felt some sympathy for the little girl.

And despite her tears and breakdown, the little girl carefully handed Jiang Tian a handkerchief.

"Thank you," Jiang Tian said, wiping his hands after taking it.

"Don't be too sad. The people who attacked your parents have been killed by me, and the mastermind behind it will definitely be caught."

Jiang Tian's eyes flickered. Even if Wen Yulin was guilty of heinous crimes, he should be brought to justice.

It wasn't a matter of who could be so heartless as to attack so recklessly.

Most importantly...

Before, Jiang Tian might have had to consider the consequences, making him somewhat hesitant in his actions.

But this incident completely exposed the criminal organizations in Yunhai, no, the entire Yun Province.

This showed not only Yun Province, but even Kyoto, just how ruthless the criminal organizations in Yun Province had become.

It's no exaggeration to say that this is just the tip of the iceberg.

They're already capable of indiscriminately arresting and killing suspects without needing evidence.

However...

After Jiang Tian said that...

Captain Wang swallowed hard.

These seemingly ordinary words of comfort carried such terrifying information.

What did "killed them all" mean?

No one escaped.

Even if some survived, perhaps only those three prisoners.

Hiss!!!

"Team Leader Jiang, were all the attackers killed? Was the six o'clock position also dealt with?" Captain Wang gasped.

Jiang Tian turned and nodded slightly. "There were more than twenty people at the six o'clock position, but when I arrived, they had split into two groups and fled. I wasted a little time chasing them; otherwise, if Wen Yulin had received emergency treatment earlier, perhaps his condition wouldn't be so serious."

Gulp!!!

Captain Wang swallowed hard, looking at Jiang Tian with an expression as if he were a ghost.

Just then.

Accompanied by a buzzing sound, armed helicopters were rapidly transporting personnel.

In addition, military vehicles were parked on the perimeter for security.

As for such a major incident,

Frankly speaking, it was the kind of thing that had blown up, and no one could cover it up.

Especially since the ambulances had also arrived at the scene after clearing a road.

In short, the chaotic scene was stabilized; at least, more and more onlookers were being dispersed.

As for the traffic jam, it was gradually clearing under the direction of a large number of police officers.

Whoosh!!

Liao Ying jumped quickly from the ambulance.

Her face was exhausted.

So tired.

She didn't know what was wrong with her lately; in short, there were more and more emergency rescues, giving her no time to catch her breath.

Especially for the emergency department.

As the most authoritative and best hospital in Yunhai, the Municipal Hospital is usually the one most frequently dispatched for major accidents.

Especially in cases like this one.

What's most baffling is that...

In the past, at most, only car accidents would keep people busy.

The most serious and heaviest incident was a chain-reaction car accident.

However,

while rescuing others, they were prevented from doing so.

In this situation,

Wen Yulin was undoubtedly the most seriously involved, and also the most important.

According to criminal law, Wen Yulin would certainly be executed, but at the same time, Wen Yulin was absolutely key to dismantling the entire criminal organization.

Even if the armed police were injured, in this situation, the first person to be rescued would still be Wen Yulin.

"What happened?"



Chapter 373 The Unending Support, the Bomb That Was Detonated Completely


When Liao Ying arrived, she was completely bewildered.

Her adorable face was blank.

She stared blankly at the injured person lying on the ground, especially the several silver needles inserted into their skull, from which blood was still dripping.

“Brainstem hemorrhage. I can only provide emergency drainage. Other injuries will need to be assessed at the hospital, but I estimate that the cervical and lumbar vertebrae are most likely fractured, and internal organs are also damaged to varying degrees.”

Just then, a shadow appeared before Liao Ying's eyes, followed by a familiar voice.

Liao Ying looked up.

Her face instantly filled with astonishment as she stared at Jiang Tian.

It was Jiang Tian again.

Oh no.

It was quite normal for Officer Jiang to be at a crime scene like this, especially one that looked like an arrest scene.

But the crucial issue was, draining a brainstem hemorrhage like this.

Liao Ying had never heard of such a thing before.

"Officer Jiang, this...you did this? You even know medicine?"

If it were true, Liao Ying's scalp tingled. How profound must his medical skills be?

Even if Liao Ying were certain, it was impossible for him to divert blood like this. Not to mention her, she felt that there were definitely no more than a handful of people in the entire country who could do this, and dared to do it.

"A little bit," Jiang Tian said modestly.

Pfft!!!

Good heavens.

Liao Ying was stunned.

Her profession was apprehending criminals, and rescuing people by swimming was even more miraculous.

Now you're saying even rescuing someone is this outrageous?

"Be careful when you lift him," Jiang Tian wasn't exaggerating. He had just glanced at the injury and understood about 70-80% of it.

Following his advice and providing immediate treatment would definitely be fine.

Liao Ying also underwent a check-up immediately.

Especially when Liao Ying saw the silver needles on the wounded soldier's head, a large amount of blood was already gushing out, and it was still flowing continuously.

Such a massive amount of bleeding.

If it weren't for drainage...

Hiss!!!

This was certain; aside from brainstem hemorrhage, one of the most deadly injuries, how could so much blood possibly drain from the brain?

Without drainage, such a terrifying amount of bleeding would leave seven or eight out of ten patients in a vegetative state, even in a hospital, with only one or two lucky enough to regain consciousness.

Furthermore, if they rushed here and then took the patient back for emergency treatment, there would be absolutely no chance of even a vegetative state.

But it was precisely because she was certain that Liao Ying felt a chill run down her spine.

Immediately, Liao Ying didn't delay.

She quickly instructed her men to carefully move the wounded soldier into the ambulance.

As Liao Ying approached Jiang Tian, ​​her gaze fell upon Wen Jing, standing beside him. Wen Jing's delicate yet pitiful appearance instantly stirred a hint of resentment in Liao Ying's eyes.

This made Jiang Tian's eyelids twitch.

However, Jiang Tian wasn't afraid. As the saying goes, "A clear conscience fears no accusation," and Jiang Tian was upright and honest.

He had absolutely no fear.

"Hmph."

Liao Ying snorted through her nose. Even with her mask on, the displeasure in her tone was clearly audible.

Of course, Liao Ying knew the gravity of the situation. After giving Jiang Tian a sidelong glance, she quickly led the others back into the ambulance.

After all, if Wen Yulin's situation was truly as Jiang Tian described, it was indeed extremely serious.

As for Jiang Tian...

He had initially been worried and wanted to leave with him, hoping his presence would be helpful in case of an emergency and provide first aid.

However, the situation at the scene was far too dire.

A large number of armed police and military personnel had completely surrounded the area.

Moreover, what stunned Jiang Tian was that the number of people surrounding the area not only didn't decrease, but actually increased. Eventually, more than ten armed helicopters arrived, hovering overhead.

As for disturbing the peace...

What a joke.

What kind of situation is this?

This is practically a slap in the face.

Perhaps the criminal group has been ruling Yunnan Province for far too long, so long that they've lost all sense of awe.

They're only thinking about getting rid of Wen Yulin.

Once Wen Yulin is dealt with, they'll definitely be safe.

Just like before, they continued to maintain a facade of peaceful coexistence, but they overlooked one crucial point: this kind of criminal case, this kind of attack, is an absolutely unforgivable issue for the entire Yunnan province, and even for the official authorities.

As the saying goes, if the situation allows, there's no limit to calling for backup; no matter how powerful you are, you can even call in missiles.

While Jiang Tian was still pondering the situation,

he had already led his men towards the outer perimeter.

Surrounded by layers of people, a large number of leaders were already waiting.

Of course, Jiang Tian understood their mobilization; after all, such a serious matter required almost all leaders to be on the scene.

It was urgent.

Especially regarding the casualties and other aspects.

This could be considered, without a doubt, one of the most heinous cases in recent years.

Besides the previous large-scale attacks on high-ranking leader Gao Xiaolong, the sheer scale of the attack and the ruthlessness of the methods used, especially given the massive number of onlookers, meant that even with a news blackout, it was impossible to completely suppress the information; leaks were inevitable.

The armed police were attacked in large numbers.

The perpetrators even used high explosives.

A large number of personnel were deployed to attack the armed police in public in the city.

If the perpetrators escaped,

who could bear the responsibility?

When Huai Sheng arrived at the scene, his heart sank.

In the conference room, Huai Sheng received Jiang Tian's message and, almost without delay, immediately organized manpower.

Immediately afterwards, he followed Shen Han in an official vehicle to support the scene.

Simultaneously, support from the military and other sources was mobilized almost entirely.

It was truly a three-layered encirclement.

Of course, without Jiang Tian's intervention, even with such a large force...

However, in the dead of night, with those criminals fleeing into the mountains,

it's possible to catch some, but a large number will inevitably escape.

These people will change clothes, hide their weapons and ammunition, and once they enter the city, it will be like finding a needle in a haystack.

Even if someone threw down their weapon and disappeared into the crowd, it would be difficult to identify them.

Because no one knew who had launched the attack.

The provincial authorities arrived immediately.

Yunhai's direct superiors and city leaders also rushed to the scene in a panic.

"Based on the situation at the scene and what we know, the armed attackers clearly scouted the location beforehand and chose an ambush point conducive to retreat. Furthermore, from the initial explosion to the subsequent gunfire, they used only three minutes. After those three minutes, they quickly retreated without lingering. We have far too little information; we don't even know their age or gender. We can only search the entire mountain."



Chapter 374 The leader is furious: If one of you runs away, I'll fire you all.


Jiang Peng, his forehead beaded with cold sweat, provided a detailed analysis of the situation to the leaders on site.

Those who heard Jiang Peng's words understood what he meant.

"The most important thing now is the casualty situation, and most importantly, what happened to Wen Yulin?" Huai Sheng said grimly.

Although Huai Sheng wasn't exactly a new official, having only been in his second year in office, he had only been transferred here shortly before the new year.

"We just sent rescue vehicles in for rescue operations, but we can't confirm the perpetrator's information yet. We can only wait until the on-site operations personnel come out."

Shen Han's face was also grim.

Like Jiang Peng.

Such a big incident had occurred.

Jiang Peng, as the director of Yunhai Bureau, bore responsibility.

He, Shen Han, was also among those responsible. It could be said that with such a high-profile case, if handled poorly, he could be dismissed entirely.

There was absolutely no room for negotiation.

Therefore...

Shen Han had already mobilized all available manpower from the provincial department, including a large number of armed police personnel from provincial units, to provide support.

Military forces stationed in Yunnan Province were also continuously arriving to provide reinforcements.

“However, according to the information we currently have, there are probably more than fifty criminals. During the attack, more than ten of our armed police officers were killed on the spot.”

Jiang Peng wiped the sweat from his brow.

God knows!

He and Gao Xiaolong were at the city police station, directing the operation, still marveling at how smoothly today's arrest operation had gone. Who the hell could have predicted this?

In the blink of an eye, such a major case had occurred.

It's no exaggeration to say that the sky has fallen.

Shen Han and Jiang Peng felt a chill.

Because no one understood better than them that in this situation, especially with prior planning, even with so many people deployed, they could only arrest a portion at most.

To achieve the goal of capturing everyone as ordered by their superiors was virtually impossible.

Even if the mountain is completely sealed off, who can guarantee that no one will come down to change clothes?

This isn't like having a target, knowing who it is, and directly issuing a warrant for arrest.

They've been organizing their forces for almost thirty minutes now.

From the initial attack to its end, the criminals took three minutes to attack and twenty-seven minutes to escape.

Thinking about it this way,

a full search of the mountain might only find half of them.

Moreover, they're under immense pressure.

But the other leaders can't escape either.

At most, they'll bear the main responsibility, but for every single one of these high-ranking leaders, with such a major case, the entire Yunnan province will be under national scrutiny.

So many people are at the scene.

It can't be hidden, it's simply impossible to hide.

Especially in Beijing, it's impossible to keep it a secret.

"No matter the cost, investigate to the end. Not a single one can be let go. And today, those people dared to be so depraved, committing such an audacious attack! What is this? This is a provocation against the entire Yun Province, against all law enforcement personnel! If we cannot annihilate them with lightning speed, similar incidents will never stop. This is a heinous attack without any bottom line. I don't care about the difficulties or problems you face; I have only one demand: arrest all those involved in the operation."

Provincial Governor Huai was now completely enraged.

Before this, Provincial Governor Huai had always been a good guy.

Or rather, his emotions had always been relatively stable; he was the kind of person who could handle and control the situation without changing his expression, no matter what happened.

But now...

Provincial Governor Huai was also completely enraged.

"Anyone who can't do this, whether you're the director of the Yunhai Bureau or the director of the Yun Province Department, will all have to take off your hats."

Provincial Governor Huai said coldly and mercilessly.

Boom!!!

As Huai Sheng finished speaking,

the scene fell into an instant, so quiet you could hear a pin drop.

Although there were many leaders present, everyone was sweating profusely.

Because, upon investigation, this case had reached an extremely terrifying level.

Even Huai Sheng would be held accountable for this.

Such a terrible problem occurring in your jurisdiction proves your incompetence.

You still say you bear no responsibility?

And if even Huai Sheng can't escape responsibility, what about the leaders below him?

Could these leaders have been completely unaware of the situation in Yunhai? Impossible.

Otherwise, how could Jiang Peng have been appointed to such an important position as bureau chief?

It's because no one could handle it.

Ultimately, the criminal organization's power was too terrifying and too vast.

From the beginning, or even if someone had discovered it, no one dared to uncover the tip of the iceberg.

Perhaps some had been involved, but before that, they had all died in various accidents.

That might be understandable.

But with such a powerful explosive attack, who could sit idly by? Who could remain uninvolved? In the entire Yunnan province, no one could remain uninvolved.

“Regardless of the process or the outcome, everyone will be executed. One escaped, but you two will face punishment.”

Huai Sheng was expressionless, his words utterly ruthless. It was clear he was truly enraged.

Or rather…

If Huai Sheng had any reservations before,

then, upon arriving at the scene and seeing the armed police transport vehicles gradually burning into scrap metal in the distance,

and the seriously injured personnel,

and the loss of more than ten armed police officers,

Huai Sheng completely lost his composure. More than three people—that's already a case that reaches the highest levels of government; let alone more than ten people, attacked in public. Who could cover this up? No one.

“Yes, sir.”

Director Shen’s face was full of bitterness.

What did he do to deserve this?

But, one must fulfill one's responsibilities in a position of power.

He assumed the power of this position.

Naturally, he must also bear the responsibilities that come with it.

Even if it might be an impossible task.

Jiang Peng sighed and said nothing.

He knew in his heart that this was too difficult; how could this be possible from this geographical location!

While he was still worrying,

just then,

a group of figures suddenly appeared before Jiang Peng's eyes.

In the next few moments, Jiang Tian, ​​along with Captain Wang and his team, and almost half of the armed police officers who were injured, appeared. His pupils contracted involuntarily.

Because Jiang Peng understood Wen Yulin's importance, he had mobilized almost an entire battalion of armed police to cooperate with Jiang Tian's operation.

Adding Jiang Tian's own emergency response team, the force was close to fifty people.

But now, almost everyone was injured.

Especially the armed police officers who were with Jiang Tian; Jiang Peng glanced at them, and his heart sank.

An entire battalion, a full complement of forty armed police officers, was deployed; now, only twenty-odd remained, and almost all of them were injured.

More than twenty casualties.

And the perpetrators?

"Quickly, tell us the escape route of the perpetrators. We need to start the arrest operation immediately," Jiang Peng said urgently.

Jiang Tian looked at him, puzzled.

Jiang Peng was extremely anxious: "What's going on?"

"The perpetrators...they've all been killed!"



Chapter 375 Let it be chaotic, the more chaotic the better, let it become utter chaos.


“Just now, I've basically killed all the perpetrators of the attack. Only about three survived,” Jiang Tian said slowly.

Although Jiang Tian's voice wasn't loud, it filled Jiang Peng, the father, with disbelief and surprise.

On the way, Jiang Peng and his team had received definite information:

The attackers numbered at least forty or fifty, not much fewer than the armed police force. Otherwise, so many armed police wouldn't have been killed so quickly.

But now?

You're telling me they've all been wiped out?

“Did they send forty or fifty thugs in the attack?” Jiang Peng asked, bewildered.

“Based on my observation, there were roughly fifty-two people. I killed forty-nine on the spot.”

Jiang Tian calculated the number of bullets he used; it was roughly one bullet per person.

In the end, he used exactly eight magazines of bullets.

Hiss!!

"Already killed?"

Jiang Peng gasped sharply.

Just now, he'd received a death order; failure to meet it would mean his immediate dismissal. This unexpected surprise was completely unexpected.

"Good lad, are you hurt?" Jiang Peng hurriedly asked.

"No, most of the time I was chasing and killing them. By the time I made my move, they were already running away," Jiang Tian shook his head.

That's why Jiang Tian's scanning radar is such a powerful weapon.

Whenever a red dot appeared, Jiang Tian could quickly ascertain their situation. If a red dot remained stationary, it was basically preparing for a sneak attack.

The red dots moved rapidly, and Jiang Tian sped up to eliminate them.

It only took a few minutes in total.

In this pitch-black jungle, to be honest, moving around wasn't difficult for Jiang Tian; in fact, it allowed him to maximize his mobility.

The cheetah's terrifying speed, coupled with its unerring accuracy and even invincible stamina, and most importantly, Jiang Tian's ability to pinpoint locations, meant that if those people tried to escape, Jiang Tian would be doomed.

Meanwhile,

Behind Jiang Peng, Shen Han was using a walkie-talkie to direct his forces.

"Proceed with the original plan. Tighten the encirclement and ensure all the criminals are apprehended."

Shen Han's gaze was grave.

He felt immense pressure.

Of course.

Most importantly, Governor Huai was truly enraged.

Although saying he would be removed from his post might seem like an angry outburst, if this matter wasn't handled well, Shen Han's future was essentially over.

The provincial deputy-level position he had longed for for so many years was now just a dream.

Ultimately.

Governor Huai hadn't been in Yun Province for long.

We only have a basic understanding of the economic environment and other aspects of Yunnan Province, as well as its political developments and future direction. We've only just begun preparing for action.

And now this huge problem has arisen.

And you're telling me now that the criminal organizations in Yunnan Province are this terrifying?

You only told me this in your second year here.

It turns out I was parachuted in from above.

Not to develop the economy, but to be the vanguard of a criminal organization and criminal gang.

Especially now, with such a large-scale and egregious case occurring, Huai Province is practically bursting with rage.

Shen Han dared not delay for a moment.

Just now, he mobilized every available manpower.

The provincial armed police, and even the military, were immediately mobilized.

To ensure absolute success,

the military alone deployed thousands of personnel into the mountains to ensure a complete blockade of all escape routes for the criminals.

As for whether the operation was too late, or whether the criminals would have already escaped by the time the encirclement was tightened,

Shen Han could only leave that to fate.

Just after Shen Han issued the orders,

he saw Jiang Peng arrive with Jiang Tian, ​​his face beaming with joy.

"Comrade Jiang, please cooperate fully with the arrest of those criminals."

Jiang Tian had arrived relatively early, and Shen Han assumed he had some knowledge of the attackers.

"Director Shen, there's no need to mobilize more personnel," Jiang Tian said quietly.

Seeing layers upon layers of armed men still arriving in droves, Jiang Tian was practically trembling with fear.

"What do you mean?" Shen Han looked at Jiang Tian, ​​puzzled.

The next moment, a voice came through Shen Han's walkie-talkie.

"Report, we've discovered a large number of bodies of unknown armed personnel, presumably the attackers from the recent attack."

"Director, a large number of bodies of attackers have also been found at the six o'clock position."

Just moments before, after Jiang Tian and Captain Wang came out, Director Shen had dispatched provincial armed police personnel to quickly and cautiously enter the jungle for a detailed investigation.

And now.

They had just found the bodies.

Shen Han stared blankly at the road ahead and to the left.

"How many bodies?" Shen Han instinctively asked into the walkie-talkie.

"According to our detailed search, there are about twenty bodies at the six o'clock position, all armed." Just then, the voice on the walkie-talkie crackled to life.

A moment later.

"Report, a large number of bodies have appeared at the eleven o'clock position. Our detailed search has revealed approximately thirty bodies."

Two voices spoke in quick succession.

Shen Han fell into a long silence.

He seemed still immersed in the voice on the walkie-talkie, unable to snap out of it.

"Director Shen?" Jiang Tian waved.

At this moment, Shen Han seemed to suddenly come to his senses, then stared intently at Jiang Tian, ​​probing, "You did it?"

"If you're referring to the attackers, Director Shen, I've killed most of them, except for three prisoners." Jiang Tian nodded matter-of-factly.

It wasn't difficult, really.

However, this wasn't difficult for Jiang Tian.

Or rather, Jiang Tian had handled many major cases.

This led him, to some extent, to underestimate the impact of this particular incident and issue.

It's no exaggeration to say...

This was an exaggerated level of enormity, reaching even the highest levels of government.

No one could cover it up.

The final ultimatum issued by Huai Province was to ensure the capture and annihilation of all criminals, allowing absolutely no one to escape. This was already a very basic ultimatum.

Because even Huai Province itself didn't know what the higher-ups' attitude would be after learning about the situation.

Of course, Jiang Tian himself didn't feel anything.

Because he knew from the beginning.

The higher-ups weren't afraid of chaos; what they feared most was a lack of chaos.

Because everyone knows which is easier to deal with: a deep-sea monster in stagnant water or a turtle causing trouble in a shallow pool.



Chapter 376 Heavy Losses, Leadership Shaken


For Kyoto, if they can find you, if you can come forward, they can kill you.

However, Kyoto's biggest fear right now is that these criminal forces are lurking in the depths of this pool, and the crucial point is that no matter who they bring in, they can't solve the problem or uncover the truth behind this deep pool.

Even if they wanted to take action, they wouldn't know where to begin.

"Where are those three thugs?" Shen Han asked.

Upon hearing this, Jiang Tian immediately waved to Captain Wang in the distance.

After seeing Jiang Tian's gesture, Captain Wang

hurriedly led the three prisoners closer.

"Reporting, Chief, all three prisoners are here," Captain Wang pointed behind him.

Actually, if Jiang Tian wanted, he could have kept quite a few prisoners, at least a dozen or twenty.

But Jiang Tian thought about it again; besides increasing the uncertainty, keeping so many prisoners wouldn't be of much use.

Aside from the three who surrendered, Jiang Tian easily killed the rest.

As for these three, Jiang Tian, ​​skilled and daring, had already pursued the person at six o'clock before Captain Wang arrived.

In other words, these three had a chance to escape before Captain Wang arrived.

The prerequisite was that they could outrun Jiang Tian's pursuit; they might escape, but only by a hair's breadth.

Shen Han's gaze gradually fell upon the three prisoners.

At this moment, the three men...

showed no despair or pain, no disorientation, befitting their capture.

On the contrary, they gave Director Shen the illusion of having survived a calamity.

These criminals, practically certain to be executed, were captured, yet they looked like they had escaped a calamity?

Is this reasonable?

"We didn't arrest the wrong person, did we?" Director Shen swallowed hard.

“These guys were terrified, who knew they were so easily defeated?” Jiang Tian muttered speechlessly.

He had thought these attackers would be incredibly fierce or tough.

After all, they'd done something like this.

The personnel involved should at least be highly qualified; otherwise, how could they have committed such a serious crime?

“You attacked our armed police?”

Shen Han looked at the three thugs huddled together, confused.

“That’s right, it was us.”

“Yes, boss, it was us.”

“We’ll confess to everything. Someone paid us to do it.”

The three men spoke one after another.

Upon hearing this, Shen Han’s face instantly turned grim.

For the entire Yunnan province,

the current problem is that there are far too many underdeveloped areas.

The situation in Yunhai is relatively better; economic development and various other issues related to backwardness and poverty have been resolved, and in fact, economic development has even taken the lead in the country.

However, due to various objective factors such as road conditions, the envisioned scenario of the wealthy leading the less fortunate to prosperity has not materialized.

The villages surrounding Yunhai remain extremely backward and underdeveloped.

To the point that...

due to extreme poverty, many remote areas are even under illegal control.

Many villages are located deep in the mountains or on cliffs.

The fight against poverty has always been one of the biggest problems in Yunnan Province.

In this situation, backwardness easily breeds many ignorant criminals.

These people, given some money, goods, or a gun, will do whatever they're told.

It's not that Jiang Tian didn't want to stay and lead them.

It's just that it's difficult to distinguish them.

Because these people are not first-time offenders; they all have a lot of red aura about them.

Clearly, even if they were quiet before, they were probably involved in smuggling.

Or perhaps they also had some illegal activities in Myanmar, after all, the distance between here and Myanmar is too close.

"Xiao Jiang, come with me."

After making up her mind, Shen Han glanced at Jiang Tian and spoke, then led Jiang Tian straight to the group of leaders behind them.

Meanwhile, Huai Sheng stood in front of the official car, not approaching.

Although they were leaders...

But as the saying goes, leave professional matters to professionals. Their involvement now would only cause trouble and increase risks.

In apprehending criminals, Shen Han, Jiang Peng, and the military leaders are all professionals.

Huai Sheng loosened the zipper of his jacket.

Despite the darkness and cool breeze, the temperature wasn't high, but Huai Sheng's face was already covered in sweat.

"Xiao Jiang, are you alright?"

Huai Sheng received the news immediately from Jiang Tian.

Seeing Jiang Tian arrive, Huai Sheng, though anxious, still smiled and shook hands with him.

This scene...

Instantly, the air around the leaders became quieter.

It could be said that...

Now, there weren't many people in the entire Yun Province who didn't know Jiang Tian.

Especially these leaders.

What a joke.

As the first recipient of the Special Merit Citation in nearly several decades, and a six-time recipient of the First-Class Merit Citation...

Moreover, he was only a temporary recipient of the Six Merits Award, and recently, he still owed Jiang Tian a First-Class Merit Award, which was currently being processed.

They already knew that more than one First-Class Merit Award was about to be awarded to Jiang Tian, ​​and another First-Class Heroic Model Award was on its way.

Such a formidable figure, with such a hero appearing in their territory, even they themselves felt a sense of pride.

At least, even leaders who hadn't yet met Jiang Tian felt a surge of goodwill upon seeing him.

But now.

Seeing Huai Sheng's warm welcome, these leaders instantly had a host of ulterior motives.

Because they had no idea when Jiang Tian and Huai Sheng had developed such a good relationship.

If you were to say he had a good relationship with Lao Mu, given his relationship with Gao Xiaolong, that would be understandable.

But the relationship between Huai Sheng and Jiang Tian was truly baffling to many leaders.

"I heard there were over fifty thugs attacking. Were you injured?"

Huai Sheng knew that Jiang Tian had immediately joined the fight after hanging up the phone.

It could be said that Huai Sheng was capable of unreservedly, even angrily, berating and rebuking Director Shen and leaders like Jiang Peng.

However, when facing frontline personnel like Jiang Tian, ​​the attitude was absolutely fervent; Jiang Tian, ​​at least, deeply felt it.

“When I arrived, it was very unfortunate that more than ten armed police officers had been killed.”

Jiang Tian’s words were followed by an even deeper silence.

Many leaders visibly paled.

The loss of more than ten armed police officers was a major incident.

“What about the key suspects you apprehended?” Huai Sheng asked in a deep voice.

Huai Sheng had personally participated in the Wenye Group case and understood its specifics. Even Shen Han had no idea what had happened.

“The important criminals, Wen Yulin and his wife, were being transported in a vehicle that was destroyed on the spot. Wen Yulin’s wife and the five escorts inside were all killed instantly, while Wen Yulin is critically injured,” Jiang Tian said, shaking his head.

Even Huai Sheng’s face turned grim at that moment.



Chapter 377 Alone? Oh no, the clown is actually myself?


"Those bastards," Huai Sheng gritted his teeth.

But the next moment, Jiang Tian spoke up: "The good news is, all the perpetrators were killed on the spot. Not a single one escaped. A total of fifty-two people were involved; forty-nine were killed on the spot, and three were captured."

Jiang Tian already had a general direction for the investigation.

The most crucial thing was that sentence, or that place, Wen Yulin had said to Jiang Tian.

That sentence could be the key to Jiang Tian earning a special merit.

Therefore, Jiang Tian didn't hold back much hope regarding whether to spare the perpetrators or whether he could extract valuable intelligence or information from them.

Because the fact that these people were brought out at this time meant it was highly unlikely that they would implicate those behind them.

Those criminal groups, arrogant as they may be,

still had some brains when they acted.

Otherwise, he wouldn't have been able to dominate Yunhai, and even Yun Province, for so many years; he must have some real skill.

Jiang Tian's words...

indeed caused an instant uproar among the leaders present.

Even Huai Province officials were filled with disbelief.

They had only heard rumors, hearsay.

It was all hearsay.

But now...

Hearing firsthand about the terrifying achievements of the person involved, even these leaders couldn't sit still.

"Little Jiang, this should be the result of the efforts of everyone present. How can you say you wiped them out yourself?"

Just then, a leader stepped out from the crowd, looking at Jiang Tian and speaking slowly.

Jiang Tian glanced at him, and upon recognizing him, couldn't help but frown slightly.

They had no real connection in this life.

However, in his previous life, Jiang Tian had interacted with this leader.

Fang Jue, the leader of the Yunhai Discipline Inspection Commission...

However, Jiang Tian didn't understand why this person had said those words at this moment.

It was practically a veiled criticism of Jiang Tian, ​​implying he was too ambitious and greedy, especially in front of so many leaders. This could leave the impression that Jiang Tian was arrogant, lacking in teamwork and consideration for his colleagues.

Jiang Tian racked his brains but couldn't understand it.

Since, aside from their previous encounter during the investigation of Jiang Peng in his previous life, the other party had always maintained a strictly professional attitude.

Why would they suddenly step forward now?

This was practically a blunt and unforgiving remark to Jiang Tian.

As expected.

Jiang Peng, following behind Jiang Tian, ​​subtly changed his expression.

Even if Jiang Peng's abilities were somewhat lacking, this was relative. His years of hard work and rise from humble beginnings in the system showed he wasn't stupid.

He naturally understood the devastating impact these words had on Jiang Tian's subconscious.

Sure enough, Jiang Peng's eyes flickered.

Jiang Peng noticed several leaders glancing at him with slightly furrowed brows.

Subconsciously, they probably also thought that with so many armed police officers involved, it was impossible for Jiang Tian to have done it himself, and besides, it wasn't even certain.

Jiang Peng felt a chill run down his spine.

Just then,

Captain Wang, who had been following Jiang Tian, ​​suddenly stepped forward.

Looking at the disciplinary committee leader who had just spoken, he suddenly said, "Sir, I think you might have misunderstood something. Unfortunately, after the initial attack, we didn't rush in to claim credit. It was only after Officer Jiang's operation that we arrived; by then, all the criminals had already been killed. It was all Officer Jiang's doing."

*Whoosh!*

Fang Jue was utterly astonished: "Didn't you lose more than ten men?"

That's right.

More than ten men lost, and you're telling me you didn't kill a single one?

Captain Wang shook his head: "The enemy attacked us instantly from the six o'clock and eleven o'clock directions. More than fifty people attacked us in a flash. We didn't even have time to react before we were killed on the spot."

*Hiss!*

Fang Jue instantly...

felt a burning pain on his face.

Of course.

No one could see the ugly look on his face now.

Because Huai Sheng's gloomy face was now filled with a smile.

"Good, good, well done, Comrade Jiang, thanks to you this time, you've done a great service again." Huai Sheng's heart pounded.

His eyes were fixed on Jiang Tian.

At this moment.

Huai Sheng suddenly felt.

The breakthrough point for being thrown into this chaotic place in Yunnan Province might lie with Jiang Tian.

That's right.

When he first arrived, without understanding the situation, Huai Sheng wasn't in a hurry, only able to be described as diligent.

But the problems in Yunnan Province were too serious, and as a newcomer, he was completely unfamiliar with many aspects.

He had always felt that the public security environment in Yunnan Province seemed okay, and he didn't understand why everyone across the country was talking about Yunnan and the "Little Gotham."

It wasn't until Jiang Tian kept uncovering the criminal organizations.

So, it wasn't until now that he realized the problem with a chill.

Then, after the problems of the Wenye Group erupted.

Huai Sheng instantly understood.

In other words.

The real reason the higher-ups placed him in Yunhai was this:

If he could completely resolve the crime situation and problems in Yunhai, or even Yun Province, then his future might be bright.

But if he couldn't, it would prove his incompetence.

When the higher-ups deem him incompetent, what can he do?

Retire, of course.

That's why Huai Province took it so seriously after such a major situation arose.

But now.

Looking at Jiang Tian, ​​he seemed to see a knife plunged deep into the criminal organization.

Indeed.

When Huai Province enthusiastically inquired about the details from Jiang Tian, ​​Jiang Tian didn't hide anything.

He simply recounted how he circled around, opened fire, and then the criminals were blown to the head one by one.

In short.

It gave the impression that it was very simple, as if he could do it too.

They were all like live targets, it was described too simply.

But.

Isn't this just nonsense?

Those were over fifty thugs!

Three of them were practically paralyzed with fear!

After dealing with the leaders, Jiang Tian took Wen Jing directly to the hospital.

After all, Wen Yulin played a crucial role in Jiang Tian's subsequent actions.

Because he felt that Wen Yulin hadn't lied to him.

The more this was the case, the more terrifying the implications of what was going on.



Chapter 378 caused a sensation. Who was the mastermind behind this?


After Jiang Tian left, corpses were immediately carried out of the jungle.

They were then placed before the assembled leaders.

Each corpse, as always, had died at Jiang Tian's hands with a single, deadly bullet; the bullet holes between their eyebrows were chillingly accurate.

The sight of over fifty corpses laid out before them sent a chill down everyone's spine.

Huai Sheng examined each corpse.

This was Huai Sheng's first encounter with Jiang Tian's operations, and his first time at such close range.

On the way, he had understood the gravity of the situation.

Therefore, upon arrival, he had issued a direct order.

And now.

"Very well, very well."

Huai Sheng let out a deep breath.

It wouldn't matter abroad; they could cause all sorts of trouble. But here in Yunhai, in this very place, such a problem had arisen—it was an outrageous incident.

Most importantly, there were so many onlookers.

If there wasn't an explanation...

Even if they try to suppress the news, all sorts of rumors will likely still circulate.

But at the same time, Huai Sheng felt relieved.

"In the past, I often heard people say, and often complained, that most of the heroin in the country flowed out from Yunhai. The criminal groups in our Yunhai province had reached an appalling level. To be honest, I never wanted to believe that such a small criminal force could be so arrogant."

Huai Sheng let out a deep breath.

His tone was filled with emotion.

This was no secret.

Helpin.

It was unavoidable in Yunhai province.

While it couldn't compare to the Triangle region,

the chaotic situation behind the scenes was absolutely no less serious.

Huai Sheng had tried to investigate before, but every attempt was fraught with obstacles, and even the smallest clues yielded nothing.

Huai Sheng had always felt...

The waters in Yunnan Province were like an abyss.

Now, looking at the assailant's corpse, Huai Sheng was both furious and extremely agitated, because it meant the criminal group was panicked.

Their panic meant Jiang Tian had absolute evidence.

Wen Yulin definitely held a crucial position within the Yunhai criminal group.

And as Wen Yulin's only remaining relative outside,

Wen Min's identity was exceptionally important.

Especially given her dangerous nature.

Those people dared to attack Wen Yulin so ruthlessly; what about Wen Min?

They would likely try to wipe her out as well.

Clearly.

Jiang Tian, ​​with his "King of Criminal Investigation" title, had also considered this.

This made Jiang Tian realize that the level of protection for Wen Min needed to be increased.

He understood even more why Wen Yulin had ultimately entrusted Wen Min to him.

"Unbelievable, you old fox. You even foresaw all this?" Jiang Tian narrowed his eyes.

Although he'd been outmaneuvered, Jiang Tian realized that

he could only protect Wen Min.

Whether for the sake of his police uniform or for the case.

Jiang Tian was unaware of this.

Just as he was rushing to the hospital,

the ambulance had already arrived.

Leaving aside other matters,

the most crucial witness and perpetrator, Wen Yulin, was subjected to the highest level of security measures.

Jiang Tian stayed behind to deal with the leaders, but sent Chu Jing and Lü Chun, along with two armed police officers, to the ambulance.

So, after the two fully armed police officers got out of the ambulance first, Chu Jing and Lü Chun followed closely behind.

Meanwhile, Director Zhou of the Municipal Hospital was already waiting at the hospital entrance with his team.

He was surrounded by the hospital's top surgical experts.

Because the ambulance was on its way.

Provincial Governor Huai personally called the provincial leaders in charge of the health department, demanding that no effort be spared to bring Wen Yulin, the witness, to justice.

With Governor Huai's personal order,

Director Zhou naturally organized the hospital's most professional experts, who were waiting at the hospital entrance.

"The wounded, quick, quick, Dr. Ding!"

Dean Zhou, eager to show off, stood at the forefront, organizing the operation.

What? You say the leaders aren't here? Who are you trying to impress with this show?

What a joke.

Even if the leaders aren't here, the wounded absolutely cannot have any problems. Besides, this is a task personally assigned by the leaders; it must be resolved.

Sure enough.

As the emergency gurney was brought down,

the experts didn't dare to delay in the slightest, hurriedly gathering around to assess the situation.

But what they saw...

Dr. Ding's eyes widened.

"This, what is this? What is this for? Who did this?" Dr. Ding pointed to the silver needles in Wen Yulin's head.

Sure enough, Dr. Ding's voice rang out.

Everyone's gaze instantly fell on Wen Yulin's condition.

Dean Zhou's breath caught in his throat. He looked at Liao Ying, shocked, and asked, "Director Liao, what's going on? Why are there so many silver needles in the patient's head? What's this for?"

Liao Ying smiled bitterly.

She seemed to have anticipated all of this.

Even when she first saw it, she couldn't help but find it unbelievable.

Just like them.

So, she immediately described the injuries Jiang Tian had mentioned.

As Liao Ying finished speaking,

Director Ding stepped forward and examined the needles. Sure enough, blood was still dripping from them.

It must be understood that the impact on a cerebral blood vessel is extremely powerful.

Once a blood vessel in the brain ruptures, the intracranial pressure will rise rapidly in a short time, compressing the brain tissue inside the skull.

Brainstem hemorrhage is the most dangerous. Not only are there few survivors outside, but even with timely emergency treatment in a hospital, there's no guarantee of survival.

After observing Wen Yulin, whose life force was weak but who was receiving a blood transfusion, and whose vital signs had stabilized,

"This...you did this?"

Expert Ding looked at Liao Ying in shock.

"No, no, no, how could I possibly have this level of skill?" Liao Ying hurriedly shook her head.

What a joke.

This terrifying medical skill—even nationwide, you couldn't find many people who could perform it.

This requires not only terrifyingly high levels of traditional Chinese medicine skill, but also extensive experience.

And a strong mental fortitude to perform this treatment on-site.

“Who? This acupuncture technique—there are very few people nationwide who can do it. Which master is it?” Expert Ding asked breathlessly.

Dean Zhou instantly understood what he meant.

His gaze immediately turned to Liao Ying.



Chapter 379 A 20-Year Lead, A Complete Explosion


In his view, this must be an important person. Because they were so important, it was likely that higher-ups had invited a renowned expert to provide emergency treatment.

“No, no, no, you've misunderstood. It wasn't some expert; it was Officer Jiang who did it, and these judgments were also made by Officer Jiang,” Liao Ying quickly interjected.

Traditional Chinese medicine is gradually integrating with Western medicine.

Among the experts present, most had some understanding of traditional Chinese medicine.

That's why they were astonished by this single acupuncture technique and the locations where the needles were inserted.

Some of the acupuncture points were even potentially fatal.

But at this moment, they had become crucial life-saving points.

Such terrifying medical skill.

These experts were unfamiliar with it.

However, when Dean Zhou heard the name Officer Jiang, he was instantly dumbfounded.

After all, Jiang Tian had been hospitalized twice, and Dean Zhou had met Jiang Tian twice.

“You mean Jiang Tian?” Dean Zhou inquired.

"It's him." Liao Ying nodded with absolute certainty.

"He has this level of skill?"

Dean Zhou's eyes widened, his eyes red.

It must be understood.

An expert's skill is immediately apparent. Dean Zhou himself is a professional, but by chance, he rose to an administrative position due to national needs.

However, this doesn't mean Dean Zhou lacks professional knowledge.

On the contrary.

Dean Zhou even comes from a family of traditional Chinese medicine practitioners, with a lineage of TCM knowledge.

He himself has been researching it.

With just one glance, Dean Zhou understood just how terrifyingly skilled the acupuncturist was.

Calling him a grandmaster would not be an exaggeration.

And what is a grandmaster?

It's someone who can turn the impossible into reality, someone who can snatch lives from the jaws of death!

Currently, nationwide, there are fewer than a handful of people with this level of TCM skill, and in Yunnan Province, there isn't a single one.

If this is confirmed...

Such a young grandmaster of TCM.

Yet he ended up in such a dangerous profession as a narcotics police officer.

What a waste!

"Has Officer Jiang arrived yet?" Dean Zhou asked excitedly.

He was now impatient to find Jiang Tian to confirm.

Now, every master doctor is practically a treasure.

Not to mention anything else, just this one acupuncture technique alone—no more than five people in the entire country can perform it.

"He should be here soon," Liao Ying said uncertainly.

Dean Zhou was breathing heavily, pacing back and forth.

Heaven knows.

For a hospital, what is the value of having someone with such medical skills?

So, when Jiang Tian arrived,

he saw a group of people staring intently at him.

A group of old men, all wearing white coats, were looking at him expectantly.

"Dean Zhou, why aren't you rushing to save people? What are you doing here?" Jiang Tian asked, completely bewildered.

Silence.

No one spoke.

Dean Zhou continued to stare intently at him.

Jiang Tian scratched the back of his head in disbelief.

The next moment.

Dean Zhou suddenly strode forward, grabbing Jiang Tian's hands tightly, as if afraid he would run away: "Little Jiang, you've finally arrived. We've been waiting for you."

"Waiting for me?" Jiang Tian asked, confused.

"Just as you predicted, the patient has a high cervical spine fracture, and not just in one place. Besides the neck, there's also a lumbar spine fracture, and massive brainstem hemorrhage. Fortunately, you took emergency measures, preventing increased intracranial pressure. There are also numerous bleeding points in the abdominal cavity," Dean Zhou continued.

"Since you've already finished the examination, why didn't you start treatment immediately?" Jiang Tian retorted speechlessly.

"Officer Jiang, your acupuncture skills are unheard of. We were just afraid that if we intervened, it would disrupt your treatment plan and lead to irreversible consequences," Dean Zhou explained.

In fact, if it weren't for Jiang Tian's acupuncture skills, they might not have been able to save the patient even with just the increased intracranial pressure.

Of course, on the other hand, the patient was now temporarily stabilized.

His vital signs had stabilized, so Dean Zhou actually wanted to witness Jiang Tian's miraculous acupuncture skills firsthand.

Putting everything else aside, this acupuncture alone

was enough to make him a legend in the medical field.

Jiang Tian didn't think much of Dean Zhou's ulterior motives.

First, Chu Jing and Lü Chun were assigned to keep an eye on Wen Min.

Actually, Wen Min should have been sent to the Municipal Public Security Bureau for a more detailed investigation, and should remain in custody during this period.

However, Jiang Tian understood Wen Min's situation, and even at the Municipal Public Security Bureau, she wasn't necessarily safe.

Just like the previous poisoning case.

For the time being, until this case was resolved, Jiang Tian's primary concern was protecting the young girl's safety.

The operating room was quite cold.

When Jiang Tian arrived,

he immediately saw Wen Yulin on the operating table. Her face was extremely pale, and her breathing was very weak, but thankfully, her vital signs were relatively stable.

"The brainstem hemorrhage hasn't been resolved yet. I'll remove the needle first, and you'll perform craniotomy at this bleeding point."

Jiang Tian pointed to Wen Yulin's skull.

The most important thing to address now was the life-threatening issue.

The bleeding issue was also a concern.

As for things like spinal fractures, those could only be addressed after her life was saved.

And then...

Jiang Tian didn't try to hide anything. He swiftly removed the silver needles from Wen Yulin's head, then flicked his wrist, inserting several more into Wen Yulin's abdomen.

He used hemostatic injections at several key points to stop the bleeding.

It was fast.

The acupuncture was incredibly fast.

Dean Zhou only saw it happen in the blink of an eye, less than half a second, and over ten silver needles were inserted into Wen Yulin's abdomen.

This left Dean Zhou and the several old men behind him dumbfounded.

Especially since Jiang Tian hadn't even looked at the examination reports.

He was directing the surgeons.

During this time, Jiang Tian also displayed medical treatment ideas far beyond his time.

Jiang Tian's red-highly recognized medical expertise was, without exaggeration, twenty years ahead of his time.

Many of his treatment ideas were simply unavailable in this era.

Numbness.

These people were completely numb.

Dean Zhou, who knew Jiang Tian's background, was utterly dumbfounded.

How could one person be so versatile?

He knew perfectly well that even though Jiang Tian was currently only a narcotics officer, he possessed multiple first-class merit medals.

Why would such a medical giant become a narcotics officer?

Why couldn't he make his mark in medicine?

The more Dean Zhou thought about it, the more distressed he became.

What a waste!

"This is a prodigy who could advance medicine by at least ten or twenty years!"

In the end, Jiang Tian even personally presided over a cervical spine fracture surgery.

Without taking or reviewing X-rays, he directed the surgical experts to suture the abdominal bleeding points and personally oversaw the spinal surgery.



Chapter 380 A Visitor from Kyoto, An Unexpected Guest


This involved neurological issues. In later generations, a spinal fracture would almost always result in high-level paraplegia, and the risk was very high; nine out of ten would never be able to stand.

If medical conditions were like this twenty years later, imagine how much worse it would be in this era.

But after Jiang Tian, ​​with his microscope in hand, sewed the nerves together with dazzling speed,

Dean Zhou was stunned again.

“Multiple nerve ruptures. Whether he can stand up depends on him. The brain injury is severe, but with proper rest, he should wake up soon.”

Jiang Tian let out a deep breath after finishing.

It can only be said that this surgery felt more exhausting than killing dozens of drug dealers.

Of course, the result was good.

Making the patient stand up directly would be like magic. It can only be said that between the absolute certainty of high-level paraplegia and a vegetative state, Jiang Tian forcibly gave the hope of recovery and becoming an ordinary person.

Expert Ding, who was standing in place of the nurse fetching tools for Jiang Tian, ​​was now looking at him with eager, pleading eyes.

The thirst for knowledge in his eyes was practically begging to be his apprentice.

“Someone must have said that this witness is very important and doesn’t need to be sent to the ICU. Just arrange for him to stay in the safest and most prestigious ward,” Jiang Tian said to Dean Zhou.

“I know, Officer Jiang, don’t worry,” Dean Zhou nodded repeatedly.

"Hmm."

Jiang Tian walked to the hospital entrance, looking at the dozen or so elderly men in white coats still following closely behind. His lips twitched involuntarily. "Is there anything else?"

"Um..."

Dean Zhou hesitated, then finally grasped Jiang Tian's hands. "Officer Jiang, I wonder if you could take a temporary position at our city hospital?"

"Yes, Officer Jiang, if this patient can stand up in the end, it would be a miracle for the entire medical community."

"Officer Jiang, your medical skills are absolutely twenty years ahead of the rest of the world!"

"It's unbelievable! To be able to perform surgery to this extent is simply perfect."

"Not only that, but you even combined it with acupuncture, perfectly handling various unexpected situations during the surgery. Many times, after the acupuncture, the bleeding stopped completely."

Exaggerated.

Numerous experts unanimously praised him wildly.

If it weren't for Jiang Tian...

After examining this patient, they all said he would have died halfway through the surgery.

Let alone being able to perform such a perfect operation.

They believed that almost no one in the world could do it.

“Forget it, you all know I'm a policeman. Being a doctor? That's a joke,” Jiang Tian refused firmly.

Mainly, he felt that saving lives was easier than arresting criminals.

Even easier than killing.

Besides, killing one person could save many more.

Although he gained sin points from saving lives, Jiang Tian received very few after treating Wen Yulin, almost none of the sin points he gained from arresting Wen Yulin.

“No, no, no, what we mean is, Officer Jiang, could you work part-time as a specialist at our city hospital? We'll pay you 100,000 a month. You don't need to work regular hours; you just need to come and help with consultations and lectures during emergencies,” Dean Zhou hurriedly said.

One hundred thousand a month right off the bat.

And this was in this era.

But Jiang Tian looked at the bustling city hospital. As almost the most authoritative hospital in Yunnan Province, it was the first choice for critically ill patients across the province.

The second choice was naturally to go out of the province.

Of course, nowadays, the city hospital couldn't treat many illnesses, so most patients went to hospitals in other major cities outside the province.

To be honest...

This condition...

Jiang Tian fell into deep thought.

He wasn't short of money.

Not to mention anything else, just with the blessing of the God of Wealth, he now earned over 100,000 yuan a month.

Moreover, he owned an office building, and the rent alone would be a huge income.

Besides that, Jiang Tian had nearly three million yuan in his bank account. This money came from bounties he had recently received for capturing a large number of wanted criminals, as well as other rewards.

Of course, according to the system's explanation, every penny of the system's rewards, the office building, etc., was traceable, completely legal, and without any violations.

Jiang Tian quickly reconsidered. Money wasn't the main issue; having more jobs meant more security.

Besides, if the opportunity arose, making the city hospital one of the best in the country would be quite fulfilling.

The prerequisite was that it wouldn't take up too much of his time.

"Okay, I can give it a try," Jiang Tian hesitated for a moment before nodding slightly. "However, it can't interfere with my regular work."

"Yes, yes, yes, definitely, definitely," Dean Zhou nodded hurriedly.

What a joke.

100,000 a month, and Jiang Tian doesn't have to do anything? It seems like they're losing out big time.

This was simply ridiculous.

Knowing that Wen Yulin's injury, once fully recovered, would be considered a medical miracle nationwide, even worldwide, was a given.

Using this as publicity, their city hospital could become famous throughout the country and even the world; the resulting profits were unimaginable.

"Dean Zhou, but... Officer Jiang doesn't have a medical degree or a medical license. Won't that have an impact?" Liao Ying asked worriedly.

But Dean Zhou sneered, "These rules are only for charlatans. A true medical master wouldn't have such rules."

At Jiang Tian's level, who could guarantee they wouldn't get sick and need his help?

Besides, this level of medical skill had been verified on-site.

It couldn't be faked.

After the surgery, when they took the X-rays, they were utterly dumbfounded.

The suturing and treatment were done perfectly, flawlessly.

Of course.

These were just minor incidents for Jiang Tian.

"You two, keep an eye on Wen Yulin. Be vigilant for any suspicious individuals." Jiang Tian checked the time; it was already past nine o'clock at night.

Although there had been many events today, including this major incident, they had only arrested three insignificant prisoners, and the interrogation wouldn't yield any useful information.

Jiang Tian was too lazy to return to the city police station.

"Yes, sir."

Chu Jing and Lü Chun looked at Jiang Tian, ​​couldn't help but swallow hard, and then left in a daze.

After all...

They had encountered a god today.

A narcotics officer and a medical luminary—two seemingly unrelated professions. They couldn't fathom how one person could be so proficient in both.

Moreover, each had reached such a terrifying level.

"What are your plans now?"

Actually, Jiang Tian was now facing a dilemma, looking at Wen Min.

After all, this girl was indeed in danger. If left unattended, she probably wouldn't live more than a few days before being killed by the criminal group.

But having taken her in, Jiang Tian, ​​seeing the news of a special merit award, was also planning to inquire about it.

"Officer Jiang, I'll stay at the hospital to take care of her," Wen Min said, her lips moving.

At this moment, no amount of gratitude could be uttered.

Because Wen Min knew that if it weren't for Jiang Tian, ​​her father would have died countless times over.

"That's fine," Jiang Tian nodded slightly.

Just then, Jiang Tian's phone suddenly rang.

When Jiang Tian answered the phone, Zhao Qiuni's voice came through: "Officer Jiang, some of my friends from Kyoto want to meet you."



Chapter 381 Reunion with Zhao Qiuni, Family, Top-Tier Second Generation


"A friend, a friend from Beijing?"

Jiang Tian squinted, glancing at the time. He had originally intended to visit the place Wen Yulin had mentioned, but it was only seven or eight o'clock in the evening; there was still plenty of time.

Besides, seven or eight o'clock wasn't exactly a safe time for reconnaissance. Of course, most importantly, Jiang Tian hadn't forgotten about the follow-up to the Wenye Group.

At midnight, Jiang Tian agreed to Su Tong's request. It was a perfect opportunity to discuss the follow-up issue regarding the Wenye Group with Su Tong.

Jiang Tian had a pretty good impression of Su Tong. Although there might have been some misunderstandings at the time, this young woman was undeniably honest; she was truly willing to jump into the water, willing to risk her life to atone for her guilt.

He could absolutely trust someone like her, and he had no fear of any underhanded tactics.

"Okay, I'll head over now."

Jiang Tian nodded in agreement, thinking he had plenty of time.

Given the current situation...

Jiang Tian already had a strong network of connections.

However, who would complain about having too few friends?

Besides, if Jiang Tian remembered correctly, the people from Beijing had consistently provided him with considerable assistance.

Take, for example, the matter of making the news.

Thinking of the news, Jiang Tian thought of Zhao Cainan, Zhao Qiuni's aunt, who had helped him a great deal.

Most importantly, making the news, even though Jiang Tian appeared anonymously, the assistance it provided was absolutely terrifying.

It was practically a goldmine for Jiang Tian, ​​an invincible one at that. Even if Jiang Tian encountered any problems in the future, those people would have to think twice.

Anyone registered in Beijing, or even someone who had made the news, was someone they could easily influence.

"Where are you? I'll come pick you up," Zhao Qiuni said, her tone clearly filled with surprise and joy.

“It’s okay, several of my friends want to see you, and besides, I have your car, don’t bother taking a taxi. The city hospital is very close, I’ll be there quickly.” Zhao Qiuni hung up the phone quickly after saying that.

It probably wasn’t even ten minutes.

A Phaeton was already parked at the hospital entrance.

After Jiang Tian got in, he saw that Zhao Qiuni was alone. He sat in the passenger seat: “Where did you guys go?”

It was clear that Zhao Qiuni had a good time and was in a good mood.

Her eyes narrowed when she saw Jiang Tian.

Eat?

Jiang Tian thought for a moment and realized that he really hadn't had time to eat tonight.

"Never mind, you guys come with me, it's my treat tonight," Jiang Tian said with a wave of his hand.

"Brother Jiang, you're so kind!" Zhao Qiuni said, her face beaming with joy.

Of course, considering that the guests were all from Beijing, Jiang Tian thought for a moment and arranged for them to dine at a relatively upscale and private restaurant in Yunhai.

Jiang Tian hadn't actually been here many times.

He had only been here once, when Gao Xiaolong had taken him. That was a dinner hosted by a Yunhai leader for Gao Xiaolong, and it was Jiang Tian's first time there.

"Among the eight major cuisines of China, Shandong cuisine is the most prominent. And the owner of this Shandong restaurant, I heard, was once an apprentice of a royal chef. I don't know if it's true or not, but I've eaten there once, and the food is indeed excellent. The only downside is that it's a bit pricey." Jiang Tian wasn't short of money now, so he didn't care about the price.

Of course, considering that these people from Beijing were all wealthy and powerful, Jiang Tian felt it was necessary for him to present himself well.

"This place is very expensive, should we go somewhere else?" Zhao Qiuni pulled Jiang Tian, ​​instantly understanding the level of the establishment just by looking at the decor.

"Alright, tell your friend to come over quickly."

Jiang Tian came here for another reason: it wasn't crowded.

At least there was no need to queue.

Of course, this was only temporary.

As his living standards improved, Jiang Tian knew that in a few years, he probably wouldn't even be able to get a reservation here.

Of course, the economy hadn't fully taken off yet, and wealthy people were still few and far between.

After reserving a private room, Jiang Tian ordered the food.

Suddenly, he remembered that he seemed to have some culinary skills.

If he upgraded them, he might not be any worse than this professional chef.

"Oh well, I won't need them anyway," Jiang Tian sighed.

Zhao Qiuni looked at the menu, her face clearly showing pain.

This almost made Jiang Tian laugh: "I'm treating, why are you so stingy? Anyone who didn't know better would think you were paying."

Zhao Qiuni pouted: "If you're going to treat them, just go to a street stall. Coming here is a complete waste."

Hearing this, Jiang Tian's eyes flashed instantly.

From that short sentence, Jiang Tian could glean some meaning.

'It seems Zhao Qiuni's family background is among the best in Beijing, even within this group. That's interesting. Why was Zhao Wei sent to Yunhai?'

Jiang Tian narrowed his eyes.

However, Zhao Qiuni clearly hadn't thought that far ahead.

"Actually, I invited you along this time because I wanted to introduce you to someone," Zhao Qiuni suddenly said.

"Oh?" Jiang Tian showed some interest.

She just felt like she could help Jiang Tian.

As for Jiang Tian, ​​coming from Beijing to the Discipline Inspection Commission for "gilding his resume" was something he was indifferent to.

Because his job had absolutely nothing to do with the Discipline Inspection Commission.

After all, if there was any connection, it would only be if Jiang Tian were involved in corruption or bribery.

"Actually, there's no need."

Jiang Tian suddenly felt that he shouldn't have come.

Of course, Jiang Tian had nothing to hide, so he didn't really care.

However, seeing Zhao Qiuni's enthusiasm, Jiang Tian didn't refuse her request.

I waited for about ten minutes.

Four people entered the private room: two men and two women.

One of the men was in his thirties, and the other looked to be in his mid-twenties.

The two girls looked much younger; Jiang Tian even felt they might be about his age.

"When you came, didn't you want to see Officer Jiang? Look, this is Officer Jiang."

Zhao Qiuni pointed at Jiang Tian.

The two young girls who entered the private room were beaming with surprise.



Chapter 382 Flowers in a Greenhouse, Each More Terrifying Than the Last


“Officer Jiang, just call me Han Lu. I just graduated from university this year and am doing my internship at the Beijing Procuratorate.”

One of the young women approached Jiang Tian and shook his hand with delight.

“Hello.”

Jiang Tian was taken aback by the two young women's enthusiasm.

Mainly, it was too much enthusiasm.

The mention of the Beijing Procuratorate, however, made Jiang Tian's heart skip a beat.

Goodness.

Sure enough, nothing here is simple.

Right after graduation, she's already working in a Beijing institution.

Through introductions, the other young woman, Si Cheng, also just graduated this year and is interning at the Supreme People's Court in Beijing.

Both are already pursuing graduate studies while working.

In short, none of them are simple.

As for the two men.

One is a businessman, though Jiang Tian didn't inquire about his profession.

However, it's related to real estate.

The one who caught Jiang Tian's attention was the older man, probably close to thirty.

This is what Zhao Qiuni was referring to.

The disciplinary inspection personnel who are about to come to Yunhai from Beijing.

You should know.

People with these kinds of connections generally wield immense power.

And their information network is extremely efficient. Under normal circumstances, they would never be able to enter uncontrollable environments; even their routes are planned.

Why are they coming to work in Yunhai?

Is Beijing planning some kind of operation against Yunhai?

Jiang Tian pondered this in his mind.

Since acquiring the talent of the King of Criminal Investigation, not only in solving cases, but Jiang Tian's abilities in psychology and analysis have also increased dramatically.

He is often able to glean crucial clues from limited information, like this person.

“I’d heard of Officer Jiang’s name in Beijing. The problems in Yunhai are known nationwide, even within the police system. Almost half of all drugs flowing from Yunhai, and even Yunnan province, into the rest of the country each year. Hence the saying, ‘All the heroin in the world comes from Yunhai.’”

After introductions, everyone took their seats. The oldest person there was named Han Qi.

“Han Qi is Han Lu’s brother. I know him through Han Lu,” Zhao Qiuni whispered to Jiang Tian.

So they were siblings.

Jiang Tian’s eyes instantly understood.

This Han Qi was the one who would soon be working for the Discipline Inspection Commission in Yunhai.

“Don’t worry, Brother Han. Rumors are just rumors. You’ll understand when you get to Yunhai,” Jiang Tian smiled slightly.

You’ll understand once you get to Yunhai.

Yunhai is far more outrageous than the rumors suggest.

"That's why I don't believe it. After all, I worked in the Discipline Inspection Commission in Beijing and saw information about Yunhai. I understand that rumors are just rumors and shouldn't be taken seriously. It's just because it's close to overseas that these rumors surface. Regarding crime, Yunhai has long had a low crime rate, even below average," Han Qi said with a smile.

Although he was smiling,

Jiang Tian clearly saw a glint in Han Qi's eyes beneath that smile.

Clearly.

This Han Qi wasn't as simple as he appeared.

Even more so.
