"I've always believed that breaking the law and committing crimes are unacceptable. Ultimately, one should maintain moral integrity. We must resolutely crack down on those who willingly degenerate. Officer Jiang, I hope we'll have the opportunity to cooperate again after you come to Yunhai."

Han Qi clearly seemed to be filled with a sense of justice.

Even...

a certain kind of justice, well, it gave off a somewhat affected air.

But when Jiang Tian noticed Han Qi's gaze frequently glancing at Zhao Qiuni beside him, he seemed to understand something.

Jiang Tian had no interest in these matters.

No matter how many corrupt officials there were, it had nothing to do with Jiang Tian.

For Jiang Tian, ​​his job was simply to fulfill his duties.

If he were in the Discipline Inspection Commission, his job might be to fight against corrupt officials, but in the anti-drug team, his job was naturally to arrest drug dealers and crack down on drug lords.

Everything else was irrelevant to Jiang Tian.

As for why the two girls were so fond of and curious about Jiang Tian...

After chatting for a while, Jiang Tian realized that the two girls, Han Lu and Si Cheng, had heard about it from Zhao Qiu Ni.

Of course, Zhao Qiu Ni herself didn't know the specifics of Jiang Tian's actions.

But she did know one thing:

that Jiang Tian had saved her and Zhao Wei from criminals at a crucial moment.

Therefore, Zhao Qiu Ni raved about Jiang Tian, ​​and the two girls were filled with curiosity about him.

As for Han Qi,

Although he concealed it well, the hostility in his eyes and the competitive tone in his words were imperceptible to others, but Jiang Tian was extremely sensitive to these emotions.

"Officer Jiang, I've heard Qiu Ni talk about you a lot. Tell me, do drug dealers all have guns? Are they as dangerous and fraught with danger as in the movies?" Han Lu looked at Jiang Tian with eyes full of curiosity.

Jiang Tian pondered for a moment and shook his head slightly.

"I knew it! Movies and TV shows are all exaggerated; they're not realistic at all. It's peacetime now; there aren't that many gunfights," Han Qi chuckled.

"No."

Jiang Tian shook his head with a half-smile. "What I mean is, movies and TV shows are fake. Real combat scenes and arrests are countless times more dangerous than depicted in movies."

Indeed.

Jiang Tian thought back to his recent experience, especially when he rescued Gao Yangyang in Myanmar.

What happened?

Aside from Jiang Tian, ​​practically no one else would have survived, let alone rescuing someone.

Pfft!!!

The next moment, Han Qi burst out laughing.

"Officer Jiang, I understand you want to exaggerate, but there's no need for that. Dangerous? Tell me, how dangerous could it be? I know people in the anti-drug squad in Beijing; it's not nearly as dangerous as you make it out to be," Han Qi said with a smile.

Jiang Tian smiled.

He had to admit...

There are differences between this era and later generations.

Indeed, this era was somewhat more chaotic than later generations.

However, it's important to understand that the more backward a region is, the more chaotic and disorderly it tends to be.

Disorder breeds the three traditional vices: gambling, prostitution, and drugs.

These are the three most significant types of crime in the underworld.

In fact, more developed cities also have a lot of these crimes.

However, compared to Yunhai, they are far more serious.

Especially the capital city.

What kind of place is that? Under the emperor's nose.

Even if there are drug dealers willing to take the risk, they would absolutely not dare to overstep their bounds in the capital.

Chaos can happen anywhere else, but it will never happen in the capital.



Chapter 383 A small case gave the flowers a big shock.


The security situation there is unparalleled nationwide.

What makes Yunhai so special is that beneath its terrifyingly strong economic environment lies a chaotic underworld.

Wow.

Simply poverty doesn't breed widespread disorder, and wealthy areas are generally strictly regulated.

Yunhai, however, is both wealthy and comparable to the Triangle District.

It's practically a paradise for criminals.

Comparing Yunhai to the safest places in the country—not the worst, but not far behind—to Beijing,

it's safer than anyone else.

Too inexperienced.

Jiang Tian could tell at a glance that he was simply too naive.

He looked to be in his thirties, but his experience was still too limited; in other words, he was a flower grown in a greenhouse.

If he were to stumble headlong into the criminal world of Yunhai, a bloody head would be the least of his worries.

“Logically speaking, I shouldn’t reveal this to you.”

Jiang Tian said calmly.

“Is it that I can’t, or that I don’t?” Han Qi instinctively thought Jiang Tian was exaggerating.

“Han Qi.”

At this moment, Zhao Qiuni also noticed something: Han Qi’s attitude towards Jiang Tian was unfriendly.

This instantly caused Zhao Qiuni’s previously smiling face to darken.

You see, Zhao Qiuni initially had a strong opinion of Jiang Tian, ​​believing that Zhao Wei had been fooled by him and was a big liar and a conman.

But then, during their first meeting, they were kidnapped by criminals, and Jiang Tian single-handedly rushed in and took down the criminals.

This instantly caused Zhao Qiuni’s mindset to completely change.

From that moment on, Zhao Qiuni instantly became Jiang Tian’s biggest admirer and believer.

And as time passed, when Jiang Tian was injured and hospitalized, Zhao Qiuni was incredibly worried. During this process, a feeling that had probably transcended friendship began to develop.

However, Zhao Qiuni herself didn't realize that seeing Jiang Tian being targeted had ignited a surge of anger within her, making her want to speak up.

But Jiang Tian seemed to sense Zhao Qiuni's anger the moment before, casually patting the back of her hand, instantly turning the angry red on Zhao Qiuni's face into a blush of embarrassment.

Her mind went blank for a moment.

Until Jiang Tian's voice rang out.

"Of course, because there are quite a few onlookers, this case is practically out in the open. There will likely be all sorts of rumors flying around tomorrow, so telling you wouldn't hurt."

Han Lu and Si Cheng, the two young girls, looked at Jiang Tian with great curiosity.

Han Qi, however, smiled slightly, seemingly wanting to see what kind of case it was.

"Coincidentally, just an hour ago, during an operation I led—a whole battalion of armed police—to apprehend a major drug lord and escort him back, a TNT bomb attack occurred on the road, instantly killing three armed police heroes. Then, over fifty armed thugs attacked the armed police, resulting in the immediate deaths of more than ten armed police officers. Now, tell me, was it dangerous?"

Jiang Tian casually asked.

Actually, it was only out of respect for Zhao Qiuni that Jiang Tian used this case to give Han Qi a warning.

Regardless of the circumstances...

In Jiang Tian's view, Han Qi's background was questionable; whether he had connections or came from Kyoto, it seemed he intended to make a big splash in Yunhai.

It's fair to say that without Jiang Tian, ​​several bureau chiefs in Yunhai would have died unexpectedly in his previous life.

At the same time...

Jiang Tian also wanted to give him a piece of advice.

Some might know about Jiang Tian's achievements.

Others might know that Zhao Wei had reached the pinnacle of his career, receiving numerous collective awards and even sharing two individual second-class merits with Jiang Tian.

This had attracted some people, who thought Yunhai was a lucrative opportunity to gain experience.

But Jiang Tian was now making it very clear: Yunhai offered a vast amount of merit.

Criminals were an inexhaustible source of income.

In Kyoto, killing ten people with a brick would result in eight section chiefs.

But in Yunhai, killing ten people with a brick would result in eight major drug lords.

This place was truly dangerous!

If you have the ability, you can take any merit you want.

Incompetence.

Coming here, one wrong step and you're dead.

Even with Jiang Tian leading the way, look at how many people have been killed in recent operations—quite a few hundred.

Especially the Yunhai anti-drug squad, which has lost more than half its personnel.

They even had to borrow anti-drug personnel from subordinate branches to keep up.

“Are they crazy? How dare they do this?”

“You're not lying to us, are you?”

“How is this possible? More than fifty armed thugs, attacking an armed police brigade in public, this…”

Hiss!!!

It has to be said.

As Jiang Tian finished speaking…

These people, being sheltered, were truly witnessing such a scene for the first time.

Jiang Tian described it calmly.

But the danger and horror were palpable in his calm tone when he spoke of the deaths of more than ten armed police officers—those were lives lost!

"Impossible, Officer Jiang, you're exaggerating. Over fifty thugs attacking an armed police unit in public? If they really dared to do that, it would be a monumental case, shaking the entire capital. Who would dare do that? They'd have to be insane!" Han Qi shook his head frantically, his face full of disbelief.

What a joke.

How could there be such terrifying criminals?

These aren't just thugs.

This is practically a rebellion!

Unless they're completely insane.

"That's right, they are insane. And do you think those drug lords are playing house with you? Are they playing cops and robbers? Don't be ridiculous. This is a life-or-death struggle. Catching them means their death. Faced with death, they're capable of anything."

Jiang Tian chuckled and finally shook his head.

Overall, it's all very simple.

Of course, that's all.

Jiang Tian wouldn't reveal any more details about the case.

"Besides, you'll all know about such a big case tomorrow through the news. I have no reason to lie to you. To be honest, the impact is terrible. What you said makes sense; the capital will definitely be shaken. After all, even in Yunhai, this kind of case is the worst, bar none."

Jiang Tian sighed slightly.

He could only say...

This Han Qi seems a bit naive.

But his judgment is still sound.

Jiang Tian knows a bit about people from the capital.

Take Zeng Fan, for example.

He noticed a common thread.

Even though Zeng Fan came from a local area, he shared a common trait with those from Beijing.

That is, an inaccurate understanding of local conditions; they still viewed things from a Beijing perspective. This inevitably led to disastrous results.

Buzz!!!

Han Qi seemed somewhat bewildered. After all, Jiang Tian described such a horrific case as a coincidence. But was such a massive, horrific case really just a coincidence?

Wasn't this one of the top cases nationwide?



Chapter 384 Ingratitude: I invited you to dinner, and you suspect me of embezzlement?


These people were all from Kyoto, having grown up there. They'd never witnessed or even heard of anything like it in Kyoto.

They'd just arrived in Yunhai and yet they'd stumbled upon such a horrific case.

If what Jiang Tian said was true, then how vicious and cruel were these criminals?

Especially Han Qi, who appeared calm on the surface.

But he was already holding a cup, unconsciously taking a sip of water to calm his nerves.

Unconsciously,

the table was already laden with dishes.

Jiang Tian, ​​showing his respect, had ordered quite a lot of food.

"Brother Jiang, you eat first."

Zhao Qiuni, her eyes practically shining, picked up her chopsticks and unceremoniously put food into Jiang Tian's small dish.

"Thank you." Jiang Tian didn't think much of it.

But this scene made Han Qi's expression worsen.

First impressions mattered.

Han Qi felt that Jiang Tian was just bluffing.

He had only just arrived in Yunhai. If Yunhai were a foreign country, that would be one thing.

Within this peaceful and prosperous land, how could such a horrific case exist?

Remember, a few years ago, the attack on Beijing, which resulted in the deaths of several armed police officers, caused a terrifying uproar.

It was directly called the most horrific and heinous major case since the founding of the nation.

But for over fifty criminals to attack armed police, and blatantly attempt to kill witnesses to silence them, the Beijing case from a few years ago suddenly seemed like a joke.

He didn't believe that criminals could be so brazen.

Therefore, Jiang Tian was just bragging.

Seeing Zhao Qiuni's admiring expression, Han Qi was even more furious.

Of course, while Han Qi was angry, he was still well-mannered. In his opinion, Jiang Tian was not worth getting close to.

Not only was he not worth getting close to.

Han Qi even felt that Jiang Tian was rather mercenary, vulgar, and a habitual liar.

Thinking this way, Han Qi lost even more interest in Jiang Tian.

And Jiang Tian wasn't being unreasonable; he had just been openly targeted by Han Qi, and the hostility towards him was completely inexplicable.

Let alone someone from the Discipline Inspection Commission.

Through Zhao Qiuni's explanation, Jiang Tian roughly understood.

Han Qi, thirty-one years old this year, transferred to the Discipline Inspection Commission in Yunhai. Of course, having come from Beijing, he was promoted one rank, eventually becoming a deputy director-level cadre.

In short, Jiang Tian roughly understood where that inexplicable arrogance came from.

He seemed to have a warm and approachable demeanor, but this Han Qi, in the end, was just an idealist with a bright and noble appearance, but actually an exceptionally narrow-minded heart.

And the kind who was self-righteous.

As a result, Han Qi became quieter, focusing on his meal, while Jiang Tian ignored him.

Han Qi's behavior instantly irritated Zhao Qiuni.

In Zhao Qiuni's eyes, Han Qi was simply looking for trouble.

Jiang Tian hadn't done anything after sitting down, and Han Qi immediately started targeting him.

Most importantly...

This whole thing wasn't actually Zhao Qiuni's idea.

Initially, Zhao Qiuni, along with her best friends Han Lu and Si Cheng, couldn't stop talking about Jiang Tian. Then Han Qi found out and insisted on meeting Jiang Tian.

After the meeting, it was as if Jiang Tian owed Han Qi money.

This really put Zhao Qiuni in a bind.

And Zhao Qiuni, to be honest, wasn't a good-tempered person.

She appeared harmless, and as Jiang Tian's biggest hater and most loyal supporter, it was quite remarkable that she didn't explode on the spot when her idol was being targeted like this.

Han Lu and Si Cheng also sensed that the atmosphere was a bit off.

Si Cheng quickly tried to ease the situation with a smile.

Han Lu didn't know what her brother was thinking today, but Jiang Tian had clearly done nothing wrong, so why was he so hostile?

"Wait, brother seems to be..."

Han Lu's eyes darted around, glancing at Han Qi and then at Zhao Qiuni, her expression lost in thought.

Han Qi is 31 and still unmarried, which is causing his family a lot of anxiety. He's turned down all the potential partners he's been introduced to, always saying he has someone he likes.

But he hasn't said who it is.

Could it be?

Han Lu looked at her best friend, Zhao Qiuni, who had just graduated this year.

Han Qi, on the other hand, is over 30.

Perhaps he's just too shy to speak up.

Han Lu suddenly felt like she'd stumbled upon some truth.

Meanwhile, Jiang Tian wore a smile. Despite Han Qi's age difference, he looked like a complete greenhorn.

He chatted with the others as usual.

Because of Han Qi, Jiang Tian checked the time and decided not to linger.

"I have some things to do. You guys do as you please, I'll be going now." Jiang Tian noticed it was almost 10 PM.

He couldn't just say he'd arranged to meet Su Tong at midnight and actually go to see her at midnight.

Jiang Tian wasn't sleeping, but the young lady was.

"Thank you for your hospitality, Officer Jiang." Si Cheng and Han Lu said goodbye with smiles.

This included Ji Song, who was sitting with Han Qi.

However, when it was Han Qi's turn, although he smiled, his expression was off. "Officer Jiang, you've gone to great lengths tonight."

"It's nothing," Jiang Tian replied politely.

"This meal must have cost a lot, right?" Han Qi sighed.

"It's alright," Jiang Tian replied dismissively.

But the next moment, Han Qi changed the subject. "Officer Jiang, for us civil servants, including anti-drug officers like you, our annual salary might not even be enough to afford this one meal."

These words instantly froze the atmosphere.

Even Jiang Tian frowned deeply; even with his usual temper, he was getting angry.

Damn it, I treated you to a nice meal.

What?

You have a problem with that?

"I've heard a lot about Yunnan Province. Although Yunhai appears prosperous and glamorous on the surface, with its GDP soaring, there are still many people who can't afford to eat, and quite a few are selling their children," Han Qi said.

This was true.

Jiang Tian wholeheartedly agreed.

It really wasn't a lie.

Jiang Tian glanced sideways slightly, surprised that Han Qi had actually done his homework on Yunnan Province.

“That’s right, Yunnan Province’s development has a long and arduous road ahead, and it requires everyone’s joint efforts. After all, Yunnan Province’s battle against poverty has been ongoing,” Jiang Tian nodded.

“It seems Officer Jiang agrees with me,” Han Qi said with a smile.

“So, Mr. Han, what are you trying to say?” Jiang Tian asked expressionlessly.

He had already guessed what Han Qi wanted to say.

“Brother, you’ve had too much to drink,” Han Lu said, pulling Han Qi along.



Chapter 385: The truth comes out, no more pretending, a real slap in the face.


But Han Qi shrugged off Han Lu and stared directly at Jiang Tian, ​​saying, "This meal included two bottles of Moutai, and the dishes all looked incredibly luxurious, especially this 'Yunlu Delicacies.' So many people in Yunnan Province are struggling to make ends meet, while we, as public servants, ordinary civil servants, have spent several years' worth of money on a single meal. Does Officer Jiang feel ashamed of this?"

"Han Qi, what do you mean?"

Zhao Qiuni stood directly in front of Jiang Tian, ​​her face turning pale with anger.

She was trembling with rage.

So angry.

At this moment, Zhao Qiuni practically wanted to kill Han Qi.

Mainly because, before this, although Han Qi seemed somewhat unreliable, he was generally quite steady in his work, and his values ​​seemed quite upright.

Plus, Han Qi wanted to get to know Jiang Tian, ​​so Zhao Qiuni introduced them.

But then, Han Qi said these things.

She treated him to a good meal, and he wanted to overturn the table—and he did it right after the meal!

Jiang Tian smiled slightly, showing no anger.

He gently pushed Zhao Qiuni away and smiled at Han Qi, saying, "I'm not ashamed. This is what I should eat."

What a joke.

Jiang Tian had never considered forcing himself to do something he shouldn't.

Otherwise, what was the point of his desperate pursuit of credit?

What was the point of his reckless rush to the front lines?

These enjoyments were all his due.

"As a righteous policeman, a public servant, don't you feel even a little shame?" Han Qi said angrily.

"Fine, then I'll tell you why."

As Jiang Tian finished speaking,

everyone's gaze turned to Jiang Tian.

Even Han Qi's expression became somewhat subtle as he looked at Jiang Tian's self-righteous demeanor.

Because, those who embezzle don't usually speak so righteously.

Jiang Tian said expressionlessly, "I've earned several first-class merits; this money is a national reward. Is that enough?"

"I've captured several wanted criminals; are the bounties from those criminals enough for this meal?"

"I worked tirelessly for days to catch drug dealers; is a good meal enough?"

"Or do you think you can just sit in your office and control the world with a few words?"

Jiang Tian's tone was calm and unhurried,

but every word was like a muffled thunderclap.

Han Qi's face turned pale.

"Are these reasons enough?"

Jiang Tian asked.

"You said it yourself; who can verify it?" Han Qi's face burned.

Jiang Tian smiled and said, "By the way, I'm not afraid to tell you, I was recently on the evening news. Qiu Ni's aunt did an exclusive interview with me. If you have time, you might want to check it out."

Jiang Tian patted Han Qi's shoulder.

What deep-seated hatred!

So much targeting, so eager to play the clown.

Jiang Tian was truly helpless.

He came here today to meet these young masters.

Of course, putting everyone else aside, Han Qi alone had left a really bad impression on Jiang Tian.

Ability.

Probably nothing.

If it weren't for his good background, he might not even be able to get a deputy section chief position.

"Han Qi, Brother Jiang kindly treated you to a nice meal, and you actually tried to accuse him of embezzlement? That's one thing, but Brother Jiang almost died fighting criminals, and you still have the audacity to stand on your moral high ground and criticize him?"

Zhao Qiuni stared coldly at Han Qi.

This made Han Qi's face burn even more.

Just then, the waiter came in.

"Sir, the total is 25,000 yuan. Would you like to pay by card or cash?"

Jiang Tian had already called the waiter to settle the bill, which was why Han Qi suddenly stood up and started his attack.

"By card," Jiang Tian said, handing over his bank card.

While they were paying the bill...

Not to mention Han Qi...

At this moment, the other three were sitting on pins and needles.

After all...

It seemed as if every bite they ate was Jiang Tian's money earned from risking his life.

It was all the reward he received on the front lines, fighting criminals.

"Over 20,000...that must have meant fighting criminals in a hail of bullets for a long time to earn such a large reward, right?"

*Whoosh!*

Instantly.

Don't even mention whether Jiang Tian felt ashamed.

Anyway, Ji Song, standing next to Han Qi, was already blushing.

It felt like he'd swallowed a bellyful of needles.

He desperately wanted to vomit.

It felt like he'd swallowed the reward the country had given Jiang Tian during his life-or-death crisis; every penny was hard-earned.

Of course, most importantly, the news.

"Qiu Ni, Officer Jiang is on the news, why didn't you tell us?"

Han Lu asked, her face full of astonishment.

Upon hearing this, Han Qi also looked at Zhao Qiu Ni.

"Brother Jiang belongs to the anti-drug team; he's protected by confidentiality, even his identity is kept secret. Some things are fine with me, but how could I say something like this?" Zhao Qiu Ni said.

“I knew it! I think I saw it a while ago, though his face was blurred. That officer's interview moved me to tears, especially the part about how, after I died, my body was there the first day, my enemies were happy, and my family was grieving…” Si Cheng stared wide-eyed at Jiang Tian, ​​then her fingers trembled as she asked, “Officer Jiang, did you say that?”

Zhao Qiuni's face instantly lit up with smugness.

She grabbed Jiang Tian's arm with both hands, saying triumphantly, “That’s right, it was Officer Jiang who said it. And the reason I didn’t tell you was because if Officer Jiang’s identity were exposed, it would lead to the exposure of many criminals. There was a time before when those thugs tried to retaliate against Officer Jiang’s family. Those are organized and premeditated criminals; they’re terrifying. If they knew Jiang Tian’s identity, who knows how terrible the retaliation would be.”

Jiang Tian thought to himself, his identity had already been exposed.

Actually, Jiang Tian had already considered just coming clean; there was no need to hide it anymore.

Because hiding it was no longer necessary.

Those who could retaliate against him basically already knew his identity; those who didn't wouldn't retaliate.

However, this still needed a catalyst.

Zhao Qiuni's words completely confirmed Jiang Tian's earlier statement.

Han Qi took two steps back, his face paling considerably.

Ji Song beside him looked at Han Qi with some pity.

Good heavens.

Absolutely good heavens.

A clown.

This is more than just being a clown.

Look at Han Qi's face, it's as red as a monkey's bottom. He's been repeatedly slapped in the face.

He stood on his moral high ground to criticize him, only to find out that the other person is the embodiment of morality.

"If a hero like this doesn't deserve a good meal, then tell me, who does?" Zhao Qiuni pressed Han Qi relentlessly.

She hadn't forgotten how Han Qi had just berated Jiang Tian.

At this moment, she was practically bursting with anger.

But now, in an instant, she felt completely relieved.



Chapter 386 Contrast, Asset Entrustment, Loyal Fans


Jiang Tian felt the touch on his arm and subtly tried to pull his hand away.

Because he hadn't thought much of it at first, now he sensed that Zhao Qiuni's attitude towards him was a bit off.

This made Jiang Tian's forehead sweat slightly.

After all, Zhao Qiuni's identity was a bit too terrifying. Jiang Tian only wanted to maintain a good relationship with her, but he absolutely dared not take it any further.

However, perhaps he was overthinking it.

Looking at Zhao Qiuni's smug and boastful expression, Jiang Tian realized he was probably overthinking it.

As for Han Qi, he seemed too ashamed to stay any longer.

His face was flushed, like a monkey's bottom, and he turned and left without a word.

"Officer Jiang, let's keep in touch sometime. Han Qi just drank too much; I apologize on his behalf."

Ji Song gave Jiang Tian an awkward smile.

However,

Jiang Tian knew this was just polite talk.

To be honest, let alone contacting this guy.

Jiang Tian was afraid of getting splattered with blood.

This hothead has headed straight for Yunhai.

Unless he can keep his tail between his legs, he's definitely going to mess things up, and he'll be lucky to even survive.

This is Yunhai, not Beijing.

This place is full of chaos.

And meeting this guy is definitely not going to end well.

Jiang Tian remained calm and nodded slightly. Only after the two left did Zhao Qiuni's anger seem to subside a little.

However, it was clear that she was still quite angry.

Han Lu and Si Cheng looked at Jiang Tian with curiosity.

"You two continue playing, I've already paid the bill." Jiang Tian felt uneasy under the two girls' gazes.

"Officer Jiang, could we exchange contact information?" Si Cheng pulled Jiang Tian aside.

"Well, okay."

Jiang Tian exchanged contact information.

Originally, Jiang Tian wanted to expand his network, but after making contact, he realized that these people were all likely to be trouble.

He decided to keep his distance from these privileged kids from now on.

It's fair to say...

Han Qi left a very bad impression on Jiang Tian during their first meeting.

However, completely avoiding them wasn't reasonable.

Take Zhao Cainan, for example. She conducted a very smooth interview with Jiang Tian, ​​and could even directly contact him through them in Beijing.

Problems that were insurmountable at the local level,

but in Beijing, they might be trivial matters, easily resolved with a single word from a leader.

In short, it was better to be prepared.

Jiang Tian understood this.

Si Cheng was a rather artsy young woman, especially after hearing Jiang Tian's words during that interview; she practically idolized him.

According to her, after hearing that interview, she dreamed of meeting him.

This trip to Yunhai was to see if she could meet that police officer.

She doesn't ask for much; just being able to meet and chat with him would be enough to satisfy her—she's practically a die-hard fan.

And the kind of fanatic who's completely obsessed.

Even Jiang Tian couldn't do that.

As for Han Lu, while it wouldn't be as serious,

she understands what Jiang Tian went through to make the news.

No need to elaborate; the harrowing and terrifying crisis involved is probably unimaginable to these sheltered flowers.

This is why Han Lu felt even more guilty about Jiang Tian being misunderstood by her brother.

However, Jiang Tian truly didn't have time.

He needed to meet with Su Tong as soon as possible and then quickly investigate the case leads.

Delay could lead to unforeseen circumstances.

What if those people were prepared after Wen Yulin was arrested?

Jiang Tian's approach to cases was always swift and decisive.

Previously, he might have chosen methods like setting up blockades and surveillance, arresting people and seizing evidence at the scene of a transaction with irrefutable proof.

But now, there was no need.

Scanning radar provided full coverage, and the "Judgment Eye" pinpointed the target.

What's the point of arresting people and seizing evidence?

Once the people are arrested, the evidence will come out directly.

Why would Jiang Tian waste time cautiously and nervously following those worthless criminals around?

After leaving his contact information with Si Cheng, Jiang Tian also left it with Han Lu.

Although these two were siblings,

Jiang Tian never judged people by their appearance. Han Qi was Han Qi, and Han Lu was Han Lu.

Jiang Tian understood Han Qi well; he wasn't malicious, just overly idealistic and dreamy—in short, a bit pretentious.

Furthermore, he himself was narrow-minded.

“Aren’t you using the car?” Zhao Qiuni asked, pointing to the Phaeton.

“No, I have things to do later, and it’s inconvenient to drive this car.” Jiang Tian shook his head, leaving the car behind.

Because of this car, what if Jiang Tian needed to apprehend a criminal?

Good heavens.

If the Phaeton got destroyed, Jiang Tian would be heartbroken.

It cost over a million.

And he hadn’t even driven it for long.

"Since that's the case, let Zhao Qiuni see it first."

"Brother Jiang, thank you so much!" Zhao Qiuni's face was full of surprise.

Her eyes practically screamed, "I want to kiss Jiang Tian a few times!"

"Alright, I'm leaving."

Jiang Tian waved, leaving Zhao Qiuni with his back turned.

That back view left Zhao Qiuni with a deep feeling.

The smile and joy on her face gradually faded; everything that had just happened seemed like an illusion.

She didn't know why.

Jiang Tian's departing back, combined with the gloomy night sky and the light drizzle under the streetlights,

as he walked further and further away,

gave Zhao Qiuni the illusion that Jiang Tian would never return.

"Brother Jiang, please don't let anything happen to you." Zhao Qiuni's smile faded, her lips a little dry.

Despite her fiery and flamboyant appearance, Zhao Qiuni possesses a delicate and sensitive heart.

Jiang Tian had been frowning ever since they met, clearly troubled.

And today, such a serious case had occurred.

For some reason, Zhao Qiuni felt a little flustered and irritated as she watched Jiang Tian's departing figure.

"From now on, if Han Qi is with you when you go out, don't call me."

At this moment, Zhao Qiuni suddenly spoke to Han Lu and Si Cheng.

Si Cheng was fine.

But Han Lu smiled bitterly, "My brother, he... sigh..."

"If you say another word for him, we won't even be friends anymore," Zhao Qiuni said firmly.

And indeed.

As Zhao Qiuni's expression turned completely serious, Han Lu realized the gravity of the situation.

Her face also filled with bitterness.

Of all the women in the group, Zhao Qiuni came from the best family.

It was as terrifying as it could possibly be.

Han Lu had originally wanted to matchmake Han Qi and Zhao Qiuni, but now she understood.

It was simply impossible.

"Also, I plan to settle down in Yunhai from now on," Zhao Qiuni said, her eyes gleaming.

Just before they arrived,

Jiang Tian had told her some things.

This was the first time Zhao Qiuni had learned this.

It turned out that Jiang Tian possessed so many terrifying assets.

And so many investments.

Jiang Tian, ​​thinking that some needed management, directly entrusted them to Zhao Qiuni.

Anyway, Zhao Qiuni was in business, and with her teacher Qin Shiyu helping her, Jiang Tian wasn't afraid at all.

Besides, it was a free gift from the system anyway, so even if they lost money, it wouldn't matter.

"Hey, Qiuni, what are these people doing?"



Chapter 387 Drunk and disorderly conduct, daring to cause trouble in the capital?


"Officer Jiang is incredibly busy, he hasn't had a break for days. You all saw it; it was a real struggle for him to squeeze in some time to have dinner with us, especially considering such a big case just happened."

After Jiang Tian left, the three women who followed him walked to the restaurant entrance, and only spoke with deep emotion after his figure disappeared from sight.

Si Cheng, in particular, stared at Jiang Tian's retreating figure, unable to recover for a long time.

Of course, this was purely out of genuine admiration.

Putting everything else aside, the kind of work with almost no off-hours and constant overtime—putting herself in his shoes, Si Cheng felt she couldn't handle it.

And it was such a dangerous job.

"Yes, it's so dangerous. He should at least have enough rest time, otherwise, wouldn't it be even more dangerous? How can he fight criminals?"

Han Lu followed closely behind, even feeling a pang of guilt towards Jiang Tian.

She was fair, not biased.

Tonight, in Han Lu's view, her brother was simply being unreasonable.

Jiang Tian kindly invited them to dinner, and he just flipped the table, accusing them of embezzlement.

This…

Even Han Lu looked embarrassed in front of Zhao Qiuni: "Qiuni, don't take tonight's events to heart. You know my brother's temper has always been like this. He's offended quite a few people before because of things like this, but deep down he's still quite upright."

"Han Lu, if you consider me a friend, don't mention him. I don't want to see him again." Zhao Qiuni said calmly.

However, Han Lu and Si Cheng, who understood Zhao Qiuni, instantly perked up, their expressions changing slightly.

Because they understood Zhao Qiuni, they knew even better.

The calmer she remained, the more it showed that Zhao Qiuni's inner anger was about to explode.

Han Lu nodded with a wry smile.

But in her heart, she was still unwilling to give up, thinking about how to ease the tension.

Moreover, it's not certain whether Jiang Tian's words are true; perhaps he exaggerated or was just putting on a show?

Of course, Han Lu is different from Han Qi; she wouldn't bring it up directly.

At least she's a bit more sensible than her brother, Han Qi.

As the three of them talked, they walked out of the alley.

That's why it's called.

This restaurant's main selling point is understated luxury with sophistication, and most importantly, it's incredibly secluded.

It's a quiet oasis amidst the hustle and bustle.

Located in the city center, the entrance is a forest park, and there's a long alley to walk into.

So although it's in the heart of the city, it has a very secluded feel, especially around 9 or 10 pm, when it's quite dark.

And the parking area isn't inside the restaurant, but in a small parking lot outside the alley.

But as they walked out of the alley,

suddenly, several people appeared beside them.

"Who are you?"

The three women were startled when they realized who they were.

The main problem was that there was hardly anyone around, and the sudden appearance of several burly men startled them.

"Ladies, why are you walking so fast? Come and have a drink with us."

Just then, a slightly drunken voice rang out.

As the sound rang out, two frivolous young men emerged, their eyes gleaming as they looked at Zhao Qiuni and her two companions.

Putting aside everything else,

the main reason was that these three were absolutely stunning.

Si Cheng and Han Lu were already quite beautiful, but Zhao Qiuni was even more so.

Normally, Fang Tai would have had the desire but not the courage.

However, he happened to be having dinner with some friends from the underworld that day, and one of them saw several very beautiful girls in the restroom.

They weren't stupid, though, and didn't do anything inappropriate in a restaurant of this caliber.

But as they were leaving, they noticed that the male companions of these beautiful women had all left. Suddenly, fueled by alcohol, Fang Tai dared to take a peek, and that one glance sent a chill down his spine.

Beautiful.

Too beautiful.

Each one more beautiful than the last.

His heart pounded.

If they hadn't been drinking, Fang Tai might have been able to tell from their composure and even their accents that these women were no ordinary people. But now, under the influence of alcohol, he didn't care about anything else.

"Not interested. A good dog doesn't block the way."

Han Lu's face turned cold.

"Fine, I like a feisty woman like you, Lao Jiu."

Fang Tai's eyes lit up instantly.

The young man called Lao Jiu, who was beside Fang Tai, also seemed to have drunk quite a bit.

Hearing Fang Tai's words, his eyes blazed with lust.

"You're asking for it." Lao Jiu's temper flared.

At this moment, they didn't even realize anything was wrong.

Because, from beginning to end,

to them, they were just three ordinary young girls, and clearly, this wasn't the first time; they seemed to have plenty of experience.

At worst, they'd just give them some money as compensation afterward.

“Have a drink with us, and everything will be fine today. If you don’t, you know what will happen.” Fang Tai continued his threatening and enticement tactics.

“Heh, you beast.” Zhao Qiuni scoffed.

She had already texted Jiang Tian.

Jiang Tian should have received it by now.

Actually, Zhao Qiuni had spent some time in Yunhai and had a fairly good understanding of the security situation there.

Before, when she had free time, Zhao Qiuni generally didn’t go out at night.

Recently, Zhao Qiuni had even been considering buying a house in Yunhai.

Mainly because Zhao Qiuni had originally planned to go to the international metropolis of Donghai with her mentor, Qin Shiyu, but later, Qin Shiyu also encountered some problems.

The market environment in Yunhai, which had seemed hopeless, now seemed to have taken a turn for the better.

Of course, Zhao Qiuni was unaware of this.

After reconciling with the Mu family, Jiang Tian specifically called Qin Shiyu, directly stating that the economic environment in Yunhai was good and hoping that his actions wouldn't affect her normal investments.

Qin Shiyu understood.

Actually, putting aside these unforeseen circumstances, this investment was,

a sure thing.

Yunhai is currently developing rapidly, with leading policies, support, and economic environment nationwide.

This is why many large companies choose to establish themselves in Yunhai.

Without Jiang Tian's subjective influence, Qin Shiyu re-established contact with Mu Feng to reassess the investment issues, and Zhao Qiuni is now in charge of this aspect.

However, these investments won't be completed overnight.

During this time, Jiang Tian, ​​knowing that Zhao Qiuni wouldn't be leaving anytime soon, conveniently handed over all his assets to her for management, becoming a hands-off manager.



Chapter 388 He went berserk, like a demon lord descending from the heavens.


"Little girls, don't struggle, or don't blame us for being rough."

Seeing the three women remain unmoved, Fang Tai and Lao Jiu's expressions instantly turned cold.

In their eyes, this was tactless, ungrateful.

Lao Jiu stepped forward, leering at the three women, swallowing hard.

"If you don't want to be taken down by all my brothers, have a drink with us, and we'll let you go." Lao Jiu pressed on relentlessly.

This tactic had almost always worked.

A slap followed by a sweet treat usually gave them complete control.

So they were very experienced with this trick.

Once they agreed to have a drink, they were out of their league.

"Scum of society, I spit on you," Si Cheng sneered.

Although four or five blond-haired men approached, they weren't actually panicked.

Jiang Tian hadn't gone far, probably hadn't even managed to hail a taxi.

Zhao Qiuni had messaged Jiang Tian, ​​and he'd likely be back soon.

However, through tonight's events, they suddenly realized that the security situation in Yunhai seemed pretty much as Jiang Tian had described.

No wonder.

Those who came to Yunhai, after returning, almost all spoke ill of the place.

That wasn't just slander.

This was the real Little Gotham.

Chaos.

Disorder.

They, back in Beijing, had almost never seen anything like this.

If they hadn't known Jiang Tian would probably be here soon, they'd probably be panicking and at a loss right now.

"You're asking for trouble."

Old Nine frowned.

He took a step forward, about to slap Si Cheng, a sudden movement that caused Si Cheng's pupils to contract rapidly.

But just as Old Nine made his move...

In the darkness of night.

A gust of wind blew past.

A cool breeze blew through the drizzling rain.

The next moment, Old Nine felt a burning pain on his face.

No, at first it was a burning pain, then the next moment, a searing pain of breaking bones.

*Thud!*

Blood splattered.

Accompanied by a cracking sound.

And Old Nine's screams as he fell to the ground, someone had unknowingly appeared before him.

It was too fast.

By the time everyone reacted, Old Nine was already on the ground, clutching his face and screaming in agony.

His nose was completely broken, blood constantly flowing from between his fingers.

Illuminated by the streetlights, Old Nine's gruesome state sent chills down everyone's spine.

"Who? I'll kill you!"

Old Nine clutched his nose, tears mingling with blood. He spoke while writhing in agony. In Yunhai, when had he ever endured such humiliation?

"Kill who?"

The next moment, a voice rang out, and Jiang Tian, ​​rubbing his wrist, approached Old Nine step by step.

"You fucking know who I am? You're finished! I'm telling you, you're finished! In Yunhai, no one can save you!" Old Nine screamed in agony.

At this moment, even Fang Tai was startled to see Old Nine injured.

"Kid, do you know who you hit?" Fang Tai was also startled.

To be honest.

Jiang Tian tilted his head; he really didn't know who he had hit.

But...

Jiang Tian's Eye of Judgment scanned them.

Good heavens.

Both Lao Jiu and Fang Tai, the loudest one, had already surpassed a thousand in sin points.

"What are you all standing there for? Kill this son of a bitch!"

Fang Tai commanded his henchmen, still cursing Jiang Tian.

But Jiang Tian glanced at them.

To be honest...

Jiang Tian had previously dealt with ruthless criminals.

Mostly major drug lords.

As for these two, although their sin points had exceeded a thousand,

Jiang Tian had discerned from their recent intelligence reports that, while not drug dealers, they had preyed on many innocent young women.

The recent intelligence reports indicated that these two had just forced themselves on several young women yesterday.

As for these blond-haired thugs...

Jiang Tian frowned slightly.

"Kid, you dare hit Ninth Brother? You're really asking for death!" one of the blond-haired thugs sneered as he approached.

Then, with a swishing sound, the thugs pulled out butterfly knives from their pockets.

"Brother Jiang, be careful!"

Zhao Qiuni's eyes widened as she quickly warned him.

Si Cheng and Han Lu, standing behind Jiang Tian, ​​turned even paler upon seeing the weapons.

Jiang Tian turned and gave them a reassuring smile.

Then, turning back, Jiang Tian's face turned icy.

Regardless, these people were all from Beijing. To be honest, they didn't seem like much.

But a single sentence...

could often have a significant impact, especially if shared with elders at home. Such a statement could have a huge negative influence on Yunhai.

Tonight, he had to show them a satisfactory attitude.

Moreover, these scumbags may seem far worse than drug lords, but their level of wickedness is absolutely no less.

"Be careful when you make your move, don't hurt those three women." Fang Tai helped Lao Jiu to his feet and gave orders to the four or five blond-haired men.

"Kill him, stab him to death, it's on me if you kill him." Lao Jiu seemed to have done this before.

This made Jiang Tian ponder slightly.

It seemed these two leaders probably had powerful backgrounds!

But regardless, murder was inevitable.

And in this situation where the conditions for confrontation were met—possessing a weapon and able to defend himself without restrictions—Jiang Tian could kill him with a single kick.

But there was an easier way, and no need.

So, the next moment.

Click!!!

The sound of a pistol being cocked rang out.

Those unfamiliar with the situation didn't care, but those who were familiar with it watched Jiang Tian's actions. Fang Tai didn't feel anything, but Lao Jiu's pupils contracted.

"Wait..."

But before Lao Jiu could finish speaking,

Jiang Tian, ​​holding a black pistol, had already aimed it at the nearest blond-haired man with a knife.

In an instant.

Silence.

Deathly silence.

Sweat beaded on the blond-haired man's forehead, his pupils trembling uncontrollably.

"I..." the blond-haired man opened his mouth, trying to say something.

Bang!!!

A gunshot rang out.

With a thud, the blond-haired man didn't even have time to say anything before a bloody hole appeared in his forehead, and he fell to the ground.

Silence.

Deathly silence.

But.

After Jiang Tian made his move, how could he give them a chance to lay down their weapons?

"Quick, put down your weapons!" Old Nine seemed to have thought of something and hurriedly shouted.

But the next moment...

Their speed in lowering their weapons couldn't match Jiang Tian's speed in taking action.

In fact, just before Lao Jiu shouted, Jiang Tian's gunshots rang out, as if timed perfectly.

If there was any regret...

it was probably that Lao Jiu and Fang Tai hadn't immediately used their weapons. Otherwise, Jiang Tian wouldn't have hesitated to shoot each of them.

After all, Jiang Tian had so many cases and so many things to deal with; he didn't have time to play cop-and-robber games with these people. If he could kill them, he would kill them; it would be doing a service to society.



Chapter 389 My name is Jiang Tian. If you want revenge, I'm ready anytime.


With the sound of gunfire, four or five blond-haired men, weapons still in hand, collapsed to the ground, unconscious.

Jiang Tian's gun was still smoking, but the muzzle was pointed at Fang Tai and Lao Jiu.

The two men were sweating profusely, their hands and feet ice-cold, staring at Jiang Tian in terror.

They felt their scalps tingling.

Even they were like this.

Zhao Qiuni had witnessed Jiang Tian kill robbers and drug dealers once before, so she was somewhat able to accept it.

Si Cheng and Han Lu nearly collapsed to the ground.

When they came to their senses and saw the four or five blond-haired men lying dead on the ground, their eyes wide open, they immediately began to retch.

This was a conditioned reflex, a natural bodily reaction to witnessing the death of one of their kind.

"You'll get used to it after a few more times."

A gentle voice came from beside them as they were retching.

Si Cheng and Han Lu looked up and saw Jiang Tian's warm smile. It was as if he hadn't just fired the gun.

No, even after killing several people, Jiang Tian's expression hadn't changed at all.

It was a genuine, heartfelt, or rather, feeling of complete detachment.

"You kill people, and you don't feel any psychological burden?" Han Lu gagged violently, wiping away the rising stomach acid, looking at Jiang Tian with disbelief.

Yes, after killing, there's often a tearing away of inner calm, or utter, helpless madness.

Only Jiang Tian, ​​in every action and every aspect, remained frighteningly calm.

"Psychological burden?" Jiang Tian's eyes were puzzled.

It seemed he was asking in return, was it really worth it for just a few people?

"Just a few people, is there any psychological burden to be had?" Jiang Tian shook his head.

Then he gave Zhao Qiuni a look. Zhao Qiuni had experienced it before; the room was splattered with various tissues and blood.

That was the most horrific time, the whole room reeking of blood. Zhao Qiuni had seen a lot, so this scene wasn't a big deal for her.

Humans are incredibly adaptable.

"You'd be lucky if this happened outdoors. The first time I saw dead people was when Brother Jiang killed over twenty thugs in that room; the floor was covered in blood, there wasn't even room to step." Zhao Qiuni was naturally carefree, and even had a mischievous streak.

Fear? Zhao Qiuni didn't really know what fear was.

"Ugh!!!"

It would have been better if she hadn't said it. After she did, Han Lu and Si Cheng almost vomited. They hadn't felt anything before, but seeing the blond-haired thugs being shot dead almost broke their spirits.

The main reason was that none of them expected Jiang Tian to act so ruthlessly.

He killed the few blond-haired men without giving them a chance.

Of course.

They didn't know either.

The current situation in Yunhai.

It's a state of strict crackdown.

Especially for the anti-drug police, who are of paramount importance.

While Yunhai appears calm on the surface, undercurrents are already surging, especially since the anti-drug police's authority to fire has been greatly reduced after today's events.

Even if these people were unarmed, let alone armed, Jiang Tian could kill them and escape unscathed.

Under this strict crackdown, everyone is equal.

"Don't fire."

Old Nine, punched in the face, had bloodshot eyes, but the dark muzzle of the gun was still crystal clear, sending shivers down his spine.

Fang Tai was even more terrified.

Usually a spoiled brat, he'd never seen anything like this before, and was practically wetting himself with fear.

“Kneel down, and I’ll blow your heads off with both hands.”

Jiang Tian raised his gun and said calmly.

Old Nine and Fang Tai’s legs went weak, and they hesitated almost instantly, displaying their cowardice and bullying to the fullest extent.

But Jiang Tian, ​​a master of psychology, saw no remorse on their faces, only gritted teeth.

They were probably seething with resentment.

“Judging by your looks, you’re not convinced, thinking you can kill me after this is over?” Jiang Tian approached, squatted down, and looked at the two with a faint smile.

“No, no, no.” The two shook their heads, their pupils contracting.

"It's nothing. If you could come out, I'd really like to let you come after me and kill me." As Jiang Tian spoke, he calmly pulled out his identification from his pocket.

Then he opened it, letting them examine it closely.

"My name is Jiang Tian, ​​head of the Special Operations Team of the Municipal Narcotics Control Detachment. Got it? Wait for your revenge." Jiang Tian continued.

Buzz!!!

Hearing this name, and his position...

Both women stared at Jiang Tian in utter bewilderment.

Han Lu and Si Cheng's eyes widened even more, watching Jiang Tian casually reveal his name, which sent chills down their spines.

Recently, the underworld in Yunhai has been in chaos.

Anyone involved in organized crime, or even just connected to it, knows the three old vices: gambling, prostitution, and drugs. The last one, drugs, is terrifying in the eyes of many.

Large numbers of drug dealers have been arrested and imprisoned; nine out of ten of them face execution.

Not only in Yunhai, but also because of the news reports praising him,

he became famous nationwide.

Especially for Jiang Tian's solo mission, in a foreign land, bravely infiltrating a drug lord's headquarters, killing dozens, even hundreds, of drug dealers and rescuing an undercover agent.

Although such deeds cannot be openly publicized,

which leader in Beijing hadn't noticed Jiang Tian's name and praised him highly? This is actually why Jiang Tian was exceptionally awarded a special merit.

In short,

In Yunhai, the drug trafficking industry is gripped by fear.

And the anti-drug police, as law enforcement officers, are naturally extremely famous.

"You're Jiang Tian, ​​you're Jiang Tian?"

The next moment.

Old Jiu's face turned deathly pale, and he shouted at Jiang Tian in a trembling voice.

"What a coincidence, it seems you recognize me." Jiang Tian nonchalantly put away his identification.

He didn't change his name or identity.

Jiang Tian was truly unafraid.

During this crackdown, those who led their men to attack anti-drug police and powerful second-generation rich kids with influential connections in Beijing...

Even if they weren't armed and had incredibly powerful backers, they'd be permanently imprisoned.

Of course, the difference is, if their connections were stronger, they'd probably at least get a suspended death sentence.

Old Nine's face was ashen.

A person's reputation precedes them.

Never heard of him? Fine.

But anyone who knows the name Jiang Tian, ​​or has even heard of him...

wouldn't tremble with fear.

“Besides you, no one else in Yunhai has the guts to start shooting people right away.” Old Jiu was sweating profusely, his face growing increasingly ashen.

He only knew one thing.

It's over.

Completely finished.

Jiang Tian, ​​gun in one hand, pointed it at them, while pulling out his phone with the other and dialing Zhao Wei's number.

“Boss, what’s up?” There was a commotion on Zhao Wei's end; he seemed incredibly busy.

“Bring some men to me. I had a few armed men attacking, and I killed a few. Come sweep the floor.” Jiang Tian said.

After speaking, Jiang Tian hung up.

Just then.

A series of hurried footsteps sounded.

Followed by a furious and incredulous voice: “What happened?”



Chapter 390 A Wake-Up Call for Moral Hypocrites: Do You Deserve to Pretend to Me?


Just after Jiang Tian had handcuffed the two men,

a voice rang out.

Jiang Tian turned his head and saw a familiar figure. It was true.

Han Qi.

Besides Han Qi, Ji Song was also there.

Jiang Tian quickly realized that while Zhao Qiuni was messaging him, Han Lu had probably also sent a distress message to her brother.

Judging from that, their return seemed a bit late.

Jiang Tian's gaze remained unchanged.

However, when Han Qi arrived, his first concern wasn't for Han Lu and the others, but rather for the corpses scattered on the ground, his expression filled with shock and disbelief.

"Who did this?" Han Qi gasped, his gaze sharp with judgment and scrutiny.

The next moment.

All eyes were on Jiang Tian. Mainly, except for Zhao Qiuni, everyone was breathing heavily; the previous scene had given them a huge shock.

“You did it?” Han Qi approached Jiang Tian and questioned him.

“Yes, I did it,” Jiang Tian said calmly.

To be honest, Jiang Tian was speechless looking at Han Qi.

He now had a general understanding of Han Qi.

Simply put,

He was used to a life of luxury.

He hadn't experienced much, a hothouse flower, a typical idealist, and, crucially, incredibly pretentious.

He always thought he was aboveboard.

He was standing on a moral high ground.

This almost made Jiang Tian laugh.

“Who gave you the right to kill them? The Party and the State gave you the right to carry guns, just so you could use them to shoot these civilians?” Han Qi asked through gritted teeth, his anger burning.

“Han Qi, shut up!” Zhao Qiuni snapped angrily.

But before Zhao Qiuni could say anything, Jiang Tian pulled her back, his face expressionless, because there was no need to be angry.

Given Han Qi's intelligence and abilities, Jiang Tian could roughly guess what would happen to him in Yunhai; he was bound to suffer greatly, and his final fate was unknown.

But someone full of idealism and lacking common sense, even with a terrifying background, was utterly useless.

Sometimes, background isn't everything.

“Indiscriminate killing? Are you out of your mind? These people tried to rape your sister, and you call them innocent?” Jiang Tian sneered, showing no mercy.

Since Han Qi had no shame, then Jiang Tian wouldn't hold back.

Besides, Jiang Tian had already made an appointment with Su Tong before leaving, and he really couldn't be bothered to say anything to this guy.

“These matters will naturally be judged by the law. You're just a lowly policeman; who gave you the right to take these people's lives?” Han Qi pointed at the corpses on the ground and laughed angrily.

Damn.

Jiang Tian was dumbfounded.

Most importantly.

Han Qi himself thought this way was fine, and Ji Song, standing next to Han Qi, actually nodded in strong agreement.

As for the two women, Si Cheng and Han Lu, thankfully...

Jiang Tian glanced at them; both of them were glaring angrily at Han Qi.

At least their values ​​weren't completely skewed.

Oh no, it should be said that Han Qi's thought process, while seemingly unusual, was actually quite understandable to Jiang Tian after a little thought.

Ultimately, they were just a bunch of arrogant second-generation rich kids who thought they represented a righteous judgment.

They flaunted their moral high ground, always believing themselves to be right, and immediately sympathizing with the weak.

Now, who was the weak? The dead, of course.

So, Jiang Tian, ​​the one who intervened, became a source of hostility for them. Coupled with the shock of the scene, they were instantly enraged, seemingly speaking out for justice.

As for Han Lu and Si Cheng, they didn't have the same serious ideological problems as the others, and having just experienced these people's perverse pleasure...

If Jiang Tian hadn't arrived, they didn't know what would have happened to them, so they hated these blond-haired guys with a vengeance.

"Don't be hasty," Jiang Tian whispered to Zhao Qiuni.

At this moment, Jiang Tian held Zhao Qiuni's hand and could feel it trembling; she was probably furious.

She had already jumped out, and if Jiang Tian hadn't held her back, she would have been pointing her finger at them and yelling.

"Speechless now?" Han Qi's eyes gleamed with obvious smugness.

However...

Jiang Tian remained completely calm, looking at Han Qi with a cold smile: "So, you mean that since they have knives, I'd fight them bare-handed, or I'd rather your sister and the others be raped and abused than lift a finger?"

As soon as Jiang Tian uttered these words...

Not to mention others...

Han Lu and Si Cheng's eyes were already blazing with anger.

"That's not what I meant, but you can't be so heartless..." Han Qi blurted out.

Jiang Tian understood.

This guy was targeting him the moment he arrived in Yunhai.

Something was clearly wrong.

Besides accusing him from a moral high ground, there was probably another reason.

The moment the words "heartless" were uttered...

The next moment...

Bang!!!

Jiang Tian slammed his baton into Han Qi's face.

Han Qi only saw Jiang Tian pull out a police baton. Before he could even process what Jiang Tian was about to do, the black baton grew larger and larger in his eyes.

With a loud bang.

Han Qi felt a dizzying sensation.

Then he collapsed to the ground, blood gushing from his nose.

Boom!!!

As Jiang Tian made his move.

Everyone present looked at Jiang Tian in horror.

Ignoring Han Qi's background, just considering that Han Qi was about to take up his post, his own position was superior to Jiang Tian's.

But now, at this moment, Jiang Tian actually dared to kill his superior.

This is outrageous.

Ji Song, standing next to Han Qi, pointed at Jiang Tian in disbelief: "Jiang Tian, ​​are you crazy? How dare you?"

"Pah! What the hell? You think I'm some kind of big shot, trying to show off in front of me?" Jiang Tian spat.

Damn it.

They really think he has a good temper?

You have to understand, he's the kind of guy who knocked Wen Jijiang out of the water with three batons in front of Huai Sheng.

This hot temper of his, he'd already repeatedly given Zhao Qiuni face during dinner, tolerating it out of her sake.

And this son of a bitch, Han Qi, actually dared to repeatedly provoke Jiang Tian's bottom line.

His temper is really something.

"It hurts so much, it hurts so much!" Han Qi screamed, clutching his nose, his eyes bloodshot.

That one baton blow would have practically broken his nose.

Blood splattered everywhere.

Han Qi nearly fainted from the pain.

But what bewildered Han Qi even more was how dare he?

He's insane.

Is this bastard crazy?

"Jiang Tian, ​​you truly have no regard for law or discipline. Fine, fine, you just wait, you just wait." Han Qi's voice trembled. After nearly fainting from the initial intense pain, he barely caught his breath before pointing at Jiang Tian.

But when Jiang Tian approached with his baton,

Han Qi's voice abruptly stopped.

That one blow shattered his moral compass.



Chapter 391 The Terrifying Background of Kyoto, the Scheme


"I thought he was all so upright and righteous, but it turns out he's afraid of even a police baton." Jiang Tian chuckled coldly.

He understood now.

Han Qi's purpose was definitely not simple.

Of course, regardless of the other party's purpose, was Jiang Tian afraid?

He was never afraid.

Within the rules, Jiang Tian was invincible.

Outside the rules, Jiang Tian's methods were nothing to fear.

If all else failed and things escalated, Jiang Tian could simply change careers.

If the police force didn't work out, he could always go into medicine and save lives, becoming a leading figure in the medical field wouldn't be bad either.

His red-level medical skills were already twenty years ahead; upgrading to gold would be like instantly becoming a saint.

Mastering all kinds of pharmacology, he could practically snatch people from the jaws of death.

Of course, Jiang Tian's skill system was ultimately more convenient in this industry.

"Officer Jiang, please have mercy!"

Han Lu rushed over, seeing her brother being beaten. Although she felt he deserved it, she was terrified and knew things had escalated.

Han Lu couldn't understand how Jiang Tian dared to do this.

It wouldn't be so bad if he didn't know their identities, but the key was that Jiang Tian knew their backgrounds, and even knew Han Qi was about to take up a position in the Discipline Inspection Commission.

"I already showed mercy, otherwise he wouldn't be able to stand up," Jiang Tian said calmly, putting the baton away.

Zhao Qiuni had paid special attention to this.

Jiang Tian had been empty-handed just now, yet the baton seemed to have appeared out of thin air.

Now that he'd put it away, Zhao Qiuni understood.

Good heavens, the baton had electrically shortened and was tucked into Jiang Tian's lower back.

That's right, Jiang Tian deeply felt that the baton was too useful, but also inconvenient to carry, so he had a department specially make an electrically retractable one for easy transport.

"I spit on that beast! He deserves it, Han Lu. Your brother would rather watch you be raped than let Officer Jiang rid the world of this scourge. So righteous, huh?"

Zhao Qiuni sneered, pulling Han Lu over and launching into a tirade against Han Qi.

She had been waiting for this moment for a long time.

If Jiang Tian hadn't restrained her, Zhao Qiuni would have slapped her across the face.

Sure enough.

Han Lu's face darkened as Zhao Qiuni finished speaking.

Hard words, but the point.

What Han Qi meant by being upright and just was exactly this.

"No, that's not what I meant. You can use force, but is it necessary to kill them all? They're all misguided teenagers, looking no more than eighteen or nineteen. Isn't this inhuman? Officer Jiang, you're capable of using firearms to deter them, right? Besides, according to regulations, you should fire a warning shot before firing. Jiang Tian, ​​you definitely didn't fire the warning shot, did you?" Han Qi covered his nose, his eyes blazing with anger.

He practically wanted to devour Jiang Tian.

"You're really being a jerk! What does it matter to you whether I fired the warning shot or not? You've read a little bit of school and you think you know everything? Do you even know what you're saying? Don't you know that an enemy wielding a weapon can retaliate without restraint until they are no longer a threat?"

Jiang Tian sighed, his temper flaring.

With a snap, he pulled out his baton.

Han Qi hurriedly retreated: "You're crazy! You're a lunatic!"

"Officer Jiang, please have mercy!"

"Officer Jiang, a few more hits and things will get serious."

Han Qi and Si Cheng were startled and hurriedly stepped in front of Jiang Tian.

Even the lawless Zhao Qiuni's eyelids twitched.

Thinking of Han Qi's background and identity, and glancing at the police baton in Jiang Tian's hand.

Good heavens, Zhao Qiuni was genuinely afraid that Jiang Tian might go too far and kill her, which would really cause a lot of trouble.

Although, in Zhao Qiuni's eyes, Han Qi's family background was nothing special.

In fact, Zhao Qiuni was well aware of Han Qi's intentions—to climb the social ladder through the Zhao family—and she looked down on him even more.

But if he were really killed, the trouble would be immense.

Just then, thankfully, a series of sirens blared.

Several police cars pulled up at the alley entrance.

Actually, by this time,

a crowd had already gathered nearby. Although the area was secluded, it wasn't truly deserted; besides, there was a restaurant inside, and it was doing good business.

Some people who had finished eating came out at this time, but seeing the scene before them, even those who had been drinking, they were all too frightened to move.

"Boss."

Zhao Wei led his men to Jiang Tian.

Jiang Tian nodded slightly: "These people are all rapists. This group tried to rape these girls, but I stopped them. They were armed, so I shot most of them dead, and handcuffed two others."

After speaking, Jiang Tian glanced at Han Qi.

It was only because Han Qi held a position, and a relatively high one at that, belonging to the Discipline Inspection Commission, that Jiang Tian didn't say anything.

Otherwise, Jiang Tian wouldn't care about his background; he'd just take him to the Municipal Public Security Bureau and give him a good beating.

"Yes, sir." Zhao Wei replied without hesitation.

Glancing at Zhao Qiuni beside Jiang Tian, ​​Zhao Wei immediately understood what was going on.

However, Zhao Wei pulled Jiang Tian to a secluded spot.

“Boss, do you know that Han Qi?” Zhao Wei asked hesitantly.

“Tell me more,” Jiang Tian said, offering a cigarette.

Jiang Tian subtly glanced at Han Qi. He had to admit, at this moment, although Han Qi was covering his face and wiping blood with tissues, he was surprisingly calm.

This made Jiang Tian keenly aware of something unusual.

Sure enough.

This Han Qi wasn't brainless; he had made a scene just now.

He probably had some genuine motives, but he likely had other intentions as well.

So, what were those intentions?

To scheme against him?

Jiang Tian's lips curled into a smile.

Now, Jiang Tian wasn't afraid of any of that.

Even if he were beaten up or killed, Jiang Tian would only face some trouble. His supposedly invincible reputation would likely only result in him resigning. Of course, that would be a sham resignation, but his identity within the security agency would remain.

This is an agency with unlimited law enforcement power.

“Boss, this Han Qi was very pretentious back in Kyoto, and he's never married. He's got designs on my sister, probably trying to climb the social ladder,” Zhao Wei said.

“Hmm, is his family poor?” Jiang Tian's heart skipped a beat.

“He used to be quite successful, but now…”

Zhao Wei didn't finish his sentence, but Jiang Tian immediately understood.

“Besides, back in Kyoto, I always disliked this guy. He was too pretentious, always acting like he was righteous and everyone else was crooked,” Zhao Wei grumbled.

Then Zhao Wei realized and said, "By the way, this guy's face is covered in blood. Who hit him? That was so satisfying."

"I did," Jiang Tian said quietly.

"Pfft!!!" Zhao Wei stared wide-eyed at Jiang Tian.



Chapter 392 What Seven Merits King? They're all serving the people!


"You said he was too pretentious. Is your boss the type to swallow his pride?" Jiang Tian chuckled.

"Boss, you..." Zhao Wei's eyes widened. "He..."

"Didn't you say his family was quite prestigious? What are you so scared of?" Jiang Tian said calmly.

"Damn, boss, you're really something. But you're probably in big trouble. After my uncle got promoted, I heard the bureau chief is Han Qi's uncle." Zhao Wei's lips twitched.

Although he disliked Han Qi, he hadn't really considered taking action against him!

"Han Qi's uncle, his own uncle?" Jiang Tian's brow twitched.

He knew someone was getting restless.

In the past, before it attracted attention, the bureau chief position in Yunhai was a hot potato; no one dared to take it.

But since Jiang Tian directly exposed the Yunhai criminal organization, all the cover-ups were gone, and the situation in Yunhai changed instantly.

"Boss, you have no idea how much of a stir Yunhai has caused in Kyoto. Uncle's promotion is finalized, but the vacant bureau chief position is going crazy in Kyoto. Everyone knows that coming to Yunhai means you can easily rack up some achievements and gain prestige. You know how much a simple second-class merit can affect someone's chances of promotion within the system." Zhao Wei seemed quite knowledgeable about this.

Jiang Tian's eyelids twitched; he knew too.

The numerous achievements he'd casually acquired were incredibly enviable and sought after by others, especially those within the system.

"The key is, my dad's promotion hasn't been finalized yet." Jiang Tian's face was strange. Jiang Peng's promotion wasn't completely confirmed; at least for now, Jiang Peng was still trembling with fear, afraid it would be canceled.

How come the higher-ups have already chosen a replacement?

“Boss, Uncle’s promotion is a sure thing. If you want to come here to claim credit, you can’t be shameless. Uncle’s promotion is absolutely guaranteed. The forces that have already taken over won’t allow Uncle to fail,” Zhao Wei explained.

Jiang Tian immediately understood.

But…

“The fight’s already happened, there’s no point in saying anything now,” Jiang Tian said quietly.

He wasn’t really afraid.

The fight was over.

Besides, did they really think the bureau chief had any real authority over Jiang Tian?

Do they know who’s in charge at the city bureau right now? Gao Xiaolong! With Gao Xiaolong here, Jiang Tian wouldn’t back down from anyone.

If they don’t cause trouble, everyone’s on good terms. But if they dare to cause trouble, even if you’re parachuted in, nobody cares.

“Officer Jiang, you will definitely face the full force of the law. Just you wait.”

It was Han Qi who spoke.

Even when he was just making threats, he did it with such pretense.

Jiang Tian drew his baton, and Ji Song grabbed Han Qi and ran frantically outside.

"Damn it, they don't know what's good for them," Jiang Tian cursed coldly.

"Awesome, so awesome, boss!"

Zhao Wei exclaimed excitedly.

Back in Beijing, it was fair to say that Zhao Wei's status in Beijing was roughly equivalent to Jiang Tian's in the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

Being neither good at academics nor martial arts wasn't inherently a problem, given his wealthy family background.

But the key issue was that Zhao Wei, young and naive in Beijing, had made quite a few embarrassing mistakes.

He was directly reassigned to Yunhai.

Zhao Wei initially treated Jiang Tian so well because he saw a reflection of himself in him, and they were both in the same boat.

Han Qi, back in Kyoto, had often used him to humiliate Zhao Wei when they first met.

Finally, the group lost all interest in sightseeing.

"Han Lu and Si Cheng are staying with me," Zhao Wei said.

Zhao Qiuni, sitting in the passenger seat, understood that such a major incident required at least a statement from even the closest connections; so many people had died.

Therefore, Jiang Tian took the group directly to the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

The Municipal Public Security Bureau was bustling with activity, as they were conducting a raid on the entire Wenye Group criminal gang that evening.

There were many police cars coming and going.

Even the interrogation rooms were already packed with people.

They had arrested too many people that day.

Later, they even borrowed the interrogation rooms from the Criminal Investigation Division, and even had their own officers come back to help with overtime interrogations.

In short, it was a scene of hectic activity.

As for Jiang Tian killing several rapists today, it didn't cause much of a stir; it was just a matter of taking statements according to procedure.

Then Jiang Tian drove them home.

After all, such a big incident had ruined their mood for sightseeing.

“Brother Jiang, want to come up for a cup of tea?”

Han Lu and Si Cheng went upstairs. In the empty parking lot, Zhao Qiuni leaned against the driver's side window and asked Jiang Tian.

“It’s too late, I’ll make time.” Jiang Tian glanced at her out of the corner of his eye.

Tonight, Zhao Qiuni was wearing a loose dress. Normally, it wouldn't have been a problem, but leaning against the car window, Jiang Tian kept his eyes straight ahead, afraid of looking too much.

“It’s a deal.” Zhao Qiuni’s face showed a hint of surprise.

She had thought it might be inconvenient with people at home, but that wasn't why she was staying here specifically. Zhao Qiuni said to Jiang Tian, ​​“Brother Jiang, don’t worry, I’ll call my dad later and have him put in a good word with their family. They won’t dare do anything to you.”

Jiang Tian was slightly touched, but he still felt a bit like she was living off him.

But then he thought, "Damn, I saved them today!"

Thinking this way, Jiang Tian felt at ease.

"It's not your business, don't worry about it," Jiang Tian shook his head.

Mainly, Jiang Tian wanted to know their purpose.

Meanwhile, while Jiang Tian was driving to the place where he had arranged to meet Su Tong...

In the emergency room of the city hospital, Han Qi, whose wounds were being treated, held his phone.

He kept gasping for breath as his wounds were being treated.

"I knew it. I sensed something was off when I arrived in Yunhai. Sure enough, this is how law enforcement operates. How can things be okay here in Yunhai? That Jiang Tian is definitely a black sheep," Han Qi said angrily.

"Yeah, those people certainly made mistakes, but they didn't deserve to die," Ji Song chimed in.

"So, Yuan Guangliang was pretty much right," Han Qi said thoughtfully.

As he spoke, Han Qi's phone rang.

After glancing at the contact name, Han Qi answered the phone: "Guangliang, I believe you now. Some people are probably just a cancer in Yunhai, relying on their connections and background to commit numerous evils and deceive their superiors."

"Why would I lie to you? Jiang Tian is a black sheep in Yunhai. Otherwise, why would we be keeping an eye on him?" Wang Guangliang's voice came from the other end of the line.

“Alright, we’ll act immediately after I take office.” Han Qi seemed impatient.

“Don’t worry, we have irrefutable evidence. Jiang Tian is definitely guilty of embezzlement and bribery. However, he has some connections, and I’m afraid…” Wang Guangliang hesitated.

“Heh, no matter how big the connections are, it’s useless. I said, how can I allow such a scourge to run rampant in Yunhai? Jiang Tian, ​​this black sheep, must be brought to justice.” Han Qi was furious.

But while they were talking, they didn’t notice a beautiful figure passing by the emergency room door. Upon hearing Jiang Tian’s name, her footsteps stopped abruptly.

A moment later.

“Minister Tan, I understand. Don’t worry, the ‘Seven Merits King’ thing is just a joke outside. Why are you taking it so seriously? It’s all for the people!”



Chapter 393 A Shocking Secret: Attention from a Big Shot


In a quiet restaurant, candlelight flickered on the table in the dim lighting.

Jiang Tian sat before a huge floor-to-ceiling window, his gaze lingering with a touch of melancholy as he looked across the window at a similarly sizable CBD office building.

Below, the city was ablaze with lights and bustling with traffic.

Clearly.

Jiang Tian could see that, with the passage of time, the entire Yunhai area was developing at an unprecedented pace.

Indeed.

Or rather, the entire nation was now accumulating strength, and the next era was about to witness explosive growth.

In this era, countless waves rose, becoming the wealthiest individuals of the time.

Countless people struggle to make ends meet, while city after city rises to the forefront of this rapidly evolving era.

Jiang Tian understood this perfectly.

The next decade or so after 2000 would unleash a terrifying surge of energy across the country, a veritable frenzy, with countless individuals reaping the benefits of national prosperity and standing at the forefront of this upheaval.

Yunhai, however, was supposed to reach the pinnacle of this era within the next ten or twenty years.

It will become a landmark of the entire Yunnan Province, or rather, the Pearl of the Orient in Southeast Asia.

At that time, not only Yunhai, but the entire Yunnan Province will be at the forefront of the times, and even at the forefront of all provinces in the country.

But Jiang Tian thought of the time before his transmigration.

Yunnan Province, as a severely affected area in the fight against poverty.

Ultimately, it still failed to develop.

But now, he's here.

"Looking at it this way, it's quite luxurious," Jiang Tian smiled slightly.

Indeed, across from him, in the CBD, was Jiang Tian's office building.

However, Jiang Tian had been so busy lately that he hadn't even had time for the handover of the property, let alone management. Plus, there was no one at home capable of managing it, so in the end, Jiang Tian entrusted it directly to Zhao Qiuni.

Before him, a candlelight dinner was laid out.

Jiang Tian felt slightly awkward, but having been reborn, he firmly believed that if he wasn't the one feeling awkward, then it was someone else.

The entire restaurant was completely empty; it was already past ten o'clock at night.

At this hour, Jiang Tian glanced around and, unsurprisingly, found that everyone dining in the restaurant was a couple.

This was a couples' restaurant.

Su Tong sat across from him at the table, glancing at him every now and then. Jiang Tian subconsciously touched his face, ultimately concluding that perhaps he was just overthinking things.

"Xiao Jiang, the entire reform opportunities for Yunhai, even Yun Province, rest on your shoulders. To be honest, without you, I don't know what kind of future or outcome Yunhai or even Yun Province would face."

Minister Tan's voice came through the phone.

Of course, Jiang Tian wouldn't arrogantly admit anything, even though that was the reality.

"Your superior may not have told you yet, but your first-class merit award is on its way and will be delivered to you soon," Minister Tan continued.

Jiang Tian was pondering.

"This merit is to commend you for your timely intervention in Yanjiang, preventing a huge loss and rescuing Secretary Mu and Miss Su. Given the difficulty and danger of this operation, after serious and rigorous discussion, it was determined that you fully deserve this first-class merit award," Minister Tan couldn't help but admire.

Damn it.

Even if Elder Mu had fallen alone, it wouldn't have been enough for a First-Class Merit.
