The selection process for First-Class Merit is very rigorous, considering a comprehensive evaluation of the extent of loss saved for the country and organization, and the magnitude of the negative impact mitigated.

It's not enough to simply say you risked your life in the process.

While the process might be considered more significant if you returned lying down, normally the evaluation criteria are relatively minor. The focus remains on the extent of loss saved for the country and the benefits generated.

And clearly,

Elder Mu himself might not have enough, only qualifying for a Second-Class Merit, but with Su Tong included, the combined impact is terrifying.

This First-Class Merit is absolutely not something to be underestimated.

"So, perhaps your title of 'Six Merits King' will need to be changed," Minister Tan said with a hint of envy.

Seven Merits King.

Wow.

One of them is even a Special-Class Merit.

Even Minister Tan is envious.

"Just ask me, who wouldn't be envious?"

Even Minister Tan only has two First-Class Merits. The key point is that he's been a police officer for decades, and even now, the top officer only has two.

Normally, most people would only need one Second-Class Merit for their entire lives, or even be enough to last a lifetime.

"Merit doesn't matter. To me, one more or one less doesn't matter, after all, it's all about serving the people," Jiang Tian said with a slight smile and a relaxed tone.

Damn it.

Minister Tan's eyelids twitched.

This emotionless voice was the greatest insult to him, but thinking of Jiang Tian's merits, Minister Tan's lips twitched.

"Also, the First-Class Hero award for you has been sent along as well," Minister Tan sighed.

This had already been decided; it had been finalized during the overseas mission.

And the First-Class Hero award made Jiang Tian's eyes light up slightly.

Originally, they were going to award him the title of Second-Class Hero, but they ran out of manpower, so they submitted the First-Class Hero award instead.

It was a mid-way change.

However, such matters require rigorous review from the highest levels.

“Wasn’t the application period for First-Class Hero award over?”

How could they change it mid-way?

Jiang Tian had almost given up hope.

“Mainly because someone higher up spoke up; someone is paying attention to you,” Minister Tan said calmly.

Hiss!!!

Jiang Tian gasped.

To be honest.

Jiang Tian had gained a lot along the way.

But without a doubt, among the many leaders he had come into contact with, the highest-ranking were Provincial Governor Huai, Minister Tan, and Chief Gao.

Even Minister Tan seemed extremely wary, which made Jiang Tian gasp in shock.

Minister Tan didn't give Jiang Tian a chance to speak, continuing, "You're lucky, kid. That person originally wanted to meet with you, but coincidentally, Wenye Group ran into trouble, so it was temporarily put on hold. But once everything is settled, you'll probably have to go to Beijing."

Jiang Tian's heart raced.

"Everything you do is under the watchful eyes of those above, down to the smallest detail. So, go ahead and do it with confidence. Yunhai isn't afraid of chaos, but as long as we can reshape Yunhai and curb crime throughout the entire province, even if the whole province is turned upside down, we're not afraid. The sky won't fall," Minister Tan said.

To be honest,

Minister Tan was genuinely shocked.

Because of this kid.

Heaven knows, Minister Tan had just been calculating how many first-class merits Jiang Tian had taken away from him when Gao Xiaolong called again, applying for a first-class merit.

Who could handle applying for a first-class merit every day?

The key is, his merits are more than enough; after all, he saved two people in Yanjiang, and both were quite influential.



Chapter 394 President Tan has gone mad, he's bestowing upon you the title of Eighth Prince.


For a normal person, one Elder Mu would probably be enough.

Even with Jiang Tian's considerable achievements, with Su Tong's presence, wow, the influence of the two of them is increasingly outrageous; a first-class merit is inevitable.

Before Minister Tan could even catch his breath, the phone rang again.

The Wenye Group had collapsed.

A pillar industry!

The massive Wenye Group collapsed in an instant.

It was all cleared out; not only was the Wenye Group connected to foreign forces, but its entire history of rise was that of a drug lord.

This, wow, this is absolutely unbelievable.

The sky has fallen.

Minister Tan instantly realized the sky had fallen. This was an absolutely groundbreaking case in Yunnan Province; Minister Tan even keenly sensed a premonition—a premonition that the clouds had parted to reveal the sun.

This, without a doubt.

It was a huge, groundbreaking case.

And the result was as expected. When Minister Tan asked who did it...

Gao Xiaolong simply mentioned Jiang Tian. The entire case, from initial clues to the final discovery, was almost entirely Jiang Tian's doing, even collecting all the evidence.

They were merely the executors.

"Very well."

First-class merit.

Minister Tan had just submitted another application for Jiang Tian's first-class merit.

Strictly speaking, this wasn't just a seven-time merit king; it was an eight-time merit king.

And the kind that only counts as first-class and special-class merits.

Who could withstand that?

"Don't call yourself Jiang Tian anymore. How about you choose a code name like 'Eighth-Class Sage King'?" Minister Tan said with a smirk.

Eighth-Class Sage King?

Jiang Tian instantly understood what he meant. He had considered his own merits, but this made his brow furrow.

Eighth-Class Sage King meant that Jiang Tian's sixth first-class merit was on its way, and his seventh was already being applied for?

Goodness.

Jiang Tian hung up the phone, rubbing his temples as he processed the news from Minister Tan.

The main issue was the sheer volume of information that was making his heart flutter.

Undoubtedly.

The higher-ups were watching.

Yunhai had become a grand stage.

All sorts of shady characters had jumped in, after all, Jiang Tian's actions in Yunhai were being constantly monitored by some unspeakable entity.

How could the people in Kyoto not notice?

Plus, Jiang Tian's name was extremely well-known in Kyoto.

Instantly, Yunhai became a hot commodity.

But Jiang Tian thought of Han Qi, a slight smirk playing on his lips. He could only say…

The Yunhai stage is big enough; come if you dare.

“Smiling so sinisterly, what are you thinking?” Su Tong rolled her eyes at Jiang Tian.

“Am I?” Jiang Tian touched his lips.

Su Tong chuckled, "Just kidding."

Jiang Tian's forehead creased with annoyance.

But then Su Tong's expression turned thoughtful, and Jiang Tian pondered how to bring up the issue of taking over the Wenye Group.

"Actually, I've always wanted to sincerely apologize to you for that night," Su Tong said, staring intently into Jiang Tian's eyes.

"You were deceived too," Jiang Tian said, taking a sip of red wine.

Jiang Tian had drunk wine before, but he had to admit, he'd never tasted anything this bitter. He'd also heard that the better the wine, the more bitter it was.

"Is it not good?" Su Tong asked Jiang Tian, ​​frowning.

"It's not that it's bad, I just don't like it. Back when my family was poor, I loved drinking five-yuan bottles of wine. We couldn't really tell the difference between good and bad," Jiang Tian shook his head.

This made Su Tong smile slightly. "Actually, ever since that night, I've always thought of you as a mysterious, elusive hero. But today, you've shown me another side of a hero."

"What hero? I'm just an ordinary person," Jiang Tian said, still considering how to begin.

After all, Wenye Group wasn't a small matter. Taking it over carried certain risks; there was always a feeling of being taken advantage of.

But currently, it seemed only Tianxing Group could guarantee its survival.

Su Tong readily agreed: "That day, when you got out of the car, it really startled me. I heard people talking about the danger of the Yanjiang River, and I thought you were trying to commit suicide. I thought, what kind of person would be so foolish as to jump in and kill themselves? I thought you were some kind of thief."

Jiang Tian smiled slightly: "I didn't expect you to think that way back then. Rather than saying I was trying to commit suicide, I was confident. It was you jumping in that was real suicide. I had just rescued a few people I could, and then I heard another young girl jumped in. I almost fainted."

Su Tong blushed slightly and explained: "I didn't think that much at the time. Your bureau chief came and said you were a great hero, a hero with a first-class merit. I thought, if you really died there, I think I would feel guilty for the rest of my life."

They clinked glasses, and Jiang Tian drank it all in one gulp.

Looking at Su Tong, whose face was slightly flushed, Jiang Tian said, "You're quite foolish. I never do anything I'm not confident about. Even if I am, it's always because my sacrifice can avert a huge loss for the country."

At least, a loss greater than his own.

But so far, Jiang Tian felt that nothing could be a greater loss than his own sacrifice.

These words, however, deeply shocked Su Tong.

A tremendous shock.

Su Tong's heart raced.

She stared blankly at Jiang Tian for a long time before finally speaking, "After you saved me, I wanted to know more about you, but when I tried to investigate you, my father stopped me. He didn't tell me about your past, only that you're a narcotics officer, a very dangerous policeman, and that you're probably the greatest policeman in the country right now, without a doubt. I've always been curious, you look about my age, how did you manage to do that?"

"Perhaps it's talent, or perhaps it's my ability that's enough, which has given me a greater responsibility to solve problems," Jiang Tian replied.

"I imagine you must have fought your way through the battlefield, invincible against drug lords, to become a hero. You must have killed a lot of people, right?" Su Tong asked, her voice brimming with curiosity.

This question startled Jiang Tian, ​​who chuckled in surprise. His mind went blank; even with his photographic memory, he couldn't recall the exact number. Since his rebirth, he seemed to kill countless people every day, but those were all drug dealers. So Jiang Tian nodded, "Many, countless."

Su Tong's smile deepened.

Her face was flushed a vibrant red, whether from drinking or something else.

She was very beautiful.

At this moment, even with his expertise in criminal investigation, Jiang Tian couldn't help but fall into deep thought.

After all, a woman's heart is unfathomable. Even someone as brilliant as him couldn't fathom what Su Tong was thinking or what she wanted to do.

"Miss Su..."

"My name is Su Tong. You can call me by my name. Calling me 'Miss Su' is too formal, after all, you're my savior," Su Tong corrected him.

"Su Tong, I wonder if you've heard of the Wenye Group?" Jiang Tian inquired.

Su Tong's eyes flickered.

And Jiang Tian's eyelids twitched uncontrollably.



Chapter 395 A Horrifying Discovery: The Greatest Case in History


*Hiss!*

A gust of wind blew by, and Jiang Tian, ​​standing on the bridge over the Yanjiang River, couldn't help but shiver.

At this moment.

Jiang Tian recalled his conversation with Su Tong and couldn't help but gasp.

He hadn't driven.

Because he'd been drinking.

Of course, Jiang Tian was rational and understood Su Tong's status.

So, from the beginning, Jiang Tian hadn't intended to do anything with Su Tong. However, later, during their conversation, Su Tong expressed a desire to drink, and Jiang Tian had no choice. After all, he'd heard that business deals were often made over drinks.

For the sake of the happiness of tens of millions, even hundreds of millions, of people in Yunhai—no, the thirteen cities of Yun Province—he drank quite a bit of red wine with Su Tong.

Then, when the issue of the Wenye Group was raised, Su Tong nodded without hesitation.

This completely baffled Jiang Tian. After all, he had prepared a PowerPoint presentation beforehand, ensuring there were no problems, but without any analysis, she agreed immediately.

Finally.

Su Tong added, "What you said is absolutely true. In my heart, you are the strongest hero, an invincible general. I often think about how you fought those drug lords, how dangerous and how lonely you were. I would reject anyone else, but I would never reject you."

Okay, okay, Jiang Tian felt a chill run down his spine. Not because of that, but because Su Tong later hugged him and warmly invited him home.

After Jiang Tian politely declined, Su Tong's reaction startled him.

He...

Sensed murderous intent.

From Su Tong's gentle and grateful face, he sensed the madness hidden in those beautiful eyes.

Two words instantly flashed through Jiang Tian's mind:

Yandere.

Jiang Tian wiped the cold sweat from his forehead in lingering fear.

Who could have imagined that a young girl with an extremely strong sense of self-respect and a firm moral compass, so consumed by guilt that she contemplated suicide, was secretly such a terrifyingly obsessive and possessive person?

Jiang Tian quickly hailed a taxi and left after escaping.

He didn't drive, because he knew better than to drink and drive. Jiang Tian had drunk quite a bit that day, finishing two whole bottles with Su Tong.

Su Tong herself had drunk more than a bottle, but didn't seem too drunk.

This made Jiang Tian realize that Su Tong was not simple.

"If it's not necessary, try to avoid contact in the future," Jiang Tian said, still shaken.

If he had a choice, he would rather fight hundreds of drug dealers than have anything to do with Su Tong. In just a short while, Jiang Tian was already covered in cold sweat.

A cold wind blew past, and Jiang Tian gradually calmed down.

Regardless, the outcome was still good.

Su Tong had agreed. Of course, in this kind of situation, it would be best if Su Zhenxing were involved, but Su Tong's involvement provided some flexibility.

Of course, there were risks, but the rewards were equally staggering.

By shedding those illegal assets and starting afresh, Tianxing Group had made a huge profit.

Overall, Tianxing Group wasn't at a loss.

As for what happened next...

Jiang Tian checked the time; it was already past 11 PM.

The reason he got off at Yanjiang was because of what Wen Yulin had said today.

He hadn't said much.

Just news of a special merit award.

"Shimiao Village."

Wen Yulin hadn't revealed much at the time, so Jiang Tian planned to investigate first.

To be cautious, Jiang Tian specifically went to investigate at night.

If he had concrete information and details, Jiang Tian planned to call for backup. If there were no problems, sending too many people might alert the enemy.

Shimiao Village was located in Yanjiang, along the river, crossing two mountains.

Even just to be on the safe side, Jiang Tian didn't take the main road. Upon arriving, he discovered that Shimiao Village only had one paved road leading out.

Reaching the outskirts of the village, Jiang Tian crouched on a tree and observed the surroundings.

What he saw was...

Peaceful and tranquil.

It had rained a little that day, making the air somewhat damp, but the moonlight was exceptionally bright, even in the dead of night.

There was no sound or movement within the village.

It was eerily quiet.

But then...

With the sound of cars approaching, Jiang Tian's gaze swept across the area.

Several cars drove out of the village.

The year 2000—this era was gradually heading towards chaos, an era about to unleash a great storm.

In this era, most of the future giants and tycoons had already begun to establish themselves, waiting for the opportune moment to take off, and some had already begun to show their talents.

Even so, the overall economic environment at the grassroots level was still in a slump.

Most wealthy cities had barely managed to achieve basic subsistence; being a household with ten thousand yuan was considered remarkable.

While Yunhai was also a first-tier city, many areas deep in the mountains remained undeveloped, and the people there still struggled on the edge of poverty.

Especially Shimiao Village.

Jiang Tian had crossed two mountains, winding and turning, to finally arrive.

The location was extremely remote, even near the border.

The village, in theory, had no potential for development.

However, the sheer number of cars was clearly suspicious.

This wasn't the era twenty years from now, where every family owned a car.

"Something's not right," Jiang Tian muttered to himself, stroking his chin.

So, when Jiang Tian activated the scanning radar...

Hiss!!!

Holy crap.

Jiang Tian gasped.

Not to mention anything else, the red dots on the cars were practically everywhere.

Every single one was bright red.

Over a thousand crime points.

Especially since Jiang Tian was now approaching the edge of the village, this kilometer-long area covered the entire village.

So, when Jiang Tian looked over, good heavens, the scanning radar was practically covered with a dense mass of red dots.

In other words...

This is...

Jiang Tian's head was buzzing.

If it were just ordinary drug crimes, that would be one thing.

But...

When Jiang Tian saw the red light on the map, he instantly understood why Wen Yulin had called it a Special Merit.

This was more than just a Special Merit.

It was practically the biggest case ever.

The number of people involved was enormous.

Most importantly...

How much crime value could they possibly accumulate from this single operation?

Jiang Tian swallowed hard. If they could wipe them all out, he'd be rolling in money.

The key point was...

This village, in the entire Yunhai market, was undoubtedly a significant force.

This wave of attacks dealt a massive and devastating blow to drug-related crimes.



Chapter 396 Contrast, Giving You More Responsibility, Yu Hui's Surprise


Immediately, Jiang Tian began to ponder Shimiao Village in his mind.

What truly puzzled and perplexed Jiang Tian about Shimiao Village at this moment was that, logically, such a large-scale case shouldn't have gone unnoticed so quietly.

Or rather, in his past life, if Shimiao Village had truly been targeted, Jiang Tian wouldn't have been unaware of it; otherwise, he would have noticed it beforehand.

However, even though Jiang Tian now possessed a photographic memory, he had absolutely no recollection of Shimiao Village. This could only mean two things.

First, Shimiao Village in his past life had simply remained undiscovered and undetected.

After all, in his past life, the criminal organizations throughout Yunnan Province had completely succumbed to the rampant growth of the times. The entire province was practically intertwined with criminal groups, leaving the authorities with no choice but to make a painful but necessary decision.

A swift and decisive crackdown, including the military sealing off the border and a thorough purge from top to bottom.

In this massive crackdown, the drug crimes in a single village, compared to the criminal gangs across Yunnan Province, didn't seem like particularly big or outrageous news.

Unfortunately, these measures resulted in Yunhai's economy plummeting to the bottom of the national rankings, essentially halting its development altogether.

Just as it was about to take off, the rampant and out-of-control drug crimes shattered all hope for Yunhai, bringing all development to a complete standstill.

Almost all the companies that could relocate fled at the first sign of trouble.

In short, in Jiang Tian's memory, there was a famous book from later generations called "Yunhai's Lost Twenty Years," written by a Yunhai author twenty years later.

It detailed how Yunhai, once one of the few first-tier cities in China, could have become a central city in Southeast Asia.

However, it was completely brought to a standstill by the criminal gangs.

Without the influence of the criminal gangs, Yunhai's future development might have even surpassed several other cities in China, perhaps even becoming a super-first-tier city.

Because, aside from Kyoto and Donghai, Yunhai was planned from the outset to become the economic center of Southeast Asia.

This included establishing land trade routes, among other things.

However, unfortunately, the rampant spread of various criminal groups and individuals, escalating to the point of near-out-of-control, was a clear realization from above.

If crime couldn't be controlled, it was better to halt it.

The safety of all cities in the country couldn't be jeopardized because of Yunhai.

In other words, the details of Yunhai's lost twenty years revealed that interaction with Southeast Asia inevitably led to criminal groups, while eliminating criminal groups made trade with Southeast Asia extremely difficult.

This seemed to present an unsolvable dilemma.

Even Jiang Tian,

could only do what he could, within his capabilities,

to minimize crime in Yunhai.

Lost in thought, Jiang Tian glanced at Shimiao Village but didn't linger. The situation was serious, and Jiang Tian couldn't afford to launch a direct attack that would alert the enemy.

That would be utter nonsense.

Leaving aside how Jiang Tian is currently fighting with the entire village, it's possible that all the village's key criminals will flee after the fight breaks out.

Therefore, dealing with Shimiao Village won't be so simple.

Of course, Jiang Tian hasn't been idle. He thoroughly scouted the area around the village and discovered that besides the main road at the front, there's a smaller path at the back of the village.

Following this path, Jiang Tian walked for a while and surprisingly discovered it was a smuggling route. What could be smuggled from a village almost entirely involved in criminal activities?

It was undoubtedly drugs.

This was precisely why Jiang Tian's brow furrowed. Just think, how many drugs could this drug den produce, capable of creating such terrifying criminals?

How much drug could they manufacture in a day?

And how much drug would they sell in a year?

This is a terrifying number that cannot be calculated in detail.

As expected.

After realizing the problem, Jiang Tian returned the way he came almost without hesitation and headed straight for the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

That's why, even though it's overtime, everyone except those on duty has already gone home at this hour.

The work is busy, but no matter how busy the Narcotics Control Detachment is, they still need time to rest; they can't work 24/7.

"Sister Yu Hui, why are you on duty again?"

Jiang Tian saw Yu Hui yawning as he entered.

"Why are you here?" Yu Hui asked in surprise.

"Truth or lie?" Jiang Tian sat across from Yu Hui at her desk.

Bright sunlight streamed in, and Jiang Tian rubbed his temples. Without a doubt, besides Yu Hui, there were probably many others working overtime.

It was already past midnight.

They were still working past midnight.

No wonder people say criminal investigation is stressful; Jiang Tian figured maybe those in drug enforcement just didn't have the chance to say that.

"What do you mean by 'lie'?" Yu Hui perked up a bit.

Looking at Yu Hui's dark circles—no, her entire eye area was practically black—Jiang Tian noticed they had two prominent dark circles under her eyes.

Especially the way her eyes stared at Jiang Tian, ​​giving off a sinister feeling.

Jiang Tian vividly remembered how Yu Hui used to be so radiant at work, always wearing exquisite light makeup, her fair skin flawless.

Now, looking at the bloodshot eyes and those dark circles, a wave of guilt instantly washed over Jiang Tian.

“The lie is, I heard you were working overtime today, Sister Yu Hui, so I came to cover for you,” Jiang Tian said with a smile.

Yu Hui’s face lit up with surprise for about half a second, then her gaze turned cold as she looked at Jiang Tian: “What’s the truth?”

“Congratulations, Sister Yu Hui, you might have to shoulder a bit more responsibility,” Jiang Tian said after a moment’s thought.

Although Jiang Tian really wanted to take over Shimiao Village right now, after all, it was a top-tier merit award!

If he took over this village, Jiang Tian was certain he’d get a top-tier merit award.

Because the criminals there were terrifying, and most importantly, the scale of their crimes was enormous.

If Shimiao Village were to be eradicated, all the drug dealers and others who had traded with it in recent years would be brought to justice.

The scale and the scale of this repercussions would be immense and terrifying.

It is foreseeable that an unprecedented anti-drug storm is about to sweep through.

However, haste makes waste. Jiang Tian understands that resolving the Shimiao Village issue requires consideration of many aspects and cannot be rushed.

Many factors need to be considered. Even if reports are submitted in a short time, information and clues need to be gathered, and some evidence needs to be obtained.

Otherwise, Jiang Tian's words alone are insufficient to convict someone, especially in such a horrific case. He cannot claim to possess a scanning radar.



Chapter 397 Hacker Entries: A Thorough Investigation of Shizhu Village


As expected.

These words ignited a blazing light in Yu Hui's dark-circled, bloodshot, and lifeless eyes.

"Add...add more responsibility?" Yu Hui stammered, unable to believe it, unable to accept the sudden surprise.

After all, within her system, what adding responsibility meant was obvious to everyone.

Yu Hui's heart pounded.

Jiang Tian looked at Yu Hui with a beaming smile. After all, Yu Hui was only about two years older than Jiang Tian, ​​and had only recently become a full-fledged officer after working for a little over two years.

If it came to adding responsibility, Yu Hui, now a third-level police superintendent, would soon become a second-level police superintendent.

That would be a great honor for her family.

Although she couldn't compare to an freak like Jiang Tian, ​​who had risen to the rank of police inspector and team leader in such a short time as an intern.

Although she was just a junior clerk.

But even a junior clerk has her own dreams!

"Team Leader Jiang, don't worry, I will definitely not let you down." Yu Hui's eyes burned with passion.

But Jiang Tian swallowed hard, looking at Yu Hui, who was noticeably thinner than before. He remembered what Yu Hui looked like at the beginning.

She was about 1.6 meters tall, but with a very full figure.

Now, her once plump and cute face looked sinister.

Jiang Tian was even afraid that Yu Hui might suddenly die.

"Of course, so, Sister Yu Hui, I need your help to solve the case." Jiang Tian couldn't bear to deceive Yu Hui's feelings.

But the moment Jiang Tian uttered those words,

he seemed to hear the sound of shattering glass.

No.

Jiang Tian came to his senses. It wasn't shattered glass, but rather, Sister Yu Hui's sincere heart.

Jiang Tian looked at Yu Hui, who stood frozen in place.

Her already sinister face now seemed twisted, her bloodshot eyes glaring at Jiang Tian as if she wanted to transform into a vengeful ghost and devour him.

“Solve…the case?” Yu Hui’s voice trembled slightly.

Jiang Tian forced a slight nod, suppressing his inner turmoil.

“A new case?” Yu Hui’s voice sounded panicked.

Jiang Tian glanced at the mountain of documents piled on Yu Hui’s desk.

For a moment, he had a sudden, almost fatal, feeling.

If he remembered correctly, during the day, there was a stack of documents in front of Yu Hui’s desk that barely reached her head; now, it had doubled, almost overflowing her desk.

However, these documents were clearly few compared to the new case. Jiang Tian was certain that once they cracked Shimiao Village, they would have three more stacks of documents this high to verify and investigate.

“Team Leader Jiang, can’t you rest for a bit?” Yu Hui clutched her heart.

Working overtime like this, she felt like she was about to collapse from exhaustion, with the job seemingly endless.

Yu Hui even assured her that her workload over the past two years didn't match the workload of the last three days.

"Sister Yu Hui, you've worked so hard. I'll treat you to a late-night snack later," Jiang Tian quickly apologized.

But to be honest, Jiang Tian was finding Yu Hui, this mature and capable woman, increasingly useful.

Coincidentally, Jiang Tian's team was indeed short of clerical staff.

Currently, Song Qiuwan was handling almost all the clerical work, as well as data investigations, evidence collection, and so on.

Ultimately, however, it was somewhat wasteful of Song Qiuwan's skills for this task, given that Jiang Tian's team was already short-handed.

Replenishing manpower was also a challenge. From Jiang Tian's observation, the new recruits were of varying quality. Some of the replacements from lower-level branches even had a red glow on their faces—a closer look revealed, wow, undercover agents had joined them.

Of course, none of this escaped Jiang Tian's notice; he had just conducted a cleanup and reported on the situation.

In short, the municipal bureau was actually doing a pretty good job in this regard.

Drug enforcement officers.

They face various temptations every moment.

A drug dealer's slight action could be equivalent to a narcotics officer's total income for a year, several years, or even a decade.

Besides the danger to their lives, they also endure tests of their humanity every moment.

Jiang Tian had even discussed this with Jiang Peng. Previously, those drug lords hadn't taken the police seriously; they were in a weaker position, so they hadn't been forced to pay a high price.

But as the sense of crisis intensified, the drug lords were gradually approaching madness.

Recently, they had repeatedly attempted to bribe officials with huge sums of money, but all their attempts were exposed, followed by coordinated arrests. They managed to apprehend a few minor drug dealers, and a few even received commendations.

But this was the glory of the municipal bureau.

Not the branch bureaus.

Even Jiang Tian's previous cases, which garnered him numerous collective credits—the bonuses alone were more than enough.

But the branch bureaus didn't.

The municipal bureau could resist the temptations of profit.

It's normal for some branch bureaus to succumb to the pressure occasionally.

But now, Jiang Tian was powerless to change this situation. All he could do was try his best to bring all the drug lords to justice.

To make Yunhai a no-go zone for drug lords.

And to successfully restore Yunhai after the twenty years it lost.

Or perhaps, keeping pace with the times, Yunhai might truly become the largest city in Southeast China in the future.

It might even become the central city of Southeast Asia.

Its future development prospects are no less than those of Donghai, known as the Pearl of the East.

“The village chief of Shimiao Village is named Shi Hailiang. He is forty-six years old this year and was elected village chief three years ago.”

Yu Hui stared at the information she had found on the computer.

As she said before, the narcotics police have vast authority; they can find a lot of information.

“Can you check their bank statements?” Jiang Tian asked.

“Although it’s a bit troublesome, it’s still possible.” Joking aside, Yu Hui’s focus when working was off the charts.

Most importantly.

If Jiang Tian remembered correctly, the narcotics squad’s authority would be available a few years from now.

“When I was in school, I studied computer science and even won awards,” Yu Hui explained.

“So, you’re planning to…?” Jiang Tian’s heart skipped a beat.

Could it be…?

The legendary king of hackers.

He wants to witness the legendary hacker firsthand and break into the bank's system.

Jiang Tian's eyes lit up. Excellent! Going to the bank to retrieve the statements would be quite troublesome. Yu Hui's method was much simpler and more convenient, and crucially, it could be done silently.

Of course, most importantly, Jiang Tian was eager to see the hacker's skills; he was itching to try.

Having dealt with so many drug dealers, Jiang Tian had accumulated a wealth of hacking entries.

Many of his entries hadn't been synthesized because he had more than enough sin points, so he had stored them away for future use.

Among these were hacking entries.



Chapter 398 Are you crazy? Using the Eighth Prince as a stepping stone to your fame?


Yu Hui, her eyes bloodshot, couldn't help but glance at Jiang Tian with disdain: "Brother, if I were that awesome, would I be sitting here working overtime?"

"Then how?" Jiang Tian asked.

"Just hack into Shi Hailiang's phone and we'll find out," Jiang Tian said, his face full of contempt.

Jiang Tian suddenly understood.

But looking at Yu Hui, Jiang Tian's eyes couldn't help but light up.

Excellent.

A commendation, a huge commendation.

"Don't worry, Sister Yu Hui, I'll apply for a second-class merit for you later," Jiang Tian said directly, his mind racing.

He had done so much; giving Yu Hui a second-class merit was no problem at all.

"The merit is yours, you think you can just give it to me like that!" Yu Hui looked incredulous.

Jiang Tian didn't explain; after all, Yu Hui knew nothing about the case in Shimiao Village.

Soon.

Through Yu Hui's actions, they hacked into Shi Hailiang's phone and obtained his account information.

“One, ten, hundred, thousand, thirty-two million, wow!”

Yu Hui’s eyes widened.

Jiang Tian wasn’t surprised either, after all, this was the combined strength of an entire village.

Moreover, according to the investigation, everyone in Shimiao Village shared the same surname.

Unlike many criminal groups plagued by infighting and internal conflicts, they were exceptionally united. Furthermore, with everyone working together, they weren’t afraid at all in the dark market.

Under these circumstances, after three years, earning only ten million a year, wasn’t much for the village as a whole.

“Looks like Shi Hailiang is a good village chief, not taking too much of a cut,” Jiang Tian chuckled.

Based on his observations, while it might be an exaggeration to say every household in Shimiao Village lived in luxury, every family owned a small villa.

Although two thousand years ago, being a “ten-thousand-yuan household” wasn’t as extravagant as it was ninety years ago, it was still a significant status symbol.

Even in later generations, how many people had ten thousand yuan in net savings and no debt?

Two thousand years ago, every family lived in a small villa; it would be a joke if there weren't ghosts inside.

Sure enough, as the investigation progressed...

The obvious things were easy to uncover.

For example, the entire village had 730 households.

This was a relatively large village.

Moreover, the village was quite exclusive, with only one surname: Shi.

The 730 households had a population of approximately 2,000.

After removing the elderly and children, about 600-700 young adults were counted and investigated. What they found shocked even Yu Hui.

Of these 700-plus people, not necessarily 100%, but 60-70%, they had savings in the millions.

Millions in two thousand years—what a terrifying concept!

And the remaining, those who seemed poor, weren't necessarily innocent. Investigations into their families revealed that a large portion of their money was in the hands of their parents.

Moreover, not everyone could save money; these people also spent it lavishly.

It inevitably involves things like gambling, prostitution, and drugs, especially gambling, which has led to many losing everything.

In short, it's no exaggeration to say that no one is innocent.

"It's past two o'clock, let's go eat."

Jiang Tian looked at the time, feeling a pang of shame. Seeing Yu Hui in this state because of him, he truly felt terrible.

The key issue was that Jiang Tian had eaten three meals that evening, while Yu Hui, due to overtime work, hadn't even had dinner. She'd only just eaten instant noodles when Jiang Tian arrived. Her life was so miserable it would make a dog weep.

“This is what you owe me. I’m going to treat you to a big meal,” Yu Hui’s eyes lit up.

Jiang Tian readily agreed.

Then Jiang Tian went into the Municipal Public Security Bureau and, after a detailed review of the situation, had basically sorted things out.

Especially the files on the fifty-odd people; besides Yu Hui, two newly hired young women were also helping with the work.

Those two young women were also working overtime, tears streaming down their faces, and they watched longingly as Jiang Tian took Yu Hui out for a late-night snack.

“Alright, you can come along,” Jiang Tian waved his hand dismissively.

The Municipal Public Security Bureau had recently expanded its staff considerably. Not only others, but even Yu Hui had been assigned two apprentices.

Unfortunately, even with the help of two apprentices, Yu Hui had still endured so much hardship to reach this state. It was hard to imagine how terrifying the workload would be if she were working alone.

"Long live Team Leader Jiang!"

Also in the anti-drug squad, the two young women, though newcomers, had heard plenty of Jiang Tian's legendary stories.

Especially knowing that Jiang Tian was their age but was already a police inspector made it feel like a dream.

"Go ahead and eat whatever you want. Team Leader Jiang has enough bounties from arresting criminals to last you three lifetimes." Yu Hui followed weakly behind Jiang Tian. Exhaustion had overwhelmed her after the initial excitement.

But after they left and Jiang Tian got into the car with the three young women,

"Team Leader Jiang, is there something you need?" one of Yu Hui's apprentices asked.

"Nothing." Jiang Tian turned his gaze away from one direction.

Because he didn't see any red dots, but he keenly sensed being watched.

"Report, target spotted."

A short distance from the city police station, a small black car, its interior pitch black, suddenly erupted with a voice.

*Whoosh!*

Accompanying the sound, with a swishing sound, a figure slumped forward from the back seat.

Upon closer inspection, one could see a white bandage wrapped around its nose, the entire area almost swollen to face level.

It looked utterly comical and ridiculous.

"The target got into the car with three young girls, at 2 AM, what a beast!" A man in a suit in the passenger seat couldn't help but feel envious, his eyes reddening as he began to curse.

"He really is a black sheep, a cancer." The figure in the back seat, with its swollen nose, spoke in a low, clenched voice.

"Director, should we continue monitoring him?" the man in the passenger seat couldn't help but ask.

"Follow him from a distance. By the way, did you take pictures just now?" Han Qi said through gritted teeth.

That's right, it was Han Qi.

Back home, Han Qi grew angrier the more he thought about it.

Plus, coincidentally, the person Han Qi spoke to, or rather, someone in Yunhai, was an old classmate of Han Qi's who was very familiar with him, but that old classmate worked for the procuratorate.

During the conversation, that old classmate accused Jiang Tian of numerous crimes.

For example, embezzlement and bribery.

And falsely claiming credit.

They had already been watching him for a while, but Han Qi hadn't believed it at first.

That's why I decided to meet him.

Unexpectedly, he saw through my scheme during the meal. He was truly brazen, spending tens of thousands of yuan on a single meal—what else could it be but embezzlement?

The most terrifying thing was that that evening, my old classmate even brought up Jiang Tian's assets.

It's confirmed, the case is solved.

So I'm keeping an eye on him.

"Let this black sheep become my whetstone in Yunhai. His rank is more than enough to declare that I, Han Qi, Deputy Director Han, am here in Yunhai to get things done." Han Qi's lips curled into a cold smile.



Chapter 399 He was simply a disaster.


"Guangliang, you're absolutely right. This Jiang Tian really has huge problems. Just now, under our surveillance, he actually went out with three young girls. Putting everything else aside, with such a chaotic private life, what kind of hero is he? He's just a fraud, a shameless black sheep."

Holding the phone, Han Qi dialed Yuan Guangliang's number.

That's right.

Actually, while Han Qi's life was ordinary, it wasn't particularly ordinary either. His family was probably quite powerful in their hometown.

But in Beijing, what kind of place is that? To put it bluntly, if you threw a stone at someone, eight out of ten people might be section chiefs.

His family was well-off because Han Qi's uncle had held a relatively important position. In Beijing, there's a saying: "Don't underestimate a section chief."

The relationships in Beijing are very complex. Some section chiefs might be useless, but others are so powerful that even provincial leaders have to queue up for them.

Han Qi's uncle held a position far higher than a department head, and more importantly, he held an important post. Furthermore, Han Qi's grandfather had left behind some political legacies.

However, while Han Qi's uncle had recently been promoted, he was no longer in a crucial position. While not entirely idle, his future prospects were bleak.

It was under these circumstances that Han Qi was transferred to Beijing at great expense.

On the way, Zhao Qiuni explained all this to Jiang Tian, ​​giving him a basic understanding of Han Qi. Therefore, Zhao Qiuni didn't take the matter to heart, and after Jiang Tian asked her not to interfere, she didn't say much.

Because, in her view, this family background was nothing special.

They weren't even particularly prominent in Beijing.

However, they were still not to be underestimated.

According to Zhao Qiuni, with a lack of promising young talents, Han Qi's family had practically exhausted all the connections and influence of the older generation to capitalize on Yunhai's potential.

In short, there wasn't much else they could realistically hope for.

The higher-ups did consider giving their family a chance, so Han Qi was transferred to the Discipline Inspection Commission. Han Qi's uncle, however, used immense connections, even pushing Jiang Peng towards promotion before Jiang's was even confirmed.

All this was to ensure Han Qi's uncle could succeed him as bureau chief, securing a path to advancement.

Jiang Tian, ​​on the other hand, was currently just a lowly police inspector.

While it seemed impressive, it wasn't anything extraordinary, and it was normal for him to be largely ignored.

However, what Jiang Tian couldn't understand was why some people would eat his food and then curse him.

Ingratitude.

And the worst part was, they were so self-righteous about it.

That's why Jiang Tian directly attacked him at the restaurant.

He was the one who opened up the situation in Yunhai; without him opening that situation, they wouldn't have had any chance of operating in Yunhai.

After arriving, they arrogantly believed they represented absolute justice—what a load of rubbish!

Of course, Jiang Tian was unaware of this.

Before Han Qi came, his family instructed him to find a way to make a name for himself in Yunhai. Yunhai is different from Beijing; opportunities abound, and simply put, even small acts of kindness could earn him personal merit.

With good luck, a second-class merit award could pave a smooth and easy path to advancement. Or, if he did something unexpected, he might even have a chance to earn a first-class merit award.

Wow, with a first-class merit award and a powerful background, future promotions would be virtually guaranteed—who could compare?

So Han Qi rushed here immediately, eager to make his mark.

And desperately wanted to establish a foothold.

Coincidentally, Han Qi had an old classmate in Yunhai working at the procuratorate, a minor leader in the anti-corruption bureau. The information this classmate provided further excited Han Qi.

According to Yuan Guangliang, Jiang Tian was already being watched.

Because of his good relationship with his old classmate, he was involved in this case. Han Qi was initially wary, but now…

"Whether he proves himself or not is unimportant. The main issue is that if such a black sheep remains in the organization, I can't imagine the disaster he'll bring."

"Such a cancerous growth, promoted to a high position, is simply a catastrophe!"

Han Qi sighed.

A large portion of Jiang Tian's information was top secret, and it would be very difficult for Han Qi to obtain Jiang Tian's files from the Discipline Inspection Commission.

Therefore, much of the information about Jiang Tian was obtained through Yuan Guangliang.

It recorded that Jiang Tian had received multiple first-class merit awards, but the difficulty of obtaining these awards was cleverly concealed, giving the impression that it was very easy—"I could do it too"—and even raising suspicions of fabricated merits.

"However, according to the information you provided, after examining Jiang Tian's accounts, you should have been able to confirm embezzlement and should have been placed under investigation long ago, not waiting until now." Han Qi wasn't stupid; he was somewhat wary.

In Han Qi's mind, upholding justice was paramount, and eliminating bad apples was his life's creed. He might ignore such bad apples if he didn't know them, but once he did, he would eliminate those who betrayed the organization.

"Hey, you found out? Actually, the main thing is, do you even know Gao Xiaolong?" Yuan Guangliang countered.

"Gao Xiaolong?" Han Qi exclaimed.

How could he not have heard of that name?

After all, Gao Xiaolong held a terrifying position; one more step and he'd be on his way to the top.

"According to our investigation, Jiang Tian once saved Chief Gao during a mission, and then had a close relationship with Chief Gao's daughter. This bad apple might be deceiving Chief Gao. We're afraid Chief Gao will object to our actions, so we haven't had the chance. If you don't dare, then forget it." Yuan Guangliang seemed genuinely speaking the truth.

"Gao Yangyang?"

Han Qi's eyes widened. He was in this circle; how could he not know her?

Especially Gao Yangyang.

"That's right, you know him?" Yuan Guangliang asked in surprise.

"I've met him before, but we've never spoken."

Han Qi was somewhat evasive, mainly because he wasn't of a high enough rank.

Locally, they're incredibly powerful, but in Kyoto, they're only considered slightly above the middle class. The key issue is that their family's breadwinner, the old man, passed away years ago, and the family is facing a transitional period. However, even a weakened giant is still powerful; many people will still give them a chance out of respect for the old man.

Like this time.

They practically exhausted all their connections and resources, managing to secure two opportunities at once.

All this so that after gaining experience here, they could rise to the top in one fell swoop.

Han Qi, whether for the sake of his family's responsibilities or to uphold justice, absolutely cannot turn a blind eye to such a black sheep.



Chapter 400 CS, you actually brought a few...


“If you say such things again, we're no longer friends. I, Han Qi, am upright and honest. No matter how powerful his background, he can never betray justice. I can hardly imagine how desperate and helpless the people would feel if even law enforcement started to violate justice. If I didn't see it, I might let it go, but since I did, I absolutely cannot turn a blind eye.” Han Qi was moved by his own words.

“Perhaps, this is the meaning of our existence,” Yuan Guangliang echoed, also moved by his own words.

“Wait for my good news. Now that we've identified this black sheep, I'll gather some evidence and then we'll close the net.” Han Qi's gaze was heavy as he looked at the brightly lit Yunhai. At this moment, it felt as if the safety of tens of millions of Yunhai residents rested on his shoulders.

Heavy.

“Don't worry, our anti-corruption bureau is also keeping a close watch, but don't act rashly. Keep a close watch first. I also need your help; don't let Jiang Tian, ​​this black sheep, get away.” Yuan Guangliang instructed.

"Okay, I got it, I'm hanging up now."

Han Qi hung up the phone with a disdainful look, then slumped back in his seat like a Ge You (a famous Chinese comedian known for his relaxed, reclining posture).

Under his gaze.

Jiang Tian, ​​driving an ordinary Volkswagen, drove away from the Municipal Public Security Bureau with three young girls.

Han Qi chuckled as he looked at the phone. This Yuan Guangliang really thought he was a fool. How could he have acted without solid evidence?

After all, he carried a heavy burden—the burden of the people, and the responsibility of his family.

A heavy burden indeed.

Without a brain, would he have become a key figure in his family's career?

He stopped pretending with Jiang Tian because Jiang Tian had slipped up.

Those tens of thousands of yuan seemed insignificant, but Han Qi's sharp eyes saw through it immediately. He showed no remorse whatsoever, still claiming innocence.

Earned money earned through legitimate channels, hard-earned and risky, wasted so easily.

So righteous on the surface.

In Han Qi's eyes, Jiang Tian was a lurking tiger, a black sheep, secretly accepting countless bribes from drug dealers.

Of course, Jiang Tian was unaware of these things.

However, this didn't stop Jiang Tian from realizing he was being followed.

He hadn't acted because the person following him wasn't on the radar, meaning they weren't a criminal.

This was the radar's only drawback.

It could accurately locate criminals, but those without a criminal record wouldn't appear on the radar, except for those who genuinely committed crimes.

For example, peeping at the neighbor's aunt showering wouldn't accrue any guilt.

But this didn't guarantee the person wasn't involved with a criminal organization. What if they were coerced, like the driver who blocked their police car earlier?

Paid for a job.

The first time he committed a crime due to ignorance and backwardness, there was no red dot.

However...

When Jiang Tian glanced at the rearview mirror again, it was blocked by the balloon.

Out of the corner of my eye, I saw Yu Hui in the passenger seat, touching up her light makeup in the vanity mirror. But that wasn't the most important thing; the main thing was that the girl's face was completely flushed.

"Sister Yu Hui, are you running a fever?" Jiang Tian exclaimed in surprise, suddenly realizing something.

As he spoke, he anxiously touched Yu Hui's forehead.

Good heavens, such a competent workhorse—no, a competent partner—was truly hard to find. In the entire Criminal Investigation Division, Jiang Tian felt that Yu Hui was even more reliable than Zhao Wei in assisting with solving cases.

Whatever information was needed, a message was sent, and she'd bring it back in three minutes.

If she got overworked, the difficulty and time it would take Jiang Tian to solve cases would increase by at least ten percent.

So, Jiang Tian's action was purely out of concern.

But Yu Hui blushed.

Her face was completely red.

It was indeed burning hot.

When Jiang Tian placed his hand on her forehead, Yu Hui's scalp felt like it was steaming; it instantly turned a warm red.

“Team Leader Jiang, oh no, you’re the one with a fever! Your whole family has a fever! And besides, you can’t just touch a girl’s forehead like that! If you do, you have to take responsibility. Can you afford to take responsibility?” Yu Hui exclaimed in a panic.

“Huh?” Jiang Tian felt he was making a mountain out of a molehill.

Mainly, Jiang Tian, ​​to be honest, didn’t have any thoughts on these things, cough cough, but right now, with the mission at hand, he really didn’t have much of a thought for them.

Even if he had thought about it, he’d have to finish the mission first.

Most importantly, Jiang Tian found Yu Hui pleasing to the eye. This girl was so cute; he wanted to tease her all the time, but he definitely didn’t have any dirty thoughts.

So when she saw the excessively pure look in Jiang Tian’s eyes,

a wave of guilt washed over Yu Hui.

“Master, you’re making a mountain out of a molehill.”

“Yeah, yeah, Team Leader Jiang clearly saw you had a fever and was worried you’d overwork yourself.”

The two apprentices in the back seat couldn’t stand it anymore and started pointing fingers at Yu Hui.

Yu Hui's face flushed even more.

Mainly, Yu Hui couldn't control her emotions because she had just had the illusion that Jiang Tian kept turning his head to look at her balloon.

But thankfully, Jiang Tian was actually looking at the car following them.

Playing around as usual, Jiang Tian joked with Yu Hui and teased the girl from time to time—after all, Yu Hui used to be such a lively person, but lately she'd become quiet and withdrawn from work.

This wouldn't do; a gloomy mood wasn't conducive to work.

However, Jiang Tian kept observing the car following them, even while joking around, but from Yu Hui's perspective, Jiang Tian was staring at her...

And he kept staring, endlessly.

It was past 2 AM, and there wasn't much food available.

Jiang Tian, ​​along with the three girls, found a restaurant that was still open at 2:30 AM to cook, and ate a little midnight snack to fill their stomachs.

"Waaah, this is so wonderful! If only I could have such a delicious late-night snack every day!"

Yu Hui's little apprentice burst into tears.

Jiang Tian glanced at her and couldn't help but sigh. The little girl looked so malnourished, it was so pitiful.

"Officer, that'll be thirty-two yuan."

He hadn't even changed out of his police uniform in his haste to leave, and the owner was being careful when bringing the food.

Looking at the bill, Jiang Tian couldn't help but sigh.

It was 2003.

Prices hadn't skyrocketed yet.

There were still remote areas where people couldn't even afford food.

Tonight, Jiang Tian treated three young girls to dinner for thirty-two yuan, and they were moved to tears, deeply grateful to him.

This inevitably reminded Jiang Tian of the night before when he spent over twenty thousand yuan on dinner, only to find out they were investigating him for embezzlement and bribery.

Some people, you have to admit, are just crazy.

"I'll see you through to the end," Jiang Tian said, offering to be your protector. "It's not safe for young ladies to go home so late."
